articles.headline: Both chloroquine plus low-dose primaquine and pyronaridine-artesunate plus low-dose primaquine achieved >98% day-28 adequate clinical and parasitological response (ACPR: 98.7% and 98.8%) against uncomplicated P. vivax in a phase 2/3 randomized trial of 176 Ethiopian adults. Day-42 ACPR for pyronaridine-artesunate was 96.4%. Both regimens were well-tolerated with only mild adverse events
articles.title_original: Efficacy of chloroquine plus low-dose primaquine and pyronaridine-artesunate plus low-dose primaquine against Plasmodium vivax malaria in adults and transmission to mosquitoes in Ethiopia: a phase 2/3, observer-masked, randomised, parallel-group trial.

articles.headline: In 51,457 children under 5 from 17 sub-Saharan African countries, >one-third were infected with malaria. Risk factors included age 1–2 years (AOR 1.46), low household wealth (AOR 1.94), rural residence (AOR 2.69), and high community illiteracy (AOR 1.76). Protective factors were maternal primary education (AOR 0.84) and insecticide-treated bed nets (AOR 0.80)
articles.title_original: Determinants of malaria prevalence among children under 5 years of age in 17 sub-Saharan African countries: insights from Malaria Indicator Surveys.

articles.headline: Qualitative study of 23 interviews and 4 focus groups with caretakers of children with neurologic deficits (motor, cognitive, behavioral, sensory, epilepsy) after severe malaria in Uganda revealed mixed perceptions, community mistreatment, mental/emotional distress, financial strain, and limited access to specialized care. Caretakers had misconceptions about causes but learned to adapt
articles.title_original: Perceptions and experiences of caretakers of children with neurologic deficits after severe malaria in Uganda.
articles.journal: Int J Qual Stud Health Well-being

articles.headline: Phase 2/3 randomized trial of 199 P. falciparum-infected adults in artemisinin-resistant endemic Ethiopia found artemether-lumefantrine plus primaquine achieved 96.8% day-28 PCR-corrected ACPR and pyronaridine-artesunate plus primaquine achieved 98.9%; day-42 ACPR for pyronaridine-artesunate was 96.5%. Both showed efficacy despite prevalent pfkelch13 R622I resistance mutations and pfhrp2/3 deletions, with only mild adverse events
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the effectiveness of artemether-lumefantrine and artesunate-pyronaridine each combined with single low-dose primaquine in adults with Plasmodium falciparum infection in Ethiopia: a phase 2/3, observer-masked, randomised, parallel-group trial.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 16 observational studies (1,104 malaria patients with acute kidney injury) identified factors significantly associated with mortality: ventilator requirement (OR 30.35), ARDS (OR 18.50), hypotension (OR 18.23), neurological involvement (OR 12.83), disseminated intravascular coagulation (OR 12.00), oliguria (OR 3.41), and hyponatremia/acidosis/hyperkalemia/jaundice (OR 3.67–5.03)
articles.title_original: Prognostic factors in malaria patients with acute kidney injury: a systematic review and meta analysis.

articles.headline: Among 49,590 blood donors in Saudi Arabia, malaria prevalence was significantly higher in family replacement donors (5.3%) versus voluntary donors (2.9%), with the difference particularly evident among non-Saudi nationals. No significant differences were observed in HBV, HCV, HIV, or syphilis between donor groups
articles.title_original: Transfusion-transmitted infections among blood donors in Saudi Arabia: demographic and malaria risk differences by donation purpose.

articles.headline: Review of 66 clinical trials and post-marketing studies involving 64,101 participants receiving 143,170 doses of Matrix-M-adjuvanted COVID-19, influenza, combination, or malaria vaccines demonstrated all vaccines were well-tolerated across diverse populations. R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccine and NUVAXOVID had favorable reactogenicity profiles compared to licensed vaccines, with >10 million doses administered worldwide
articles.title_original: Safety of Matrix-M-adjuvanted COVID-19, seasonal influenza, combination influenza-COVID-19, and malaria vaccines: a review of the evidence.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 31 studies (2010–2024) found chlorfenapyr-pyrethroid nets consistently outperformed pyrethroid-only nets, with 1.8-fold increased mosquito mortality (95% CI 1.5–2.1) and 40–60% reductions in malaria incidence in community trials. However, early chlorfenapyr resistance emerged in Anopheles gambiae in Central Africa (RR 2.4, p=0.01), linked to CYP6P4 overexpression
articles.title_original: Chlorfenapyr-pyrethroid nets for pyrethroid-resistant malaria vectors: efficacy, resistance risks, and policy implications.

articles.headline: Community cross-sectional study of 3,489 Tanzanians found asymptomatic malaria prevalence of 26.2% by qPCR. Among 802 sequenced P. falciparum isolates, 3.0% from high-transmission areas carried pfk13 partial-resistance markers (Y493H 0.2%, R561H 2.0%, A675V 0.7%) versus none from low-transmission areas; 48% carried NFD haplotype associated with lumefantrine reduced susceptibility
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic malaria: a hidden reservoir for the transmission and circulation of artemisinin partial-resistant Plasmodium falciparum in Tanzania.

articles.headline: In vitro and in vivo mouse studies demonstrated cold stress (27–33°C in culture, 4°C in vivo) suppressed P. falciparum growth in a temperature-dependent manner. Cold stress enhanced suppression when combined with low-dose artesunate and decreased erythrocyte invasion efficiency through downregulation of invasion-associated genes including ama1
articles.title_original: Cold stress restricts malaria parasite growth by affecting erythrocyte invasion.
articles.authors: Odagawa T, Saiki E, Kanuka H

articles.headline: Genome-wide RNA editing analysis of stage-specific P. falciparum 3D7 at 16h, 24h, 32h, and 40h revealed extensive A-to-G, G-to-A, and C-to-T editing events peaking at 32h trophozoite stage and declining by 40h schizont stage. G-to-A and C-to-T conversions predominated; RNA editing-associated pathways were downregulated at 40h
articles.title_original: Genome-wide detection of conserved stage-specific RNA editing events for nuclear genes in the Plasmodium falciparum 3D7 malaria parasite.

articles.headline: Phylogenomic analysis of 52 P. relictum (avian malaria) infections using sequence capture revealed two cryptic species with genetic introgression, including species GRW4 that devastated Hawaiian avifauna approximately 100 years ago. No evidence of positive selection was found in the Hawaiian GRW4 clade despite hypothesized release from host selective pressures
articles.title_original: Phylogenomic analyses reveal introgression and cryptic speciation in the globally distributed, vector-transmitted pathogen Plasmodium relictum.

articles.headline: Ethnopharmacological review of 208 Indonesian medicinal plants with reported antimalarial activity; 99 crude extracts/fractions from 61 species evaluated against P. falciparum, with 17 species undergoing chromatographic isolation yielding 146 compounds. Four compounds exhibited potent anti-plasmodial activity: samaradine X (IC50=14 nM), norcaesalpinins E (IC50=90 nM), methyl 15-acetoxylansiolate (IC50=170 nM), and dehatrine (IC50=170 nM), all superior to chloroquine (IC50=290 nM). Findings support Indonesian medicinal flora as a source of novel antimalarial agents
articles.title_original: Harnessing Indonesian natural products against neglected tropical diseases: Malaria.

articles.headline: Pooled analysis of 206,723 children across 17 sub-Saharan African countries (DHS/MIS, 2006–2022) examining malaria RDT positivity by housing quality. Modern housing was independently associated with lower odds of RDT positivity (β=−0.543, p<0.001) net of socioeconomic status, sanitation, and net usage; the protective effect persisted across diverse ecological and climatic contexts. Housing quality functions as a durable, non-insecticidal structural determinant of malaria vulnerability
articles.title_original: Housing and malaria risk: Evidence across ecological and climatic gradients in sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling study comparing a comprehensive malaria control model with a reduced-complexity version via Bayesian MCMC validation, parameter identifiability, and sensitivity analysis. The reduced model captured essential transmission dynamics of the full model while providing improved computational efficiency and flexibility. Simplified model demonstrates utility for evaluating and implementing diverse malaria control interventions
articles.title_original: Model reduction and analysis: A case study of a malaria control model.

articles.headline: Comment on comparative surveillance analysis of malaria and Chagas disease in non-endemic regions of Brazil (2007–2024)
articles.title_original: Comment on "Malaria and Chagas disease in non-endemic settings in Brazil: a comparative analysis of national surveillance data (2007-2024)".

articles.headline: Analysis of genetic polymorphism and natural selection in Plasmodium vivax merozoite surface protein-1 carboxy-terminal 42 kDa (PvMSP-142) across 88 Vietnamese isolates from four endemic provinces. PvMSP-142 exhibited polymorphisms from nucleotide substitutions and recombination with balancing selection maintaining diversity; most polymorphic sites concentrated in the 33 kDa fragment while the 19 kDa fragment was well-conserved globally. Findings inform rational design of MSP-1-based malaria vaccines
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism and natural selection of merozoite surface protein 1 C-terminal 42 kDa in Vietnamese Plasmodium vivax isolates.

articles.headline: Genotyping of 70 P. falciparum isolates from Gabon (2019, ~19 years after chloroquine withdrawal) for pfcrt and pfmdr1 drug-resistance mutations. SNPs detected in 25.71% of pfcrt (most frequent: N75E/K at 24.3%, M74I at 22.86%, K76T at 17.14%) and 20% of pfmdr1 (Y184F at 18.57%); wild-type haplotypes predominated (CVMNK 74.29%, NEYND 80%), with mutant haplotype frequencies substantially lower than historical reports. Results indicate progressive restoration of chloroquine susceptibility over time
articles.title_original: Status of pfcrt and pfmdr1 mutations in Plasmodium falciparum isolates in Gabon in 2019 after chloroquine withdrawal.

articles.headline: Review of human immunodeficiency virus, tuberculosis, and malaria as leading infectious causes of critical care admissions in low- and middle-income countries (>80% of global population). Article synthesizes burden of disease, clinical presentation, diagnostic strategies, and management principles for these pathogens in critically ill patients
articles.title_original: The Global Intensive Care Unit: Human Immunodeficiency Virus, Tuberculosis, and Malaria.
articles.authors: Riunga F, Wambua GM, Lee BW

articles.headline: In vitro study of saturated anacardic acid (SAA) on P. falciparum- and P. berghei-stimulated THP-1 monocytes and BV-2 microglia, plus in vivo cerebral malaria model in CBA mice infected with P. berghei ANKA. SAA increased survival in infected mice and decreased RelB, TNF-α, IL-8, MCP-1, ROS, NO, and COX-2 production while modulating NF-κB pathway signaling. Results suggest SAA as a promising adjunctive therapy to prevent severe malaria progression
articles.title_original: Saturated anacardic acid shows promise as adjuvant therapy for preventing severe malaria via attenuation of inflammatory response by modulation of the NF-κB pathway.

articles.headline: Corrigendum to paper on interactions between urbanization, malaria infection, and avian cloacal microbiome
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to 'Interactions between urbanization, malaria infection and avian cloacal microbiome' [Environ. Res. 297 (2026) 124073].

articles.headline: Immunoinformatic design of three multi-epitope subunit vaccine constructs targeting five Plasmodium proteins, incorporating CTL epitopes, HTL epitopes, B-cell epitopes, adjuvants, and linkers. All constructs were antigenic, non-allergenic, and non-toxic; construct 1 showed most stable TLR-4 binding with strong molecular dynamics stability. In silico immune simulations predicted immunogenic potential and cloning analysis indicated efficient E. coli expression; however, in vitro and in vivo validation studies remain necessary
articles.title_original: Designing a next-generation multi-stage vaccine for malaria prevention.
articles.authors: Arya H, Tripathi H, Bhatt TK

articles.headline: Molecular epidemiology of Plasmodium vivax merozoite surface protein 3α and 3β (Pvmsp-3α and Pvmsp-3β) genetic diversity in 207 field samples from Azad Jammu and Kashmir, Pakistan. PCR detected P. vivax in 188/207 (90.82%) and P. falciparum in 19/207 (9.18%). Among P. vivax samples, Pvmsp-3α amplified in 98/188 (52.13%) showing three allelic types (A: 66.33%, B: 11.22%, C: 13.27%), while Pvmsp-3β amplified in 90/188 (47.87%) with two types (A: 76.67%, B: 23.33%). Findings characterize P. vivax polymorphism and support control strategy design
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity of plasmodium vivax merozoite surface protein 3α and β field isolates.

articles.headline: Preclinical study in high-fat diet-fed mice (20–25 g, n=8/group) with streptozotocin-induced type 2 diabetes and P. berghei NK65 infection, treated with black cumin-enriched acha-based cookies for 14 days. Intervention suppressed parasitemia, reduced blood glucose, enhanced pancreatic and endogenous antioxidant enzyme activities, and decreased inflammatory markers (NF-κB, increased IL-10). Black cumin-enriched acha cookies demonstrated therapeutic potential for malaria-diabetes comorbidity
articles.title_original: Functional-Diet Potential of Black Cumin-Enriched Acha-Based Cookies on Plasmodium berghei-Infected Diabetic Mice.

articles.headline: Multi-component transmission-blocking vaccine (APN-1, CelTOS, cd-HAP with adjuvants CPG, MPL, QS-21) elicited robust Th1-biased IgG responses in BALB/c mice and substantially reduced P. falciparum NF54 oocyst load in Anopheles stephensi membrane feeding assays. The vaccine induced increased IFN-γ and TNF production without antigenic interference, demonstrating efficient transmission-blocking immunity potential
articles.title_original: Multi-antigen transmission-blocking malaria vaccine elicits Th1-biased antibody responses and SMFA-confirmed oocyst reduction in Anopheles stephensi.

articles.headline: A novel copper(II) complex containing 2-(tert-butoxy)-6-(1H-imidazol-1-yl)pyridine showed significantly higher in vitro activity against P. falciparum 3D7 (chloroquine-sensitive) than the free ligand, with low hemocytotoxicity (≤20% at >500 µg/mL). Coordination of the imidazole-based ligand to the metal center substantially enhanced antiparasitic activity
articles.title_original: Synthesis, Characterization, and Anti-Plasmodium falciparum Activity of a New Copper(II) Complex Containing 2-(Tert-Butoxy)-6-(1H-imidazol-1-yl)pyridine.

articles.headline: Systematic review of antimicrobial peptides (AMPs) from APD3 and PubMed (1965–June 2025) identified defensins and cecropins as primary natural anti-malarial AMP classes against Plasmodium spp., with promising efficacy but varying toxicity. Synthetic AMPs designed to enhance activity and selectivity while reducing toxicity show potential as alternative therapies for multidrug-resistant malaria
articles.title_original: Antimicrobial peptides in malaria and tuberculosis management: a systematic review of emerging evidence.

articles.headline: Retraction notice for study on TiO₂ nanoparticles prepared from seaweed Sargassum wightii and their larvicidal activity against malaria and filariasis vectors (Environ. Res. 2023)
articles.title_original: Retraction notice to 'Seaweed Sargassum wightii mediated preparation of TiO(2) nanoparticles, larvicidal activity against malaria and filariasis vectors, and its effect on non-target organisms' [Environ. Res. 225 (2023) 115569].

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 422 individuals in Al-Zuhra District, Yemen found 5% asymptomatic malaria prevalence (95% P. falciparum). Absence of window screens (APOR=9.56, 95% CI 2.15–42.51, p=0.003) and proximity to stagnant water (APOR=4.93, 95% CI 1.50–16.23, p=0.009) were strongly associated with infection, while education was protective (APOR=0.35, 95% CI 0.13–0.97, p=0.043)
articles.title_original: Prevalence of asymptomatic malaria and its associated factors in the Al Zuhrah district, Al-Hodeidah Government, Yemen, 2022.
articles.authors: Hajjam MA, Al Amad MA, Nasher SS

articles.headline: Analysis of 172 P. falciparum isolates from asymptomatic Tanzanian carriers revealed low nucleotide diversity in Pf12 (π=0.00042±0.00007) and Pf41 (π=0.00061±0.00004) with exceptionally high IgG seropositivity (97.7% and 98.3% respectively) and strongly correlated antibody responses (ρ=0.721, p<0.001). These conserved 6-cysteine merozoite antigens display limited polymorphism consistent with exposure-driven immunity and warrant vaccine development investigation
articles.title_original: Conserved genetic and antigenic profiles of Pf12 and Pf41 in asymptomatic Plasmodium falciparum carriers in Tanzania.

articles.headline: Review of Plasmodium host-pathogen interactions examining parasite invasion mechanisms, clinically relevant receptor-ligand antigens, antibody-antigen interactions, and parasite immune evasion strategies that inform ongoing vaccine and therapeutic development for malaria prevention and treatment
articles.title_original: Host-Pathogen Interactions in Malaria: Invasion, Neutralization, and Evasion.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 20 studies (14,556 households, 122,258 individuals, 2003–2023) on insecticide-treated net (ITN) utilization in Southeast Asia identified geographic region as strongest non-use determinant (coastal vs. delta OR=4.73, 95% CI 1.59–14.06), followed by socioeconomic status (poorest vs. richest OR=1.70, 95% CI 1.01–2.86). Substantial disparity between ITN ownership and actual use highlights need for area-specific interventions toward elimination
articles.title_original: Insecticide-treated nets utilization and determinants in Southeast Asia toward malaria elimination: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Characterization of 83 monoclonal antibodies against P. falciparum RIPR (component of PCRCR blood-stage invasion complex) revealed individual mAbs showed minimal neutralizing activity, but pools targeting RIPREGF(6-8) domains achieved high-level synergistic inhibition by constraining RIPR-tail conformation. These findings provide structural basis for next-generation blood-stage vaccine design combining multiple anti-RIPR mAbs with anti-CSS antibodies
articles.title_original: Analysis of monoclonal antibodies against the malaria invasion complex protein RIPR reveals the structural basis for synergistic antibody protection.

articles.headline: Implementation of Cambodia's Android-based Malaria Information System health center app across 761 health centers in 21 provinces demonstrated 99% data completeness (89/90 fields, 2024), 95.7% (66/69) case notification within 1 day, 100% reactive case detection within 3 days, and 96.3% user satisfaction. Real-time surveillance and rapid response capabilities supported malaria elimination with only 69 cases nationally reported (Jan–July 2025)
articles.title_original: Digital Health Center App for Community and National Malaria Surveillance in Cambodia: Implementation Case Study.

articles.headline: Ginsenoside Rg1 (G-Rg1), identified as P. falciparum hexose transporter (PfHT) inhibitor via molecular docking, effectively suppressed growth of both artemisinin-sensitive and artemisinin-resistant P. falciparum in vitro and potentiated dihydroartemisinin efficacy in combination and ring survival assays. In P. berghei ANKA-infected mice, G-Rg1 alone and combined with artemisinin reduced parasite multiplication and increased mean survival time
articles.title_original: Selective targeting of Plasmodium falciparum hexose transporter by phytochemical Ginsenoside Rg1 disrupts glucose metabolism and blocks development of parasite.

articles.headline: In 105 pregnant women living with HIV receiving monthly intermittent preventive treatment with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHA-PPQ) versus placebo (Mozambique and Gabon, 2021), median maternal dolutegravir was 835.1 ng/mL (248.1–1,687.6) with cord blood 1,204.2 ng/mL (456.4–1,883.3); median lamivudine 108.3 ng/mL (28.8–246.1) and 186.5 ng/mL (81.9–427.6) respectively. No significant differences between treatment and placebo arms; DHA-PPQ for IPTp does not affect ARV concentrations
articles.title_original: Effects of malaria intermittent preventive treatment with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine on antiretroviral drug concentrations in African pregnant women living with HIV.

articles.headline: Ferritin knockdown in adult Anopheles stephensi females caused profound reproductive tissue defects; β-thujaplicin identified as transmission-blocking agent significantly suppressing Plasmodium oocyst development; establishes iron homeostasis as dual-target axis for transmission-blocking interventions
articles.title_original: Targeting Iron Homeostasis Curtails Mosquito Fertility and Plasmodium Transmission Potential.

articles.headline: Case-control study of 250 P. falciparum cases and 200 controls in Saudi Arabia; IL-33 rs10118795 CT increased susceptibility (OR=1.584, p=0.022) while rs55726619 CT was protective (OR=0.042, p<0.001); rs10815393 CC and rs55726619 CC strongly enriched in children 1-5 years (adjusted OR=650.46 and 172.69, Fisher's p=7.12×10⁻¹¹ and 5.91×10⁻⁸); haplotype set 4 associated with susceptibility (p=2.76×10⁻⁶)
articles.title_original: Association of interleukin-33 gene polymorphisms with susceptibility to Plasmodium falciparum infection.

articles.headline: Ecological analysis of Mozambique surveillance data by district and region with precipitation events using Poisson GLMMs; nationally 8-week temporal lag between precipitation and increased malaria relative risk; regional variation: 6-8 weeks Northern, 10-12 weeks Central, 10-13 weeks Southern
articles.title_original: Exploratory analysis of the correlation between precipitation and storms and malaria cases from national surveillance in Mozambique.

articles.headline: Cell culture study with L. donovani and P. falciparum; NR-7h PROTAC degraded p38-MAPK dose-dependently reducing parasite load; for P. falciparum impaired growth and merozoite invasion; enhanced efficacy with Amphotericin B; no in vitro resistance or genetic determinants observed
articles.title_original: Chemical degradation of Human p38-MAPK reveals it as a potential host target to combat parasitic infections by Leishmania donovani and Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Validation study: 116 malaria-positive, 109 negative controls; extract-free RPA-CRISPR for P. falciparum and P. vivax; clinical sensitivity 93.1%, specificity 100% vs qPCR; detection limit ~12-20 parasites/μL; sensitivity by density: 97.8% high, 95.1% moderate, 82.8% low; 40-min ambient-temperature assay
articles.title_original: Extract-free one-pot ambient RPA-CRISPR detection of Plasmodium in whole blood.
articles.authors: Liao J, Su Y, Jiang F

articles.headline: Structure-guided optimization of KRS inhibitors; Compound 30 achieved >1000-fold selectivity, long half-life, single-dose cure in SCID mice, activity against drug-resistant P. falciparum strains and field isolates; unfortunately lacked oral bioavailability preventing clinical development
articles.title_original: Structure-Guided Optimization of Novel Inhibitors of Plasmodium Lysyl-tRNA Synthetase with Multistage Activity against Malaria Parasites.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study: 385 symptomatic individuals, Dejen Primary Hospital Ethiopia, April-Nov 2024; malaria prevalence 46.2%, P. falciparum 56.1% of cases; determinants—no formal education (AOR=2.16, p=0.001), proximity to breeding sites (AOR=2.15, p=0.020), no ITN use (AOR=1.72, p=0.001), no IRS (AOR=1.02, p=0.035)
articles.title_original: Assessment of malaria prevalence and determinants among clinically suspected individuals attending Dejen Primary Hospital, East Gojjam Zone, northwest Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Duplex ddPCR validation for P. vivax; Tak 2008 (N=25): 16% with multiple pvmdr1 copies, all single pvcrt-o; Kanchanaburi 2023-2025 (N=97): no multiple copies detected; quantification limits 22.4±3.5 (pvmdr1/pvtubulin) and 21.5±2.6 (pvcrt-o/pvtubulin) copies/μL
articles.title_original: Droplet digital PCR assays for Pvmdr1 and Pvcrt-o gene copy number variation determination in Plasmodium vivax.

articles.headline: Rwanda TES comparing capillary electrophoresis (msp1, msp2, glurp, microsatellites) vs amplicon sequencing (cpmp, ama1, cpp, csp) for recrudescence detection; TA40 and PfPK2 most sensitive for minority clones; WHO algorithm identified fewest recrudescences, 2/3 most; amplicon sequencing most consistent across algorithms
articles.title_original: Comparison of techniques and markers to distinguish Plasmodium falciparum recrudescence from new infection in Rwanda.

articles.headline: Geospatial analysis: Global Flood Database (2000-2018), malaria incidence, population data from 492 flood zones in 38 Sub-Saharan African countries; East/West Africa elevated malaria relative risk in flood zones; ~12 million P. falciparum-exposed during floods, representing one-third of flood-affected population
articles.title_original: Geospatial mapping of malaria risk in flood-prone zones of Sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: CHMI: 12 malaria-naïve adults received single MMV367 doses (3-1500 mg) day 8 post-P. falciparum 3D7 inoculation; parasite clearance half-life 2.2-4.3 hours (≥20 mg), viable parasite elimination 1.1-2.7 hours; MIC 6.3 ng/ml, MPC90 54 ng/ml; no in vitro resistance; cmax 2-4 hours, half-life 12.9-18 hours
articles.title_original: The pyrrolidinamide antimalarial drug MMV367 rapidly clears blood-stage Plasmodium falciparum in healthy adults with experimental malaria.

articles.headline: Experimental cerebral malaria (C57BL/6/Pb.ANKA); IV MSC administration day 0 reduced parasitemia, improved survival, preserved BBB integrity; macrophage polarization shifted with downregulated M1 markers (CD86, CD38), upregulated M2 markers (CD163, CD206, EGR-2); upregulated Arginase-1, GPR-18, FPR-1, PTGES-2; downregulated iNOS-2
articles.title_original: Macrophage markers and gene signature profiling reveals mesenchymal stem cells mediated immune modulation in Plasmodium berghei ANKA infection.

articles.headline: Using conditional mutants, researchers found that IMC55, an inner membrane complex protein predicted to be essential in P. falciparum, is dispensable for asexual replication, showing no defect in replicative fitness during the blood stage and suggesting the protein function is either redundant or primarily required in mosquito-stage development
articles.title_original: The inner membrane complex protein, IMC55, is dispensable for intraerythrocytic development of Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Researchers synthesized HLQ-102, a 2-position isomer of the quinolone ELQ-300, and demonstrated it targets the Qi site of the P. falciparum cytochrome bc1 complex, suggesting distinct binding pockets can accommodate large substituents at either the 2- or 3-position of the quinolone ring regardless of placement
articles.title_original: Revisiting 2-Substituted-4(1H)-Quinolones for Targeting the Plasmodium falciparum Cytochrome bc(1) Complex.

articles.headline: A qualitative study of 97 community members and healthcare providers in a Ghanaian subdistrict following pilot mass drug administration found perceived notable reductions in malaria episodes, with some households transitioning from frequent infections to rare/no illness, decreased healthcare costs, mild transient adverse events, and high reported willingness to participate in future MDA rounds
articles.title_original: Community perspective on feasibility of malaria mass drug administration: a qualitative evidence from the Eastern Region of Ghana.

articles.headline: This methodological paper presents a mathematical and time-series analysis of compartmental stock-and-flow models for insecticide-treated nets, proposing an iterative EM algorithm for calibration and applying it to delivery and household survey data from 44 countries over 24 years to estimate net distribution volumes
articles.title_original: Dynamical and time-series approach to understanding compartmental stock and flow models: a case study in malaria intervention models.

articles.headline: Among 353 febrile patients in Uganda, P. falciparum prevalence was 60.6% (n=214). Genotyping revealed pfmdr1 SNPs at codon 1034-1042 (29.6%), 86-184 (28%), and 1246 (25.8%), with combined prevalence of these and K13 propeller mutations at 90.1% (n=318); median parasite loads varied significantly among treated patients with mutations (p≤0.0001 to p=0.012)
articles.title_original: Polymorphism of pfmdr1 Gene Mutation Conferring Resistance to Artemisinin-Based Combination Therapy in Plasmodium falciparum in Patients at Gulu Regional Referral Hospital in Northern Uganda.

articles.headline: An ethnographic study in Ethiopia documented how malaria treatment-seeking is shaped by overlapping cultural (evil eye/buda, seasonal fever/busa) and biomedical explanatory models, with pluralistic pathways beginning with home remedies and progressing to biomedical care; gender dynamics and structural barriers (poverty, drug stockouts, weak infrastructure) significantly constrained timely care access
articles.title_original: The social experience of malaria treatment-seeking: pathways, gender, and inequality in Jimma Zone, Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Gerbi T, Teshome D, Sudhakar M

articles.headline: A case report describes a 29-year-old female with SLE, non-adherence to immunosuppressants, multivalvular Libman-Sacks endocarditis, and concurrent mixed P. vivax and P. falciparum infection presenting with hemorrhagic stroke (7×5.2 cm lesion) and high parasitaemia; despite IV artesunate and maximal supportive care, the patient died due to resistant thrombocytopenia and coagulopathy
articles.title_original: Libman sacks endocarditis with multivalvular involvement, hemorrhagic stroke, and concurrent mixed plasmodium malaria in systemic lupus erythematosus: a case report.

articles.headline: A quasi-experimental evaluation of malaria training for Nigerian community pharmacists and patent medicine vendors (baseline-follow-up Feb 2022–Dec 2022) found training significantly increased knowledge of diagnosis and treatment (β=0.052; p=0.03) and acceptability of WHO test-treat-refer guidelines (β=0.432; p=0.03), but had no effect on actual compliance or identification of quality drugs
articles.title_original: Effect of malaria training on community healthcare providers' compliance with malaria treatment guidelines in Nigeria using propensity score matching.

articles.headline: Among 3,009 hospitalized children in Gabon, 480 (15.9%) met WHO criteria for severe P. falciparum malaria with median age 6 years; overall mortality was 0.7% and 8.5% (n=41) required ICU transfer. Impaired consciousness (aOR 14.86; p<0.01) and coma (aOR 53.3; p<0.01) were strongest predictors of ICU transfer, with risk increasing sharply beyond three concurrent WHO severity criteria (aOR 10.59; p<0.01)
articles.title_original: Clinical profiles and predictors of intensive care unit transfer among children hospitalized with severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria in a tertiary care hospital in Libreville, Gabon: a retrospective study.

articles.headline: Among 1,034 febrile children (<15 years) in Gabon, the Sysmex XN-31 automated analyzer achieved malaria sensitivity of 98.4% (95% CI 96.9–99.9) and specificity of 99.9% (95% CI 98.8–99.9) compared to microscopy, though sensitivity was lower in children <5 years (96.9%) and at low parasitaemia <100/µL (90.7%); ROC analysis showed AUC of 0.991 (95% CI 0.984–0.997)
articles.title_original: Field diagnostic performance of the Sysmex XN31 automated hematology analyser for malaria screening among febrile children in a sentinel site for malaria surveillance site in Libreville, Gabon.

articles.headline: In a P. berghei ANKA experimental cerebral malaria model in rodents, IL-9 expanded myeloid-derived suppressor cells and enhanced pro-inflammatory phenotypes in macrophages and dendritic cells; IL-9 neutralization in vivo reduced immunopathology and increased host survival, while MDSC depletion decreased Th-9 cell populations and reduced their migratory potential through a positive feedback mechanism
articles.title_original: IL-9 Orchestrates MDSC Expansion and Inflammatory Programming to Amplify Immunopathology During Experimental Cerebral Malaria.

articles.headline: A case report documents cryptic (non-imported) severe P. falciparum malaria in an Italian patient with no malaria-endemic travel history, presenting with marked thrombocytopenia and 10% parasitaemia after staying in the Maremma receptive area; the patient was successfully treated with IV artesunate, with clinical improvement, and no transmission chain identified
articles.title_original: Cryptic severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Maremma (Tuscany, Italy), 2024: a case report from a historically receptive area.

articles.headline: Virtual screening of 1,500 compounds against P. falciparum DHODH identified three lead candidates; binding free energy calculations favored compounds 17433008 (−86.46 kcal/mol) and 17507474 (−85.91 kcal/mol) as most thermodynamically stable in 500-ns molecular dynamics simulations. Both proposed as promising leads targeting parasite pyrimidine biosynthesis pathway
articles.title_original: Virtual screening and molecular dynamics analysis of Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) inhibitors targeting pyrimidine biosynthesis pathway.

articles.headline: In naturally exposed children and vaccinated adults, IgG specific for P. falciparum surface antigens PfEMP1 and PfGARP showed marked Fc-afucosylation; antibodies to merozoite and sporozoite antigens were predominantly Fc-fucosylated. Children with Fc-afucosylated PfGARP-specific IgG had significantly reduced parasitemia risk independent of antibody levels
articles.title_original: Fc-fucosylation of Plasmodium falciparum-specific IgG varies with antigen and immunization.

articles.headline: WHO announced first malaria drug approved for infants and new rapid diagnostic tests for malaria detection
articles.title_original: WHO Announces First Malaria Drug for Infants and New Rapid Diagnostic Tests.

articles.headline: Transcriptome analysis of three lizard-infective Plasmodium species (P. floridense, P. azurophilum, P. leucocytica) revealed all transcribe canonical hemoglobin digestion pathway genes despite P. azurophilum and P. leucocytica producing no hemozoin. Evidence of selective pressure shifts on digestive enzymes indicates pathway conservation across Plasmodium with variable functional importance
articles.title_original: Hemoglobin Digestion Genes Are Conserved in Lizard-Infective Plasmodium Species With Different Host Cellular Niches.

articles.headline: In P. berghei ANKA-infected mice, hepatic copper accumulation paralleled cuproptosis marker upregulation (FDX1/DLAT/DLST) and hepatic stellate cell activation. Copper ionophore disulfiram exacerbated parasitemia and fibrosis; copper chelator tetrathiomolybdate reversed effects. Malaria-induced copper overload triggers cuproptosis-mediated HSC activation, exacerbating acute liver injury
articles.title_original: Copper overload drives hepatic stellate cell activation via cuproptosis to exacerbate acute liver injury in murine malaria.

articles.headline: Narrative review comparing murine malaria models with organoid and organ-on-chip platforms. Mouse models remain genetically tractable and cost-effective but poorly reproduce human malaria, particularly cerebral and placental forms. Organoids and organs-on-chips bridge reductionist assays and human disease, offering complementary tools for parasite-host interaction and drug response studies
articles.title_original: Beyond the mouse: organoids, spheroids, and organs-on-chips as the (inevitable) future of malaria research?
articles.authors: Dormoi J, Amalvict R, Pradines B

articles.headline: TaqMan real-time PCR assays targeting 18S rRNA genes were developed for P. inui, P. cynomolgi, and P. coatneyi with detection limit of approximately 5 parasites, amplification efficiency >95%, and linear relationship across 10–10⁶ DNA copies. Assays demonstrate high specificity and are suitable for routine zoonotic malaria surveillance and submicroscopic infection detection
articles.title_original: Accurate and sensitive TaqMan real-time PCR assays to detect and quantify simian malaria parasites Plasmodium cynomolgi, Plasmodium inui and Plasmodium coatneyi.

articles.headline: Novel surface plasmon resonance biosensor with multilayer N-FK51a+SiO2+Cu+HfO2+black phosphorus structure achieved angular sensitivity 541.42 deg/RIU, FWHM 1.98°, detection accuracy 0.502 deg⁻¹, and quality factor 272.21 RIU⁻¹. Artificial neural network modeling enhanced prediction accuracy for ring-stage malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Artificial intelligence enabled performance evaluation of an enhanced SPR biosensor for malaria diagnosis.

articles.headline: Analysis of malaria microscopy competency in China: 64 WHO-ECAMM participants (average age 41 years) with 29 certified Level 1 and 17 Level 2; 72 NCAMM participants with 10 Level A and 33 Level B. Years of microscopy experience correlated moderately with competence (ρ=0.4, P<0.01); species identification scores improved significantly (Z=−7.3, P<0.01)
articles.title_original: Development of human resources in malaria microscopy during and after the malaria elimination phase in China.

articles.headline: In 56 surveyed Indian water buffaloes, PCR detected P. bubalis in 8.93% (5/56) versus 5.36% (3/56) by blood smear; human RDT was negative. Clinical signs: fever, tachycardia, tachypnoea, icteric mucous membranes. Hematobiochemical changes: marked anaemia, thrombocytopenia, hyperbilirubinemia, hypoalbuminemia. Phylogenetic analysis confirmed first report of P. bubalis Type I in domestic Indian buffaloes
articles.title_original: Clinical, hematobiochemical and phylogenetic studies of bubaline malaria in Indian water buffaloes (Bubalus bubalis).

articles.headline: Extracellular vesicles mediate P. falciparum heat shock adaptation through ESCRT-dependent trafficking. In PfVps60 knockout parasites, EV export of chaperones PfHsp70-x and PfHsp110 was impaired, causing early viability loss after >40°C stress. Supplementation with heat-stressed EVs partially rescued tolerance dose-dependently, suggesting EV-mediated proteostasis as antimalarial vulnerability
articles.title_original: Extracellular vesicles participate in proteostasis and heat shock adaptation in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Study optimizing the impact of a novel spatial repellent to reduce malaria incidence in Western Kenya; specific methods and results not provided in title
articles.title_original: Optimizing the impact of a novel spatial repellent on malaria incidence in Western Kenya.

articles.headline: Among 240 P. falciparum cases, IgG responses to PfHRP2 peptide were significantly higher in severe malaria (SM), cerebral malaria (CM), and non-severe cerebral malaria (SNCM) compared with mild malaria (MM, P<0.0001), while Pfhrp2 gene copy numbers were similar across groups. PfHRP2-specific IgG responses positively correlated with age and var P5 peptide (P<0.01), suggesting host-pathogen immune interactions determine severity outcomes rather than parasite genetics
articles.title_original: Differential IgG responses to PfHRP2 across the malaria severity spectrum.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: Cross-sectional analysis of 1,924 Mozambican households (2022-3 DHS) found household net use had no independent association with child malaria positivity after controlling for wealth. Wealth index showed strong dose-response: middle-wealth 43% less likely (aOR=0.57; P=0.0048), highest-wealth 84% less likely (aOR=0.16; P<0.0001) versus lowest-wealth. Nampula and Cabo Delgado showed significantly higher risk (aOR 18.97 and 14.70)
articles.title_original: Risk factors for malaria among children under five living in net-owning households in Mozambique from the 2022-3 Demographic and Health Survey.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: Light microscopy in Ethiopia (474 cases) showed 24.7% misdiagnosis among positive cases and 18.8% among negative compared to qPCR reference. Species-specific misdiagnosis: 21% P. falciparum, 28.3% P. vivax, 34.8% mixed infections; 18.8% microscopy-negative cases were qPCR-positive due to submicroscopic infections or reading errors. Competency-based training and quality assurance are essential to prevent continued transmission
articles.title_original: Diagnostic gaps in light microscopy for malaria case management and control in Dilla Zuria Health Facility, southern Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Corrigendum to previously published study on multi-antigen transmission-blocking malaria vaccine targeting Th1-biased antibody responses and oocyst reduction in Anopheles stephensi
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "Multi-antigen transmission-blocking malaria vaccine elicits Th1-biased antibody responses and SMFA-confirmed oocyst reduction in Anopheles stephensi" [Vaccine 84 (2026) 128683].

articles.headline: RNA interference knockdown of peritrophins Per-44 and Per-48 in Anopheles stephensi mosquitoes impaired vector fitness with increased mortality and reduced fecundity; both genes were upregulated during Plasmodium ookinete invasion. Per-48 knockdown caused the most severe phenotype with significantly reduced oocyst burden. These Class III chitin-binding proteins are essential for peritrophic matrix integrity and represent viable transmission-blocking targets
articles.title_original: Molecular characterization of midgut Peritrophin-44 and Peritrophin-48 and their role in Plasmodium development.
articles.authors: Dubey A, Chauhan VK, Chaitanya RK

articles.headline: Systematic review of delayed tafenoquine (≥300 mg, ≥3 days post-chloroquine initiation) for P. vivax radical cure identified only 2 eligible RCTs (1996-1999) using higher-than-current TQ doses (500-600 mg on day 4-6). Based on limited data, the review concluded insufficient evidence to definitively recommend delayed TQ, though it appeared superior to chloroquine alone and potentially preferable to 14-day primaquine in low-adherence settings
articles.title_original: Efficacy of Delayed Tafenoquine Administration for Radical Cure of Plasmodium vivax Malaria: A Systematic Review.

articles.headline: Theoretical design of a multilayer surface plasmon resonance biosensor for simultaneous glucose and P. falciparum HRP-II quantification achieved 1.82-fold sensitivity gain over single-layer reference, with angular sensitivities of 221.3 deg/RIU (glucose) and 212.7 deg/RIU (HRP-II), detection limits of 0.15 mg/dL and 0.8 ng/mL respectively, and high computational accuracy (R²≥0.994). Per-chip material cost estimated USD 1.50-4.00
articles.title_original: Machine Learning Surface Plasmon Resonance Biosensor for Simultaneous Detection of Glucose and Plasmodium falciparum Biomarkers.

articles.headline: Individual patient data meta-analysis of 5 Ethiopian studies (1,378 patients) found P. vivax recurrence by day 180: 61.0% (95%CI 55.3-66.8) without primaquine, 16.6% (12.0-22.7) with low-dose 3.5 mg/kg, 8.9% (6.4-12.4) with high-dose 7 mg/kg. High-dose reduced recurrence versus low-dose (aHR 0.40; 95%CI 0.24-0.68; p=0.001). Both doses showed acceptable tolerability with uncommon clinically significant hemolysis in G6PD-normal patients
articles.title_original: Analysis of the effect of primaquine dose on efficacy, safety, and tolerability in patients with Plasmodium vivax malaria in Ethiopia: a systematic review and individual patient data meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Review synthesizing knowledge on zoonotic malaria vectors (particularly P. knowlesi) in Southeast Asia, highlighting taxonomic complexity within vector species complexes, landscape changes from deforestation and habitat fragmentation, and gaps in vector surveillance and control strategies. Emphasizes need for integrated, context-specific, One Health approaches to address transmission challenges in rapidly changing environments
articles.title_original: Zoonotic malaria in Southeast Asia's changing landscapes: vector complexity, challenges, and future strategies.

articles.headline: Scoping review of 6,849 records identified 33 serological surveillance studies across 7 Southeast Asian countries (2000-May 2025): 26 cross-sectional, 5 longitudinal, 1 prospective cohort, 1 control trial. Research shifted from high-transmission to elimination contexts with increasing multiplex assays and protein microarrays detecting P. falciparum, P. vivax, and P. knowlesi. Seroconversion estimation and machine learning effectively identified transmission declines and residual hotspots missed by microscopy/molecular methods
articles.title_original: Serological surveillance for malaria elimination in Southeast Asia: a scoping review.

articles.headline: Next-generation ITN durability in Mozambique (2020-2022, 24 months) showed all types maintained 100% efficacy against susceptible mosquitoes. Against resistant Anopheles: Olyset Plus declined 73% to 40%, Royal Guard improved 43% to 87%, Interceptor G2 maintained ≥92%. Hut trials confirmed Interceptor G2 superiority. Chemical analysis revealed PBO 8% and chlorfenapyr 32% remaining at 24 months, supporting pyrethroid-chlorfenapyr prioritization in resistant areas
articles.title_original: Durability of the insecticidal activity of next-generation insecticide treated nets distributed for malaria control in Mozambique: Findings from the New Nets Project (2020-2022).

articles.headline: Investigation of three approved amide-based drugs binding P. falciparum Plasmepsin V (PfPlmV) showed saquinavir as the most potent inhibitor: competitive inhibition with IC50=0.35±0.01 μM and Ki=0.14±0.02 μM, directly engaging the catalytic dyad (Asp118, Asp365). 5-Fluorouracil showed mixed-type inhibition with higher IC50/Ki; favipiravir bound weakly at the cleft periphery. Results suggest saquinavir repurposing potential as a selective PfPlmV-targeting antimalarial
articles.title_original: Repurposing amide-based drugs unveils inhibition mechanisms of plasmodium falciparum Plasmepsin V enzymatic activity for antimalarial therapy.

articles.headline: Subnational analysis of P. falciparum and P. vivax incidence and mortality among children 0-4 in Somalia (2010-2019) using modeled annual estimates across 18 regions. P. falciparum incidence peaked at 51.6% in Awdal (2015), with Bay (32.0%) and Juba Dhexe (31.1%) remaining elevated through 2019; between-group variance declined from 64.3 (2015) to 20.2 (2019), ratio 5.6 to 1.9. Mortality inequalities widened: difference rose from 33.9 to 118.5, variance from 100.6 to 1152.7, ratio remained 4.24 in 2019. Children in central/southern Somalia were over four times more likely to die than those in least affected regions, reflecting structural healthcare inequalities despite narrowing incidence gaps
articles.title_original: Spatial and temporal inequalities in malaria incidence and mortality among children aged 0-4 years in Somalia: a subnational analysis, 2010-2019.

articles.headline: Pregnancy cohort study (n=1,144 children, Ghana) with prenatal malaria assessed by placental histopathology (21.9% prevalence) and early child malaria by surveillance/RDT (45.9% prevalence), measuring growth trajectories at 0, 3, 6, 9, 12 months. Prenatal malaria associated with poorer length growth (multivariable OR=1.46, 95% CI 1.05–2.02), worse length-for-age z-scores (OR=1.53, CI 1.14–2.05), smaller head circumference (OR=1.43, CI 1.04–1.98); early child malaria with poorer length-for-age z-score (OR=1.12, CI 1.01–1.24). Prenatal malaria showed trend for persistent stunting (OR=1.63, CI 0.98–2.71). Malaria beginning in pregnancy was associated with poorer growth outcomes
articles.title_original: Prenatal and child malaria and child growth: evidence from a Ghanaian pregnancy cohort.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional analysis of 3,953 laboratory-confirmed malaria cases in Sorong City, Papua, Indonesia (2024), where P. vivax predominated (65.5%), nearly all uncomplicated (99.9%), with ~21% receiving non-standard antimalarial regimens. Predictors of hospital attendance: older age (aOR 1.02, p=0.001), non-Papuan ethnicity (aOR 1.77, p=0.001), housewife status (aOR 1.57, p=0.041), student status (aOR 1.56, p=0.029), moderate/low endemicity areas (aOR 2.01/4.57, p=0.001). Children <15 years more likely hospitalized (aOR 1.9, 95% CI 1.18–3.06, p=0.009). P. vivax dominated malaria burden; strengthening community services and early treatment-seeking among children critical to reduce hospital burden
articles.title_original: Epidemiological patterns and healthcare utilization among malaria patients in Sorong City, Papua, Indonesia: A cross-sectional analysis of national surveillance data.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional screening of 1,373 samples across Cameroon (microscopy, RDT, qPCR, Duffy genotyping; n=75 PvDBP1 sequences) found P. vivax prevalence 10.8% in hospital patients (86/793) and 5.5% in community (32/580), all in Duffy-negative individuals with significantly higher parasitemia in hospital cases. PvLDH-based RDTs failed to detect >85% of qPCR-confirmed infections. PvDBP1 analysis identified 8 nonsynonymous mutations (I379L 74.1%, E225K 61.3%). Cameroonian P. vivax clustered with Botswana isolates, distinct from East African/Asian lineages, indicating regional adaptation. First integrated epidemiological/PvDBP1 characterization in Duffy-negative Central Africans reveals widespread subclinical infections and poor diagnostic performance
articles.title_original: Epidemiological insights and genetic diversity of the Duffy binding protein of Plasmodium vivax in Duffy-negative Cameroonians.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 400 respondents (200 urban, 200 rural) in Nsukka, Nigeria assessing use of Cajanus cajan (pigeon pea) and Azadirachta indica (neem) for malaria/typhoid treatment via GLMs and multinomial logistic regression. Rural residents relied more on herbal remedies due to accessibility, affordability, family traditions; urban respondents despite higher education also used herbal extracts, alternating with prescription drugs. Residence, occupation, age were significant predictors of knowledge and use (P<0.05); education was not. Perceived efficacy and high prescription costs sustained herbal use. Socio-cultural and economic factors sustain reliance on traditional remedies; public health education and integration into primary healthcare needed
articles.title_original: Family traditions, accessibility and cost shape the continued reliance on Cajanus cajan and Azadirachta indica for malaria and typhoid despite prescription medicines in urban and rural Nsukka, Nigeria.

articles.headline: Mechanistic study of CNBP role in IL12β-IFN-γ axis during P. falciparum, P. chabaudi, and P. berghei ANKA infection in mice and human dendritic cells. CNBP translocated to nucleus upon parasite exposure and was required for optimal IL12β induction; genetic ablation/siRNA knockdown markedly reduced IL12β and IFN-γ responses. In vivo hematopoietic-specific CNBP deletion resulted in elevated peak parasitemia, impaired parasite clearance, and relapse; mice lacking CNBP paradoxically showed delayed mortality in lethal infection model. CNBP is a pivotal modulator of the IL12β-IFN-γ axis during malaria with potential as a therapeutic target to fine-tune immune responses
articles.title_original: Cellular nucleic acid-binding protein (CNBP) dependent cytokine programming shapes host defense against Plasmodium infection.

articles.headline: Case report of 29-year-old Ghanaian man in South Sudan presenting with gastrointestinal symptoms (nausea, vomiting, diarrhea) initially misdiagnosed, then developing high-grade fever, headache, tachycardia, hypotension. Malaria RDT positive, blood smear P. falciparum parasitemia (++); labs showed elevated total bilirubin (39.6 µmol/L), creatinine (120 µmol/L), prolonged prothrombin time (22.4s), INR 1.84, thrombocytopenia (50×10⁹/L platelets). Treated with IV artesunate, fluids, resuscitation; afebrile after 3 days and hemodynamically stable. GI-onset severe malaria may delay diagnosis; early recognition and timely treatment are critical determinants of favorable prognosis
articles.title_original: Severe Plasmodium falciparum Malaria Presenting with Gastrointestinal Symptoms in South Sudan: A Case Report.
articles.authors: Tran AD, Nguyen HT, Pham HK

articles.headline: Characterization and optimization of Aotus nancymaae non-human primate model of P. vivax blood stage infection; Vietnam-IV strain selected as most virulent from seven monkey-adapted strains; infectious dose optimization (0.3, 1.0, 2.5×10⁶ iRBC); anti-DBP-II monoclonal antibody (mAb 099100) efficacy tested. P. vivax Vietnam-IV at 2.5×10⁶ dose achieved infection kinetics with lowest coefficient variation (15–23%); CBC showed lymphocyte increase (5.0–5.3 to 7.0–8.5×10⁹/l) and decreases in neutrophils (1.9–3.0 to 0.6–1.2×10⁹/l) and platelets (413–458 to 23–68×10⁹/l); anti-DBP-II mAb protected 1/3 monkeys vs 0/3 controls. Well-characterized model provides foundation for evaluating P. vivax vaccines, monoclonal antibodies, and prophylactics
articles.title_original: Development of novel Aotus nancymaae non-human primate model for the evaluation of Plasmodium vivax blood stage vaccines and immunoprophylactics.

articles.headline: Retrospective observational study at Barcelona International Health Unit (June 2015–May 2025) screened 6,293 individuals for malaria; 404 positive (6.4%, 95% CI 5.8–7.1%), of whom 55 were P. malariae (13.6%, 95% CI 10.6–17.3). Most infections (52/55, 94.5%) detected in asymptomatic migrants from sub-Saharan Africa during routine screening with very low parasite densities (≤80 parasites/µL); 13/55 living in Spain >12 months before diagnosis. One symptomatic traveler experienced clinical relapse after artemisinin-based combination therapy. High proportion of asymptomatic chronic carriers represents silent parasite reservoir; improved diagnostic strategies needed to control this neglected species
articles.title_original: Ten years of Plasmodium malariae infections in an International Health Reference Center in Barcelona: temporal trends and insights.

articles.headline: Case or clinical study of intravenous artesunate treatment for artemisinin-resistant severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Uganda. [No abstract available; no quantitative data provided in title.]
articles.title_original: Intravenous Artesunate in Artemisinin-Resistant Severe Malaria in Uganda.

articles.headline: Structural determination of malaria-protective monoclonal antibodies targeting P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein (PfCSP) NANP repeats using X-ray crystallography and cryo-EM; two Fabs (7160, 7118) tested in unliganded forms and with PfCSP peptides/recombinant CSP. Both Fabs bound NANP6 repeats with high affinity (KD 6–10 nM); Fab 7160 formed germline-encoded homotypic Fab-Fab interactions similar to Fab 399, while Fab 7118 adopted distinct binding mode precluding such interactions. CDR loop variations modulate antibody conformation and Fab-Fab interactions. VH3-49/VK2D-29-derived antibodies achieve high-avidity binding and protective immunity through or independent of pre-configured Fab-Fab interactions, informing germline-targeting vaccine design
articles.title_original: Structural basis for conserved and distinct antigen recognition by a lineage of malaria-protective antibodies.

articles.headline: Investigation of PF3D7_0707400, a P. falciparum ATAD3A homolog mitochondrial protein, for essentiality and functional roles in parasite survival. PF3D7_0707400 is essential to parasite survival and present in a megadalton complex critical for mitochondrial RNA stability, membrane potential, ultrastructure, and protein import. First characterization of ATAD3A in a unicellular organism. Findings expand knowledge of apicomplexan mitochondrial biology and provide a potential antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: ATAD3 megadalton complex in Plasmodium falciparum is essential for mitochondrial and cellular viability.

articles.headline: Ecological time-series analysis of Burkina Faso's National Malaria Control Program (2020-2024) assessed LLIN, SMC, RDT, and ACT coverage and incidence. LLIN use reached 81.02% in pregnant women and 68.2% in children <5 years, SMC 97.8%, RDTs 98.5%, and ACTs 97.96%, reducing malaria cases from 537.5 to 446.4 per 1,000 people annually; forecasts predict incidence rising to 473.06 per 1,000 by 2030 (95% CI: 242.81-703.28). Supply chain disruptions and inefficient resource use hinder success, requiring sustained governance and resource improvements
articles.title_original: Evolution of the Performance of the Malaria Control Program in Burkina Faso: Analysis from 2020 to 2024.

articles.headline: French diagnostic guidance (ANOFEL/LABAC) standardizes Plasmodium detection using sensitive molecular techniques or thick smears, with thin smears for species identification and parasitemia estimation; rapid diagnostic tests are supplementary only. No quantitative results presented; this documents standardized malaria diagnosis recommendations per NF EN ISO 15189
articles.title_original: [Malaria diagnosis: French recommendations ANOFEL/LABAC].

articles.headline: Nested case-control study of P. vivax patients in Brazil evaluated 180-day recurrence risk by CYP2D6 metabolizer phenotype in primaquine (PQ) versus tafenoquine (TQ) recipients. TQ efficacy was unaffected by CYP2D6 polymorphisms (p=0.172), but PQ showed higher recurrence risk overall (p=0.016), with intermediate/poor metabolizers experiencing higher recurrence than normal metabolizers (p=0.036). CYP2D6 variability influences PQ but not TQ hypnozoitocidal efficacy in P. vivax
articles.title_original: Differential impact of Tafenoquine and Primaquine on Plasmodium vivax recurrence: the role of CYP2D6 variants.

articles.headline: Observational study of 515 pregnant women at first ANC in Burkina Faso (June 2023-June 2024) detected submicroscopic malaria by PCR in 30.1% (155/515) versus 8.2% by microscopy and 8.7% by RDT; each additional IPTp-SP dose reduced infection prevalence (aPR 0.89, 95% CI 0.80-0.99), primigravidae faced 2.14× higher infection risk (95% CI 1.21-3.79), and PCR-confirmed infection predicted low birthweight (aPR 1.52, 95% CI 1.01-2.28). Only 18.9% received ≥5 recommended IPTp-SP doses, necessitating earlier and more frequent ANC
articles.title_original: Submicroscopic malaria infections and inadequate IPTp-SP coverage among pregnant women in rural Burkina Faso: An observational study.

articles.headline: Historical account of Francesco Torti (1658-1741) developing cinchona bark as the first effective malaria treatment, detailed in his 1712 book on intermittent fevers. No quantitative results; documents the transition from humoral theory toward empirical, patient-centered medicine in malaria treatment
articles.title_original: Francesco Torti (1658-1741) and his description on the therapeutic use of quinine: the first effective method of treating malaria based on an extract of the bark of the Peruvian cinchona tree.

articles.headline: Review synthesizing evidence on P. falciparum placental malaria involving VEGF sequestration and dysregulated angiogenic signaling (VEGF, sVEGFR1, Angiopoietins, sEndoglin, PLGF) that may lead to placental insufficiency and fetal growth restriction. Identifies gaps in placental malaria research, recommending quantitative plasma VEGF studies and comparative immunohistochemistry in endemic regions
articles.title_original: Placental malaria and VEGF modulation by P. falciparum: Bridging the immunopathological gap using evidence from human cerebral and severe, non-cerebral malaria studies.

articles.headline: Dynamic Species Distribution Models (MaxEnt) integrated monthly Ae. aegypti and An. darlingi occurrence records and climatic variables (2016-2022) to estimate dengue and malaria Incidence Rate Ratios in Brazil. Ae. aegypti: AUC=0.8, TSS=0.6; An. darlingi: AUC=0.9, TSS=0.8; vapor pressure dominated predictions; suitability-case associations were significant (p<0.01), with strong seasonal peaks for Ae. aegypti and Amazon Basin concentration for An. darlingi. Temporal dynamics enabled identification of seasonal vulnerability windows
articles.title_original: Predicting short-life-cycle species suitability via dynamic species distribution models: Applications in dengue and malaria epidemiology in Brazil.

articles.headline: Case report of a 54-year-old woman with untreated P. falciparum infection from Central Africa presenting with fever and headache, progressing to cerebral malaria, multiorgan failure, and death on hospital day 4. Autopsy confirmed P. falciparum-induced microvascular sequestration and perivascular hemorrhages, highlighting the need to consider P. falciparum in febrile travelers
articles.title_original: Malignant malaria: autopsy findings and morphological features.

articles.headline: Pooled analysis of 18 sub-Saharan African countries using DHS/MIS data (2018-2024) among 63,561 women assessed combined ITN and IPTp (≥3 doses) use. Only 28.9% (95% CI 28.4-29.3%) used both ITN and IPTp, while 17.6% (95% CI 17.2-18.0%) used neither; multilevel regression identified age, education, household head sex, wealth, media exposure, residence, and sub-region as significant predictors. Achieving 90% malaria reduction by 2030 requires targeting community and individual-level determinants
articles.title_original: Use of insecticide-treated nets (ITN) and intermittent preventive treatment (IPTp) for malaria prevention and its associated factors among pregnant women in Sub-Sahara Africa.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 411 febrile patients in Northwest Ethiopia (February-April 2024) detected malaria in 34.1% (140/411) by microscopy: P. falciparum 20.9%, P. vivax 9.7%, mixed 2.4%. Independent risk factors included age <5 years (AOR 2.77, 95% CI 1.09-7.00), age 6-15 years (AOR 3.85, 95% CI 1.49-9.92), illiteracy (AOR 2.83, 95% CI 1.13-7.08), lack of regular ITN use (AOR 2.08, 95% CI 1.04-4.17), and absence of IRS (AOR 1.54, 95% CI 1.29-3.24). Strengthening consistent ITN use and IRS is essential for reducing malaria burden
articles.title_original: Prevalence of malaria and associated factors among febrile patients visiting Gissa Health Center, Awi Zone, Northwest Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Scoping review of 25 publications (2014-2024) on malaria elimination strategies in Cambodia examined progress, barriers, and priorities. While interventions reduced transmissions and deaths, barriers including service gaps, inadequate infrastructure, low coverage, and poor access persist, particularly in high-risk areas. Public health strategies must prioritize malaria surveillance, case management, and reduce inequities in access to care
articles.title_original: Advancing malaria elimination in Cambodia: a scoping review of progress, challenges, and strategic priorities.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 5,394 malaria-symptomatic individuals across 10 Kenyan counties (2022) compared PfHRP2-based and PfLDH-based RDTs with sequencing for pfhrp2/pfhrp3 deletions and pfk13 mutations. HRP2 and PfLDH RDTs showed 44% and 45% positivity with 1.26% discordance; no discordant samples carried pfhrp2 deletions; among 598 sequenced, pfhrp2 deletion prevalence was 4.5%, pfhrp3 was 12.4%, with no double-deletions; pfk13 mutations 0.7% (A578S, A675V). HRP2-based RDT reliability is sustained; A675V detection suggests limited artemisinin resistance spread
articles.title_original: Absence of false negative RDT results due to pfhrp2 deletion among symptomatic malaria infections in Kenya: findings from the 2022 Kenya national longitudinal surveillance of pfhrp2/3 deletions.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional Pf41 serology in 120 individuals from 4 Indonesian villages (Jan-June 2025) found 71% overall anti-Pf41 IgG seropositivity, with marked village variation (Kembaran 93%, Sedayu 83%, Wadas 60%, Kemejing 47%), and mean standardized MFI significantly higher in exposed groups vs controls (p<0.001). Site-specific associations emerged: nighttime outdoor activity and livestock feeding correlated with higher Pf41 antibodies. Pf41 serology can complement surveillance by detecting past malaria exposure in low-transmission settings despite declining clinical case reports
articles.title_original: Pf41 serological profiling reveals potential malaria exposure in low transmission setting in the Menoreh Hills, Central Java, Indonesia.

articles.headline: Phase 3 trial (Malawi, 2018-2023) randomized 57 children with cerebral malaria and severe brain swelling to mechanical ventilation (n=23), 3% hypertonic saline (n=17), or usual care (n=17). Neither intervention reduced 7-day mortality versus controls (ventilation risk difference –0.075, 95% CI –0.27 to 0.17, p=0.59; HTS RD 0.002, 95% CI –0.22 to 0.28, p=1.0); HTS increased cerebral blood flow but caused worse outcomes in children with baseline cerebral hyperemia (RR 3.0, 95% CI 1.02–10.9, p=0.04). Non-phenotype-guided neuroprotective strategies are ineffective; cerebrovascular endotype-informed approaches are needed
articles.title_original: Adjunctive Mechanical Ventilation or Intravenous Hypertonic Saline in Malawian Children with Cerebral Malaria and Highly Increased Brain Volume: A Randomized, Phase 3 Clinical Trial.

articles.headline: Bayesian spatio-temporal modeling (INLA, 2016–2022) of malaria in Tete Province, Mozambique identified precipitation and illiteracy rate as significant predictors of increased incidence (6.7% and 1.6% per unit increase, respectively). Temporally, incidence peaked shortly after rainy-season onset and declined during winter. Spatially, Macanga, Chiuta, and Moatize districts showed elevated risk while Tete City showed lower incidence. Climatic and social factors fundamentally shape regional transmission dynamics
articles.title_original: Climatic and social health determinants of malaria incidence in Tete, Mozambique: Bayesian spatio-temporal modeling of routine data (2016-2022).

articles.headline: Laboratory experiments in Anopheles stephensi demonstrated that relative humidity systematically alters juvenile life history trait responses to temperature, shifting projections of population growth rates. Temperature-humidity interactions acting through juvenile traits alone can qualitatively change inferences about when and where temperature-only models over- or underestimate environmental suitability. Incorporating humidity alongside temperature is essential for accurately assessing ectotherm responses to climatic variation in malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Beyond Temperature: Relative Humidity Systematically Shifts Juvenile Thermal Performance and Projected Population Growth in a Malaria Vector.

articles.headline: Spatiotemporal GIS analysis of 2,683 probable malaria-attributed deaths in 19th-century Ontario (1869–1900) revealed significant spatial clustering that became progressively less concentrated through time. Mortality locations shifted away from mapped wetlands as drainage intensified; settlement proximity was the strongest independent predictor of mortality density, while wetland distance was not significant once settlement effects were controlled. Malaria transmission was embedded within colonial settlement practices and infrastructure rather than static environmental features
articles.title_original: Ecology of Malaria Mortality: A Spatiotemporal Mapping Approach.

articles.headline: Theoretical optimization of a one-dimensional photonic crystal biosensor (Air/(PbS/SiO₂)₄/defect/(PbS/SiO₂)₄/substrate) for malaria detection by refractive index achieved maximum sensitivity of 1163.22 nm/RIU (at 1.4da defect thickness) with quality factor 82.57×10³, detection limit ~10⁻⁶ RIU, and strong resonant peaks at 1550 nm across refractive index range 1.371–1.402 RIU. The design demonstrates strong potential for rapid, accurate, cost-effective early malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Highly Sensitive 1D Photonic Crystal Biosensor for Early Malaria Detection via Refractive Index.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey (2020–2021) in Kayin State, Myanmar screened 5,509 individuals for sub-RDT P. vivax and G6PD deficiency: mRDT detected only 0.5% (26 P. vivax, 1 P. falciparum), while rRT-PCR found 14.3% P. vivax (317/2219) and 8.3% P. falciparum (185/2219); sub-RDT P. vivax prevalence was 14.1% (311/2211). G6PD deficiency was 21.7% in males and 10.6% in females (p<0.001), with no association to sub-RDT infection. Sub-RDT P. vivax reservoirs coexisting with high G6PD deficiency severely limit elimination prospects
articles.title_original: Sub-RDT Plasmodium vivax infections and G6PD deficiency in Kayin State, Myanmar.

articles.headline: Spatial analysis of paediatric malaria in Nigeria using Global Moran's I, Getis-Ord Gi*, and stepwise regression identified significant spatial clustering with hotspots distributed across the country while coldspots clustered in the southwest. Poverty emerged as the dominant predictor (R²=0.435) of paediatric malaria incidence. Free anti-malaria treatment for children and poverty reduction are essential to reduce Nigeria's paediatric malaria burden
articles.title_original: Geographic Patterns and Drivers of Paediatric Malaria in Nigeria: Walking in the shadow of Poverty.

articles.headline: Machine learning models trained on 2020 Nigerian Demographic and Health Surveys Geospatial Covariate data predicted malaria prevalence with high accuracy: random forest regressor R²=0.9937, multiple linear regression R²=0.9916, and ridge regression R²=0.9924 on test sets, with consistent improvement in performance as data volume increased. Shifting from reactive diagnostics to proactive risk prediction enables health authorities to allocate resources more effectively and reach underserved rural communities with precision
articles.title_original: Application of machine learning models in predicting malaria prevalence in Nigeria: an analysis of the 2015-2020 demographic and health surveys.

articles.headline: Surveillance data from Hormozgan Province, Iran (April 2018–May 2023) captured 289 malaria cases: 87.5% male, predominantly P. vivax (78.9%), with 56.7% Afghan-imported, 30.8% Pakistani-imported, and 12.5% Iranian autochthonous cases; asymptomatic cases accounted for 45.25%, with case numbers peaking in 2023. Imported malaria significantly contributes to maintenance of autochthonous transmission, requiring cost-effective surveillance, rapid diagnosis, and case follow-up targeting imported and asymptomatic infections
articles.title_original: Malaria dynamics in Iran: insights from autochthonous and asymptomatic.
articles.authors: Sadeghi F, Nodez SMM, Khosravani M

articles.headline: Analysis of 1,312 P. falciparum Pfs230 sequences from Ethiopia, Kenya, Tanzania, and Uganda (MalariaGEN Pf7 database) revealed low nucleotide diversity (π=5.9×10⁻⁴) and high haplotype diversity (Hd=0.999 with 496 haplotypes); only 8.1% of haplotypes were shared between populations, with non-synonymous/synonymous ratio of 0.43 and negative Tajima's D values across all countries (significant in Kenya, p<0.01 and Tanzania, p<0.05). Low genetic diversity and lack of population differentiation strongly support Pfs230 as a promising transmission-blocking vaccine candidate
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity of the Plasmodium falciparum Pfs230 gene in four East African countries supports its potential as a malaria transmission‑blocking vaccine candidate.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey (Jan–June 2024) of 34 HMIS managers from 30 Ugandan districts found that all reviewed reports received (75.8% documented errors), 81.6% of expected reports arrived at district level (25.4% requiring active follow-up), and 93.9% received data validation queries (mean 4.2 per facility). Support supervision was limited: 39.4% had received 1–2 visits while 51.5% provided supervision to their facilities over 12 months. Capable human resource teams are present; gaps remain in standard operating procedures, scheduled supervision, and advanced data-use analytics
articles.title_original: Attitude and practices of district-level HMIS managers towards malaria routine reporting in Uganda: a 2024 cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: In a genome-wide association study of 1,368 people with mild malaria in northern Ghana, researchers replicated previously identified associations with sickle hemoglobin (HbS) at parasite loci Pfsa1 and Pfsa3, identified a novel association at Pfsa4 within the FIKK4.2 kinase gene (confirmed in Mali), and found no associations with hemoglobin C. Pfsa mutations vary widely across Africa with high correlation between loci, providing new complexity to human-parasite coevolution
articles.title_original: Associations between the Plasmodium falciparum genome and sickle haemoglobin identified in mild malaria cases from Ghana.

articles.headline: Using Raman spectroscopy with confocal and spatially offset techniques coupled with interpretable machine learning (SHAP analysis), researchers identified discriminative hemoglobin-associated spectral regions and biochemical features in synchronized P. falciparum cultures. The method demonstrated sensitivity to infection signatures beneath skin-mimetic phantoms, establishing a biochemical basis for stage-specific Raman classification for noninvasive, stage-aware diagnostics capable of identifying infection and transmission potential
articles.title_original: Population-Level Raman Biochemical Staging of Malaria in Human Red Blood Cells Using Interpretable Machine Learning.

articles.headline: In a prospective study of children from Western Kenya with different P. falciparum malaria exposure histories and children with Burkitt lymphoma, malaria-exposed children showed increased aged neutrophil subsets and reduced mature active frequencies. A strong positive correlation (rs = 0.7; p < 0.0001) in immature neutrophils was observed between malaria-exposed and Burkitt lymphoma children, suggesting malaria-associated expansion of the immature neutrophil subset may create a permissive environment for lymphoma development
articles.title_original: The impact of malaria-induced neutrophil subset shift and a link to Burkitt lymphoma.

articles.headline: In 91 Venezuelan individuals with uncomplicated P. vivax malaria, three ICOS gene polymorphisms were genotyped and anti-PvMSP19 IgG antibodies measured, detecting antibodies in 70.3% of participants. Three-cluster analysis revealed significant differences in antibody reactivity (low: 0.408, medium: 0.862, high: 1.267; p < 0.001), and responses strongly correlated with prior malarial episodes (r = 0.803, p < 0.0001), but ICOS polymorphisms showed no significant association with antibody levels across any genetic inheritance model
articles.title_original: No Evidence for Association Between ICOS (a T-Cell Co-Stimulatory Molecule) Gene Polymorphisms and Anti-MSP-1(19) IgG Response in Venezuelan Endemic Plasmodium vivax Malaria.

articles.headline: A whole-genome analysis of the Neotropical malaria vector Anopheles darlingi revealed geographically structured populations with no evidence of sympatric species and strong signals of widespread insecticide resistance through convergent evolution, addressing longstanding knowledge gaps regarding the evolutionary drivers of this major malaria vector
articles.title_original: Reassessing malaria-transmitting mosquito evolution with a neotropical lens.

articles.headline: In 195 P. falciparum patients in Laos treated with artemether-lumefantrine for 3 days, PCR-adjusted adequate clinical and parasitological response was 96% (95% CI 92-98) at day 28, with 2% treatment failure and 2% reinfection. One recrudescent isolate showed markedly reduced lumefantrine susceptibility (IC50 59.9 nM, 2.5-fold higher than median) and high artemisinin resistance (35.8% ring-stage survival) with pfkelch13 R539T. Prevalence of pfkelch13 R539T was 5% and C580Y was 23%, both associated with day-3 microscopy-positive parasitaemia, indicating potential lumefantrine resistance emergence
articles.title_original: Artemether-lumefantrine for the treatment of Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Laos: a therapeutic efficacy study coupled with genomic and in vitro phenotypic analyses.

articles.headline: In 774 Thai and 923 Brazilian participants evaluated for P. vivax exposure, researchers identified extensive genetic diversity in MSP5 (π = 14.8 × 10-3) and DBPII (π = 7.7 × 10-3) antigens. New haplotypes reflecting global diversity showed strong regional immunogenicity differences for RBP2a and DBPII, but for five of eight antigens, these differences did not significantly affect accuracy of identifying recent exposure. Adding multiple antigens to classification models overcame affected performance, indicating highly diverse antigens can still be effective serological markers
articles.title_original: Assessing the effect of parasite antigen genetic diversity on performance of Plasmodium vivax serological exposure markers for malaria: a multicentre observational diagnostic accuracy study.

articles.headline: A retrospective study of 171 children treated for imported malaria at 9 US hospitals (2016-2023) found most had traveled to West Africa; 90% presented with fever and 66% with abdominal symptoms. Thirty-two percent were diagnosed with severe malaria, with no deaths. Delayed diagnoses occurred in 26% across all hospitals and were a risk factor for severe disease, highlighting the importance of prompt diagnosis and chemoprophylaxis for travel-acquired malaria in US pediatric populations
articles.title_original: Malaria in Children at 9 US Hospitals: 2016-2023.

articles.headline: A case series of 9 imported malaria cases in Iran's malaria-free northwest region identified 3 P. falciparum and 6 P. vivax infections, all with travel to endemic areas. Given that the region's northern areas and rice-growing districts provide potential malaria vector breeding habitats, the influx of infected individuals during peak mosquito season creates conditions for local transmission. This underscores the need to strengthen healthcare training for rapid diagnosis and effective surveillance to sustain Iran's malaria elimination progress
articles.title_original: Challenges and strategies in malaria control: A case series from East Azerbaijan Province, 2023-2024.

articles.headline: A cross-sectional survey of 37,849 households in 10 Indian malaria-endemic states found government healthcare facilities accessible within 2.5 km, yet only 57.4% of febrile individuals visited a government provider within 24-48 hours.
