While 89.2% of households had adequate malaria prevention knowledge and 73.6% used long-lasting insecticidal nets regularly, lack of formal education and tribal population status were high-risk factors for malaria. The study reveals a significant gap between healthcare facility accessibility and its utilization
articles.title_original: Comprehensive Analysis of Health Service Utilization for Febrile Illness and Malaria in Malaria-Endemic States in India: LLINs as a Pillar of Control Strategies.

articles.headline: Researchers determined the 1.8 Å resolution crystal structure of Plasmodium vivax macrophage migration inhibitory factor (PvMIF), which crystallized with a prototypical MIF trimer as the biological unit (PDB entry 9b0m). PvMIF binds to host CD74 and suppresses host MIF signaling; plasma concentrations correlate with severity of P. vivax malaria. As a candidate malaria vaccine, understanding PvMIF's structural properties provides a basis for vaccine development targeting this virulence factor
articles.title_original: Crystal structure of Plasmodium vivax macrophage migration inhibitory factor.
articles.journal: Acta Crystallogr F Struct Biol Commun

articles.headline: This opinion article discusses how genomic approaches using polygenic scores can improve surveillance of insecticide resistance in malaria vectors. Genomic methods can more accurately characterize patterns of insecticide resistance and estimate impacts on vector control tools like long-lasting insecticide nets and indoor residual spraying before intervention failure occurs, providing essential data for evidence-based vector control strategies as global malaria burden continues to rise
articles.title_original: Polygenic scores for genomic surveillance of insecticide resistance in malaria control.

articles.headline: ProteinMPNN-guided design of stabilized surrogates for PfBDP1-BRD, PfBDP4-BRD, and PfK13-KREP showed markedly increased thermal stability while retaining wild-type binding thermodynamics; PfBDP4-BRD surrogate enabled 1.25 Å co-crystal structure determination with a newly identified inhibitor, demonstrating utility of computational stabilization for structure-enabled drug discovery against challenging malaria proteins
articles.title_original: Stabilizing Plasmodium falciparum proteins for small molecule drug discovery.

articles.headline: Among 176 P. vivax patients treated with chloroquine plus 14-day primaquine in Ethiopia, 54.6% (96/176) experienced recurrent parasitemia over one year. Gametocytes remained infectious in 70.2% (167/238) of mosquito feedings from baseline and recurrent symptomatic episodes, with infection rates correlated with parasite and gametocyte density (ρ=0.29-0.32, p<0.0001). Relapses (45.7% of recurrences) showed lower mosquito transmission than new infections (OR 0.48, p=0.016), supporting strengthened radical cure
articles.title_original: Parasite and gametocyte dynamics and infectivity to mosquitoes during recurrent Plasmodium vivax infections: A longitudinal study from Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Among 75 P. vivax cases detected in North India (0.99% of 7530 screened), pvmdr1 sequencing of 36 isolates revealed M908L and F1076L mutations fixed in 100% of isolates and T898A in 5.6%, clustering with Madagascar and Afghanistan haplotypes. Although these mutations are nearly universal, their role in clinical chloroquine resistance remains uncertain absent Y976F; therapeutic efficacy studies and continuous surveillance recommended
articles.title_original: Molecular detection of chloroquine resistance in Plasmodium vivax isolates from North India: Analysis of single nucleotide polymorphisms in pvmdr1 gene.

articles.headline: Population genomic analysis of 2283 Anopheles specimens across 35 Gambian locations (2005-2021) using >200K SNPs identified BISSAU molecular form as genetically discrete, temporally stable, and reproductively isolated from sympatric An. gambiae and An. coluzzii with no admixture detected. BISSAU form occurs with highest probability on Gambia's north bank 10-120 km from the coast
articles.title_original: Genomic insights into the population structure of malaria vectors of the Anopheles gambiae complex in The Gambia reveals distinctiveness and spatio-temporal stability of BISSAU molecular form.

articles.headline: Behavioral and chemical assays of An. arabiensis infected with Microsporidia MB transmission-blocking symbiont found no preferential attraction to human versus cattle odours, no association between infection and selective human feeding, but detected differences in cuticular hydrocarbon profiles between infected and non-infected males. Microsporidia MB imposes no cost on host-seeking behavior yet may alter mating behavior via chemical signaling changes
articles.title_original: Behaviour of the malaria vector Anopheles arabiensis infected with the endosymbiont Microsporidia MB: host seeking and cuticular hydrocarbons associated with mating.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 8 studies showed RTS,S/AS01 malaria vaccine achieved 23% malaria risk reduction in 6-12-week-old infants (RR 0.77) with strong antibody response; both RTS,S/AS01 and R21 achieved RR 0.61 in 5-17-month-olds with declining efficacy over time, supporting need for booster doses. R21 showed high efficacy (event rate 0.22) but more frequent adverse events than RTS,S/AS01
articles.title_original: Efficacy and immunogenicity of RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccines: - Systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Review proposing that innate immune memory—epigenetic reprogramming of innate immunity independent of adaptive immunity—may explain the dynamics of recurrent Plasmodium infections in endemic areas. Suggests innate memory in non-immune cells may have significant consequences in the context of Plasmodium's complex lifecycle
articles.title_original: The case for exploring innate memory in Plasmodium infection.

articles.headline: Review identifies DNA markers in detoxification genes associated with metabolic insecticide resistance in malaria vectors: An. gambiae (CYP6P4, CYP6P3, GSTe2) and An. funestus (CYP6P9a/b, CYP6P4a/b, CYP9K1, GSTe2, ASL). These markers enable genomic surveillance of resistance evolution and assess impact on control tools, cross-resistance patterns, and fitness costs
articles.title_original: Metabolic insecticide resistance DNA markers in malaria vectors.

articles.headline: Review of recent technological advances—microscopy, single-cell RNA sequencing, and genetic screening—that have revolutionized study of Plasmodium sporogony within Anopheles mosquitoes, with potential applications for informing development of novel malaria transmission control tools
articles.title_original: Demystifying Plasmodium development within the Anopheles mosquito.

articles.headline: Correspondence raising concerns regarding study design of spatial emanators for malaria control
articles.title_original: Concerns regarding study of spatial emanators for malaria control.

articles.headline: Authors' response to concerns raised about spatial emanator studies for malaria control
articles.title_original: Concerns regarding study of spatial emanators for malaria control - Authors' reply.
articles.authors: Allan RJ, Cross CL, Messenger LA

articles.headline: Pan-Plasmodium RT-qPCR assay targeting conserved 18S rRNA achieved 100% clinical sensitivity across all five human-infecting Plasmodium species and 100% clinical specificity (95% CI 99-100%), with 95% limit of detection of 5.3 iRBCs/mL (95% CI 3.2-8.6) and amplicon sequencing capability for species-level identification, supporting blood donor screening and surveillance of asymptomatic parasitemia
articles.title_original: Development and validation of a pan-Plasmodium molecular assay as a confirmatory method for blood donor screening.

articles.headline: Review of P. vivax immunometabolism showing metabolic-mitochondrial shifts in activated monocytes with enhanced phagocytic activity but elevated proinflammatory cytokine and reactive oxygen species production; discusses how these changes contribute to acute disease signs and provides mechanistic insights from human and experimental studies
articles.title_original: The mitochondrial metabolic shift in inflammatory monocytes during Plasmodium vivax malaria.

articles.headline: Surveillance analysis (2007–2024) of 10,146 confirmed cases in non-endemic Brazil: malaria (n=9,755; 78% male, 89.7% non-autochthonous) showed significant decreasing trend (β=−0.0174, p=0.040), while Chagas (n=391; 50.1% female, 53.2% oral transmission) showed increasing trend (β=0.0342, p=0.027); forecast models project stable incidence through 2030
articles.title_original: Malaria and Chagas disease in non-endemic settings in Brazil: a comparative analysis of national surveillance data (2007-2024).

articles.headline: Analysis of 18,217 malaria cases (2017–2024) in Sonbhadra district, India, showed 95% cumulative case reduction with annual parasite incidence declining from 2.7 to 0.1; P. falciparum and P. vivax high-risk clusters persisted at sub-district level with the most significant hotspot at Chopan block, indicating need for sustained targeted interventions
articles.title_original: Identification of micro-level malaria hotspots and its implications for achieving malaria elimination target in a remote district of India.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 408 samples (June–September 2023, Burkina Faso) found dengue-malaria coinfection prevalence at 17.64%, with acute coinfection (DENV RT-PCR + malaria) at 7.10%; among RT-PCR-positive dengue cases, 100% showed Plasmodium coinfection by PCR (68.96% by microscopy), with elevated temperature and high CRP (mean 83.79 mg/l) associated with acute coinfection
articles.title_original: Molecular detection and epidemiological characterization of dengue and malaria coinfection in the Guiriko region, Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: Molecular screening of 217 birds (27 resident, 7 migratory species) in tropical dry forest, Colombia, identified avian Plasmodium and Haemoproteus infections in 11 individuals across 6 species, yielding 5.1% overall prevalence and several novel host-parasite associations for Colombia
articles.title_original: Molecular detection of avian hemoparasites (Plasmodium and Haemoproteus) in resident and migratory birds from an urban fragment of tropical dry forest in northern Colombia.
articles.authors: Rodríguez MM, Flores FS, Castro LR

articles.headline: Observational study of peripheral blood erythrophagocytosis (red blood cell destruction by phagocytes) as a potential hematologic manifestation of P. falciparum malaria infection
articles.title_original: Peripheral Blood Erythrophagocytosis in Plasmodium falciparum Malaria.

articles.headline: Review of infant malaria immunity and P. falciparum infection covering epidemiology, age-related immune response differences, and vaccine efficacy (RTS,S/AS01E and R21/Matrix-M); discusses vaccine limitations in infants under five months and emerging preventive strategies including monoclonal antibodies targeting circumsporozoite protein
articles.title_original: Malaria immunity in the infant: progress and pitfalls.

articles.headline: Perspective on novel approaches to radical cure and relapse prevention in P. vivax malaria
articles.title_original: Reimagining radical cure for Plasmodium vivax.

articles.headline: EFFORT trial (960 enrolled, 70% male) comparing unsupervised 7-day high-dose primaquine (7 mg/kg), single-dose tafenoquine (300 mg), and 14-day low-dose primaquine (3.5 mg/kg) in uncomplicated P. vivax with G6PD ≥70%. At 6 months: 7-day group 13.0% recurrence (HR 0.66, p=0.063), tafenoquine 12.6% (HR 0.64, p=0.041) versus 14-day 18.5%; drug-related adverse events: 42.9% (7-day), 22.2% (tafenoquine), 34.2% (14-day)
articles.title_original: Effectiveness and safety of 7-day high-dose primaquine and single-dose tafenoquine versus 14-day low-dose primaquine in patients with Plasmodium vivax malaria (EFFORT): a multicentre, open-label, randomised, controlled, superiority trial.

articles.headline: Diagnostic study examining discrepancies in white blood cell count channel parameters as a potential laboratory clue to P. falciparum malaria infection
articles.title_original: Discrepancy Between White Blood Cell Count Channels as a Clue to Malaria Infection.

articles.headline: Perspective highlighting the gap between static laboratory conditions used in genetically modified mosquito (GMM) target gene identification and natural environmental variability; argues that incorporating realistic environmental variation in upstream GMM development is critical to prevent compromise of genetic modification function under field conditions
articles.title_original: Beyond the static lab: environmental variability in genetically modified mosquito target gene identification for malaria control.

articles.headline: Historical analysis of malaria therapy for neurosyphilis at Mont Park Hospital (1927–1928) using medical records: patient outcomes were notably less positive than Ellery's published accounts; diagnostic drift, adjunct interventions, and healthy-patient selection contributed to overestimation of effectiveness. Conclusion: malaria was not an effective treatment for neurosyphilis
articles.title_original: Malaria therapy for neurosyphilis at Mont Park Hospital for the insane in Australia, 1927-1928.

articles.headline: Amplicon sequencing of 69 P. vivax isolates from northwestern Thailand (2022-2023) revealed predominantly VK210 (n=66) and rare VK247 (n=3) CSP variants. The most common VK210 haplotype H2, representing half of VK210 sequences, produced more salivary-gland sporozoites per oocyst than other haplotypes in Anopheles dirus feeding assays, indicating that repeat-region polymorphisms modulate vector competence
articles.title_original: Genetic signatures of Plasmodium vivax Circumsporozoite Surface Protein during malaria resurgence in Thailand.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional analysis of 616 individuals (270 malaria-infected, 346 non-infected) from Thai-Myanmar and Thai-Malaysian borders found malaria patients had higher prevalence of NN antigen (22.2% vs 11.1%, OR 3.7, p<0.0001) and FYA/FYA or FYA/FYB genotypes with wild-type Duffy promoter (76.1% vs 46.9%, OR 4.7, p<0.0001), indicating the majority of the population has increased malaria infection susceptibility
articles.title_original: Red blood cell membrane protein polymorphisms related to risk of malaria infection in the population living in malaria-endemic areas of Thailand: An analytic cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Molecular docking of 2754 Central and West African medicinal plant compounds from ConMedNP targeting P. falciparum DHFR-TS achieved mean binding affinity of -8.8±1.4 kcal/mol, with 75% outperforming artemether (-8.0 kcal/mol). A Random Forest QSAR model (test R²=0.8321) identified top candidates RDC0118 (-13.5 kcal/mol), RDC0119 (-13.4 kcal/mol), and CA0001 (-13.0 kcal/mol). CA0001 showed superior ADMET properties, positioning it as a promising PfDHFR-TS inhibitor
articles.title_original: Discovery of novel Plasmodium falciparum PfDHFR-TS inhibitors from ConMedNP natural compounds: a multi-computational approach.

articles.headline: Scoping review of 17 studies examining lipid-based nutrient supplements combined with IPTp found LNS supplementation did not significantly affect malaria incidence, anemia, or maternal nutritional status overall. Some studies reported birth outcome improvements in specific subgroups with baseline undernutrition, but evidence for combined intervention's effects on malaria or maternal anemia remains inconclusive
articles.title_original: Effects of Combined Lipid-Based Nutrient Supplements and Malaria Preventive Treatment during Pregnancy on Malaria, Maternal Nutrition, and Birth Outcomes: A Scoping Review.

articles.headline: External validation of the Malaria Scoring System in 365 emergency department patients (114 with malaria, 31.2%) demonstrated strong discriminatory ability (AUC 0.902, 95% CI 0.869-0.935) and good calibration (Hosmer-Lemeshow p=0.195). Malaria patients showed higher MSS scores (median 4 vs 1, p<0.001) and more frequent thrombocytopenia (78.1% vs 8.4%), lymphopenia (71.9% vs 32.7%), and anemia (53.5% vs 35.1%)
articles.title_original: External validation of the Malaria Scoring System in a non-endemic emergency department.

articles.headline: Plasmodium yoelii infection triggers ferroptosis in splenic macrophages with HMOX1 upregulation and HIF-1α downregulation. Fer-1 treatment reversed ferroptosis biomarkers in infected mice, while GPX4 conditional knockout mice (GPX4flox/flox Lyz2Cre+) displayed worsened parasitemia. Identical ferroptosis signatures detected in monocytes from malaria patient blood samples, identifying the HIF-1α/HMOX1 axis as a therapeutic target
articles.title_original: HIF-1α/HMOX1-Mediated Ferroptosis in Macrophages Exacerbates Malaria Progression.

articles.headline: P. yoelii 17XL ΔMSP8 knockout mice showed reduced peak parasitemia, attenuated splenomegaly, and self-resolving infection. MSP8 deletion upregulated splenic CCL19 and preserved CCR7, increasing splenic and serum IFN-γ levels. Functional CCR7 knockdown partially abrogated the survival advantage, identifying CCL19-CCR7 signaling as essential for MSP8-deletion-mediated protection from lethal malaria
articles.title_original: Deletion of Plasmodium yoelii Merozoite Surface Protein 8 Protects Mice From Lethal Blood-Stage Malaria and Is Associated With Enhanced Splenic CCL19-CCR7 Signaling.

articles.headline: Interrupted time series analysis of Cameroon DHIS2 surveillance data (2017-2022) showed COVID-19 in March 2020 caused an immediate 36.9% decline in reported malaria incidence in children under 5 (IRR 0.631, p<0.001), followed by 3.1% monthly recovery (IRR 1.031, p<0.001). Counterfactual analysis estimated 69,708 missing cases in 2020-2021 and 74,831 excess cases in 2022, with marked regional heterogeneity (Centre IRR 0.467, East IRR 0.544)
articles.title_original: Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on reported malaria incidence in children under five years of age in Cameroon: an interrupted time series analysis with regional heterogeneity (2017-2022).

articles.headline: Climate analysis in Malawi identified two distinct ocean-based patterns driving interannual malaria variability: warm tropical Atlantic drives wet conditions and increased cases, while warm Indian Ocean drives hot, dry conditions and reduced cases. Soil moisture is the crucial mechanistic link between remote climate drivers and local disease dynamics; climate change is expected to reduce soil moisture by 2100
articles.title_original: Tropical oceans drive variability in soil moisture and malaria in Malawi.

articles.headline: Differential transcriptome analysis identified two P. vivax hypnozoite-expressed RNA-binding proteins (IESI-1 and IESI-2). When individually expressed in P. yoelii liver stages, each blocked schizogony with parasites remaining small and uninucleate. RNA sequence screening revealed enrichment of distinct, highly specific motifs bound by each protein, providing unprecedented functional evidence for the molecular mechanism suppressing liver-stage replication in hypnozoites
articles.title_original: Two Plasmodium vivax hypnozoite-expressed RNA-binding proteins inhibit liver stage replication.

articles.headline: HyperLOPIT spatial proteomics of 3000 P. falciparum schizont proteins resolved 24 distinct cellular niches, with supervised machine-learning classifying 1646 proteins into defined compartments including exported host-cell sites. Comparative genomic analyses revealed spatial patterns of parasite cell evolution and ongoing adaptive pressures, advancing mechanistic understanding of invasion biology
articles.title_original: The spatial proteome of the Plasmodium falciparum schizont illuminates the composition and evolutionary trajectories of its organelles.

articles.headline: Monte Carlo simulations optimized age-based sampling for malaria seroconversion rate (SCR) estimation under stable and changing transmission. In stable settings with known seroreversion rates, sampling older individuals improved precision in low-transmission areas, while younger individuals were more informative in high-transmission settings. Application to Sri Lanka serological data demonstrated that alternative age-structured sampling could substantially improve precision and power to detect transmission changes
articles.title_original: Optimizing age-structured sampling for estimating the seroconversion rate in malaria seroepidemiology: a simulation study.

articles.headline: Single-cell RNA sequencing of P. falciparum-infected RBCs identified distinct transcriptional responses to artemisinin, atovaquone, and ganaplacide. Artemisinin treatment enriched gametocyte-associated genes while atovaquone enriched pyrimidine biosynthesis transcripts in bulk analysis. scRNA-seq can profile diverse drug responses across parasite lifecycle stages and classify drug mechanisms
articles.title_original: Single-cell analysis of Plasmodium falciparum transcripts after drug perturbation identifies feedback regulation as well as increased transmission potential.

articles.headline: In silico screening of 21 compounds from B. sapida ethanolic leaf extract against P. falciparum DHORDH: three compounds showed strong binding affinities (−8.1 to −10.0 kcal/mol; positive control −8.9 kcal/mol), with 2-methyl-6-(4-methylenecyclohex-2-en-1-yl)hept-2-en-4-one most stable (RMSD=0.12). Compounds showed favorable drug-likeness and ADMET profiles
articles.title_original: In-silico study of the bioactive compounds of Blighia sapida koenig revealed a novel Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase inhibitor for malaria.

articles.headline: Topological Fuzzy AHP geospatial assessment in Nigeria mapped malaria vulnerability using five socioeconomic indicators. Maternal and gender-linked factors accounted for 34.63% of overall vulnerability; approximately 62.52% of very high malaria incidence zones coincided with high/very high vulnerability areas, supporting model utility for spatially targeted interventions
articles.title_original: Topological Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process (ToF-AHP) for geospatial assessment of malaria vulnerability in Nigeria.

articles.headline: Genetic disruption of Pf MD3 (CCCH-type zinc-finger RNA-binding protein) revealed male fertility defect that decreased infectivity to mosquitoes; Pf MD3 binds mRNA transcripts associated with male gamete development and affects their translation
articles.title_original: The CCCH-type zinc-finger Pf MD3 promotes translation for malaria parasite transmission.

articles.headline: MaxEnt ecological niche model for An. pseudopunctipennis in Argentina using 312 spatially-thinned locality records. Best model: 10% omission rate, validation AUC 0.83, lowest AICc. Climatically suitable areas concentrated in northwestern provinces (Jujuy, Salta, Tucumán, Catamarca) with additional foci in other regions
articles.title_original: MODELING THE POTENTIAL CLIMATIC SUITABILITY OF ANOPHELES PSEUDOPUNCTIPENNIS, THE HISTORICAL VECTOR OF MALARIA IN ARGENTINA.

articles.headline: Multivariable Poisson regression in four Indonesian districts (480,575 mobility events, March 2020–May 2022): increased mobility associated with higher malaria incidence (IRR 1.25, 95% CI 1.21–1.30); malaria incidence 64% lower outside Java. Malaria screening (IRR 2.33) and COVID-19 positivity (IRR 1.12) also independently associated with incidence
articles.title_original: Mobility and malaria incidence in Indonesia's low-endemic areas during the COVID-19 period.

articles.headline: Dynamic mode decomposition of South Korean malaria incidence (2001–2024) across 17 provinces evaluated two control programs. South-North Joint Control (2008–2011) achieved 63% incidence reduction and disrupted regional synchrony; 5-Year Re-Elimination Plan (2019–2023) achieved 49% decrease from 2018–2021 but 30% rebound by 2023 with partial re-emergence of spatial coupling
articles.title_original: Evaluating the impact of South-North Joint Malaria Control Project on spatiotemporal transmission dynamics in South Korea using dynamic mode decomposition.

articles.headline: Retrospective comparative in Zinder, Niger (2021–2023) of SMC (sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine + amodiaquine) extended to children aged 5–10 years. Uncomplicated malaria incidence in 5–10 year-olds was 33.34 per 1,000 (2021); significant decreases in uncomplicated and severe cases by 2023 in intervention district; no significant change in control district
articles.title_original: Extension of seasonal malaria chemoprevention to older children: a pragmatic comparative study in two health districts in the Zinder region, Niger.

articles.headline: Oxford nanopore WGS protocol for P. falciparum from DBS and whole blood optimized enrichment strategies (McrBC/MspJI, NMDEK, sWGA). Results: >75% parasite DNA; >90% genome coverage; median read length >2 kb; >30× depth; 99.8% accuracy. Detected additional mutations and copy number variations (pfmdr1, pfgch1) compared to Sanger sequencing
articles.title_original: Development of an Oxford nanopore sequencing technology-based whole genome sequencing method for Plasmodium falciparum to support malaria molecular surveillance.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional in Malawi (913 female caregivers, May–July 2021) identified barriers to prompt malaria care-seeking. Delay 1 (decision-making) predominant: incorrect symptom knowledge (aOR 0.71, 95% CI 0.53–0.97), negative attitudes (aOR 0.58, 95% CI 0.41–0.82), incorrect knowledge of care location (aOR 0.19, 95% CI 0.07–0.50), and far facility distance (aOR 0.67, 95% CI 0.49–0.93)
articles.title_original: Three delays of malaria care-seeking in Malawi: a cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Pediatric SCA cohort in Angola (65 patients) genotyped CD36 and ICAM-1 variants. CD36 rs3211891_C identified as anemia severity modulator; variants rs3211938_G, ICAM-1 rs5491_T, and rs5496_A significantly impacted hematological phenotype. ICAM-1 rs5494_T associated with 5.63-fold increased risk of malaria infection versus wild-type
articles.title_original: Genetic variants in red blood cell adhesion-related genes influence the severity of sickle cell anemia in a malaria-endemic region : Short title: Genetic variants in red blood cell adhesion-related genes in sickle cell anemia.

articles.headline: In vitro study identified P. falciparum glycophorin binding protein-130 (PfGBP-130) as the LFA-1 ligand on infected RBCs. PfGBP-130 promotes NK cell activation, degranulation, and contact-dependent parasite killing; neutralizing antibodies significantly impaired these responses, identifying PfGBP-130 as a potential host-directed intervention target
articles.title_original: LFA-1 interaction with GBP-130 on Plasmodium falciparum-infected red blood cells mediates NK cell activation and parasite control.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 422 primigravidae in Tanzania found placental malaria (PM) prevalence of 39.8% by histology (95% CI 35.1-44.5%) and 23.7% by microscopy, with 19.7% maternal peripheral malaria and 6.9% congenital malaria. PM was associated with low birth weight (p=0.01), preterm birth (p=0.001), and small-for-gestational-age neonates (p=0.048); risk factors included fewer than four antenatal care visits (AOR 1.75, p=0.04) and moderate anemia (AOR 1.84, p=0.04). Authors conclude that early ANC attendance and improved uptake of IPTp are critical for reducing malaria-related maternal and neonatal complications
articles.title_original: High burden of histology-confirmed placental malaria and associated adverse birth outcomes among primigravidae in Northwestern Tanzania: a cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Investigation of MaRNAV-1, an RNA virus infecting Plasmodium vivax across multiple life cycle stages in Cambodian and Ethiopian parasites, demonstrated association with increased parasite transmission (higher gametocyte abundance and oocyst prevalence/intensity in membrane feeding assays) and altered host immunity. MaRNAV-1 loads were higher in symptomatic infections, associated with elevated body temperature independently of parasitemia, and correlated with elevated circulating cytokines (IFN-γ, CXCL10, IL-1RA, IL-6) and dendritic cell activation. Authors conclude that MaRNAV-1 is a genuine parasite-infecting virus that modulates clinical outcomes and host immune response in P. vivax infections
articles.title_original: MaRNAV-1, an intracellular virus of Plasmodium vivax , is associated with increased parasite transmission and altered host immune response.

articles.headline: Laboratory study of secondary metabolites from entomopathogenic fungus Purpureocillium lilacinum demonstrated potent activity against Anopheles gambiae larvae and adults. Leucinostatin A showed LC₉₀ of 9 µg/mL against fourth-instar larvae and LD₉₀ of ~1 µg per adult female mosquito; purified pulixin LC₉₀ was 68 µg/mL. Extended fermentation increased larvicidal potency (crude extract LC₉₀ reduced from 129 to 61 µg/mL). Authors propose leucinostatin A as cost-effective, environmentally friendly dual-action agent blocking malaria transmission via larval inhibition and adult mosquito killing
articles.title_original: Larvicidal and adulticidal activities of Purpureocillium lilacinum Secondary Metabolites against the Malaria Vector Anopheles gambiae.

articles.headline: Retrospective qPCR analysis comparing five P. falciparum diagnostic targets in 150 positive and 50 negative samples found pfgdh sensitivity 69% (specificity 98%, AUC 83%), compared to pfhrp2 (72% sensitivity, 100% specificity, AUC 86%), pfhrp3 (71%, 96%, 84%), pfaldolase (29%, 100%, 64%), and pfldh (19%, 100%, 60%). Authors conclude that pfgdh demonstrates notable diagnostic potential as a promising alternative for detecting P. falciparum, particularly valuable for overcoming challenges posed by pfhrp2 gene deletions
articles.title_original: Comparative evaluation of Plasmodium falciparum glutamate dehydrogenase with known Plasmodium falciparum diagnostic targets using quantitative real-time polymerase chain reaction.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis of three studies (1,353 participants) evaluated adding dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHA-PPQ, 40/320 mg, three doses) to trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole (800/160 mg) for intermittent preventive treatment in HIV-positive pregnancies. Addition of DHA-PPQ showed non-significant trend toward lower maternal parasitaemia (OR 0.83, 95% CI 0.52-1.33, p=0.44) and placental malaria (OR 0.66, 95% CI 0.40-1.08, p=0.10), but significantly reduced maternal adverse events (OR 0.78, 95% CI 0.67-0.91, p=0.003), with no significant differences in fetal adverse events or birth outcomes. Authors conclude DHA-PPQ shows promising efficacy in reducing malaria and maternal toxicity in HIV-positive pregnant women
articles.title_original: Maternal, Fetal, and Neonatal Outcomes of Additional Dihydroartemisinin-Piperaquine to Trimethoprim-Sulfamethoxazole in Intermittent Preventive Treatment for Malaria in HIV-Positive Pregnancies: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.

articles.headline: First continuous in vitro culture of Plasmodium coatneyi established in Macaca fascicularis erythrocytes, sustained >40 days with parasitaemia reaching 3% post-cryopreservation recovery. While P. coatneyi successfully invaded human erythrocytes, asexual development arrested post-invasion with parasitaemia <detection limit by day 9, identifying failure to complete the 48-h developmental cycle in human cells. Authors identify a putative biological barrier to sustained zoonotic transmission and provide a platform for investigating severe malaria pathogenesis
articles.title_original: First continuous in vitro culture of Plasmodium coatneyi reveals insights into barriers to human infection.

articles.headline: EFFORT trial dissemination meeting in Indonesia (May 2025) reporting on evaluation of high-dose primaquine (7 mg/kg over 7 days) and single-dose tafenoquine (300 mg) versus standard 14-day low-dose primaquine for radical cure of Plasmodium vivax, co-administered with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine in Indonesia. Stakeholders identified evidence gaps regarding tafenoquine with artemisinin combination therapies, feasibility of implementing high-dose primaquine, and need for subnational strategies reflecting diverse epidemiological contexts. Authors emphasize need for further tafenoquine-ACT studies while recognizing high-dose primaquine as immediately implementable pending strengthened G6PD testing
articles.title_original: Dissemination of EFFORT trial results in Indonesia: a stakeholder meeting report on tafenoquine and primaquine for radical cure of Plasmodium vivax.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 134 participants (80.6% inpatients, 19.4% caregivers) in secondary hospitals in Abeokuta, Nigeria found high malaria prevalence of 74.6% overall (75.0% patients, 73.1% caregivers), with 81.3% of Anopheles females blood-fed with human blood and 2.5% (2/80) testing positive for P. falciparum sporozoites; only 3.7% reported mosquito net use during admission. Authors conclude coexistence of asymptomatic parasitaemic patients, infective vectors, and inadequate protection indicates plausible nosocomial transmission risk, requiring strengthened malaria screening, vector control, and ITN access
articles.title_original: Nosocomial malaria transmission risk in hospital wards in Abeokuta Nigeria: entomological and parasitological evidence from secondary healthcare facilities.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 11 randomized controlled trials (27,178 children <5 years: RTS,S n=21,589; R21 n=5,589) found RTS,S efficacy 32% against first malaria episode (HR 0.68, 95% CI 0.61-0.76), 29% against multiple episodes (IRR 0.71, 95% CI 0.55-0.91), and 17-22% against severe malaria (RR 0.78-0.83). R21 demonstrated higher efficacy—68% against first episodes (HR 0.32, 95% CI 0.26-0.39) and 67% against multiple episodes (IRR 0.33, 95% CI 0.27-0.41) in single phase 3 trial. Pooled severe adverse event incidence was 11.2% (RTS,S) and 3% (R21), with acceptable safety profiles and no vaccine-attributable deaths
articles.title_original: Efficacy and safety of RTS, S and R21 malaria vaccines in children under five in Africa: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional mixed-methods study of 384 community members and 15 healthcare workers in Jozini, KwaZulu-Natal (2025) found high malaria awareness (96%), with 92% correctly identifying mosquito bites as transmission route and 97% demonstrating high knowledge overall. However, only 79% reported positive attitudes and 64% good prevention practices; knowledge was associated with age (χ²=9.61, p=0.01) but not gender, education, employment, or residence duration. Authors conclude that despite high awareness, misconceptions and inconsistent practices persist, with healthcare worker knowledge undermined by operational barriers including irregular training, staffing gaps, and commodity shortages—necessitating targeted behavioral interventions
articles.title_original: Community and healthcare worker contributions to malaria prevention: a culturally tailored behaviour change approach in Jozini, South Africa.

articles.headline: Cryo-electron microscopy study determined structure of Plasmodium falciparum Multidrug Resistance Protein 1 (PfMDR1) in complex with antimalarial candidate ACT-451840 at 3.42 Å resolution, revealing that ACT-451840 binds within the central cavity and locks PfMDR1 in an inward-open conformation, thereby inhibiting basal ATPase activity. Structural comparison with mefloquine-bound state explains how ACT-451840 resistance mutations confer mefloquine re-sensitization. Authors conclude findings provide structural basis for ACT-451840's selective inhibitory mechanism against PfMDR1, informing development of future antimalarial candidates targeting drug-resistant parasites
articles.title_original: Structural and mechanistic insights into the inhibition of Plasmodium falciparum MDR1.

articles.headline: Genetic analysis of P. falciparum pyrimidine biosynthesis enzymes investigated aspartate transcarbamoylase (PfATC), carbamoyl phosphate synthetase II (PfCPSII), and dihydroorotase (PfDHO) via knockdown and deletion studies. PfATC knockdown proved lethal with impaired parasite growth upon N-terminal truncation, PfCPSII knockdown revealed essentiality for proliferation, and conditional deletion of PfDHO in uracil-salvaging parasites resulted in uracil auxotrophy. Authors identify multiple vulnerable nodes within the pyrimidine biosynthesis pathway, supporting it as compelling drug target alongside the clinically advancing rate-limiting enzyme dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (PfDHODH)
articles.title_original: Genetic analysis of pyrimidine biosynthetic enzymes in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study (249 participants, Emana village, Cameroon) assessing knowledge, attitudes, and practices (KAP) toward malaria. Overall prevalence 24.9%; significantly higher in 5–18-year-olds (13.25%, p=0.027). Despite satisfactory knowledge and attitudes, practices were poor (women performed better than men, p=0.014), revealing a persistent knowledge-to-practice gap. This gap contributes to silent transmission, particularly in the 5–18-year age group, suggesting need for strengthened messaging and enhanced surveillance
articles.title_original: Knowledge, attitudes and practices towards malaria in a peri-urban Cameroonian village: Persistent silent transmission despite satisfactory awareness.

articles.headline: Protein microarray study (79 PfEMP1 fragments including domain cassettes 1, 4, 5, 8, 13, 15) measuring serological responses in Malian children with cerebral malaria (CM), severe malarial anemia (SMA), and concurrent CM/SMA versus uncomplicated malaria controls. Severe malaria cases showed distinct antibody gaps: DCs 1, 8, 13 for SMA; DC13 for CM. Antibodies to downstream PfEMP1 domains may reflect severe malaria exposure but could represent protective natural immunity
articles.title_original: Distinct Plasmodium falciparum Erythrocyte Membrane Protein 1 Domain Cassette-Associated Antibody Gaps Specific to Cerebral Malaria and Severe Malarial Anemia.

articles.headline: Functional study of PfSR-X2, a serine/arginine-rich RNA-binding protein in P. falciparum, using CRISPR-Cas9 conditional knockdown. PfSR-X2 is essential for asexual blood-stage growth; depletion reduced parasite proliferation and merozoite production. Transcriptomic analysis revealed stage-specific changes affecting RNA metabolism, antigenic variation, and ApiAP2 regulatory pathways; rif family strongly perturbed. PfSR-X2 functions as stage-specific post-transcriptional regulator rather than global splicing factor
articles.title_original: The serine-arginine-rich protein PfSR-X2 modulates human malaria parasite gene expression during the intraerythrocytic developmental cycle.

articles.headline: Functional characterization of prokaryotic-like cardiolipin synthase (PfCLS) in P. falciparum mitochondria. PfCLS knockout disrupts parasite development and kills parasites; depletion impairs mitochondrial respiratory super-complex assembly and causes hypersensitivity to atovaquone. Loss of cardiolipin content and widespread phospholipid perturbations occurred; metabolomics showed disrupted TCA cycle and altered amino acid levels. PfCLS is essential for asexual blood stages, controlling mitochondrial membrane biogenesis and phospholipid homeostasis
articles.title_original: A prokaryotic-like Cardiolipin Synthase is essential to maintain mitochondrial function, lipid homeostasis and survival of blood stage malaria parasite Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study (274 sickle cell disease patients, Gulu Regional Referral Hospital, Northern Uganda, June–August 2025) using hierarchical diagnostic framework for subclinical malaria. Overall SCM prevalence 23.4% (by blood smear or RDT); smear positivity 11.7%, RDT positivity 22.3%. Only microscopy-confirmed parasitemia associated with lower hemoglobin (6.87 vs 7.82 g/dL, p=0.0027). Rural residence was sole independent predictor; RDTs had limited clinical utility due to antigen persistence
articles.title_original: Subclinical malaria in sickle cell disease in Northern Uganda: prevalence, predictors, and hematologic impact.

articles.headline: Murine model of P. yoelii NSM using scRNA-seq and flow cytometry to characterize LEF1-LAG3 axis in CD4⁺ T cells. Infection induced splenomegaly, upregulated activation markers (ICOS, CD69), and markedly increased LAG3 expression on CD4⁺ T cells. LEF1 was downregulated post-infection and acts as transcriptional repressor at Lag3 promoter; Wnt agonist CHIR99021 dose-dependently reduced LAG3⁺CD4⁺ T cells. LEF1-LAG3 modulation offers avenue to fine-tune anti-malarial immune responses
articles.title_original: The LEF1-LAG3 axis regulates CD4(+) T cell function during Plasmodium yoelii NSM infection.

articles.headline: Prime-and-trap vaccination strategy in mice evaluating minimal requirements for P. yoelii liver-stage protection via CSP-specific CD8⁺ T cells. Sterile protection achieved with CSP-HDT doses as low as 5 ng DNA; protection was lost at ultra-low 50 pg dose, associated with insufficient hepatic CSP-specific CD8⁺ T cells. Results indicate a threshold of liver-resident CSP-specific CD8⁺ T cells is required for sterile protection against sporozoite challenge
articles.title_original: A prime-and-trap vaccination strategy to evaluate minimal requirements for malaria liver stage protection in mice.

articles.headline: Review examining the progressive decentralization and diversification of malaria parasite genomics practice, enabled by wider access to sequencing and cost-effective targeted methods. Highlights the field's evolution from descriptive discovery toward routine global surveillance informing public health decisions, while outlining key development needs and growth opportunities for standardizing data formats, analysis pipelines, and translating genomic insights into action
articles.title_original: Malaria Parasite Genomics: Decentralization, Diversification, and Development Goals.

articles.headline: Multilevel logistic binary model assessing P. vivax malaria risk in Brazilian municipalities under climate change scenarios (RCP 4.5 and RCP 8.5) for 2030 and 2050. Maximum temperature was most influential factor (β=0.35), followed by Simple Daily Intensity Index (β=0.19) and relative humidity (β=0.12). Results project growing malaria risk across Brazil, with most significant impacts in the Amazon expanding to Southeast and Northeast by 2050
articles.title_original: Climate change and Plasmodium vivax Malaria Risk in Brazil: Developing adaptive tool for Brazilian Municipalities.
articles.authors: C M Sousa T, et al.

articles.headline: Study examining genetic diversity of RIPR, a key invasion protein in P. vivax malaria. (Abstract not provided; no specific results available.)
articles.title_original: Unraveling the genetic diversity of RIPR, a key invasion protein in Plasmodium vivax malaria.

articles.headline: Systematic review of hydroxamic acids as HDAC inhibitors for malaria, assessing antiplasmodial structure-activity relationships in >750 compounds with in vitro activity against P. falciparum. In 2024, malaria caused ~282 million cases and 610,000 deaths, mostly P. falciparum in Sub-Saharan Africa. HDAC inhibitors are prospective drug targets because histone/lysine deacetylases control transcriptional and developmental processes essential to parasite survival
articles.title_original: Hydroxamic Acids as HDAC Inhibitor Drug Leads for Malaria.

articles.headline: 12-month longitudinal entomological study (September 2023–August 2024, Penka-Michel, Cameroon highlands) of malaria transmission and insecticide resistance. Three vectors: An. funestus (43.7%, IR 7.8%), An. gambiae (42.9%, IR 5.4%), An. ziemanni (13.5%); annual EIR 839.5 infective-bites/human/night. P. falciparum predominated (70.2%) but significant non-falciparum burden: P. malariae (27.5%), P. ovale (22.1%). Widespread resistance in 70% of infected samples; L119F-GSTe2 associated with P. ovale curtisi (IRR 1.15, p=0.03), L1014F-kdr with P. malariae (IRR 2.2, p=0.04)
articles.title_original: Complex non-falciparum transmission and widespread insecticide resistance in Penka-Michel, a hyperendemic malaria focus of the Cameroon highlands.

articles.headline: P. falciparum merozoite invasion ligand transcript levels differ regionally: Malaysian isolates showed higher eba175 but lower eba181 and Rh2b transcripts than Gambian isolates. Among Malaysian isolates, eba181 transcript levels correlated with parasite multiplication rates, suggesting regional differentiation in ligand expression may affect vaccine candidacy
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum invasion ligand gene transcript profiles in different populations.

articles.headline: Trinidad imported malaria surveillance (December 2018–December 2023): 56/153 suspected cases (36.6%) confirmed positive; P. vivax, P. falciparum, and P. ovale confirmed imported; P. malariae identified in 3 samples, 2 without travel history indicating local transmission. Headache and nausea were significant risk factors (p < 0.05)
articles.title_original: Imported malaria in Trinidad, West Indies: Detection of multiple non-endemic Plasmodium species.

articles.headline: Benin vector surveillance using multimethod approach: 2,337 adult mosquitoes collected; 7 species identified including Aedes aegypti (predominantly diurnal), Anopheles gambiae (nocturnal), and Culex species (endophilic). Concurrent detection of An. gambiae and Ae. aegypti during both day and night represents epidemiological signal for combined malaria-dengue transmission risk
articles.title_original: Species diversity and human biting rates of malaria and dengue vectors in central and northern Benin.

articles.headline: Eastern Indian HBT cohort: HLA-A*33 conferred significant protection against Toxoplasma gondii through restricted intracellular replication and enhanced CD8+ IFN-γ+ responses, driven by higher A*33–SAG2C binding affinity. In contrast, HLA-C*07 showed potential negative association with P. falciparum infection through strong MSP3 peptide binding with minimal cross-reactivity, indicating pathogen-specific antigen recognition
articles.title_original: HLA polymorphisms shape divergent outcomes of Toxoplasma and Plasmodium infection in Eastern Indian HbE/β-thalassemia cohort.

articles.headline: Ghana cross-sectional study (388 participants, 3 sites): thrombocytopaenia most consistently associated with P. falciparum; malaria cases median platelet 152.0 × 10⁹/L vs 234.0 × 10⁹/L controls (p < 0.001), adjusted OR = 11.8 (95% CI 6.18–24.7). Haematological-indices diagnostic model achieved superior AUC (77.6%) vs sociodemographic model (57.9%)
articles.title_original: Association between thrombocytopaenia and Plasmodium falciparum infection in Ghana.

articles.headline: Ethiopian scoping review (13 studies, 84.6% cross-sectional, 7.7% RCT): identified light microscopy, RDT, PCR, and LAMP as current malaria diagnostics. Findings underscore need for tailored, accurate, user-friendly diagnostics addressing unique limitations of each modality to support malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Current practices and innovative diagnostics for Malaria in Ethiopia: a scoping review.

articles.headline: Kenya pregnant women diagnostic study (236 participants, April 2023–March 2024): mRDT (First Response™) showed higher sensitivity 90% (95% CI 0.77–1.0) but lower specificity 80% (95% CI 0.74–0.85) than microscopy (sensitivity 80%, specificity 89%) versus peripheral qPCR. Placental malaria prevalence 15.7% by qPCR vs 7.2% by microscopy; P. falciparum predominated; anaemia highly prevalent (69.9%) but not significantly associated with malaria or ABO blood group (P > 0.05)
articles.title_original: Evaluation of microscopy and first response™ malaria Ag PAN/Pf RDT compared to qPCR and hemoglobin levels in pregnant women at delivery in Busia County, Western Kenya.

articles.headline: Madagascar 2024 national micronutrient survey (23 regions): malaria 4–14%, inflammation 21–46%, anaemia 42% in preschool children and ~33% in older groups, iron deficiency 26% preschool/6–16% older, zinc deficiency ~33% all groups, riboflavin deficiency 75% preschool/~90% older. Multiple conditions co-occurred frequently but not exceeding expected by chance; integrated strategies combining fortification, supplementation, dietary diversification, and infection control needed
articles.title_original: Burden and Co-Occurrence of Anemia, Micronutrient Deficiencies, Inflammation, and Malaria Among Women, Adolescents, and Children in Madagascar: A National Cross-Sectional Survey.

articles.headline: Nepal qualitative study (January–June 2020): 2,000+ participants across 700 sessions in 7 provinces and 72 municipalities; 100 key informant interviews. Community engagement process identified priorities including HIV testing at borders, multi-disease diagnostic platforms, and strengthened community surveillance, which were incorporated into national strategic plans; government pledged 20% domestic co-financing
articles.title_original: Community and stakeholder engagement in national priority setting and participatory research for HIV, Tuberculosis, and Malaria programs in Nepal.

articles.headline: Uganda Apac District cross-sectional study (240 households): Cox proportional hazards regression identified factors delaying treatment-seeking—secondary education increased delay risk (HR = 1.67, p = 0.044), while no hospital medicine (HR = 0.61, p = 0.001) and financial constraints (HR = 0.49, p < 0.001) associated with significant delays. All caregivers sought treatment; 70% female
articles.title_original: Determinants of time to malaria treatment-seeking behaviour among caregivers of children under five in Uganda: a case study of Apac district.
articles.authors: Odur B, Opio B, Nabulya O

articles.headline: Mozambique systematic review and meta-analysis (6 studies, 1,862 participants): artemether-lumefantrine uncorrected efficacy 91.0% (95% CI 84.2–94.6), PCR-corrected efficacy 98.3% (95% CI 96.9–99.1) for uncomplicated P. falciparum. Early treatment failure 0.05% (95% CI 0.01–0.38), late clinical failure 2.73% (95% CI 1.65–4.50), recrudescence 1.8% (95% CI 1.20–2.70), day 3 parasite positivity 0.78% (95% CI 0.21–2.81)
articles.title_original: Efficacy of artemether-lumefantrine for uncomplicated plasmodium falciparum malaria treatment in mozambique: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Historical analysis of South Korea's National Malaria Eradication Service (NMES, early 1960s–1969): examines government strategies to market public health to Korean public and rural villages post-Korean War. No empirical research findings; focuses on transformation of NMES from outside force to community partner through citizen participation
articles.title_original: Mobilizing Public Health in South Korea: NMES (National Malaria Eradication Service) and its Legacy, 1945-1969.

articles.headline: Reviews supply chain and logistical challenges for adjuvants used in new tuberculosis and malaria vaccine development
articles.title_original: Understanding adjuvant supply chains for new TB and malaria vaccines.
articles.authors: Gotham D, Frick M, Harrington M

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 330 pregnant women in Southwest Ethiopia (March–June 2024) found malaria prevalence of 25.2% (83/330) and soil-transmitted helminth prevalence of 29.4% (97/330), with Ascaris lumbricoides most common. Rural residence (AOR=2.011, p=0.024) and farmer occupation (AOR=4.801, p=0.048) were significant malaria risk factors, while poor handwashing before meals (AOR=6.468, p=0.002) and after toilet (AOR=8.826, p=0.001) were associated with helminth infection. Authors recommend integrated antenatal interventions including health education, sanitation improvements, insecticide-treated nets, and deworming
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Malaria and Soil-Transmitted Helminths and Associated Factors Among Pregnant Women Visiting Mizan-Tepi University Teaching Hospital, Southwest Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Describes development and validation of PvGAP, a highly-multiplexed microhaplotype amplicon panel with 80 high-diversity targets and 8 drug-resistance markers for genotyping Plasmodium vivax infections. Panel was evaluated using field samples from Ethiopia and in silico analysis of whole genomes from the MalariaGEN Pv4 database, demonstrating robust amplification and competitive accuracy for relatedness inference across three geographic regions. Authors conclude PvGAP is cost-effective for P. vivax genomic epidemiology, supporting drug resistance surveillance and discrimination of local versus imported cases
articles.title_original: PvGAP: Development of a globally-applicable, highly-multiplexed microhaplotype amplicon panel for Plasmodium vivax.

articles.headline: Laboratory study of Atriplex halimus aerial parts extract evaluated against Anopheles pharoensis mosquito. UPLC/MS analysis identified 78 secondary metabolites, predominantly flavonoids. The dichloromethane fraction at 6.67 mg/cm² achieved 81.92% repellency against starved A. pharoensis females. Findings suggest A. halimus as a source of bioactive compounds with mosquito-repellent potential for natural product-based vector control
articles.title_original: Phytochemical profiling, metabolomics, and molecular docking studies of Atriplex halimus aerial parts revealing potential insecticidal activity against the malaria vector Anopheles pharoensis.

articles.headline: Opinion article advocating for open access to malaria intervention data, noting that malaria caused an estimated 610,000 deaths in 2024, predominantly in sub-Saharan Africa. Authors argue that accessible data on diagnostics, antimalarial drugs, and vector control tools is essential for innovation and equitable health outcomes. Using India as case study, they demonstrate gaps in district-level data availability and propose standardized data platforms to accelerate malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Public Availability of Malaria Intervention Data Will Be Vital for the Acceleration of Its Control and Elimination.

articles.headline: Analysis of WHO malaria data from 2015–2024 in High Burden to High Impact countries using Mann–Kendall trend test identified sustained case increases in Nigeria (MK=3.94, p=0.002), Democratic Republic of Congo (MK=3.76, p=0.002), Cameroon (MK=3.40, p=0.005), Uganda (MK=2.86, p=0.02), and Mali (MK=2.50, p=0.04). These countries face emerging threats including antimalarial drug resistance, insecticide resistance, pfhrp2 gene deletions, and Anopheles stephensi invasion. Authors emphasize urgent need for integrated surveillance, noting Cabo Verde's recent malaria-free certification demonstrates elimination is achievable
articles.title_original: Turning the tide against malaria in high-burden African countries: Trends, threats, and solutions.

articles.headline: Geospatial risk mapping study in Nsanje District, Malawi, using environmental variables (temperature, rainfall, elevation, slope, proximity to rivers) and socioeconomic factors to assess malaria hazard and vulnerability. Results show 20.4% of district in high-hazard zones and 35.1% in very high-hazard zones; overall 43.1% classified as high risk and 40.5% as moderate risk. Temperature and rainfall were largest contributors to hazard with 35.1% and 20.4% contribution respectively. Authors recommend enhanced healthcare accessibility and early warning systems to support targeted prevention efforts
articles.title_original: Mapping malaria risk using geospatial and AHP approaches in Nsanje District, Malawi.

articles.headline: CDC operational guidance document for investigating locally acquired, mosquito-transmitted malaria cases in the United States, published 2026
articles.title_original: CDC Operational Guidance for Investigating Locally Acquired Mosquito-Transmitted Malaria - United States, 2026.

articles.headline: Time-delayed mathematical model of human-malaria transmission incorporating incubation period and awareness program implementation delays, calibrated using case data from India, Democratic Republic of Congo, and Uganda. Multiple delays destabilize system earlier than single delays, producing periodic oscillations and potential chaos at high delay values. Optimal control analysis showed that combined insecticide measures and treatment together are most effective for controlling malaria spread
articles.title_original: Effects of insecticides and awareness on the dynamics of a delayed malaria model: A real-data calibration.

articles.headline: Nested study in MIC-DroP trial (NCT04978272) with 195 infants completing PCV-10 pneumococcal vaccine series in Uganda. Neither infant perennial malaria chemoprevention nor early-life malaria exposure (through 14 weeks) affected PCV-10 immunogenicity or seroconversion. Low birthweight (<2500g) associated with 82% lower antibody fold-change (p=0.003) and reduced seroconversion odds (OR=0.19, p=0.003); preterm birth (<37 weeks) showed similar patterns (79% lower fold-change, p=0.018; OR=0.181, p=0.009)
articles.title_original: Low birthweight and prematurity, but not malaria chemoprevention, are associated with reduced pneumococcal vaccine immunogenicity in Ugandan infants.

articles.headline: Hospital-based case-control study in Sudan with 105 children (3 months–16 years): 35 with severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria, 35 with uncomplicated malaria, 35 healthy controls. Plasma IFN-γ and IL-10 significantly elevated in severe malaria versus uncomplicated malaria and controls (p<0.001). Strong positive correlation between IFN-γ and IL-10 (rho=0.688, p<0.001) and between IL-10 and platelet count (rho=−0.338, p=0.009). IL-10 (AUC=0.720) and IFN-γ (AUC=0.687) showed moderate diagnostic performance for discriminating severe from uncomplicated malaria
articles.title_original: Interferon-γ and Interleukin-10 Profiles Correlate with Disease Severity in Sudanese Children with Plasmodium falciparum Malaria.

articles.headline: Molecular surveillance of 41 Plasmodium falciparum dried blood spot samples from patients treated with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHA-PPQ) in East Nusa Tenggara, Indonesia. All 41 patients (100%) showed adequate clinical and parasitological response (ACPR) at Day 42. Pfmdr1 gene copy number variation analysis identified 36 isolates (87.81%) with single copy and 5 isolates (12.19%) with multiple copies. Findings indicate genetic variation related to selective pressure against piperaquine, with authors recommending proactive surveillance to detect drug sensitivity shifts before widespread clinical failure
articles.title_original: The Impact of pfmdr1 Gene Copy Number on the Efficacy of Dihydroartemisinin-Piperaquine in Treating Malaria in West Sumba and Kupang Districts, East Nusa Tenggara, Indonesia.

articles.headline: Systematic review of P. falciparum resistance markers in Angola (20 studies, 2000–2024) found chloroquine-resistant pfcrt CVIET in northern/coastal provinces, SP-resistant pfdhfr/pfdhps IRNGE in Cabinda/Zaire, and no validated artemisinin-resistance pfk13 mutations; pfmdr1 wild-type haplotypes predominated with declining 86Y/1246Y frequencies, though emerging CNV amplification occurred in southern Angola. Heterogeneous provincial distribution demands expanded molecular surveillance to protect ACT efficacy
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum antimalarial resistance in Angola: a systematic review.

articles.headline: Cluster-randomized trial in Bankass, Mali (100,000+ population, 3 years) comparing proactive CHW household visits vs fixed-point services found no difference in parasite prevalence among all ages, fever, care-seeking, or diagnostic access in children <5 years, though intervention CHWs achieved target household visit frequency in <50% of homes. Both strategies extended comparable access to malaria case management despite incomplete intervention coverage
articles.title_original: Impact of proactive community case management of malaria on malaria prevalence in Bankass, Mali: a cluster-randomised controlled trial.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 33 African countries found asymptomatic malaria prevalence of 34% (95% CI: 28–40%) among schoolchildren (range 5–53%), with P. falciparum at 29.3%, mixed infections 2%, P. vivax 1%, and P. malariae 1%. Infection associated with male gender (OR=1.26), anemia (OR=5.51), bednet non-use (OR=1.99), and prior malaria treatment (OR=3.82); context-specific interventions necessary given substantial heterogeneity
articles.title_original: Prevalence and determinants of asymptomatic malaria infection among schoolchildren in Africa: insights from a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Qualitative systematic review of 10 studies (7 Sub-Saharan African countries, 2010–2024) identified eight themes shaping community indoor residual spraying engagement: operational burdens, perceived efficacy concerns, socio-political dynamics, communication quality, gender dynamics, structural incompatibility, implementation strategy, and local infrastructure. Coverage requires human-centered approaches reducing household labor, addressing nuisance insect concerns, ensuring respectful conduct, and strengthening community trust
articles.title_original: Community-level barriers and facilitators of indoor residual spraying for malaria control: a systematic review of qualitative evidence from sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: Tetracycline-affinity proteomics identified an apicoplast-localized uncharacterized transmembrane protein (UCT) with structural homology to CorA-family metal transporters. UCT proved dispensable for blood-stage P. falciparum, but UCT knockdown impaired gametocyte development and sexual parasite maturation, suggesting a role in transmission-stage parasites critical for mosquito infection
articles.title_original: Identification of a Putative Metal Transporter in the Apicoplast of Malaria Parasites.

articles.headline: Retrospective study of 85 P. falciparum patients (21 [24.7%] with severe malaria) found higher D-dimer in severe vs non-severe disease (10,943 vs 2,245 μg/L FEU, p=0.0005); D-dimer associated with increased severity odds (aOR 1.14 per 1000 μg/L, 95% CI: 1.06–1.23), with sensitivity 0.76, specificity 0.73, and accuracy 0.74 at cutoff 4,864 μg/L FEU. D-dimer showed moderate utility as a severity marker
articles.title_original: D-dimer as a marker of severe disease in imported malaria: a retrospective observational study.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 14 Ethiopian studies (8,762 participants) estimated microscopy-confirmed malaria prevalence at 7.1% (95% CI: 4.9–10.2%), highest in Amhara (10.7%). Key risk factors: stagnant water (OR=6.71), absent IRS (OR=5.90), breeding sites (OR=6.13), endemic travel (OR=5.86), eaved houses (OR=4.5), prior infection (OR=3.54), and bednet non-use (OR=4.8). Strengthened ITN use and environmental management could reduce transmission
articles.title_original: Prevalence and associated factors of microscopy-confirmed malaria among the general population in Ethiopia: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Protocol for household randomized trial in Homa Bay County, Kenya (500 households, 3 arms) evaluating PBO-ceiling nets, PBO-LLINs, and combination strategies in populations with limited conventional LLIN access. Primary outcome: Plasmodium infection prevalence by PCR at 12 months; secondary outcomes include vector density by CDC light traps. Trial will generate epidemiological and entomological evidence for novel vector control
articles.title_original: Evaluating the impacts of piperonyl butoxide-treated ceiling nets on malaria risk among high-risk populations with limited access to conventional LLINs in Homa Bay Country, Kenya: study protocol for a household randomized control trial.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 10 Ethiopian studies found malaria-HIV coinfection prevalence of 9% (95% CI: 3–15) among people living with HIV. Associated clinical conditions: severe anemia 31%, hyperparasitemia 41%, and CD4+ counts ≤200 cells/µL 35%. Overall malaria-HIV coinfection burden remains significant in Ethiopia, with concurrent management implications
articles.title_original: Burden of malaria-HIV coinfection and associated adverse outcomes in Ethiopia: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Landscape analysis identified 67 malaria RDTs from 27 international manufacturers; most provide results within 30 minutes on whole blood with high diagnostic sensitivity and specificity (≥98%). Although many WHO-endorsed, clinical and implementation science studies are needed to determine optimal delivery and care strategies for RDTs in endemic settings
articles.title_original: Advances in Rapid Diagnostic Tests for Malaria: A Landscape Review and Analysis.

articles.headline: Genomic surveillance of 437 Eswatini malaria cases (March–December 2023) revealed significant clustering of resistance markers: kelch13 P553L in 0.9%, dhps/dhfr sextuple mutant in 7.8%, nearly fixed MDR1 N86, >60% polyclonal infections, and <1% non-falciparum coinfections. Integrating routine genomic surveillance into national programs is essential for tracking and responding to resistance emergence in low-transmission settings
articles.title_original: Genomic Surveillance Reveals Clusters of Plasmodium falciparum Antimalarial Resistance Markers in Eswatini, A Low-Transmission Setting.

articles.headline: Vector Control Optimization Model parameterized with Tanzanian field data (Anopheles gambiae s.l.) found 61% immature mosquito mortality from larviciding; combining 80% ITN coverage with ≥60% larviciding reduced annual entomological inoculation rate below 1 ib/p/year, the transmission interruption threshold. Larviciding effectively complements ITNs for achieving malaria elimination in rural settings
articles.title_original: Modelling the impact of larviciding as a supplementary malaria vector control intervention in rural south-eastern Tanzania: A district-level simulation study.

articles.headline: Gene disruption of PyCryPH2 (pleckstrin homology domain protein) in P. yoelii ookinetes produced irregular crystalloid microstructure and significantly reduced sporozoite invasion of mosquito salivary glands and mouse liver, indicating PyCryPH2 is essential for properly organized crystalloids and functional sporozoite maturation
articles.title_original: A pleckstrin homology domain protein is involved in crystalloid formation in Plasmodium ookinetes and affects the maturation of infective sporozoites.

articles.headline: Prospective observational study of 3,195 febrile children in Gabon (Jan 2021–July 2023): 41.6% tested positive by thick blood smear, 21.4% had severe malaria. Despite negative microscopy, 86.7% received presumptive diagnosis and 98.8% antimalarial treatment; asthenia (aOR=1.41), headache (aOR=1.71), and nausea-vomiting (aOR=1.95) independently predicted presumptive diagnosis, revealing high overtreatment despite diagnostic capacity
articles.title_original: Diagnostic and therapeutic practices for paediatric malaria: Gap between guidelines and clinical reality in south-east Gabon.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 2021–2025 studies (10 studies; crude: 6 studies, 7,466 participants) found no significant association between ITN/LLIN use and P. falciparum infection: crude pooled OR=0.67 (95% CI 0.42–1.07, p=0.078), adjusted aOR=0.88 (95% CI 0.42–1.86, p=0.71), with substantial heterogeneity (I²=88.7%) explained partly by population type and study design, suggesting context-dependent efficacy
articles.title_original: Impact of Insecticide-Treated Nets on Plasmodium falciparum Infection Rates: A Meta-Analysis.

articles.headline: Non-concurrent cohort of 733 pregnant women in Sierra Leone (2024) receiving intermittent preventive treatment for malaria in pregnancy (IPTp): 55% received first dose, only 8% received all three recommended doses. Women with <4 antenatal contacts had 2.7× higher non-compliance risk (aRR=2.7, 95% CI 1.6–4.5), implicating inadequate antenatal engagement as the primary barrier
articles.title_original: Compliance with intermittent preventive malaria treatment in pregnancy at a tertiary hospital in Sierra Leone.

articles.headline: Cohort of 735 children <5 years with suspected severe malaria in Sierra Leone (2024): 88% received all three parenteral artesunate doses, but only 57% received correct weight-based dosing. Of 735 admissions, 87% were discharged and 10% (73/735) had unfavourable outcomes including 59 deaths; underweight status, multiple convulsions, and incorrect artesunate dosing independently predicted adverse outcomes
articles.title_original: Parenteral artesunate compliance and hospital outcomes in children under five with suspected severe malaria in Sierra Leone.

articles.headline: Geospatial analysis of 7.4 million malaria cases in Sierra Leone (2021–2024): chiefdom incidence ranged 21.2–>750 per 1,000 population with significant spatial clustering (Moran's I >0, p<0.01). Space-time analysis identified high-risk districts (RR=1.2–1.9, p<0.01) and low-risk districts (RR=0.5–0.9, p<0.01); no national-level association between incidence and rainfall, demonstrating spatially heterogeneous transmission requiring targeted subnational intervention
articles.title_original: Mapping malaria hotspots through spatial and spatio-temporal analysis in Sierra Leone, 2021-2024.

articles.headline: Correction to comprehensive genomic study of P. falciparum in Sierra Leone examining genetic variation and antimalarial resistance patterns. [Editorial/correction notice; substantive results not provided in title alone.]
articles.title_original: Correction: Comprehensive genomic study of Plasmodium falciparum in Sierra Leone: genetic variation and resistance patterns.

articles.headline: Ecological study across four villages in Menoreh Hills, Central Java (Indonesia), a near-elimination setting: Anopheles vectors persisted throughout night and were closely associated with livestock environments; serological evidence showed marked village-to-village variation in malaria exposure despite minimal clinical cases, indicating that vector ecology, livestock placement, and opportunistic feeding behavior sustain persistent transmission in low-transmission areas
articles.title_original: Anopheles ecology and serological evidence of malaria exposure in clinically malaria-free areas of Menoreh Hills Central Java, Indonesia.

articles.headline: Engineered HC-04 hepatocytes expressing Cas9-mCherry enabled CRISPR-based screening of P. falciparum sporozoite-host interactions. Cas9 activity and guide-RNA-directed gene disruption were validated; disruption of 10 human genes previously linked to bacterial and viral pathogen infection confirmed platform utility for genome-wide identification of hepatocyte factors essential for P. falciparum liver-stage development
articles.title_original: Cas9-expressing HC-04 hepatocytes facilitate CRISPR-based analysis of Plasmodium falciparum sporozoite-host interactions.

articles.headline: Comment on rapid whole-genome sequencing of Plasmodium DNA from cryptic malaria cases in UK travellers regarding insights into infection origins, transmission, and antimalarial resistance. [Editorial/comment notice; substantive results not provided in title alone.]
articles.title_original: Comment on Rapid Whole genome sequencing of Plasmodium DNA from cryptic malaria cases in UK travellers provides insights into infection origins, transmission, and antimalarial resistance.

articles.headline: Prospective multi-centre study (2016–2021) of 273 travel-associated vector-borne disease patients including 109 (40%) with P. falciparum malaria: at M3, 11% of malaria patients had persistent symptoms vs. 25–69% of arbovirus patients; by M12, only 1% (1/109) malaria patients remained symptomatic vs. 5–17% arbovirus patients. Impaired quality of life at M3 affected only 4% of malaria patients vs. 18–66% with dengue/Zika/chikungunya, indicating post-malaria fatigue resolves within 3 months
articles.title_original: Frequency and persistence of post-acute symptoms after chikungunya, dengue, Zika, and malaria in travellers: a prospective multi-centre study.

articles.headline: Review synthesizing epidemiological burden, mechanisms, and diagnostic challenges of subclinical and asymptomatic Plasmodium infections, which sustain transmission despite low parasitemia undetectable by conventional diagnostics. Mechanism involve parasite adaptations (density thresholds, antigenic variation, drug resistance) and host factors (acquired immunity, genetics); these hidden reservoirs are associated with anemia, impaired cognition, and adverse pregnancy outcomes, necessitating deployment of sensitive field diagnostics for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Mechanistic insights into subclinical Plasmodium infections: Unveiling the silent drivers of malaria transmission.

articles.headline: Community-based entomological surveillance over 1,620 trap nights in Zambia compared three indoor residual spraying regimes—DDT alone, clothianidin+deltamethrin (CD) alone, and household-level micro-mosaic of DDT versus CD—in the context of LLIN coverage. Anopheles species densities were approximately comparable across all regimes (p≥0.1420), with no differences in An. funestus group or An. gambiae complex composition. Household-level micro-mosaic IRS applications may be as immediately effective as conventional macro-mosaic deployments, offering a potential strategy for mitigating resistance evolution
articles.title_original: Indoor residual spraying of distinct insecticide formulations in a household-scale micro-mosaic format: Feasibility and effectiveness for malaria vector control in rural Zambia.

articles.headline: Retrospective review of P. vivax cases in NYC (2018–2024) showed prevalence rising from 2.9% (6/206) in 2018 to 18.2% (63/346) in 2023. Of 41 hospital admissions, 97.6% (40/41) were recent migrants; severe malaria occurred in 24.4% (10/41), with 12.2% (5/41) requiring intensive care. Relapses occurred in 17% (7/41), predominantly in patients not receiving liver-stage therapy; primaquine was prescribed after G6PD testing in 78% (32/41). Timely diagnosis and liver-stage therapy are critical to reducing morbidity and relapses
articles.title_original: An Increase in Imported Plasmodium vivax Malaria in New York City: Clinical and Demographic Trends Following Recent Migration.

articles.headline: Case report of a nulliparous patient at 36 weeks+4 days gestation with severe P. falciparum malaria (5.4% parasitemia) acquired during travel to Ghana. Initial peripheral smear equivocal; diagnosis confirmed by repeat testing; treatment escalated to intravenous artesunate after artemether-lumefantrine initiation. Clinical course complicated by spontaneous preterm premature rupture of membranes, worsening thrombocytopenia, and postpartum preeclampsia with severe features; patient recovered, newborn remained stable without congenital malaria. Case highlights diagnostic challenges, rapid disease progression, and gaps in pregnancy-specific management guidance
articles.title_original: Severe malaria in pregnancy in the United States: A case report and management considerations.

articles.headline: Series of 4-amino-quinazoline-based sulfonamides demonstrated nanomolar potency against P. falciparum blood stage cultures with up to 300-fold selectivity versus mammalian cells. Compounds were active against transmissible stages and refractory to resistance development, acting as reaction-hijacking inhibitors targeting P. falciparum aminoacyl tRNA synthetases, particularly asparagine synthetase (Pf AsnRS). These findings suggest a novel antimalarial scaffold with low resistance risk
articles.title_original: Potent reaction hijacking inhibitors of Plasmodium falciparum asparagine tRNA synthetase.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 92 studies (2013–2025) on Anopheles vectors and zoonotic malaria in Thailand. Pooled nonhuman primate malaria infection prevalence was 0.01 (95% CI 0.00–0.03); An. sawadwongporni, An. minimus, and An. dirus were incriminated as zoonotic vectors with 0.2% pooled infection prevalence. Findings highlight the need for targeted, context-specific vector control interventions addressing species-specific behaviors and bionomics alongside human mobility and monkey-human interactions
articles.title_original: Systematic review of Anopheles abundance and meta-analysis of nonhuman primate malaria infection in mosquitoes in Thailand.

articles.headline: Hybrid Inception-v3 convolutional neural network trained on 15,938 thin blood smear microscopic images from the National Institute of Malaria Research achieved 98.9% training accuracy, 97.3% sensitivity, and 99.9% specificity for malaria diagnosis. Model performance was comparable to or exceeded state-of-the-art machine learning classifiers and conventional microscopy. These results suggest potential to reduce reliance on expert microscopists and improve diagnostic precision in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: A hybrid machine learning approach for automated malaria diagnosis from thin blood smear images.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 217 participants in N'Djamena, Chad (July–September 2024) found 23.5% P. falciparum prevalence with 94.11% molecular frequency of the msp2 3D7/IC allelic family. Single-clone infections predominated (64.65%), followed by double infections (33.33%) and triple infections (2.08%); phylogenetic analysis revealed eight clades. Findings demonstrate substantial genetic diversity and multiplicity of infections, reflecting ongoing malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of Plasmodium falciparum MSP-2 (3D7/IC) and multiplicity of infection in N'Djamena, Chad.

articles.headline: Single case of a new Plasmodium species (P. borealis sp. nov.) identified in an adult male Cory's Shearwater (Calonectris borealis) stranded in Brazil, with parasitemia ranging from 0.04% to 2.09%. PCR sequencing was 100% similar to Plasmodium lineage pLK06; phylogenetic analysis placed it within the Novyella clade. Necropsy showed hepatosplenomegaly with hepatitis and pulmonary hemorrhage. This represents the first haemosporidian infection in this seabird species and first identification of pLK06 in Procellariiformes
articles.title_original: A new Plasmodium Marchiafava & Celli, 1885 (Apicomplexa: Haemosporida) species in Cory's Shearwater (Calonectris borealis) [Cory]) (Aves: Procellariiformes) stranded in a coastal area in Brazil.

articles.headline: Hospital-based study in southern Angola (849 febrile patients, September–October 2023 and May–June 2024) found 67.6% tested negative for malaria; upper respiratory (13.0%), lower respiratory (19.5%), and gastrointestinal infections (16.7%) were leading nonmalarial causes. Malaria and lower respiratory infections carried increased hospitalization risk (OR=3.20 and 5.17, p<0.0001), while malaria and CNS infections were associated with higher mortality (OR=1.90 and 6.30, p<0.0001). Most fevers are nonmalarial, highlighting need for improved diagnostics to guide specific treatment
