articles.title_original: Age-Related and Seasonal Variation in Malaria and Other Causes of Fever and Their Association With Clinical Outcomes in Southern Angola: A Hospital-Based Study.

articles.headline: Quantitative PCR and 16S rRNA sequencing of wild-caught An. demeilloni from Democratic Republic of Congo (2021, 2024) showed Wolbachia (wAnD strain) prevalence significantly higher in abdomens versus cephalothoraxes (p<0.0001). Overall sporozoite prevalence was 1.3% (9/704); P. falciparum sporozoites were detected only in Wolbachia-negative cephalothoraxes. Wolbachia-positive abdomens were associated with lower sporozoite rates (p=0.0329); cephalothorax microbiome composition differed significantly between Wolbachia-positive and Wolbachia-negative mosquitoes (p<0.05). The wAnD strain affects cephalothorax microbiome and potentially blocks sporozoite transmission
articles.title_original: The presence of the wAnD strain of Wolbachia is correlated with lower levels of Plasmodium sporozoites and a less diverse microbiome in wild Anopheles demeilloni mosquito cephalothoraxes.

articles.headline: Systematic review per PRISMA guidelines synthesizing 57 studies (2004–2025) on malaria and leishmaniasis epidemiology in Thailand. Nine Plasmodium species documented, with P. vivax and P. falciparum predominating; P. falciparum showed declining trend since 2012, while P. knowlesi and P. cynomolgi emerged as zoonotic parasites. Six Leishmania species identified; seven provinces reported both diseases, associated with shared ecological conditions and forest-related occupational exposure. Integrated surveillance and control strategies within a One Health framework are needed
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of malaria and leishmaniasis in Thailand (2004-2025): A systematic review.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study (Nigerian hospital, 304 analytical cohort: 152 microscopy-positive, 152 microscopy-negative; 40 with plasma biomarkers) evaluated IL-6 and HO-1 as malaria biomarkers. IL-6 was significantly higher in malaria-positive participants (median 141.28 vs 106.58 pg/mL; p=0.027); HO-1 showed non-significant elevation (230.98 vs 193.73 ng/mL; p=0.131). IL-6 had moderate discriminatory performance (AUC 0.709, sensitivity 72.7%, specificity 76.5%), while HO-1 had lower performance (AUC 0.641). IL-6 is a more informative adjunct inflammatory marker than HO-1 for malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Plasma haem oxygenase-1 and interleukin-6 as adjunct host biomarkers associated with malaria.

articles.headline: Excito-repellency assay comparing natural and synthetic repellents against laboratory and field strains of Anopheles epiroticus in Thailand. Laboratory strain showed highest escape responses with deltamethrin (42%) and permethrin (35%) in contact trials, and citronella oil (>50%) in non-contact assays; field strain differed markedly with DEET (64%) and deltamethrin (60%) most effective in contact conditions, while citronella and vetiver oils showed minimal non-contact effects (~14%), demonstrating behavioral variation between strains critical for vector control strategies
articles.title_original: Do natural or synthetic excito-repellents work better? A study on coastal malaria vector Anopheles epiroticus in Ko Chang, Thailand.

articles.headline: Literature review examining insecticide resistance effects on Anopheles vector competence for malaria transmission. While some studies report impaired parasite development from insecticide exposure and others report enhanced vector competence, the relationship remains poorly understood and contradictory; authors recommend integration of omics-based approaches to address current contradictions and accurately predict epidemiological implications of insecticide resistance
articles.title_original: Insecticide resistance and malaria transmission: divergent effects on Anopheles vector competence.
articles.authors: Adeboye SO, Nwinyi OC, Ayanda OI

articles.headline: Cross-sectional genotyping study of Anopheles funestus across Kenyan regions separated by the Rift Valley examining resistance markers. 4.3kb-SV and G454A-Cyp9k1 resistance alleles nearly fixed in western Kenya but declined toward coast; L119F-GSTe2-RR genotype associated with lower Plasmodium infection (OR 0.2, P=0.046) versus RS, while R allele of 4.3kb marker showed higher infection (OR 5.7, P=0.049). High pyrethroid resistance observed region-wide with intensity higher in Kerio Valley; coastal populations exhibited extreme permethrin resistance (<10% mortality at 10× diagnostic dose)
articles.title_original: Signature of resistance gene evolution and pyrethroid resistance escalation in the major malaria vector Anopheles funestus across Kenyan malaria-endemic regions separated by the Rift Valley.

articles.headline: Structural and biochemical characterization of PfMyoF, a Plasmodium falciparum class XXII myosin. PfMyoF is a plus-end-directed, processive motor binding up to six calmodulin homologue copies and uniquely contains WD40 and Rab-like tail domains not found in other eukaryotic myosins; localizes to perinuclear membranes and transient knockdown impairs growth, suggesting critical function in membrane trafficking during asexual parasite replication
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum PfMyoF contains a Rab-like tail domain and associates with perinuclear membrane trafficking proteins.

articles.headline: High-resolution microhaplotype genotyping of Plasmodium vivax from South Asia (Bangladesh n=35, Bhutan n=27, Nepal n=19) compared with Afghanistan, India, Pakistan (2013-2024). High-complexity infections ranged from 27.04% in Afghanistan to 3.70% in Bhutan; genetic analysis revealed three main clusters partitioning Bangladesh and Bhutan from remaining countries, with Bhutan harboring two sub-populations distinguishing local from cross-border imported cases, validating high-resolution genotyping for monitoring transmission intensity and importations
articles.title_original: Genetic evidence of cross-border Plasmodium vivax spread in a malaria pre-elimination region of South Asia.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 195 infants aged 6-24 months in Cameroon examining primary Epstein-Barr virus infection as risk factor for severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria. 24% were serologically positive for active EBV, with highest proportion of severe malaria cases in EBV-infected children; EBV+ infants showed elevated magnitude and breadth of anti-P. falciparum antibody responses with increased in vitro invasion-blocking capacity despite similar circulating parasitaemia, suggesting primary EBV predisposes to severe falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: Infants under 24 months with severe malaria have a serological profile of active infection with Epstein Barr Virus.

articles.headline: Noninvasive surveillance of Plasmodium knowlesi in wild macaques across 9 endemic countries using 4,752 fecal samples from Southeast and South Asia. Parasite DNA detected in 390 samples (8.2%), with positivity rates ranging 1.4%-18.4%, validating fecal sampling as practical, scalable tool for tracking zoonotic malaria and supporting integration into forest-runoff and wastewater surveillance systems at human-wildlife interface
articles.title_original: Tracking Plasmodium knowlesi Through Fecal DNA for Monitoring Zoonotic Transmission in Wild Macaques Across Southeast and South Asia.

articles.headline: Open-label, dose-escalation phase 1 trial of single-dose intravenous 6-diazo-5-oxo-L-norleucine (DON) in 80 Malawian adults (40 healthy, 38 with uncomplicated malaria) at doses 0.1-10 mg/kg. Transient asymptomatic creatinine elevation occurred in 18% at 12 hours postinfusion; nausea and vomiting were uncommon at 0.1-1.0 mg/kg but more frequent at higher doses; all adverse events resolved rapidly with terminal half-life 1.7-4.1 hours, supporting DON tolerability for adjunctive therapy in cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of 6-Diazo-5-Oxo-L-Norleucine in Malawian Adults With and Without Malaria: A Phase 1 Dose-Escalation Clinical Trial.

articles.headline: Observational study of house sparrows examining associations between habitat urbanization, avian malaria infection (Plasmodium/Haemoproteus), and cloacal bacterial microbiome composition. Urban birds showed lower parasite prevalence than natural and rural populations; in natural habitats, infected birds exhibited nearly significant increase in bacterial richness and altered taxa abundance, whereas infection status was not associated with microbiome parameters in rural and urban habitats, indicating habitat type modulates parasite-microbiome relationships
articles.title_original: Interactions between urbanization, malaria infection and avian cloacal microbiome.

articles.headline: Direct skin feeding assay study of 491 participants aged >5 years (median parasite density 5.1 parasites/μL) in Tanzania examining asymptomatic malaria transmission. 22% of participants were infectious to Anopheles gambiae; RDT-positive individuals infected approximately 2-fold more mosquitoes than RDT-negative/PCR-positive persons; 9.1% (342/3,741) of fed mosquitoes became parasite-positive; top 16 transmitters (3%) accounted for 57% of infected mosquitoes. School-age children (6-15 years), comprising 27% of cohort, contributed 58% of infected mosquitoes. Unexpectedly, 44% of oocyst-positive feeds tested positive for Plasmodium ovale
articles.title_original: Uneven Plasmodium falciparum Transmission and Cryptic ovale Transmission From the Asymptomatic Reservoir in Bagamoyo, Tanzania.

articles.headline: DNA vaccine study in BALB/c mice using vaccines encoding Pfs230 domains D7-D10 as transmission-blocking vaccine candidate for Plasmodium falciparum. Potent domain-specific antibody responses induced with strong dose-dependent transmission-reducing activity against P. falciparum in membrane feeding assays independent of complement; antibodies predominantly targeted D7, with D7 and D7D8 vaccines similarly inducing strong transmission-reducing activity, identifying Pfs230-D7 as novel transmission-blocking vaccine target
articles.title_original: Pfs230 Domains 7-10 Encoded by DNA Vaccines Induce Potent Complement-Independent Transmission-Blocking Antibody Against Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: In vitro study using 3D human brain microvessel model examining febrile exposure effects on ring-stage Plasmodium falciparum adhesion to brain endothelium. Febrile exposure increased transcript levels of exported parasite genes involved in PfEMP1 trafficking, accelerated PfEMP1 surface display in ring-stage infected erythrocytes, and produced 2-fold increase in binding to brain endothelial cells; febrile exposure also enhanced binding of uninfected erythrocytes, suggesting complex interplay between fever and parasite biomass in cerebral malaria pathogenesis
articles.title_original: Febrile Temperature Augments Ring-stage Plasmodium falciparum Adhesion to Brain Endothelial Cells.

articles.headline: Bivalent HBsAg VLPs displaying PfSPECT-1 plus CSP epitopes (NANP and C-terminal region) were evaluated in prime-boost regimens followed by challenge with transgenic P. berghei expressing P. falciparum antigens in BALB/c mice. The N4 bivalent construct with Matrix-M adjuvant provided similar protective efficacy to R21, while NANP-specific IgG negatively correlated with blood-stage parasite load. A second candidate (N2) showed significantly reduced efficacy with PfSPECT-1 addition, but findings demonstrate PfSPECT-1 can be incorporated into HBsAg VLPs co-displayed with CSP epitopes without impairing immunogenicity
articles.title_original: Bivalent virus-like particles expressing SPECT1 and CSP trigger pre-erythrocytic malaria immunity and protect against transgenic Plasmodium falciparum sporozoite challenge in mice.

articles.headline: Conditional knockout of PfAPR3 (an apical polar ring protein) in P. falciparum revealed its essential role in asexual replication; PfAPR3-knockout parasites contacted host RBCs but failed to form tight junctions, resulting in complete invasion block. Three additional APR proteins (PfAPR4, PfCHAKRA, PfAPR5) were identified; PfCHAKRA was critical for cytoskeletal organization. Ultrastructure microscopy defined APR biogenesis and revealed PfAPR3 exhibits dual apical-basal ring localization during schizogony
articles.title_original: The apical polar ring is essential during the blood stage of Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Geospatial analysis in the Wabi Shebele sub-basin, Ethiopia identified environmental factors influencing mosquito breeding and malaria transmission. Bare land (33.66°C), grassland (31.76°C), and agricultural land (31.42°C) with high land surface temperature provided favorable breeding conditions, while forested areas (26.98°C) showed low risk. Agricultural land (24.78% of area), high temperature, low elevation, high soil moisture, and moderate-to-high rainfall were primary factors facilitating malaria distribution, suggesting interventions focus on environmental management in high-risk areas
articles.title_original: Environmental factors influencing mosquito breeding and malaria transmission using geospatial technologies in the Wabi Shebele sub-basin, Ethiopia.

articles.headline: A 2-year survey of 51 cerulean warblers in southern Indiana documented avian haemosporidian infections using microscopy and molecular methods. Overall prevalence was 31.4%, parasitemia was low (<1% of cells) indicating chronic infections, and 11 unique lineages were identified (2 novel). No negative effects of infection on male body condition were observed. Study provides first documentation of haemosporidian lineages for this declining species and advances understanding of host-parasite interactions in avian malaria biology
articles.title_original: EFFECTS OF AVIAN MALARIA IN A POPULATION OF CERULEAN WARBLER (SETOPHAGA CERULEA) WITH FIRST DOCUMENTATION OF HAEMOSPORIDIAN LINEAGES INFECTING THE SPECIES.

articles.headline: Two new multiplexed amplicon sequencing panels for P. vivax were developed: PvGTSeq (249 amplicons for resistance and population structure, requiring selective whole-genome amplification) and PvCRiSP (4 highly polymorphic amplicons for complexity of infection). Both achieved ≥75% amplicon recovery at parasitemias ≥5 parasites/μL with high sequencing accuracy (error rate 3.85e-4% to 2.87e-3%), efficiently detected minority clone alleles in polyclonal infections, and were validated across 5 countries and in-silico across 16 countries
articles.title_original: PvGTSeq and PvCRiSP: Two amplicon-based targeted sequencing panels for Plasmodium vivax.

articles.headline: Post hoc analysis of 735 children (1–4 years) from an RTS,S phase IIb trial revealed that high IgG responses to NANP2 epitope (aHR 0.838, 95% CI 0.716–0.981, p=0.028) and J1 epitope (aHR 0.718, 95% CI 0.611–0.844, p<0.001) were significantly associated with reduced clinical malaria risk and higher Fc-mediated functional activities. These findings identify NANP2 and J1 as promising immunological correlates of protection to guide next-generation CSP-based vaccine development
articles.title_original: Antibody fine specificity correlates with protection from malaria for the RTS,S vaccine in young African children: A post hoc analysis of a phase IIb randomised controlled trial.

articles.headline: Household survey of 1,131 individuals with malaria episodes (January 2019–May 2022) across 9 municipalities in the Brazilian Amazon estimated total economic burden at ~US$181.9 million PPP-USD in 2019, with the Unified Health System bearing 72.4% of costs primarily for control and prevention. Household burden was driven predominantly by indirect costs, mortality costs, and health-related quality-of-life losses, demonstrating the critical role of public systems in shielding families from direct medical costs while revealing substantial indirect burdens
articles.title_original: Economic burden of malaria in the Brazilian Amazon from a societal perspective.

articles.headline: Study examines the role of activated platelets in cerebral malaria pathogenesis (abstract not provided; title indicates investigation of platelet-mediated mechanisms in cerebral malaria complications)
articles.title_original: The role of activated platelets in cerebral malaria pathogenesis.

articles.headline: Generative deep learning was applied to infer joint evolutionary history of Anopheles gambiae populations from Guinea and Burkina Faso. Model selection demonstrated that a migration model fit these populations better than a non-migration model, with the method accurately capturing population genetic differentiation. Findings demonstrate machine learning and generative models are valuable for understanding malaria vector evolution, including demography and adaptation
articles.title_original: On the use of generative models for demographic inference in malaria vectors from genomic data.

articles.headline: Phenotypic assays and transcriptional analysis examined the transition from immature to infectious P. falciparum gametocytes using deformability and exflagellation assays. Data revealed major transcriptional differences between immature and deformable gametocytes but high similarity between deformable and infectious stages. Transmission studies upon mosquito feeding showed deformable gametocytes are immediately infectious upon release from the bone marrow
articles.title_original: Defining the signature of deformable and infectious Plasmodium falciparum gametocytes.

articles.headline: Ruthenium complexes of atovaquone (ATV) were synthesized and evaluated for antiplasmodial activity against asexual and sexual stages. Complexes demonstrated broader activity and faster action than ATV alone and blocked parasite transmission from humans to insects for both gametocytes and oocyst stages, whereas ATV solely blocked oocysts. This represents the first example of metal complexes inhibiting both asexual and sexual parasites with similar potency
articles.title_original: Ruthenium Complexes of Atovaquone Acting on Multiple Stages of the Plasmodium Life Cycle.

articles.headline: Conditional knockout of mTOR in platelets (mTORplt−/−) was evaluated in Plasmodium berghei ANKA-infected mice. mTORplt−/− mice with experimental cerebral malaria showed significantly increased survival independent of parasitemia or thrombocytopenia, with reduced platelet deposition in brain, improved cerebral blood flow, and reduced vascular permeability. Heme-induced platelet mTOR activation occurred downstream of ITAM signaling through CLEC-2; blocking heme-induced platelet activation significantly reduced ECM-associated mortality
articles.title_original: Heme-induced ITAM signaling exacerbates malaria-associated neuropathogenesis through activation of platelet mTOR.

articles.headline: In 218 Kenyan children at moderate malaria transmission and 90 transmission-free controls, cumulative P. falciparum infections showed no association with telomere shortening (p>0.05). Asymptomatic parasitaemia marginally increased telomere length by 0.22 kb (95% CI 0.02–0.42, p=0.03), suggesting suppression of cellular activation during asymptomatic infection may preserve telomeres
articles.title_original: Cumulative Plasmodium falciparum infections do not drive long-term telomere shortening in Kenyan children.

articles.headline: Review synthesizing current understanding of merozoite physiology, emphasizing how motility, environmental signal sensing (ion changes, red blood cell contact), and secretion of specialized organelles coordinate invasion of RBCs, with implications for identifying novel malaria control interventions
articles.title_original: Physiology of invasive Plasmodium merozoites.

articles.headline: Paired in vivo and ex vivo transcriptome analysis of Ethiopian P. vivax isolates showed short-term culture enriches late asexual stages and causes selective clonal depletion (reduced haplotype diversity), but induces no major transcriptional program changes when adjusted for parasite developmental stage, improving interpretation of natural P. vivax infection complexity
articles.title_original: Stage-aware transcriptomics reveals selective haplotype persistence in short-term ex vivo cultured Plasmodium vivax.

articles.headline: In 500 Ghanaian children, logistic regression found no association between FHR-1 deficiency (present in one-third) and severe P. falciparum malaria risk. FHR-1 deficiency was overrepresented among children with severe malarial anemia and significantly lower hemoglobin levels, indicating deficiency correlates with anemia rather than overall malaria severity
articles.title_original: Factor H-related protein 1 deficiency and risk of severe falciparum malaria in Ghanaian children: a cohort analysis.

articles.headline: Three-dimensional paper-based lysis devices achieved 100% red blood cell lysis regardless of sample composition and detected malaria biomarkers equivalently to standard liquid lysis. Dried devices maintained antigen stability >1 week at room temperature, enabling delayed laboratory testing and expanding sample collection sites in low-resource settings
articles.title_original: Creating an improved workflow for paper-based malaria diagnostics by integrating total lysis of whole blood.

articles.headline: In vitro and in vivo comparison of artemisinin derivatives (dihydroartemisinin, artemether) versus iron chelator desferrioxamine showed artemether plus desferrioxamine was less potent than artemether alone, and desferrioxamine failed to inhibit P. yoelii in vivo. Artemisinin's superior activity derives from accumulation in parasitic digestive vacuoles and disruption of heme/iron homeostasis
articles.title_original: Why are iron chelators not as effective as artemisinin in killing malaria parasites?

articles.headline: Exposure of P. falciparum-infected RBCs to physiological febrile temperature (39°C) enhanced cytoadhesion through increased surface trafficking of major virulence factor PfEMP1 and nutrient channel PSAC in four geographic clinical isolates, without altering overall protein expression or developmental stage. Enhanced export could increase disease severity through accelerated RBC sequestration
articles.title_original: Physiological febrile heat stress increases cytoadhesion through increased protein trafficking of Plasmodium falciparum surface proteins into the red blood cell.

articles.headline: Reverse genetics identified two soluble pyrophosphatase isoforms (PfsPPase1, PfsPPase2) from a single P. falciparum locus, with PfsPPase1 localizing to cytoplasm and PfsPPase2 to mitochondrion, apicoplast, and cytoplasm via unique 51-amino acid localization peptide. Both are essential for asexual blood stage development and represent promising novel antimalarial drug targets
articles.title_original: Pyrophosphate homeostasis in multiple subcellular compartments is essential in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: In vitro cerebral malaria model revealed P. falciparum hemozoin-associated proteins (not the crystal itself) disrupt human brain microvascular endothelial cell barrier integrity; protease treatment abolished disruption while nuclease treatment had no effect. Protein-hemozoin binding represents a key mechanism in cerebral malaria pathogenesis and potential therapeutic target
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum hemozoin-associated biomolecules induce brain endothelial cell barrier disruption in an in vitro model of cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: GBD Study 2021 analysis (1990–2021) demonstrated global malaria burden declined, especially in Sub-Saharan Africa, while NTD incidence rose until 2016 then declined. Vector autoregression modeling showed sociodemographic index significantly influenced disease burden in low-SDI regions; environmental sanitation and behavioral risk factors substantially impacted malaria burden reduction
articles.title_original: Global epidemiological trends, distribution of NTDs and malaria, and disease burden projections for the next 15 years.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional comparison of 417 paired Ethiopian samples showed RDT-extracted DNA achieved 89.2% positive percent agreement for P. falciparum (95% CI 84.7–92.5) and 81.9% for P. vivax (95% CI 74.6–87.6) versus dried blood spot standard. Overall agreement (Kappa=0.64) improved to Kappa=0.75 in high parasite load infections (>100,000/μL), supporting RDTs as alternative DNA source
articles.title_original: Rapid diagnostic test kit as a source of DNA in comparison with filter paper for malaria molecular surveillance and drug resistance monitoring: a cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Pbnek4 gene deletion in P. berghei zygotes caused complete developmental arrest: MTOC duplication and microtubule formation were blocked, chromatin remained uncondensed, and nuclear migration and cell polarity failed. Transcriptomic and phosphoproteomic analyses revealed collapsed transcriptional networks governing meiosis and cytoskeleton, establishing NEK4 as essential regulator of meiotic entry and zygote maturation
articles.title_original: A divergent Plasmodium NEK4 acts as a key regulator driving the early events of meiosis.

articles.headline: Commentary on cerebral malaria research methodologies comparing rodent and human models
articles.title_original: Comment on: Cerebral malaria: of mice and men.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 137 P. falciparum infections from 481 samples in Cameroon found 15 distinct msp2 alleles with 14.15% genetic diversity and mean multiplicity of infection of 1.20 (81.68% mono-infections, 18.18% dual, 1.33% triple). The substantial genetic diversity and multiple infections in the region indicate high transmission intensity requiring additional molecular markers for comprehensive parasite characterization
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity of the IC/3D7 allelic family of the merozoite surface protein 2 (MSP2) of Plasmodium falciparum and multiplicity of infection in four health facilities in the Mbouda Health District, Cameroon.

articles.headline: Ugandan follow-up study of 889 children tested 4–15 years post-severe malaria found cerebral malaria and severe malarial anemia associated with persistent cognitive impairment (adjusted mean difference in overall cognition: −0.41 [95% CI −0.74 to −0.09] and −0.31 [−0.61 to −0.01], respectively) and reduced math achievement (−0.46 [−0.78 to −0.14] and −0.32 [−0.61 to −0.03]). Acute kidney injury, hyperuricemia, and elevated angiopoietin-2 at acute illness predicted worse long-term cognitive outcomes
articles.title_original: Long-Term Cognitive Ability and Academic Achievement After Childhood Severe Malaria.

articles.headline: Longitudinal study examining long-term neurocognitive outcomes in survivors of severe malaria infection
articles.title_original: Long-Term Neurocognitive Outcomes After Severe Malaria Infection: No Peace of MIND.

articles.headline: Genetic cross and drug-selection experiments identified P. falciparum PX1 PIN haplotype (L1222P, M1701I, D1705N) as the strongest correlate of reduced dihydroartemisinin and lumefantrine susceptibility. This haplotype was highly enriched under drug pressure in Ugandan isolates, confers asexual-stage growth advantage, and is proposed as a robust surveillance marker for artemether-lumefantrine resistance in African parasites
articles.title_original: A Plasmodium falciparum PX1 haplotype is associated with reduced susceptibility to artemisinin and lumefantrine.

articles.headline: Cost-effectiveness analysis of P. knowlesi control in Sabah, Malaysia found outdoor residual spraying (ORS) averted 13 cases at MYR 2046 per case (~USD 470), outperforming long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs) and combined ORS+LLINs in incremental cost-effectiveness ratios. ORS demonstrated the most favorable cost-effectiveness profile across sensitivity analyses, supporting a stratified approach prioritizing ORS for resource-limited malaria control
articles.title_original: Cost-effectiveness of outdoor residual spraying, insecticide-treated nets, and their combination for zoonotic malaria control in Sabah, Malaysia: a comparative analysis.

articles.headline: Population genetic analysis of 17,565 P. falciparum isolates from 39 countries identified region-specific adaptive patterns: Vietnam parasites all carry pfkelch13 C580Y artemisinin-resistance mutation with high relatedness; Brazil shows elevated identity-by-descent around pfcrt; South America and Horn of Africa show alternative artemisinin resistance pathways via pfKIC7/pfKIC9. Findings enable improved genomic surveillance of evolving drug-resistant parasite populations
articles.title_original: Global-scale population genetic analysis of Plasmodium falciparum identifies region-specific patterns of malaria parasite adaptation.

articles.headline: Decentralized P. falciparum genomic surveillance across six African countries sequenced 1,065 samples in one year using rapid nanopore sequencing (~5 hours, <$25 USD per sample). The novel protocol provided robust coverage across parasitemia levels and laboratories, accurately identified antimalarial resistance markers and hrp2/3 deletions, and detected mutations in minor clones, establishing a feasible continental surveillance approach
articles.title_original: Continental-scale genomic surveillance of Plasmodium falciparum malaria across sub-Saharan Africa with rapid nanopore sequencing.

articles.headline: Brazilian RCT of 419 P. vivax patients compared dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine versus chloroquine with concurrent or delayed (day 42) primaquine. Day-42 recurrence rates: chloroquine+immediate primaquine 2.0% versus DHA-piperaquine+immediate primaquine 1.0% (HR 0.48, p=0.44); DHA-piperaquine+delayed primaquine 2.4% versus chloroquine+delayed primaquine 27.3% (HR 0.08, p<0.0001). Both regimens were well tolerated, supporting DHA-piperaquine for P. vivax treatment in Latin America
articles.title_original: Dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine versus chloroquine for the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium vivax malaria with concurrent or delayed high-dose primaquine in Brazil (Curavivax): an open-label, single-centre, randomised clinical trial.

articles.headline: Knockout of cardiolipin synthase (Cls) in rodent malaria parasite P. berghei impaired asexual blood-stage proliferation with reduced cardiolipin abundance and lowered mitochondrial membrane potential and basal oxygen consumption. PbCls-deficient parasites exhibited severe liver-stage maturation defect despite normal mosquito development and sporozoite infectivity, establishing Cls as critical for mitochondrial function and parasite development
articles.title_original: A bacterial-type cardiolipin synthase in Plasmodium spp. supports mitochondrial respiration and is important for liver stage maturation.

articles.headline: Live imaging of P. falciparum egress revealed signal-dependent exocytosis initiates ≥3 hours before merozoite emergence, with parasitophorous vacuolar membrane rupturing at a single site before rapidly expanding. Conditional PfERC mutants confirmed the endoplasmic reticulum calcium-binding protein is required for exocytosis of egress-specific vesicles, establishing the temporal and spatial choreography of parasite egress mechanisms
articles.title_original: Spatiotemporal dynamics of signal dependent exocytosis and parasitophorous vacuolar membrane rupture during Plasmodium falciparum egress.

articles.headline: Systematic review protocol (not yet completed) to evaluate cost-effectiveness evidence of artemisinin-based combination therapies versus non-ACT or alternative ACT regimens as first-line malaria treatment. Protocol specifies JBI methodology with GRADE certainty assessment; primary outcomes are incremental cost-effectiveness ratios, cost per DALY averted, or quality-adjusted life years gained
articles.title_original: Cost-effectiveness of artemisinin-based combination therapies for first-line treatment of malaria among pediatric and adult populations: a systematic review protocol.

articles.headline: Scoping review of 16 studies (2015–2025) in Southeast Asia found P. falciparum polyclonal infection rates of 3.9–73.9% (mean MOI 1.05–4.9) and P. vivax rates of 16.9–71.4% (mean MOI 1.1–1.91, heterozygosity 0.66–0.87); P. malariae showed low MOI; P. knowlesi ranged 7.3–21% (MOI 1.04–1.06). P. falciparum and P. vivax remain genetically diverse with heterogeneous transmission; standardized molecular surveillance needed for P. malariae, P. knowlesi, and P. ovale
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity and multiplicity of Plasmodium spp. infections in Southeast Asia: a scoping review.

articles.headline: Title only; no abstract available to extract results or specific clinical findings
articles.title_original: Severe Malaria.

articles.headline: Title only; no abstract available. Topic: cultural history of malaria in Hungary
articles.title_original: [Data of cultural history of malaria in Hungary].

articles.headline: Cluster-randomised implementation evaluation (158 clusters across Ghana, Kenya, Malawi; 46-month follow-up) found RTS,S/AS01E vaccine coverage of 82.8% for dose 1 and 71.1% for dose 3. Among vaccine-eligible children: 5,576 deaths in implementation vs. 6,152 in comparison areas (mortality rate ratio 0.87, 95% CI 0.77–0.97, p=0.016), averting approximately one in eight deaths with moderate three-dose coverage
articles.title_original: Impact of introducing RTS,S/AS01(E) malaria vaccine on mortality in young children in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi: an observational evaluation of a cluster-randomised implementation programme.

articles.headline: Case report of juvenile Fatu Hiva monarch found dead June 2022 (Pomarea whitneyi, <20 remaining). Necropsy and molecular testing confirmed fatal P. relictum (GRW4) infection with high parasitaemia and hepatosplenomegaly. First documented fatal avian malaria in this critically endangered species; highlights urgent need for disease surveillance and vector management in conservation planning
articles.title_original: First record of fatal Plasmodium relictum (GRW4) infection in a highly endangered Fatu Hiva monarch (Pomarea whitneyi) fledgling: a case report.
articles.authors: Igual I Beltrán M, et al.

articles.headline: Active/passive detection in DRC-Uganda border region (May–June 2023; 536 villagers, 49.8% P. falciparum positive) identified artemisinin partial-resistance markers: pfkelch13 A675V in 4.3%, C469Y in 1.3%; pfmdr1 NFD haplotype in 50.3%; pfmdr1 CNV 11.7% (1.4 threshold); pfpm2/3 CNV 7.0%. WGS confirmed cross-border spread of resistant parasites from Uganda. Alternative ACTs (artesunate-amodiaquine) merit evaluation
articles.title_original: Cross-border molecular epidemiology of Plasmodium falciparum drug susceptibility: insights from the Uganda-Democratic Republic of the Congo border.

articles.headline: Molecular dynamics study modeled PfPLP1 (Plasmodium falciparum perforin-like protein 1) structure comprising MACPF and APCβ domains. Initial membrane binding occurred via cationic residues; water penetration between tetramers and lipid bilayer initiated pore opening with lipids reorganizing into toroidal edges. Provides mechanistic insights into pore-forming behavior relevant for therapeutic targeting
articles.title_original: Deciphering Membrane Pore Formation Mechanisms of Plasmodium falciparum Perforin-Like Protein 1 (PfPLP1).
articles.authors: Patil SB, Dasgupta S, Bhaumik P

articles.headline: Longitudinal study of 597 children (3,064 samples, 2011–2017) in high-malaria (Tororo) vs. low-malaria (Jinja) Uganda districts found tetanus antibody half-lives of 2.8 years in Tororo vs. 15.8 years in Jinja (p<0.001), with 2.7-fold higher sero-reversion hazard in Tororo (95% CI 1.7–4.1). High malaria transmission accelerates tetanus antibody waning; suggests malaria endemicity should inform booster timing
articles.title_original: Assessing the effect of malaria exposure history on tetanus antibody waning rates among children in Jinja and Tororo Districts, Uganda.

articles.headline: Surveillance (2022–2025) of P. vivax and P. falciparum drug-resistance genes in Korean military and civilian cases. P. vivax: Pvmdr-1 mutations S515R, G698S, L845F, M908L, T958M, F1076L nearly universal; T529, E1233 moderate prevalence. P. falciparum: Y184F highly prevalent; A675V (PfK13) detected only in soldiers. Significant group differences (p<0.05); no temporal trends. Surveillance supports malaria eradication in ROK
articles.title_original: Molecular Surveillance of Plasmodium vivax and Plasmodium falciparum Drug Resistance Genes in the Republic of Korea: 2022-2025.

articles.headline: Study of PBMCs from P. falciparum-infected and non-infected individuals in Brazilian Amazon evaluated GMZ2.6c chimeric vaccine (MSP-3–GLURP–Pfs48/45 fusion). Infected individuals showed higher CD8+ activation to MSP-3 and Pfs48/45; elevated IL-6 and TNF in both groups. T cell epitope prediction identified IFN-γ-inducing sequences. GMZ2.6c showed cellular immunogenicity; P. falciparum infection modulates response toward pro-inflammatory phenotype
articles.title_original: Cellular Immune Response and T Cell Epitope Mapping of Plasmodium falciparum Chimeric Vaccine Candidate GMZ2.6c and Its Components (MSP-3, GLURP and Pfs48/45) in Individuals Naturally Exposed to Malaria in Brazilian Amazon.

articles.headline: Mouse model: co-infection with non-lethal influenza X31 protected against malarial acute lung injury and death by expanding Ly6C+ monocyte-derived alveolar macrophages that inhibited pathogenic CD8 T cells. Protection occurred independent of parasite burden and type I interferon signaling. Suggests virus-induced suppressive myeloid cells as potential adjunctive therapy for severe malaria-associated lung immunopathology
articles.title_original: Protective coinfection: influenza reprograms myeloid cells to limit CD8 T cell-mediated malaria pathology.

articles.headline: GWAS of 35 O'ahu 'amakihi (Hawaiian honeycreeper) identified 582 SNPs associated with P. relictum infection. Annotations included genes regulating intracellular calcium flux, transcription, apoptosis, T-cell/complement activation, and autophagy. Host resistance to avian malaria involves restricting intracellular components essential for parasite replication, extending beyond adaptive immunity mechanisms
articles.title_original: Evolution of Resistance to Avian Malaria Infection in an Endemic Hawaiian Honeycreeper.

articles.headline: A malaria vaccine reduced child deaths by 13% in African populations according to WHO reporting
articles.title_original: Malaria vaccine reduces child deaths by 13% in Africa, WHO study reports.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling projects that RTS,S/AS01 childhood vaccination at DTP3-equivalent coverage combined with long-lasting insecticidal nets would reduce malaria clinical cases and deaths by 40% in Beninese children under five years old from 2025-2034 compared to nets alone; however, a 1.5-fold decrease in net utilization could offset these gains
articles.title_original: Estimating the impact of the RTS,S/AS01 malaria vaccine in Benin: A mathematical modelling study.

articles.headline: During COVID-19, an international non-governmental organization maintained malaria services through a coordinated response including a dedicated taskforce, four-phase outbreak management plan, and context-adapted protocols. Reflections from staff decision-makers provide practical insights for future operational challenges
articles.title_original: Delivering malaria services during the COVID-19 pandemic: challenges and lessons from an international non-governmental organisation.

articles.headline: Political economy analysis of Nigeria's malaria program identified that slow progress toward elimination is driven by low government prioritization, weak citizen advocacy, poor inter-partner coordination, and absent communication with decision-makers. Advancing elimination requires sustained political will with predictable financing, citizen demand, better program coordination, and integration into national development priorities
articles.title_original: Accelerating Nigeria towards malaria elimination requires moving away from business as usual: insights from a political economy analysis.

articles.headline: Molecular analysis of 100 P. falciparum isolates from Mozambican pregnant women found 54% harbored quintuple and 7% sextuple pfdhfr/pfdhps sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance mutations. Gametocytes were detected in 34% of isolates and significantly associated with quintuple mutations (AOR=7.5, p=0.001), accounting for 80% of gametocyte-positive infections, indicating ongoing surveillance is needed to guide future intermittent preventive treatment strategies
articles.title_original: Prevalence of pfdhfr-pfdhps Sextuple and Gametocyte-Associated Quintuple Sulfadoxine-Pyrimethamine Resistance Mutations in Plasmodium falciparum Isolates from Pregnant Women in Mozambique.

articles.headline: A cross-sectional survey of 297 children aged 18 months to 6 years in malaria-endemic Bioko Island found only 29% fully immunized per national schedule. Younger children (18 months to 2 years) had higher completion than older children (45% vs 22%, aOR=0.42), suggesting improved recent program performance, though coverage remained below the 80% national target
articles.title_original: Immunization Coverage and Associated Factors Among Children Aged 18 to 72 Months in Communities With High Malaria Transmission, Bioko Island.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 96 studies found continental variation in artemisinin resistance: southeast Asian kelch13 mutations produced prolonged parasite clearance half-life with strong concordance to ring-stage survival, while east African kelch13 mutations showed faster clearance despite elevated ring-stage survival. This discordance risks under-detecting resistance in Africa; region-specific surveillance integrating multiple molecular and phenotypic markers is urgently needed
articles.title_original: Artemisinin partial resistance at a crossroads: evidence for continental variation in Plasmodium falciparumclearance phenotypes.

articles.headline: Ongoing open-label multicentric randomized controlled trial (target n=496) at four Indian sites compares low-dose primaquine 0.25 mg/kg day 1 versus standard-dose 0.75 mg/kg day 2 for gametocytocidal efficacy and safety in adults with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria. Primary outcomes include gametocyte density and prevalence by day 14 (blood smear and Pfs25 mRNA) and hemoglobin concentration through day 28; enrollment began August 2025
articles.title_original: Protocol for a multicentric open-label randomized controlled trial comparing low-dose (0.25 mg/kg) and standard-dose (0.75 mg/kg) primaquine for gametocytocidal efficacy and safety in Plasmodium falciparum malaria.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 10,228 individuals across five rural Tanzanian regions found 77.6% ITN ownership and 77.2% use with significant district variation. Females had higher odds than males (aOR=1.27, p<0.001), under-fives had higher odds than adults (aOR=2.26 for use, p<0.001), and higher education and socioeconomic status independently predicted increased ownership and use, though coverage remained below the 80% national target with persistent sociodemographic disparities
articles.title_original: Socio-demographic predictors of insecticide-treated bed net ownership and utilization for protection against malaria by rural community members across five regions of Mainland Tanzania.

articles.headline: Screening of a Streptomyces culture library identified two actinomycin derivatives with improved selectivity: actinomycin X2 (IC50=0.241 nM, selectivity index 27.9) and actinomycin X0ß (IC50=7.69 nM, selectivity index 106), exhibiting 2- and 7-fold greater selectivity, respectively, compared to commercial actinomycin D (selectivity index 14.3), indicating these derivatives are promising antimalarial candidates
articles.title_original: Discovery of actinomycin derivatives with improved selectivity against malaria parasites from a Streptomyces culture library.

articles.headline: Mechanistic studies show P. falciparum PfCoronin mutations disrupt the protein's membrane localization and reduce hemoglobin uptake by ring-stage parasites, limiting heme-dependent artemisinin activation. Similar to Pfkelch13-mediated resistance, PfCoronin mutations contribute to artemisinin resistance through altered endocytic access to hemoglobin and reduced drug activation without direct Pfkelch13 interaction
articles.title_original: Cellular and molecular basis of PfCoronin function in artemisinin resistance in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: A 1-month longitudinal study of 134 Cameroonian primary school children with 85.8% asymptomatic P. falciparum infection and 87.4% soil-transmitted helminth seropositivity found no significant difference in STH exposure between asymptomatic and symptomatic malaria cases (85.7% vs 90.9%, P>0.05). No correlation between anti-STH antibody levels and parasite burden was observed, indicating STH exposure is not a major contributor to persistent asymptomatic P. falciparum carriage
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic Plasmodium falciparum Infection Was Not Associated with Exposure to Soil-Transmitted Helminths in Children in Esse, Cameroon.

articles.headline: This is a correction notice to a previous article (DOI: 10.1371/journal.pgph.0006286) on malaria elimination in the Thai-Myanmar border region; no substantive research findings to report
articles.title_original: Correction: Challenges and responses of malaria elimination in a high-endemic area along the Thai-Myanmar border: A health systems perspective.

articles.headline: Review article discussing dCas9-SSAP as a cleavage-free CRISPR-based genome editing tool for Plasmodium parasites, contrasting it with traditional nuclease-based CRISPR-Cas9 systems that cause double-strand breaks and genotoxic scarring in parasite genomes
articles.title_original: The dCas9-SSAP as a promising genome editing tool in malaria parasites.

articles.headline: In mice immunized with P. falciparum-infected or uninfected red blood cells, polyclonal sera showed specific binding to Pf-iRBC surface antigens and significant parasite-killing activity in growth inhibition assays; phage display screening identified both known and novel Pf-iRBC surface antigens as potential blood-stage vaccine candidates
articles.title_original: Identification of Novel Malaria Antigens Expressed on the Surface of RBCs Infected with Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Case report of a 40-year-old woman from Seychelles presenting with severe P. falciparum malaria complicated by thrombosis, hyperparasitemia, acute kidney injury, and metabolic acidosis; genetic testing identified ACE I/D polymorphism along with thrombophilic variants, suggesting a potential role in malaria severity
articles.title_original: Potential Role of ACE Polymorphisms in Severe Malaria: A Case Report.

articles.headline: Three long-acting ivermectin formulations (LAIFs) were tested in cattle using Anopheles feeding assays; mdc-STM-001 achieved peak concentration 34.5 ng/ml with 12% variability, hazard ratio >4 and >50% mortality against both susceptible and resistant A. gambiae for ≥60 days, and maintained 10-day LC50 for resistant mosquitoes ≥126 days, meeting WHO PPC for malaria vector control
articles.title_original: Selection of the optimal long-acting injectable formulation of ivermectin for use in humans to target malaria vectors in Western Africa: evaluation of pharmacokinetics and mosquitocidal efficacy in cattle under laboratory conditions.

articles.headline: No abstract provided; title indicates a 23-year longitudinal analysis of climatic factors and vector control's influence on malaria incidence in Southeast Iran
articles.title_original: The influence of climatic factors and vector control on malaria incidence in Southeast Iran: a 23-year longitudinal analysis.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 379 adults in a remote DRC village found malaria prevalence 51.7% (95%CI 46.7–56.7) and arboviral seroprevalence 78.4% (dominated by O'nyong-nyong 42.8%, Rift Valley fever 32.0%, chikungunya 23.4%), with concurrent malaria and arboviral exposure in 40.4% of participants, indicating urgent need for integrated surveillance
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Malaria and Arbovirus Co-Exposure in the Boende Health Zone, Northwestern Democratic Republic of the Congo.

articles.headline: Evaluation of 440 long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs) 18 months post-distribution in a Cameroon refugee camp found 60.7% in good condition, 14.5% acceptable, and 24.8% deteriorated; all 30 nets tested showed inefficacy against field A. gambiae s.l. with only 1.5% knock-down and 3.1% mortality, with A. gambiae and A. coluzzii comprising 86.9% and 13.1% of vectors respectively
articles.title_original: Physical integrity and bioefficacy of long-lasting insecticidal nets 18 months after use among Central African Republic refugees in Gado-Badzere, a high malaria burden camp in Cameroon.

articles.headline: National survey of 4,850 febrile patients in Madagascar (January–May 2024) identified 1,944 P. falciparum infections; of 963 pfkelch13 sequences obtained, 91.9% were wild-type 3D7, with 1.8% non-synonymous mutations, 6.2% synonymous mutations, and no WHO-validated artemisinin partial resistance markers detected, indicating no established resistance emergence
articles.title_original: Updated mapping of Plasmodium falciparum kelch13 gene polymorphisms in Madagascar, 2024.

articles.headline: In mice co-infected with P. berghei XAT and B. rodhaini, prior P. berghei infection conferred cross-protection against B. rodhaini by reducing parasitemia and splenic damage through macrophage-driven innate immunity (IFN-γ, IL-12p70, TNF-α with balancing IL-10), independent of adaptive immunity and NK cells, demonstrating macrophage-targeted immunomodulation as a strategy for multivalent vaccines
articles.title_original: Immunity beyond borders: non-lethal Plasmodium confers cross-protection against lethal Babesia via macrophage activation.

articles.headline: Three computationally designed Plasmodium chabaudi-derived peptides (PcDBS1R1, PcDBS1R5, PcDBS1R9) showed no cytotoxicity to murine macrophages; PcDBS1R5 and PcDBS1R9 displayed antibacterial activity against A. baumannii, PcDBS1R1 showed antibiofilm effects, and all significantly reduced nitric oxide production in lipopolysaccharide-stimulated macrophages, indicating potential as scaffolds for peptide-based treatments
articles.title_original: Computer-Aided Design of Plasmodium chabaudi-Derived Peptides with Dual Antibiofilm and Anti-inflammatory Activities.

articles.headline: In pregnant women receiving intermittent preventive treatment, sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP) produced 126g higher birthweight than dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (95%CI 22.6–229.3g; p=0.019), with gut microbiome remodeling accounting for ~45% of SP's gestational weight gain advantage over DP; SP suppressed pathobionts and enriched anaerobic commensals, suggesting antibiotic-independent mechanism distinct from conventional antibiotic dysbiosis
articles.title_original: Beyond malaria prevention: sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine treatment in pregnancy selectively remodels the maternal gut microbiome to increase gestational weight gain and improve birthweight.

articles.headline: Quantized Vision Transformers (Swin-Tiny) achieve 3.9× compression with 0.11% accuracy loss on malaria microscopy images but show critical vulnerability: zero defense against white-box iterative PGD attacks (0% accuracy) despite maintaining 81% accuracy against black-box transfer attacks, exposing a security gap in edge-deployed diagnostic AI for resource-constrained settings
articles.title_original: Evaluating the Adversarial Robustness and Clinical Safety of Quantized Hierarchical Transformers for Edge-Based Malaria Microscopy.
articles.authors: Hasan U, Alghamdi TG, Nayeem MA

articles.headline: In silico screening of Streptomyces hygroscopicus fractions identified 44, 24, and 18 compounds from three fractions; erucamide and dibenzylamine showed higher binding affinity to Plasmodium DXR (a target in the MEP pathway) than the native ligand, with erucamide-DXR complex more stable over 100 ns molecular dynamics simulation, though in vitro and in vivo validation is required to confirm antimalarial efficacy
articles.title_original: Antimalarial Candidate Study from Three Different Fractions of Streptomyces hygroscopicus subsp. Hygroscopicus targeting Plasmodium DXR: An In Silico Approach.

articles.headline: In 7,124 Ugandan women (2018–2019 UMIS), knowledge of malaria prevention varied markedly: 76.5% knew bed nets versus 9.1% ITNs, 6.2% preventive medicine, and 4.3% IRS. Cumulative multi-channel media exposure associated with ITN knowledge (p<0.001), preventive medicine (p=0.002), and IRS (p<0.001); secondary/higher education doubled likelihood of exposure to multiple channels (IRR 1.86, 95% CI 1.59–2.17) compared to uneducated women
articles.title_original: Effect of cumulative exposure to media channels for malaria messages on knowledge of malaria prevention among women (15-49 years) in Uganda.

articles.headline: Methylene blue suppressed ring-stage Plasmodium falciparum at low nanomolar concentrations with no kelch13-mediated cross-resistance: IC50 23 nM (wild type), 27 nM (R561H), and 14 nM (P441L), indicating methylene blue retains activity against early-ring artemisinin-resistant parasites and may be a viable option where artemisinin resistance is established
articles.title_original: Methylene blue retains in vitro activity against early ring-stage artemisinin-resistant Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 16 studies (2005–2025, 5,753 Sudanese participants) found P. vivax infection in Duffy-negative individuals at 11.7% prevalence (95% CI 7.2–17.3%; I²=56%), with substantial PvDBP genetic diversity including multiple haplotypes and gene duplications, challenging the historical assumption of complete Duffy-mediated protection and suggesting adaptive mechanisms enabling infection despite Duffy negativity
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax infection in Duffy-negative populations in Sudan: a systematic review and meta-analysis of host-parasite genetic adaptation.

articles.headline: In southwest Spain, environmental factors differently shaped avian Plasmodium communities in two Culex species: in Cx. pipiens, prevalence increased with NDVI while lineage richness declined with land surface temperature; in Cx. perexiguus, both prevalence and richness increased with minimum temperature but diversity declined with land surface temperature, indicating vector identity influences parasite community structure
articles.title_original: Environmental factors affect the communities of avian Plasmodium in two species of Culex mosquitoes.

articles.headline: In a controlled human malaria infection study (n=86/142 with clinical immunity), antibodies against merozoite antigens MSP1, MSP11, RAMA, MSP7, PF3D7_1401600 (PHISTB), and PTEX150—identified via KILchip screening of 70 recombinant antigens—were consistently associated with clinical immunity and in combination predicted complete protection, identifying priority vaccine candidates
articles.title_original: Controlled human malaria infection in adults identify combinations of merozoite antigens associated with clinical immunity.

articles.headline: In 142 Yemeni IBS patients, malaria co-infection prevalence was 45.1% (64/142); malaria-positive patients had significantly higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (NLR: +0.56, p<0.001) and platelet-to-lymphocyte ratio (PLR: +22.6, p<0.001) and lower mean platelet volume (−0.60 fL, p<0.001), with elevated NLR (adjusted OR 3.21) and PLR (adjusted OR 4.02 per 10-unit increase) independently predicting malaria positivity
articles.title_original: Systemic inflammation and malaria co-infection in irritable bowel syndrome: a cross-sectional study of 142 Yemeni patients.

articles.headline: In Ugandan children, CXCR6+CD127− Tr1 cells comprise ~90% of Pf antigen-specific CD4+ T cell pool and potently suppress proliferation via IL-10 secretion; higher Tr1 frequencies correlated with lower symptom probability given parasitemia but delayed parasite clearance, suggesting these cells mediate clinical immunity while facilitating parasite persistence through immune regulation
articles.title_original: CXCR6+CD127- Tr1 cells balance immunity and persistence in Plasmodium falciparum infection.

articles.headline: Whole-genome sequencing of 77 P. malariae isolates (Cameroon, DRC, Nigeria, Tanzania; 2015–2021) revealed no geographic population structure, significantly lower nucleotide diversity than co-localized P. falciparum, and higher linkage disequilibrium, with evidence of selective sweeps at 4 erythrocyte invasion ligands and 6 resistance orthologs, indicating P. malariae is genomically atypical and recovering from a bottleneck
articles.title_original: Population genomics of Plasmodium malariae from four African countries.

articles.headline: Quasi-experimental study comparing West Nile (IRS + ITNs) to Acholi (ITNs only, control) over 4 years found IRS with clothianidin-deltamethrin (IRS-1) reduced incidence by 15% versus control (not significant: aIRR 0.85, 95% CI 0.69–1.04), but subsequent pirimiphos-methyl IRS (IRS-2) achieved 73% incidence reduction (aIRR 0.27, 95% CI 0.22–0.34, p<0.001), highlighting insecticide selection importance in high-pyrethroid-resistance contexts
articles.title_original: Impact of indoor residual spraying and insecticide-treated nets on malaria burden in 8 districts in West Nile and Acholi regions, Uganda: a quasi-experimental study.

articles.headline: Structure-based optimization of hydroxypyrazole hit compounds against P. falciparum DHÖDH yielded derivatives (compounds 6, 7, 12, 13, 22) with enzymatic IC50 1.3–5.9 μM, selectivity >30 over human ortholog, EC50 15–22 μM against P. falciparum 3D7 cultures, and CC50 >150 μM in mammalian cells, providing structure-activity relationship guidance for next-generation DHÖDH inhibitors despite poor overall antiplasmodial activity
articles.title_original: Structure-based design of new pyrazole inhibitors targeting Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (PfDHODH).

articles.headline: AgMOBP1 (Anopheles gambiae Midgut Ookinete Binding Protein 1), a mosquito midgut epithelial factor induced by blood feeding, binds P. falciparum syntaxin-5-like protein PfSyn5 to promote oocyst burden; blocking this interaction with anti-PfSyn5 antibodies achieves complete transmission inhibition at 1 μg/mL with IC50 ~3 ng/mL (~20 pM)—approximately one million-fold more potent than total plasma IgG—establishing AgMOBP1-PfSyn5 as a transmission-blocking vaccine and antibody target
articles.title_original: Disruption of the AgMOBP1-PfSyn5 interface abolishes malaria transmission.

articles.headline: During Plasmodium male gamete formation, Calcifer is a late-recruited basal body stabilizer that protects the structurally simplified basal body from mechanical stress generated by axoneme release, representing an evolutionary solution to a severe biomechanical challenge
articles.title_original: Organelle mechanics: A brace for the Plasmodium basal body.

articles.headline: Analysis of 252,610 blood films from Bishoftu Town Health Office (2011–2022) revealed 5,696 (2.3%) malaria cases: P. vivax 63.8%, P. falciparum 31.0%; peak incidence 804 cases in 2017 with individuals >14 years comprising 66.3% of cases, indicating persistent malaria with fluctuating transmission trend despite being 40 km from malaria-free Addis Ababa, raising spillover concerns
articles.title_original: The persistent threat of malaria in Bishoftu Town: twelve-year trends and their implications for Addis Ababa.

articles.headline: Serendipitous detection of invasive Anopheles stephensi in Kisumu, Kenya (June 2022) identified from 19.6% (11/56) of ASVs at an urban slaughterhouse, with genetic analysis confirming an. stephensi haplotypes clustering with samples from northern Kenya, Ethiopia, and Sudan—the first report south of the equator, signaling expanded geographic range of this invasive urban malaria vector
articles.title_original: Serendipitous detection of invasive malaria vector Anopheles stephensi in Kisumu, Kenya in June 2022.

articles.headline: Dose-fractionation study of anticancer kinase inhibitor berzosertib in P. falciparum-infected humanized mice, with pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic modeling predicting that six daily 75 mg intravenous doses will achieve sustained concentrations above minimum parasiticidal concentration (MPC) for uncomplicated blood-stage malaria—substantially lower than the 440 mg twice-weekly cancer dose—though further preclinical development needed
articles.title_original: Repurposing Drugs for Malaria through a Human Dose Prediction: A Case Study with Berzosertib.

articles.headline: Computational screening of 216 antibiotics identified PfRAD5 binders (talampicillin, dicloxacillin, raltegravir) and PfWD11 binders (cervinomycin A2, puromycin); in vitro testing showed puromycin 85% inhibition at 100 μg/mL, triple combination (puromycin + raltegravir + dicloxacillin) achieved complete inhibition, and puromycin + chloroquine exhibited synergistic effects, establishing PfRAD5 and PfWD11 as novel antimalarial targets relevant to artemisinin resistance
articles.title_original: Repositioning Antibiotics Against Plasmodium falciparum RAD5 and WD11 as Novel Antimalarial Targets.
articles.authors: Albaqami AS, Oyewole OA, Adam KM

articles.headline: Plasmodium protein Calcifer, essential for male gametogenesis, translocates to the basal body after axoneme assembly and forms a wrapping stabilization structure at the axoneme minus end; parasites lacking Calcifer form intact axonemes but exhibit rapid disintegration during exflagellation, a defect rescued by inhibiting axoneme beating, revealing a mechanical stabilization mechanism protecting basal body integrity during transmission
articles.title_original: Mechanoregulation of basal body integrity during Plasmodium exflagellation.

articles.headline: Analysis of 41,863 blood tests from District Malakand, Pakistan (2011–2017) yielded 6,246 positive cases (14.92% test positivity rate); annual positivity declined from 32.97% (2011) to 10.77% (2017), with autumn peak (46.73%) and winter nadir (7.09%), predominantly affecting males (58.89%) and individuals aged 1–20 years, with spatial hotspots identified in Gharhi, Dargai, and Haryankot
articles.title_original: Spatial Distribution, seasonal dynamics and molecular confirmation of malaria in District Malakand, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: Artemisinin partial resistance (ART-R) detected via molecular surveillance in eastern Africa, Horn of Africa, and southern Africa, confirmed in Rwanda, Uganda, Eritrea, and Tanzania with additional signals in neighboring countries, threatening artemisinin-based combination therapy (ACT) efficacy that has been malaria treatment backbone for >20 years across Africa
articles.title_original: Africa cannot afford to wait for malaria treatment failure.

articles.headline: Retrospective cross-sectional analysis of 35,934 confirmed malaria cases (2019–2021) in Kolondieba district, Mali revealed distinct hotspot dynamics: Kadiana (high-risk area) maintained stable persistent clusters (RR 1.8–6.3) across all eight periods, while Kolondieba Central (moderate-risk) expanded from localized to extensive clusters (RR 1.7–2.2), with Kolondieba town persistent hotspot, demonstrating fine-scale transmission heterogeneity even within moderate-risk areas
articles.title_original: Analysis of clinical malaria hotspots according to transmission levels in two health areas of the Kolondieba health district, Mali.

articles.headline: Screening 233 mosquito pools (6,170 females from seven species) across Illinois identified 44 (18.9%) positive for avian haemosporidians; eleven lineages detected (nine Plasmodium, two Haemoproteus); Culex restuans harbored highest lineage diversity and infection rate, with pTUMIG03 (P. unalis) most frequent and pSYAT05 (P. vaughani) showing broadest distribution, establishing Cx. restuans as primary vector for local avian Plasmodium enzootic circulation
articles.title_original: Heterogeneous distribution of avian malaria parasites across mosquito species: disproportionate involvement of Culex restuans in the Midwestern United States.

articles.headline: In silico systematic approach identifying hundreds of putative cis-regulatory elements (CREs) correlated with A. gambiae germline gene expression, linked to specific sperm developmental stages and chromosomal locations suggesting roles in dosage compensation and meiotic silencing; in vivo dual-reporter validation of top-ranked testis-enriched CRE confirmed that conserved nucleotide mutations drastically alter male germline expression, providing first nucleotide-resolution regulatory genome annotation of A. gambiae germline for gene-drive promoter design
articles.title_original: Decoding cis-regulatory elements in the germline of the human malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.

articles.headline: Mixed-methods analysis of DHIS2 surveillance data (Jan 2020–Mar 2025) from 17 Sudanese states showed missing malaria reports increased substantially after April 2023, with significant inter-state variation. Interrupted time series analysis of 11 states found statistically significant decline in quarterly reported malaria case rates per 100,000 population at conflict onset (p=0.02), with no significant post-conflict trend. Health facility destruction, workforce shortages, and communication breakdowns were identified as major barriers
articles.title_original: Impact of the 2023-armed conflict on routine malaria imapact indicators in Sudan (January 2020-March 2025): a mixed-methods analysis of surveillance data.

articles.headline: Suppressive test in P. berghei-infected mice evaluated alpha-mangostin chitosan alginate nanoparticles (ACAN) combined with chloroquine at three weight ratios (189:1, 63:1, 567:1). All combinations demonstrated synergistic activity with ∑FED50 values of 0.069, 0.113, and 0.414 (all <1, indicating synergy), with ratio-1 achieving ED50 of 9.196 mg/kg and strongest synergistic antimalarial effect
articles.title_original: Synergistic Antimalarial Activity of Alpha-Mangostin Chitosan Alginate Nanoparticles with Chloroquine in Mice Infected with Plasmodium berghei: The Potential of a New Antimalarial Drug Combination.

articles.headline: Topographic analysis across Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province, Pakistan (2019–2022) found malaria transmission varied inversely with altitude: low-altitude regions showed high transmission rates while high-altitude regions experienced low transmission, suggesting topography should inform targeted malaria control strategies in the region
articles.title_original: A Topographic Analysis of Malaria Transmission - Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan, 2019-2022.

articles.headline: World Malaria Day 2026 campaign theme: 'Driven to End Malaria
articles.title_original: World Malaria Day 2026: Driven to End Malaria.

articles.headline: Clinical review: cerebral malaria occurs when parasite-infected red blood cells adhere to endothelial cells lining brain microvasculature, obstructing vascular lumens, though detailed pathophysiology remains unclear. Intravenous artesunate should be initiated immediately upon clinical suspicion of cerebral malaria with impaired consciousness
articles.title_original: [Cerebral Malaria].

articles.headline: Serological study of P. falciparum-exposed individuals found B cell responses dominated by IgM targeting the N-terminal intrinsically disordered region (IDR1) of asparagine-rich merozoite antigen (PfARMA); monoclonal antibodies to IDR1 showed non-neutralizing activity. Plasma IgM and IgG responses predominantly targeted IDR1 and increased with P. falciparum exposure, but notably did not correlate with protection from malaria in age- and exposure-matched children, suggesting these responses reflect recent exposure rather than functional immunity
articles.title_original: The N-terminal region of malaria vaccine candidate asparagine-rich merozoite antigen is immunodominant and targeted by polyreactive antibodies.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 35 studies (27 bird species, 85 effect sizes) found no significant association between individual genetic diversity and avian malaria infection prevalence or intensity across Plasmodium, Haemoproteus, and Leucocytozoon. Parasite genus, infection assessment method, host migratory behavior, latitude, and MHC parameters did not significantly modify this relationship, suggesting general genetic diversity measures may not reliably predict infection outcomes in wild bird populations
articles.title_original: Avian Malaria Infections Are Not Associated With Host Genetic Diversity: A Meta-Analysis.
articles.authors: Podmokła E, Pluciński B, Dubiec A

articles.headline: Review of digital molecular diagnostic tools' role in malaria detection and control strategies
articles.title_original: How can the use of digital molecular diagnostic tools help to tackle malaria?

articles.headline: Molecular epidemiology study of 286 samples from two Cameroon health facilities detected P. falciparum in 87 (30.41%), with A581G Pfdhps mutant allele frequency of 67.60% and A437G frequency of 45.07%, compared to wild-type frequencies of 22.53% (A581G) and 48.87% (A437G). High frequency of dihydropteroate synthase mutations indicates substantial sulfadoxine resistance pressure in the region, though pyrimethamine resistance assessment was not conducted
articles.title_original: Polymorphisms of the plasmodium falciparum dihydropteroate synthase gene among patients attending LEPI and ADLUCEM hospitals in the west region of Cameroon.

articles.headline: Molecular survey of 141 Indigenous community members and 341 nonhuman primates (10 species) in Peruvian Amazon (2007–2020) detected Plasmodium in 43.3% of humans and 51.9% of NHPs, with P. vivax/simium predominating in humans (42.1%) and P. brasilianum/malariae in NHPs (24.6%). Phylogenetic analysis revealed >99.9% identity between human and NHP lineages, supporting shared parasite lineages, while one-third of human infections were asymptomatic
articles.title_original: Zoonotic and Anthroponotic Plasmodium spp. Circulation between Wild Primates and Indigenous Community, Peruvian Amazon, 2007-2020.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling using wavelet spectral analysis of weekly hospital admission data from five Limpopo Province districts (South Africa) identified meteorological drivers and critical thresholds of temperature, rainfall, and evapotranspiration for malaria spread. Disease projections were generated for 2021–2050 and 2051–2080 under Representative Concentration Pathways (RCP2.6 and RCP8.5) scenarios to assess climate change impacts
articles.title_original: Understanding and modeling meteorological drivers of the incidence and spread of malaria in South Africa.

articles.headline: Mathematical model compared oral ivermectin regimens (3×300 μg/kg or 1×400 μg/kg monthly for 3–4 months) with long-acting injectable BEPO formulation (0.6–1.0 mg/kg) in populations with 50–90% coverage. Oral regimens reduced clinical cases by 7–38% depending on regimen and transmission setting; single long-acting injectable campaign achieved 16–29% reduction, rising to 33–57%, 48–74%, and 59–78% with two, three, and four campaigns respectively
articles.title_original: Modelling the impact of ivermectin-based optimal strategies on malaria control: the role of formulation, coverage, and mosquitocidal efficacy timing.

articles.headline: Examines state-of-the-art pharmacotherapy approaches for treating malaria in infants
articles.title_original: The impact of state-of-the-art pharmacotherapy for malaria in infants: a breakthrough?

articles.headline: Cross-sectional PCR screening of 713 poultry samples in Malaysia identified avian Plasmodium gallinaceum, P. parasitese, and Haemoproteus species; PCR detected Plasmodium at 30.6% and Haemoproteus at 10.7%, substantially higher than microscopy (4.6% and 1.1%), revealing high parasite diversity in domestic and wild poultry
articles.title_original: Molecular detection and genetic diversity of avian haemosporidia (Plasmodium and Haemoproteus) infecting red jungle fowls (Gallus gallus spadiceus) and domestic poultry in peninsular, Malaysia.

articles.headline: First documentation of Plasmodium huffi in New World vultures (black vulture Coragyps atratus, n=29) and yellow-headed caracaras (Daptrius chimachima, n=6) in Brazil; additional infections in red-legged seriemas (n=14) confirm broad host tropism with high phenotypic plasticity and gametocytes present for transmission potential
articles.title_original: No host too tough: New records of Plasmodium huffi in competent avian hosts, including Brazilian vultures and falcons.

articles.headline: Systematic review of agricultural insecticides (neonicotinoids, pyrroles, broflanilide, meta-diamides, ivermectin, methoxyfenozide, halofenozide) against malaria vectors; some are incorporated in WHO-endorsed tools (insecticide-treated nets, indoor residual sprays, larvicides) while others show promise in trials with dual action against vector survival and Plasmodium development
articles.title_original: Malaria vector control: Can repurposed agricultural insecticides offer solutions to resistance issues?

articles.headline: Discusses development of a new class of malaria vaccines by Eizo Takashima
articles.title_original: Eizo Takashima-developing a new class of malaria vaccines.

articles.headline: Among 742 COVID-19 patients in Kenya and Burkina Faso, 151 (20%) had P. falciparum co-infection treated with artemether-lumefantrine or pyronaridine-artesunate; co-infection did not increase symptom duration (median 9 vs 10 days, aHR 1.14, p=0.26) or mortality (1% both), though SARS-CoV-2 clearance was slower at day 7 (aHR 0.69, p=0.017); prior malaria exposure modified outcomes with faster clearance
articles.title_original: Uncomplicated malaria as a risk factor for COVID-19 duration and severity in western Kenya and Burkina Faso (MALCOV): a prospective cohort study.

articles.headline: Explores the relationship between malaria and COVID-19 co-infection
articles.title_original: The fascinating malaria-COVID-19 relationship.

articles.headline: Analysis of 2,270 genome-sequenced P. falciparum from 283 malaria posts in Myanmar (2015-2020) during intensive elimination efforts (97% transmission reduction) showed decreasing parasite effective population size and minimal artemisinin resistance changes until 2020, when kelch13-R561H became sole predominant genotype; mass drug administration caused founder effects demonstrating genomic evidence of intervention efficacy
articles.title_original: Impact of intensive control on malaria population genomics under elimination settings in Southeast Asia.

articles.headline: Integrative structural analysis combining cross-linking mass spectrometry, cofractionation, and AlphaFold modeling identified host-parasite and pathogen-pathogen membrane protein complexes in P. falciparum gametocytes, revealing potential targets for transmission-blocking interventions
articles.title_original: Deciphering Membrane Protein Complexes in Plasmodium falciparum Gametocytes via Integrative Structural Systems Biology.

articles.headline: Perspective arguing quantitative proteomics is core to malaria systems biology, enabling investigation of protein abundance, modifications, and interactions in host and parasite to illuminate mechanisms of cytoadhesion, endothelial dysfunction, immune modulation, and therapeutic variability inaccessible through genomic approaches
articles.title_original: Integrative proteomics to illuminate host-parasite interactions in malaria.

articles.headline: Among 485 mining workers surveyed in Haut-Katanga Province, DRC, 365 (75.3%) had adequate malaria knowledge but significant knowledge-practice gaps existed; middle school education (aOR 0.37, p<0.01) and early/evening shifts (aOR 0.46, p=0.02) reduced knowledge, while 99.3% reported using long-sleeved clothing and 91.1% reported insecticide-treated nets, indicating need for sustained adoption strategies
articles.title_original: Knowledge, attitudes and practices of malaria control and prevention in a high-exposure occupational group: A cross-sectional survey of mining workers in Haut-Katanga Province in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

articles.headline: Qualitative study of 21 Sudanese migrants (90.5% female) in Egypt found 90.5% initially used traditional remedies or self-medication without testing despite recognizing malaria severity; barriers included lack of trust in healthcare, stigma, quarantine fears, and drug concerns, while systemic factors encompassed affordability, accessibility, and quality—indicating need for targeted health education and system improvements
articles.title_original: Sudanese migrants' pathways to malaria care in Egypt: An in-depth qualitative study.

articles.headline: Using 2021 GBD data (1990–2021), this analysis assessed malaria burden attributable to growth failure in children <5 years. Globally, burden declined since 1990 (AAPC −1.26; 95% CI: −1.36 to −1.17) but increased after 2019 (APC 3.66; 95% CI: 2.38 to 4.80). Males and infants aged 6–11 months bore disproportionately high burden (ASMR 17.59 and 31.63 per 100,000 respectively), with West/Central Africa most affected (ASMR 88.09 per 100,000)
articles.title_original: The unequal malaria burden attributable to child growth failure: a global analysis among children under five from 1990 to 2021.

articles.headline: This study developed Pfs47-RTS,S-BP, a dual-antigen vaccine combining circumsporozoite protein-derived RTS,S antigen with sexual-stage Pfs47 subdomain in biopolymer particles via engineered E. coli. Pfs47-RTS,S-BP generated RTS,S-specific antibodies 5.3-fold higher than RTS,S/AS01 protective thresholds, achieved 80.4% protection against mosquito bite challenge, and demonstrated 68.15% transmission-reducing activity, though in vivo efficacy remains untested
articles.title_original: Robust Bioconjugated Antigens Induce Immune Responses Preventing Malaria Infection and its Transmission.

articles.headline: Structure-based design of hydroxamic acid inhibitors targeting P. falciparum aminopeptidase P (PfAPP), essential for hemoglobin digestion during erythrocytic stages. Optimized compound 6e achieved Ki=24 nM (versus apstatin Ki=16 μM), with crystal structures revealing P4′ phenylalanine as the selectivity driver via Tyr617 interaction, enabling potent and selective PfAPP inhibitor design
articles.title_original: Hydroxamate-based inhibitors reveal structural determinants of selectivity for Plasmodium falciparum aminopeptidase P.

articles.headline: Single-center retrospective study of 778 febrile travelers returning to Italy (2018–2024) tested for malaria. Dengue was the most common diagnosis (95 cases, 12.2%), followed by malaria (72 cases, 9.3%) and pneumonia (42 cases, 5.4%), with 57% (446/778) remaining undiagnosed overall; diagnostic capacity improved from 29.4% (2018) to 57.8% (2024)
