articles.title_original: High rate of undiagnosed cases of fever among returning travelers from tropical areas tested for malaria: a single-center retrospective study.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 13 cross-sectional studies comparing G6PD point-of-care diagnostic accuracy against spectrophotometry. CareStart G6PD at 30% threshold: sensitivity 0.82 (95% CI: 0.55–0.95), specificity 0.96 (95% CI: 0.93–0.98). STANDARD G6PD Biosensor demonstrated superior performance (sensitivity 0.99, specificity 0.98 at 30% threshold), supporting its use for guiding 8-aminoquinoline radical cure in malaria-endemic settings
articles.title_original: Diagnostic Accuracy of Point-of-Care Devices for Glucose-6-Phosphate Dehydrogenase Deficiency Detection Among Malaria Patients: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.

articles.headline: Brazilein, the oxidized form of brazilin from Caesalpinia sappan heartwood, inhibits P. falciparum by coordinately binding labile Fe²⁺ pools within the parasite and host red blood cells, blocking hemoglobin digestion and erythrocyte invasion while inducing pyknotic death. Real-time fluorescence tracking enabled monitoring of brazilin-brazilein transformation and anti-plasmodial mechanism
articles.title_original: Brazilin and its oxidized form "brazilein", the natural fluorescently traceable agents, toxify Plasmodium falciparum to pyknotic death via disrupting its iron homeostasis.

articles.headline: Among 151 febrile children in Libreville, Gabon, DENV RNA was detected in 18.5% (DENV-1 and DENV-3 confirmed; DENV-4 possible), malaria prevalence was 56.9%, and dengue-malaria co-infection occurred in 9.3%. Children aged 5–10 years showed higher odds of malaria (OR: 2.61; p=0.03) and co-infection (OR: 16.28; p=0.002)
articles.title_original: Molecular evidence of co-circulation of different dengue virus serotypes associated with malaria co-infection among febrile children in Libreville, Gabon.

articles.headline: Computational screening identified tetrandrine, a bis-benzyl-isoquinoline alkaloid, as targeting P. falciparum heme detoxification protein (HDP). Tetrandrine showed the highest docking affinity (−13.0 kJ/mol) among candidate compounds and demonstrated favorable thermodynamic binding (MM-PBSA: −30 kcal/mol) in 100 ns molecular dynamics simulations
articles.title_original: Atomistic simulations identify the Tetrandrine as potent anti-malarial drug candidate against Plasmodium falciparum targeting Heme Detoxification Protein (HDP).

articles.headline: Mass spectrometry analysis of urine from P. vivax-infected patients identified 252 human proteins present only in infected samples and 28 P. vivax proteins including rifin-like protein, single-stranded DNA-binding protein, and profilin, establishing potential biomarkers for non-invasive malaria detection
articles.title_original: Discovery of parasite proteins in the urine of vivax malaria patients through mass spectrometry.

articles.headline: Title-only summary: Examines reconsidering the biological identity and clinical interpretation of malaria-derived particles
articles.title_original: "What are we measuring?" reconsidering the biological identity and clinical interpretation of malaria-derived particles.

articles.headline: Six-month pragmatic trial in 24 Nigerian refugee camps enrolled 1,655 children aged 6–10 years (842 intervention, 813 control) to assess spatial repellent emanators. Emanators significantly reduced malaria incidence (IRR 0.713, 95% CI: 0.584–0.869; p=0.001) with 22.5% protective efficacy (HR 0.775; p=0.011), and reduced pyrethroid-resistant Anopheles gambiae s.l. density (IRR 0.0231; p<0.0001)
articles.title_original: The effectiveness of long-lasting spatial repellent emanators against malaria in humanitarian crisis settings in northern Nigeria: a two-arm pragmatic, open-label, controlled trial.

articles.headline: X-ray crystallography (3.1 Å) of P. berghei oocyst rupture protein (ORP1-ORP2) histone-fold domain heterodimer revealed a druggable dimerization interface. Yeast two-hybrid screening of cyclic peptide library identified six candidates that inhibit ORP1-ORP2 dimerization in vitro, targeting a critical transmission event in the Anopheles vector
articles.title_original: Targeting the histone-fold dimerization interface of oocyst rupture proteins from Plasmodium berghei for antimalarial inhibitor discovery.

articles.headline: Flow cytometry quantified malaria-derived particles (MDPs) per μL blood in P. falciparum and P. vivax infections, with MDP levels strongly correlating with infected RBC percentage (R²=0.94 for both species) and parasite density (P. falciparum R²=0.94, P. vivax R²=0.91). The method reliably distinguishes infected samples from controls (<1 MDP/μL) and shows potential for routine laboratory diagnosis and treatment monitoring
articles.title_original: A novel flow cytometry approach to quantify malaria-derived particles.

articles.headline: First clinical evidence that a multistage malaria vaccine targeting both pre-erythrocytic (PfCSP) and transmission stages (Pfs230, Pfs48/45) achieves meaningful efficacy
articles.title_original: First efficacy evidence for a multistage malaria vaccine targeting infection and transmission.

articles.headline: Phase 2 controlled human malaria infection trial in Mali (n=34, 18–50 years old) compared ProC6C-AlOH/Matrix-M (100 μg ProC6C + 50 μg adjuvant, three IM doses 4 weeks apart) vs. rabies vaccine control. ProC6C reduced P. falciparum parasitemia at day 28 (6/16 vs. 13/16, p=0.0022) with 76% vaccine efficacy by time-to-event (95% CI 36–91) and delayed parasitemia onset by 2 days (p=0.049). This is the first anti-PfCSP subunit vaccine demonstrating >50% efficacy against controlled challenge at 12 weeks post-vaccination
articles.title_original: Efficacy of ProC6C-AlOH/Matrix-M against Plasmodium falciparum infection and mosquito transmission: a phase 2, randomised, controlled human malaria infection study.

articles.headline: In vitro study of P. falciparum lsa3 knockout parasites reveals that merozoite dense granule protein LSA3 is essential for completing RBC invasion, with ΔLSA3 parasites showing 65% reduction in successful invasion and frequent accolé-form morphology (aberrant membrane-proximal positioning)
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Liver Stage Antigen 3 plays a role in completing the final stages of merozoite invasion of red blood cells.

articles.headline: Computational structural analysis of P. falciparum food vacuole protein PfHDDCP (HotDog domain) predicts two ligand-binding sites: canonical site 1 for Acetyl-CoA/Acyl Carrier Protein (suggesting lipid metabolism function), and allosteric site 2 for antimalarial compounds that may modulate substrate binding. Molecular dynamics show stable complexes and ligand-induced conformational changes, but findings remain predictive pending experimental validation
articles.title_original: Computational characterization of a HotDog domain-containing hypothetical protein from the food vacuole of Plasmodium falciparum reveals potential allosteric regulation by antimalarials.

articles.headline: Surveys of subclinical infections (n=1,241) and clinical cases (n=1,775) across Ethiopian highlands (altitude <1900–2900 m) found P. falciparum prevalence declining with altitude (67% at 1850 m to 16% at 2850 m) while P. vivax remained 10–34% without clear trend. Only 20.3% reported recent travel, which did not significantly reduce infection risk, suggesting sustained local transmission. LDH-based RDT detected ~2-fold more infections than HRP2, and sites up to 2500 m should be classified as endemic
articles.title_original: High-Altitude Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax Reservoirs in Ethiopia Are Not Linked to Recent Travel.

articles.headline: National genomic profiling of P. falciparum drug resistance markers in Zambian children from the 2018 malaria indicator survey
articles.title_original: National genomic profiling of Plasmodium falciparum antimalarial resistance in Zambian children participating in the 2018 malaria indicator survey.

articles.headline: Molecular characterization and transmission indices of malaria vectors in a rural Osun State, Nigeria community
articles.title_original: Molecular Characterization and transmission indices of malaria vector in a rural community in Osun State south-west Nigeria.

articles.headline: Mixed-methods implementation audit in 53 Ethiopian health facilities (38 public, 15 private) during Feb–Mar 2025 malaria resurgence found mean test-and-treat guideline fidelity of 64.3% (SD 12.1), with 40% achieving high fidelity (≥75%) and 13% below 50%. Public facilities exceeded private (67% vs. 63%, p=0.041), and higher fidelity correlated with provider responsiveness (β=3.4, p<0.001), facilitation strategies (β=2.8, p<0.001), and lower perceived complexity (β=2.1, p<0.001). Diagnostic/treatment deviations and supply constraints were primary barriers
articles.title_original: Implementation fidelity of Ethiopia's Malaria test-and-treat guideline amid a resurgence in Amhara Region: A mixed-methods study.

articles.headline: Entomological surveys (Mar 2022–Feb 2023) in five Burkina Faso villages near the Soum dam collected 11,378 Anopheles (An. gambiae s.l. 86.5%, An. funestus 10.6%; within complex: 91.5% An. coluzzii, 8.3% An. arabiensis). Vector density was highest near the dam (49.7% in Soum village) but declined with distance, while P. falciparum sporozoite prevalence showed inverse pattern (6.2% overall; Seguedin 9.5%, Soala 8.7%), indicating localized transmission dynamics unrelated to proximity to the dam
articles.title_original: Characteristics and dynamics of malaria vectors around the Soum dam in Nanoro Health District, Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: Commentary or perspective on an unconventional Plasmodium entry mechanism into host cells
articles.title_original: Plasmodium gets inside through the back door.

articles.headline: Case series of 6 active-duty personnel (mean age 23) with P. vivax relapse diagnosed 57 days (range 8–121) after exposure in the Korean Peninsula (Dagmar North, 2021), all with uncomplicated malaria (parasitemia <1%, median hemoglobin 12.1 g/dL, platelets 123×10³/μL). All cleared parasitemia within 2–4 days on artemether/lumefantrine plus primaquine without chemoprophylaxis. Findings highlight long latency of vivax illness onset in non-endemic settings and need for pre-deployment preventive measures
articles.title_original: To Break a Fever: Relapse of Long-Latency Plasmodium vivax Malaria After Service in the Korean Peninsula.

articles.headline: P. knowlesi in vitro adaptation to Duffy-negative erythrocytes revealed a stable chimeric dbpαγ gene formed by recombination, essential for Fy- invasion and resistant to α-DARC antibody inhibition. CRISPR-Cas9 reversion confirmed dbpαγ necessity. This demonstrates greater invasion plasticity than previously recognized, with implications for P. knowlesi spread beyond Southeast Asia
articles.title_original: Plasmodium knowlesi can adapt to infect Duffy-negative erythrocytes.

articles.headline: Review of compartmentalized heme metabolism in malaria-infected RBCs from nested sources: host erythrocyte biosynthesis, parasite synthesis, and hemoglobin digestion. Exogenous 5-ALA can increase intracellular protoporphyrin IX and sensitize parasites to oxidative stress, though susceptibility depends on parasite stage and exposure duration
articles.title_original: Russian Dolls of Heme Metabolism in Malaria-Infected Red Blood Cells: Nested Vulnerabilities and Therapeutic Opportunities.
articles.authors: Adapa SR, Siddiqui FA, Jiang RHY

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 7,199 P. falciparum samples across Tanzania found mean complexity of infection 1.5 with 59.6% monoclonal. Mean COI significantly higher in high/moderate vs. low/very low transmission strata (p<0.001); polyclonal infection odds significantly lower in moderate/low/very low strata (p<0.001). Genetic differentiation very low (FST 0-0.006), suggesting COI metrics useful for transmission surveillance
articles.title_original: Genetic metrics decode Plasmodium falciparum diversity: complexity of infection, parasite connectivity, and transmission intensity in Mainland Tanzania's diverse regions.

articles.headline: Case report: 46-year-old Taiwanese returning from Indonesia with fever and thrombocytopenia where automated hematology analyzer misclassified Plasmodium vivax as artefactual cells. Blood smear and PCR confirmed P. vivax, treated with artemether-lumefantrine and primaquine with rapid recovery
articles.title_original: Classification of Plasmodium vivax as artefactual cells by the CellaVision DM9600: a system limitation in a non-endemic area.

articles.headline: Swiss methodology for classifying malaria risk areas in travelers using current epidemiological data and harmonized preventive measures. Maps provide evidence-based guidance to travel medicine experts and primary care physicians for consistent prevention advice
articles.title_original: [Travel and malaria prevention : what lies behind the maps].

articles.headline: Among 294 PCR-negative Myanmar migrants in Thailand, 35.4% showed predicted recent P. vivax exposure by serology. Associated with age 30-39 (aOR=3.11), secondary+ education (aOR=3.28), Karen ethnicity (aOR=3.68); protected by seasonal work (aOR=0.28), temporary work (aOR=0.26), income >5,000 THB (aOR=0.17). Antibody levels increased with age (β=0.12/year, p<0.001), agricultural work (β=2.58, p=0.049); decreased with higher income (β=-3.83, p=0.002)
articles.title_original: Sociodemographic and behavioral determinants of Plasmodium vivax-specific antibody responses among short-term Myanmar migrants in Thailand.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 290 Ghanaian high school students found 23.1% malaria prevalence and 49.0% anemia. Day students had significantly higher malaria than boarders (59.2% vs. 15.8%, p<0.001); non-ITN users had higher infection (39.7% vs. 5.0%, p<0.001). Malaria-infected students had lower hemoglobin (11.50±1.43 vs. 12.56±1.35 g/dL, p<0.001); infection (aOR=9.36, p<0.001) and male sex (aOR=2.26, p=0.028) predicted anemia
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic malaria parasite carriage and anaemia among senior high school students in Tempane District of the Upper East Region of Ghana: a cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: In Uganda cohort of 640 participants, S. mansoni prevalence 49.1% associated with fecal calprotectin (detectable: OR 1.20; ≥100 µg/g: OR 1.11; >250 µg/g: OR 1.26; continuous β .06). HIV associated with fCal ≥100 µg/g (OR 2.52); malaria and hepatitis B uninformative. Findings indicate persistent gut inflammation in chronically infected populations despite praziquantel treatment
articles.title_original: Schistosoma mansoni Infections Are Associated With Fecal Calprotectin Markers of Gut Inflammation After Accounting for HIV, Hepatitis B, and Malaria.

articles.headline: Genetic crosses of fluorescently-labeled P. falciparum clones with genotyping of 8,540 oocysts from 435 mosquitoes (7-14 days post-infection) revealed decreasing proportions of outcrossed and increasing selfed oocysts over time. Results suggest faster maturation of outcrossed versus selfed oocysts, indicating fitness advantage of outcrossing likely due to removal of deleterious mutations
articles.title_original: Genetic crosses using fluorescently labelled malaria parasites indicate advantages of outcrossing in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Flow cytometry analysis of CD4 T cells in adults with P. falciparum or P. vivax showed comparable phenotype, activation, and proliferation between species. Within peripheral T follicular helper cells, P. falciparum showed increased pTfh1 and increased IL-10 production including IL-21-producing CD4 T cells, indicating species-dependent differences that may affect antibody development
articles.title_original: Comparison of CD4 T-Cell Response in Plasmodium falciparum and vivax Malaria.

articles.headline: In vitro study of neutrophil FcγRs using TNF/PIPLC treatment (79% reduction of FcγRIIIb, 82% increase of FcγRIIa) showed antibody-dependent neutrophil phagocytosis of P. falciparum-infected RBCs increased 585%±108% (p=0.042). FcγRIIa blocking reduced ADNP >5-fold (~17%, p<0.05); combined FcγRIIa/FcγRIII blocking achieved ~24% (p<0.05), indicating FcγRIIa is the main phagocytic receptor while FcγRIIIb acts as decoy
articles.title_original: Antibody-Dependent Neutrophil Phagocytosis of Plasmodium falciparum-Infected Erythrocytes Is Mediated by FcγRIIa.

articles.headline: Longitudinal cohort of 1,918 Ugandans over 14 months recorded 283 P. falciparum infections (244 symptomatic, 39 asymptomatic). Travel to high-incidence nearby areas associated with malaria in low-transmission villages (OR=4.38, 95% CI 1.80-10.64). Population attributable fractions showed overnight travel accounted for 14% of malaria cases overall, rising to 30% during low transmission periods
articles.title_original: Quantifying the Role of Importation on Sustained Malaria Transmission in a Low-to-Moderate Burden Region of Southwest Uganda.

articles.headline: In vitro study of Dobinin K against chloroquine-resistant P. falciparum Dd2 demonstrated concentration- and time-dependent antiplasmodial activity across all parasite developmental stages. Transcriptomic analysis revealed Dobinin K disrupts parasite redox homeostasis by increasing oxidative products, elevating GSSG/GSH ratio, inhibiting antioxidant enzymes (PfGR, PfTrxR, PfSOD), decreasing NADP+/NADPH ratio, disrupting mitochondrial membrane potential, and ultimately inducing apoptosis. Unlike artemisinin, this redox-targeting mechanism offers a novel strategy against chloroquine-resistant P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Antimalarial Effects of Dobinin K against Chloroquine-resistant Plasmodium falciparum Through Disruption of the Parasite's Redox System.

articles.headline: Automated LAMP assay for P. falciparum detection in Anopheles mosquitoes showed lower sensitivity than real-time PCR in artificial exposure studies (34.9% vs 51.2% detection in P. berghei-exposed mosquitoes; 95% CIs 20.1-50.9% vs 35.5-66.7%) but comparable performance in field samples (7.0% vs 7.6% positivity; 95% CIs 3.7-11.9% vs 4.1-12.6%). Overall field infection rate 1.2% (20/1711) with P. falciparum, P. malariae, and P. ovale. LAMP proved suitable for xenomonitoring in tropical field conditions
articles.title_original: Assessment of automated loop-mediated isothermal amplification-(LAMP-)based xenomonitoring for Plasmodium spp. in Anopheles mosquitoes.

articles.headline: Correction to prior publication (DOI: 10.1371/journal.ppat.1007906). No substantive study content provided
articles.title_original: Correction: Controlled human malaria infection with Plasmodium falciparum demonstrates impact of naturally acquired immunity on virulence gene expression.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling predicted P. falciparum wastewater-based epidemiology infeasible in non-endemic countries (>1000-fold case increase needed for 50% detection at 8 PCR replicates, process limit of detection 3.0 log₁₀ GC/L) but feasible in ~84% of endemic African countries. Experimental wastewater decay followed first-order kinetics (0.035-0.11 h⁻¹ at 4–35°C) with minimal impact on detection, but freeze-thawing caused >70% DNA loss and HA membrane filtration recovery <2%, suggesting optimization of preanalytical methods needed
articles.title_original: Modeling and Laboratory Experiments to Assess Detection of Plasmodium falciparum in Wastewater: Insights for Wastewater-Based Surveillance of Malaria.

articles.headline: CRISPR/Cas9pyrR marker-free gene editing in P. falciparum successfully generated transgenic lines expressing GFP-tagged nuclear envelope protein PfGEX1 (gametocyte-specific) and nuclear pore protein PfNUP116 (early gametocyte localization, not detectable in asexual parasites). Sequential engineering of PfNUP116-GFP parasites with additional tags demonstrated dynamic compositional changes of the nuclear periphery during sexual differentiation and validated this technique for iterative genetic modifications
articles.title_original: A heterologous marker-free selection approach for CRISPR/Cas9-based gene editing in the malaria parasite Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Cluster-randomized trial in Ghana (n=1,806 children aged 6–35 months) found 12.5 mg daily elemental iron micronutrient powder for 5 months did not improve height-for-age (β=0.026, p=0.609), weight-for-age (β=−0.015, p=0.719), or weight-for-height (β=−0.035, p=0.463) z-scores but reduced malaria-induced anaemia risk (risk difference 0.050, 95% CI 0.004–0.096; p=0.032). Iron fortification was safe in this malaria-endemic setting but did not address persistent growth faltering
articles.title_original: Effect of Iron-Containing Micronutrient Powders on Growth and Malaria-Induced Anaemia Among Preschool Children in Ghana: A Cluster-Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial.

articles.headline: Screening of 400 compounds identified 20 invasion inhibitors and 2 partial egress inhibitors against P. falciparum at 1 μM concentration. Six compounds showed similar growth and invasion IC₅₀ values; two (MMV006931 and MMV024850) directly blocked merozoite invasion. Whole-genome sequencing of MMV006931-resistant parasites identified mutations in P. falciparum sterol exporter PfNCR1, confirmed as the drug target through detergent lysis assays, offering a novel invasion-targeting mechanism
articles.title_original: Unpacking boxes: identification of novel inhibitors of malaria parasite invasion.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 293 HIV-positive Ugandan children on ART found anaemia prevalence 6.14% (95% CI 3.4–8.9%), predominantly normocytic normochromic (72.2%). Multivariate analysis identified recent malaria episode (AOR=9.61, 95% CI 3.26–28.34), opportunistic infection (AOR=6.78, 95% CI 1.28–35.83), and poor ART adherence (AOR=14.75, 95% CI 1.31–165.65) as independent correlates of anaemia, highlighting malaria control and adherence support as critical interventions
articles.title_original: Anemia in HIV-Infected Children on ART in Uganda: High Risk Associations with Malaria, Opportunistic Infections, and Poor Adherence.

articles.headline: Four-year repeated cross-sectional study in Rohingya camps (n=582; 345 confirmed malaria cases from 486 refugees) found seasonal transmission peaking June–September with P. falciparum peaks earlier and sharper than P. vivax. Relative humidity showed strongest correlation with incidence (r=0.724–0.77), followed by rainfall and temperature. Infected individuals exhibited anaemia, thrombocytopenia, and elevated ESR; P. falciparum caused more pronounced alterations, supporting humidity-driven, species-specific malaria control
articles.title_original: Seasonal Climate Drivers and Immunohematological Profiles of Malaria in Rohingya Refugee Camps, Bangladesh: A 4-Year Repeated Cross-Sectional Study.

articles.headline: Retrospective spatial analysis of malaria in pregnancy surveillance (5.8 million pregnant women, 53,478 cases, 2018–2024, Amhara, Ethiopia) found annual incidence 9.2 per 1,000. Seven high-risk spatial clusters identified. Spatial regression showed malaria negatively associated with ANC4 utilization (IRR=0.92, 95% CI 0.91–0.94), soil moisture (IRR=0.85, 95% CI 0.80–0.90), and temperature (IRR=0.65, 95% CI 0.58–0.73); positively with ANC1 (IRR=1.07, 95% CI 1.06–1.08), indicating ANC service disparities and climate drivers require geographically targeted intervention
articles.title_original: Mapping inequity: spatial clustering of malaria in pregnancy in the Amhara region, Northwest Ethiopia (2018-2024).

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 270 febrile Ghanaian children found malaria prevalence 33%, bacteremia 31%, coinfection 10.4% (±3.6%, 95% CI). Seizures independently associated with increased coinfection odds (aOR=3.57, 95% CI 1.28–9.95, p=0.015). S. aureus predominated gram-positive isolates (43.8%); P. aeruginosa was most common gram-negative (6.3%). Multidrug-resistant bacteria in 34.5% of isolates, warranting antibiotic stewardship and resistance surveillance programs
articles.title_original: Malaria and bacteremia in febrile children presenting at a teaching hospital in Kumasi, Ghana.

articles.headline: Review of malaria prevention strategies emphasizing comprehensive approach combining chemoprophylaxis (intermittent preventive treatment in vulnerable groups, mass drug administration in endemic areas), vector control, and vaccination. WHO-endorsed RTS,S/AS01 (Mosquirix) and R21/Matrix-M vaccines deployed in sub-Saharan Africa provide moderate protection in young children while significantly reducing severe disease and death, considered safe. Monoclonal antibody development underway for immunocompromised populations; personalized recommendations for travellers ongoing
articles.title_original: Malaria Prevention: Progress to Date.

articles.headline: ORP3 is the third subunit of a trimeric NF-Y-like complex essential for Plasmodium oocyst excystation and sporozoite release; orp3-knockout mutants showed defective oocyst opening similar to orp1 and orp2 mutants, with the conserved helix A1 critical for trimerization and excystation
articles.title_original: Sporozoite egress from Plasmodium oocysts requires a trimeric NF-Y-like complex.

articles.headline: Fab fragments and Fc-mutated antibodies against P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein had protective efficacy comparable to intact antibodies, indicating limited role of Fc functions; binding to a single (NPNA)3 epitope was sufficient to strongly reduce liver burden, with only two separate epitopes needed for sterile protection
articles.title_original: Relevance of the antibody Fc fragment and epitope valency in protection against malaria sporozoites.

articles.headline: Among 215 Anopheles mosquitoes from Meghalaya, India (2019-2023), P. falciparum infection was detected in 49 (27.68%) of 177 An. maculatus sensu stricto and 16 (48.48%) of 33 An. pseudowillmori; blood meal analysis showed opportunistic feeding with 15% human, 16% cattle, and 17% mixed meals
articles.title_original: Anopheles maculatus s.l as potential malaria vectors with opportunistic feeding in Meghalaya, Northeast India.

articles.headline: A nanoparticle-displayed CIS43-based junctional epitope vaccine achieved comparable reduction in P. falciparum liver burden to R21 at 1 μg dose; at 0.25 μg, the junctional vaccine showed modest advantage, and combining with R21 provided no substantial additional benefit, though the junctional vaccine elicited broader antibody specificity than R21 alone
articles.title_original: Protective immunity against malaria by a nanoparticle CIS43-based junctional vaccine alone or in combination with R21.

articles.headline: In a cross-sectional survey of 200 febrile patients in Oguma, Nigeria, anti-chikungunya virus antibodies were detected in 9 (4.50%), with 3 (1.5%) co-infected with malaria and 1 (1.0%) with chikungunya alone; 60 (30%) had malaria; active chikungunya infection occurred only in males [4 (4.3%)]
articles.title_original: Seroprevalence of chikungunya virus infection and co-occurrence with malaria among patients with febrile illness in Oguma, the middle belt region of Nigeria.

articles.headline: Review of mass drug administration (MDA) strategies amid 282 million estimated malaria cases in 2024 and stalled control progress; MDA faces limitations including drug resistance and sustainability challenges; proposes integrated 'MDA+' strategy combining vaccines, digital monitoring, and cross-border cooperation for P. falciparum and P. knowlesi elimination
articles.title_original: Application Strategy and Research Progress of Large-Scale Population Drug Intervention in Malaria Control.

articles.headline: RCT (N=761) of IPTp regimens in pregnancy: IPTp-DP+SP versus IPTp-SP decreased weight-for-length z-score by 0.18 [95% CI -0.03, 0.39] at 1-3 months and 0.28 [95% CI 0.07, 0.49] at 4-6 months; lower placental malaria risk provided modest offset (+0.06 z-score); IPTp-DP+SP had 0.28 lower length-for-age z-score at 7-13 months versus IPTp-DP
articles.title_original: Effect mechanisms of different malaria chemoprevention regimens in pregnancy on infant growth outcomes: causal mediation analysis of a randomized controlled trial.

articles.headline: Susceptibility evaluation of 2,020 Anopheles (1,480 wild-collected) to PBO-LLINs in Kenya: mortality was 68% with new nets and 34% with 24-month-old nets in wild-collected mosquitoes, primarily An. arabiensis; reflects very high pyrethroid resistance in the local vector population
articles.title_original: Reduced Efficacy of Piperonyl Butoxide Based Long-Lasting Insecticide-Treated Nets Against Malaria Vectors in Western Kenya.

articles.headline: Phase 2 RCT (N=324) of L9LS monoclonal antibody (10-20 mg/kg) against P. falciparum in children 5-59 months in Kenya: two-dose L9LS resulted in 70 (66%) of 106 infected over 12 months versus 91 (83%) of 110 placebo (protective efficacy 42.7%, 95% CI 22.5-57.7, p=0.0003); well tolerated with no serious treatment-related adverse events
articles.title_original: Safety and efficacy of the monoclonal antibody L9LS for malaria prevention in children exposed to perennial malaria transmission in Kenya: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 2 trial.

articles.headline: Title only: evaluates antibody-based interventions for malaria prevention in settings with intense perennial P. falciparum transmission; no quantitative efficacy results provided in available abstract
articles.title_original: Antibody-based malaria prevention in an intense perennial transmission setting.

articles.headline: Review of safety monitoring for RTS,S/AS01 vaccine implementation: employed sentinel hospital and community-based mortality surveillance, pharmacovigilance for rare unanticipated signals, and cohort event monitoring for special-interest conditions; summarizes strengths and weaknesses of these complementary approaches
articles.title_original: Assessing safety in the RTS,S/AS01 malaria vaccine implementation programme: an overview and comparison of methods.

articles.headline: Perspective on malaria research priorities amid stalled gains and deepening funding shortfall: advocates dual mandate protecting lives now while preparing for emerging biological and systemic threats; prioritizes pragmatic implementation research, sustained innovation in chemoprevention and vaccines, and research on sustainable financing to improve decision-making
articles.title_original: Prioritising in a crisis: what next for malaria research?

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 358 caregivers in Nigeria found only 36.3% aware of the R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccine, yet 85.8% willing to vaccinate. Main barriers were fear of adverse reactions (45.1%), lack of interest (19.6%), and spousal disagreement (13.7%). Female caregivers and civil servants showed significantly higher acceptance (p=0.006 and p=0.018 respectively). Authors recommend targeted awareness campaigns addressing safety concerns and spousal engagement to translate willingness into actual uptake
articles.title_original: Awareness, acceptability and determinants of the new malaria vaccine among caregivers of under-five children in Asaba, Nigeria: a multi-centre cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Expert commentary marking World Malaria Day 2026, featuring three researchers sharing experiences on progress toward WHO's 2016-2030 goal of 90% reduction in malaria incidence and mortality. No quantitative results; describes challenges and opportunities in molecular surveillance, epidemiology, and implementation research across the Peruvian Amazon, Horn of Africa, and Asian-Pacific regions
articles.title_original: Unite against Malaria: challenges and achievements in malaria control around the globe.

articles.headline: Narrative review synthesizing mechanisms of artemisinin resistance in P. falciparum, including Kelch13 mutations in the propeller domain and additional genetic determinants: reduced haemoglobin endocytosis, increased phosphatidylinositol-3-kinase levels, unfolded protein response activation, and polymorphism in other genes. No experimental results presented; review emphasizes multiple pathways contribute to resistance development alongside Kelch13 mutations
articles.title_original: Insights into the factors contributing to artemisinin resistance in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: Perspective article on how structural biology—particularly cryogenic electron microscopy (cryoEM) and artificial intelligence-enhanced data analysis—is accelerating rational design of blood-stage malaria vaccines by revealing atomic-level detail of parasite surface complexes involved in red blood cell invasion. No quantitative vaccine efficacy data; focuses on technical advances enabling structural understanding of Plasmodium multiprotein complexes
articles.title_original: Atomic clarity: how structural biology is shaping blood-stage malaria vaccines.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: Two-stage cluster randomised trial in Kenya (47,614 participants, 84 clusters, 2021–2024) evaluated adaptive vector control strategies. Stage 1: piperonyl butoxide-treated nets (PBO nets) reduced malaria incidence vs standard nets (adjusted incidence rate ratio [aIRR] 0.75, 95% CI 0.69–0.81, p<0.0001 at 1–6 months). Standard nets plus annual indoor residual spraying (IRS) showed aIRR 0.74 (p<0.0001). Stage 2 in non-responders: adding IRS to PBO nets more effective than larviciding (aIRR 0.89, p=0.0081); enhanced IRS outperformed biannual spraying (aIRR 0.67, p<0.0001). All adaptive strategies significantly reduced incidence vs standard nets (all p<0.01), with PBO-based strategies showing greater sustained benefit. No adverse events recorded
articles.title_original: Adaptive intervention strategies for malaria vector control in western Kenya: a cluster, sequential, multiple-assignment, randomised, open-label trial.

articles.headline: In vivo study in mice tested artesunate (ART) and Tinospora crispa extract (TCE) alone and combined against P. berghei. ED50 values: ART 200.31 mg/kg, TCE 1.96 mg/kg. Combination therapy showed synergistic antimalarial activity (combination index <1 at ED50, ED50/2, ED50/4) with significantly greater parasitemia inhibition than either monotherapy, enhanced protection against packed cell volume reduction and body weight loss, and prolonged mean survival time. Authors propose synergistic combination as novel strategy to mitigate drug resistance
articles.title_original: Antimalarial Potency of Tinospora crispa Stem Extract and Artesunate Against Plasmodium berghei: A Study on Combination Therapy.

articles.headline: Narrative review synthesizing evidence on malaria in children <6 months, highlighting diagnostic challenges, variable prevalence data reflecting methodological heterogeneity, and limited safety/efficacy evidence for antimalarial agents in this age group. Until recently, no agents were licensed for neonates or infants weighing <4.5 kg. Authors conclude current treatment recommendations inadequately address this vulnerable population and call for harmonized international policy amendments
articles.title_original: Malaria in children aged <6 months: a narrative review of current evidence, recommendations and practice gaps.

articles.headline: Ecological study of Uganda district-level malaria surveillance (2014–2023) mapped spatial heterogeneity and forecasted incidence using emerging hotspot analysis and ARIMA(2,1,1) modeling. Results showed higher burden in West Nile, Acholi, and Lango; lower in Tooro, Kigezi, and southern Buganda. Emerging hotspot analysis identified sporadic hotspots (West Nile), oscillating hotspots (Karamoja), and consecutive hotspots (Lango–Busoga corridor). Mann-Kendall trend testing showed upward trends concentrated in Karamoja. ARIMA forecast projected approximately 1.0–1.2 million monthly malaria cases through 2030 with recurring April–June seasonal peak. Authors recommend geographically targeted intensified control in high-burden districts
articles.title_original: Hotspot Mapping, Spatiotemporal Clustering and Forecasting of Malaria Incidence in Uganda, 2014-2023: Strategies to Inform Targeted Public Health Interventions.

articles.headline: Systematic review of malaria rapid diagnostic test accuracy in febrile adults from endemic regions. Database search of MEDLINE, EMBASE, Cochrane, and Google Scholar identified 138 papers; 6 met inclusion criteria. Limited data presented; no specific accuracy metrics reported in abstract
articles.title_original: Are rapid diagnostic tests reliable for the detection of malaria in the emergency department?

articles.headline: Cross-sectional microbiome study of Ghanaian children found asymptomatic P. falciparum and P. malariae infections altered gut bacterial composition: Firmicutes decreased from 52.5% to 44.0%, Bacteroidetes increased from 34.7% to 45.6%, Proteobacteria increased from 3.0% to 4.8%. At genus level, Prevotella_9 was most abundant and variable; Faecalibacterium significantly declined in infected vs healthy controls. Beta-diversity analysis showed malaria status significantly affected microbial composition (PERMANOVA p<0.05), explaining 19.1% of variation. Authors highlight potential link between malaria infection and gut microbiota alterations
articles.title_original: Gut microbiome alterations among Ghanaian children with asymptomatic malaria infections.

articles.headline: Study of 40 subjects with acute P. vivax malaria (23 seropositive for anti-PvDBPII antibodies) examined circulating T follicular helper 2 cells (cTfh2) and memory B cell interactions. Results: 3 high responders produced inhibitory antibodies against PvDBPII-erythrocyte binding; cTfh2 cells expanded in seropositive subjects with higher ICOS expression in high responders; PvDBPII antigen specifically activated cTfh2 cells; cTfh2-memory B cell co-cultures promoted differentiation into antibody-secreting cells producing anti-PvDBPII IgG. Authors conclude cTfh2 activation (marked by ICOS upregulation) and cTfh2-memory B cell crosstalk drive anti-P. vivax humoral immunity
articles.title_original: A functional crosstalk between circulating follicular helper 2 T cells and memory B cells drives anti-Plasmodium vivax antibodies.

articles.headline: Open-label randomised trial in Madang Province, Papua New Guinea (183 mothers, 151 infants completing 6-month follow-up) compared no treatment vs postpartum artemisinin combination therapy (artemether-lumefantrine or dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine). Primary outcome was malaria incidence by microscopy and/or PCR in infants assessed monthly. Results: slide-positive malaria 1.3% (95% CI 0.2–5.2; 2 cases: 1 P. falciparum, 1 P. vivax); 3 additional PCR-detected infections (2 P. falciparum, 1 P. vivax); overall 6-month slide-/PCR-positive incidence 3.3% (95% CI 1.2–8.0), with 4 of 5 cases in untreated group infants. No significant between-group differences in infant growth or developmental milestones. Authors conclude malaria risk in early coastal PNG infancy is low with typically asymptomatic infections
articles.title_original: Malaria in Papua New Guinean infants: a prespecified analysis of a randomised postpartum antimalarial treatment trial.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: Integrated strategy review proposing combination of zooprophylaxis, insecticide-treated livestock (ITL), and insecticide-treated nets (ITNs) for malaria vector control, targeting outdoor-feeding and zoophilic mosquito species to address ITN limitations including outdoor/early-evening biting and insecticide resistance
articles.title_original: Integrating zooprophylaxis, insecticide-treated livestock and mosquito nets for malaria control: a One Health proposal.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: Paleoclimatic and epidemiological analysis of sub-Saharan Africa (74,000–5,000 years ago) found that Plasmodium falciparum malaria transmission risk inversely correlated with human habitat occupation, suggesting disease ecology shaped early human spatial organization and dispersal patterns independent of climate
articles.title_original: Malaria shaped human spatial organization for the past 74 thousand years.

articles.headline: Interpretation of the Chinese health industry standard WS/T 10029-2025 for Plasmodium spp. rapid diagnostic tests using immunochromatographic assays, providing standardized technical guidelines, sample collection procedures, and result interpretation protocols to improve accuracy of malaria antigen detection and timely diagnosis of imported malaria cases
articles.title_original: [Interpretation of a health industry standard Detection of Plasmodium spp. Immune-chromatographic test (WS/T 10029-2025)].
articles.authors: Xu S, Zhu G, Cao J
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi

articles.headline: Decision-analytic model of placental malaria vaccine administered pre-pregnancy in sub-Saharan Africa (baseline: 40% efficacy, 30% uptake, $5 dose) found an incremental cost-effectiveness ratio (ICER) of $321 per perinatal DALY averted for primigravidae versus $4,444 for multigravidae. Best-case: $225 and $3,148, respectively. Vaccine demonstrated robust cost-effectiveness for primigravidae, supporting prioritized deployment targeting primiparous women
articles.title_original: Modelling the Cost-Effectiveness of a Placental Malaria Vaccine in Sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: Review of Plasmodium falciparum cerebral malaria (CM) in Sub-Saharan Africa documenting high mortality (15%–30%), neurological sequelae in survivors (10%–30%), and widespread misdiagnosis (up to 23% of clinically diagnosed cases). Identifies critical diagnostic and therapeutic barriers in resource-limited settings including inadequate neuroimaging, lack of biomarkers, and absent rehabilitation services
articles.title_original: Cerebral malaria in resource-limited African regions: Diagnostic and therapeutic challenges.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 9,222 households across 11 Ethiopian regions (Oct 2024–Jan 2025) found household LLIN ownership of 71.5% but insufficient nets (58.3%) and low utilization (59.9%; 72.7% rural vs. urban). Age and pregnancy were strongest predictors of use (ORs: 0.258, 0.662). Barriers included 60.5% lacking confidence in hanging nets and 64.1% misconceiving malaria as seasonal
articles.title_original: Assessment of Long-Lasting Insecticidal Net (LLIN) Ownership, Utilization, and Associated Barriers in Malaria-Endemic Communities of Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Case report of 7-year-old child with acute motor axonal neuropathy (asymmetric, relapsing onset) following malaria infection, illustrating atypical Guillain-Barré presentation in malaria-endemic, resource-limited settings. Highlights importance of early clinical recognition for improved outcomes despite diagnostic and systemic constraints
articles.title_original: Guillain-Barré Syndrome After Malaria: A Case Report of a 7-Year-Old Child With Asymmetric Onset of Acute Motor Axonal Neuropathy.

articles.headline: Observational finding: Ugandan children surviving severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria performed worse on cognitive tests years after infection, indicating potential long-term neurological deficits
articles.title_original: Malaria may cause long-term brain deficits, study shows.

articles.headline: Longitudinal study of 476 Ugandan adolescents (mean age 16.4 years) found SARS-CoV-2 seropositivity increased from 63% at baseline to 94.9% at 12 months; 61.3% had prior clinical malaria, 12.0% prior asymptomatic parasitaemia. Multivariable analysis showed no consistent association between prospectively measured life-course malaria exposure and SARS-CoV-2 seropositivity or antibody levels
articles.title_original: Life Course Malaria Exposure and SARS-CoV-2 Seroepidemiology in Ugandan Adolescents: A Longitudinal Study Nested in a Birth Cohort.

articles.headline: Analytical validation of Abbott Bioline Malaria Ag P.f/P.f/P.v RDT with 40 infected-patient samples (10 P. falciparum, 10 P. vivax, 15 P. malariae, 5 P. ovale) accurately detected intended species but showed significant cross-reactivity: 12/14 P. malariae (86%) and 4 P. ovale false-positive on Pf-pLDH line; specificity against P. malariae only 14.29% (p<0.05)
articles.title_original: Strong Cross-Reactivity of Anti-Plasmodium falciparum pLDH Monoclonal Antibodies With Plasmodium malariae pLDH in the Abbott Bioline Malaria Ag P.f/P.f/P.v Rapid Diagnostic Test.

articles.headline: Synthetic optimization of syringolin natural products enabled generation of compounds targeting Plasmodium falciparum 20S proteasome with nanomolar antiparasitic activity and high selectivity for Plasmodium over human proteasomes. Cryo-EM structures revealed structural determinants of species-selective inhibition, providing framework for developing novel antimalarial drugs targeting essential parasite biochemical pathways
articles.title_original: An Optimized Route to the Syringolin Natural Products Enables Combinatorial Synthesis of Selective, Bioactive Inhibitors of the Plasmodium falciparum 20S Proteasome.

articles.headline: Synthesis and evaluation of mefloquine (MQ) complexed with platinum or palladium metal centers and phosphine or bipyridine coligands against Plasmodium falciparum and Schistosoma mansoni revealed that metal centers augmented antiparasitic activity through thioredoxin/glutathione reductase targeting while coligands modulated reactivity. MQ-platinum complexes demonstrated high in vivo efficacy
articles.title_original: Examination of Coligands in Mefloquine-Metal Complexes Reveals the Structural Determinants of Activity against Plasmodium falciparum and Schistosoma mansoni.

articles.headline: This scoping review protocol will systematically examine facilitators and barriers to malaria surveillance in community pharmacies and over-the-counter medicine sellers across sub-Saharan Africa through searches in PubMed, CINAHL, Scopus, Web of Science, and Hinari (inception to June 15, 2026), with findings disseminated to WHO and national malaria programs
articles.title_original: Facilitators and barriers to malaria surveillance in community pharmacies and over-the-counter medicine sellers in sub-Saharan Africa's health sector: a scoping review protocol.

articles.headline: This review synthesizes archaeological, biological, and ecological evidence on human-Plasmodium interactions across East, Southeast, and South Asia, examining parasite evolution and its role in human demographic shifts, highlighting how human agricultural practices (particularly rice cultivation) inadvertently created favorable malaria transmission environments
articles.title_original: Historical texts and archaeological evidence for malaria and its interactions with humans from East to South Asia.
articles.authors: Li G, Yuan X, Wang T

articles.headline: This review synthesizes knowledge on Plasmodium oocysts—the longest parasite life stage representing a critical population bottleneck—and identifies major gaps in understanding molecular and cellular regulation, nutrient acquisition, and host-parasite interactions, with implications for developing transmission-blocking interventions toward malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Oocyst: knowns and unknowns about the lengthiest life stage of the malaria parasite.
articles.authors: Bailey AJ, Vlachou D, Christophides GK

articles.headline: In a dose-escalation induced blood-stage malaria study, 4 adults received intravenous inoculation of P. knowlesi YH1-strain parasites at ~2,800–280,000 parasites; 2 developed parasitaemia, with one peaking at 202 parasites/mL (parasite multiplication rate 32h = 2.33, 95% CI 1.66–3.25); GMP-grade parasite bank was successfully established and safely treated with artemether-lumefantrine, supporting evaluation of infection susceptibility factors
articles.title_original: Development of an experimental human blood-stage model for studying Plasmodium knowlesi.

articles.headline: This perspective discusses China's strategic participation in international malaria control cooperation with African countries during the 2026 China-Africa Year of People-to-People Exchanges, proposing optimized cooperation models and policy recommendations for accelerating progress toward global malaria elimination
articles.title_original: [Practice and prospect of China's participation in international cooperation for malaria control from the perspective of the "China-Africa Year of People-to-People Exchanges"].
articles.authors: Liang C, Zhu G, Cao J
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi

articles.headline: This study presents a magneto-optical imaging approach integrated into conventional polarizing microscopes that achieves label-free, quantitative detection of malaria by exploiting the magnetic anisotropy and optical dichroism of hemozoin crystals, enabling quantitative correlation between signal strength and parasite load with automated, objective parameters suitable for routine diagnosis in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Magneto-optical microscopy platform for quantitative imaging of hemozoin in blood for malaria diagnosis.
articles.authors: Kinyua DM, Kotar J, Cicuta P

articles.headline: A Plasmodium falciparum MKT1 gene deletion mutant (Pfmkt1¯) proliferated asexually and differentiated into gametocytes indistinguishably from wildtype NF54, underwent normal gametogenesis with no apparent defect in male and female gamete formation, demonstrating MKT1-like protein is dispensable for both asexual and sexual stage development
articles.title_original: An MKT1 domain protein is dispensable for erythrocytic stages of plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Chakraborty B, McGee SM, Kumar S

articles.headline: Among 5,300 seropositive individuals across 32 Gambian villages post-mass drug administration, random forest models using antibody responses to 19 P. falciparum antigens discriminated low versus high detection populations with precision-recall AUCs of 0.92–0.93 (<5% PCR prevalence) and 0.89–0.90 (<10% prevalence) using an 8-antigen panel, identifying surveillance blind spots in low-transmission settings
articles.title_original: Malaria antibody responses augment surveillance in low-transmission settings in the Upper River Region, the Gambia.

articles.headline: In silico analysis of the human Rac1 promoter (-2000 to +500 bp) identified a GC-rich (57.34%), CpG-rich (164 sites, o/e 0.75) regulatory region containing binding motifs for mineralocorticoid receptor, hypoxia-inducible factors, inflammatory mediators, and GATA-1, suggesting molecular basis linking antihypertensive therapy resistance (ACE inhibitors/ARBs) with enhanced malaria susceptibility in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: The Rac1 Promoter as a Molecular Nexus for Antihypertensive Therapy Resistance and Enhanced Malaria Susceptibility.

articles.headline: Analysis of 9 malaria routine data quality audits (2022–2024) across 1,189 health facility catchment areas in Zambia found average economic costs of $24,938 per intervention ($205 per HFCA), with personnel (64%) and transportation (25%) as primary drivers; optimized scenarios incorporating virtual meetings and streamlined teams reduced costs by 41%, while digital tools projected additional savings up to 52%
articles.title_original: A framework for identifying cost efficiencies in malaria routine data quality audits: methodology and application in Zambia.

articles.headline: A baseline household survey (July–October 2019) of 4,065 households with 4,948 children aged 5–48 months across 46 Kenyan sub-counties found P. falciparum malaria prevalence of 22% (95% CI 19–26, range 1–71%), high ITN ownership (93%) and use (87%), but suboptimal second-year vaccine coverage (measles dose 2: 49%), vitamin A supplementation (46%), and deworming (50%); 98% caregiver willingness for RTS,S despite only 36% prior awareness
articles.title_original: The Malaria Vaccine Programme Evaluation in Kenya: results of a baseline household survey prior to the introduction of the RTS,S/AS01 vaccine.

articles.headline: A Microsoft Excel-based economic model for 2022 estimated 30.8 million malaria-related disability-adjusted life years in sub-Saharan Africans ≥15 years, representing socioeconomic burden of ~$82.8 billion; artemether-lumefantrine treatment of uncomplicated malaria generated estimated socioeconomic benefit of $24.2 billion by avoiding productivity losses, years of life lost, and caregiving days
articles.title_original: Socioeconomic Impact of Artemether-Lumefantrine in Malaria Treatment across Sub-Saharan Africa: A Model-Based Historical Analysis with 2022 as Reference Year.

articles.headline: NK cells exhibited transient cytotoxicity enhancement during acute P. falciparum infection via granzyme B-mediated killing and elevated PRF1/GZMB/GZMA expression, supported by increased plasma granzymes and cytokines but dampened by soluble cytokine receptor inhibitors. The host microenvironment orchestrates NK cell activation and downmodulation, potentially informing strategies to harness NK cells for malaria control while minimizing immunopathology
articles.title_original: NK cell cytotoxicity is transiently enhanced during acute malaria and modulated by the host microenvironment.

articles.headline: Optimized SELEX and structure-guided dimer engineering generated a symmetric inverted dimeric aptamer (IDA) targeting P. falciparum lactate dehydrogenase with picomolar affinity (>800-fold higher than existing aptamers) and enhanced nuclease resistance. Clinical evaluation of 52 blood samples achieved 81.8% sensitivity and 100% specificity with limit of detection 3.3 ng/mL, demonstrating strong translational potential for point-of-care malaria diagnostics
articles.title_original: Engineering High-Performance Aptamers via Optimized SELEX and Structure-Guided Dimer Assembly for Clinical Malaria Diagnostics.

articles.headline: Palmitoyltransferase (PATs) activity varied significantly across P. falciparum developmental stages (P<0.001). Inhibition with 2-bromopalmitate (10 μmol/L) reduced asexual parasitemia (area under curve: 8.37±1.75 vs 25.70±0.70, P<0.001), merozoite production per schizont (14.02 vs 18.05, P<0.001), and gametocyte production (7.51 vs 18.90, P<0.001), identifying protein palmitoylation as a potential antimalarial target
articles.title_original: [Role of protein palmitoylation modification in the proliferation and gametogenesis of Plasmodium falciparum].
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi

articles.headline: Retrospective study of 311 malaria patients (2022–2025) found P. vivax predominance (51.1%) followed by P. falciparum (40.2%), with severe malaria in 43.7% (primarily severe anemia 26.0% and jaundice 23.2%). Independent predictors of severe malaria were higher parasitemia (aOR=1.70 per 1%, p<0.001) and non-Saudi nationality (aOR=2.40, p=0.027); 84% of cases occurred in 2024–2025 outbreak period
articles.title_original: Clinical Characteristics and Outcomes of Malaria Patients in the Aseer Region, Saudi Arabia: A Retrospective Study (2022-2025).

articles.headline: Among 159 Fulani pregnant women in Ghana, ITN ownership was 54.7% and regular use among owners only 52.9%. Predictors of ownership were awareness of malaria prevention (aOR=3.45), prior counseling (aOR=4.52), and lower wealth (aOR=0.21); utilization was associated with knowledge of symptoms (aOR=0.18), >3 children (aOR=0.02), and multiple ITNs (aOR=0.07). ITN coverage and use remained far below national and global targets
articles.title_original: Factors Associated With Insecticide-Treated Bed Net Possession and Utilization in Malaria Prevention Among Fulani Pregnant Women in the Savannah Hinterlands of Ghana: A Cross-Sectional Study.

articles.headline: Azadirachta indica and Khaya senegalensis decoctions were formulated into capsules using kaolin (35 mg/dose) and bentonite (50 mg/dose) as absorbents. Formulated capsules showed disintegration times of 10.01±0.34 min (AI) and 4.48±0.23 min (KS), cumulative drug release of 78.13±0.37% (AI) and 95.63±1.00% (KS), and antiplasmodial activity with IC50 values of 2.74±0.70 μg/mL (AI) and 24.37±4.19 μg/mL (KS), demonstrating selectivity for Plasmodium parasites
articles.title_original: Formulation and Evaluation of Capsules From Azadirachta indica and Khaya senegalensis for Malaria Treatment.

articles.headline: Unmatched 1:2 case-control study comparing 100 P. falciparum malaria deaths with 200 survivors (2020–2024, Uganda) identified independent mortality predictors: convulsions (aOR=17, 95% CI 4.2–71), loss of consciousness (aOR=14, 95% CI 1.4–113), severe anemia (aOR=3.4, 95% CI 1.4–8.2), vomiting (aOR=3.1, 95% CI 1.4–6.9), and delayed care-seeking >24 hours (aOR=8.8, 95% CI 2.3–34). Early recognition of danger signs could reduce pediatric malaria mortality
articles.title_original: Risk factors for malaria-related mortality among children under five at Mbale Regional Referral Hospital, Uganda, 2020-2024: A case-control study.

articles.headline: Commentary discussing differential effects of malaria treatments in pregnancy on maternal outcomes versus fetal and neonatal outcomes; no quantitative data available from title alone
articles.title_original: Malaria in pregnancy: what's good for the goose may not be good for the goslings.

articles.headline: Double-blind randomized trial comparing intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy (IPTp) with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP), dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DP), or DP+SP in 871 infants found similar clinical malaria incidence (DP vs SP: IRR=1.04, 95% CI 0.78–1.38; DP+SP vs SP: IRR=0.93, 95% CI 0.68–1.26) and parasite prevalence (21.8%). DP-containing arms showed transiently increased wasting at birth (resolved by 3–6 months), with no significant long-term infant outcome differences
articles.title_original: Impact of intermittent preventive treatment for malaria in pregnancy with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine, dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine, and their combination on infant outcomes: A randomized controlled trial.

articles.headline: Narrative review identified five mechanistic pathways linking malaria to carcinogenesis in sub-Saharan Africa: oxidative stress and chronic inflammation driving genomic instability, microbiome dysbiosis, shared molecular targets (EPCR, oncofetal chondroitin sulfate), cooperative interactions with oncogenic viruses (EBV), and vitamin D deficiency. Antimalarial agents (artemisinin derivatives, chloroquine, quinacrine) demonstrate anticancer properties, suggesting opportunities for dual-action therapeutics in malaria-endemic populations
articles.title_original: Malaria driven mechanisms shaping cancer risk and aggressiveness in African populations.

articles.headline: Discussion of P. vivax's potential role in Guillain-Barré Syndrome (GBS) with emphasis that P. vivax causality should only be established after excluding alternative etiologic triggers; no quantitative data available from title alone
articles.title_original: Plasmodium Vivax Can Only Be Responsible for GBS if All Other Triggers Have Been Ruled Out.

articles.headline: Historical and cultural data on malaria in Hungary; focused on epidemiological and social history rather than clinical findings; no quantitative results available from title alone
articles.title_original: [Data for cultural history of malaria in Hungary].

articles.headline: Workshop report discussing malaria vaccine development for pregnancy prevention. Identified vaccine candidates targeting VAR2CSA antigen (PRIMVAC, PAMVAC) and strategies to repurpose existing antimalarial vaccines. Key barriers include weak pharmacovigilance systems and vaccine hesitancy. Recommendations included strengthening community engagement, defining efficacy endpoints for clinical trials, reinforcing safety monitoring, and planning for regulatory alignment to enable future MiP vaccine implementation
articles.title_original: Immunization strategies to prevent malaria in pregnancy - a multistakeholder workshop.

articles.headline: Study developing PvAmpSeq, an amplicon sequencing assay targeting 11 SNP-rich regions to classify P. vivax recurrences. In Solomon Islands trial participants, 50% of recurrences showed >50% identity-by-descent (probable relapses/recrudescences with wide uncertainty). In Peruvian cohort, 68% exhibited <25% relatedness. PvAmpSeq enables high-resolution P. vivax genotyping for distinguishing relapse, reinfection, and recrudescence—critical for evaluating treatment efficacy and transmission
articles.title_original: Understanding Plasmodium vivax recurrent infections using an amplicon deep sequencing assay, identity-by-descent and model-based classification.

articles.headline: Integrated long-read nanopore sequencing, ribosome profiling, and single-cell transcriptomics characterizing P. falciparum long non-coding RNAs (lncRNAs) across asexual and sexual stages. Most lncRNAs showed pronounced stage-specific expression, particularly abundant in mature gametocytes with differential expression between female and male gametocytes. Many lncRNAs exhibited co-expression with sense mRNAs or negatively correlated patterns, suggesting roles in regulating gene expression during gametocytogenesis
articles.title_original: Dynamic regulation of long non-coding RNAs across asexual and gametocyte development in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Intensive pyrethroid spray catch sampling over 60 months (2012–2019) in four Burkina Faso villages analyzing variance in Anopheles density and species composition. Between-house variation was the largest component for density, followed by between-year and between-village; household size increased density but bed nets had no correlation. For species composition, between-village variance contributed 76% of total random variation. Variance components parameterize field trials for novel vector control programs
articles.title_original: Variance partitioning reveals contrasting random effect contributions to the density and species composition of malaria-transmitting mosquitoes in western Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study (September–November 2022) of 5,096 patients across three epidemiological zones in Niger. PCR testing of 304 samples identified Plasmodium in 55% (166/304): P. falciparum 90% (149/166), P. ovale 5% (9/166), P. malariae 3% (5/166), P. vivax 2% (3/166). P. vivax was detected only in hypo-endemic areas. Findings show strong P. falciparum predominance and identify P. vivax circulation warranting diagnostic/management strategy adaptation
articles.title_original: Prevalence and spatial distribution of malaria-causing Plasmodium infections in the Niger Republic.

articles.headline: Systematic review synthesizing recent structural and functional advances in Plasmodium protein-protein interaction (PPI) networks driving the parasite life cycle. Background: 282 million cases and 610,000 deaths in 2024, with widespread insecticide and artemisinin resistance. Reviews PPI complexes in asexual stages and sexual development using cryo-EM, biotinylation, and genetic tools. Concludes PPI-targeted approaches offer resistance-resilient antimalarials and transmission-blocking interventions with low mutational potential
articles.title_original: Targeting protein-protein interactions in Plasmodium: from asexual replication to sexual development.

articles.headline: Experimental hut trial evaluating transfluthrin-based Mosquito Shield emanator against pyrethroid-resistant An. gambiae s.l. in Benin over two 32-day cycles. Mosquito Shield produced 43.0% protective efficacy (95% CI 24.0–57.0; p<0.001) against An. gambiae and 38.0% (95% CI 12.0–57.0; p=0.008) against Mansonia africana via human landing catches; aspiration data showed 64% personal protection (95% CI 21.9–81.8; p<0.001), 48.5% deterrence, and 49.0% An. gambiae mortality. Significantly reduced mosquito landing, blood-feeding, and survival
articles.title_original: Experimental hut evaluation of mosquito shield, a transfluthrin spatial emanator, for control of wild pyrethroid resistant malaria vectors in Benin.

articles.headline: Cohort study (2015–2022) of 2,269 participants across seven Kinshasa sites assessing RDT accuracy using PCR gold standard. One-year cumulative incidences: 23.1% false-positive RDTs, 17.8% false-negative; by seven years: 77.7% false-positive, 83.4% false-negative. Misdiagnosis correlated with age, recent treatment, transmission intensity, and parasite density. 58% of RDT-negative participants received antimalarials; 4% of RDT-positive did not. Findings highlight RDT limitations and need for improved clinical decision-making in high-burden settings
articles.title_original: Challenges in malaria diagnosis and treatment in Kinshasa Province, Democratic Republic of the Congo.

articles.headline: Bayesian interrupted time-series analysis of confirmed malaria cases (2018–2023) in Western North Region, Ghana following Interceptor G2 (IG2) dual-active ingredient ITN distribution in 2021. Malaria cases reduced 30% immediately post-intervention (OR 0.696; 95% CrI 0.623–0.778), sustained at 6 months (26% reduction; OR 0.739; 95% CrI 0.653–0.837) and 30 months (40% reduction; OR 0.594; 95% CrI 0.492–0.718). Effect confirmed in 7/9 districts, supporting scale-up in pyrethroid-resistant areas
articles.title_original: Reductions in malaria cases after deployment of dual-active ingredient insecticide-treated nets in Ghana-a Bayesian interrupted time series analysis.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 337 healthcare professionals in Kalima district, DRC assessing acceptability of Artemisia annua tea for malaria treatment. Clinical acceptability: 81.0%; perceived effectiveness: 82.8%; rural residency strongest adoption predictor (AOR 6.847; p=0.003). 49.0% cited lack of clinical evidence as major barrier; 91.4% demanded standardized dosage training. Reveals policy-practice gap between international guidelines and field reality, with providers showing readiness contingent on standardization to mitigate artemisinin-resistance selection
articles.title_original: Public Health Perspectives on Integrating Artemisia annua Tea for Uncomplicated Malaria Treatment: A Cross-Sectional Study of Perceptions and Acceptability Among Healthcare Workers in Kalima District, Maniema, DRC.
articles.authors: Munyangi Wa Nkola J, et al.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 1,389 mosquito-related publications (2010 and 2020) across PubMed and Web of Science. Culex reporting significantly lower in malaria-endemic (30.2%) and dengue-endemic (34.2%) countries versus non-endemic (64.5%). Aedes reporting increased over the decade while Anopheles decreased, reflecting global research shift including 2015–2017 Zika emergence. Concludes that presence of human-amplified pathogens (malaria, dengue) suppresses Culex research; recommends researchers include additional mosquito community data when publishing vector studies
articles.title_original: Forgotten in the tropics: research on Culex mosquitoes is overshadowed in malaria and dengue-endemic regions.

articles.headline: Multilevel analysis of 9,157 households from 2022–2023 Ethiopian National Health Equity Survey examining malaria risk perception. Overall 76.4% perceived risk (95% CI 74.9–77.9). Rural households 54% more likely to perceive risk (AOR 1.54; 95% CI 1.22–1.95); higher education nearly doubled perception (AOR 1.89; 95% CI 1.36–2.63); older heads half as likely (AOR 0.49; 95% CI 0.35–0.67). 38% variability occurred between woredas (Median OR 2.62) with significant spatial autocorrelation (Global Moran's I 0.42; p<0.001). Perception shaped by socioeconomic disparities and geographic clustering
articles.title_original: Household malaria risk perception in Ethiopia: multilevel and spatial analysis using the 2022-2023 National Health Equity Survey.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 717 malaria-suspected patients in Sudan (2023-2024) found regional variation: Kassala 62.6% malaria prevalence (95% CI 58.2-67.0%), predominantly P. vivax (54%); Kosti 52% prevalence, predominantly P. falciparum (97.7%). Submicroscopic infections occurred in 8.7% overall. G6PD B variant predominated; novel African A− variant identified in 3.5% of Kosti patients. No significant association between G6PD genotype and parasite density. Study identified regional parasite species variation and G6PD polymorphisms in conflict-affected areas with equal health impact on internally displaced persons and residents
articles.title_original: Characterisation of malaria and glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency in conflict-affected zones of southern and eastern Sudan.

articles.headline: Among 307 adult malaria patients admitted to four Ethiopian hospitals (Feb-Apr 2024), acute kidney injury occurred in 28.3% (87/307), with serum creatinine rising from mean 1.43±0.29 to 1.72±0.74 mg/dL within 48 hours. Significant associations: P. falciparum infection (AOR 2.73, 95% CI 1.51-4.95), mixed infection (AOR 3.30, 95% CI 1.44-7.55), high parasitemia (AOR 2.71, 95% CI 1.23-5.94), age ≥60 years (AOR 4.46, 95% CI 1.38-14.38), dehydration (AOR 1.74, 95% CI 1.01-3.02), and male sex (AOR 1.98, 95% CI 1.12-3.48). Nearly one-third developed AKI, highlighting need for renal assessment in malaria patients
articles.title_original: Prevalence of acute kidney injury and its associated factors among malaria patients admitted to public hospitals in the Ilu Aba Bora and Bedelle Zones of Oromia region, Southwest Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Ghana's transition from organophosphate to neonicotinoid-based indoor residual spraying (2018-2019) resulted in 26% higher malaria incidence than expected (IRR 1.26, 95% CI 1.07-2.08) across 22 districts. Regional differences: Northern (IRR 1.76, 95% CI 1.04-2.74), North East (IRR 1.83, 95% CI 1.08-3.40), and Upper East (IRR 4.26, 95% CI 2.17-8.50) showed significant increases; Upper West (IRR 1.25, 95% CI 0.90-1.70) did not. Interrupted time series analysis (2015-2022) suggests neonicotinoid IRS reduced effectiveness in northern Ghana
articles.title_original: Malaria incidence rose following the introduction of neonicotinoid-based IRS in selected districts in northern Ghana: An observational analysis.

articles.headline: Computational finite element modeling of P. falciparum merozoite invasion predicted minimum invasion force of 11 piconewtons. Merozoite invasiveness decreased with increased erythrocyte sphericity (relevant to hereditary spherocytosis). Membrane damage induced early in invasion increased invasiveness more than sustained damage throughout the process. Nanoindentation simulations indicate potential for screening invasion-blocking antimalarial drugs targeting merozoite invasion mechanics
articles.title_original: In silico analysis of the invasion mechanics and invasiveness of the Plasmodium falciparum merozoite.
articles.authors: Msosa C, Abdalrahman T, Franz T

articles.headline: EMOD modeling of Guinea's malaria burden (2025-2030) across nine epidemiological archetypes found that selective free healthcare for children <5 and differentiated diagnosis increased artemisinin-based combination therapy access index from 0.60 (Tougue) to 0.81 (Nzerekore). Combined strategy projected to prevent 13,428 complicated pediatric malaria cases (95% CI 7768-19,088). Low-incidence areas showed incidence dropping below 50 cases/1000 by 2028, demonstrating effectiveness of targeted age-based interventions for reducing severe cases
articles.title_original: Model-Based Assessment of the Effects of Selective Free Healthcare and Differentiated Diagnosis on Malaria Burden in the Republic of Guinea.

articles.headline: Case-control study of 50 women at delivery (21 with placental malaria, 29 without) showed compartment-dependent γδ T cell differentiation in placental malaria. IL-8 and IL-33 associated with increased terminally differentiated effector memory (TEMRA) phenotype frequency in peripheral blood. Enhanced mitochondrial metabolic activity occurred in predominantly terminally differentiated γδ T cells, while non-terminally differentiated cells showed glycolytic activity. Findings reveal cytokine and metabolic regulation of γδ T cell exhaustion during placental malaria
articles.title_original: Ex vivo cytokine responses and metabolic reprogramming are associated with γδ T cell differentiation in Plasmodium falciparum placental malaria.

articles.headline: Ex vivo chloroquine susceptibility testing in Gabon (2004-2024) demonstrated stepwise IC50 reduction: 113.8 nM (2004) → 46.7 nM (2009) → 15.6 nM (2017-2018) → 2.1 nM (2024). Chloroquine-sensitive pfcrt CVMNK haplotype increased from 3.6% (1/28, 2009) to 98.2% (56/57, 2024); wild-type pfmdr1 N86 rose from 23.1% (6/16, 2009) to 100% (19/19, 2024). Paired molecular and ex vivo analyses confirmed association between IC50 and CVMNK/N86, documenting reestablishment of chloroquine-sensitive P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Reversal of Chloroquine Resistance in Plasmodium falciparum in Gabon: A Phenotype-Genotype Relationship over the Last 20 Years.

articles.headline: Assessment of 23 health zones in Kinshasa (2020-2023) found malaria incidence of 30% with upward trend peaking in 2022. Rapid diagnostic testing diagnosed 83.1% of suspected cases; confirmed cases received antimalarial treatment. Long-lasting insecticidal net distribution to pregnant women: 62% (2020), 61% (2021), 45% (2022), 88% (2023). Only 4 health zones achieved ≥95% RDT diagnosis target, and 34 achieved 95% antimalarial treatment. Strategic plan objectives only partially achieved
articles.title_original: Evaluation of Performance Indicators for Malaria Control in Kinshasa from 2020 to 2023, the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

articles.headline: 2-piperazino-pyrimidine compound (FPSA) demonstrated P. falciparum-killing activity by blocking merozoite release from infected erythrocytes. After FPSA selection, five resistant clones emerged: two harboring plasmepsin X (PMX) mutations (I252V or E55G), three with PMX gene duplications. FPSA inhibited recombinant PMX proteolytic activity and PMX substrate cleavage in parasites, with transcriptomic patterns similar to WM382 (PMIX/PMX dual inhibitor). FPSA represents a novel class targeting plasmepsin X for antimalarial drug development
articles.title_original: A novel 2-piperazino-pyrimidine compound exhibits asexual antimalarial activity by targeting Plasmodium falciparum plasmepsin X.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist

articles.headline: Virtual screening of South African Natural Compounds Database against five Plasmodium species' NDH2 identified SANC00101, SANC00344, and SANC00418 with strong PfNDH2 binding affinities (-8.6, -8.8, -10.0 kcal/mol). However, molecular dynamics simulations revealed these compounds also stabilized human AIF-M1 (the closest human homologue), with Phe482 identified as critical off-target anchor. Species-specific analysis showed PkNDH2 destabilized despite favorable docking scores. Pan-Plasmodium NDH2 inhibition requires simultaneous optimization against human AIF-M1
articles.title_original: Comparative Structural and Functional Analysis of Plasmodium Type II Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide: Quinone Oxidoreductase and its Human Homologue, Apoptosis-Inducing Factor, Mitochondrion-associated 1 (AIF-M1).

articles.headline: Antibiotic-induced microbiota depletion in mice (ampicillin, neomycin, metronidazole, vancomycin) pre-infection with P. berghei ANKA resulted in ~80% avoiding experimental cerebral malaria with reduced blood-brain barrier disruption and brain lymphocyte infiltration. 16S rRNA sequencing identified specific bacterial species contributing to protection or pathogenesis. Mono-colonization of germ-free mice confirmed distinct species independently attenuated or exacerbated cerebral malaria symptoms, demonstrating bidirectional gut microbiota influence on disease severity
articles.title_original: Gut microbiota bidirectionally influences protection and severity in cerebral malaria in mice.

articles.headline: Re-evaluation of 384 malaria-suspected individuals in four Ethiopian health centers (Jul-Aug 2024) using LAMP molecular assay versus routine microscopy revealed 30.0% discordance. Discordant results comprised false-negatives (18.8%), false-positives (7.6%), and species misidentification (3.6%). McNemar's test showed statistically significant difference (p<0.001). High microscopy misdiagnosis prevalence undermine malaria control; while microscopy remains WHO-endorsed in resource-limited settings, strengthening accuracy through training and quality assurance is essential for effective case management
articles.title_original: Unveiling the overlooked burden of malaria misdiagnosis using lamp-based re-evaluation of routine malaria diagnosis in health centers: implications for public health and clinical practice in Northwest Ethiopia.

articles.headline: In 440 Ugandan children with severe falciparum malaria, P. falciparum mono-infection occurred in 74.8%, while P. falciparum with P. ovale mixed infections occurred in 22.8%. Children with P. falciparum, P. ovale, and P. malariae triple infections had dramatically higher mortality (33% vs. 7% in mono-infection; OR=7.0, 95% CI 1.8–28, p=0.005) and more respiratory distress (67% vs. 35%, p=0.08), suggesting that mixed non-falciparum parasites in severe malaria warrant broader surveillance
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium ovale and Plasmodium malariae Mixed Infections and Associated Mortality in Children with Severe Falciparum Malaria.

articles.headline: upsML is a machine-learning classifier trained on 2,530 curated P. falciparum var genes that achieves 83% accuracy for DBLα-tag sequences and 92% for full-length PfEMP1 sequences, substantially outperforming existing tools. The classifier can process 20 genomes in under one second and reveals higher frequency of internal var genes in Asian P. falciparum populations
articles.title_original: upsML: A high-accuracy machine learning classifier for predicting Plasmodium falciparum var gene upstream groups.

articles.headline: Rapid whole-genome sequencing via Illumina or Oxford Nanopore of Plasmodium DNA from 9 UK traveller isolates, analyzed with Malaria-Profiler, successfully identified drug-resistance markers and predicted geographic origins in 4 cryptic P. falciparum cases and characterized P. ovale species clusters, demonstrating that real-time WGS with AI-enhanced tools supports timely public health response and malaria elimination efforts in non-endemic settings
articles.title_original: Rapid whole genome sequencing of Plasmodium DNA from cryptic malaria cases in UK travellers provides insights into infection origins, transmission, and antimalarial resistance.

articles.headline: Reply to a cluster-randomized trial on ivermectin for malaria control; no abstract provided
articles.title_original: Ivermectin to Control Malaria - A Cluster-Randomized Trial. Reply.

articles.headline: Cluster-randomized trial evaluating ivermectin for malaria control; no abstract provided
articles.title_original: Ivermectin to Control Malaria - A Cluster-Randomized Trial.
articles.authors: Last A, Hayes R, Bradley J
