articles.headline: Reply addressing permethrin-treated baby wraps for malaria prevention in children; no abstract provided
articles.title_original: Permethrin-Treated Baby Wraps for the Prevention of Malaria in Children. Reply.
articles.authors: Boyce RM, Shook-Sa BE, Mulogo EM

articles.headline: Evaluation of permethrin-treated baby wraps for malaria prevention in children; no abstract provided
articles.title_original: Permethrin-Treated Baby Wraps for the Prevention of Malaria in Children.
articles.authors: Javelle E, de Santi VP, Briolant S

articles.headline: CRISPR-based two-step transfection strategy revealed that CLAG3 helix 44 (α-H44) is critical for plasmodial surface anion channel (PSAC) activity in malaria parasites; truncation without α-H44 abolished nutrient uptake, while two engineered mutants showed altered PSAC phenotypes and failed to expand in nutrient-restricted medium. This approach enables saturation mutagenesis to guide PSAC-targeted drug discovery
articles.title_original: A CRISPR-Based Mutagenesis Strategy for Examining CLAG3 Helix 44 Contribution to Malaria Parasite Nutrient Uptake Channels.

articles.headline: A two-track adaptive-weighted ensemble model combining classical machine learning and end-to-end convolutional networks achieved 96.35% accuracy (with reduced variance across folds) in classifying malaria parasites on thin blood smears. Optimized pairwise ensembling reduced false negatives for subtle parasites and false positives from staining artifacts, enabling diagnostic accuracy suitable for resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: CT-Malaria Detection via Adaptive-Weighted Deep Learning Models.

articles.headline: A library of 310 P. vivax peptides (MPv310) stimulated distinct CD4+ T cell responses in symptomatic-before-treatment (SY-I), symptomatic-after-treatment (SY-T), and asymptomatic (ASY) individuals. SY-I and SY-T patients showed increased CD154+ and CD69+CD154+ frequencies, while SY-T and ASY individuals had elevated IFN-γ+TNF+ responses, revealing that different P. vivax clinical forms have distinct antigen-specific immune signatures relevant to protection
articles.title_original: Different clinical forms of Plasmodium vivax infection result in unique T cell epitope-specific immune responses.

articles.headline: A narrative review of 10 Ethiopian studies identified 46 cases of congenital and neonatal malaria, with P. falciparum predominant (35 cases), P. vivax (8 cases), and mixed infections (3 cases); 40 were classified as congenital and 6 as neonatal. The review concludes that these conditions are underrecognized in Ethiopia despite the country's high malaria burden, necessitating greater clinical awareness and larger epidemiological studies
articles.title_original: Congenital and Neonatal Malaria in Ethiopia: A Narrative Review.
articles.authors: Leake Y, Hagos HH, Reta BK

articles.headline: In a Malaysian case-control study of 106 P. knowlesi cases versus 89 controls, G6PD deficiency variants were significantly less frequent in P. knowlesi cases (3.8% vs. 12.4%; OR=0.28, 95% CI 0.09–0.91, p=0.034), with Viangchan variant (871G>A) predominant at 71%, conferring substantial protection against P. knowlesi infection. Filipino β-thalassemia showed no protective association
articles.title_original: G6PD variants but not Filipino beta-thalassemia are associated with reduced risk of Plasmodium knowlesi infection in Sabah, Malaysia.

articles.headline: The PfGID E3 ubiquitin ligase complex regulates P. falciparum gametocyte development through targeted ubiquitination; deletion of PfGID components arrests development and prevents mosquito transmission by controlling ZFP36 family protein GD1 and PfDPL cryptochrome-like protein expression levels, with GD1 suppressing transcripts and PfDPL regulating male-specific protein expression essential for gametogenesis
articles.title_original: GID/CTLH E3 ligase complex control cell fate programs for sexual development of Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional vector surveillance across five Tanzanian districts (August–October 2023) collected 19,898 mosquitoes: An. funestus was the predominant vector (95.3% of malaria vectors), with An. funestus s.s. accounting for 96.3% of the group. P. falciparum infection rate was 0.5% (9/1,799) by light trap, with annual entomological inoculation rates of 242.6 (Kyerwa), 66.5 (Nyasa), and 13.0 (Muheza) infectious bites per person per year
articles.title_original: Molecular surveillance of malaria in Tanzania: Baseline surveillance of vectors that drive persistent malaria transmission in regions with varying transmission intensities.

articles.headline: Among 4,273 pregnant women across 662 Ethiopian health facilities, only 7.9% received all integrated malaria services (ITN, testing, treatment) during antenatal care. Significant factors improving uptake included ≥3 ANC visits (AOR 1.58, 95% CI 1.04–2.40), receiving an ITN during ANC (AOR 2.81, 95% CI 1.29–6.12), malaria-trained providers (AOR 4.24, 95% CI 1.80–10.00), and rural facility attendance (AOR 2.73, 95% CI 1.04–7.19), though regional disparities persisted
articles.title_original: Integrated malaria service delivery and its determinants among pregnant women in Ethiopia: Multi level analysis of 2021/22 National Service Provision Assessment Survey.

articles.headline: P. falciparum VAR2CSA-expressing parasites showed exclusive var2csa transcription with all other var genes silenced, strongly associated with depletion of repressive H3K9me3 marks and significant repositioning of var2csa relative to heterochromatic telomeric clusters on chromatin conformation capture, while 5-methylcytosine was present in all var genes but independent of transcriptional repression and switching
articles.title_original: Epigenetics and chromatin structure regulate var2csa expression and the placental-binding phenotype in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Article on P. vivax malaria (no abstract provided)
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax Malaria.
articles.authors: Menkin-Smith L, Koval K, Samandari T

articles.headline: James Young Simpson's 1835 inaugural lecture to the Royal Edinburgh Medical Society presented what was probably the first documented case of malaria pigmentation in placental tissue from a 4-month gestation sample, pioneering early academic understanding of placental inflammation and infection associated with maternal malaria
articles.title_original: Placental malaria in nineteenth-century Scotland.

articles.headline: Among 364 Gambian P. falciparum isolates, two main multi-locus drug-resistance backgrounds were identified: CVIET (62%) showing chloroquine resistance with highest IC50s when combined with pfmdr1 NFD haplotype (31.7% prevalence), and CVMNK showing reduced sensitivity to mefloquine and dihydroartemisinin. Artemisinin-based combination therapies remained effective with no validated pfkelch13 mutations detected, though reversion to chloroquine sensitivity may compromise artemisinin efficacy
articles.title_original: Chloroquine resistance transporter drives divergent multi-locus drug resistance genetic backgrounds and susceptibility in Gambian Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: The acridone compound T111 demonstrates potent activity against blood, liver, and mosquito stages of Plasmodium, achieving oral single-dose cure in rodent asexual blood-stage models and showing efficacy against relapsing parasites in non-human primate hepatocytes. T111 inhibits mitochondrial electron transport chain (distinct mechanism from other antimalarials), shows synergy with tafenoquine, and exhibits a favorable safety profile: no hemolytic toxicity and low genotoxicity and cardiotoxicity potential
articles.title_original: Potent acridone antimalarial against all three life stages of Plasmodium.

articles.headline: This 2022–2025 review evaluates electrochemical, optical, and piezoelectric biosensors for point-of-care malaria diagnosis, emphasizing signal transduction mechanisms and microfluidic integration strategies. Challenges addressed include asymptomatic carriers, clinically atypical malaria presentations, antimalarial drug resistance, insecticide resistance, and limited healthcare infrastructure in malaria-endemic regions requiring affordable, field-deployable diagnostic technologies
articles.title_original: Potential biomarkers and their recent applications ofr point-of-care biosensors for the diagnosis of malaria.

articles.headline: A multiplexed isothermal nucleic acid amplification assay (iso-IMRS) detected P. falciparum and T. vaginalis at 100 copies/μL in processed human urine, providing noninvasive, multiplexed detection for malaria in pregnancy where placental sequestration causes underdetection by microscopy or rapid diagnostic tests. The assay completed testing within a typical prenatal visit timeframe without thermocycling, though neat urine processing requirements may limit point-of-care deployment
articles.title_original: Multiplexed Isothermal Amplification Assay for the Detection of Plasmodium falciparum and Trichomonas vaginalis in Urine.

articles.headline: Hotspot-based integrated serosurveillance across four coastal regions of Guyana using multiplex bead assay among 300 individuals per site detected elevated lymphatic filariasis seropositivity (Wb123, Bm14 reactivity) consistent with previous transmission reports and higher P. falciparum and P. vivax seropositivity among adults. Seroprotection was highest for measles and rubella in children/older adults with gaps in young adults, and minimal for tetanus and diphtheria across all ages
articles.title_original: Hotspot-based integrated serosurveillance of neglected tropical diseases, malaria, and vaccine-preventable diseases in urban Guyana.

articles.headline: This protocol describes development of the first core outcome set (COS) for severe malaria treatment trials through systematic review (326 records screened, 5 eligible RCTs from 2020–2024 in Africa and Asia), qualitative interviews with 26 parents/caregivers, a 2-round Delphi survey targeting 60–80 multistakeholder participants, and online consensus meeting. Outcome measurement instrument selection and final COS dissemination strategy are expected by September 2026
articles.title_original: Synthesis and Standardization of Outcomes in Severe Malaria Treatment Trials: Protocol for the Development of a Core Outcome Set (the COSSMaT Study).

articles.headline: A 10-year interrupted time-series study (5 surveys, ~2,000 participants each, 2012-2022) in Ghana tracked asymptomatic Plasmodium infections after IRS and SMC+LLIN interventions. P. malariae prevalence fell from 13.7% (2012) to 1.4% (2015 post-IRS), then rebounded to 2.9% (2017) and 7.4% (2020); P. ovale fell 5.7%→0.4% then rebounded 4.0% and 2.6%. Both interventions reduced minor species infections, but SMC alone was less effective than IRS in older age groups after IRS discontinuation
articles.title_original: The dynamics of asymptomatic Plasmodium spp. infections following 10 years of malaria control interventions in Northern Sahelian Ghana.

articles.headline: Laboratory characterization of P. falciparum PfRESA3 merozoite protein showed it exports to inner RBC membrane and associates with cytoskeleton; full-length recombinant PfRESA3 stimulated human HsHSPA8 ATPase activity and enhanced malate dehydrogenase refolding, suggesting it functions as a co-chaperone hijacking host proteins during early erythrocyte infection
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum ring-infected erythrocyte surface antigen 3 (PfRESA3) is a cytoskeleton-interacting protein and potential co-chaperone.

articles.headline: A review of human immune responses to blood-stage Plasmodium infections and parasite immune evasion mechanisms
articles.title_original: Cellular and molecular interactions underlying the peripheral immune responses to blood-stage malaria infections.

articles.headline: A mixed-methods rapid formative assessment (5-year retrospective case analysis 2016-2020; 16 FGDs + 17 KIIs with 116 respondents) documented 432 military malaria cases (18% of 2,425 provincial total) in Thailand, with 96% diagnosed at MoPH facilities (41% indigenous, 40% imported). Qualitative findings identified key opportunities to strengthen military-civilian coordination on prevention, diagnosis, and follow-up
articles.title_original: Combatting malaria together: key findings from a rapid formative assessment on Royal Thai Army malaria practices and military-civilian collaboration in Sisaket Province, Thailand.

articles.headline: Testing 11 solid substrates for Beauveria bassiana production against Anopheles stephensi (4 replicates, 20 mosquitoes each): white rice yielded highest conidia (6.8×10⁵ CFU/g) with 37% germination at 8 hours and 54.3% at 16 hours, causing 90% mosquito mortality (median survival 5 days, χ²=200.32, p<0.01) on cement and 86.25% on mud; broken wheat showed 56% germination
articles.title_original: Effect of solid substrates on the production and germination of Beauveria bassiana (Balsamo) Vuillemin, and their infectivity against the malaria vector, Anopheles stephensi (Diptera: Culicidae).
articles.authors: Siddaramegowda R, Chalageri VH, Eapen A

articles.headline: A retrospective study of 1,262 pregnant women attending antenatal care in Madagascar (2021) found 64.9% received ≥1 IPTp-SP dose but only 49.9% completed ≥3 doses; malaria RDT positivity was 21.1% among 289 symptomatic women (4.8% overall). Adequate IPTp-SP coverage (≥3 doses) was associated with living <5km from facility (aOR=1.54, 95% CI 1.14-2.08) and ≥4 ANC visits (aOR=2.38, 95% CI 1.76-3.21)
articles.title_original: Prevalence of malaria during pregnancy and coverage of intermittent preventive treatment with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine in urban Amboasary-Sud, Southern Madagascar.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional surveys in 5 Tanzanian villages during dry (Sept-Nov 2021) and rainy (Feb-May 2022) seasons mapped 2,824 aquatic habitats, 27% positive for An. funestus. Year-round presence in river streams, ground pools, and ditches, but expanded habitat use in wet season (spring-fed wells, rice fields, dug pits), reflecting the species' ecological adaptability to seasonal habitat shifts
articles.title_original: Seasonal variation in aquatic habitat availability and use by the malaria vector, Anopheles funestus.

articles.headline: Ex-vivo susceptibility testing of field isolates from Ghana and Mali showed P. ovale had decreased DHA susceptibility (median IC50=3.962 nM, p=0.0371 vs P. falciparum) but comparable cabamiquine susceptibility (median IC50=0.9441 nM); P. malariae less susceptible to cabamiquine (median IC50=3.915 nM, p=0.001) but not to DHA. Both non-falciparum species remained overall susceptible to cabamiquine
articles.title_original: Antimalarial cabamiquine inhibits blood-stage of different field isolates of Plasmodium species.

articles.headline: Study examining malaria incidence, risk factors, hematological and immunological parameters, and fractional mathematical modeling. No abstract provided; quantitative results unavailable from title
articles.title_original: Incidence and risk factors of malaria with hematological and immunological evaluation and integrable analysis of a fractional mathematical model.

articles.headline: Characterization of germline-specific promoters (spo11, vasa1) in A. gambiae enabled development of Sex Distorter Male Drive (SDMD) technology producing male-biased progeny via super-Mendelian inheritance. This approach offers potential for resistance-resilient vector population suppression without traditional target-site resistance challenges
articles.title_original: Sex distorter male drive for resistance-resilient population control of the human malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.

articles.headline: Retrospective study of 165 adult severe malaria patients (April 2023-April 2025; 57% male, mean age 34.4 years) found 91.5% recovered, 13.94% referred, zero deaths. Referral was significantly associated with hyperparasitemia (AOR=2.083), severe malarial anemia (AOR=3.052, 95% CI 2.066-13.232), and significant bleeding (AOR=8.012, 95% CI 2.066-21.232), highlighting importance of early detection
articles.title_original: "Referral patterns and associated factors among adult patients admitted with Severe Malaria at Shedeho Meket Primary Hospital, Northeast Ethiopia".

articles.headline: Editorial on dissecting malaria protective immunity acquired through natural infection and/or vaccination
articles.title_original: Editorial: Dissecting malaria protective immunity: acquired by natural infection and/or vaccination.

articles.headline: In mice, intravenously inoculated P. yoelii and P. berghei sporozoites were retained in the splenic parenchyma for ≥3 hours post-inoculation, as detected by live fluorescent imaging. The findings suggest the spleen plays a role beyond simply trapping/destroying sporozoites in transit from skin to liver
articles.title_original: Retention and survival of Plasmodium sporozoites in the spleen.

articles.headline: P. yoelii infection in mice caused significant accumulation of lung type 2 conventional dendritic cells (cDC2) that was mediated by suppressed autophagy via the TLR3/7-STAT3-IRF4 pathway. JAK inhibitor JSI-124 nearly abolished this effect, and IRF4-deficient mice showed restored autophagy, reduced cDC2 accumulation, and attenuated lung inflammatory damage, identifying STAT3 inhibition as a potential malaria-associated lung injury therapeutic target
articles.title_original: Plasmodium yoelii infection induces lung injury by modulating type 2 conventional dendritic cells autophagy via the STAT3-IRF4 signaling.

articles.headline: P. falciparum-infected RBC exposure of human tonsil and spleen organoids significantly increased Vδ2+ γδ T cell frequencies with clonally expanded, cytotoxic phenotypes expressing elevated granzymes and interferon-stimulated genes. Validation in Ugandan children with asymptomatic parasitemia confirmed expanded Vδ2+ T cells with enhanced cytotoxic potential compared to uninfected controls, though malaria pre-exposure did not significantly alter subsequent influenza vaccine responses
articles.title_original: Cytotoxic Vδ2+ T cell subsets expand in response to malaria in human tonsil and spleen organoids.

articles.headline: Chemical screening identified TC-E 5003, a protein arginine methyltransferase inhibitor, as potent against multiple P. falciparum developmental stages in vitro, likely targeting PfPRMT1 with distinct structure-activity relationships demonstrated by TC-E 5003 analogs, opening a novel pathway for multistage antiplasmodial drug development
articles.title_original: Protein Arginine Methyltransferase Inhibitors Target Multiple Stages of Plasmodium falciparum Parasites In Vitro.

articles.headline: Among 245 P. vivax-positive cases confirmed by microscopy in southwestern Ethiopia, qPCR revealed 87.7% were Pv, 3.7% P. falciparum, 3.3% P. malariae, 2.4% P. ovale, and 2.8% mixed infections, demonstrating significant diagnostic misidentification of non-vivax species. The findings underscore the need to integrate P. malariae and P. ovale into Ethiopia's malaria elimination program and strengthen species identification training and sensitive diagnostic capacity
articles.title_original: The hidden malaria: Misidentification of Plasmodium malariae and Plasmodium ovale in low-transmission setting targeted for elimination in Ethiopia.

articles.headline: In a 28-day WHO-protocol efficacy study of 496 children (6 months–5 years) with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria in Sierra Leone, Kaplan-Meier uncorrected efficacy was 89.5–96.1% for artemether-lumefantrine (AL) and 92.0–94.5% for artesunate-amodiaquine (ASAQ); PCR-corrected efficacy reached 100% for AL and 99.1–100% for ASAQ. No validated Pfk13 mutations were detected, confirming both AL and ASAQ remain effective treatments in Sierra Leone
articles.title_original: Efficacy and safety of artemether-lumefantrine (AL) and artesunate-amodiaquine (ASAQ) for the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria among children 6-59 months in three sentinel sites of Sierra Leone, 2021-2022.

articles.headline: Evaluation of malaria microscopy in 29 rural Kenyan health facilities across 1,494 blood smears revealed 91% concordance between routine facility microscopy and expert re-reading, with facility sensitivity 76% and specificity 92%, resulting in 24% undertreatment and 8% overtreatment. Accuracy was markedly lower at parasitemia 1–100 p/μL (OR=0.12) but higher at >10,000 p/μL (OR=6.08), highlighting the need for continued quality assurance despite overall good performance
articles.title_original: Malaria microscopy evaluation and quality assurance in rural clinics of Rarieda and Alego Usonga sub-counties of Siaya County, western Kenya.

articles.headline: Analysis of Malaria Indicator Survey data from under-five children in four West African countries identified country-specific determinants of delayed malaria treatment: in Senegal, child sex (AOR=0.41), maternal wealth (AOR=0.037), and rural residence (AOR=1.82); in Nigeria, maternal wealth (AOR=0.54) and education (AOR=0.49); in Niger, rural residence (AOR=1.24); and in Mali, maternal age (AOR=0.6). Targeted interventions addressing these factors could reduce malaria mortality in affected regions
articles.title_original: Determinants of late malaria treatment among under-five children in West Africa.
articles.authors: Shittu SB, Shittu RO, Adedini SA

articles.headline: CRISPR-Cas9 conditional knockdown of PfVPS4 (ESCRT complex AAA-ATPase) in P. falciparum reduced asexual parasite progeny by 84% and gametocyte conversion rate by 46%, with biochemical analysis showing optimal ATPase activity at pH 7.5 and 37°C (Vmax 2.23±0.053 U/mg, Km 0.086 mM), enhanced by the PfVta1 cofactor. These results establish PfVPS4 as essential for blood-stage replication and sexual conversion, making it an attractive antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: PfVPS4, an ESCRT AAA-ATPase, is essential for asexual proliferation and gametocyte sexual conversion in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: During World War II, media campaigns including posters and films featuring humor, command authority, and appeals to patriotism were deployed alongside military and medical efforts to combat severe malaria casualties affecting US troop readiness. Contributors included later-famous artists like Dr. Seuss, whose cartoons promoted soldier compliance with effective but imperfect mosquito control and chemoprophylaxis measures
articles.title_original: Attention for Compliance: Visual Media in Second World War Anti-Malaria Efforts.

articles.headline: Mixed-methods assessment of malaria elimination in Tha Song Yang District, Thailand, revealed that adherence to the 1-3-7 elimination framework reached 96% for Day 1 and 100% for Day 3 activities by 2022 but declined sharply to 69% for Day 7, while household surveys showed 75.8% malaria knowledge and 94.6% positive prevention attitudes. Malaria persistence was driven primarily by systemic constraints—short-term migration, workforce shortages, reduced funding, and geographic barriers—managed through adaptive local responses including digital tools and committed leadership
articles.title_original: Challenges and responses of malaria elimination in a high-endemic area along the Thai-Myanmar border: A health systems perspective.

articles.headline: The mini chamber test, a novel bioassay for evaluating pyrethroid-chlorfenapyr insecticide-treated nets, achieved 98–99% 72-hour mortality with overnight exposure regardless of mosquito number (10–30) or chamber orientation, and achieved 95% mortality compared to the gold-standard tunnel test's 70% with equivalent overnight exposure. The method shows promise for chlorfenapyr ITN bioefficacy testing in settings lacking tunnel test capacity, with potential application to durability monitoring
articles.title_original: The mini chamber test: a novel bioassay for bioefficacy testing of chlorfenapyr treated nets used in malaria control.

articles.headline: In 727 malaria cases from Brazilian Amazon, molecular diagnosis revealed P. vivax frequently missed by microscopy in mixed infections. Pvmdr1 amplification detected in 60% of Roraima isolates (versus nearly all single-copy in western Amazon), rising to >70% after 2020. Inadequate or unintended drug exposure may drive selection of reduced-susceptibility parasites
articles.title_original: Hidden Infections, Emerging Resistance: Underdiagnosed Plasmodium vivax and multidrug resistance-1 (pvmdr1) gene Amplification in the Guiana Shield.

articles.headline: Among 89,432 Nigerians in 2018 national survey, current/recent P. falciparum prevalence was 38.3% (95% CI 37.2–39.5%) with seroprevalence 48–87%. Age 5–14 years showed highest odds of active infection; wealthiest category had 5-fold lower odds of active infection and 10-fold lower exposure odds versus poorest; urban living reduced odds 30% (active) and 60% (exposure). Heterogeneous malaria epidemiology requires serological surveillance
articles.title_original: Risk factors and geospatial associations with Plasmodium falciparum malaria infection and IgG seroprevalence: Nigeria, 2018.

articles.headline: Among 58 imported malaria patients at a German hospital (2012–2023), median age was 37.5 years, 31.6% required ICU care, and survival rate was 98%. P. falciparum accounted for 96% of cases; only 17% had taken prophylaxis. Delayed diagnosis contributes to preventable deaths from severe imported malaria in nonendemic settings
articles.title_original: Malaria: 11-Year Experience With Imported Cases at a German University Hospital and Epidemiological Trends Amid the COVID-19 Pandemic.

articles.headline: In South African provinces (2000–2024), consistent minimum-temperature warming and rainfall extremes occurred. Same-year humidity positively associated with malaria incidence in KwaZulu-Natal and Mpumalanga; lag-1 rainfall significant in Limpopo. Post-shock migration (April–May 2022 floods) temporally aligned with malaria tail years, elevated rainfall, and increased minimum temperatures. Climatic extremes and mobility-integrated surveillance essential for elimination planning
articles.title_original: Malaria at the margins: climatic extremes, migration, and urban informal settlement risk in South Africa.
articles.authors: Matamanda SH, Musakwa W, Dzomba A

articles.headline: Three avian Plasmodium species (P. circumflexum, P. vaughani, P. relictum) from wild birds were transferred to Eurasian siskins and monitored for 185 days. All recipient birds susceptible; no tissue stages detected, suggesting persistence via erythrocytic merogony. First detection of Matryoshka RNA virus (MaRNAV-9) associated with P. circumflexum. Study provides baseline on avian malaria diagnostics and persistence mechanisms
articles.title_original: New experimental insights into the biology of avian malaria parasites.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling across 108 low- and middle-income countries (2000–2023) estimated HTM scale-up averted 6.9 billion outpatient visits (95% UI [4.4, 10.5]) and 3.9 billion inpatient bed-days (95% UI [2.5, 5.9]), equaling US$135 billion in averted costs (95% UI [71, 250]). Greatest reductions in sub-Saharan Africa and East Asia/Pacific. Benefits represent median 4.4% of hospital bed capacity and 1.6% of government health spending in 2023
articles.title_original: The benefits of investments to combat HIV, tuberculosis, and malaria for primary healthcare from 2000 to 2023: An economic modeling analysis.

articles.headline: MosQNet-SA, a convolutional-attention network trained on 1,000 images of three mosquito species (Aedes, Anopheles, Culex), achieved 99.42% accuracy with 10-fold fewer parameters than existing approaches. Explainability enhanced via Saliency, GradCAM, LIME, and Kernel SHAP. RESTful API implementation demonstrates practical applicability for real-time mosquito classification and disease risk mapping in public health surveillance
articles.title_original: MosQNet-SA: Explainable convolutional-attention network for mosquito classification with application as a RESTful API for dengue and malaria risk mapping.

articles.headline: CRISPR-Cas9 editing introduced three globally dominant K13 mutations (C580Y, F446I, R561H) into Bangladeshi P. falciparum isolates. C580Y and R561H conferred marked artemisinin resistance in both strains; R561H showed extreme resistance in resistant background (RSA 30.09% ± 1.9%), asexual growth advantage (5.1% per generation), and highest post-treatment recovery. Findings demonstrate CHT parasites genetically primed to sustain high-level artemisinin resistance
articles.title_original: Functional assessment of prevalent kelch13 mutations reveals high-level artemisinin resistance potential in Bangladeshi Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: In rodent malaria model, IFN-γ signaling in T cells plays key role in malaria-associated lung pathology. Disruption of IFN-γ signaling reduced parasite load in lungs and attenuated pathology by enhancing T cell–monocyte interaction, increasing CD8+ Ly6C+ proinflammatory monocytes with elevated phagocytic capacity. Findings highlight complex immune network and importance of IFN-γ signaling in T cell–monocyte interaction during Plasmodium infection
articles.title_original: Comprehensive immune profiling reveals IFN-γ signaling in T cells mediates parasite phagocytosis in a rodent malaria model.

articles.headline: Among 39 evaluable P. falciparum isolates from UK travelers (2012–2023) across 15 countries, parasites carrying novel/established pfk13 variants showed reduced in vitro artemisinin susceptibility (isolates from Kenya, Uganda, Zambia, Namibia). Six of ten isolates least susceptible to lumefantrine were from Uganda. Both pfk13-dependent and -independent partial artemisinin resistance detected in multiple African settings
articles.title_original: Novel Pfk13 and Pfubp1 genotypes in African Plasmodium falciparum isolates exhibiting reduced susceptibility to the antimalarials artemisinin and lumefantrine.

articles.headline: Among 100 P. falciparum samples from Cameroon (2022–2023), 4CAST sequencing successfully genotyped csp in 35%, identifying 22 haplotypes (He = 0.908; π = 0.021). Vaccine-matched haplotype present in 20% of genotyped infections; csp TH2/TH3 epitopes showed signs of balancing selection. Within-participant haplotype diversity highest for ama1 (mean 2.8), sera2 (2.1), csp (1.3). High csp diversity may influence RTS,S vaccine efficacy
articles.title_original: Haplotype diversity of Plasmodium falciparum circumsporozoite protein prior to malaria vaccine implementation in Kaelé Health District, Cameroon, 2022-2023.

articles.headline: In phase 1 trial of MSP2 vaccine (malaria-naïve adults) and phase 2b RTS,S trial (African children), IgG magnitude correlated strongly with antibody avidity but avidity was not a major determinant of Fc-mediated functional activity (complement fixation, Fcγ-receptor binding, opsonic phagocytosis). IgG magnitude was stronger predictor of function; avidity showed only weak, antigen-specific association with antibody longevity
articles.title_original: Relationship between antibody avidity, Fc-mediated functional activity and longevity of malaria vaccine responses in clinical trials.

articles.headline: P. vivax parasites with pvdbp gene amplification evade anti-PvDBP antibodies in vivo. In a 21-month longitudinal cohort of 657 participants across 9 Cambodian villages, 87% (40/46) with high binding-inhibitory antibodies harbored multi-copy parasites versus 38% (193/514) without such antibodies (p<0.0001). Multi-copy parasites were more frequent in asymptomatic carriers, demonstrating that pvdbp duplication protects parasites from naturally acquired immunity
articles.title_original: PvDBP gene amplification is associated with functional immune evasion by Plasmodium vivax in vivo.

articles.headline: Hospitalized severe malaria cases in the US (1,466 cases adjudicated, 2012–2018) were geographically concentrated but predictable: 95.8% occurred within 2 hours driving time of a level-1 trauma center, with highest concentrations in New York, Maryland, Texas, DC, and Pennsylvania. This heterogeneous but consistent distribution enables targeted intravenous artesunate supply planning at priority facilities
articles.title_original: Mapping Hospitals That Treat Severe Malaria Cases in the United States: Evidence to Inform Health Systems Strategies for Intravenous Artesunate Supply.

articles.headline: Narrative review of artesunate treatment for severe malaria in non-endemic countries, addressing clinical decision-making around timing, location, administration methods, and current implementation status and challenges
articles.title_original: Artesunate Treatment of Severe Malaria in Nonendemic Countries: When, Where, How, and What Now?

articles.headline: Among 239 febrile children at 39 Kenyan pharmacies, 69% received malaria diagnostics; 59% tested positive; 78% of confirmed malaria cases received appropriate antimalarial treatment; 88% of treated children fully recovered at follow-up. Pharmacies demonstrated high guideline adherence with malaria-treatment concordance, though care quality varied by caregiver education and provider experience
articles.title_original: Guideline-concordant care and outcomes for pediatric malaria cases: descriptive evidence from pharmacy-based fever management in Kenya.

articles.headline: Dak Lak province, Vietnam reported 2,147 malaria cases (2017–2022): 58.2% indigenous and 39.1% imported cases, with dramatic decline approaching zero since 2021. Cases peaked October–November and were significantly associated with temperature, rainfall, male gender, ethnic minority status, and high-endemicity residence, requiring sustained prevention targeting mobile populations
articles.title_original: Malaria situation in a highly endemic province in the Central Highlands of Vietnam: a retrospective analysis.

articles.headline: Systematic review found elevated renal and endothelial injury biomarkers (serum creatinine, cystatin C, endothelial markers) consistently associated with increased risk of malaria-associated acute kidney injury complications and mortality. These biomarkers enable earlier detection and improved risk stratification beyond traditional late markers, supporting their use for clinical management
articles.title_original: Kidney and endothelial injury biomarkers as predictors of complications and mortality in patients with malaria: a systematic review.

articles.headline: Mass drug administration with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine in Mozambique (target ~59,271 people, December 2022–February 2023) achieved 96.2% acceptance (607/631 eligible), 90.2% operational coverage, and 86.0%–96.0% staff commitment, but reached only 73.9% programmatic coverage, falling short of WHO's 80% target. Community and staff engagement remain critical for scaling MDA campaigns
articles.title_original: Operational performance of a programmatic mass drug administration campaign for malaria in southern Mozambique: a comprehensive mixed-methods evaluation of implementation outcomes.

articles.headline: Among 1,004 febrile children screened in Busia and Kisumu Counties, Kenya, 21.4% (215) had P. falciparum alone, 8.9% (90) had dengue fever alone, and 7.5% (75) had co-infections, with Busia showing highest P. falciparum prevalence (23.4%) and Kisumu highest dengue (12.6%). Dengue is a significant but under-recognized contributor to non-malarial febrile illness in malaria-endemic regions
articles.title_original: Unmasking non-malarial mosquito-borne infections among febrile children in malaria-endemic regions of western Kenya.

articles.headline: Genome-wide association study in 3,928 African children identified new African-specific loci: GTF3C5 (rs2905094) and DUP4 (rs552439837) confer protection against severe malaria; CHCHD7/SDR16C5 (rs73596248) reduces Klebsiella pneumoniae and Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia risk. European polygenic scores showed limited transferability to African populations, emphasizing the need for population-specific genetic insights
articles.title_original: African-specific genetic loci determine iron status and risk of severe malaria and bacteremia in African children.

articles.headline: Policy perspective on the critical role of public awareness and community health education in supporting successful malaria vaccine rollout and adoption in Sudan
articles.title_original: Public awareness and education: key to the success of the malaria vaccine rollout in Sudan.

articles.headline: Narrative review of biological malaria control approaches including entomopathogenic fungi, genetic techniques (sterile insect technique, genetically modified mosquitoes), natural predators, and plant-derived agents as alternatives to conventional insecticides, addressing insecticide resistance and environmental sustainability challenges
articles.title_original: Exploring the Scope and Status of Biological Interventions in Malaria Control.

articles.headline: In 2,397 Tanzanian children (30,079 monthly visits over 18 months), malaria reduced height velocity 0.14 cm/month (95% CI: −0.23 to −0.05); respiratory infections reduced height 0.10 cm/month and weight 37 g/month; diarrhea reduced height 0.26 cm/month and weight 120 g/month. These three infections significantly impair childhood growth, with diarrhea having the largest impact
articles.title_original: Associations of Malaria, Diarrhea, and Respiratory Infections with Growth Velocity of Children in Tanzania.

articles.headline: Using mutagenesis and redox buffering in Dd2 and 7G8 P. falciparum strains, researchers demonstrated that chloroquine transport by PfCRT is regulated by redox potential. Disulfide bonds at C289/C312 and C301/C309 are required for full CQ transport activity, while additional cysteines (C101, C139, C171, C328) modulate transport. Molecular dynamics showed large conformational changes in the drug-binding domain upon SH/S-S interconversion, proposing a redox-active mechanism in the parasite digestive vacuole
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Chloroquine Resistance Transporter (PfCRT) Is a Redox-Dependent Drug Transporter.

articles.headline: Phase I/IIa dose-escalation trial of ChAdOx1 LS2 and MVA LS2 vectored vaccines expressing liver-stage antigens LSA1 and LSAP2 in 13 UK adults (3 receiving low-dose, 10 receiving prime-boost) versus 6 unvaccinated controls, with controlled human malaria infection by mosquito bite. All vaccinated volunteers became infected (median 13 days to diagnosis, identical to controls' 13.25 days) despite high CD8+ T-cell responses (median 4473 SFC/10⁶ PBMC), indicating the novel antigen combination did not confer protection from infection
articles.title_original: Phase I/IIa study to assess the safety, immunogenicity and efficacy of ChAdOx1-MVA vectored vaccines expressing a novel liver-stage malaria dual antigen LS2 by sporozoite challenge in malaria-naïve adults.

articles.headline: Scoping review of 28 peer-reviewed sources (1993–2024) on insecticide resistance in Odisha malaria vectors assessed resistance using WHO mortality criteria (>98% = susceptible, 90–97% = possible, <90% = confirmed). An. culicifacies showed confirmed DDT resistance in 26 districts, malathion resistance in 17, and deltamethrin resistance in 10. The emergence of multiple insecticide resistance in this dominant vector requires novel insecticides and integrated management strategies
articles.title_original: Mapping the Insecticide Resistance Landscape of Malaria Vectors in Odisha, India (1993-2024): A Scoping Review.

articles.headline: Narrative review examining malaria-HIV coinfection epidemiology, risk factors, and outcomes in sub-Saharan Africa, highlighting diagnostic limitations, treatment interactions, and health system constraints. Evidence indicates closer coordination of malaria and HIV services may improve patient outcomes. Review identifies persistent gaps in surveillance, clinical management, and integrated control strategies requiring targeted research and policy intervention
articles.title_original: Insights into Malaria and HIV Co-infection: Trends and Impact in Sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: Anopheles coluzzii larvae sampled from 12 Nigerian states and screened for 2La inversion karyotypes showed clear geographic structuring aligned with eco-climatic gradients. The 2La/2La homokaryotype predominated in hot, arid northern zones; 2La+/2La+ in humid southern forests; heterokaryotypes at intermediate frequencies in central Nigeria with peak heterozygosity. These climatic-driven inversion frequencies suggest the 2La polymorphism influences vector survival, insecticide response, and persistence relevant to malaria control
articles.title_original: Spatial eco-climatic gradients drive adaptive 2La chromosomal inversion polymorphism in Anopheles coluzzii, a major malaria vector in Nigeria.

articles.headline: Narrative review and case studies from Uganda and Mozambique evaluating housing-based structural modifications (screened windows, sealed eaves, elevated flooring, insecticide-treated barriers) and improved sanitation for malaria and cholera prevention. Integrated community approaches combining structural modifications and behavioral changes are low-cost, culturally acceptable, and easily implementable strategies complementary to large-scale control programs, though effectiveness varies by setting and depends on affordability and local adaptation
articles.title_original: Household and community-based approaches to malaria and cholera prevention: A narrative review with case studies from Uganda and Mozambique.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional assessment of avian malaria prevalence in two mosquito species across urban and peri-urban habitats found 0% infection in urban areas but 2.53% prevalence in native Culex pipiens from peri-urban sites, demonstrating urbanization's significant influence on parasite transmission dynamics
articles.title_original: Urbanization and Vector Species Shape Avian Malaria Prevalence in Mosquitoes.

articles.headline: Case report of a 37-year-old woman from eastern Democratic Republic of Congo presenting with probable Loa loa-associated encephalopathy after ivermectin administration for loiasis. Blood smear revealed concurrent Plasmodium trophozoites and Loa loa microfilariae; cerebrospinal fluid analysis confirmed Loa loa microfilariae. Following albendazole and supportive care, she achieved complete neurological recovery with microfilarial clearance after 19 days hospitalization, highlighting ivermectin's neurological risks in Loa loa-endemic settings with concurrent infections
articles.title_original: Probable Loa loa-associated encephalopathy following ivermectin administration in a patient with malaria infection: A case report from the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

articles.headline: Narrative review (2007–2025) of malaria in pregnancy (MiP) in DRC conflict zones reports prevalence of 17–40% with 26% pooled asymptomatic estimate creating silent transmission. Early parasitemia linked to reduced umbilical artery resistance and fetal growth restriction. Armed conflict caused net and intermittent preventive treatment stockouts; innovations like R21/Matrix-M vaccine offer potential but require strengthened health systems and sustained global funding
articles.title_original: Time to Act; Let Us Unite to Eliminate Malaria Among Pregnant Women in War Zone From Eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo: A Review.

articles.headline: Stochastic spatiotemporal agent-based model of P. falciparum transmission integrating mosquito climatic suitability and household location data in Vietnam. Simulations showed distributing interventions to a broad range of households (lower protection per household) reduced malaria prevalence more effectively than concentrating strong protection on limited households, informing optimal intervention distribution strategies
articles.title_original: Spatio-temporal agent-based modelling of malaria.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 21 observational studies reporting P. falciparum-Schistosoma coinfection prevalence in sub-Saharan African children (0–17 years). Pooled P. falciparum-S. haematobium coinfection was 10.0% (95% CI 6.0–14.0%, I²=98.0%); P. falciparum-S. mansoni was 13.0% (95% CI 6.0–24.0%, I²=99.28%) with marked regional variation (East Africa 25.0% vs Central/West Africa 2–3%). Diagnostic methods significantly affected estimates, with microscopy-based approaches yielding higher prevalence than PCR-only methods
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium falciparum coinfection with Schistosoma haematobium and Schistosoma mansoni among children in sub-Saharan Africa: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Prospective observational study of 97 Malawian children (6 months–12 years) with WHO-defined cerebral malaria found 28% met respiratory distress criteria. Despite elevated median lactate (7.0 mmol/L), median pH remained 7.41; only 12% had isolated metabolic acidosis while 33% showed primary respiratory alkalosis. Lung ultrasound revealed subcentimeter consolidations in 88% of children. Respiratory distress was not associated with increased mortality, suggesting malarial pneumonopathy—a novel mechanism involving pulmonary microvascular obstruction and central respiratory dysregulation—rather than metabolic acidosis alone
articles.title_original: Malarial pneumonopathy in Malawian children with cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Pharmacovigilance monitoring of RTS,S/AS01E vaccine in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi found no new safety concerns or confirmation of Phase 3 adverse event signals in serious adverse events following immunization. Implementation through routine surveillance systems was successful but hampered by underreporting, data delays, and poor report quality, highlighting the need for sustained investment in pharmacovigilance capacity and health worker training in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Safety monitoring of the RTS,S/AS01(E) malaria vaccine: experiences and lessons from routine pharmacovigilance in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi.

articles.headline: Bayesian spatial joint modelling of malaria incidence and Anopheles vector abundance in 8 southwestern Benin communes identified hotspots on Lake Ahémé and specific districts. Contrary to assumptions, An. funestus showed stronger spatial correlation with malaria (119.95 km²) than An. gambiae s.l. (89.90 km²), with 67.29 km² showing synergistic effects, suggesting species-targeted rather than universal vector interventions are needed
articles.title_original: Identification of malaria hotspots in southwestern Benin through spatial joint modelling of malaria incidence and vector abundance.

articles.headline: Retrospective study of 74 children (median age 9.0 years) with malaria in Milan 2004–2024 (62.2% P. falciparum, 27% P. vivax) found severe malaria in 23% with no difference between non-immune and presumed-immune groups (p=0.783). Hyponatremia (ρ=−0.284, p=0.020) and elevated CRP (ρ=0.256, p=0.033) predicted worse clinical outcomes more reliably than geographic proxies of immunity, and intravenous artesunate replaced quinine as standard therapy post-2015
articles.title_original: Pediatric malaria in a non-endemic European setting: clinical patterns, laboratory markers, and the ambiguous role of immunity.

articles.headline: Hospital-based cross-sectional study of 102 children with P. falciparum complicated malaria in eastern India found hyperuricemia in 80.4%, elevated liver enzymes (SGOT 72.5%, SGPT 83.3%, bilirubin 65.7%), and adverse outcomes in 29.4%. SGOT, SGPT, and bilirubin levels significantly associated with adverse outcomes (p=0.011, 0.004, 0.004 respectively), but serum uric acid was not (p=0.115), suggesting hepatic markers are more reliable severity predictors
articles.title_original: Serum Uric Acid as a Biomarker of Disease Severity in Children With Complicated Malaria: A Cross-Sectional Study From Eastern India.

articles.headline: Review synthesizing WHO World Malaria Report 2024 and Global Burden of Disease estimates on global malaria epidemiology, transmission modes (Anopheles mosquitoes, transfusion, congenital), and control strategies. Identifies emerging challenges including artemisinin resistance, climate change, and limited funding in low- and middle-income countries; recommends global, multifactorial, locally-tailored approaches with strengthened health education to achieve WHO 2030 malaria elimination goals
articles.title_original: Malaria in the 21st Century: Global Disease Burden, Epidemiological Insights, and Strategic Control Approaches.

articles.headline: Molecular surveillance using ultra-sensitive qRT-PCR on 7,127 adult dried blood spots from 2019–20 Rwanda Demographics and Health Survey detected P. falciparum prevalence of 7.7% (95% CI 6.8–8.7%), predominantly low-density infections (median 7.3 parasites/μL), representing a 53% decrease relative to 2014–15 survey despite rising artemisinin-resistance mutations. Male sex, lower wealth, lower education, and lower elevation were significant risk factors, demonstrating effectiveness of national malaria control efforts
articles.title_original: Molecular surveillance of Falciparum malaria in Rwanda: Shifts in parasite prevalence and risk factors between the 2014-15 and 2019-20 Rwanda Demographics and Health Surveys.

articles.headline: Whole-genome sequencing and gene editing of artemisinin-resistant Cambodian Plasmodium falciparum Kelch13 mutants exposed to artefenomel (OZ439) identified a novel K13 A212T mutation; the R539T+A212T double mutation confers OZ439 resistance, also observed in C580Y+A212T parasites. Resistant parasites showed increased glutathione, nucleotide, and aspartate-glutamate metabolism, indicating resistance operates through enhanced oxidative stress tolerance rather than altered drug activation
articles.title_original: Double Mutations in Plasmodium falciparum Kelch13 drive resistance to next-generation artemisinin derivatives in malaria parasites.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 42 studies across 9 Asia Pacific countries (435,454 malaria cases) found pooled prevalence of imported malaria at 89.0% (95% CI 75.9–95.5), with higher prevalence in males (94%), P. falciparum cases (57.0%), and cases from Africa (64.3%); occupational variation ranged from 53.0% in labourers to 40.0% in farmers. Pre-elimination phase countries showed 84.6% imported cases, emphasizing critical need for cross-border surveillance coordination
articles.title_original: Estimating the burden of imported malaria in the Asia Pacific Region: A Systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: GBD 2023 estimates for women of reproductive age across Eastern Sub-Saharan Africa (1990–2023) found highest malaria prevalence in Mozambique (19,627 per 100,000 WRA; 95% UI 12,347–27,601) and lowest in Eritrea (842; 95% UI 450–1,430) and Rwanda (1,603; 95% UI 587–3,180). The region demonstrated substantial reductions from 1990 except in Djibouti and Ethiopia; rates remain high and heterogeneous, requiring sustained health system strengthening and malaria control programs
articles.title_original: Fatal and non-fatal health outcomes of malaria among reproductive age women in Eastern Sub-Saharan Africa, from 1990 to 2023.

articles.headline: Baseline study of 778 children (6 months–14 years) in Muheza, Tanzania found malaria prevalence 40.2% and anaemia 55.2%, with An. funestus s.l. (70.2%) and An. gambiae s.l. (29.8%) comprising vectors; annual entomological inoculation rates were 14.2, 13.5, and 27.7 for each species and combined. High pyrethroid resistance (56.5% permethrin, 52.8% deltamethrin mortality); children ≥5 years had 3-fold higher infection odds (aOR 3.03, 95% CI 2.09–4.40), while bed nets were protective (aOR 0.36, 95% CI 0.18–0.70)
articles.title_original: Baseline malaria burden and pyrethroid resistance in Muheza, Tanzania informing a cluster randomized trial of the 3D window screens.

articles.headline: Comparison of standard household-based versus risk-based reactive case detection in Aceh Province, Indonesia among 47 index malaria cases (majority P. knowlesi infections) found standard RACD tested 847 household members with 0.1% infection yield (1/847; 95% CI 0.0–0.7%). Risk-based RACD targeting forest-exposure contacts tested 179 individuals, yielding 4.5% LAMP-positive infections (8/179; 95% CI 1.9–8.6%) and 1.7% PCR-confirmed (3/179; 95% CI 0.3–4.8%), substantially improving detection
articles.title_original: Adapting reactive case detection strategies for high-risk populations to increase malaria infection detection in Aceh Province, Indonesia.

articles.headline: Correction to prior publication; no substantive research content
articles.title_original: Correction: A matter of timing: Biting by malaria-infected Anopheles mosquitoes and the use of interventions during the night in rural south-eastern Tanzania.

articles.headline: In a 20-year Health Demographic Surveillance System analysis of children under five in Burkina Faso, rainfall was positively associated with malaria mortality (mortality rate ratio [MRR] = 1.59; 95% Bayesian credible interval [BCI] 1.18–1.95), while land surface temperature decreased mortality (MRR = 0.68; 95% BCI 0.52–0.86). Insecticide-treated net (ITN) coverage reduced mortality (MRR = 0.59; 95% BCI 0.42–0.79), but artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs) were not statistically significant. ITN coverage was more effective than temperature, though rainfall had the greatest opposing impact on malaria mortality
articles.title_original: Assessing the role of interventions and climate on malaria mortality among children under five years of age: insights from two decades of data from the Health Demographic Surveillance System of Nouna, Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional analysis of 24 surveys (16,771 women, 24,707 children) from the BRINDA project examined relationships between body mass index, inflammatory markers (C-reactive protein, α1-acid glycoprotein), and vitamin A biomarkers (retinol, retinol-binding protein). Inflammation partially mediated the relationship between BMI/BMI-for-age z-score and vitamin A status in only 3 of 13 women's surveys and 1 of 22 children's surveys, with inconsistent directions. In populations without undernutrition or malaria, inflammation from overweight or obesity does not substantially impact vitamin A assessment
articles.title_original: Inflammation Does Not Mediate Relationships between BMI/BMI-for-Age Z-Score and Retinol or Retinol-Binding Protein in Adult Women or Young Children without Underweight, Wasting, or Malaria: An Analysis of 24 Surveys.

articles.headline: Policy review of 111 documents across five Sub-Saharan African countries (Kenya, Malawi, Mozambique, Nigeria, Zambia) assessed integration of social protection mechanisms in TB, HIV, and malaria control strategies. Overall readiness scores ranged 2.6–2.9 (on 0–3 scale), with Malawi (2.9) and Zambia (2.8) scoring highest; notably, no country institutionalized catastrophic-cost monitoring. Implementation and coordination were strongest domains (3.0), while financial protection was weakest (2.1), revealing significant gaps in financial-risk monitoring and accountability frameworks
articles.title_original: Assessing the integration of social protection into infectious disease policies: a multi-country analysis of TB, HIV, and malaria strategies in Sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.authors: Munyangaju I, Osório D, Nacarapa E

articles.headline: In 80 Burkinabe children (ages 0–60 months), IgG responses to 271 Plasmodium falciparum erythrocyte membrane protein 1 (PfEMP1) domains were examined pre-season and during peak transmission. IgG responses to 132 domains (49%) remained significantly protective against clinical malaria after false discovery rate correction, whereas only 5 IgM responses were nominally protective and none survived FDR correction. Opsonic phagocytosis activity correlated with protection for only one antigen, suggesting non-phagocytosis mechanisms mediate PfEMP1-specific antibody protection in young children
articles.title_original: IgG and IgM responses to PfEMP1 domains associated with semi-immunity to clinical malaria in Burkinabe children under five.

articles.headline: Cohort of 59 Cambodian Plasmodium vivax patients treated with artesunate were followed for 90 days after relocation to a malaria-free area. Relapses occurred in 81% of patients within 90 days (up to four consecutive relapses, median time-to-first relapse 20 days), with genetically diverse parasites detected via genome sequencing. Despite low parasitemia and absence of symptoms in most cases, relapse infections were frequently infectious to Anopheles dirus mosquitoes, highlighting the transmission potential of hypnozoite-driven infections
articles.title_original: Frequent and genetically diverse Plasmodium vivax relapses contribute to malaria transmission in Cambodia.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of Ghanaians in four endemic communities screened for Plasmodium falciparum asymptomatic infection using PfHRP2. Hemoglobin AA phenotype showed significantly higher odds of asymptomatic malaria (adjusted odds ratio [AOR] = 2.1, p = 0.02) compared to HbAS, though ABO blood group polymorphisms and RhD types showed non-significant associations in multivariate analysis. High prevalence of asymptomatic malaria was observed, confirming complex host genetic influences on parasite carriage
articles.title_original: The Role of Haemoglobin Variants and Red Blood Cell Polymorphisms in Asymptomatic Malaria: A Cross-Sectional Study.

articles.headline: In-silico molecular docking evaluated cinnamoyl sulfonamide hydroxamate derivatives (NMJ 1–8) against Plasmodium falciparum metallo-aminopeptidase PfA-M1, a zinc-dependent enzyme validated for antimalarial targeting. NMJ-2 and NMJ-3 demonstrated strongest binding affinities with induced-fit docking scores ranging −1984.16 to −1989.93 kcal/mol and formed stable complexes via molecular dynamics simulations. Interaction patterns overlapped with the reference inhibitor tosedostat, indicating NMJ-2 and NMJ-3 as promising scaffolds for preclinical development against drug-resistant P. falciparum
articles.title_original: In silico targeting of plasmodium falciparum metallo-aminopeptidases using hydroxamate inhibitors.

articles.headline: Facility-based cross-sectional study of 485 microscopy-confirmed Plasmodium vivax patients (Ethiopia, January–June 2024) examined Duffy blood group genotype frequencies. Among samples, 75.1% were heterozygous Duffy-positive and 24.5% homozygous Duffy-positive, while only 0.4% (n=2) were Duffy-negative; both Duffy-negative cases presented with mixed P. vivax and P. falciparum infections. Duffy negativity is not an absolute barrier to P. vivax infection, suggesting alternative erythrocyte invasion pathways exist
articles.title_original: Genetic variation in the Duffy blood group among vivax malaria patients and its impact on disease susceptibility.

articles.headline: Entomological survey in Cameroon (April–July 2019) collected 3,651 Anopheles mosquitoes (1,817 in forest, 1,834 in savanna) using human landing catches, with Plasmodium detection via qPCR. Anopheles gambiae s.l. predominated in forest (68.96%) while An. funestus s.l. dominated savanna (49.95%); entomological inoculation rate (EIR) was higher in forest (indoors 1.47, outdoors 1.00 infectious bites per person per night) than savanna (indoors 0.25, outdoors 0.23). Substantial outdoor and early morning biting activity (peak 4–5 am) occurred in both settings
articles.title_original: Mosquito vector composition and biting behavior of malaria vectors in forest versus humid savanna eco-epidemiological settings of Cameroon.

articles.headline: Prospective cohort of 855 infants in Busia District, Uganda (2021–2025, pre-vaccine rollout) followed from 4–8 weeks to 1 year of age over 706.7 person-years identified 662 malaria episodes. Microscopic parasitemia was 7.9% and combined microscopic/submicroscopic parasitemia was 21.8%; hemoglobin AS conferred 39% protection against symptomatic malaria. District-wide distribution of long-lasting insecticide-treated nets containing alpha-cypermethrin plus chlorfenapyr in October 2023 resulted in 80% reduction in malaria incidence
articles.title_original: Natural History and the Burden of Malaria During the First Year of Life in the High-Transmission Setting of Uganda.

articles.headline: Time-series analysis (wavelet and SARIMA) of confirmed malaria cases in three Nigerian states (Nasarawa, Kwara, Zamfara) from January 2015 to December 2024 characterized seasonal patterns and transmission synchrony. Nasarawa exhibited very strong annual seasonality with September peaks 70% of the time; Kwara showed variable cycles with August peaks; Zamfara showed yearly cycles with September peaks and near-perfect synchrony with Nasarawa. SARIMA forecasts for 2025–2026 predicted monthly cases of 51,482 (95% CI 23,899–116,073) in Nasarawa, 20,850 (95% CI 3,381–202,511) in Kwara, and 67,463 (95% CI 27,317–171,418) in Zamfara
articles.title_original: Exploring the past and forecasting the future of malaria in selected Nigerian states: A time series modelling approach using wavelet and SARIMA.

articles.headline: Whole-genome sequence analysis of 100 Omani individuals examined adaptive admixture at the Duffy (ACKR1) locus for Plasmodium vivax susceptibility. Genetic ancestry inference revealed 15.6% (95% CI 10.8%–20.3%) African ancestry overall, but 76% African ancestry at the Duffy locus with high FyES (Duffy null) frequency. Selection coefficient at FyES was estimated at 0.038 (95% CI 0.027–0.051) with introduction dating to at least 59 generations ago, suggesting long-standing P. vivax selection pressure shaped local malaria epidemiology
articles.title_original: Adaptive admixture at ACKR1, the Duffy blood group locus, may have shaped Plasmodium vivax prevalence in Oman.

articles.headline: Computational framework TEpiNom analyzed 42 P. falciparum vaccine candidate proteins across 18 African countries and 24 sub-Saharan populations, identifying 2265 MHC I and 1992 MHC II conserved T cell epitopes using NetMHCpan-4.1 and NetMHCIIpan-4.1. Prioritized MHC I epitopes from pre-erythrocytic antigens (FabZ, FabG, p36, PKG) achieved 100% predicted inter-locus coverage; epitope-dense regions provided complete coverage across parasite life stages. Framework systematically prioritizes T cell epitopes for preclinical malaria vaccine development
articles.title_original: TEpiNom: A computational framework integrating population data to prioritize Plasmodium falciparum T cell epitopes.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 2590 adults across 6 Ghana regions found malaria vaccine hesitancy prevalence of 44.8%, with greater perceived control/understanding of malaria associated with increased hesitancy (aPR=1.15; 95% CI: 1.09-1.21; p<0.001). Familiarity with malaria paradoxically weakens preventive vaccine behavior. Locally resonant, language-appropriate health communication needed to clarify vaccination's complementary role with other malaria control measures
articles.title_original: Impact of caregivers' perceptions of malaria on their hesitancy toward childhood malaria vaccination.

articles.headline: BALB/c mice (n=8/group) receiving three doses of Fe3O4-COOH superparamagnetic iron-oxide nanoparticles (SPIONs) conjugated with recombinant 19-kDa P. falciparum merozoite surface protein-1 (PfMSP-1, 25 μg on days 0/21/42) generated significantly higher and more durable anti-PfMSP-1 IgG titers than soluble protein alone. SPIONs markedly enhance antibody magnitude and longevity, supporting their use as a practical vaccine-delivery platform for blood-stage malaria antigens
articles.title_original: Iron-oxide nanoparticles (SPIONs) enhance malaria vaccine antibody response.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey (Jan-Apr 2025) of 181 poultry samples across 18 Kalasin Province districts found overall Plasmodium spp. prevalence of 62.98% (114/181), with higher infection in Thai native chickens (67.85%) than fighting cocks (55.07%, p>0.05) but significant district variation (20%-100%, p<0.05). All isolates were P. juxtanucleare (99%-100% nucleotide identity to COXIII gene). Similar infection rates across management systems indicate environmental vector exposure drives transmission
articles.title_original: Molecular prevalence and phylogenetic characterization of Plasmodium juxtanucleare in Thai native chickens and fighting cocks across Kalasin Province, Northeast Thailand.

articles.headline: Molecular survey of 307 P. falciparum isolates across 14 Indian states (1993-2023) found sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance widespread: Pfdhfr S108N (77.8%), C59R (61.7%); Pfdhps A437G (31.9%), K540E/N (4.98%); 83% carried resistant alleles with double/triple mutants predominant. SP resistance shows strong regional differences with gradual rise of higher-order mutants, particularly from North-East, threatening India's 2030 malaria elimination target
articles.title_original: Genetic profiling of sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance markers in Indian Plasmodium falciparum field isolates.

articles.headline: Review of quality assurance in malaria rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs); billions of units have been distributed globally over the past decade, with microscopy as the reference standard. Inaccurate RDT performance leads to misdiagnosis, inappropriate treatment, increased mortality, and potential emergence of drug-resistant strains. Strengthening QA protocols is crucial for diagnostic accuracy and supporting global malaria elimination efforts
articles.title_original: Quality assurance in rapid diagnostic tests for accurate diagnosis of malaria.

articles.headline: Investigation using P. yoelii gene-edited parasites found that tryptophan-rich antigen 7 (PyTRAg7) interacts with vimentin on mouse splenic fibroblasts, activating NF-κB p65 signaling and increasing ICAM-1 and integrin β1 expression. PyTRAg7 deletion reduced infected RBC adhesion to fibroblasts, lowered splenic parasite burden, delayed parasitemia onset, and prolonged survival with preserved splenic architecture. PyTRAg7 is a key mediator of parasite sequestration and potential malaria intervention target
articles.title_original: Plasmodium yoelii tryptophan-rich antigen 7 mediates infected erythrocyte adhesion to splenic fibroblasts via binding to vimentin.

articles.headline: Comparative evaluation of 4 P. falciparum 3D7 synchronization methods: D-sorbitol achieved 88.37% ring-stage purity; 60% Percoll-Sorbitol showed 87.47% PFMs20 binding for late-stage enrichment; 70%-40% Percoll gradient yielded 88.12% schizont binding efficiency with visible hemozoin layer separation; Plasmion yielded 73.37% late-stage binding. Results provide decision matrix: D-sorbitol for basic ring-stage studies, Percoll-based gradients for rigorous stage-specific investigations, Plasmion for resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Comparative evaluation of synchronization methods for Plasmodium falciparum: Efficiency, practicality and experimental applicability.

articles.headline: Case report: 47-year-old HIV+ woman on HAART (Tenofovir-Lamivudine-Atazanavir, undetectable viral load) developed opsoclonus myoclonus syndrome 2 weeks after treatment for severe P. falciparum malaria, presenting with ocular saccades, myoclonus, ataxia, and behavioral disturbances. Initial IV methylprednisolone produced partial improvement; rituximab addition achieved complete symptom resolution and functional autonomy restoration. Suggests malaria-OMS relationship in HIV context; IV corticosteroid plus rituximab may substitute for IV immunoglobulins
articles.title_original: Opsoclonus myoclonus syndrome in an HIV patient, following plasmodium falciparum infection.

articles.headline: No abstract provided; title indicates discussion of malaria vaccine impact on mortality trends but specific findings cannot be summarized
articles.title_original: Vaccines mean malaria deaths should be falling - not rising.

articles.headline: Established platform for synchronized release of invasion-competent P. vivax merozoites from ex vivo cultures using ML10 (cGMP-dependent protein kinase inhibitor) for egress inhibition and liposome-based mechanical extrusion, achieving >75% recovery rate with high purity. Demonstrated antibody-opsonized P. vivax merozoites activate human natural killer cells via antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity mechanisms. Overcomes technical barriers enabling direct investigation of P. vivax merozoite invasion biology and immune responses
articles.title_original: Accessing invasion-competent Plasmodium vivax merozoites reveals antibody-dependent NK cell activation.

articles.headline: Study of 423 children <5 years with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria in northern Burundi (Dec 2023-Jun 2024) treated with artemether/lumefantrine found 4.5% remained parasitemic on day 3, with no pfkelch13 mutations (validated ART-R markers) detected. Antifolate/aminoquinoline resistance markers widespread: dhfr triple mutant N51I/C59R/S108N at 92%, dhps double/triple mutants at 41%/47%, pfcrt CVIET at 84%. Delayed clearance and near-fixed multidrug resistance warrant urgently strengthened surveillance
articles.title_original: Border Region Surveillance of Malaria Drug Resistance, Northern Burundi, 2023-2024.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional DHS study of 126,424 individuals in 19 sub-Saharan African countries found pooled malaria prevalence 24.0% (95% CI 18-30%), highest in Central Africa (26%). ITN users had 13.23% prevalence vs. 32.75% in no-net users and 28.91% in untreated-net users. Spatial analysis revealed significant clustering (Moran's I=0.159, p≤0.001) with 34 identified hotspots, indicating geographic heterogeneity despite ITN availability
articles.title_original: Spatial epidemiology of malaria by bed net utilization in 19 sub-Saharan African countries: a DHS-based study (2013-2023).

articles.headline: Three-year incidence study of 100 children in Bandiagara, Mali examined P. falciparum AMA1 cluster-one-loop haplotype association with clinical malaria (fever >37.5°C, parasitemia ≥2,500/μL). Specific c1L haplotypes showed no significant association with malaria symptoms after adjusting for location, suggesting clinical malaria driven by novel parasite alleles rather than previously encountered variants
articles.title_original: Distribution of Plasmodium falciparum apical membrane antigen-1 cluster one loop haplotypes and their association with malaria symptoms in Bandiagara, Mali.

articles.headline: Synthesis from 6 African nations (Chad, Côte d'Ivoire, DRC, Madagascar, Rwanda, Senegal) documents how climate variability alters malaria epidemiology through rainfall and vector habitat changes. National malaria control programmes employ climate-health data integration and early warning systems, but implementation faces barriers—limited data capacity, infrastructure gaps, and financial constraints—requiring sustained political commitment and long-term health system investment
articles.title_original: Climate change and malaria control in Africa: country experiences and strategic responses.

articles.headline: Laboratory study of ZY19489 (triaminopyrimidine in Phase Ib trial) showed resistant P. falciparum acquire novel PfCRT mutations reducing parasite growth and fitness. Critically, ZY19489-resistant parasites lose chloroquine resistance and become hypersusceptible to piperaquine through blocked efflux transport. This evolutionary trap supports pairing ZY19489 with piperaquine as a resistance-refractory antimalarial combination
articles.title_original: Collateral hypersensitivity between ZY19489 and piperaquine neutralizes PfCRT-mediated drug efflux and Plasmodium falciparum resistance.

articles.headline: Scoping review protocol designed to synthesize knowledge and perspectives of African stakeholders on gene drive and genetically modified mosquitoes for malaria control (no empirical results reported; protocol registered on Open Science Framework)
articles.title_original: Knowledge, perspectives, and risk perceptions on gene drive and genetically modified mosquitoes for malaria control among African stakeholders: a scoping review protocol.

articles.headline: High-throughput phenotypic screen tested 139,916 compounds from MMV Hit Generation Library against H. contortus larvae, identifying 272 primary hits (0.194%); 110 confirmed active compounds with 39 showing IC50<10 μM and 33 causing complete developmental arrest at ≤12.5 μM. Four compounds non-toxic to HepG2 cells; 16 met medicinal chemistry criteria, demonstrating the antimalarial library yields potent nematocidal scaffolds
articles.title_original: High-throughput phenotypic screening of Medicines for Malaria Venture's Hit Generation Library 1 identifies new nematocidal chemotypes.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist

articles.headline: Case report of P. vivax gametocytes identified in bone marrow of patient with pancytopenia and splenomegaly
articles.title_original: A mysterious foe in a case of pancytopenia with splenomegaly: Plasmodium vivax gametocytes in the bone marrow.

articles.headline: Evidence synthesis indicates pre-infection gut microbiome composition predicts Plasmodium parasitemia level in both rhesus macaques and human volunteers, with Bifidobacterium identified as potentially protective, suggesting microbiome as underappreciated malaria parasite burden determinant
articles.title_original: Gut check: converging evidence links microbiome to malaria risk.

articles.headline: Mechanistic study reveals transient var2csa activation mediates P. falciparum var gene switching; non-coding RNAs from var2csa regulate switching frequency, while var2csa upstream ORF destabilizes mRNA via nonsense-mediated decay, promoting alternative var activation and enabling chronic infection through antigenic variation
articles.title_original: Mechanistic insights into coordinated var transcriptional switching in malaria parasites.

articles.headline: Review exploring single-drug polypharmacology strategy in antimalarials, examining how natural products (quinine, artemisinin) with multitarget mechanisms reduce resistance likelihood and discussing opportunities for achieving combination-therapy efficacy within single compounds (conceptual review; no new empirical data)
articles.title_original: Polypharmacology in malaria treatment: single drugs, multiple mechanisms, greater impact.

articles.headline: Analysis of 99 P. falciparum isolates from Roraima State, Brazil (2016-2020) detected: pfmdr1-NFD in 6% (6/99), pfcrt-C350R in 7% (7/99), pfpm2 amplification 5% (5/94), pfpm3 amplification 6% (6/97), and novel pfpm2/3 hybrid in 4% (4/99); no validated pfk13 artemisinin-resistance mutations detected. These variants, associated with reduced lumefantrine and piperaquine susceptibility, represent early warning signals for artemisinin-based combination therapy partner drug resistance in northern Brazil
articles.title_original: Emergence of artemisinin-based combination therapy resistance markers in Plasmodium falciparum from the Brazilian tri-border region of the Guiana Shield.

articles.headline: Critical review of Anopheles gut microbiome evidence for malaria control, evaluating bacterial, fungal, and viral mechanisms (immune modulation, nutrient competition, antiparasitic activity). Identifies significant methodological limitations: laboratory bias, oversimplified microbial consortia, context-dependent outcomes, and ecological uncertainties challenging field deployment of microbiome-based interventions
articles.title_original: Exploring the gut micriobiome in Anopheles mosquitoes: A promising new approach to malaria control.

articles.headline: Full-length var2csa sequencing of 31 P. falciparum isolates from Thailand identified 24 haplotypes and two major lineages (3D7-type dominant, Dd2-type with 7 isolates) with high diversity (h=0.97±0.02). Nucleotide diversity was significantly higher at the 3' region (π=0.4253 vs 0.1033, p<0.0001) with pronounced lineage divergence (Dxy=0.506, Da=0.444), indicating bipartite variation essential for developing broadly protective VAR2CSA vaccines against placental malaria
articles.title_original: Two divergent VAR2CSA lineages with distinct evolutionary signatures circulate in Plasmodium falciparum from Thailand.

articles.headline: Structure-activity optimization of bisquinoline analogs identified compound 25 with potent in vitro activity (IC50<20 nM) against drug-sensitive and multidrug-resistant P. falciparum, superior metabolic stability (t1/2=121 min), and enhanced in vivo efficacy (ED50=0.32 mg/kg/day) achieving complete curative protection despite moderate oral bioavailability (F=43%) and shorter half-life (T1/2=27.2 h)
articles.title_original: Designing novel bisquinoline antimalarials from historical 4-aminoquinolines to combat drug-resistant malaria.

articles.headline: 5-aminolaevulinic acid (5-ALA), which potentiates artemisinin in cancer, does not enhance dihydroartemisinin activity against P. falciparum parasites. Co-administration of artemisinin and 5-ALA is therefore not a promising strategy for improving malaria treatment
articles.title_original: 5-ALA does not potentiate dihydroartemisinin against Plasmodium falciparum malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Behrens HM, Henshall IG, Spielmann T

articles.headline: Genomic profiling of neotropical vector Anopheles aquasalis identified 241 digestion-related genes: 56 for carbohydrate digestion, 51 for lipid digestion, and 134 for protein digestion, with orthologous clusters conserved across anophelines. Larvicidal toxin receptors, α-amylase, and serine proteases are of particular interest as targets for malaria vector control strategies
articles.title_original: Genomic profiling of digestion related enzymes in Anopheles aquasalis a major coastal neotropical malaria vector.

articles.headline: In a traveller cohort, pfhrp2/3 deletions occurred in 4 of 115 patients (3.5%), with no double deletions, and 51.3% of patients had visited countries anticipated to surpass the WHO 5% pfhrp2/3 threshold by 2028. Continued monitoring of these deletions in non-endemic settings is needed to guide rapid diagnostic test strategies
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium falciparum histidine rich protein 2/3 gene deletions affecting rapid diagnostic test performance in travellers.

articles.headline: Modelled estimates from Swedish refugee data (2015-2022) indicate 3,930 of 56,248 sub-Saharan African asylum applicants/quota refugees carried asymptomatic P. falciparum infections (7%), with approximately 491 infections arriving per year (range 356-546), 80% from high/moderate-transmission countries. Number needed to screen was 2-14 for high/moderate-transmission populations, guiding effective screening strategies
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum infections and number needed to screen among refugees arriving from sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: ISTNet is a multi-scale transformer-based architecture developed for malaria cell classification. No abstract available to extract specific design, findings, or performance metrics
articles.title_original: ISTNet: a multi-scale transformer-based architecture for malaria cell classification.
articles.authors: Wu P, Gao X, Yu H
