articles.headline: This is a correction notice to a prior publication on mapping endothelial adhesion receptor expression in fatal cerebral malaria in Malawian children. No new primary data provided
articles.title_original: Correction to: Mapping the expression of endothelial adhesion receptors for Plasmodium falciparum-infected erythrocytes in fatal cerebral malaria in Malawian children.

articles.headline: Analysis of 898 P. falciparum isolates from Southeast Asia (1995-2023) revealed geographic variation in pfcrt mutations and piperaquine resistance: H97Y reached 15% in Cambodia, I218F 24.4% in Thailand, and T93S 21% in Vietnam; over 65% of parasites with pfcrt mutations also carried pfplasmepsin2 amplification; Cambodia pfplasmepsin2 prevalence peaked at 88% in 2015 after DHA-piperaquine introduction. Ongoing genetic monitoring is essential to preserve artemisinin-based combination therapy efficacy
articles.title_original: Changing genetic profiles of Plasmodium falciparum piperaquine resistance in Southeast Asia over 25 years.

articles.headline: This review of placental malaria infection during pregnancy identifies that P. falciparum placental infection causes inflammation, maternal morbidity, and adverse infant immune and neurocognitive outcomes. Despite parasite resistance to sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine, it remains beneficial for birth weight; dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine does not improve birth outcomes, highlighting the need for first-trimester interventions and vaccines targeting primigravidae
articles.title_original: Malaria in pregnancy at the frontline: a delicate balance.
articles.authors: Kokuhennadige V, Aitken E, Rogerson S

articles.headline: Case-control study of 228 P. vivax malaria cases and 2,149 controls in Maharashtra, India identified significant ABO blood group associations: B+ increased risk (OR=1.36, 95% CI 1.02-1.80, p<0.05) while A+ was protective (OR=0.72, 95% CI 0.52-0.99, p<0.05). Homozygous α-globin 3.7 deletion dramatically increased susceptibility (OR=3.33, 95% CI 2.00-5.53, p<0.001), indicating region-specific genetic factors influencing vivax malaria susceptibility in Indian populations
articles.title_original: Genetic Determinants for Vivax Malaria Susceptibility in Chiplun (Ratnagiri), Maharashtra, India: A Case-Control Study.

articles.headline: Comprehensive review of CRISPR-Cas9 applications in antimalarial drug-resistance research across Africa emphasizes its potential to elucidate resistance pathways, validate molecular markers, and enhance diagnostics. Despite significantly low current adoption due to technological and regulatory barriers, CRISPR holds strong potential for accelerating antimalarial drug discovery and advancing precision medicine in malaria-endemic regions
articles.title_original: Innovative approach in malaria research: Harnessing CRISPR-Cas9 for antimalarial drug-resistance studies in Africa.

articles.headline: Monocyte-to-lymphocyte ratio (MLR) was higher in malaria-infected third-trimester pregnant women (0.48 vs 0.40; p=0.021) and independently predicted malaria infection (OR=4.19) and high parasitemia (OR=7.16) in 270 women in Nanoro, Burkina Faso, though with modest diagnostic performance (ROC 0.60–0.61). Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio was elevated in high parasitemia (3.06 vs 2.55; p=0.029). MLR and NLR may serve as supportive markers for malaria in pregnancy to aid early risk stratification in resource-limited, malaria-endemic settings
articles.title_original: The clinical significance of monocyte-to-lymphocyte and neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratios in malaria infection and parasitaemia among pregnant women in Nanoro, Burkina Faso.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: In a murine cerebral malaria model (Plasmodium berghei ANKA), ferrostatin-1, a ferroptosis inhibitor, reduced parasitemia, extended survival, alleviated neurological symptoms, and improved blood-brain barrier integrity. Mechanistically, ferrostatin-1 enhanced dendritic cell antigen presentation and M1 macrophage polarization, increased IFN-γ+ Th1 and Treg cell proportions, and inhibited ferroptosis-induced endothelial damage with reduced chemokines (CXCL9/CXCL10) and adhesion molecules (ICAM-1/VCAM-1). This dual "immune activation–endothelial protection" mechanism suggests ferroptosis-targeted therapy for cerebral malaria prevention and treatment
articles.title_original: Ferrostatin-1 alleviates experimental cerebral malaria by regulating immune cell functions and brain endothelial ferroptosis.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist

articles.headline: Computational screening identified spiroindolone (EP4), asperpenazine (SP4), and strychnine (ST6) as the strongest indole alkaloid inhibitors of 3-hydroxykynurenine transaminase (3-HKT), with binding free energies of −7.38, −7.21, and −7.12 kcal/mol respectively. Molecular dynamics and MMPBSA analyses showed EP4 and SP4 exhibiting stable binding (−69.658 to −77.025 and −68.658 to −75.947 kJ/mol), while ST6 had weaker interactions. EP4 and SP4 are promising candidates for anti-mosquito insecticide design targeting Plasmodium gametogenesis disruption in Anopheles vectors
articles.title_original: Computational evaluation of indole alkaloids as inhibitors of 3-hydroxykynurenine transaminase in malaria vector control.

articles.headline: This review examines genetic control of malaria transmission via salivary gland-centric mechanisms, specifically sporozoite recognition and invasion pathways. Despite extensive study of Plasmodium development stages in mosquitoes, critical molecular mechanisms governing parasite–salivary gland interactions remain poorly understood. Better characterization of these processes is essential for designing effective transgenes for genetic control and population replacement strategies
articles.title_original: Genetic control of malaria transmission: a salivary gland-centric perspective.

articles.headline: Among 1,860 asymptomatic individuals in Lagos, Plasmodium falciparum detection rates were 2.7% (microscopy), 8.8% (RDT), and 11.7% (PCR); RDT sensitivity was 34.4% versus microscopy 22.0%, but microscopy had higher positive predictive value (96.0% vs 46%). Asymptomatic malaria prevalence was higher in wet season (14.9%, peak 36.0% in June) versus dry season (7.2%), and more frequent in school-age children (14.1%) and males (12.9% vs females 10.7%). Early surveillance focused on wet season and younger individuals is needed to reduce transmission
articles.title_original: Longitudinal Assessment of Asymptomatic Malaria Infections in a Suburban Community in Lagos, Nigeria.

articles.headline: In Plasmodium falciparum, combined knockdown of three lysophospholipases—PARE, XL2, and XLH4—eliminated the parasite's ability to scavenge fatty acids and choline from lysophosphatidylcholine (LPC) and prevented replication in defined media with LPC as the sole fatty acid source. Loss of PARE alone had no effect, but combined with XL2/XLH4 deficiency impaired LPC catabolism. These findings demonstrate P. falciparum depends entirely on parasite-encoded enzymes for LPC hydrolysis, ruling out host erythrocyte enzymes as physiologically relevant contributors
articles.title_original: Defining the minimal enzymatic requirements for fatty acid scavenging from lysophosphatidylcholine by erythrocytic Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Computational drug repositioning identified FDA-approved drugs—Lumacaftor, Vilazodone, Tucatinib, Lenvatinib, and Hydrocortisone Cypionate—as potential PfEMP1 inhibitors for cerebral malaria, with docking energies ranging from −9.1 to −8.3 kcal/mol and favorable oral bioavailability. Molecular dynamics simulations confirmed stable drug-PfEMP1 interactions with favorable potential energy profiles, and Gibbs free energy analysis supported complex stability. These findings support computational approaches for repurposing FDA-approved therapeutics against cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Computational Drug Repositioning for Targeting PfEMP1: Potential Therapeutics for Cerebral Malaria in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: In a facility-based cross-sectional survey in Malawi, 97% of women reported willingness to accept a future maternal malaria vaccine, though knowledge of placental malaria varied by district (higher in Thyolo than Mpemba, aOR 0.30, 95% CI 0.16–0.57). General malaria knowledge and preventive practices were high; 53.4% reported fever during pregnancy, and 92% acknowledged possible vaccine harm. Context-specific delivery strategies tailored to local epidemiology and knowledge levels are recommended for effective vaccine introduction
articles.title_original: Assessing Readiness for Future Maternal Malaria Vaccines: Knowledge, Practices, and Vaccine Attitudes Among Women of Reproductive Age in Malawi.

articles.headline: A factorial life-table experiment spanning five relative humidities (30–90%) and seven temperatures (16–38°C) demonstrated that humidity significantly modifies thermal performance of fitness traits in Anopheles stephensi, an invasive malaria vector. When integrated into demographic models, humidity markedly altered population fitness projections compared to temperature-only models, suppressing growth in hot, arid regions while enhancing fitness in humid or high-elevation climates of South Asia and Africa. Multiple environmental drivers must be incorporated in vector distribution projections
articles.title_original: Humidity shapes the thermal niche of Anopheles stephensi, an invasive malaria vector.

articles.headline: Knockout or ligand-dependent knockdown of acyl carrier protein (ACP) in blood-stage Plasmodium falciparum was lethal despite FASII pathway dispensability at this stage. ACP loss impaired isoprenoid biosynthesis and blocked apicoplast biogenesis by destabilizing pyruvate kinase II (PKII), the apicoplast's sole source of nucleoside triphosphates required for isoprenoid synthesis. This reveals an essential ACP function independent of fatty acid synthesis, critical for apicoplast metabolism and organelle biogenesis
articles.title_original: Acyl Carrier Protein is Essential for Apicoplast Biogenesis in Malaria Parasites Independent of Fatty Acid Synthesis.

articles.headline: An entomological adaptive surveillance framework (EASF) employing Bayesian exceedance probability methods outperformed routine surveillance in Ghana and Mozambique, yielding higher standardized mosquito catch rates, more robust models with lower coefficients of variation, and proportionally fewer obsolete sampling locations. EASF's evidence-based site selection significantly reduced uncertainty and improved predictive accuracy while maintaining operational feasibility. Integration of adaptive and routine designs enhances surveillance efficiency and enables dynamic adaptation to transmission system changes
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the Entomological Adaptive Surveillance Framework for malaria vector monitoring: a comparative field trial with routine surveillance in Ghana and Mozambique.

articles.headline: Among 269 children under five in Nigeria, malaria and soil-transmitted helminth (STH) co-infection (most common in 1–2 year-olds, 40.9%) caused macrocytic hypochromic red cell changes with increased mean corpuscular volume and reduced mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration (p<0.001), despite mild non-significant reductions in hemoglobin. Children with sickle cell disease (6.3%) showed the greatest haematological derangement. Parasitemia correlated weakly with hemoglobin, RBC, and PCV, suggesting early red cell alterations were driven more by host factors than parasite burden
articles.title_original: Haematological responses in children under 5 years with malaria and soil-transmitted helminth coinfections in Delta State, Nigeria: a cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Secondary analysis of 12,498 Nigerian women from 2018 DHS evaluated intermittent preventive therapy with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (IPTp-SP) during ANC. Among women attending ≥4 ANC visits, 75% received <3 IPTp-SP doses (42.5% overall). Worst-case scenario (≥4 ANC visits, <3 IPTp-SP doses) prevalence was 64% in poorest quintile vs 44.7% ages 40-49 (p<0.001). Critical intersectional inequality: 75% probability of worst-case for poorest + no education vs 5% for wealthiest + higher education, requiring urgent policy attention
articles.title_original: Prevalence and inequalities of missed opportunities for intermittent preventive therapy (IPTp) of malaria among pregnant women: an analysis of 2018 Nigeria DHS.

articles.headline: Among 441 malaria-symptomatic Mozambican children aged 2-10, 22% of 74 with malaria were coinfected with soil-transmitted helminths (STH). STH coinfection associated with significantly lower P. falciparum parasitemia (p=0.005), reduced fever (p=0.047), and decreased pro-inflammatory cytokines IL-8, TNF, IFNα, IP-10, MCP1, GM-CSF (p<0.05). Antibody responses to P. falciparum antigens SSP2, PTRAMP, MSP3, RH5 were elevated in STH-coinfected children (p<0.05), suggesting helminth coinfection mitigates severe malaria through enhanced protective immunity
articles.title_original: Helminth coinfections mitigate clinical, parasitological, and immune outcomes in Mozambican children with malaria.

articles.headline: Analysis of 1.4 million encounters from 936 community health volunteers (CHVs) and 86 health facilities across 713 villages in Siaya County, Kenya (2021-2023) evaluated malaria case management effectiveness. CHV coverage was adequate (≥1 per 500 people) in 82.5% of villages vs 30% for health facilities. CHVs managed 28.1% of encounters with higher test positivity rate (82.0% vs 64.3%) and higher fever prevalence in suspected cases (77.0% vs 53.1%). Incorporating CHV data increased malaria incidence estimates by 18% (549 vs 467 per 1,000 population)
articles.title_original: Community health volunteers improve access to malaria case management in Siaya County, Kenya.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 216 under-five children in Senga Hill District, Zambia (Nov 2022–Aug 2023) using Care Start RDT for P. falciparum/P. vivax detected 31% malaria prevalence (95% CI 26–38%). Correct ITN hanging reduced infection odds by 88% (OR=0.012, 95% CI 0.005–0.304); two ITNs and IRS reduced odds by 86% (OR=0.14, 95% CI 0.040–0.510) and 58% (OR=0.42, 95% CI 0.21–0.85) respectively. Male children had 204% increased odds (OR=3.04, 95% CI 1.52–6.07)
articles.title_original: Prevalence and factors associated with malaria amongst under-five children in Senga Hill District, Northern Province, Zambia, a community-based cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 10 RCTs (63,192 participants) evaluated ivermectin vs placebo/standard care for malaria transmission reduction. Community-level analysis showed no significant reduction in malaria prevalence with very wide confidence intervals reflecting substantial imprecision. Entomological trials demonstrated increased mosquito mortality following ivermectin (RR 1.89; 95% CI 1.26–2.83), though heterogeneity was substantial (I²>97%). Ivermectin was well tolerated. Current evidence does not support consistent malaria incidence or prevalence reductions with ivermectin administration
articles.title_original: Efficacy of ivermectin for malaria vector control: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized clinical trials.

articles.headline: Qualitative study (Dec 2022–March 2023) of 63 individual interviews and 18 focus groups with healthcare providers across 9 Guinean health districts identified significant gaps in malaria control implementation despite high provider knowledge of malaria risks. Key barriers included gaps in ITN distribution and use, delayed seasonal malaria chemoprevention implementation, and non-compliant practices (overcharging, poor patient reception, over-reliance on clinical diagnosis). Providers often held negative views of malaria patients. Study highlighted persistent operational barriers requiring improvements in free case management, SMC implementation, and ITN distribution
articles.title_original: Healthcare providers' knowledge, perceptions and practices of malaria control strategies in the Republic of Guinea: insights from a qualitative study.

articles.headline: Study of 1,200 Anopheles mosquitoes across 12 Nigerian ecozones identified 999 An. gambiae s.l. (39.7% An. gambiae s.s., 51.5% An. coluzzii, 8.7% An. arabiensis); 399 An. gambiae s.s. karyotyped for 2La inversion. Distinct south-north cline observed: 2La/2La predominated in arid northern savannas; 2La+/2La+ in humid southern forests; heterokaryotype 2La/2La+ in transitional zones. Significant correlation between inversion frequency and latitude (R²=0.68–0.73, p<0.001). Findings indicate 2La inversion supports ecological adaptation affecting vector-control effectiveness by region
articles.title_original: Spatial distribution of the 2La chromosomal inversion in Anopheles gambiae populations across Nigeria: ecological associations and implications for malaria control.

articles.headline: Microfluidic device study with four channels simulating post-capillary venule flow conditions assessed P. falciparum parasite invasion under physiological shear stress. Testing wild-type and gene-deleted parasite lines revealed deletion of erythrocyte binding antigen 175 (PfEBA175) significantly impacted invasion under flow (p<0.05) but not in static culture, establishing for the first time that flow-induced shear stress critically affects parasite invasion in genotype-dependent manner. Method applicable to other biological processes affected by fluid dynamics
articles.title_original: Microfluidic platform for automatic quantification of malaria parasite invasion under physiological flow conditions.

articles.headline: Immunization study in BALB/c and C57BL/6 mice with liver-stage malaria antigens PfCSP (P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein) and PbSLTRiP (P. berghei liver stage protein) with or without dual TLR adjuvants Poly(I:C) and R848. Coadministration with adjuvants evoked greater antibody responses than antigen alone. PfCSP-specific IgG remained detectable 80 days post-immunization, declining gradually. PbSLTRiP showed progressive IgG increases with detectable IgG2c responses, though adjuvant regimen differences were not statistically significant
articles.title_original: Poly(I:C) and R848 adjuvants elicit sizeable humoral immunity to liver stage malaria antigens.

articles.headline: Literature review (PubMed/MEDLINE 2015–2025) on malaria-associated acute kidney injury (MAKI) epidemiology and outcomes. MAKI results from multifactorial processes: hemolysis, microvascular obstruction, inflammation, endothelial dysfunction, volume depletion. P. falciparum most implicated; P. vivax and P. knowlesi increasingly recognized. MAKI associated with prolonged hospitalization, increased renal replacement therapy need, elevated mortality, and survivors at risk of incomplete recovery and chronic kidney disease progression. Early recognition and post-recovery renal monitoring essential
articles.title_original: Malaria-Associated Acute Kidney Injury: A Key Driver of Mortality in Endemic Regions.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 1,127 children (6 months–10 years) in Burkina Faso (July 2022) using HRP2-RDT, microscopy, and qPCR found 28.0% asymptomatic P. falciparum prevalence by RDT and 31.9% by microscopy. School-aged children (5–10 years) had 66.5% microscopy positivity vs 21.6% under-five. Gametocyte carriage was 3.9% overall, twice as high in school-aged children (67 vs 32 gametocytes/μL). Microscopy superior sensitivity (76.7% vs 67.7%) and PPV (100% vs 83.8%) compared to RDT using qPCR as reference
articles.title_original: School-aged children as a silent reservoir of Plasmodium falciparum: findings from a cross-sectional survey conducted in Burkina Faso in 2022.

articles.headline: Retrospective hospital-based study (Jan 2023–April 2024) of 40 P. knowlesi malaria patients confirmed by microscopy/PCR in Aceh, Indonesia. Median age 31 years; 67.5% farmers/plantation workers, 10% orangutan caretakers. All presented with fever and headache; treated with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine and primaquine with complete recovery. Parasite density was high in 42.9%, moderate in 45.7%, low in 11.4% (n=35). Median hospital stay 3 days, longer for lower parasite density. Most cases occurred April–July 2023
articles.title_original: Socio-demographic characteristics and parasite density patterns of Plasmodium knowlesi Malaria in Aceh, Indonesia: insights from hospital-based data.

articles.headline: Mouse anti-PfVFT1 antibodies were detected in all protected individuals from CHMI studies and inhibited P. falciparum parasite reinvasion into RBCs, promoted complement-mediated parasite lysis, and supported phagocytosis and antibody-dependent cellular inhibition, demonstrating that PfVFT1-specific antibodies engage multiple effector mechanisms relevant to antimalarial immunity
articles.title_original: Vaccine-induced mouse antibodies targeting Plasmodium falciparum PfVFT antigen inhibit blood stages through multiple mechanisms.

articles.headline: Correction article describing how Plasmodium kinases regulate MTOC, axoneme and kinetochore organization during male gametogenesis, providing insights into potential transmission-control targets (no quantitative results reported in abstract)
articles.title_original: Correction: Divergent Plasmodium kinases drive MTOC, kinetochore, and axoneme organisation in male gametogenesis.

articles.headline: Among 424 Nigerian primary health center patients, malaria was most common (293/424, 61.1%); AKI occurred in only 2/301 adults (0.6%) but in 70/123 children (56.9%)—stage 1: 25 (20.3%), stage 2: 26 (21.2%), stage 3: 19 (15.4%)—with 49/70 pediatric cases (70%) having malaria, highest in children <5 years, highlighting AKI as a common severe malaria complication in pediatric populations
articles.title_original: A Study Using Point-of-Care Creatinine Testing in a Nigerian Primary Health Care Centre: Malaria as the Leading Cause of Acute Kidney Injury Particularly in Children.

articles.headline: STAT1 genetic ablation in P. yoelii 17XL-infected mice enhanced survival, reduced peripheral parasitemia and splenic parasite burden, decreased IFN-γ-producing CD8+ T cells and IL-10-producing CD4+ T cells at day 7, and increased hemoglobin and reticulocyte proportions compared to wild-type mice, indicating STAT1 deficiency alleviates malarial anemia through IFN pathway modulation
articles.title_original: Role of STAT1 in modulating the host immune response to Plasmodium yoelii 17XL-infected murine blood-stage malaria.

articles.headline: Among 262 Indian blood collection facilities testing 9,275,688 donations (2020–2024), malaria positivity was 13.27 per 100,000 units overall with rising annual rates from 3.4 to 22.0 per 100,000 units, yet only 8 facilities (3.1%) reported transfusion-transmitted malaria cases despite 97.7% performing screening, suggesting TTM is substantially under-recognized and underreported despite increasing donor positivity
articles.title_original: Transfusion-transmitted malaria in India: A national survey of epidemiology and testing practices.

articles.headline: Conditional knockdown of pfap2-v (PF3D7_1239200) in P. falciparum significantly decreased intraerythrocytic proliferation, caused infected RBC surface topological abnormalities, and markedly reduced cytoadhesion to CD36, ICAM-1, and PECAM-1/CD31, establishing PfAP2-V as a master transcriptional regulator governing parasite proliferation and virulence genes including var clusters and PfEMP1 cytoadherence factors
articles.title_original: The DNA-binding protein PfAP2-V regulates erythrocyte invasion and pathogenesis of the human malaria parasite Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Across 25 Tanzanian sentinel districts, Anopheles arabiensis was widespread while A. gambiae s.s. and A. funestus s.s. were localized; host-seeking A. arabiensis were predominantly outdoor (64.9%) compared to A. gambiae s.s. (58.4%) and A. funestus s.s. (42.4%); humans were most active outdoors 18:00–21:00 with adolescents more active than young children, and adolescent males showed lowest bed net use, informing vector control optimization
articles.title_original: Nationally representative programmatic surveillance of mosquito and human behaviours that influence human exposure to malaria transmission and the impact of vector control across Tanzania.

articles.headline: Among 35,720 malaria RDTs in 16 Beninese facilities, healthcare worker interpretations agreed with expert reference panel in 94.3% (Cohen's kappa=0.88), but false positives (5.0% misrecorded) exceeded false negatives (0.7%); accuracy was 96.7% in children <5 years versus 91.6% in patients >15 years, with better performance in HCWs with ≥10 years experience, highlighting that experience and facility infrastructure drive diagnostic fidelity
articles.title_original: The accuracy of recording malaria rapid diagnostic test (RDT) results in public health facilities in Benin; results from the MaCRA project.

articles.headline: In 4,745 Ethiopian community members, microscopy detected malaria in 2.7% (126/4745) but nested PCR on 1,183 samples confirmed only 67% of microscopy-positive cases (PCR-corrected prevalence 1.4%) and identified 6.1% (66/1086) submicroscopic infections; species misclassification was frequent (19.5% P. falciparum as P. vivax, 14.0% P. vivax as P. falciparum), demonstrating microscopy substantially underestimates true malaria prevalence
articles.title_original: Microscopy underestimates submicroscopic malaria infections in Ethiopia's southern rift valley: a community-based cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Loss-of-function of the bridge-like lipid transfer protein PfVPS13L1 (which mediates bulk lipid transfer between ER and nascent inner membrane complex at membrane contact sites) reduced IMC growth, produced smaller anucleated progeny, and impaired schizogony, establishing VPS13L1 as critical for P. falciparum invasive stage formation
articles.title_original: A bridge-like lipid transfer protein is critical for generation of invasive stages in malaria parasites.

articles.headline: Wild-caught house sparrows with 70% baseline chronic malaria infection exposed to tebuconazole (triazole fungicide) for 11 weeks (late autumn to mid-winter) showed 20% increase in malaria prevalence while control birds exhibited expected seasonal decline; parasite load did not differ between groups, indicating tebuconazole promotes parasite reactivation from exoerythrocytic stages rather than enhanced replication
articles.title_original: Chronic exposure to a common azole fungicide is associated with reactivation of chronic Plasmodium infections in farmland birds.

articles.headline: Naturally acquired antibodies against P. vivax liver-stage antigen Pv_LISP-2 were detected in infected individuals from the Brazilian Amazon; acute-phase responses were characterized by high IgM followed by IgG induction (predominantly against Pv_L.seq1, with IgG1 and IgG3 subclasses predominant); IgM correlated positively with parasitemia and symptom duration but inversely with hemoglobin and hematocrit, supporting Pv_LISP-2 as a potential liver-stage vaccine candidate
articles.title_original: Naturally acquired antibodies to Plasmodium vivax Pv_LISP-2, a potential liver stage vaccine antigen.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 9,222 Ethiopian households (Oct 2024–Jan 2025) found LLIN ownership at 71.5% but utilization only 59.9% (72.7% rural vs lower urban); pregnant women reached 78.5% use and children <5 years 67.2%. Multivariable logistic regression identified age (OR 0.258, p<0.001) and pregnancy (OR 0.662, p<0.001) as strongest predictors; 60.5% lacked confidence hanging nets and 64.1% incorrectly believed malaria only occurs during rainy season. Utilization below the 80% threshold needed for community-wide protection, requiring skill-based interventions and targeted communication
articles.title_original: Assessment of Long-Lasting Insecticidal Net (LLIN) Ownership, Utilization, and Associated Barriers in Malaria-Endemic Communities of Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Analysis of P. falciparum isolates from therapeutic-efficacy studies in Kolda and Kédougou (2020–2023) showed pfdhfr triple mutant (N51I+C59R+S108N) near-fixed at >90%, pfdhps A437G at 47–100%, and pfdhps A581G emerging in 2022 (8.1% Kolda) rising to 14.9% Kolda and 6.5% Kédougou by 2023. K540E remained rare/sporadic (<2%). Although sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine remains clinically effective, rising A581G prevalence above 10% warrants close monitoring
articles.title_original: Emergence of Plasmodium falciparum pfdhps A581G mutation in Southern Senegal under Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention pressure, 2020-2023.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 113 imported malaria cases in Nepal (Nov 2024–Jan 2025) found 99.11% had traveled to India for work, predominantly to Maharashtra (96.47%) and South Mumbai (95.60%), with 71.43% in catering services and 85.84% lacking fixed housing. Only 56.64% received malaria information before travel and 59.30% refused LLINs citing unsuitability for living conditions. Risk concentrated in defined urban hotspots among young male labor migrants from Muslim communities, highlighting need for targeted, migrant-sensitive prevention strategies and cross-border collaboration
articles.title_original: Understanding imported malaria in western Nepal: Implications for malaria control and elimination.

articles.headline: Survey of An. gambiae complex larvae across natural ecosystem gradients in southern Tanzania identified An. arabiensis in fully domesticated areas and An. quadriannulatus dominating conserved ecosystems and Nyerere National Park. An. arabiensis showed positive association with human/cattle activity; An. quadriannulatus with impala, warthog, and bushpig signs, indicating zoophagic preference. An. arabiensis persisted >40 km inside protected areas, suggesting wild-blood-host refugium populations that may confound vector elimination but enable insecticide-resistance management strategies
articles.title_original: Blood host preferences and competitive inter-species dynamics within an African malaria vector species complex inferred from signs of animal activity around aquatic larval habitats.

articles.headline: Retrospective Brazilian cohort of 466 P. vivax-infected patients treated with chloroquine and primaquine (3.5 mg/kg) showed null/low CYP2D6 activity (AS ≤0.25) had 2-fold relapse risk vs normal activity (HR=2.22, p=0.004), but community vivax malaria incidence (26.6 cases/100 person-years over 5 years in 997 Amazonians) did not differ across CYP2D6 activity levels. Patients with AS ≤0.25 require alternative relapse-suppression regimens; intermediate activity (AS 0.5–1.0) did not require alternative treatment
articles.title_original: Reduced cytochrome P-450 (CYP) 2D6 activity and Plasmodium vivax malaria risk in Amazonians: A retrospective, population-based cohort study.

articles.headline: Federated learning system using Vision Transformers for automated malaria detection from blood smear RBC images achieved ViT-B/16 accuracy of 98.15%, FedDistill-DeiT 97.79%, FedAvg-Swin-T 97.75%, and FedDistill-Swin-T ROC-AUC of 0.9977, outperforming CNN ensemble (ResNet50+VGG16). Privacy-preserving federated architecture enabled cooperative training across decentralized institutions without exchanging sensitive data, providing reliable, scalable, interpretable malaria screening
articles.title_original: FedMal-XAI: an explainable federated vision transformer leveraging knowledge distillation for privacy-preserving malaria detection.

articles.headline: Review of host-derived Gp96 (Grp94) and parasite PfGp96 in malaria pathogenesis. Elevated circulating Gp96 associated with severe malaria phenotypes and acts as danger-associated molecular pattern; PfGp96 expressed by P. falciparum with conserved Hsp90 domain architecture but parasite-specific adaptations. Limited direct evidence supports PfGp96 as drug target; article synthesizes current knowledge and outlines priorities for functional and translational research on Gp96-related biomarker and therapeutic development
articles.title_original: Heat shock protein Gp96 (Grp94) in malaria: functional insights at the host-parasite interface and therapeutic perspectives.

articles.headline: Mechanistic study identified PfNCR1 localization to narrow (~3–4 nm) membrane contact sites between P. falciparum and host RBC via a unique 141-amino-acid HLH (helix-linker-helix) domain. HLH domain physicochemical properties necessary for efficient targeting; degree of PfNCR1 localization at contact sites qualitatively correlates with cholesterol homeostasis maintenance. HLH domain established as key element for PfNCR1 localization and transport effectiveness
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Niemann-Pick Type C1-Related protein relies on its physicochemical properties for membrane contact site localization required for cholesterol homeostasis.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional amplicon deep sequencing of pvmsp1 in 370 samples from 215 individuals in Ethiopia identified 67 unique haplotypes with high population diversity (Hd=0.799, π=0.044, HE=0.826) but low individual MOI (1.4; 34.6% polyclonal). Of 82 patients with 1–5 recurrent infections, paired analysis showed 81.3% genetic homology and 55.6% shared identical alleles, indicating most relapses. High diversity driven by migrant workers; highlights need for surveillance and targeted interventions for mobile populations
articles.title_original: High genetic diversity, clonal activation of hypnozoites and relapse of Plasmodium vivax isolates in low-transmission setting of Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Community-based qPCR screening of 525 Ethiopian participants (Sept–Dec 2024) detected substantially higher asymptomatic malaria prevalence (164/525, 31.2%) and sub-microscopic infections (72/433, 16.6%) compared to microscopy (asymptomatic 17.5%) and RDTs (asymptomatic 13.1%). Sub-microscopic breakdown: P. vivax 7.9%, P. falciparum 7.2%, mixed 1.6%. Infections more common in ages 15–24 and females. Asymptomatic and sub-microscopic infections reveal persistent transmission reservoir missed by standard diagnostics
articles.title_original: Uncovering silent malaria reservoirs in Southern Ethiopia: community-based molecular detection of asymptomatic and sub-microscopic infections and their implications for diagnosis and control.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 15 Ethiopian studies (11,269 samples, 5,521 P. falciparum cases) found pooled kelch13 mutation prevalence of R622I 11%, A675V 1%, P441L 0%, and Pfmdr1 mutations D1246Y 6%, Y184F 77%. Y184F prevalence increased significantly from 66% (2007–2014) to 91% (2015–2025). While most resistance markers remain rare or absent, rising Y184F frequency represents growing concern for antimalarial drug resistance in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Incidence of kelch13 and Pfmdr1 gene mutations associated with antimalarial drug resistance in Plasmodium falciparum isolates from Ethiopia: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Mini-PIE evaluation of malaria vaccine introduction in 42 Cameroonian health districts (April 2024) found monthly report completeness >95% except North-West and Adamawa (87%); vaccine coverage first dose 56%, second 31%, third 28%, with high drop-out rates all regions. Among 48 caregivers, 88% were aware of introduction, 60% informed by health workers, 81% cited protection as reason to vaccinate, and 60% knew to continue LLINs. Introduction largely successful despite misinformation; mini-PIE methodology recommended for other countries
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the first malaria vaccine introduction: ongoing learning opportunities during the introduction into routine immunisation in Cameroon.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 2,244 patients (153 malaria cases) in Indonesian clinics identified P. vivax (97), P. knowlesi (35), P. malariae (12), P. falciparum (3), and P. ovale (1). Mean total cost was US$33 for malaria and US$17 for non-malarial fever, with 70% and 61% attributable to lost productivity; 16% of malaria and 11% of other fever patients faced catastrophic health expenditure. Despite subsidized care, patients experience substantial out-of-pocket and indirect costs requiring consideration in malaria control policy design
articles.title_original: The household economic burden of human-only and zoonotic malaria, compared to other causes of acute febrile illness in Indonesia.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 297 individuals in Mozambique examined head of household occupation and sunrise/sunset activity as malaria risk factors. Adjusted prevalence difference for outdoor versus indoor occupation was 0.015 (95% CI: −0.12 to 0.15); for sunrise/sunset activity, 0.10 (95% CI: −0.063 to 0.26). Interaction analysis showed difference of 0.33 (95% CI: 0.037–0.62) in those active at sunrise/sunset with outdoor-occupation household heads, suggesting occupational and temporal patterns may differentially affect malaria risk
articles.title_original: The Effect of Head of Household Occupation and Activity at Sunrise and Sunset on Malaria Prevalence in Sussundenga, Mozambique.

articles.headline: Whole-genome analysis of 1,094 Anopheles darlingi from six South American countries (median 18× depth) revealed deep geographic population structure, high genetic diversity with 13 putative segregating inversions, and no evidence of sympatric cryptic taxa despite high interpopulation divergence. Strong selection signals appeared on cytochrome P450 genes, indicating insecticide-driven selection. Results will facilitate mosquito surveillance and control while highlighting challenges for malaria elimination in the Western Hemisphere
articles.title_original: Population genomics of Anopheles darlingi, the principal South American malaria vector mosquito.

articles.headline: In murine malaria, Plasmodium yoelii tryptophan-rich antigen 7 (TRAg7) interacted with macrophage CD71 receptor, activating NF-κB p65 signaling and promoting proinflammatory factors. Genetic deletion of TRAg7 led to decreased parasitemia, reduced inflammation, and improved host survival. These findings demonstrate that exported parasite proteins drive inflammatory responses via immune cell receptors, offering a conceptual framework for understanding malaria pathogenesis and developing therapeutic interventions
articles.title_original: Interaction of Plasmodium yoelii tryptophan-rich antigen 7 with CD71 on macrophage membrane regulates host inflammatory response.

articles.headline: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout study in HeLa cells identified four endolysosomal SNAREs (VAMP7, VAMP8, Vti1B, Stx7) recruited to the Plasmodium berghei parasitophorous vacuole membrane with distinct temporal profiles. Single gene knockouts showed minimal impact, but VAMP7–VAMP8 and VAMP7–Vti1B double knockouts significantly reduced parasite infection and growth, with Vti1B playing a dominant role. Plasmodium hijacks coordinated host SNARE networks for lysosome–PVM fusion during nutrient uptake, identifying Vti1B as a promising host-directed antimalarial target
articles.title_original: Host SNARE Proteins Mediate Lysosome and PVM Fusion to Support Plasmodium Liver Infection.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 45 studies (1964–2024) across 23,166 individuals identified glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase (G6PD) deficiency prevalences (<30% activity) of 0.0–19.9%, female intermediate/deficient (30–70% activity) of 0.8–44.6%, and allele frequencies of 0.0–25.9% across Indonesian sites; 15 class B variants were documented. Findings support introducing routine G6PD screening to guide high-daily-dose primaquine (1 mg/kg/day × 7 days) for P. vivax hypnozoites, though heterogeneous prevalence and data gaps in high-endemicity areas require further investigation to support P. vivax elimination
articles.title_original: G6PD deficiency in Indonesia: a systematic review and update of prevalence and variant maps in the context of malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Assessment of Anopheles gambiae s.l. across 15 Accra sites during dry and rainy seasons (2022–2024) collected 20,945 host-seeking and 1,613 resting mosquitoes. Abundance was highest in irrigation and peri-urban sites and lowest in low socioeconomic areas; Anopheles gambiae s.s. dominated host-seeking populations while Anopheles coluzzii dominated resting populations. Irrigation and peri-urban areas were identified as transmission hotspots requiring targeted surveillance and control measures
articles.title_original: Urban malaria vector dynamics in Accra, Ghana.

articles.headline: In IL-10-deficient (Il10−/−) mice infected with Plasmodium yoelii, enhanced IL-12 and IFN-γ production did not fully explain the lower parasite load observed. IL-10 deficiency reduced germinal center B cells and plasmablasts; B cells proved essential for survival but not critical for early parasite control. Even Il10−/− mice lacking both IFN-γ and B cells could limit early parasite expansion, indicating IL-10 suppresses host-protective immune pathways beyond B cell and IFN-γ-dependent mechanisms
articles.title_original: Early enhanced control of Plasmodium yoelii infection in IL-10-deficient mice is independent of IFN-γ, IL-12, and the humoral response.

articles.headline: Stochastic P. falciparum transmission model evaluated catch-up vaccination and booster doses of R21/Matrix-M vaccine across transmission settings. Catch-up campaigns targeting ages 6 months–2 years in 45% PfPR2-10 averted 358 (95% CrI 113–570) uncomplicated cases per 1000 doses; extra booster 10 years post-primary averted 12 (95% CrI 6–18) severe cases per 1000 doses in 5% PfPR2-10 but −4 (95% CrI −11–3) in 45% PfPR2-10 settings. Catch-up campaigns and boosters provide per-dose benefits approaching routine vaccination depending on transmission intensity
articles.title_original: Public health impact of catch-up vaccination or additional booster doses with pre-erythrocytic malaria vaccine R21/Matrix-M: a modelling study.

articles.headline: Analysis of malaria outbreak years in India over the past decade, defined as cases exceeding 3-year moving average plus 2.5× moving standard deviation, identified 402 district outbreak-years with peaks in 2011, 2014, and 2015. After 2015, outbreak frequency sharply declined, with 2020 recording the lowest number; seven states (Mizoram, Tripura, Jharkhand, Odisha, Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Meghalaya) were identified as outbreak-prone with >70% districts experiencing ≥1 outbreak year (2011–2020). Heightened surveillance and targeted interventions are needed in outbreak-susceptible districts
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of malaria outbreak years in India provides key insights into malaria elimination.

articles.headline: In rodent and human studies, macrophages/monocytes (but not neutrophils or NK/NKT cells) promoted malaria transmission by suppressing transmission-blocking microbiota (E. anophelis, S. sonnei, K. oxytoca) in mosquito midguts via scavenger receptor A (SR-A)-mediated non-opsonic phagocytosis. Depletion of macrophages/monocytes or anti-SR-A neutralizing antibodies significantly enhanced anti-Pfs25 monoclonal antibody efficacy, achieving complete blockade of P. berghei-pfs25 transmission. Parasites co-opt host immune cells to eliminate transmission-blocking bacteria, suggesting anti-SR-A antibodies could improve antimalarial transmission-blocking strategies
articles.title_original: Host macrophages/monocytes promote malaria transmission by modulating mosquito microbiota via SR-A-mediated phagocytosis.

articles.headline: Screening of 69 microscopy-confirmed malaria samples from Timika, Papua (2022) identified 3 patients (4.3%) co-infected with DENV-2 (1 case) and DENV-4 (2 cases), alongside P. vivax, P. falciparum, and P. malariae. Whole-genome sequencing revealed DENV-4 genotype II closely related to Makassar strains. Malaria-dengue co-infections pose diagnostic and clinical challenges due to overlapping symptoms and potential complications, highlighting the need for combined clinical and molecular surveillance approaches in endemic regions
articles.title_original: Co-infections of malaria and dengue in Timika, a highly endemic malaria area in Central Papua, Indonesia.

articles.headline: Mass drug administration reduced malaria incidence by 55% in a trial in Senegal; transportability models predicted reductions of 36-65% in non-trial communes when accounting for weather, vegetation, and population density, with larger effects in areas with higher precipitation and denser vegetation, though MDA was ineffective in the post-intervention year
articles.title_original: Mapping the local effectiveness of mass drug administration for malaria using transportability methods.

articles.headline: Hydroalcoholic extracts of Dorema aucheri demonstrated dose-dependent larvicidal activity against Anopheles stephensi, achieving up to 98% mortality at 400 mg/L (LC₅₀ 98.54-104.72 mg/L) and 85-90% repellency at 0.5 mg/cm² with protection times of 52-64 minutes at 5% dose, suggesting potential as an eco-friendly alternative to synthetic insecticides
articles.title_original: Eco-friendly mosquito control: Assessing the larvicidal and repellent effects of Dorema aucheri extract on Anopheles stephensi, the malaria vector.

articles.headline: A systematic review of 59 studies (2000-2025, 83% from sub-Saharan Africa, 85% low bias risk) identified key behavioral barriers to malaria intervention effectiveness: ownership-use gaps for ITNs due to discomfort, healthcare-seeking delays driven by cost and distrust, and diversion to informal providers, indicating that technical strategies must address experiential and trust-based obstacles
articles.title_original: Investigating the impact of human behavioural factors on the effectiveness of malaria interventions in malaria-endemic regions: A systematic review.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis

articles.headline: In mice infected with Plasmodium berghei-ANKA, anti-GPI scFv treatment (given days 0, 3, 6 post-infection) prevented lethality in 71.4% of animals, reduced parasitemia, promoted weight recovery, and inhibited neurological and systemic signs with improved hematological and histopathological parameters, demonstrating promising potential for cerebral malaria intervention
articles.title_original: Anti-GPI scFv as a Promising Tool for Intervention Against Cerebral Malaria in Mice.

articles.headline: A narrative review of educational interventions for malaria found that theory-grounded programs consistently improved knowledge and preventive practices, but most lacked explicit instructional design and failed to address ecological, occupational, and cultural dimensions relevant to Plasmodium knowlesi transmission in Southeast Asia, proposing an integrated framework combining behavioral theory, participatory design, and structured instructional models
articles.title_original: Educational frameworks for zoonotic malaria prevention: a narrative review integrating behavioural theory, participatory design, and instructional models for Plasmodium knowlesi in Southeast Asia.

articles.headline: A systematic review of 102 epidemiological models applied to sub-Saharan African malaria (PRISMA 2020) found transmission-focused models predominant (52.9%), followed by intervention (21.6%), optimal control (9.8%), and combined cost-effectiveness models (15.7%), with key gaps including limited incorporation of drug/insecticide resistance, migration dynamics, and climate variability
articles.title_original: A systematic review of epidemiological models for malaria transmission in Sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: Title only provided; abstract not available. Describes a rare case of acute liver failure due to Plasmodium falciparum in a non-endemic country
articles.title_original: Acute Liver Failure Due to Plasmodium falciparum: A Rare Case from a Non-Endemic Country.
articles.authors: Batıbay E, Yüksekyayla O, Polat M

articles.headline: CRISPR-mediated deletion of CLIP in Plasmodium berghei impaired blood-stage growth and prevented merozoite erythrocyte invasion, while in transmission stages, CLIP deletion dramatically reduced salivary gland invasion and impaired hepatocyte invasion due to defective gliding motility, demonstrating an essential role in host-cell invasion by both merozoites and sporozoites
articles.title_original: The CLAMP-Linked Invasion Protein (CLIP) plays an essential role in Plasmodium berghei zoites.

articles.headline: Integrative GWAS, transcriptomic, and eQTL analysis of Plasmodium knowlesi clinical isolates identified distinct parasite transcriptional programs associated with severe disease characterized by stress-response and host-interaction pathway activation, independent of parasite burden, with genetic regulation of immune-evasion (SICAvar, kir) and chromatin-associated regulators
articles.title_original: Integrative genomics of Plasmodium knowlesi reveals parasite-intrinsic regulators of severe human malaria.

articles.headline: Historical account of malaria control in Palestine from 1918 onward: the Zionist movement developed the first sustainable, community-based malaria control program for an entire country (1922 onward), establishing an early model of decolonized malaria control that engaged all inhabitants in disease management
articles.title_original: The Zionist decolonisation of rural malaria control in Palestine from 1922 onwards - what it teaches.

articles.headline: The odorant-binding protein AsOBP21f in Anopheles sinensis showed selective binding to hydrophobic human odors (C10-C15 carbon chains), with high affinity for methyl tridecanoate and dodecanal; RNAi-mediated silencing significantly reduced antennal electrophysiological responses to host odors and markedly decreased blood-feeding success, establishing AsOBP21f as essential for host-seeking behavior
articles.title_original: Functional characterization of AsOBP21f, an odorant-binding protein involved in human odor detection in the malaria vector Anopheles sinensis.

articles.headline: The Institution-based Network on China-Africa Cooperation for Malaria Elimination (INCAM), established in 2018, convened six collaborative fora between 2019-2025 to promote demand-driven research, innovative anti-malarial product development, and evidence-based policy adaptation, demonstrating transcontinental partnership potential to advance malaria elimination in Africa
articles.title_original: The Institution-based Network on China-Africa Cooperation for Malaria Elimination: fostering a transcontinental hub for strengthening China-Africa cooperation on malaria control and elimination efforts.

articles.headline: Retrospective analysis of 368,490 suspected malaria cases across central Ethiopian districts (2019-2024) found 23.5% Plasmodium-positive (86,604): 61% P. falciparum, 39% P. vivax. Cases increased since 2019, peaking in May (15.9%) and autumn (31.5%), with significant variation by district and season
articles.title_original: A six years trend analysis of malaria prevalence in Central Ethiopia Region.

articles.headline: Clinical case report examining angiotensin II as supportive therapy for severe P. falciparum malaria with refractory septic shock
articles.title_original: Angiotensin II use in refractory septic shock due to Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Review of antimalarial prophylaxis strategies and drugs for malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Malaria Prophylaxis.
articles.authors: Stachler L, DeVos E, Samandari T

articles.headline: Plasmodium falciparum heterochromatin protein 1 (PfHP1) undergoes phase separation to organize heterochromatin and silence var genes. Point mutations disrupting phase separation caused dispersed nuclear localization and derepression of up to 54 of 60 var genes; Hi-C analysis confirmed loss of heterochromatic interactions critical for antigenic variation control
articles.title_original: Phase separation of heterochromatin protein 1 regulates virulence gene expression in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Anopheles pseudopunctipennis (Mexico's primary malaria vector) showed primary oviposition peak 18:00-20:00, with nulliparous females ceasing after 24:00 and parous females continuing until 06:00. Parous females laid more eggs; pyrethroid insecticide application by ULV or thermal fogging during dusk-midnight is proposed
articles.title_original: Daily pattern of oviposition and fecundity in Anopheles pseudopunctipennis Theobald, the main vector of malaria in Mexico.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 62 malaria vector control cluster RCTs (1992-2021, 74% African) identified 13 different entomological outcomes and 12 sampling methods. Seventy percent had high design risk from lacking power analyses and inadequate randomization; only cluster number significantly predicted outcome precision
articles.title_original: A systematic review of entomological outcomes and sampling approaches used in the evaluation of cluster randomised controlled trials for malaria vector control products.

articles.headline: Historical data and cultural history of malaria in Hungary
articles.title_original: [Data for cultural history of malaria in Hungary].

articles.headline: Indole peptidomimetics LSPN954 and LSPN959 showed potent P. falciparum activity (IC50 3D7: 1.7 and 1.0 μM), high selectivity indices (59–95), slow-acting kinetics, and additive effects with artesunate. Both retained efficacy against multidrug-resistant strains (Dd2, K1, Dd2R_DSM265, 3D7R_MMV848) with no cross-resistance
articles.title_original: Indole Peptidomimetics Show Potent and Selective Activity against Drug-Resistant Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Review of P. falciparum vaccine development amid major global burden (282 million cases, 610,000 deaths in 2024, concentrated in children <5 and pregnant women in sub-Saharan Africa). Summarizes current vaccine strategies, key limitations, and emerging opportunities for overcoming complex lifecycle-related challenges
articles.title_original: Recent Advances, Bottlenecks, and Future Directions in Plasmodium falciparum Vaccine Development.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling (EMOD) of RTS,S/AS01 vaccine in Benin (2024-2030) compared nine scenarios with different coverage and dose schedules. Ninety percent coverage with two boosters achieved highest impact: 9.8% parasite prevalence averted, 11.06% clinical cases averted, 21.83% severe cases averted by 2030. Booster strategies showed sustained reductions versus limited gains at lower coverage
articles.title_original: Assessing the potential impact of the RTS,S/ASO1 vaccine on malaria burden in Benin: a mathematical modelling approach.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 180 pregnant women in Eastern Uganda found placental P. falciparum malaria in 11.67% (microscopy) and 23.89% (qPCR). Age 15–19 years (aOR 5.87, p=0.023) and absence of antenatal care visits (aOR 4.27, p=0.011) significantly increased infection risk
articles.title_original: Prevalence of placental Plasmodium falciparum malaria and associated factors among pregnant women in health facilities in Eastern Uganda.

articles.headline: Prospective follow-up of 74 asymptomatic Tanzanians with P. ovale parasitemia found 55% developed recurrent infection at median 10 weeks; nine experienced a second recurrence. Most infection pairs showed heterologous genotypes. The contribution of relapse versus reinfection to recurrence remains unclear in this coastal setting
articles.title_original: Frequent Plasmodium ovale recurrence in coastal Tanzania.

articles.headline: Genomic analysis of 4 P. simium/P. vivax isolates plus 405 P. vivax and 19 P. simium isolates revealed recent genetic exchanges between species and evidence that P. simium originated from a host jump approximately 1–2 centuries ago; positive selection signals were detected in P. simium genes including PvRBP2a, PvRBP1b, and PvCMRP1, underscoring zoonotic risks requiring inclusion of monkeys in malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Genomic Insights Into Host Shifts Between Plasmodium vivax and Plasmodium simium in Latin America.

articles.headline: In C57BL/6 mice infected with P. berghei NK65, DHA-rich fish oil supplementation partially preserved intestinal morphology (reduced shortening) but did not improve pulmonary outcomes, demonstrating strain- and pathogenesis-dependent effects of ω-3 supplementation across different Plasmodium infection models
articles.title_original: Differential Effects of DHA-Rich Fish Oil Supplementation on Intestinal and Pulmonary Alterations in Experimental Malaria Caused by Plasmodium berghei NK65.

articles.headline: Among 134 hospitalized malaria patients in rural Madagascar (median age 15 years), P. falciparum predominated (94.0%), 20.1% had severe malaria including 10.4% cerebral malaria, and median length of stay was 2 days (IQR 2–3); prolonged hospitalization (≥5 days, 12.7%) was significantly associated with cerebral malaria, parasitemia ≥5%, blood transfusion, and age <15 years (p≤0.034)
articles.title_original: Clinical Characteristics and Outcomes of Hospitalized Malaria Patients in Rural Madagascar.
articles.authors: Kasprowicz D, Korzeniewski K, Wilczyńska W

articles.headline: Across 70 sites in four Amazonian ecoregions, 14,689 mosquito bites were recorded from Nyssorhynchus darlingi; human biting rate was highest in areas with intermediate forest degradation, positively associated with temperature, and negatively with humidity, with floodable vegetation as the strongest land-cover predictor, indicating need for climate-sensitive, spatially targeted vector control
articles.title_original: Reframing vector control: land use and climate shape malaria risk in Amazonian communities.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 12 studies (9.28 million individuals) found no significant difference in progression from uncomplicated to severe malaria across different intervention combinations (OR 0.56; 95% CI 0.31–1.02; p=0.06), but simultaneous deployment of multiple interventions significantly reduced severe malaria (OR 0.41; 95% CI 0.30–0.56; p<0.001), parasitemia (OR 0.52; p<0.001), and gametocytes (OR 0.41; p<0.001) versus single or no interventions
articles.title_original: Progression from uncomplicated to severe malaria among children in settings receiving different combinations of malaria control interventions in sub-Saharan Africa: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Among 2,021 discarded malaria RDTs from Haiti (2021–2023), flavivirus screening identified 68 samples positive for DENV-3 (all from 2023, with phylogenetic similarity to Caribbean 2022–2023 sequences); no samples tested positive for alphavirus (0/517) or Oropouche virus (0/293), demonstrating the utility of discarded RDTs for remote arbovirus surveillance during periods of interrupted malaria programs
articles.title_original: Characterization of arbovirus infections in patients within Haiti by screening discarded malaria rapid diagnostic test cassettes.

articles.headline: In 151 malaria patients versus 151 controls in Ethiopia, P. falciparum predominated (70.86%) with parasitemia densities distributed as 30.46% low, 46.36% moderate, and 23.18% high; mean AST, ALT, ALP, and bilirubin were significantly elevated (p<0.001) while total cholesterol, HDL, and LDL were significantly reduced (p<0.05), indicating liver dysfunction and dyslipidemia warrant assessment in malaria patients
articles.title_original: Malaria parasitemia and its association with liver function parameters, and lipid profile among malaria-infected adult patients in western Ethiopia: A comparative cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Computational epitope discovery identified 123 P. falciparum B-cell epitopes (58 conserved), including 2 highly conserved Plasmodium epitopes (KHVETWTQRVQNM, LHSNFYIKRF) predicted to bind antibody Fab regions; a bispecific antibody model (COMED hook) was computationally designed to target both parasite and erythrocyte epitopes, with predictions requiring experimental validation
articles.title_original: Computational epitope discovery and in silico design of a bispecific antibody model (COMED hook) to block Plasmodium invasion into erythrocytes.

articles.headline: A novel flow cytometry method was developed to quantitatively assess hepatic infection by non-fluorescent Plasmodium parasites using HSP70 and UIS4 markers, enabling rapid, cost-effective measurement of intracellular parasite development without reporter genes and addressing technical challenges for evaluating reporter-free whole-sporozoite vaccine candidates
articles.title_original: Flow cytometry-based evaluation of hepatic infection by non-fluorescent Plasmodium parasites.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 8 studies found thalassemic individuals had lower odds of developing malaria (OR 0.856) compared to non-thalassemic individuals, though this association was not statistically significant and exhibited substantial heterogeneity; mechanistically, alpha thalassemia reduces cytoadhesion and rosette formation while enhancing immune clearance, with favorable interactions observed with Hp2-1 haptoglobin phenotype
articles.title_original: Exploring the protective effects of thalassemia against malaria in Africa: a systematic review.

articles.headline: Population pharmacokinetic analysis of 40 Gabonese patients (aged 3–57 years) receiving artesunate (2 mg/kg), fosmidomycin (30 mg/kg), and clindamycin (10 mg/kg) every 12 hours for uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria demonstrated adequate fosmidomycin exposure across all ages, but clindamycin exposure was substantially lower in children <12 years; dose simulation suggests increasing clindamycin to 12 mg/kg in children <35 kg would equalize drug exposure
articles.title_original: Population pharmacokinetics of fosmidomycin and clindamycin in combination with artesunate for uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Gabonese children and adults.

articles.headline: Evaluation of MERISCREEN Malaria Pf HRP-II Ag® RDT in 404 Ghanaian children under 5 years showed sensitivity 98.1% (95% CI 92.6–99.7), specificity 92.6% (95% CI 88.9–95.2), PPV 82.4%, NPV 99.1%, and overall accuracy 94.8%; real-world performance was marginally lower than manufacturer-reported values but sufficient to guide malaria treatment in resource-limited settings lacking microscopy services
articles.title_original: Evaluation of malaria rapid diagnostic test kit performance in a high-transmission setting: a facility-based cross-sectional study in rural Ghana.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional evaluation of NxTek and Biocredit RDTs in 384 malaria-suspected patients (Sept-Nov 2024, Ethiopia) for P. falciparum and P. vivax detection. Biocredit Pf/Pv RDT achieved 97.9% sensitivity and 97.4% specificity for P. falciparum (vs microscopy) and 94.5% sensitivity and 97.5% specificity for P. vivax; significantly outperformed SD Bioline (51.3% and 86.3% sensitivity, respectively). Against qPCR, Biocredit Pf/Pv showed 95.5% sensitivity and 96.4% specificity for P. falciparum, and 90.8% sensitivity and 99.1% specificity for P. vivax, meeting WHO diagnostic criteria
articles.title_original: Evaluation of NxTek™ Eliminate Malaria-PF test and biocredit rapid diagnostic tests for detecting Plasmodium species in Southern Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Spatio-temporal analysis of 9,507 febrile children across 44 Libreville neighborhoods (2012–2023) showed P. falciparum prevalence (PfPR) of 50.9% in semi-rural areas, 46.5% in semi-urban, 44.6% in shanty-towns, and 38.0% in urban zones (2023). WHO classification shifted from mostly mesoendemic (2015) to 41.5% hyperendemic (2023), with Gi* hotspot analysis identifying recurrent clusters in semi-urban and semi-rural neighborhoods that shifted dynamically over time, supporting micro-stratified malaria control
articles.title_original: Spatio-temporal heterogeneity of urban malaria in Libreville, Gabon (2012-2023): neighborhood-level hotspot analysis for micro-stratified control.

articles.headline: Review of malaria control challenges and recent progress (2025), focusing on drug resistance, treatment strategies including artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs), Pfkelch13 mutations, and pipeline candidates (Z439, KAF156, MMV371, MMV055/167, INE963, GSK701). Emphasizes urgent need for new drug regimens due to rising artemisinin resistance and describes innovations in formulation development and diagnostic improvements from microscopy to PCR
articles.title_original: Advancing global malaria control: therapeutic strategies, drug discovery, and formulation innovations.
articles.authors: Sarma K, Sarma H, Deka J

articles.headline: Review proposing a unified 'microbiota-barrier-innate immunity axis' hypothesis for malaria pathogenesis and control. Describes a hierarchical three-tier intervention pyramid (foundational nutritional regulation, targeted microbial intervention, precision bioengineering) tailored to African resource-limited settings to address drug resistance and vaccine shortages
articles.title_original: Reinventing malaria control: hierarchical intervention strategies for African settings based on the microbiota-barrier-innate immunity axis.

articles.headline: Case series of 15 patients with recurrent P. falciparum malaria during Ethiopia's 2024 resurgence, genotyped for antimalarial drug resistance markers. Three patients (20%) harbored K13 P441L, a WHO-candidate artemisinin partial resistance marker; other resistance markers (pfcrt, pfmdr1, pfdhfr, pfdhps) were largely fixed in the population. Findings indicate ACT resistance-associated mutations and clonal expansion in the Oromia region
articles.title_original: Recurrent parasitemias with artemisinin partial resistance mutations during the 2024 Ethiopia malaria resurgence: a case series.

articles.headline: High-density peptide array containing 4.2 million peptides comprehensively characterized immunogenic P. vivax proteins. Identified 283 proteins commonly recognized by antibodies in symptomatic individuals, including erythrocyte invasion proteins, nucleoporins, and novel proteins. Unique antibody pattern against PIR proteins in asymptomatic individuals possibly associated with protection against clinical vivax malaria
articles.title_original: Comprehensive characterization of Plasmodium vivax antigens using a high-density peptide array.

articles.headline: Case report of a 43-year-old woman with cerebral malaria caused by P. vivax (confirmed by thick blood smear) from southwestern Colombia, treated with IV artesunate followed by chloroquine and primaquine with full recovery. Demonstrates that P. vivax, traditionally associated with uncomplicated malaria, can cause severe neurological complications through mechanical and inflammatory mechanisms
articles.title_original: Cerebral malaria caused by Plasmodium vivax in an afrodescendant adult in Colombia: case report.

articles.headline: Laboratory and field evaluation of Abbott-Bioline Malaria Ag Pf/Pv RDTs in 218 febrile patients in Madagascar (70.2% PCR prevalence) versus microscopy and PCR reference standards. Against microscopy: Abbott achieved 99.1% sensitivity and 93.7% specificity; against PCR, sensitivity decreased to 73.2% (98.5% specificity). Despite identical RDT lots showing 18% sensitivity at Thailand-Myanmar border, performance varied by epidemiological context, with higher parasite densities (mean 10,006/µL) in Madagascar explaining superior sensitivity
articles.title_original: Field and laboratory evaluation of Abbott-Bioline™ Malaria Ag Pf/Pv RDT performance in a high-transmission setting: contrasting results with a low-endemic area.

articles.headline: Article discusses how plants synthesize a crucial malaria drug; full abstract not provided
articles.title_original: Botanical mystery solved: how plants make a crucial malaria drug.

articles.headline: Experimental study of coinfection with Plasmodium yoelii and the nematode Heligmosomoides polygyrus in mice showed that coinfected hosts excreted significantly more worm eggs than single-infected hosts despite equal worm biomass. Coinfection extended patent period to 99 days post-infection with higher cumulative egg shedding, modulated by suppressed IL-4, IL-13, and GATA-3+ CD4+ T cell expansion. Results demonstrate malaria coinfection increases soil-transmitted helminth transmission potential
articles.title_original: Coinfection with malaria alters the fecundity and within-host persistence of an intestinal nematode.

articles.headline: Review of P. falciparum's obligate purine salvage pathway as a drug target, focusing on purine analogs and Pf Equilibrative Nucleoside Transporters (Pf ENTs) as therapeutic conduits. Although the purine salvage system has been extensively studied, no purine-based antimalarial drug has yet reached clinical use; the review outlines framework for target-based design of purine-analog therapies
articles.title_original: Targeting Plasmodium falciparum with purine antimetabolites as a therapeutic strategy.

articles.headline: CRISPR-Cas9 introduction of P. falciparum K13 C580Y substitution into two recent Ethiopian clinical isolates demonstrated significantly elevated in vitro dihydroartemisinin (DHA) survival in edited parasites relative to isogenic controls. Ring-stage survival assays established that C580Y confers artemisinin tolerance in contemporary Ethiopian parasite genetic backgrounds
articles.title_original: Functional validation of the Plasmodium falciparum K13 C580Y mutation in recently collected Ethiopian isolates.

articles.headline: In 244 P. falciparum patients across Ethiopia, Indonesia, and Bangladesh, P. vivax serological IgG markers predicted 63-day vivax microscopy-detected episodes with 82% sensitivity but only 27% specificity; a study-specific algorithm achieved balanced 68%/68% sensitivity/specificity. Authors conclude SEMs have limited suitability for guiding universal radical cure in this setting, though may support community acceptance with appropriate messaging
articles.title_original: Predicting risk of Plasmodium vivax microscopy-detected episodes using serological markers in patients with Plasmodium falciparum malaria: a multi-country diagnostic performance evaluation.

articles.headline: Among 400 wild Anopheles funestus from southeastern Tanzania (March–June 2024), Wolbachia prevalence was 46.5% by primary PCR and 70.8% by nested PCR, with phylogenetic analysis confirming supergroup B strains. P. falciparum sporozoite prevalence was 0.8%, and only one mosquito carried Wolbachia-P. falciparum co-infection (0.3%). This is the first report of Wolbachia-P. falciparum co-infection in An. funestus in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Detection of supergroup B Wolbachia strains and their co-infection with Plasmodium falciparum in wild Anopheles funestus in southeastern Tanzania: implications for malaria control.

articles.headline: A systematic review of delayed malaria care-seeking in sub-Saharan African children under five found prevalence ranging 2–95.8%, driven by financial constraints, travel distance, traditional treatment reliance, and perception of malaria as mild. Delays were associated with increased mortality, severe malaria, and neurological/haematological complications. Interventions addressing financial barriers and strengthening health-system readiness can enhance timely access to treatment
articles.title_original: Delay in malaria care-seeking for children under five in Africa: a systematic review.
articles.authors: Amoadu M, Alhassan A, Odoom E

articles.headline: A stochastic metapopulation transmission model developed for western Kenya (2008–2019) accurately captured malaria incidence variation accounting for climate, interventions, parasite dispersal, and human mobility. The framework is generalizable to other endemic regions and can forecast incidence under future climate scenarios and intervention combinations, supporting localized malaria control planning
articles.title_original: Assessing the impact of climate and control interventions on spatio-temporal malaria dynamics using a stochastic metapopulation model.

articles.headline: The human antimicrobial peptide LL-37 showed antiplasmodial activity against drug-sensitive and drug-resistant P. falciparum in vitro, selectively targeting infected erythrocytes via phosphatidylserine externalization and cholesterol depletion. Exogenous LL-37/CRAMP administration in mice reduced parasitemia and improved survival; CRAMP-deficient mice showed higher susceptibility. However, therapeutic efficacy after infection establishment was limited, likely due to rapid in vivo peptide degradation and required concentrations exceeding endogenous systemic levels
articles.title_original: LL-37 selectively targets Plasmodium-infected erythrocytes and exhibits antimalarial activity.

articles.headline: A meta-analysis of 41 studies (11,635 children) found gross neurologic deficits at hospital discharge in 15.2% (95% CI: 11.5–18.8%) of cerebral malaria cases versus 2.4% (2.0–2.8%) in broader severe malaria, declining to 5.7% at 12 months. Sub-Saharan Africa had higher prevalence (14.2%) than East Asia & Pacific (2.3%). Risk factors included profound coma, coma ≥48 hours, multiple convulsions, hypoglycemia, and acute kidney injury
articles.title_original: Prevalence and risk factors of gross neurologic deficits in children after severe malaria: A systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: In Timika, Papua, Indonesia, asymptomatic PCR-detectable parasitemia (P. falciparum or P. vivax) was 87.9% (29/33) in trauma-related splenectomy patients versus 38.6% (697/1807) in a 2013 community survey (P<0.0001). These findings suggest chronic asymptomatic malaria parasitemia is associated with increased susceptibility to trauma-related splenic rupture, representing an underrecognized complication of chronic infection in endemic areas
articles.title_original: Chronic Malaria Is Associated With Trauma-related Splenic Rupture Requiring Splenectomy.

articles.headline: Analysis of 2018–2019 Uganda Malaria Indicator Survey data (6,786 children <5 with paired mRDT and microscopy) found false-positive mRDTs in 10.7% (849/6,786), with higher prevalence among those with recent fever (17.2%, 95% CI: 13.7–20.6%), recent antimalarial use (14.7%), or anemia (8.1%). A combined clinical/environmental/household model achieved weighted AUC 0.79. False-positive results contribute to overestimation of community malaria prevalence and potential misdiagnosis and unnecessary treatment
articles.title_original: False-positive Malaria Rapid Diagnostic Tests are Prevalent Among Children Under 5 Years of Age in Uganda.

articles.headline: A retrospective cohort of 1,737 Amazonians with 7,878.9 person-years follow-up showed heterogeneous P. vivax incidence (0–11 cases/person; average 25.8 cases/100 person-years). Fy-negativity remained a major resistance trait contributing to structural infection-free individuals. Fya/Fyb polymorphism differences in vivax infection risk diminished with age, likely because more susceptible Fy(b+) individuals develop clinical immunity faster than Fy(a+) individuals
articles.title_original: How Duffy Blood Group (FY) Polymorphism and Age Modulate Vivax Malaria Risk at the Community Level: A Population-based Retrospective Cohort Study in the Amazon.

articles.headline: In a double-blind, randomized trial of 44 asymptomatic P. falciparum-infected Gabonese adults, 40 mg/kg praziquantel daily for 3 days produced significant hourly parasitemia reduction (slope β=−0.006, P<0.001) versus placebo (β=−0.0013, P=0.3), though only 41% (9/22) achieved parasite clearance by day 7 versus 27% (6/22) placebo. Praziquantel demonstrated statistically significant but only moderate antimalarial activity; synergistic combinations warrant investigation
articles.title_original: Efficacy of Praziquantel for Treatment of Plasmodium falciparum Infection in Asymptomatic Gabonese Adults: A Double-Blind Placebo-Controlled Randomized Trial.

articles.headline: Analysis of 289 P. vivax whole genomes from Asia, the Americas, and Oceania found four blood-stage antigens (AMA1, CyRPA, RBP1a, P41) exhibiting high genetic diversity and consistent signatures of balancing selection, suggesting immune targeting. Ten other antigens (CSP, TRAP, MSP4, MSP9, MSP10, GAMA, S12, ARP, S25, S28) showed low-to-intermediate diversity. The Sal-1 vaccine strain had extremely low global frequency, suggesting current Sal-1-based formulations may have limited efficacy
articles.title_original: Extreme Genetic Diversity and Signatures of Balancing Selection in Plasmodium vivax Blood-Stage Antigens.

articles.headline: A budget impact analysis modeling incorporation of tafenoquine and G6PD quantitative point-of-care testing for P. vivax treatment in Brazilian Amazon patients under 16 years and by weight found an incremental 5-year cost of BRL 12.5 million. The model predicted 9% reduction in hospitalization costs and 11% reduction in recurrence costs. These technologies can support safer treatment decisions for P. vivax malaria in the Brazilian Amazon
articles.title_original: [Expansion of the use of technologies to reduce cases of malaria due to Plasmodium vivax in the Brazilian Amazon: a budget impact analysis].

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study (139 P. falciparum, 2022-2023, Cubal, Angola) sequenced pfk13, pfmdr1, pfdhfr, pfdhps. Results: 10% pfk13 mutations (none validated artemisinin resistance markers); Y184F (43%), N86Y (1%); pfdhfr triple mutation 77.2%; pfdhps A437G 98%, K540E 23.8%; 58.4% "partially resistant" (IRNG), 17.8% "fully resistant" (IRNGE). No validated pfk13 artemisinin markers identified; concerns about AL efficacy from pfmdr1 haplotypes; SP suitable for IPTp but fully-resistant IRNGE haplotypes present risk
articles.title_original: Molecular markers of antimalarial drug resistance in pfk13, pfmdr1, pfdhfr, and pfdhps genes of Plasmodium falciparum in a rural municipality in Cubal, Benguela Province, Angola (2022-2023).

articles.headline: In vitro phenotypic/genotypic characterization of 5 P. falciparum field isolates (3 pfhrp2+, 2 pfhrp2-) from Peruvian Amazon. Results: pfhrp2+ isolates showed rosetting (R+, CS-, Hu-) or CSA cytoadherence (R-, CS+, Hu-); pfhrp2- isolates showed HUVEC adhesion (R-, CS-, Hu+); pfhrp2 expression higher in pfhrp2+ isolates; genomic stability maintained during year-long culture. Different cytoadherence profiles observed by pfhrp2 status, with preliminary findings requiring verification in larger panels
articles.title_original: Phenotypic and genotypic characterization of Plasmodium falciparum field isolates lacking pfhrp2 gene.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional serological study of Toxoplasma gondii, Plasmodium spp., and Leishmania spp. co-infections (n=253, 2012-2022, French Guiana). Results: ~60% seropositive for ≥2 pathogens; 2.4% concomitant infections; significant differences in IL-10, IL-17, IL-33, and IFN-α between single and co-infected patients. High prevalence of multi-infections with distinct cytokine profiles provides biomarkers for developing public health strategies in endemic regions
articles.title_original: Unravelling complex interactions during Toxoplasma, Plasmodium, and Leishmania co-infections in French Guiana.
