articles.headline: MaxEnt species distribution modeling of invasive Anopheles stephensi in Hormozgan Province, Iran (72 occurrence points, WorldClim v2.1 bioclimatic data). Results: AUC 0.765, TSS 0.519; projected significant decline in environmental suitability under future climate scenarios; habitat loss in western/central Hormozgan; stable areas in Bashagard County; altitude most influential factor. Future projections indicate habitat decline in coastal areas; Bashagard may serve as stable refuge; adaptive vector control strategies needed
articles.title_original: Suitable ecological niches of invasive malaria vector under present and projected climatic conditions in South of Iran.

articles.headline: Narrative review of Bangladesh malaria epidemiology, 2000-2025. Results: >90% reduction in cases (2008-2020); disease confined to Chittagong Hill Tracts; P. falciparum dominant; emerging challenges include non-kelch13 artemisinin-partial resistance in P. falciparum, P. vivax G6PD complications, outdoor-biting vectors, and asymptomatic reservoir. 2030 elimination target attainable with strategic pivot to intelligence-driven approach integrating genomic surveillance and targeted vector control
articles.title_original: Malaria in Bangladesh, 2000-2025: Navigating the Headwinds From Control to Elimination.
articles.authors: Mamun AA, Kayenat MSA, Islam KAMI

articles.headline: Study examining healthcare workers' beliefs, readiness, and acceptance of malaria vaccine in Southwest Nigeria. Addresses misconceptions and identifies uptake barriers to vaccine implementation among healthcare workers, a key stakeholder group for vaccine deployment and community engagement
articles.title_original: Healthcare workers' beliefs, readiness, and acceptance of malaria vaccine in Southwest Nigeria: addressing misconceptions and uptake barriers.

articles.headline: Budget impact analysis (Uganda, prospective studies and literature unit costs) of RTS,S/AS01 malaria vaccine introduction. Results: Treatment costs for complicated malaria in children <5 decreased from $1.1 million to $297,465; annual vaccination cost $29 million (24% of immunization budget); 5-year total impact $145.5 million (net cost $141 million). RTS,S/AS01 introduction significantly increases Uganda's health budget; prioritized funding and cost-effective strategies targeting high-transmission areas essential for sustainability
articles.title_original: The Cost and Budget Impact of Malaria Vaccine Introduction in Uganda.

articles.headline: Age-structured deterministic mathematical model evaluating SP chemoprevention impact on P. falciparum dhps A581G resistance spread in settings where dhps K540E is near-fixed. Results: At 45% coverage, dhps A581G prevalence increased from 11% to 20.6% (3 cycles) or 27.8% (5 cycles); coverage explains 58% of resistance spread (ηp²=0.58, 95% CI 0.57-1.00), treatment cycles 9.3% (ηp²=0.093). Trade-off exists between immediate chemoprevention benefits and long-term SP resistance acceleration; coverage most influential factor affecting dhps A581G spread
articles.title_original: Modeling the Impact of Malaria Chemoprevention with sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine on the spread of Plasmodium falciparum dhps A581G mutation.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional mixed-methods study (416 mothers/caregivers, Gedarif and Blue Nile States, Sudan, 2024) of R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccine uptake. Results: Of 256 vaccine-eligible children, 36.7% (95% CI 31.1-42.8%, n=94) received vaccine; 53.1% perceived malaria as non-significant; 57.7% aware of vaccine; stockouts and geographic access identified as barriers. Suboptimal uptake not due to vaccine hesitancy but limited education and health system barriers; improved communication and supply chain sustainability needed
articles.title_original: Suboptimal uptake of the malaria vaccine in Sudan: what's behind the blinds?

articles.headline: In vitro model of cerebral malaria examining P. falciparum hemozoin role in brain endothelial barrier disruption. Results: Barrier-disrupting activity localized to hemozoin-associated biomolecules, not the crystal itself; protease treatment inhibited disruption; nuclease treatment had no effect. Proteins associated with hemozoin, not hemozoin crystal alone, mediate P. falciparum-induced human brain microvascular endothelial cell barrier disruption, identifying potential new therapeutic targets for cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum hemozoin-associated biomolecules induce brain endothelial cell barrier disruption in an in vitro model of cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: OpenMalaria individual-based stochastic modeling of RTS,S/AS01 fourth-dose timing in perennial transmission settings. Results: Primary series averts 73-93% of uncomplicated cases regardless of fourth-dose timing; fourth dose averts additional 8-38% uncomplicated and 9-36% severe cases; flexibility exists between 6-12 months post-third dose. Fourth dose at 15-21 months of age (6-12 months after third dose) with high coverage optimizes cases averted; timing can be tailored to country-specific health touchpoints
articles.title_original: Timing of the fourth dose of RTS,S/AS01 malaria vaccine in perennial settings: a modelling study.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey (women with live births in prior 24 months, Kisumu and Migori, Kenya) with mediation analysis of extreme weather on IPTp₃+ through ANC attendance. Results: IPTp₃+ lower in weather-exposed (49.5%) vs unexposed (58.8%); extreme weather associated with reduced ANC4+ (-0.23, 95% CI -0.41 to -0.05); 76.6% of total effect (-0.45, 95% CI -0.68 to -0.23) mediated through ANC unadjusted; effect attenuated after adjustment. Weather reduced IPTp₃+ uptake primarily through ANC disruption; underlying socioeconomic vulnerabilities important after adjustment
articles.title_original: Extreme weather events and malaria prevention in pregnancy: a mediation analysis of antenatal care and sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine use in two malaria-endemic counties of Kenya.

articles.headline: Case report of 23-year-old Colombian woman with P. vivax malaria (8200 parasites/μL) and acute dengue (NS1+, IgM+/IgG−) who developed acute left ACA ischemic stroke with right-leg monoparesis. Treated with artesunate-primaquine, supportive care, and aspirin; neurologic function improved. Authors conclude P. vivax-dengue coinfection can cause ischemic stroke through synergistic endothelial injury; early recognition and coinfection screening essential in endemic areas
articles.title_original: Ischemic stroke during dengue-Plasmodium vivax coinfection in a young woman: a case report.

articles.headline: Biochemical study demonstrating that two P. falciparum PPCS (phosphopantothenoylcysteine synthetase) proteins associate into a unique heteromeric complex with prokaryote-like activity unable to use ATP, unlike eukaryotic PPCS. Complex is target of multiple antiplasmodial pantothenate analogs. Findings offer opportunities for designing novel antimalarial inhibitors exploiting the unique features of PfPPCS while avoiding human counterpart toxicity
articles.title_original: The Plasmodium falciparum PPCS is a unique heteromeric complex with prokaryote-like activity and is a target of pantothenate analogs.

articles.headline: Survey across five African countries of 254 stakeholders (pregnant women n=75, CHWs n=53, clinicians n=75, policymakers n=51) on preferences for next-generation IPTp products versus sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine. Safety in all trimesters ranked most important; long-acting injectable with ≥3 months' coverage preferred by all groups. Three-quarters of pregnant women preferred antenatal clinic delivery. Authors recommend long-acting injectables and hybrid delivery models to improve adherence and uptake
articles.title_original: Improving malaria chemoprevention coverage in pregnancy: Surveying stakeholder preferences for new product profiles and community-delivery approaches across five African countries.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis protocol (prospective; no results yet) to pool laboratory-confirmed pediatric malaria data across Nigeria using WHO case definitions. Will estimate national prevalence of severe versus uncomplicated malaria, identify associated factors, and assess moderators including age, sex, socioeconomic status, and geography. Registered PROSPERO CRD420251163322
articles.title_original: Burden, risk factors, and clinical outcomes of pediatric malaria in Nigeria: A systematic review and meta-analysis protocol.

articles.headline: Microarray profiling of 22 P. falciparum clinical isolates (uncomplicated n=6, cerebral malaria n=4, hepatic dysfunction n=12) revealed distinct mitochondrial sense and natural antisense transcripts by disease group. Cerebral malaria cluster showed upregulation of tricarboxylic-acid-cycle and electron-transport genes despite known reduced mitochondrial activity. Authors nominate natural antisense transcripts from mitochondrial genome as potential non-conventional antimalarial targets
articles.title_original: Differential expression of mitochondria-associated genes in clinical samples of Plasmodium falciparum showing severe manifestations.

articles.headline: In silico analysis of 16 experimentally validated P. falciparum CD8+ T cell epitopes (CSP, AMA1, TRAP) across 14 geographic subregions and 78 countries. CSPep5 (IQNSLSTEW) demonstrated highest global coverage (>85%) and West African coverage (93.35%); CSPep4 showed minimal coverage (<5% Africa, <2% other). Eight epitopes were 100% conserved. Authors recommend combining conserved epitopes with broad HLA coverage for universal multi-epitope vaccine design
articles.title_original: Population HLA coverage of experimentally characterized P. falciparum CD8 (+) T cell epitopes as potential components of a multi-epitope malaria vaccine construct: an in silico insight.

articles.headline: Case report of 40-year-old man with P. falciparum malaria who developed anti-NMDAR encephalitis 11 days after parasite clearance, presenting with seizures, altered consciousness, and acute psychosis. CSF anti-NMDAR antibodies confirmed by immunofluorescence and cell-based assay; no concurrent infection or malignancy. Rapid clinical improvement after first-line immunotherapy. Authors report first antibody-confirmed anti-NMDAR encephalitis following P. falciparum, expanding post-malarial neurological syndromes
articles.title_original: Anti-NMDAR encephalitis following malaria: expanding the spectrum of post-malarial neurological syndromes.

articles.headline: Exploratory study of 33 primigravid women (mean age 19 years) at delivery in Burkina Faso. Multivariate analysis identified three immune clusters by cytokine profile (IL-4, IL-10, IL-6, TNF-α, IFN-γ); the highly pro-inflammatory cluster with elevated IL-6/TNF-α was 100% P. falciparum-positive (Fisher's exact p=0.04). Authors suggest cytokine profiling as hypothesis-generating tool for future longitudinal studies on immune regulation in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Cytokine-Driven Immune Phenotypes at Delivery as Indicators of Malaria Infection Among Primigravidae in Burkina Faso: An Exploratory Analysis.

articles.headline: Machine learning study using 27,558 erythrocyte single-cell crops (128×128 pixels) for automated malaria ring-form binary classification. Dual-branch architecture combining CNN with multi-head self-attention; 10-fold stratified cross-validation plus 10% hold-out test. Model#1 achieved peak cross-validation accuracy 0.9567 and test accuracy 0.97 (macro F1-score 0.97); ROC-AUC ~0.99. Authors note high performance but external validation required before clinical use
articles.title_original: Automated Malaria Ring Form Classification in Blood Smear Images Using Ensemble Parallel Neural Networks.

articles.headline: Brief examination of intravenous drug abuse as a concern for P. falciparum malaria comorbid with HIV infection (no abstract available; summary from title only)
articles.title_original: Intravenous drug abuse - a concern for falciparum malaria along with HIV.

articles.headline: Participatory co-creation workshop in Cameroon (October 2021) with government health, IDPs, and CHWs to design interventions for conflict-affected malaria care. Three innovations co-created: Community Health Participatory Approach (CoHPA) with community-led accountability; Health Voucher System covering malaria testing, treatment, and transport; Supportive Supervision Model for CHW capacity. Authors emphasize community ownership but identify concerns about scalability, sustainability, and health system integration
articles.title_original: Co-creation of community-based innovations to improve access to malaria treatment in conflict-affected regions of Cameroon.

articles.headline: National landscape analysis of Ethiopia's 2024 malaria resurgence (7.3 million confirmed cases, 1,157 deaths—highest in 7 years) across six operational domains. Insecticide-treated net ownership ~64% nationally with lower coverage in high-burden areas; indoor residual spraying in ~half of targeted zones; digital surveillance operational in ~80% of facilities but with delayed reporting; ~half of facilities achieved timely commodity replenishment within 3 months. Authors identify systemic gaps and recommend real-time surveillance, vector control adaptation, and community-centered prevention approaches
articles.title_original: Resurgence of malaria in Ethiopia: a national landscape analysis of systemic and operational gaps.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study (190 children, Uganda) found 75.3% P. falciparum RDT-positive but only 13.2% microscopically parasitemic; intestinal helminths rare (3.2%: A. lumbricoides 0.5%, H. nana 1.6%, S. mansoni 1.1%), all helminth-positive children were malaria RDT+. Low malaria-helminth coinfection reflects high efficacy of national mass drug administration for helminth control
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Coinfection with Malaria and Intestinal Helminths among School-Aged Children in Nwoya District, Northern Uganda: A Cross-Sectional Study.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study (362 participants, Ghana: 71.8% adults, 69.3% female) of P. falciparum parasitemia linked hematological and hepatic alterations—adults with high parasitemia showed significantly lower Hb, RBC, MCV, MCH, and PLT, with elevated ALT and AST (p<0.05), but no significant renal dysfunction. Findings underscore need for routine monitoring to prevent severe complications
articles.title_original: Plasmodium Parasitemia and Its Relationship With Hematological, Liver, and Kidney Function of Patients With Falciparum Malaria in the Eastern Region, Ghana.

articles.headline: Mathematical compartmental model of imported malaria in Morocco (data 1990–2023) incorporating human immigration. Parameters estimated via least-squares, Extended Kalman Filter, and LSTM neural networks. Numerical results demonstrate significant reduction in human and vector infections through combination of awareness, prophylactic treatment, infected-person treatment, and insecticide vector control, emphasizing importance of migration-accounting surveillance
articles.title_original: Data-driven modeling of imported malaria in Morocco and the impact of population migration.

articles.headline: Mixed-method cross-sectional study (200 pregnant women, Nigeria) found 34% had good malaria preventive knowledge; 62.5% owned LLINs but 47.5% used them; 50.8% used ≥1 dose IPTp-SP (sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine), 7% ≥3 doses. Multivariable analysis: occupation, knowledge, and gravidity significantly associated with IPTp-SP uptake. Barriers: stockouts, transportation costs, distance, preference for faith-based homes over ANC
articles.title_original: Factors influencing the utilisation of malaria preventive services during pregnancy in Ile Ife, Nigeria: a mixed method approach.

articles.headline: Ghana FDA pharmacovigilance database (2,097 ADRs, 2015–2023) for sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine–amodiaquine (SPAQ) seasonal malaria chemoprevention in 3–59-month children: most common ADRs were fever (22.7%), vomiting (21.5%), diarrhea (16.9%); serious reactions (8.6%) mostly hospitalization; incidence declined from 220.63 per 100,000 doses (2015) to 0.34 (2023). SPAQ generally safe; enhanced healthcare professional engagement in reporting needed
articles.title_original: Incidence and trends of adverse drug reactions during seasonal malaria chemoprevention among children: evidence from Ghana's pharmacovigilance database, 2015-2023.

articles.headline: Malaria vector surveillance in Indonesia using COX1 phylogenetics revealed 8 monophyletic clades (≥99% bootstrap support for 6); cryptic diversity detected—specimens morphologically identified as An. aconitus/minimus molecularly confirmed as An. flavirostris. An. sundaicus dominated Bangsring (68%); An. flavirostris, Hargowilis (13%). Misidentification risks ineffective vector control; molecular tools essential for accurate surveillance
articles.title_original: Malaria Vector Surveillance in Indonesia: COX1 Phylogenetic Reveals Monophyletic Clades and Cryptic Diversity in Anopheles Mosquitoes.

articles.headline: Analysis of 101 P. falciparum isolates (Northeast India) revealed significant Pfgch1 gene copy number variation, with higher copy numbers significantly associated with Pfdhfr and Pfdhps mutations conferring sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance. Positive correlation observed between Pfgch1 amplification and antifolate-resistance mutations, suggesting Pfgch1 CNV compensates for Pfdhfr/Pfdhps mutations
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum GTP-cyclohydrolase 1 (Pfgch1) Gene Copy Number Variation and Its Implications in antimalarial resistance from Northeast India.

articles.headline: Conditional mutagenesis study in P. berghei showed PbSERA3 (serine repeat antigen 3) essential for hepatic merozoite egress: edited sporozoites invaded hepatocytes and developed normally through late liver stages but failed to initiate blood-stage infection. PbSERA3 localized to ookinete/sporozoite surface and was proteolytically processed. SUB1-mediated maturation of PbSERA3/PfSERA6 is conserved and critical; inhibitors may prevent clinical malaria
articles.title_original: Plasmodium berghei serine repeat antigen 3 (PbSERA3) is required for hepatic merozoite egress.

articles.headline: Opinion piece arguing malaria control programmes face structural, not merely financial or clinical, challenges: overreliance on LLINs and IRS has driven insecticide resistance and behavioral shifts in Anopheles; climate change and An. stephensi urban expansion add complexity; programme fragmentation undermines response. Integrated vector management combining surveillance, larval control, environmental management, and coordinated national programmes needed for resilience
articles.title_original: Musings from a Vectosaur: Malaria in 2026.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study (100 children aged 1–5 years, Nigeria) found 55% malaria parasitemia, 49% G6PD deficiency, 28% anemia. Among malaria-positive children, 43.6% were G6PD-deficient; 37.5% of G6PD-deficient malaria-positive children were anemic. Malaria and G6PD deficiency showed significant combined impact on anemia (F=7.327, p=0.002), recommending mandatory G6PD screening before antimalarial treatment
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the impact of malaria and erythrocyte G6PD deficiency on anaemia outcomes among children in Jos Northcentral Nigeria.

articles.headline: Retrospective study (417 children with severe malaria, Gambella Hospital, Ethiopia, May 2022–May 2024, mean age 4.03 years) found overall case fatality rate 9.11%; P. falciparum accounted for 91.7% of admissions and 89.5% of deaths. High CFR associated with severe anemia (31.8%), convulsions (27%), pulmonary edema (19.7%), coma (18.5%). Convulsion, severe anemia, and comorbid illness independently predicted mortality
articles.title_original: Treatment outcome and associated factors of severe malaria among admitted children in the Gambella Region, Southwest Ethiopia: a retrospective study design.

articles.headline: Comparative analysis of dhfr-ts genes from 356 human malaria (P. falciparum n=241, P. vivax n=115) and 145 zoonotic parasites (P. knowlesi n=18, P. inui n=64, Thailand) revealed strong directional selection for quadruple IRNL mutant in P. falciparum; P. vivax showed incomplete fixation; zoonotic parasites exhibited higher mutation counts and haplotype diversity under mutation-selection-drift balance. Sustained drug pressure drives human parasite dhfr-ts fixation unlike zoonotic orthologs
articles.title_original: Contrasting evolutionary trajectories of pyrimethamine resistance in human and zoonotic malaria parasites.

articles.headline: Review synthesizing blood-stage P. falciparum iron metabolism from uptake through distribution and utilization. Describes unconventional homeostasis strategies parasites employ despite lacking canonical transporters and storage proteins, identifies key proteins and pathways, and outlines major knowledge gaps for future therapeutic targeting
articles.title_original: Fifty shades of iron: Unorthodox mechanisms of iron acquisition and utilization in blood-stage Plasmodium parasites.

articles.headline: Longitudinal cohort study in Papua New Guinea characterizing antibody responses to P. vivax antigens. Identified antigens targeted by multi-functional antibodies mediating Fc receptor interactions and complement fixation. Specific antigen subsets associated with protection from P. vivax, advancing development of highly protective multi-antigen vaccines
articles.title_original: A longitudinal study of children identifies antibody Fc-mediated functions and antigen targets of immunity to Plasmodium vivax malaria.

articles.headline: Field study in Madagascar highlands evaluated benzopyranone attractants for anthropophilic Anopheles. 4-hydroxycoumarin efficiently and selectively attracted anthropophilic Anopheles (KI=80%, SI=5.1); 7-decyloxychromone showed similar attraction but lower selectivity (KI=69%, SI=1.6). Selective attractants in mass trapping could reduce vector populations as complementary control approach
articles.title_original: Field evaluation of efficacy and selectivity of select benzopyranone attractants for anthropophilic malaria vectors in Madagascar.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 500 households in North Region Cameroon (2021) assessing malaria knowledge and control practices. Found 100% awareness, 99.6% identified mosquito transmission, yet 90.4% misused nets (35.8% animal tying, 7.4% fishing); 43.2% self-medicated, only 19% sought treatment within 24h. Stark gap between knowledge and detrimental practices
articles.title_original: Awareness, attitudes, and control measures regarding malaria and mosquitoes in the North Region of Cameroon.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 3,489 participants in Tanzania evaluated HRP2-based RDT diagnostic performance for asymptomatic P. falciparum. RDT detected 77.6% of qPCR-positives (710/915, 90% accuracy); pfhrp2/3 deletions in 11.8% of samples (2.4% dual-deletions, RDT-negative), more frequent in high-transmission villages (9.7% vs 2.2%). Results support continued RDT effectiveness
articles.title_original: Diagnostic challenges of histidine-rich protein 2-based rapid diagnostic tests due to pfhrp2 and pfhrp3 gene deletions in asymptomatic malaria in Tanzania.

articles.headline: WHO bottle bioassays across 9 facilities determined preliminary discriminating concentration of broflanilide for An. gambiae = 18 μg/bottle against insecticide-susceptible Kisumu strain. Provides benchmark for susceptibility monitoring of wild populations in sub-Saharan Africa where VECTRON™ T500 (broflanilide) is introduced for IRS-based malaria vector control
articles.title_original: Determination of the preliminary discriminating concentration of broflanilide against malaria vector mosquito Anopheles gambiae by multi-centre susceptibility testing.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 629 women in Lira City (Uganda) examined malaria control intervention utilization. ITN use high (84.1%), IPT moderate (68%), IRS low (32.8%); 96.8% knowledgeable about ITNs, 91.9% perceived IRS effective, but concerns about odor/discomfort (51.7%), safety (21.9%), effectiveness (17.3%) limited IRS uptake. Health education needed
articles.title_original: Utilization of malaria control interventions and associated factors among women of reproductive age in Lira City, Northern Uganda.

articles.headline: Spatiotemporal Bayesian analysis of 970 Colombian municipalities (2013-2023) examined lagged, non-linear climate effects on malaria incidence. Malaria risk increases non-linearly with temperature peaking at 28°C (minimum risk exposure 16.43°C) with lags 0-6 weeks; lower rainfall associated with higher risk (exposure minimum 0.85 mm). Specific climate thresholds enable early warning systems
articles.title_original: Lagged effect of temperature and rainfall on malaria incidence in Colombia (2013-2023): An approach with Bayesian spatiotemporal adjustment.

articles.headline: Laboratory olfactometer study of An. stephensi tested four host-odor compounds at 5 dilutions. Three compounds significantly attracted mosquitoes when combined with CO2 (P<0.05): hexanoic acid (most attractive), 3-methyl-1-butanol, and L-lactic acid. Most promising blend (3-methyl-1-butanol + hexanoic acid) significantly attracted mosquitoes (P<0.05), pending field validation
articles.title_original: Comparative evaluation of synthetic attractants against an important malaria vector, Anopheles stephensi (Diptera: Culicidae) mosquitoes in laboratory conditions.

articles.headline: Analysis of 948 febrile patients in Senegal comparing RDT/microscopy to PCR for malaria diagnosis. PCR detected 75.29% with malaria (70.25% non-falciparum: P. ovale, P. vivax, P. malariae); microscopy missed 31.5%, RDT missed 18.92% of PCR-positives. Half of non-falciparum mono-infections misdiagnosed as P. falciparum, highlighting diagnostic limitations
articles.title_original: Malaria misdiagnosis among febrile patients in Senegal: implications for the underestimation of Plasmodium species diversity and malaria burden.

articles.headline: In vitro/in silico study of chitosan-polycaprolactone nanoparticles (QN-PCL/CS NPs) loaded with quinine for malaria treatment. Achieved 81.8±6.9% encapsulation efficiency; increased safety (cytotoxicity IC50: 131.93→345.93 µg/mL, Vero cells) while enhancing antiplasmodial activity against chloroquine-resistant P. falciparum FCR3 (IC50: 130.12→32.56 ng/mL), achieving ~10-fold selectivity index increase
articles.title_original: Fabrication and evaluation of quinine-loaded chitosan-polycaprolactone core-shell polymeric nanoparticles for the treatment of malaria: an in vitro and in silico study.

articles.headline: Rodent study of rPfEF-1α (P. falciparum elongation factor-1α) with CFA adjuvant vaccine. Increased survival by 50% with half of survivors parasite-negative (vs all controls patent); produced 16-fold higher antigen-specific IgG titer with CD4+IFN-γ+ as dominant T-cell response; validated epitope 410-FAIRDMRQTI-419. Demonstrates intracellular antigen potential
articles.title_original: Immunological response of using emulsifying elongation factor 1 alpha of Plasmodium falciparum-based vaccines in complete Freund's adjuvant.

articles.headline: Capacity-building initiative in Sierra Leone partnering 19 institutions through SORT IT courses to strengthen operational research for HIV, TB, and malaria control. Program aimed to increase data-driven decision-making and action, demonstrating SORT IT's convening power for research capacity strengthening at country level
articles.title_original: Strengthening operational research to complement Global Fund grants for HIV, TB and malaria in Sierra Leone.

articles.headline: RNA-seq of 10 P. vivax isolates from Brazilian Amazon identified 492 differentially expressed genes between high and low rosetting phenotypes. Among these, 34.96% were unannotated; 65.04% highlighted membrane proteins with adhesive properties, including PHIST proteins (10% of transcribed genes) and 6-cysteine family genes upregulated in high-rosetting isolates. Results elucidate molecular basis of P. vivax rosetting
articles.title_original: Transcriptomics of Plasmodium vivax rosetting.

articles.headline: Over 100,000 malaria cases among U.S. troops in Southwest and South Pacific in 1943; relapsing P. vivax malaria with >10 relapses common at Guadalcanal and New Guinea. ~1% of patients repatriated for chronic malaria despite low treatment mortality. Post-infection symptoms severely limited return-to-duty, highlighting need to anticipate diffuse post-infection sequelae in future disease-disrupted conflicts
articles.title_original: Post-infection symptoms in U.S. soldiers with malaria during the Second World War: major limitation to return to duty.

articles.headline: Green-synthesized silver nanoparticles from Padina tetrastromatica (Ag-PT) showed potent antiplasmodial activity with lower IC50 values than chemically synthesized nanoparticles. Untargeted metabolomics revealed Ag-PT restored malaria-disrupted fatty acid and arginine metabolism, elevating pro-resolving mediators (DHA, 14-HDHA, 18-HEPE). COX-2 identified as hub target; eriodictyol strongly binds COX-2, shifting arachidonic acid toward anti-inflammatory mediator production
articles.title_original: Green synthesis of silver nanoparticles using Padina tetrastromatica against malaria and deciphering the mechanism through machine learning-driven metabolomics and network pharmacology.

articles.headline: CRISPR-based conditional knockdown of RALP1 in P. falciparum reduced parasite proliferation, impaired schizont maturation, decreased merozoite numbers, and lowered erythrocyte invasion efficiency. RNA-seq showed widespread downregulation of invasion and host-parasite interaction genes in schizonts. RALP1 contains extensive intrinsically disordered regions with epitope hotspots. Essential for schizont maturation; potential vaccine/therapeutic target
articles.title_original: RALP1 is essential for schizont maturation and erythrocyte invasion in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Mali 2024 malaria outbreak after extreme flooding: epidemic peaked week 39 with 1,014 cases; 1.688 cases per 1,000 person-weeks over 28 weeks, highest in children <5. Extended SMC (SIR 0.50; 95% CI 0.33–0.75) and mobile health teams (SIR 0.48; 95% CI 0.28–0.82) significantly reduced morbidity. Climate-driven outbreaks require weather-based preparedness with expanded SMC and mobile services
articles.title_original: Extreme rainfall, flooding and malaria in the Sahara: outbreak analysis in Kidal, Mali 2024.

articles.headline: Collection of 187 Anopheles from Malawi sugar plantations showed >40% had fed on natural sugar sources. Olfactory choice experiment with 810 laboratory-reared An. gambiae s.l. found guava twice as attractive as sugarcane (IRR 1.97; 95% CI 1.49–2.62, p<0.001), followed by banana (IRR 1.68), mango, and melon. Local sugar-source preferences should guide context-specific ATSB design
articles.title_original: Exploring the importance and preference of sugar feeding behaviour of malaria vectors in sugar plantations of southern Malawi.

articles.headline: SC83288, a chemically distinct antimalarial, disrupts P. falciparum blood-stage development by blocking DNA replication and arresting karyokinesis; primary target is PfDNMT2 (DNA methyltransferase). Resistance arises via PfATP6 mutations enabling drug sequestration away from nuclear targets, but carries substantial fitness cost. Unique resistance profile and high fitness cost support SC83288 as promising clinical candidate
articles.title_original: Mechanisms of PfDNMT2 inhibition and PfATP6-mediated resistance to the antimalarial candidate SC83288 in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: In a P. berghei ANKA malaria mouse model (48 BALB/c mice), combination therapy with chloroquine plus encapsulated H. atra (4:1 GA/SPI ratio, 100 mg/kg) significantly reduced renal and hepatic histopathological damage compared to chloroquine monotherapy, approaching negative-control levels. Encapsulation optimized delivery of antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, reducing drug-induced and malaria-related organ toxicities
articles.title_original: Hepatorenal protective role of encapsulated Holothuria atra in a mice model of Plasmodium berghei ANKA inoculation.
articles.authors: Utami PD, Setianingsih H, Sari DRT

articles.headline: DMEM/F12 supplemented with 20% FBS (DFS20) provides unified culture medium supporting robust growth of P. falciparum strains (3D7, Dd2, HB3, V1/S), B. duncani, B. divergens, and B. MO1 comparable to species-specific media. Enables standardized cross-species drug efficacy assays; recent screening identified potent antifolate candidates with broad-spectrum activity. Scalable platform for harmonized pan-antiparasitic drug development
articles.title_original: A Standardized Culture Medium for Comparative Drug Efficacy Evaluation Across Plasmodium and Babesia Species.

articles.headline: Province-stratified SEIR model evaluated RTS,S and R21 vaccines in Zambia (2024–2028). RTS,S averted 9.80×10⁵ malaria cases (95% UI 8.88×10⁵–1.08×10⁶) and ~1,960 deaths; R21 averted 1.09×10⁶ cases (95% UI 9.91×10⁵–1.21×10⁶) and ~2,188 deaths. RTS,S borderline cost-effective at $500/DALY threshold; R21 highly cost-effective and potentially dominant. Provincial stratification guides targeted deployment
articles.title_original: Provincial stratification and equity-weighted cost-effectiveness of malaria vaccines in Zambia under climate variability.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 706 Sudan parents (April–June 2025): 98.2% aware of malaria but only 38.0% (268/706) aware of malaria vaccine. Fathers more likely vaccine-aware than mothers (19.0% vs 10.7%, p=0.002); awareness higher in Kassala (30.6%), postgraduate education (11.9%), public sector (13.1%), and income >500K SDG (26.9%, p=0.010). Acceptance high but limited vaccine knowledge requires targeted awareness campaigns
articles.title_original: Assessment of parental knowledge, attitude, and acceptance toward malaria vaccine for children under five years in Sudan, 2025.

articles.headline: Two cases of mixed P. falciparum-P. vivax malaria from South Sudan presented with spontaneous splenic rupture, hemoperitoneum, and hemodynamic instability requiring emergency splenectomy; one patient survived with timely surgical intervention and supportive care, while the other died despite emergency management. Splenic hemorrhage is a rare but life-threatening complication of severe malaria
articles.title_original: Malaria-associated splenic haemorrhage requiring emergency splenectomy: a rare complication managed in a resource-constrained setting.

articles.headline: Cryo-electron microscopy of P. falciparum-purified tubulin revealed that parasite-specific tubulin sequences alter microtubule structure, creating smaller but stronger lateral contacts and a stiffer lattice compared to brain microtubules. These adaptations modify drug susceptibility and material properties of the cytoskeleton, demonstrating how evolutionary tubulin modifications drive architectural diversity in parasites
articles.title_original: Adaptations in Plasmodium tubulin determine distinct microtubule architectures, mechanics and drug susceptibility.

articles.headline: QSAR models trained on 572 hydroxamic acid structures with paired P. falciparum IC50 values achieved high validation accuracy (AUC 0.953–0.984 for models B5-B9; B7 model 81% accuracy on 60 experimentally tested compounds). Virtual screening of >36,000 compounds identified three potent antiplasmodial hits with PfIC50 values of 8, 14, and 80 nM, two demonstrating histone H4 hyperacetylation confirming HDAC inhibition
articles.title_original: Improved quantitative structure-activity relationship (QSAR) models to predict the activity of hydroxamic acids as HDAC inhibitors against malaria parasites.

articles.headline: A deep learning framework modeling CRISPR gene drive release in Anopheles mosquitoes found that reducing embryo resistance, functional resistance, and increasing drive conversion efficiency enhanced suppression of both mosquito populations and human malaria prevalence. The parameter space for malaria elimination exceeded that for mosquito elimination, suggesting imperfect gene drives may still effectively suppress malaria despite resistance mechanisms
articles.title_original: A simulation-based deep learning framework for spatially explicit malaria modeling of CRISPR suppression gene drive mosquitoes.
articles.authors: Allegretti YH, Sun W, Champer J

articles.headline: Genotyping 182 P. vivax isolates from eastern Cambodia (2014, 2015, 2019, 2023) using 93 microhaplotype markers showed polyclonal infections declined to 5% in 2023 from 22–48% in earlier years (p<0.05), while high-IBD infections (>0.95) increased to 81.4% in 2023 versus 8.9–10.8% in 2014–2019. These genetic signatures reflect transmission reduction correlating with clinical case decline and accelerated primaquine use since 2021
articles.title_original: Temporal trends in Plasmodium vivax diversity in eastern Cambodia evidence declining transmission.

articles.headline: No abstract provided. Title indicates a study of ecological impacts on malaria transmission in the Rio de Janeiro Atlantic Forest, Brazil during yellow fever activity, but specific findings cannot be extracted without the abstract
articles.title_original: When yellow fever silenced the forest: Ecological collapse and reduced malaria transmission in the Rio de Janeiro Atlantic Forest, Brazil.

articles.headline: Erratum/correction notice only; no substantive research findings to summarize
articles.title_original: Correction: 'Investigating the Yanomami malaria outbreak: gold mining and malaria' (2026), by de Angeli Dutra et al.
articles.authors: de Angeli Dutra D, et al.

articles.headline: Analysis of 5,746,571 confirmed malaria cases across 166 Ethiopian districts (2018–2024) found regional incidence increased 5.5-fold from 1.19 to 6.53 per 1,000. Higher lagged rainfall and temperature associated with higher incidence; IRS protection, ITN ownership, and larval source management were protective. Climate-incidence associations were predominantly spatial rather than temporal, indicating geographic ecological suitability rather than warming trends explain the resurgence
articles.title_original: Climate, environmental, and programmatic correlates of malaria resurgence in Amhara, Ethiopia (2018-2024): a Bayesian spatiotemporal analysis.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 85 mother-infant pairs in Nigeria found neonatal peripheral parasitemia prevalence of 8.2% (7/85), with maternal parasitemia as the primary predictor: all seven parasitemic neonates were born to parasitemic mothers (7/19) versus none from uninfected mothers (p<0.001). Maternal education, occupation, ITN use, and intermittent preventive therapy showed no significant association with neonatal infection
articles.title_original: Socioeconomic determinants and maternal malaria: impact on neonatal parasitemia in Osun State, Southwest, Nigeria.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 410 caregivers in Cameroon found only 26.6% demonstrated adequate knowledge about RTS,S/AS01 or R21/Matrix-M vaccines and 25.1% held positive attitudes, despite 60.7% having heard of vaccines. Caregivers whose children had prior malaria were less likely aware (AOR 0.55; 95% CI 0.28–0.97), while trust in healthcare workers (AOR 3.02; 95% CI 1.83–4.99) and routine immunization attendance (AOR 3.57; 95% CI 2.27–5.60) increased awareness
articles.title_original: Knowledge, attitudes and factors associated with the awareness of caregivers of under-five children regarding the malaria vaccine in the Tiko Health District, Cameroon: A community-based cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Erratum/correction notice only; no substantive research findings to summarize
articles.title_original: Correction to: Chemical Targeting of Prolyl-tRNA Synthetase Stalls Ovarian Development and Kills Malaria Vectors.

articles.headline: Genomic surveillance of 238 P. falciparum samples across 6 Indian states revealed contrasting regional resistance profiles: the Northeast showed high chloroquine resistance (Pfcrt K76T CVIET haplotype; Pfaat1 S258L) with quintuple/sextuple Pfdhfr-Pfdhps conferring complete sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance, while central India displayed emerging lumefantrine tolerance (Pfmdr1 Y184F, Pfaat1 S258L). No WHO-validated Pfk13 artemisinin resistance mutations were detected, supporting continued ACT effectiveness
articles.title_original: Targeted Genomic Surveillance Unveils Genetic Variations Linked to Regional Malaria Drug Resistance Dynamics in India.

articles.headline: National HbS median 6.5% with mean P. falciparum PfPR2-10 26.0%; greedy algorithm placement identified 50 screening hub candidates capturing 65.6% co-risk with 28 (56%) collocatable to existing health facilities. Ranked hub strategy enables phased newborn sickle cell screening aligned with malaria burden in northern and eastern Uganda
articles.title_original: A maximal-coverage approach to prioritizing newborn sickle cell screening sites in Uganda using a sickle cell-malaria co-risk surface.

articles.headline: An. gambiae dominated (>98%); kdr allele frequencies exceeded 65% at all sites indicating widespread pyrethroid resistance; pirimiphos-methyl susceptibility generally higher but reduced mortality observed in some districts. Findings support stratified vector control including next-generation nets, IRS rotation, and larval source management
articles.title_original: Entomological surveillance of malaria vector: species, behaviors, and insecticide resistance in southern and central Benin.

articles.headline: Analysis of pmama-1 gene from 95 P. malariae isolates identified 19 haplotypes with amino acid polymorphisms concentrated in domains I and II; positions and frequencies of changes varied across Southeast Asian countries. Substantial genetic heterogeneity demonstrated, requiring further studies with isolates from other global regions
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism and natural selection of apical membrane antigen-1 in Plasmodium malariae isolated from Vietnam.

articles.headline: Four population modification gene-drive strains (AgTP24, AgTP43, AcTP24, AcTP43) achieved 95–100% inheritance of gene-drive constructs; compound effector genes significantly reduced P. falciparum parasite prevalence and infection intensities in both An. gambiae and An. coluzzii. Demonstrates potential for durable malaria transmission control via gene-drive technology
articles.title_original: Compound effector genes suppress malaria parasite infections in gene-drive population modification strains of the African malaria mosquitoes, Anopheles gambiae and Anopheles coluzzii.

articles.headline: Across 4 Zambian provinces (5.96M population), LLIN distribution produced 35% reduction in seasonally adjusted malaria incidence (RR 0.65, 95% CI 0.50–0.85, p=0.002). Regular net replacement essential to maintain protective benefits and sustain malaria control effectiveness
articles.title_original: Malaria incidence before and after long-lasting insecticidal net distribution in four Zambian provinces, 2010-2021.
articles.authors: Mwenya RM, Ngulube ID, Richardson S
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: Systematic review since 2010 found 142/149 (95%) interventions successful in murine cerebral malaria model, but only 26% tested after syndrome development and 65% of adjuvants without concomitant antimalarial. Murine model demonstrates very poor predictive value for identifying effective human cerebral malaria adjuvant therapies
articles.title_original: Cerebral malaria: of mice and men.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: In 460 children given monthly SPAQ, parasitaemia prevalence declined 68%→41% overall; at 28d follow-up, 71%→44%. Preventive efficacy 97% for P. ovale but only 42% for P. falciparum; amodiaquine showed low parasitological efficacy while sulphadoxine/pyrimethamine contributed minimally. All 250 genotyped P. falciparum isolates were Pfcrt 76K (susceptible marker)
articles.title_original: Parasitological efficacy of seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Nampula, northern Mozambique.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: Systematic review of 8 studies on CYP450 polymorphisms and P. vivax treatment in Brazil revealed overall lack of consensus regarding relationship between Cytochrome P450 SNPs and antimalarial drug response. Most studies focused on CYP2D6 and treatment failure; implications for therapeutic outcomes remain unclear
articles.title_original: Polymorphisms in Cytochrome P450 genes: implications for Plasmodium vivax malaria treatment in Brazil.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: Case investigation with RACD around 28 index cases in Punjab, India identified 11/176 (6.3%) infected contacts; 3 (27.3%) were subpatent cases detected only by nPCR. P. vivax predominated (93.3% of 119 cases); RACD strategy may reduce spillover risk in mobile/migratory populations toward malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Detection of subpatent malaria among febrile mobile and migratory population individuals: a strategy for malaria elimination.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: Uganda's NMCD maintained malaria services during COVID-19 by rapidly implementing updated operational protocols and supplying >33,000 health workers and village health teams with PPE. Case study emphasizes importance of proactive risk planning, timely data reporting, and sustainable funding for service continuity during crisis
articles.title_original: Ensuring continuity of malaria services in times of crisis: lessons learned in Uganda during the COVID-19 pandemic.

articles.headline: Mathematical model of MDA in Senegal predicts optimal efficacy with high coverage, multiple rounds within a year, 5–6 week inter-round intervals, and campaigns starting mid-to-late July. Starting date and interval are highly correlated; optimizing operational factors for endemic-specific conditions substantially improves malaria incidence reduction
articles.title_original: Mathematical modeling for analyzing mass drug administration operational factors for efficient malaria incidence reduction in southern Senegal.

articles.headline: Female and orchiectomized male BALB/cJ mice had faster P. yoelii hepatic parasite clearance and higher baseline Kupffer cell and dendritic cell densities than intact males. Androgens increased parasite survival by reducing absolute Kupffer cell numbers and blunting Type I interferon responses, demonstrating sex-dependent immune control of Plasmodium liver stage infection
articles.title_original: Host biological sex directs immune control of the Plasmodium parasite liver stage in mice.

articles.headline: Two Phase 1b trials examined B and T cell responses to R21/Matrix-M (Kenya) and RH5.1/Matrix-M (Tanzania) vaccines in children. Higher NANP-specific IgG+ memory B cells were detected with 50μg versus 25μg adjuvant dose; RH5-specific responses were greatest with delayed booster dosing (10-10-10μg at 0-1-6months). Age, adjuvant dose, vaccine dose, and booster timing impact antigen-specific cellular responses, informing multistage vaccine development
articles.title_original: B and T cell responses to pre-erythrocytic R21/Matrix-M and blood-stage RH5.1/Matrix-M malaria vaccines in endemic settings.

articles.headline: Sh.463 yeast RNAi pesticide targeting A. stephensi Shaker gene silenced the target and produced high larval and adult mortality in insectary studies. Female-specific RNAi yeast larvicides significantly increased male:female sex ratios in offspring. RNAi pesticides targeting mosquito Shaker genes represent a novel biorational tool for integrated A. stephensi vector control
articles.title_original: Targeting the Highly Invasive Malaria Vector Anopheles stephensi using Yeast RNAi Pesticides.

articles.headline: Geospatial analysis in Zamfara State, Nigeria identified a west-to-east malaria vulnerability gradient. Hotspots in Gummi (CGMVI 3.86) and Bukkuyum (3.62) had low ITN usage (82-85%), high maternal education deficits (89-91%), and poverty (85-90%); northern areas showed lower vulnerability with ~92% ITN coverage. Getis-Ord Gi* hotspot analysis confirmed statistically significant clustering in western LGAs (z-scores 2.11-4.89; p<0.01)
articles.title_original: Geospatial mapping of gender-sensitive malaria vulnerability and ITN usage barriers in Zamfara State, Nigeria.
articles.authors: Atijosan A, Atijosan A, Salau AO

articles.headline: Study on structure-based discovery of Plasmodium falciparum DPAP-1 inhibitor and its experimental validation as an antimalarial agent
articles.title_original: Structure-based discovery of Plasmodium falciparum DPAP-1 inhibitor and its experimental validation as an antimalarial agent.

articles.headline: Among 88 imported malaria cases in Portugal (2020-2024), severe malaria occurred in 25.0%, significantly more frequent without prior infection history (p=0.027). Severe cases had elevated creatinine (p=0.009) and lactate dehydrogenase (p=0.038). Urea and parasitemia independently predicted longer hospital stay (p<0.001, p=0.016). Laboratory biomarkers support clinical risk stratification and semi-immunity in previously exposed individuals
articles.title_original: Imported Malaria in Portugal: A Retrospective Analysis from a Tertiary Public Hospital.
articles.authors: Valença Ferreira T, Alves Silva G, Duque V

articles.headline: Systematic review of 28 studies found pfhrp2/pfhrp3 deletions ranging 2.4-57.8% (Chhattisgarh 3.8%, Brazil ~18.3%, Southern Ethiopia 57.8%). Deletion rates exceeding WHO 5% threshold substantially reduced HRP2-based RDT diagnostic performance. PCR, WGS, and ddPCR surveillance are needed for effective detection in high-deletion regions. pfhrp2/3 deletions threaten RDT-based malaria diagnosis and elimination efforts
articles.title_original: Are HRP2/3 deletions silently crippling malaria rapid diagnostic tests?

articles.headline: Monthly malaria cases in Saki and Sepeteri peaked July-November (means 1,134 and 869, respectively). In Saki, SPI-12 increase of 1 unit associated with 267% case rise (β=1.299, p=0.011), moderated by temperature (β=-0.049, p=0.011). Lagged climate models substantially improved predictive fit (ΔAIC ≈47-59 in Saki, ≈45-50 in Sepeteri). Location-specific drought-temperature dynamics support integrating lagged drought indices into early-warning systems
articles.title_original: Hydroclimatic extremes indexed by SPI and malaria case dynamics in Oyo State, Nigeria: evidence for lagged, site-specific effects.

articles.headline: Plasmodium chabaudi AS infection in mice induced thymic atrophy at 20-30% parasitemia with decreased weight, cellularity, loss of DP/SP thymocytes, and increased apoptosis. Elevated serum IFN-γ, TNF-α, IL-6, and CXCL9 correlated with atrophy severity. After parasitemia clearance, thymic structure and cellularity recovered, demonstrating that even non-lethal P. chabaudi infection causes severe but reversible thymic dysfunction
articles.title_original: Thymic Atrophy Development in a Non-Lethal Plasmodium Infection.

articles.headline: Study examining how CCND3 enhancer variant reduces malaria risk
articles.title_original: CCND3 enhancer variant reduces malaria risk.

articles.headline: Among 71 malaria cases in Kolkata, retinopathy appeared in 75% (9/12) of severe cases versus 3.4% (2/59) of uncomplicated cases (p<0.05); severe cases included 8 P. vivax, 3 P. falciparum. Malarial retinopathy had 75% sensitivity, 96.6% specificity, and 81.8% positive predictive value for identifying severe malaria. Direct ophthalmoscopy-detected retinal changes serve as important clinical biomarker for severe malaria
articles.title_original: A Study on Prevalence of Malarial Retinopathy among Malaria Cases and to Determine Its Presence as a Marker of Severe Malaria: Observations from Eastern India.

articles.headline: Nested-PCR optimization for Plasmodium malariae 18S rRNA used MAL1/MAL2 primers with optimal conditions of 35 first-round cycles, 25 second-round cycles, and 54°C annealing. MAL1/MAL2 showed high specificity for P. malariae with no amplification of P. falciparum, P. vivax, P. ovale, or P. knowlesi, achieving 0.5 parasites/μL limit of detection. Protocol suited for laboratory use in Southern Vietnam
articles.title_original: Optimization of Nested-PCR for the detection of Plasmodium malariae in laboratory settings in Southern Vietnam.

articles.headline: Study on how climate shocks, not just warming, threaten malaria control efforts in Africa
articles.title_original: Climate shocks, not just warming, threaten malaria control efforts in Africa.

articles.headline: Genomic surveillance of Anopheles gambiae from a Uganda clinical trial revealed distinct evolutionary dynamics under pyrethroid-only nets versus pyrethroid-PBO nets. Despite reduced indoor mosquito densities, effective population size remained large with marked allele frequency shifts at resistance loci; Cyp9k1 duplication increased under pyrethroid-only conditions but decreased with PBO, while a 2La chromosomal inversion variant and X chromosome diacylglycerol kinase locus were selected under PBO exposure, indicating alternative detoxification pathways
articles.title_original: Genetic Surveillance Reveals Differential Evolutionary Dynamic of Anopheles gambiae Under Contrasting Insecticidal Tools Used in Malaria Control.

articles.headline: A scoping review (2013–2024) found malaria causes decreased semen quality (oligozoospermia, necrosperma/azoospermia) and adverse pregnancy outcomes (higher miscarriage, preterm delivery, low birthweight, neonatal and maternal deaths), while antimalarial drugs (chloroquine, quinine, quinacrine, pyrimethamine) cause male infertility. Dengue causes temporary sperm alterations and increased premature delivery and fetal death in pregnancy, with reproductive health impacts both from infection and drug toxicity
articles.title_original: Consequence of malaria and dengue on reproductive vigor. A scoping review.

articles.headline: Structure-guided design generated PfCyRPA-EM, an epitope mimic containing blades 1–2 of the six-bladed β-propeller PfCyRPA, for blood-stage malaria vaccine development. In pre-clinical models, PfCyRPA-EM elicited antibodies with superior parasite growth-inhibitory activity at lower concentrations than full-length PfCyRPA, with higher thermostability and improved I53-50 nanoparticle expression, supporting clinical development alone or combined with other immunogens
articles.title_original: Structure-guided design of a PfCyRPA-based vaccine against blood-stage malaria.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 100 long-tailed macaques from wildlife–human conflict zones in Selangor, Malaysia (September 2022–April 2023) revealed zoonotic Plasmodium prevalence: 24% P. cynomolgi, 23% P. inui, and 4% P. knowlesi, with 51% single infections, 18% dual, and 2% triple coinfections. Phylogenetic analysis identified S-type and A-type lineages across species, emphasizing the zoonotic transmission risk and need for regular surveillance
articles.title_original: Molecular and phylogenetic analysis of zoonotic Plasmodium in long-tailed macaques: insights from wildlife-human conflict zones in Selangor, Malaysia.

articles.headline: Host transmembrane protein CD44 functions as a critical coreceptor for Plasmodium falciparum erythrocyte invasion; CD44 crosslinking significantly enhances merozoite invasion efficiency, linking early parasite attachment to the final essential invasion steps
articles.title_original: With a little help from my friend: engaging CD44 facilitates Plasmodium falciparum invasion of erythrocytes.

articles.headline: Malaria parasites express distinct ribosomal RNAs at different transmission cycle stages; derepression of mosquito-specific rDNA loci is initiated by altered NAD+/nicotinamide ratios resulting from the metabolic shift parasites undergo when transitioning to mosquito-stage development
articles.title_original: Shifting metabolism alters rRNA expression in malaria parasites.

articles.headline: Study examines the role of taurine in controlling asymptomatic malaria infections and maintaining persistent parasitemia without clinical symptoms
articles.title_original: How taurine keeps asymptomatic malaria infections in check.

articles.headline: Cost-effectiveness analysis of the BOHEMIA cluster-randomized trial in Kwale county, Kenya found that ivermectin mass drug administration (monthly × 3 months, ~64% coverage) reduced malaria incidence by 26% with intervention cost of $11.83 per person. Base-case ICER was $905.23 per DALY averted (below Kenya's GDP threshold of $974.65); probabilistic analysis (10,000 iterations) gave median ICER $1107.51/DALY averted (50% credible interval 770.05–1606.77). iMDA is cost-effective as supplementary vector control in moderate-transmission settings with good ITN coverage
articles.title_original: Cost and cost-effectiveness of ivermectin mass drug administration for malaria control in Kwale county, Kenya: a modelling analysis of a cluster-randomised trial.

articles.headline: Discussion of a new vector control or diagnostic tool in the context of current challenges and trade-offs in malaria control decision-making
articles.title_original: A new tool at a complex time for malaria decision making.

articles.headline: Longitudinal plasma metabolomics in 199 Gambian individuals during high and low malaria transmission periods showed taurine levels increase during low-transmission seasons with asymptomatic P. falciparum infection. Taurine strongly inhibited and prevented malaria-infected RBC adhesion via PfEMP1 to CD36 and endothelial protein C receptor (linked to cerebral malaria), revealing a role for taurine in reducing cytoadhesion and symptomatic malaria severity
articles.title_original: Longitudinal plasma metabolomics reveals a role for taurine in asymptomatic malaria and seasonal persistence.

articles.headline: The rs112233623-T variant in the CCND3 (cyclin D3) erythroid enhancer disrupts SMAD3 binding, reducing cyclin D3 expression and suppressing the G1–S transition in erythroblasts. P. falciparum growth is impaired in erythrocytes from rs112233623-T carriers, correlating with elevated ROS levels; positive selection signatures exist in malaria-endemic Sardinia, demonstrating that reduced CCND3 expression is a malaria-resistance mechanism via the ROS pathway, analogous to G6PD deficiency protection
articles.title_original: Reduced cyclin D3 expression in erythroid cells protects against malaria.

articles.headline: Study demonstrating that repression of a cell-cycle regulatory protein (cyclin D3) confers protection against malaria through alterations in erythrocyte biology and parasite growth
articles.title_original: Malaria is hindered by repression of a cell-cycle protein.

articles.headline: All 161 P. vivax infections cleared by day 3 with artesunate 2 mg/kg/day, but 49 (30%) showed slow clearance including 9 (6%) with half-life >5h. Gene expression analysis revealed fast-clearing parasites displayed immediate transcriptomic changes while slow-clearing parasites had delayed response with downregulation of haemoglobin endocytosis and digestion genes, suggesting delayed hemoglobin metabolism may allow parasite persistence and facilitate artemisinin resistance emergence
articles.title_original: Parasite clearance in patients with Plasmodium vivax monoinfection treated with artesunate in Cambodia: an observational secondary analysis of trial data.

articles.headline: Previous blood-stage Plasmodium yoelii exposure compromised CD8+ T cell responses to radiation-attenuated P. berghei sporozoites (RAS) vaccine through haemozoin-mediated impairment of dendritic cell antigen uptake. A lipid nanoparticle-mRNA vaccine encoding CD8+ epitopes overcame this defect and restored sterilizing protection; combined RAS-plus-mRNA enhanced liver-resident memory T cells and protection in malaria-experienced mice
articles.title_original: mRNA vaccination overcomes haemozoin-mediated impairment of whole-parasite malaria vaccines in mice.

articles.headline: In vitro study of P. falciparum invasion of erythroblasts from 6 human subjects with normal, heterozygous, or hemizygous G6PD Viangchan (871G>A): parasites invaded polychromatic erythroblasts and matured to trophozoites with no significant differences in percentages or developmental stages between G6PD genotype groups, with expanded cohort validation recommended
articles.title_original: Invasion and development of Plasmodium falciparum in erythroblasts of humans carrying G6PD viangchan.

articles.headline: Duplex ddPCR assays for P. vivax parasitemia (targeting tubulin/RHCE) and G6PD Viangchan (871G>A) genotyping achieved: parasitemia LOD 3.2 parasites/µL, LOQ 400 parasites/µL, >90% accuracy, <20% coefficient of variation; G6PD assay 100% sensitivity and specificity. Clinical samples showed chloroquine reduced parasitemia 99.2–99.9% by day 2 with complete clearance by day 3
articles.title_original: Development and implementation of droplet digital PCR assays for accurate quantification of Plasmodium vivax parasitemia and G6PD viangchan genotyping.

articles.headline: Phase 2 trial of 100 uncomplicated malaria patients comparing artesunate-pyronaridine (AP), artesunate-pyronaridine-atovaquone-proguanil (APAP), and artesunate-fosmidomycin-clindamycin (AFC) found PCR-corrected ACPR at day 28 of 100% (AP/APAP) and 97% (AFC); at day 42, 87.5%, 85.3%, and 94.4% respectively. No differential efficacy observed; all regimens safe with mild-to-moderate treatment-emergent adverse events
articles.title_original: Artesunate-pyronaridine-atovaquone-proguanil and artesunate-fosmidomycin-clindamycin compared with standard artesunate-pyronaridine for the treatment of uncomplicated malaria (MultiMal): a randomised, controlled, clinical, phase 2 trial in Gabon and Ghana.

articles.headline: Geotemporal model integrating 25 years of African climate, malaria burden, and socioeconomic data projected 123 million (49.5–203 million) additional malaria cases and 532,000 (195,000–912,000) deaths in Africa 2024–2050 under current control levels. Extreme weather events emerged as primary driver, accounting for 79% (50–94%) of additional cases and 93% (70–100%) of additional deaths, predominantly intensifying existing endemic areas
articles.title_original: Projected impacts of climate change on malaria in Africa.

articles.headline: In vitro cytokine-induced killer (CIK) cells inhibited P. falciparum growth and reduced parasitemia after 24h through secretion-dependent cytotoxicity involving granzyme B, granulysin, and perforin acting synergistically. CIK cells showed comparable efficacy to chloroquine; artemisinin combined with CIK showed slight enhancement over artemisinin alone, supporting CIK as potential cell therapeutic approach against malaria
articles.title_original: Cytokine-induced killer (CIK) cells inhibit Plasmodium falciparum parasitemia through cytolytic effector activity in vitro.

articles.headline: PCR analysis of 1,030 febrile samples from Central India (June–November 2020) identified 27% (280) infected with Plasmodium: 18% mono-P. falciparum, 0.5% mono-P. vivax, 8% mixed infections—all sub-microscopic (undetected by microscopy/RDT). Follow-up of 9 mixed-infection patients over 12 months showed no recurrences or clinical development, yet high sub-microscopic burden poses significant elimination challenge
articles.title_original: High Burden of Febrile Sub-microscopic Plasmodium Mixed Infections in Central India: A Cross-Sectional Study.

articles.headline: P. berghei study identified EMAP3, a GPI-anchored protein exported to the outer surface of infected RBCs where it likely interacts with EMAP1. Parasites lacking EMAP3 showed no significant reduction in growth or CD36-mediated sequestration, suggesting EMAP3 functions as a protein scaffold for displaying P. falciparum antigens on P. berghei-infected RBCs to screen putative cytoadherence inhibitors
articles.title_original: Erythrocyte Membrane Protein 3 (EMAP3) Is Exposed on the Surface of the Plasmodium berghei Infected Red Blood Cell.

articles.headline: Mixed-methods study in Dominican Republic (489 household survey with trust scales plus 66 qualitative interviews) found trust module reliability of ordinal α=0.70; institutions rated 'very trustworthy' while neighbors and associations rated 'somewhat trustworthy.' Thematic analysis identified two value domains—trust linking people-institutions and solidarity/reciprocity linking people-to-people—both contributing to malaria intervention outcomes
articles.title_original: The importance of values in a community-based response to malaria in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic: results from a longitudinal, mixed-methods study.

articles.headline: Prospective cohort of 269 laboratory-confirmed P. vivax patients in tribal Odisha (January–December 2024) found chloroquine and artesunate-SP adherence >93%, but only 58.7% (95% CI 52.6–64.9) completed the 14-day primaquine regimen. Treatment discontinuation driven by symptom resolution (61.9%) and forgetfulness (21.4%); older age and household malaria history predicted improved adherence despite adequate drug supply and dosing
articles.title_original: Suboptimal primaquine adherence in Plasmodium vivax malaria: Evidence from high-burden tribal districts in Odisha.

articles.headline: In silico analysis of 39 full-length P. knowlesi Pk32 sequences from Malaysian Borneo showed low nucleotide diversity (π=0.0061), no geographical clustering, and strong evidence of purifying selection. B-cell epitope prediction identified three common epitopes with two conserved across all sequences (145PKERES150 and 167DIGKKQNS174), indicating Pk32 is a compelling blood-stage vaccine candidate against P. knowlesi
articles.title_original: In silico profiling of the plasmodium knowlesi 32 kDa antigen: Diversity, epitope prediction, and structural modeling.

articles.headline: Sucrose-preserved LAMP assay achieved 12-day room-temperature stability with analytical sensitivity of 10 copies/μL and 100% specificity for P. knowlesi detection, eliminating refrigeration dependence and enabling field deployment in resource-limited malaria surveillance
articles.title_original: A cold-chain free loop-mediated isothermal amplification test for detection of human malaria in point-of-care settings.
articles.authors: Rosli NN, Lau YL, Lai MY

articles.headline: Knockdown of clathrin heavy chain (PfCHC) in P. falciparum resulted in abnormally long cytostome tubes and impaired hemoglobin uptake, demonstrating that clathrins are involved in parasite cytostome processes
articles.title_original: Clathrins Are Involved in the Endocytosis of Host Cytosol in the Malaria Parasite.

articles.headline: Sequencing of Kelch-13 amplicons from 40 P. falciparum-positive samples identified three non-synonymous mutations (K430E, W706C, Q707K) in Kogi State, Nigeria; none were previously associated with artemisinin resistance
articles.title_original: Three new single nucleotide polymorphisms identified in Plasmodium falciparum isolates from Kogi State, Nigeria.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 3,363 ACT samples across four East African countries found 11.2% overall failure rate (range 4.2-33.3%), but clinical studies reported ≥90% polymerase chain reaction-corrected cure rates; emerging K13 mutations threaten long-term ACT effectiveness despite current high efficacy
articles.title_original: Artemisinin-Based Combination Therapies in East Africa: A Systematic Review of Quality, Clinical Efficacy, and Regulatory Progress Towards Sustainable Malaria Control.

articles.headline: Amodiaquine competitively inhibited P. falciparum plasmepsin V catalytic activity with IC50 of 0.42±0.04 μM by engaging the substrate-binding site, while clioquinol failed to inhibit; findings support amodiaquine repurposing for targeted antimalarial therapy
articles.title_original: Inhibiting catalytic activity of Plasmodium falciparum aspartate protease plasmepsin V: A biochemical approach to malaria intervention.
articles.authors: Prajapati AK, Kumar Y, Choudhary S

articles.headline: Ilex umbellulata bark extract (LD50 >2000 mg/kg) reduced parasitaemia to 5.00±0.97% in P. berghei-infected mice at 200 mg/kg dose, achieving 85.65±2.77% parasite suppression and 15.33±2.82 days survival, supporting its use as a promising herbal antimalarial candidate
articles.title_original: Sub-acute toxicity studies and dynamics-based molecular insights on the in vivo antimalarial activity of Ilex umbellulata (Wall.) Loes bark in Plasmodium berghei-infected mice.

articles.headline: Zanthoxylum chalybeum was most potent against P. falciparum (IC50 0.81-1.28 μg/ml), followed by Albizia coriaria and Entada abyssinica (IC50 10-50 μg/ml); all three demonstrated promising antiplasmodial activity supporting traditional use in eastern Uganda without severe acute toxicity
articles.title_original: In vitro antiplasmodial activity, acute toxicity and phytochemical quantification of selected medicinal plants used for the management of uncomplicated malaria in eastern Uganda.

articles.headline: Network meta-analysis of 22 RCTs (N=14,705) showed moderate certainty that monthly IPTp-sulphadoxine/pyrimethamine reduced maternal parasitaemia and anaemia versus placebo; 28-day polymerase chain reaction-corrected parasitological cure rates informed efficacy comparisons across chemoprevention options
articles.title_original: Comparative effect of chemoprevention in malaria infection during pregnancy: A network meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Unable to summarize: no abstract provided. Title indicates this case report describes acute acalculous cholecystitis and post-artesunate hemolytic anemia as two rare complications in severe malaria
articles.title_original: Acute Acalculous Cholecystitis and Post-artesunate Hemolytic Anemia: Two Rare Complications in Severe Malaria.

articles.headline: Conditional knockout of PfExomit1 in P. berghei sporozoites revealed that this mitochondrial ssDNA exonuclease, localized to mtDNA in both liver and blood stages, is essential for transition from hepatic to blood-stage infection
articles.title_original: Plasmodium mitochondrial exodeoxyribonucleases, Exo(mit1) and Exo(mit2), are conserved within alveolates with Exo(mit1) essential for the establishment of blood-stage infection.

articles.headline: Hydrogel microarray patches delivering primaquine and chloroquine achieved 40 mg per-drug delivery (70% efficiency) over 24 hours with Cmax values 2.5-fold (CQ) and 3.3-fold (PQ) higher than oral dosing; murine malaria efficacy studies showed 99.8% parasitaemia reduction
articles.title_original: Formulation and evaluation of hydrogel-forming microarray patches for transdermal primaquine and chloroquine delivery in malaria therapy.

articles.headline: Population pharmacokinetics in 75 women (25 non-pregnant, 30 second trimester, 20 third trimester) receiving artemether-lumefantrine 80/480 mg showed pregnancy increased clearance 23% and decreased drug exposure (AUC GMR 0.76, Cmax GMR 0.79), despite 100% polymerase chain reaction-corrected cure in both groups, raising treatment-failure risk if drug susceptibility is reduced
articles.title_original: Population pharmacokinetics of lumefantrine in pregnant and non-pregnant women with uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Western Kenya.

articles.headline: Review of Plasmodium–Anopheles molecular interactions including ookinete-midgut and sporozoite-salivary gland invasion, mosquito immune defenses, and implications for vector control strategies targeting these protein interactions
articles.title_original: Deciphering the molecular targets of Plasmodium and Anopheline interactions for malaria control.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 819 individuals in Buenaventura, Colombia found genetic malaria resistance variant prevalence: HbS 3.1%, HbC 2.2%, Duffy 72.2%, β-thalassemia 2.1–2.8%, G6PD 11%; highest resistance genotype proportion in ages 13–26 and insular communities
articles.title_original: Prevalence of the Main Human Genetic Variants Related to Resistance to Malaria in a Population of the Colombian Pacific Coast.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 54 studies in Africa: bacterial larvicides Bti and Bs reduced primary vector larval density by 50–100%, but only 14 studies reported subsequent malaria incidence reduction (31–62%); single large-scale countrywide implementation study limits evidence-based policy
articles.title_original: Systematic literature review of bacterial larvicides Bacillus thuringiensis var israelensis and Bacillus (Lysinibacillus) sphaericus for malaria control in Africa.

articles.headline: Analysis of 2020–2024 Monthly Malaria Information System data from Madhya Pradesh showed progressive weakening of Monthly Blood Examination Rate–Test Positivity Rate correlation, indicating improved surveillance saturation and reduced underlying transmission
articles.title_original: Weakening TPR-MBER correlation: Marker of success in India's malaria elimination progress.

articles.headline: Facility-based cross-sectional study in Ethiopia: 40.5% malaria prevalence by microscopy (P. falciparum 78.4%); RDT sensitivity 95.91%, specificity 91.24%; risk factors included age <5 years, low income, no insecticide-treated net ownership, and outdoor sleeping
articles.title_original: Prevalence and associated risk factors of malaria among febrile patients at Kidist Hana Health Center Fogera District, Amhara Regional State, Northwest Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 19 datasets (3923 participants) for Biocredit Pf/Pv LDH-based RDT: pooled sensitivity 91% for P. falciparum and 96% for P. vivax; pooled specificity 99% and 100% respectively; against PCR sensitivities were 81% (Pf) and 90% (Pv), with lower performance in moderate transmission areas
articles.title_original: Diagnostic accuracy of novel Biocredit species-specific LDH-based immunochromatographic assay for Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax detection: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: P. falciparum genotyping of 176 samples from Myanmar border areas (2013–2022): MAD20 most frequent Msp1 allele (89% Myitsone, 85% Bhamo, 67% Paletwa); multiplicity of infection 1.87 in migrant workers versus 1.33–1.56 in residents; multi-infection rate 72% versus 43–45% respectively
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum genetic diversity in border areas of Myanmar assessed by genotyping merozoite surface protein genes.

articles.headline: Review of heme oxygenase-1 (HO-1) in malaria pathogenesis: HO-1 plays dual protective and pathological roles—in mice, induction confers protection against cerebral malaria and organ dysfunction, while elevated expression in human immune cells associates with heightened inflammation and severity
articles.title_original: Heme oxygenase-1 and malaria pathogenesis.
articles.authors: Wakai T, Chinedu S, Afolabi I

articles.headline: In silico virtual screening of 1549 deep-sea microbial metabolites against P. falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase identified five top compounds with superior docking scores (−12.02 to −10.61 kcal·mol−1 versus primaquine −6.920) and stable MD simulations; candidates require experimental validation
articles.title_original: Structure-Based Screening of Deep-Sea Microbial Metabolites Against Plasmodium falciparum Dihydroorotate Dehydrogenase.
articles.authors: Singh A, Rengasamy KRR, Benjakul S

articles.headline: Longitudinal cohort of 659 in eastern Uganda over 3 years: P. falciparum qPCR prevalence 47–63%; malaria attributable fraction 54–64% overall, 72% in under-5-year-olds, 56% in 5–15 years, 45% in 16+ years; nearly 50% of fevers with 10–100 parasite/µL attributed to malaria
articles.title_original: Estimating malaria attributable fraction using quantitative PCR in a longitudinal cohort in Eastern Uganda.

articles.headline: P. yoelii NSM murine model: Tim-3 downregulated on splenic macrophages during infection; Tim-3 blockade increased parasitemia and impaired macrophage phagocytic capacity; TGF-β regulated Tim-3 expression, and TGF-β stimulation enhanced macrophage phagocytosis of infected RBCs
articles.title_original: TGF-β regulated Tim-3 sustains macrophage phagocytic function and confers protection in Plasmodium yoelii NSM-infected mice.

articles.headline: GIS and remote-sensing vulnerability mapping in Abaya Woreda, Ethiopia: 38.3% of area highly vulnerable, 49.1% moderately vulnerable, 12.6% low vulnerability; high-vulnerability zones associated with lower elevations, wetlands, water bodies, greater distance from health facilities, and high population density
articles.title_original: Mapping malaria vulnerability hotspots using multi-criteria decision analysis, GIS, and remote sensing: a case study in Abaya Woreda, West Guji Zone, Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Among 711 Malawian pregnant women, iron deficiency was associated with 50% reduced P. falciparum parasitemia (95% CI 30–64%, P<0.0001); iron-deficient mice showed prolonged survival against P. berghei (15.5 vs 7.0 days, WT) and protection from cerebral malaria (83% vs 17%). Iron chelation altered parasite transcriptomic and proteomic profiles. IV iron supplementation did not increase parasitemia when paired with malaria prevention, supporting WHO iron supplementation recommendations in malaria-endemic regions with adequate prevention strategies
articles.title_original: Host iron deficiency protects against Plasmodium infection and drives parasite molecular reprofiling.

articles.headline: In 96 participants (15–64 years) with COVID-19, malaria, coinfection, or control status, coinfected patients showed elevated biochemical markers and robust IFN-γ/IL-1β responses, whereas malaria alone produced higher IL-6/IL-4/IL-10, and COVID-19 alone elevated IL-2/TNF-α. ACE2 demonstrated strong discriminatory power for disease severity (AUC 0.77 malaria, 0.85 COVID-19), implicating vascular and immune biomarkers as diagnostic and prognostic tools for early risk stratification in coinfection
articles.title_original: Elevated ANG2/ACE2 and immune responses associated with Plasmodium falciparum and SARS-CoV-2 coinfection in Cameroon.

articles.headline: Among 1,215 P. falciparum samples (2003–2018) from Loreto, Peru, hrp2/3 deleted parasites became dominant despite low RDT use, showing reduced genetic diversity, increased clonality, and fixation of hrp2-/3- parasites. Simulations required selection coefficient s≥0.03 for fixation through selection; a genetic bottleneck from malaria control increased drift likelihood 4.5–17-fold. Findings indicate hrp2-/3- fixation driven by combined demographic changes and selective advantage independent of RDT use
articles.title_original: Malaria control and the unexpected spread of diagnostic-resistant Plasmodium falciparum in Peru.

articles.headline: A systematic review identified 20 post-transfusion malaria (PTM) cases in non-endemic African countries, with 6 sufficiently detailed; P. falciparum predominated, though P. malariae occurred.
