Most detailed cases involved immunocompromised recipients presenting with fever days after transfusion, diagnosed by blood smear. Strengthening hemovigilance, refining donor risk assessment, and maintaining clinical suspicion for post-transfusion fever may reduce residual PTM risk in transfusion services
articles.title_original: Post-transfusion Malaria in Morocco and Non-endemic African Countries: A Systematic Review of Reported Cases.

articles.headline: A high-resolution UAV dataset from Mtwara and Tabora regions, Tanzania, identified 19 mosquito breeding site classes across 471 aerial images (80.5% of 585 collected). Using the Gemma 3 N E4B multimodal model achieved 96.7% loss reduction (9.620 to 0.319) over 117 training steps. The dataset and approach support development of surveillance models for malaria and dengue vector breeding site identification in rural and peri-urban settings
articles.title_original: High-resolution UAV dataset for identification of breeding sites for malaria and dengue vectors in rural and peri-urban Tanzania: An experimental approach with Gemma 3N E4B architecture.
articles.authors: Nyambo DG, Lyimo TM, Sauli E

articles.headline: A systematic review (2014–2024) of 64 studies found molecular docking dominant (32.37%), followed by in vitro assays (14.39%) and ADMET profiling (10.79%). Compounds isorhamnetin and myricetin 3-O-glucoside showed exceptional binding to Plasmepsin II and PfLDH (ΔG<−13 kcal/mol); machine learning models achieved AUC up to 0.87 for bioactivity/resistance prediction, identifying flavonoids (quercetin) and terpenoids (eugenol) as candidates. Multiomics integration improves drug discovery but in vivo data gaps and methodology inconsistency limit clinical translation
articles.title_original: Gene Expression, Docking and Machine Learning in Malaria Drug Discovery: A Systematic Review.

articles.headline: Among 384 community members surveyed in south-eastern Tanzania, 86% reported climate-related changes, 67.5% recognized climate–malaria links, 59.5% reported increased vector-borne disease risk, and 70% observed higher malaria during rainy seasons. Only 26.6% regularly received climate/health information despite 96.6% expressing interest. Findings demonstrate community awareness of climate–malaria relationships and highlight need for integrating local knowledge into climate-adaptation and malaria-control strategies
articles.title_original: Community perceptions on climate change and its impacts on malaria transmission in South-eastern Tanzania.

articles.headline: At Mbale Regional Referral Hospital, Uganda, interviews with 21 healthcare providers identified facilitators (available resources, optimal working conditions, manageable workloads, caretaker cooperation) and barriers (resource constraints, staffing shortages, delayed referrals and laboratory delays, poor patient cooperation) to severe malaria guideline adherence in children. Addressing structural resource shortages, improving staffing and workload conditions, and enhancing patient education are essential for ensuring timely and effective care
articles.title_original: Facilitators and barriers to adherence to severe malaria treatment guidelines in children at Mbale Regional Referral Hospital, Uganda: a health workers' perspective.

articles.headline: Phylogenomic analysis of 40 individuals from 11 Anopheles Leucosphyrus species (2,657 nuclear genes, 13 mitochondrial genes) reveals monkey-feeding as ancestral (Pliocene, Sundaland), with anthropophily likely evolving once in early Pleistocene via adaptive introgression. Molecular dating indicates this anthropophily evolved in response to early hominin (Homo erectus) arrival around 1.8 Mya, providing non-archaeological evidence for early hominin colonization of Southeast Asia
articles.title_original: Early hominin arrival in Southeast Asia triggered the evolution of major human malaria vectors.

articles.headline: Aurora-related kinase 1 (ARK1) serves as a key component of Plasmodium inner microtubule-organizing centers and spindle formation, controlling kinetochore dynamics. Conditional ARK1 depletion disrupts spindle biogenesis, kinetochore segregation, karyokinesis, and cytokinesis in both schizogony and gametogony, affecting parasite transmission. ARK1 forms the catalytic core of a divergent, non-canonical chromosomal passenger complex, and the ARK1–INCENP interface represents a potential multistage antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: Plasmodium ARK1 regulates spindle formation during atypical mitosis and forms a divergent chromosomal passenger complex.

articles.headline: An individual-based transmission model evaluated combining new therapeutics with seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) in children <5. A pre-liver stage therapeutic with >50% initial efficacy required protection half-life >230 days to reduce cumulative severe malaria cases by >23% during 3 SMC cycles in high transmission. Multi-stage therapeutics combined with SMC increased impact both during and after deployment, indicating clinical development should prioritize multi-stage candidates for improved malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Combining seasonal malaria chemoprevention with novel therapeutics for malaria prevention: a mathematical modelling study.

articles.headline: Compound 15 (MMV1581361), identified from structure–activity studies, retained nanomolar activity against drug-sensitive, drug-resistant, and field isolates of P. falciparum and achieved efficacy in humanized P. falciparum SCID mice at 25 mg/kg single dose. It demonstrated 90% inhibition of male gamete exflagellation, 98% reduction in mean oocyst intensity, and 88% transmission blockade through PfATP4 inhibition. Further development is deprioritized due to resistance risk and availability of more advanced clinical candidates
articles.title_original: Identification of Compound 15 (MMV1581361) as a PfATP4 Inhibitor with Transmission-Blocking Activity and In Vivo Efficacy in a SCID Mouse Model of Malaria.

articles.headline: Corrigendum to a previously published study on Plasmodium falciparum Raf kinase inhibitor's lipid-binding properties and interaction with PfCDPK1, an essential plant-like kinase required for red blood cell invasion
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "Plasmodium falciparum Raf kinase inhibitor is a lipid binding protein that interacts with and regulates the activity of PfCDPK1, an essential plant-like kinase required for red blood cell invasion" [Biochem. Biophys. Res. Commun. 749 (2025) 151350].

articles.headline: Among 302 febrile pediatric patients in Northwest Ethiopia, microscopy detected 22.2% malaria prevalence vs 18.2% by nested PCR, with highest nested PCR prevalence (25.6%) in children ≥5 years. Age, non-use of bed nets, and lack of awareness of malaria symptoms were significantly associated with Plasmodium infections (p<0.05). Study highlights the need for improved microscopy training and quality control
articles.title_original: Unmasking Malaria: Microscopy and nPCR Reveal the Hidden Misdiagnosis of Plasmodium Infections Among Febrile Pediatric in Northwest Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Phylogenomic analysis of 55 genomes revealed at least five cryptic species within Anopheles (Kerteszia) cruzii with sympatry in some locations, showing high genetic differentiation (FST 0.4–0.7). Anopheles bellator comprises ≥3 distinct lineages while An. homunculus represents a single species (FST~0.2). These cryptic species may differ in vector competence, affecting malaria transmission dynamics in the Atlantic Forest
articles.title_original: Anopheles (Kerteszia) cruzii, the main malaria vector in the Brazilian Atlantic Forest, is a complex of at least five cryptic species.

articles.headline: Kinase regression analysis of P. vivax liver-stage parasites identified a subset of host kinases regulating schizont and hypnozoite numbers and schizont size. Host kinase dependencies varied significantly across patient isolates, suggesting this heterogeneity in host phosphosignaling may underlie the variable relapse timing and frequency observed in field P. vivax infections
articles.title_original: Host kinase regulation of Plasmodium vivax dormant and replicating liver stages.

articles.headline: In Kenyan children with cerebral malaria, 40% had elevated plasma cleaved high molecular weight kininogen vs 18% with uncomplicated malaria. In P. berghei ANKA-infected mice, bradykinin receptor-null, prekallikrein-null, and PRCP-deficient strains showed reduced neurologic deterioration and brain edema compared to wild-type. Combined plasma kallikrein inhibition and artesunate reversed cerebral malaria deterioration and prolonged survival vs artesunate monotherapy
articles.title_original: Bradykinin Contributes to Vasogenic Edema in Murine Experimental Cerebral Malaria.

articles.headline: A P. falciparum line expressing GFP-destabilization domain (GFP-DD) was developed to quantify proteasome activity in live parasites. GFP-DD fluorescence inversely reflects proteasomal activity, increasing with Shield-1 ligand stabilization or MG132 pharmacological inhibition in dose-dependent manner. This live-cell assay enables quantitative monitoring of proteasome function for drug screening and resistance studies
articles.title_original: Profiling 26S Proteasome Activity of Plasmodium falciparum Monitored by a Live-Cell Assay.

articles.headline: Protocol for a scoping review synthesizing malaria epidemiology data from urban and peri-urban Bamako (2002–2025) to examine trends in prevalence, incidence, clinical manifestations, mortality, and morbidity. Will analyze how rapid urbanization and emerging threats like Anopheles stephensi influence transmission dynamics to inform tailored urban malaria control strategies
articles.title_original: Trends in malaria indicators in urban and peri-urban areas of Bamako: a scoping review protocol.

articles.headline: Among 431 clinically suspected malaria cases in West Cameroon, overall prevalence was 54.8%, with P. falciparum 53.4%, P. ovale 4%, P. malariae 0.9%, and no P. vivax detected. Mixed infections occurred in 8.9%; of 12 P. ovale isolates genotyped, 6 were P. ovale curtisi, 3 P. ovale wallikeri, and 3 mixed. Non-falciparum malaria commonly found as both co-infections and mono-infections
articles.title_original: Distribution of falciparum and non-falciparum malaria among symptomatic malaria patients in Dschang, West Region of Cameroon.

articles.headline: In Ghana's 2022 Demographic and Health Survey, only 38.7% of women achieved WHO-recommended 8+ antenatal care (ANC8+) visits. After propensity score matching, ANC8+ attendance increased facility-based delivery by 8.2 percentage points (95% CI 4.6–11.9) and optimal intermittent preventive treatment with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (≥3 doses) by 11.1 percentage points (95% CI 6.8–15.4)
articles.title_original: Impact of eight or more antenatal care visits on intermittent preventive treatment of malaria uptake during pregnancy and facility-based delivery in Ghana: a propensity score matched analysis.

articles.headline: Plasmodium falciparum early transcribed membrane protein mRNAs exported from infected erythrocytes escape cytoplasmic degradation, enter host immune cell nuclei, and bind RNA-binding proteins ACIN1 and PNN. This interaction disrupts host splicing machinery, causing widespread transcript misprocessing and altered expression of immune regulatory proteins, revealing a parasite RNA-based strategy to manipulate host immune responses
articles.title_original: Nuclear import of malaria RNA rewires splicing in host immune cells.

articles.headline: Review synthesizing diagnostic approaches for malaria, leishmaniasis, and Chagas disease endemic in Brazil, examining epidemiological landscapes and roles of immunological and molecular diagnostic methods. Emphasizes integration of clinical and epidemiological information with sensitive and specific diagnostic techniques for early detection, monitoring, and control in endemic areas
articles.title_original: Diagnostic methods for protozoan diseases: a review focused on leishmaniasis, Chagas disease and malaria.

articles.headline: Among 425 children aged 6–59 months receiving Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention (SMC) in Burkina Faso (2020–2022), 260 were malnourished and 165 had adequate nutritional status (ANS). Malaria incidence per 1000 child-months was 974.47 (malnourished) vs 214.47 (ANS); malnourished children were 1.42× more likely to develop clinical malaria (IRR 1.42, 95% CI 1.03–1.94, p=0.028). Combining SMC with nutrient supplementation is needed in food-insecure areas
articles.title_original: Malaria and malnutrition in children under the Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention (SMC) coverage in the health district of Nanoro, Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 798 Chinese workers in Uganda (2022–2023) found 44.5% reported malaria infection. Male sex (AOR=1.92), longer residence (AOR=1.38), negative prevention attitudes (AOR=2.49), and higher local transmission (AOR=1.95) increased risk; higher education (AOR=0.65) and living with spouse (AOR=0.57) decreased risk. Integrated control addressing housing, healthcare access, education, and antimalarial supply chains is critical
articles.title_original: Epidemiological characteristics, prevention and control status, and factors associated with malaria infection among overseas Chinese workforce in Uganda.

articles.headline: Case report of 61-year-old with severe P. falciparum malaria (parasitemia >25%) complicated by disseminated intravascular coagulation with thrombotic predominance, presenting with thrombocytopenia, elevated D-dimer, metabolic acidosis, and multiorgan dysfunction. Despite intravenous artesunate and anticoagulation, the patient developed progressive peripheral ischemia requiring amputation and ultimately died, illustrating diagnostic and therapeutic challenges in severe imported malaria
articles.title_original: Disseminated Intravascular Coagulation With a Predominant Thrombotic Phenotype in Severe Plasmodium falciparum Malaria: A Case Report.

articles.headline: Scoping review identified only 7 studies from 658 articles on malaria control during COVID-19 in SADC E8 countries, with half published in 2021 and none in 2020. Limited research exists on malaria programme implementation during pandemics in these priority elimination settings, highlighting the need for policies promoting programme continuation and coping strategies during health emergencies
articles.title_original: Implementation of malaria control programmes during the COVID-19 pandemic in the Southern African Development Community Elimination 8 countries: A scoping review.
articles.authors: Muzamhindo DN, Chironda G, Tsoka-Gwegweni JM
articles.journal: Afr J Prim Health Care Fam Med

articles.headline: Genetic analysis of 3563 symptomatic P. falciparum cases across 26 Ugandan sentinel facilities showed parasite prevalence 5.0–49.23% and incidence 91–1667 cases per 1000 person-years. Complexity of infection metrics correlated strongly with prevalence (r=0.79) but weakly with incidence (r=0.37). Microhaplotype-based diversity metrics reliably estimate malaria prevalence across transmission zones but have limited predictive value for incidence
articles.title_original: Accuracy of Plasmodium falciparum genetic data for estimating parasite prevalence and malaria incidence in Uganda.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 619 malaria cases at five Zimbabwean health facilities found only 16.32% (101/619) reported cross-border movement history. Of border-movement cases, 96% were aged 10–49 years, with facilities providing 84.33% of treatment. Finding suggests documented cross-border movement explains a minority of cases in this border district, though underreporting is acknowledged
articles.title_original: Role of cross-border movement on the importation of malaria cases in Mudzi district, Zimbabwe.

articles.headline: Correction notice to previously published article on blood purification therapy for severe P. falciparum malaria with cytokine storm-driven multiorgan dysfunction; no new findings presented
articles.title_original: Correction: The blood purification therapy-based strategy effectively rescued the severe falciparum malaria patient experiencing cytokine storm-driven MODS.

articles.headline: Analysis of 66 P. falciparum isolates from western Kenya (November 2024–January 2025) revealed high resistance-mutation frequencies: dhfr N51I 85.2%, C59R 75.4%, S108N 32.8%, I164L 18.0%; dhps A437G 92.3%, K540E 40%. Classical triple-mutant (N51I+C59R+S108N) and rare quintuple haplotype (N51I+C59R+S108T+I164L with A437G) circulate. Continuous molecular surveillance is needed to clarify functional significance and inform intermittent preventive treatment policy
articles.title_original: Persistent and Circulating Plasmodium falciparum dhfr and dhps Mutations in Busia County, Western Kenya.

articles.headline: ATAC-seq and RNA-seq analysis of P. falciparum at low temperature (26°C vs. 37°C) identified 1083 differentially accessible regions in ring-stage parasites (1081 gains, 2 losses) enriched in promoters, with 41 genes showing concordant chromatin and expression changes. Trophozoite stage showed no significant chromatin changes, indicating stage-specific thermal sensitivity that could inform antimalarial strategies targeting temperature-responsive chromatin remodeling
articles.title_original: Integration of ATAC-seq and RNA-seq reveals temperature-responsive regulatory regions in Plasmodium falciparum asexual stages.

articles.headline: Analysis of 92 preserved blood samples from Argentine surveillance (2013–2018) identified one submicroscopic asymptomatic P. vivax infection in a 66-year-old from Salta with no endemic travel history. This first molecularly confirmed pre-certification P. vivax case highlights risks of silent circulation in malaria-free areas and underscores need for highly sensitive diagnostics and strengthened surveillance to maintain malaria-free certification
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic and submicroscopic Plasmodium vivax infection: a study previous to malaria-free certification in Argentina.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 159 nomadic Fulani pregnant women in northern Ghana (April–June 2022) found 61.0% had good malaria knowledge but 71.1% practiced poor prevention; 54.7% owned insecticide-treated nets yet only 28.9% used them the prior night. Antenatal care attendance protected knowledge (AOR=0.31). Knowledge-practice gap and low bed net usage emphasize need for ANC-based education and culturally tailored community interventions
articles.title_original: Knowledge of malaria and preventive practices among Fulani pregnant women in the savannah Region of Northern Ghana.
articles.authors: Salifu Y, Nantomah B, Lasong J

articles.headline: Spatio-temporal modeling of 4312 Anopheles from Tanga and 1450 from Unguja, Tanzania (September 2021–September 2023) revealed region-specific climatic drivers. Tanga: abundance increased with maximum temperature and lagged precipitation; Unguja: non-linear response peaking below 27.5°C. Models achieved >0.8 correlation between observed and predicted values and accurately forecasted Unguja peaks in November 2023 preceding the Zanzibar outbreak, supporting malaria early warning systems
articles.title_original: Spatio-temporal modelling and prediction of Anopheles mosquito abundance in Tanga and Unguja, Tanzania: climatic drivers and insights for malaria early warning and vector control strategies.

articles.headline: Xenomonitoring of 446 Anopheles from 12 endemic Kebeles in South Ethiopia detected P. vivax 2.0% (9/446) and P. falciparum 0.2% (1/446); 85% (378/446) contained vertebrate blood meals predominantly from goats (67%), humans (23%), and cattle (19%). Anopheles arabiensis comprised 85% (380/446) of species identified. Vector control interventions should prioritize reducing animal blood feeding and targeting An. arabiensis
articles.title_original: Molecular xenomonitoring for identifying Plasmodium parasites and blood meal sources of mosquitoes in malaria endemic villages adjacent to the southern Rift Valley Lakes, South Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional analysis of Mali DHS/MIS surveys (2012-2021) in children 6-59 months found precipitation with 3-month lag increased malaria odds (OR 1.005 [1.002-1.009]), while minimum, maximum, and average temperatures decreased odds (ORs 0.761-0.822). Temperature effects were inconsistent across lag periods. Authors conclude precipitation may be a malaria risk factor in young children, while temperature alone likely does not contribute to infection odds
articles.title_original: Associations between precipitation, temperature, and malaria prevalence in children under 5 in Mali.

articles.headline: Mechanistic study of P. falciparum Complex III reveals dual functions: ubiquinone regeneration and mitochondrial protein processing. Knockdown of Complex III component MPPα (mitochondrial processing peptidase a) proved essential even when electron transport chain inhibition was bypassed; knockdown parasites were hypersensitive to proguanil. Proteomics identified multiple mitochondrially targeted proteins associating with Complex III. Authors propose Complex III serves both redox and protein processing roles in parasite mitochondrial physiology
articles.title_original: Dual function of mitochondrial complex III in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Genomic analysis of 21 P. falciparum isolates from Malaysia (2008-2010) compared to 9,518 regional reference isolates identified 13/15 cases as likely imported from endemic regions outside Malaysia, supported by travel histories and high multiplicity of infection; two cases showed genomic evidence inconsistent with reported travel. Identity-by-descent analysis revealed clustering in only two cases. Authors conclude malaria genomics effectively distinguishes imported from locally acquired cases
articles.title_original: Whole genome sequencing to inform the epidemiology of Plasmodium falciparum malaria in the elimination setting of Malaysia.

articles.headline: High-resolution microscopy of three P. falciparum basal complex transmembrane proteins (BTP1, BTP2, BLEB) revealed BTP2-deficient parasites fail to separate from each other despite membrane enclosure, pinpointing a specific basal complex development defect. Contractile ability remains intact. Authors identified a plasma membrane region excluded from daughter cells and associated with the basal complex, proposing a three-step biogenesis model highlighting BTP2's role in daughter cell formation
articles.title_original: Transmembrane proteins mediate basal complex assembly and individual daughter cell formation in malaria parasites.

articles.headline: Review of carbamate insecticide resistance mechanisms in Anopheles gambiae, the primary African malaria vector. Resistance arises from target-site mutations in acetylcholinesterase and increased metabolic detoxification via esterases and cytochrome P450 enzymes. Authors emphasize understanding these mechanisms is crucial for sustaining vector control effectiveness, informing insecticide resistance management strategies, and guiding malaria control policy as pyrethroid resistance increases
articles.title_original: Mechanisms of carbamate resistance in the malaria vector: Anopheles gambiae.

articles.headline: Facility-based study (n=274, Dec 2024-Feb 2025) in Ethiopia compared diagnostic methods: PCR detected highest prevalence (23.4%), followed by microscopy (20.1%) and HRP2-RDT (19%). HRP2-RDT showed 79.1% sensitivity and 94.9% accuracy; microscopy showed 100% specificity but 85.9% sensitivity with moderate PCR agreement (κ=0.904), while RDT agreement was weak (κ=0.847). Authors conclude RDTs particularly weak for P. falciparum, mixed, and P. vivax infections, possibly from low parasitemia and pfhrp2 deletions
articles.title_original: Diagnostic challenges in malaria detection: A comparative diagnostic performance of HRP2-based rapid diagnostic tests, microscopy, and PCR at Bichena primary hospital, Northwest Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Minwuyelet A, Yewhalaw D, Atenafu G

articles.headline: Qualitative study (28 interviews, 2 FGDs n=20, 12 in-depth interviews, Feb 2025) in Amhara Region explored perceived drivers of malaria resurgence (cases rose from 1.0 million in 2018 to 7.3 million by Oct 2024). Community and health workers identified ecological drivers (irregular rainfall, irrigation, water storage), health system gaps (antimalarial/diagnostic stockouts, weak vector control), and behavioral factors (low perceived risk, ITN misuse, traditional healers). Authors conclude resurgence stems from interacting ecological, health system, and behavioral dynamics exacerbated by conflict
articles.title_original: Understanding malaria resurgence in the Amhara Region, northwestern Ethiopia: a qualitative study of perceived drivers from stakeholder and community perspectives.

articles.headline: Pilot diagnostic accuracy study (n=200; 21 positive/10.5%, 179 negative/89.5%) in Uganda evaluated expired vs non-expired malaria RDTs (SD Bioline Ag Pf/Pan, First Response Ag pLDH/HRP2). Tests up to 9 months post-expiration remained valid and consistent with reference RDTs. All tests 10-12 months post-expiration were invalid. Authors conclude preliminary findings support further research into using expired mRDTs in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Test Performance of Expired Malaria Rapid Diagnostic Tests: A pilot diagnostic accuracy study conducted in Western Uganda.

articles.headline: Quasi-experimental analysis (n=11,481 children) using Ghana nationally representative surveys examined ITN use effectiveness via multilevel regression. Sleeping under an ITN reduced malaria odds 13% (aOR 0.87 [95% CI 0.81-0.93]) with absolute risk reduction of 2.2% (RD -0.022). Spatial analysis revealed misalignment between intervention coverage and malaria burden. Authors conclude ITN use provides meaningful but limited protection; strengthening control requires attention to behavioral and contextual drivers
articles.title_original: Evaluating the effectiveness of insecticide-treated net use in preventing malaria among children under five: a quasi-experimental study.
articles.authors: Osei KM, Adiak AA, Han W

articles.headline: Comparative evaluation of YOLO object detection variants (v3, cascade v3, v4, v5, v8) for automated P. vivax detection from thick blood smear microscopy images. Authors propose novel framework combining YOLOv3 with modified MobileNetV2 backbone and Transformed Convolutional Layer (TCL) for multi-scale texture sensitivity and feature extraction from dense smear images. Findings provide guidance for deploying AI-based malaria diagnostic tools across clinical and field settings
articles.title_original: Enhanced YOLO-based framework and benchmarking for automated Plasmodium vivax detection.

articles.headline: In vitro study tested ofloxacin, levofloxacin, and moxifloxacin against P. falciparum plasmepsin II (PlmII), an aspartic protease essential for hemoglobin degradation. Moxifloxacin showed highest potency (IC50 0.15 ± 0.02 μM) through hydrogen bonding with catalytic dyad Asp34 and Asp214 (donor-acceptor distances 2.8-3.3 Å). NH group methylation disrupted these interactions and reduced inhibitory efficacy. Authors conclude precise hydrogen bonding with Asp34 and Asp214 is essential for effective PlmII inhibition
articles.title_original: Physico-Chemical Insights into the Catalytic Inhibition of Plasmodium falciparum Plasmepsin II Activity by Repurposing Fluoroquinolone Drugs.

articles.headline: Open-label RCT (n=56 completed feeding experiments, 49 completed 28-day follow-up) in Ethiopia compared primaquine 0.25 mg/kg/day×14 days plus chloroquine (CQ-PQ) vs chloroquine alone (CQ) in patients ≥15 years with P. vivax mono-infection. Before treatment: 57% (CQ) and 82% (CQ-PQ) infectious to Anopheles arabiensis. After 1 day: CQ-PQ group had significantly greater reduction in infectivity (P<0.0001), oocyst counts (P<0.001), and circumsporozoite protein (P=0.002). Authors conclude adding primaquine reduced transmission but some remained infectious 24 hours post-treatment
articles.title_original: Efficacy of primaquine-chloroquine combination on Plasmodium vivax transmission and parasite clearance in Ethiopia: a randomized controlled trial.

articles.headline: Among 721 Anopheles larvae collected Oct 2023–June 2024 across 4 Hadiya villages, An. gambiae s.l. dominated (43.82%, n=316); larval density varied by habitat—ditches 2.61 per sample, swamps 1.5, riverbeds 0.8, water pans 0.14 (F=4.005, p=0.012). An. gambiae s.l. identified as primary malaria vector
articles.title_original: Species Occurrence and Seasonal Variation of Malaria Vectors in Hadiya Zone, Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Among 5,674 children aged 6–59 months in 2021 Burkina Faso DHS, malaria prevalence was 14.1% (95% CI 13–15%), ranging 5.1–29.3% regionally; prevalence increased with age (aPR 1.46–2.07), rural residence (aPR 2.21), no electricity (aPR 2.01), and poverty (aPR 1.60). Sociodemographic and contextual disparities drive childhood malaria burden
articles.title_original: Sociodemographic and healthcare determinants of malaria infection among children under five in Burkina Faso: analysis of the 2021 Demographic and Health Survey data.

articles.headline: Across 23 Plasmodium pathogenic genomes, 724 high-confidence retrocopies were identified, largely intact with transcription comparable to parental genes in virulence-associated families (PPIase, PIR, actin-related); associated with cytoadhesion, immune evasion, stress response. Computational analysis found retrocopies expand episodically in primate-infecting lineages, contributing to genomic plasticity
articles.title_original: Comprehensive computational insights into Plasmodium retrocopies reveals potential roles in virulence-associated functions.
articles.authors: Das S, Ghosh S, Thakur S

articles.headline: Among 1,401 asymptomatic individuals across Ghana's ecological zones, malaria prevalence by PCR was highest in forest (36.5%) versus Sahel (27.6%) and coastal (23.5%); homozygous α-thalassemia versus wild type showed OR 1.23 for asexual parasites and OR 1.46 for gametocytes (p=0.447, not significant). No statistically significant association between α-thalassemia genotype and asymptomatic P. falciparum carriage
articles.title_original: α-Thalassemia has no association with asymptomatic Plasmodium falciparum carriage in three ecological zones of Ghana.

articles.headline: Overall P. knowlesi awareness was moderate (mean 10.7/23 ± 2.9); female sex, greater distance from health facilities, prior education, and higher attitudes significantly associated with awareness (p<0.05). Survey of 300 adults and interviews with 28 healthcare providers (April–May 2025, Thailand) found P. knowlesi awareness suboptimal; integrated, male-focused health promotion essential
articles.title_original: Community awareness and health providers' perspectives on zoonotic Plasmodium knowlesi malaria in Thailand: A mixed-methods assessment.

articles.headline: Among 23 patients (6 uncomplicated, 12 hepatic dysfunction, 5 cerebral malaria), 24 pir genes upregulated in cerebral malaria and 28 in hepatic dysfunction (≥50% of severe patients); cerebral malaria featured vir subfamily E (n=8) and pvpir H (n=6). Microarray analysis found pir genes differentially expressed across severe P. vivax manifestations
articles.title_original: Comparative clinical transcriptome of pir genes in severe Plasmodium vivax malaria.

articles.headline: Among 8 trials (3,486 participants), prevention trial reported parasitemia RR 1.21 (95% CI 0.56–2.62, p=0.63) for folic acid+iron versus iron alone with antifolates; most treatment trials used folic acid >1.0 mg/day. Systematic review of folic acid supplementation with antifolate antimalarials found limited evidence on effects in malaria prevention and treatment
articles.title_original: Folic acid supplementation and malaria susceptibility and severity among people taking antifolate antimalarial drugs in endemic areas.

articles.headline: Cribra orbitalia prevalence showed significant positive spatial autocorrelation across 28 medieval Dutch archaeological sites, with High-High clusters in Southwest and Low-Low in East; prevalence correlated with historical (1938) Anopheles atroparvus density. Spatial epidemiological analysis indicates malaria contributed to cribra orbitalia in medieval Netherlands, consistent with regional mosquito ecology
articles.title_original: Mapping medieval malaria in the Netherlands: A bioarchaeological approach using cribra orbitalia as a proxy.

articles.headline: Mathematical within-host models showed optimal transmission investment ~5% (lower than immunity-naive models); increased investment reduced infection duration; parasites benefited from delaying transmission investment. Immunity-incorporated model revealed adaptive immunity imposes a survival-reproduction tradeoff, shaping parasite evolution under control
articles.title_original: Immunity can impose a reproduction-survival tradeoff on human malaria parasites.

articles.headline: Among 7,173 screened individuals (2018–2022, coastal Tanzania), P. falciparum prevalence was 27.4%, P. ovale 11.5%; Pf associated with younger age, male sex, higher rainfall; Po peaked during low-Pf periods with fewer co-infections than expected; short wet season showed excess Pf-Po co-infections; Poc/Pow co-occurred in 23% of Po-positive samples. Seasonal antagonism and frequent Poc/Pow co-infections documented
articles.title_original: Seasonal Variation and Interspecies Dynamics Among Plasmodium falciparum and ovale Species in Bagamoyo, Tanzania.

articles.headline: Among 210 P. vivax infections (154 PCR-only, microscopy-negative), 72 (69.9%) cleared spontaneously within 3 surveys; Turnbull analysis: 25% persistent by day 47, 7% by day 232; clearance not associated with age, sex, nationality, occupation, or education. Longitudinal cohort (14 surveys, 2018–2023, ~5,000 individuals) found submicroscopic P. vivax frequently resolves spontaneously, indicating naturally acquired immunity
articles.title_original: Spontaneous Clearance of Polymerase Chain Reaction-Positive Plasmodium vivax Parasitemia in Northwestern Thailand: An Epidemiological Perspective.

articles.headline: Among 5.5 million notifications (2003–2023), nearly one-third of Amazon P. vivax infections acquired outside residence (1.7% international); large cities are receptive sinks with high-risk rural sources; parasites >1,000 km apart remain genetically connected. Whole-genome sequencing (408 isolates) and case mobility analysis highlight source-sink dynamics critical for targeting elimination efforts and preventing reintroduction
articles.title_original: The Source-Sink Dynamics of Plasmodium vivax May Undermine Malaria Elimination Efforts in the Amazon: An Epidemiological and Population Genomic Study.

articles.headline: Mice immunized with PfAg (chimeric peptide combining P. falciparum CSP and mosquito AgTRIO) generated robust humoral responses against both targets and had significantly improved survival versus controls after P. berghei-infected mosquito exposure (C57BL/6 and CD-1 strains), supporting new malaria vaccine development
articles.title_original: A Multivalent Peptide Vaccine Against Malaria, Targeting Plasmodium Circumsporozoite Protein and Mosquito AgTRIO.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 4,074 households (15,484 individuals) in India (September-November 2023) found malaria/dengue/chikungunya self-reported prevalence of 18.90 cases per 1,000 households (77 total cases). Socioeconomic factors (wealth, caste, family size) significantly associated with disease reporting and prevention behaviors; richest quintile more likely to adopt prevention but less likely to perceive mosquito risk. Water storage practices influenced disease outcomes
articles.title_original: Socioeconomic and household water management determinants of malaria and other vector-borne disease prevention in Urban Gujarat, India.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 7 phase I/II trials of DSM265 (P. falciparum DHODH inhibitor) showed increased adverse events (RR 1.46, 95% CI 1.15-1.84), with subgroup risks at low-dose (RR 1.86, 95% CI 1.36-2.54) and high-dose (RR 1.67, 95% CI 1.03-2.72). At 400 mg oral suspension: Cmax 9.3 μg/mL (95% CI 7.5-11.2), T½ 112.1 h (95% CI 91.3-132.9), AUC 1,601.9 h·μg/mL (95% CI 1,233.5-1,970.3). Greater selectivity for P. falciparum over P. vivax; favorable safety profile though preclinical signals halted development
articles.title_original: Evaluating the safety, pharmacokinetics, and antiparasitic activity of DSM265, a novel antimalarial plasmodium dihydroorotate dehydrogenase inhibitor: a systematic review and meta-analysis of early clinical trials.

articles.headline: Blood-reared and Bloodless™-reared Anopheles coluzzii and An. gambiae (Burkina Faso and Kisumu strains) compared across 7 generations for P. falciparum susceptibility. Wing size unaffected by diet; P. falciparum infection rates similar at generation 3 but by generation 6, blood-fed mosquitoes showed higher oocyst prevalence (intensity unchanged). Longevity significantly increased in Bloodless-fed by generation 7, maintaining traits essential for malaria research
articles.title_original: Effects of a bloodless diet on fitness and malaria susceptibility in Anopheles mosquitoes from Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: de Sales Hien DF, et al.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 220 patients from 3 N'Djamena facilities found P. falciparum prevalence of 39.54% (highest in 0-5 age group). Pfdhps resistance gene detected in 37.93% of positive cases (45.61% in 5-35 year group). A437G mutation 9.09% (42.10% in 5-35 years); A581G 54.54% (more prevalent in men 30% and Fansidar users 30%), underscoring persistent drug-resistance monitoring needs
articles.title_original: Resistance profile of the Plasmodium falciparum dihydropteroate synthase gene in three hospitals in the city of Ndjamena, Chad.

articles.headline: Among 1,192 screened individuals, 56 PLHIV and 66 HIV-negative had P. falciparum infection. Asymptomatic malaria more frequent in HIV-negative (60.6% vs 25.0%), while severe malaria predominated in PLHIV (48.2% vs 12.1%, p<0.01) with prostration (16.7%), seizures (14.3%), impaired consciousness (26.2%), severe anemia (14.3%), and hypoglycemia (11.9%). CTX chemoprophylaxis reduced parasitemia (p=0.03); median CD4 in severe malaria PLHIV was 150 cells/µL (IQR 90-204) versus 304 cells/µL (IQR 189-476) in asymptomatic, p=0.018
articles.title_original: Clinical and biological features of complicated and asymptomatic malaria among PLHIV and HIV-negative adults: An observational study in Libreville, Gabon.

articles.headline: Correction to prior article on P. vivax population genetics with transmission decline and rebound in two endemic areas of Papua New Guinea
articles.title_original: Correction: Population genetics of Plasmodium vivax with transmission decline and rebound in two endemic areas of Papua New Guinea.

articles.headline: Methanolic extracts of R. repens and A. millefolium showed dose- and time-dependent inhibition against P. falciparum and L. major. R. repens excelled against L. major (81% inhibition at 2.0 mg/mL, IC50 1.18 mg/mL, selectivity index 10.6); A. millefolium superior against P. falciparum (94% inhibition at 2.0 mg/mL, IC50 0.52 mg/mL, SI 26.3). Both showed low mammalian cell cytotoxicity, supporting natural product lead development for antiparasitic drugs
articles.title_original: In Vitro Antiparasitic Activity and Phytochemical Profiling of Rhaponticum repens and Achillea millefolium Methanolic Extracts Against Plasmodium Falciparum and Leishmania major.

articles.headline: Computational framework using ensemble learning, molecular docking, and 200-nanosecond molecular dynamics simulations evaluated Malaria Box compounds against P. falciparum. Classification accuracy 73.8% (10-fold CV) and 67.5% (external validation); regression within active/very-active classes yielded Q² of 0.810 and 0.793 respectively. Docking revealed strong binding affinities via π-π stacking and hydrogen bonds; MD simulations confirmed ligand-protein complex stability, identifying candidates for experimental evaluation
articles.title_original: Hybrid Computational Framework Integrating Ensemble Learning, Molecular Docking, and Dynamics for Predicting Antimalarial Efficacy of Malaria Box Compounds.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 29 studies (8 human studies in quantitative synthesis) examined helminth infection effects on antimalarial IgG responses. Helminth-infected individuals had higher antimalarial IgG (SMD -0.44; 95% CI -0.61 to -0.27; I²=79.7%; p<0.0001), while S. haematobium showed marginally lower responses (SMD 0.40; 95% CI 0.00-0.80; I²=92.2%; p=0.0492) in one analysis but better IgG3 (SMD -1.08; 95% CI -1.75 to -0.42; I²=90.2%; p=0.0015) in another. Conclusions uncertain; helminth modulation depends on infecting species and malaria antigen type
articles.title_original: The association of helminth infection against antimalarial antibody responses in malaria infection and vaccination: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Retrospective cohort of imported P. falciparum malaria patients evaluated PfHRP2 and pan-pLDH biomarkers for severity assessment via lateral flow assays and bead-based immunoassays. Pan-pLDH excelled at identifying severe malaria, outperforming PfHRP2. Smartphone video-analysis reduced testing time to under 6 minutes while maintaining pan-pLDH's performance in detecting severe malaria, enabling rapid clinical decision-making in non-endemic settings
articles.title_original: pLDH to identify severity in imported malaria: Implementing smartphone video analysis for rapid clinical decision-making.

articles.headline: Serological analysis of 300 serum samples (2014-2015) from Brazil-French Guiana border (102 malaria cases, 198 controls) tested IgM, IgG, and NS1 for dengue, chikungunya, and Zika. High IgG reactivity observed for dengue and chikungunya with frequent co-reactivity; minimal Zika detection. Overlapping P. vivax and arbovirus transmission highlights need for integrated surveillance strategies to address co-circulation and co-infection risks
articles.title_original: Co-circulation of Plasmodium vivax and Arboviruses in the Brazil-French Guiana Border.

articles.headline: Agent-based simulation in 2,275 Tanzanian children (1,135 intervention, 1,140 control) comparing DP with or without SLDPQ for seasonal malaria chemoprevention. At 125 days, prevalence fell to 4.1% (DP alone) vs 1.4% (DP+SLDPQ); at 180 days, 7.1% vs 3.9%. SLDPQ addition to DP significantly reduced malaria transmission and can be scaled in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Agent-based simulation of seasonal malaria chemoprevention strategy in Southern Tanzania: comparing dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine with or without primaquine.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional analysis of 2022 Tanzania DHS/Malaria Indicator Survey examining safer sex negotiation among women in unions. Prevalence 75.8%. Higher education (APR 1.16–1.21), wealth (APR 1.10–1.12), media exposure (APR 1.14), employment (APR 1.06), STI awareness (APR 1.17), and internet use (APR 1.04) associated with safer sex negotiation. Context-specific interventions needed to empower women
articles.title_original: Safer Sex Negotiation Among Women in Sexual Unions in Tanzania: Analysis of the 2022 Demographic and Health Survey and Malaria Indicator Survey.

articles.headline: Title only: Climate change could hinder malaria eradication efforts. No abstract provided
articles.title_original: Climate change could hinder malaria eradication efforts.

articles.headline: Time series analysis (January 2015–April 2024) of malaria incidence in Adamawa State, Nigeria, using SARIMAX modeling to examine rainfall and temperature relationships. Forecasts for May 2024–December 2025 project ~67,052 cases in August 2024 and peak ~80,004 cases in October 2025. Climate variability strongly drives malaria dynamics; forecasting essential for proactive public health planning
articles.title_original: Modelling the association of rainfall and temperature with malaria incidence in Adamawa State, Nigeria.

articles.headline: Author correction to article on malaria trends in Ethiopian highlands tracking 2000 global warming slowdown. No results provided in correction notice
articles.title_original: Author Correction: Malaria trends in Ethiopian highlands track the 2000 'slowdown' in global warming.

articles.headline: Qualitative study alongside SLIM ivermectin trial in Southwest Sumba, Indonesia (November 2022–September 2023); 75 participants in interviews/FGDs and ~650 in community events. Initial community skepticism of ivermectin livestock treatment mitigated by engagement and trust-building; acceptance demonstrated. Community open to human MDA but concerned about child safety. Community perception shaped by trust, engagement, and culture; sustained communication essential
articles.title_original: Community perceptions and acceptance of ivermectin for malaria control on Sumba Island, Indonesia.

articles.headline: Comparative study of gut microbiota in 8 wild Oregon Juncos infected with Plasmodium relictum GRW04 vs. 8 non-infected birds. Alpha diversity significantly lower in infected birds. Depleted taxa: Muribaculaceae, Acidobacteriales, Pseudomonas, Escherichia-Shingella. Infected birds' microbiota showed fewer network connections and missing hub taxa, consistent with parasite-mediated dysbiosis
articles.title_original: Microbial community composition variation in relation to malaria infections in Junco hyemalis.
articles.authors: Amaya-Mejia WS, Sehgal RNM, Yeh PJ

articles.headline: Descriptive study of P. vivax outbreak management in Pelele Tepoe, Suriname (November 2019–August 2021): 179 infections in 153 patients over 22 months. Six-pronged approach combined surveillance, education, vector control, mass screen-and-treat, MDA, and radical cure. Mass screen-and-treat less effective than MDA; directly observed treatment achieved 95.1% efficacy by day 180; radical cure marked outbreak end. Adaptive, multi-pronged strategy effective for low-endemic outbreaks
articles.title_original: A six-pronged approach to manage a Plasmodium vivax outbreak in a low- and middle-income country on the road to malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of RTS,S/AS01 vaccine uptake 1 year post-introduction in Western Cameroon; 399 children aged 6–24 months. Dose 1 coverage 31.20%, dose 2 22.61%, dose 3 17.70%; 56.49% completed 3-dose schedule; timeliness 50–57%. Parental perception strongly associated with vaccination (aOR 15.54 for dose 1, aOR 2.36 for dose 1 timeliness). Performance indicators below expectations; improved caregivers' perception messaging needed
articles.title_original: Uptake of malaria vaccine (RTS,S/AS01) among children aged 6-24 months: A cross-sectional survey conducted in the Western region of Cameroon, 1 year following the vaccine introduction.

articles.headline: Rapid systematic review of gender-responsive/transformative interventions in HIV, TB, malaria (2004–2024, LMICs): 11,844 articles identified, 42 included—all on HIV, none on TB or malaria. Five intervention categories: health systems (n=6), gender norms (n=9), social empowerment (n=8), economic empowerment (n=18), law/policy (n=1). Multi-component, multi-level interventions showed more sustained improvements. No primary evidence exists for gender-focused interventions in TB or malaria
articles.title_original: Gender-responsive and -transformative interventions in HIV, TB and malaria programmes: a review of evidence.

articles.headline: 23-year panel of P. falciparum isolates from Colombia's Pacific Coast assessed for resistance to chloroquine, mefloquine, pyrimethamine, sulfadoxine, and artemisinin (PfKelch13 sequencing). Multiple chloroquine and mefloquine resistance genotypes and phenotypes persist; no artemisinin resistance-associated polymorphisms detected. Continued resistance to historically used drugs argues against their use as artemisinin partner therapy in this region
articles.title_original: Antimalarial drug resistance in Plasmodium falciparum isolates from the Pacific Coast of Colombia.

articles.headline: Trial in Uganda comparing monthly SP, DP, and DP+SP for IPTp in pregnant women (1,377 samples sequenced). Cumulative risk recurrent parasitemia 57.8% (SP) vs 4.1% (DP) vs 3.9% (DP+SP); symptomatic malaria 9.3% (SP) vs 1.1% (DP) vs 0.3% (DP+SP); recrudescence 40.1% (SP) vs 2.0% (DP) vs 0.8% (DP+SP). SP arm selected for increased PfDHFR/PfDHPS resistance mutations. IPTp-SP had poor efficacy and promoted drug resistance
articles.title_original: Recurrent Plasmodium falciparum parasitemia and drug resistance mutations during intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy in Uganda.

articles.headline: Optimized P. falciparum in vitro PRR assay using lacZ/β-galSENSOR chemiluminescence readout reduced incubation from 14-28 days to 7 days while maintaining informative pharmacodynamic parameters (lag phase, clearance time, PRR, maximum killing effect); [3H]-hypoxanthine incorporation failed to reliably detect viable parasites and overestimated drug activity. This novel assay enables antimalarial evaluation without radioactive facilities, providing robust time-killing profiles with greater throughput and convenience
articles.title_original: Using Next Generation Chemiluminescent Probes to Improve the Plasmodium falciparum in vitro Parasite Reduction Ratio (PRR) Assay.

articles.headline: Review of nanotechnology-based herbal remedies for malaria treatment. Nanoparticle delivery systems offer advantages over conventional treatments including high drug-loading capacity, targeted delivery, improved biocompatibility, and reduced toxicity; green nanotechnology-based therapies demonstrate potential therapeutic benefits, enhanced safety, and cost-effectiveness compared to traditional approaches. Article suggests nanotechnology-based therapeutics as promising candidates with significant commercialization potential, though no specific quantitative efficacy data reported
articles.title_original: Nano-Enabled Herbal Remedies for Malaria Treatment: A Review of Recent Advances.

articles.headline: Ex vivo susceptibility testing of 26 P. falciparum isolates from three Mali endemic sites to 14 antimalarials revealed: frontline drugs remained highly potent (dihydroartemisinin IC50<6 nM, lumefantrine IC50<50 nM, pyronaridine IC50<10 nM); chloroquine showed marked variability (IC50 ~50-1300 nM); chemoprevention agents sulfadoxine and pyrimethamine displayed high IC50 (>1000 and >2000 nM); next-generation compounds cabamiquine and GNF179 demonstrated strong activity (IC50<10 nM). Authors conclude frontline therapies remain effective with next-generation candidates supporting further development
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum field isolates drug susceptibility in Mali.

articles.headline: Narrative review of Iran's malaria burden 2000-2025 documents dramatic reduction in indigenous cases from thousands in early 2000s to <300 annually by mid-2010s, with multiple consecutive years achieving zero indigenous transmission through integrated vector management and cross-border initiatives. COVID-19 interruptions and operational disruptions triggered 2022 resurgence, primarily imported cases, complicated by insecticide resistance and Anopheles stephensi spread. Authors conclude elimination remains achievable with adaptive multisectoral approaches and sustained commitment
articles.title_original: Iran's Journey Through Malaria: From Past Challenges to Future Elimination-A Narrative Review.

articles.headline: Machine learning screening of kinase inhibitor library identified three compounds with dual activity against P. falciparum β-hematin formation and PfPKG: derazantinib (β-hematin IC50 88 µM, PfPKG IC50 0.160 µM), pazopanib (IC50 219 µM and 0.330 µM respectively), and afatinib (IC50 234 µM and 2.61 µM respectively). Derazantinib showed most potent PfPKG activity, though all three compounds were less potent for β-hematin inhibition compared to chloroquine (IC50 22 µM)
articles.title_original: Small Molecule Kinase Inhibitors with In Vitro β-Hematin Formation and Plasmodium falciparum Protein Kinase G Inhibitory Activity Identified Using Machine Learning.

articles.headline: Active monitoring of 383 free-living Spanish scavenger birds identified haemosporidian parasites in 3.4% (13/383), comprising Leucocytozoon 2.3% and Plasmodium 1.6%; among infected birds, Leucocytozoon spp. (69.2%) were more common than Plasmodium spp. (46.1%). Black kites showed highest prevalence (8.3%), followed by cinereous vultures (5.3%), red kites (4.8%), and Egyptian vultures (4.6%). Study identified 10 novel host-parasite interactions and a new Leucocytozoon lineage; authors suggest global warming may facilitate spread of haemosporidian-infected vectors
articles.title_original: Unraveling Occurrence Patterns and Diversity of Avian Malaria Parasites in Iberian Obligate and Facultative Scavenger Birds.

articles.headline: SARIMAX time series model using 90 monthly malaria data points (Jan 2018–June 2025) and weather exogenous regressors forecasts increasing malaria incidence in Bahir Dar, Ethiopia for 2026-2030. The SARIMAX (1,2,2)(1,2,2,12,exog) model achieved reasonable predictive accuracy (MAPE 28.3%), but no weather variables showed statistically significant predictive relationship with incidence (p>0.05). Authors conclude the upward forecast trend suggests potential breakdown in current control strategies, necessitating targeted localized early warning systems
articles.title_original: Forecasting malaria incidence in a resource-limited urban setting with climate variables as exogenous regressors: time series analysis using a SARIMAX model in Bahir Dar, Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Hospital-based cross-sectional evaluation of miLab™ AI-assisted platform in 300 suspected malaria patients (median age 24 years) in Ghana using nPCR as reference standard. miLab™ demonstrated sensitivity 94.23%, specificity 98.98%, and accuracy 97.33%, compared to adjudicated microscopy sensitivity 85.58%, specificity 97.96%, and accuracy 93.67%. Parasite densities by miLab™ ranged 0.95–5.34 log parasites/µL (median 3.52). Authors conclude miLab™ offers superior performance to microscopy and could serve as a reliable rapid diagnostic alternative in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Evaluating the diagnostic performance of miLab™ for detection of malaria parasites using nPCR as reference standard.

articles.headline: Qualitative study using 30 focus group discussions (9 analyzed in-depth) in Kapolowe health zone, DRC on healthcare-seeking for children with severe malaria. Findings indicate mothers frequently delay seeking care and resort to self-medication with modern and traditional remedies. Barriers included geographical distance, impassable roads, lack of transportation and facilities, financial difficulties, and cultural preferences for traditional practices and prayers. Authors conclude healthcare-seeking is shaped by structural, financial, and cultural barriers requiring health system strengthening
articles.title_original: Young children with severe malaria in the Kapolowe health zone: an analysis of their parents' health-seeking behaviors and the challenges they face.

articles.headline: Secondary analysis of Nigerian Malaria Indicator Survey (NMIS) in 9,131 children 6–59 months evaluated SD Bioline Malaria Ag P.f. RDT against microscopy, demonstrating sensitivity 88.6%, specificity 75.2%, PPV 49.5%, NPV 96.0%, and accuracy 78.1% with moderate agreement (κ=0.496). High negative predictive value enables reliable malaria exclusion; however, low positive predictive value and large microscopy-RDT prevalence gap indicate overestimation risk. Authors note false negatives may result from PfHRP2/3 deletions, test handling, or non-falciparum infections, warranting confirmatory testing
articles.title_original: Field performance of the SD Bioline Malaria Ag P.f. rapid diagnostic test among children under five in Nigeria: insights from the 2021 Malaria Indicator Survey.
articles.authors: Olubiyi B, Alabi A, Isiko I

articles.headline: Prospective observational study of 260 admitted malaria patients in Jacobabad, Pakistan (2024–2025) found 217 uncomplicated cases (83.5%) and 43 severe cases (16.5%). Severe malaria was significantly associated with jaundice, bleeding, pallor, respiratory distress, seizures, cerebral malaria, anemia, and thrombocytopenia. Case fatality rate was 16.2% in pediatric age group (0.1–15 years), with cerebral malaria as the most fatal complication. Authors conclude clinical and haematological parameters serve as prognostic indicators for disease progression warranting close monitoring
articles.title_original: Burden of Plasmodium vivax and Plasmodium falciparum Malaria: clinical insights from a prospective study in Jacobabad, Sindh, Pakistan.
articles.authors: Noor M, Junaid M, Furqan A

articles.headline: Pharmacogenetic analysis of 99 P. vivax patients treated with chloroquine (25 mg/kg) and primaquine (15 mg/kg/day×14 days) examined CYP polymorphisms. All patients achieved complete parasite clearance and fever resolution without recurrence. Most frequent alleles were CYP3A5*3 (71.7%) and CYP2D6*10 (30.6%); CYP3A5*3 associated with altered desethylchloroquine levels and CYP2C19*3 with faster parasite clearance, while CYP2D6 and CYP2C8 variants showed no significant associations. Authors conclude CQ-PQ remains effective despite high prevalence of reduced-function variants, suggesting alternative metabolic pathways compensate
articles.title_original: Pharmacogenetic Profiling of Cytochrome P450 Enzymes in Plasmodium vivax Patients Treated with Chloroquine and Primaquine: Implications for Personalized Malaria Therapy.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 10 studies on paratransgenesis, an approach using engineered symbiotic bacteria (Asaia, Serratia, Pantoea, Enterobacter, Aspergillus oryzae) to produce antiplasmodial effector proteins (Scorpine, EPIP, Defensin, SM1-2). Scorpine achieved up to 97.8% inhibition of P. falciparum oocysts; multi-effector approaches surpassed 89% inhibition. Authors conclude paratransgenesis is potentially important for malaria control, though ecological safety, microbial stability, and field validation remain key obstacles
articles.title_original: Evaluating paratransgenesis using engineered symbiotic bacteria for Plasmodium inhibition in mosquito vectors: A systematic review.

articles.headline: Multiplex assay using Oxford Nanopore MinION sequencing on 150 insectary-reared mosquitoes (9 species), 150 blood-fed specimens (5 host sources), and 40 P. falciparum-infected samples. Platform accurately identified all nine mosquito species, five bloodmeal hosts up to 48 h post-feed, and detected P. falciparum with high concordance to Sanger sequencing. Authors demonstrate MinION offers a novel, cost-effective solution for combined mosquito species identification, bloodmeal analysis, and parasite detection
articles.title_original: A multiplex assay to detect mosquito species, bloodmeal host source and Plasmodium in malaria vectors using Nanopore amplicon sequencing.

articles.headline: Whole-genome sequencing of An. arabiensis across Ethiopian regions reveals geographic and temporal variability in insecticide resistance genes. Vgsc-L995F highly prevalent in northern Ethiopia; metabolic resistance (CNVs at Cyp6aa/p, Coeae2-7g) in western Ethiopia; lower frequencies in Central Rift Valley; seasonal shifts in both target-site and metabolic markers during transmission season. Findings highlight complex interplay of control interventions, agricultural practices, human behavior, and vector behavior driving insecticide resistance
articles.title_original: Spatial and temporal signatures of genomic insecticide resistance in the Anopheles arabiensis mosquito malaria vector from Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Collection of 5,697 female anophelines along 100 km transect in southern Zambia identified 9 species dominated by An. rufipes (26.8%, n=1,527) and An. pretoriensis (25.1%, n=1,427). Bloodmeal analysis of 401 specimens showed feeding primarily on goats (62.5%), humans (19.4%), cows (10.2%); human blood index 0.22. One An. rufipes tested positive for P. falciparum. Authors conclude secondary vectors feed on humans and residual transmission may extend beyond primary vectors
articles.title_original: Species composition and blood feeding patterns of understudied Anopheles mosquitoes in southern Zambia, an area of low malaria transmission.

articles.headline: Year-long cohort study in rural Malawi using molecular detection found gametocyte-containing P. falciparum infections in 23% of population, with school-age children having significantly higher incidence and high-density gametocyte infections. Authors estimate clearing infections from asymptomatic school-age children in rainy season would decrease population gametocyte abundance by 67%, making them the primary driver of P. falciparum transmission
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic school-age children carry the majority of transmissible Plasmodium falciparum infections.

articles.headline: Photonic crystal fiber optical sensor with machine learning for detecting Plasmodium developmental stages via refractive index variations in red blood cells demonstrated sensitivities of 97.45% (healthy), 96.89% (ring), 96.22% (trophozoite), and 95.45% (schizont) stages, with confinement losses of ~10⁻⁸ dB per meter at 2.2 THz. Authors highlight potential as cost-effective platform for early malaria detection
articles.title_original: Machine learning assisted malaria detection using photonic crystal fibre optical sensors.
articles.authors: Abdullah-Al-Shafi M, Sen S, Mubassera M

articles.headline: Data-driven statistical inference model in Ouagadougou, Burkina Faso comparing seasonal, regular, and permanent migration patterns found seasonal migration most accurately explains low-transmission endemicity in dry months and their amplification into rainy-season outbreaks. High seasonal migration maintained persistent low transmission, preventing local fade-out. Authors conclude incorporating human mobility into malaria transmission models can strengthen elimination programs across Sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Rural-Urban Migration and Low-Transmission Endemicity of Malaria in Sub-Saharan Africa: A Statistical Inference Approach to Uncover Possible Mechanisms.

articles.headline: Zein-based nanocarrier sustained-release formulation of artemisinin achieved 200-fold enhancement in water solubility and displayed potent inhibitory effects against ART-resistant P. falciparum in ring survival and recrudescence assays. In vivo studies showed the nanoformulation significantly prolongs ART half-life, maintains effective blood concentrations, and exhibits superior efficacy in reducing parasitemia and preventing recrudescence compared to free artemisinin
articles.title_original: Development and evaluation of novel zein-based artemisinin sustained-release formulation for treating drug-resistant malaria.

articles.headline: Murine study showed acute Plasmodium infection at rVSV/EBOV vaccination reduced vaccine-mediated protection against mouse-adapted EBOV via Plasmodium-induced interferon gamma decreasing vaccine replication in lymph node macrophages, resulting in reduced anti-EBOV glycoprotein antibody responses. Higher doses of rVSV/EBOV partially overcame antibody deficits and elicited protective responses, supporting use of high antigen loads in EVD outbreak management in malaria-endemic regions
articles.title_original: Acute malaria dysregulates specialized lymph node macrophages to suppress vaccine-elicited protection against the Ebola virus.

articles.headline: Retrospective analysis of 379 Kenyan children evaluated Angiopoietin-like 4 and Inhibin-βE as biomarkers for severe P. falciparum malaria. Biomarkers showed high specificity for severe malaria (AUC 0.82) versus non-malarial coma, with further improvement when combined with platelet levels (AUC 0.96); AUC 0.85 for predicting death by cerebral malaria. Authors conclude these biomarkers could be developed as diagnostic tools to differentiate cerebral malaria from other causes of coma
articles.title_original: Specific Biomarkers Differentiate Cerebral Malaria from Other Causes of Coma in African children.

articles.headline: In silico and in vivo study of ivermectin structures on An. dirus and An. minimus demonstrated ivermectin with both sugar rings lethal to mosquitoes, monosaccharide with one sugar ring showed partial effect, aglycone with no sugar rings showed no effect. Docking models identified new hydrogen bond between second sugar ring hydroxyl (4″-OH) and THR304 of Anopheles GluCl M2-M3 loop. Findings improve understanding of potential resistance mechanisms
articles.title_original: Lethal effects of ivermectin structures on malaria vectors and in silico analysis of interactions with their glutamate-gated chloride ion channels.

articles.headline: Experimental infections of canaries (Serinus canaria) with Plasmodium relictum showed infectiousness to Culex quinquefasciatus mosquitoes increased with parasitemia, temperature, and time since feeding. Analysis of 17 bird species in 11 Hawaiian communities revealed gradual parasitemia-infectiousness relationship and high within-species variability resulted in broad overlap in species infectiousness. Authors conclude pathogen load-infectiousness relationship shape and within-species variability determine host range and spatial spread
articles.title_original: Variation in pathogen load and the pathogen load-infectiousness relationship broaden avian malaria's distribution.

articles.headline: Analysis of 276,318 RDTs from 27 Mandoto health centers (2019-2024) identified 109,428 malaria cases (39.6% positivity); post-2021 case distribution was 71.5% P. falciparum and 28.5% P. vivax, with seasonal peaks in January and April-June. Precipitation and temperature effectively predicted malaria seasonality with stronger P. falciparum associations, enabling time-series models for case forecasting and resource allocation
articles.title_original: Association of Climate Variables with Plasmodium vivax and Plasmodium falciparum Malaria Cases in Mandoto, Madagascar: A Statistical Modeling Study.

articles.headline: Prospective cohort of 33 Burkinabè women followed from delivery to 3 months postpartum found 48.5% malaria-positive at delivery; malaria-infected women showed elevated IL-10 levels (p=0.005), decreased IL-6:IL-10 ratio, and strong IL-10/malaria correlation (σ=0.9, p<0.001). IL-10-dominant cytokine profile at delivery was strongly associated with postpartum malaria susceptibility, suggesting cytokine modulation as a novel therapeutic target
articles.title_original: Postpartum cytokine shifts and IL-10-mediated immune suppression in malaria-infected primigravid women.

articles.headline: Laboratory studies of vivax malaria identified heme and hemozoin-induced platelet apoptosis via IRE1α-dependent unfolded protein response and ferroptosis via DGAT1/2-dependent lipid peroxidation, with hemozoin-triggered necrosis predominating. These regulated cell death pathways were associated with thrombocytopenia in malaria and may have implications for other hemolytic disorders
articles.title_original: Heme and hemozoin induce platelet cell death through UPR-induced apoptosis and ferroptosis in vivax malaria.

articles.headline: A 9-year-old non-immune traveler with severe P. falciparum malaria treated with IV artesunate developed post-artemisinin delayed hemolysis (PADH) at day 10 post-treatment, presenting with hemoglobin decline, indirect hyperbilirubinemia, elevated LDH, reticulocytosis, and low haptoglobin requiring transfusion. Case highlights PADH as a delayed complication of artesunate therapy, underscoring the critical need for post-discharge hemolysis monitoring 7-30 days after treatment
articles.title_original: Severe Plasmodium falciparum Malaria Complicated by Post-artemisinin Delayed Hemolysis in a Non-immune Pediatric Returning Traveler.

articles.headline: Narrative review of malaria in Greece demonstrates successful elimination through coordinated vector control, antimalarial treatment, environmental interventions, and sustained surveillance, but documents re-emergence of locally acquired cases in the early 21st century. Greece's experience shows malaria elimination is achievable through long-term public health efforts, though continuous surveillance and rapid response capacity remain essential to prevent re-establishment of transmission
articles.title_original: Malaria in Greece: From Ancient Scourge to Eradication.

articles.headline: Narrative review of malaria in Nigerian under-5s (literature since 2010) documents high burden with marked regional disparities, highest in northern states and rural areas, driven by socioeconomic deprivation, climate variability, and suboptimal intervention coverage. Challenges include diagnostic limitations and drug resistance; review emphasizes need for coordinated multisectoral response with innovative diagnostics, vaccines, and surveillance systems
articles.title_original: Burden, challenges, and control of malaria among under-5s in Nigeria.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 18 economic evaluations (2018-2025) in Sub-Saharan Africa found LLINs and IRS highly cost-effective with costs per DALY averted below GDP thresholds; combined LLIN+IRS, SMC, IPTp, and ACT+RDT were highly cost-effective, while tafenoquine with G6PD screening showed higher ICERs. Review recommends aligning policies with economic evidence for optimal resource allocation and accelerated malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Strategic resource allocation for malaria elimination in endemic settings: a systematic review of cost-effectiveness evidence.

articles.headline: Hospital-based screening of 64 P. falciparum-positive patients in Nigeria found 83% carried the B (normal) G6PD variant; no participants had the A- (G202A) deficiency variant, and neither B nor A+ variants showed significant effects on hemoglobin or parasitemia. Absence of G202A variant in this sample suggests broader community-representative G6PD genotyping and enzyme activity testing are needed before informing treatment policies
articles.title_original: No G6PD A- (G202A) variant detected among Plasmodium falciparum-positive patients in Awka, Southeast Nigeria: a hospital-based study.

articles.headline: hmmibd-rs, a multi-threaded enhancement of hmmIBD for P. falciparum identity-by-descent detection, achieved ~100× computational speedup (5.2 days reduced to 1.3 hours for 220 million chromosome pairs using 128 threads) and supports non-uniform recombination rates. Application to MalariaGEN Pf7 dataset (n≈10,000 samples) completed within hours, enabling efficient single-day large-scale malaria genomic surveillance
articles.title_original: hmmibd-rs: an enhanced hmmIBD implementation for parallelizable identity-by-descent detection from large-scale Plasmodium genomic data.

articles.headline: SEMA ReACT is an effectiveness-implementation hybrid Type 3 study protocol in Zambia and DRC evaluating community health worker delivery of rectal artesunate plus artemisinin-based combination therapy for severe malaria in children aged 6 months–5 years. Primary outcomes include time to treatment, 28-day PCR-corrected cure rates, and formal healthcare system utilization, aiming to provide evidence for scaling community-based pre-referral treatment to reduce mortality in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Severe malaria treatment with rectal artesunate and artemisinin-based combination therapy in remote settings: an effectiveness-implementation hybrid type 3 study protocol (SEMA ReACT).

articles.headline: Cross-sectional analysis of 10,013 DRC children aged 6-59 months from 2023/24 Demographic and Health Survey found malaria prevalence of 33% (95% CI 30.2–35.6), ranging from 5% in Kinshasa to 61% in Bas-Uele. Risk factors included age 24-59 months (APR=1.25), maternal limited education (APR≈1.13–1.15), poverty (APR≈1.42–1.52), no ITN use (APR=1.18), and unimproved housing (APR=1.27–1.54), emphasizing need for scaled ITN distribution and multisectoral socioeconomic interventions
articles.title_original: Prevalence and factors associated with malaria among children aged 6-59 months in the Democratic Republic of the Congo: a nationwide cross-sectional survey.

articles.headline: Prospective cohort of 354 P. vivax-infected patients in Pakistan comparing four regimens showed Day 28 recurrence rates of 10.2% (chloroquine), 20.2% (artemether-lumefantrine), 1.1% (chloroquine+primaquine), and 3.8% (artemether-lumefantrine+primaquine); 6-month recurrence increased to 15.3%, 32.2%, 1.1%, and 6.3%, respectively. Primaquine-containing regimens demonstrated significantly lower recurrence hazards, with artemether-lumefantrine monotherapy showing higher recurrence than chloroquine monotherapy
articles.title_original: Efficacy of artemether lumefantrine vs chloroquine for the treatment of Plasmodium Vivax infection in Pakistan.

articles.headline: Longitudinal study protocol across 12 districts in 10 Indian states (25,000 individuals) to assess malaria burden, vector bionomics, insecticide resistance, and health system performance across diverse ecological contexts, using RDTs, microscopy, PCR for low-density parasitemia, 1-year follow-up of symptomatic/asymptomatic individuals, entomological surveillance, and mixed-methods assessment of vulnerable populations to generate evidence for achieving India's 2030 malaria elimination target
articles.title_original: Multicentric longitudinal study on malaria burden, vector bionomics and health system assessment in diverse eco-epidemiological settings in the context of malaria elimination in India: study protocol.

articles.headline: Plasmodium falciparum STOML (stomatin-like protein) deletion studies show significant asexual blood-stage growth defect and slower development; STOML localizes to branching points of mitochondrial networks and forms a complex with zinc-dependent metalloprotease FtsH, suggesting a STOML-FtsH complex facilitates P. falciparum blood-stage development without involvement in respiratory chain assembly
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum stomatin-like protein forms a putative complex with a metalloprotease in distinct mitochondrial loci.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 947 participants in Gabon (2015–2016) found no association between malaria and loiasis: crude OR 0.67 (95% CI 0.45–1.01, p=0.052) reversed to adjusted OR 1.31 (95% CI 0.81–2.11, p=0.276) after controlling for age, reflecting different epidemiological distributions by age rather than biological interaction
articles.title_original: There Is No Association Between Loiasis and Malaria: Findings from a Secondary Analysis of a Cross-Sectional Survey in Rural Gabon.

articles.headline: Retrospective analysis of 16 imported malaria cases confirmed in 106 screened patients in Algeria (2018–2024): 87.5% P. falciparum, 78.6% uncomplicated malaria, 21.4% severe; main findings anemia (92.8%), thrombocytopenia (42.8%), hepatic cytolysis (43%); mefloquine most prescribed (71.4%), highlighting need for strengthened pre-travel chemoprophylaxis to prevent reintroduction in malaria-free regions
articles.title_original: Imported Malaria in a Malaria-Free Region: A Retrospective Analysis of Epidemiological, Clinical, and Therapeutic Patterns in Tizi-Ouzou, Algeria (2018-2024).

articles.headline: Review of malaria epidemiology in Haryana State, India (2018–2024) shows 98.34% case reduction from 3,149 to 52 cases by 2022, followed by resurgence to 270 cases in 2024, suggesting need for urgent investigation of climate-related factors, vector control gaps, and surveillance strengthening to sustain India's 2030 elimination target
articles.title_original: Malaria Surge in Haryana - an early warning to relook ongoing elimination efforts.

articles.headline: Analysis of 7,257 fever clinic cases in South-West Delhi (2020–2024) identified 58 (0.8%) malaria positives: 94.8% P. vivax, 70.7% male, 60.3% aged 18–45 years; imported cases predominated at 51.7% overall (30/58) and increased from 52.2% to 66.7% by 2024, predominantly from Uttar Pradesh, emphasizing need for inter-state collaboration and cross-notification
articles.title_original: Imported Malaria in South-West Delhi: Five-year Epidemiological Trends (2020-2024) from a Fever Clinic and Implications for Urban Malaria Elimination.

articles.headline: Analysis of 2021 Nigeria Malaria Indicator Survey (13,727 households, 14,476 women, 12,742 children <5 years) shows 56% household ITN ownership; 50% of women and 41.2% of children slept under ITNs; utilization higher in rural areas (p=0.001), North West/East regions (p<0.001), and lower wealth quintiles (p<0.001), indicating persistent gaps despite distribution efforts
articles.title_original: Insecticide-Treated Net Utilization Among Children and Pregnant Women: 2021 Nigeria Malaria Indicator Survey Analysis.

articles.headline: Ecological time-series analysis of 2,066,711 malaria cases in Yumbe district, Uganda (2017–2021) shows rainfall positively associated with incidence at 1-month lag (β=0.38, p<0.05) with strong leading correlation (r=0.759, p<0.001), while minimum temperature inversely associated (β=−0.29, p<0.05), highlighting climate's critical role in malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Climate variability and malaria incidence trends in Yumbe District, West Nile Sub-region of Uganda (2017-2021).

articles.headline: Cross-sectional assessment of malaria case-management in 18 Nigerian hospitals (2,814 admissions, 211 slides) found composite test-and-treat performance 39.3% (children 45.7% vs adults 26.5%); 73.7% tested on admission, 90.2% with severe malaria received artesunate; microscopy sensitivity 93.2%, specificity 42.5%, indicating need for diagnostic quality assurance and improved ACT follow-up
articles.title_original: Quality of inpatient test-and-treat malaria case-management in public and private hospitals in Kano State, Nigeria.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis of P. falciparum drug resistance markers from 261 studies across 64 countries (2005–2025) shows pooled prevalence: pfdhfr 85.7%, pfcrt 78.0%, pfdhps 73.7%, pfmdr1 60.5%, and pfk13 45.0%; rising pfk13 since 2012 indicates emerging artemisinin resistance, while persistent pfdhfr/pfdhps mutations reflect ongoing sulfonamide-pyrimethamine pressure
articles.title_original: The Global Landscape of Plasmodium falciparum Drug Resistance Markers, 2005-2025: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.

articles.headline: Correction notice to prior publication on transcriptome profiling of intrahepatocytic Plasmodium and host hepatocytes; no results presented
articles.title_original: Correction: Transcriptome profiling of intrahepatocytic Plasmodium and their host hepatocytes based on the infection phase and the zonation of the liver.
