articles.headline: Functional studies in Anopheles gambiae revealed PfApiAT2 proline transporter is essential for P. falciparum oocyst development and sporogony; halofuginone-resistant mutants and PfApiAT2-knockout parasites formed severely stunted oocysts with blocked sporozoite production, a phenotype rescued by mosquito nutrient supplementation, identifying a critical parasite nutritional dependency as a transmission vulnerability
articles.title_original: PfApiAT2 is a proline transporter essential for the transmission of Plasmodium falciparum by the mosquito vector.

articles.headline: In vivo study in P. berghei-infected Wistar mice treated with Z. chalybeum aqueous rootbark extracts showed dose-dependent parasite clearance up to 76%, with significant efficacy at 100 mg/kg (MD=15.6, p<0.001), 200 mg/kg (MD=16.42, p<0.001), and 400 mg/kg (MD=17.24, p<0.001); fever reduction was significant (MD=-1.50 to -1.78°C, p≤0.009 across doses) with no remarkable hemoglobin concentration changes
articles.title_original: Antimalarial efficacy of aqueous crude rootbark extracts of Zanthoxylum Chalybeum: An in vivo experimental study in Wistar Albino Mice infected with Plasmodium berghei.

articles.headline: PbHiT is a scalable CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing platform for P. berghei utilizing single-step cloning with 100 bp homology arms and gRNA barcoding for pooled transfections; reliably generates knockout and tagged parasite lines with high efficiency, supporting both targeted functional studies and high-throughput genetic screens in rodent malaria models
articles.title_original: Plasmodium berghei High-Throughput (PbHiT): a CRISPR-Cas9 System to Study Genes at Scale.

articles.headline: Case report of 9-year-old boy with mixed P. vivax (5500 parasites/µL) and P. falciparum (562 parasites/µL) infection diagnosed by thick smear, blood film microscopy, and rapid diagnostics; artemether-lumefantrine followed by primaquine achieved complete parasite clearance and favorable outcome, illustrating diagnostic challenges of mixed infections with disparate parasitemias and underdetection risks
articles.title_original: Pediatric Mixed Plasmodium vivax-P. falciparum Infection with Disparate Parasitemias: Diagnostic and Surveillance Challenges.

articles.headline: Case report of afebrile P. falciparum malaria in 45-year-old returning traveler presenting as severe hemolytic anemia (hemoglobin 48 g/L) with thrombocytopenia and neurological complications; parasitemia of 0.9% (~1746 parasites/µL) confirmed by blood smear, HRP-2 antigen, and PCR; intramuscular artesunate followed by oral dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine achieved full recovery, emphasizing diagnostic vigilance for afebrile presentations
articles.title_original: Afebrile falciparum malaria with severe hemolytic anemia in a returning traveler: a case report.

articles.headline: Population genetic study of 2918 Anopheles gambiae and 173 A. arabiensis from 6 Ugandan sites (3 Lake Victoria islands, 3 mainland) using targeted amplicon sequencing of 62 loci revealed clear species-level differentiation but subtle island-mainland structure within A. gambiae (FST 0.00054-0.028); island populations showed reduced diversity and smaller effective population sizes (146-249) compared to mainland (4054-8190), providing critical genetic structure data for vector control intervention planning
articles.title_original: Assessing the population genetic structure and demographic history of Anopheles gambiae and Anopheles arabiensis at island and mainland sites in Uganda: implications for testing novel malaria vector control approaches.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 207 Ugandan adults with severe malaria found 14.5% bacteremia prevalence; Salmonella typhi (33.3%), Staph aureus (30%), and Streptococcus spp. (16.7%) were most common isolates. CNS symptoms, low oxygen saturation, hyperparasitaemia, and leucocytosis were significantly associated with bacteremia (p<0.05). Authors recommend ciprofloxacin plus penicillin for empiric coverage when co-infection suspected
articles.title_original: Prevalence and factors associated with concomitant bacteremia among adults admitted with severe malaria at Kayunga Regional Referral Hospital, Uganda.

articles.headline: Molecular surveillance of 302 patients in Mbouda, Cameroon identified 28.8% P. falciparum prevalence. Pfmdr1 gene detected in 95% of isolates; 94% wild-type (N86), 1% mixed (N86/Y86), 0% mutant (Y86). Authors conclude that other Pfmdr1 mutations warrant evaluation and treatment policy changes may be needed to prevent resistance evolution
articles.title_original: Molecular surveillance of the Plasmodium falciparum Pfmdr1 N86Y mutation linked to antimalarial drug resistance in Mbouda, Cameroon.

articles.headline: Whole-genome sequencing of 125 Sudanese individuals revealed strong adaptive admixture signal in Sudanese Copts at ACKR1 locus (SNP rs2814778, selection coefficient s=0.0996) conferring Duffy-null resistance to P. vivax; this adaptation was introduced 1,000–1,500 years ago through genetic admixture, providing evidence of admixture-driven malaria resistance
articles.title_original: Sudan's complex genetic admixture history drives adaptation to malaria in Sudanese Copts.
articles.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A

articles.headline: Mark-release-recapture study of 19,889 marked Anopheles farauti in tropical Queensland (2015–2017) achieved 1.55% recapture rate with daily survival of 0.68 (wet season) and 0.75 (dry season). Maximum flight distance was 3.51 km with estimated daily travel of ~73 m. Authors conclude these dispersal parameters are longer than previously documented and important for modelling malaria transmission and gene drive spread
articles.title_original: Estimating the dispersal of the malaria vector Anopheles farauti through a natural ecosystem in north Queensland, Australia using mark release and recapture experiments.

articles.headline: Analysis of 100 P. falciparum isolates from Cameroon (2022–2023) using targeted sequencing identified csp in 35% of samples with 22 haplotypes (He=0.908); vaccine-matched RTS,S strain present in 20% of genotyped infections. Complexity of infection highest for ama (mean 2.8), followed by sera2 (2.1), csp (1.3), trap (1.2). Authors highlight that high antigenic diversity may influence vaccine efficacy and continued surveillance is essential
articles.title_original: Complexity of Infection and Plasmodium falciparum Circumsporozoite Protein Diversity Prior to Malaria Vaccine Implementation in Kaelé Health District, Cameroon, 2022-2023.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 15 studies (3,522 patients) found 24.0% (95% CI 16.0%–34.0%) overall prevalence of pre-treatment antimalarial drug levels; Africa 31.0% vs Asia 11.0%. Chloroquine and lumefantrine showed highest pre-treatment prevalence (55.0% and 34.0% respectively). Authors conclude substantial pre-treatment drug levels indicate drug pressure driving antimalarial resistance emergence and spread
articles.title_original: Residual antimalarial drug concentrations before treatment in malaria patients in Asia and Africa: A systematic review and meta-analysis depicting its implication for drug pressure.

articles.headline: Case report of 31-year-old male with severe P. falciparum malaria presenting with acute kidney injury, impaired consciousness, jaundice, liver dysfunction, high parasitemia, and severe thrombocytopenia treated with artesunate injection and supportive care, achieving full recovery. Authors emphasize importance of early diagnosis and prompt treatment in managing severe malaria complications
articles.title_original: Severe falciparum malaria remains a threat in East Kalimantan: A case report.

articles.headline: Cell culture study transfecting MCF7 and HepG2 cells with hsa-miR-21-5p and hsa-miR-3158-3p mimics found hsa-miR-3158-3p significantly downregulated NF-κB expression (p<0.05) and hsa-miR-21-5p reduced NF-κB, IL-10, and TLR4 expression (p<0.05). Authors suggest these miRNAs may regulate immune pathways implicated in cerebral malaria pathogenesis
articles.title_original: Functional evidence of hsa-miR-21-5p and hsa-miR-3158-3p in regulating immune pathways relevant to cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Correction notice regarding qualitative evaluation of community intermittent preventive treatment for malaria in pregnancy in Senegal
articles.title_original: Correction: Initiating community intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy in Senegal: qualitative evaluation of stakeholder acceptability and experience.

articles.headline: Targeted sequencing of 93 P. falciparum isolates from Senegal identified 15 SNPs in PfCyRPA vaccine antigen, with 10 novel variants; dominant wild-type 3D7 allele prevalence was highest and most frequent SNP (V292F) at 9.7% population prevalence. Structural modelling predicted majority SNPs have minor effects on antibody binding. Authors conclude PfCyRPA epitopes are conserved and inform next-generation vaccine design
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity in the Plasmodium falciparum next-generation blood stage vaccine candidate antigen PfCyRPA in Senegal.

articles.headline: Comparative study of 306 sparrows (8 sites, 2019–2022) found more invasive house sparrows (Passer domesticus) had higher Plasmodium prevalence (24.1%, 95% CI 17.6%–32.0%) than less invasive Eurasian tree sparrows (11.6%, 95% CI 7.6%–17.2%), with nearly 2-fold higher odds of greater parasitemia in house sparrows. Authors challenge expectation of increased susceptibility in less invasive host
articles.title_original: Avian malaria and invasion success: Contrasting infection patterns in two introduced Eurasian sparrows in North America (Passer domesticus and Passer montanus).
articles.authors: Trigueros AC, Robertson BQ, Parker PG

articles.headline: Mechanistic study combining 5-aminolevulinic acid (ALA) with dihydroartemisinin (DHA) achieved synergistic P. falciparum elimination, including complete clearance of artemisinin-resistant isolates from the Greater Mekong Subregion with no recrudescence over 3 weeks of culture. Authors conclude this host-directed bait-and-kill strategy selectively exploits infected RBC heme metabolism to restore artemisinin efficacy while sparing uninfected cells
articles.title_original: Targeting Infected Host Cell Heme Metabolism to Kill Malaria Parasites.

articles.headline: A valine-to-leucine substitution (V2163L) in the P. falciparum transcription factor AP2-G abrogates DNA binding to the GnGTAC consensus motif and prevents gametocyte sexual commitment; the mutation reveals GDV1's essential role in ap2-g locus activation and sexual differentiation
articles.title_original: A single valine to leucine switch disrupts Plasmodium falciparum AP2-G DNA binding and reveals GDV1's role in ap2-g activation.

articles.headline: In Rivers State, Nigeria (2007-2021), a SARIMAX(1,1,0)(1,1,1)₁₂ forecasting model outperformed standard count models, identifying seasonality, rainfall, and government malaria-control policy changes as significant predictors of monthly malaria incidence for improved surveillance and response
articles.title_original: Climatic and governance determinants of malaria transmission in Rivers State, Nigeria.

articles.headline: In fatal P. falciparum cerebral malaria, S1PR1 expression in cerebral blood vessels and neurons was significantly elevated compared to controls and non-CM patients (p<0.001), while blood S1P levels decreased and were negatively correlated with S1PR1, implicating the S1P/S1PR1 pathway in cerebral malaria pathogenesis
articles.title_original: Expression of sphingosine-1-phosphate receptor 1 in the brain of fatal cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Molecular analysis of mitochondrial cytochrome oxidase sequences identified four Anopheles taxa in Guyana and Venezuela: An. albitarsis F, An. goeldii (first reported in the region), and An. oswaldoi A and B (oswaldoi A newly documented in Venezuela), with oswaldoi A and B sympatric in Bolívar State
articles.title_original: Anopheles species complexes from Guyana and Venezuela malaria endemic areas using COI sequences.

articles.headline: Over 1990-2023, Afghanistan exhibited non-linear cyclic malaria trends peaking in 2002, declining to 2013, then increasing 2014-2017; 2018-2023 revealed 96.5% P. vivax, 3.2% P. falciparum (mean 153,295 cases/year; 15.2% test positivity), with ~25% concentrated along the Pakistani border; forecasting predicts failure to achieve elimination by 2035
articles.title_original: Malaria time series analysis, forecasting, and development of curated intervention strategies for the elimination of malaria from Afghanistan.

articles.headline: In controlled human malaria infection studies, protected RTS,S vaccinees demonstrated broader antibody responses to P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein junctional, central repeat, and C-terminal regions, including higher intensity against C-terminal epitopes and elevated IgA responses to PNDPNRNV variants, compared to rCSP or unprotected recipients
articles.title_original: Vaccine-Induced Antibodies to a C-Terminal Plasmodium falciparum Circumsporozoite Protein Epitope Associated With Protection Against Malaria Challenge.

articles.headline: In a sporozoite malaria vaccine trial with controlled human malaria infection, elevated Interleukin-6 was detected on days participants experienced moderate and severe adverse events, suggesting IL-6 as a potential biomarker for systemic reactogenicity in vaccine development
articles.title_original: Cytokines Associated With Moderate and Severe Adverse Events During a Sporozoite Malaria Vaccine Trial With Controlled Human Malaria Infection.

articles.headline: Among women from Acre State in the Brazilian Amazon, CCR5Δ32 allele frequency was 2.88% in Plasmodium-infected individuals versus 1.04% in uninfected controls, notably lower than in other Brazilian regions and reflecting unique ancestry patterns in Amazonian populations
articles.title_original: Updating the frequency of CCR5Δ32 in Brazil: Descriptive analysis of malaria cases and controls from Acre State, Amazon region.

articles.headline: Among 895 P. falciparum isolates from Nigeria (2017-2021), ~30% were polyclonal; 55.1% carried wild-type chloroquine transporter (Pfcrt) alleles while CVIET chloroquine-resistant haplotype persisted in 40.9%; sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance alleles appeared in >50% and nonsynonymous Pfkelch13 mutations in a minority, indicating chloroquine sensitivity now dominates while antifolate resistance persists
articles.title_original: Molecular Surveillance of Malaria in Nigeria Reveals a Low Frequency of Antimalarial Resistance Markers and Clonal Expansion of Chloroquine-Sensitive Infections.

articles.headline: A machine learning-derived dynamic sea surface temperature (SST) index outperformed the traditional El Niño-Southern Oscillation index for long-lead malaria prediction in the Peruvian Amazon, with lead times exceeding 3 months and superior correlation coefficients, reflecting SST-mediated effects on local temperature and humidity
articles.title_original: A Machine Learning-Based Dynamic SST Index for Long-Lead Malaria Prediction in the Peruvian Amazon.

articles.headline: The National Library of Medicine screener app showed poor diagnostic performance for P. falciparum in blood donors in Ghana: sensitivity 38.9% (7/18; 95% CI 20.3-61.4), specificity 60.6% (171/282; 95% CI 54.8-66.2), PPV 5.9%, and κ=-0.001, indicating substantial improvement is needed before clinical application
articles.title_original: Diagnostic Accuracy of the National Library of Medicine (NLM) Screener Application in Screening for Malaria Parasites Among Blood Donors at the Korle-Bu Blood Bank, Ghana: A Cross-Sectional Study.

articles.headline: In P. berghei, Apicortin localizes to a ringlike structure at the apical complex of invasive parasite stages; however, Apicortin gene deletion did not affect parasite development, indicating this microtubule-associated protein is dispensable, likely due to functional redundancy or compensatory mechanisms
articles.title_original: Apicortin defines the Plasmodium apical conoid body but is dispensable for the parasite life cycle.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 11 studies (2010–2024) found general public knowledge of asymptomatic malaria ranged 14.2–79.8% across four studies, while health personnel knowledge was 88% (one study). Qualitative analyses revealed varied and lacking knowledge, with refusal to adhere to interventions. The authors conclude education on asymptomatic malaria is necessary for disease elimination
articles.title_original: To what extent do people in malaria-endemic countries know asymptomatic malaria infections? A systematic review.

articles.headline: RTS,S and R21 vaccines against P. falciparum showed safety and efficacy in clinical trials, but RTS,S efficacy was lower in higher-transmission settings, with temporary periods of negative efficacy beginning ~3 years after the primary series. The authors recommend enhanced surveillance and supplementary interventions during implementation in high-transmission settings to address expected delayed malaria cases
articles.title_original: Ensuring the Public Health Impact of Malaria Vaccination in High-Transmission Settings.

articles.headline: Gene knockout studies of TEX1 (E3 ubiquitin ligase) in P. berghei showed significant reductions in ookinete formation, oocyst numbers, and sporozoites in mosquito stages, and impaired growth, abnormal nuclear division, and dramatically reduced blood-stage infectivity in hepatic stages. TEX1 is identified as a promising target for liver-stage antimalarial development
articles.title_original: The Putative E3 Ubiquitin Ligase TEX1 Is Required for Nuclear Biology and Developmental Progression of Plasmodium berghei in the Liver.

articles.headline: Analysis of 2,189 children (6–59 months) from the 2022 Liberia Malaria Indicator Survey found malaria prevalence of 8.3% in coastal versus 12.7% in inland regions (Z=3.33, p=0.001). Risk factors included not using ITNs (OR=1.5), severe anemia (AOR=6.2; 95% CI 2.34–14.81), poor households (AOR=6.4; 95% CI 3.06–13.27), and rural residence versus urban (AOR=0.6; 95% CI 0.40–0.86). Targeted interventions should prioritize inland counties and high-risk groups
articles.title_original: Geographic variation in malaria prevalence among children under five in coastal and inland counties of Liberia: analysis of the 2022 Malaria Indicator Survey.

articles.headline: GIS-based malaria risk modeling applied to historical records from antiquity through the 12th century CE in Cilicia Pedias (Türkiye) showed model-produced risk data correlated extremely well with historical accounts of malaria-like illness. The study demonstrates the impact of Plasmodium parasites on historical outcomes and provides a replicable methodology for evaluating malaria in the premodern Mediterranean
articles.title_original: A 'useless marsh … breeding fever and insect pests': assessing malaria risk on roads through Cilicia Pedias (modern Türkiye) and its historical impact in antiquity and the Middle Ages.

articles.headline: Correction to article on metabolic rigidity as a mechanical barrier to malaria in PKLR-deficient erythrocytes
articles.title_original: Correction to "Metabolic Rigidity as a Mechanical Barrier to Malaria: Flickering Loss in PKLR-Deficient Erythrocytes".

articles.headline: 8-Fluorotryptanthrin (C1), an IDO-1 inhibitor, showed strong in vitro activity against P. falciparum (3D7, Dd2) and P. berghei ANKA. In P. berghei-infected mice, C1 (20 mg/kg) reduced parasitemia and enhanced survival; combined with artemether-lumefantrine, C1 achieved complete parasitemia suppression and full survival by days 7–14, with elevated nitric oxide and reactive oxygen species and balanced cytokine profiles. The authors propose C1 as a promising lead for immunochemotherapy against drug-resistant malaria
articles.title_original: 8-Fluorotryptanthrin, an immunomodulatory IDO-1 inhibitor enhances the efficacy of artemether-lumefantrine in the treatment of malaria.

articles.headline: Phenotypic and systems biology assays in P. falciparum revealed that VAR2CSA-expressing parasites exclusively transcribe var2csa with all other var genes silenced, and transcriptional activation is associated with H3K9me3 depletion and var2csa repositioning relative to heterochromatic telomeric clusters. The study provides high-resolution insights into epigenetic and chromatin-structure mechanisms regulating antigenic variation
articles.title_original: Epigenetics and chromatin structure regulate var2csa expression and the placental binding phenotype in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Reanalysis of genomic data from 18 worldwide P. falciparum populations revealed that genetic variation is enriched for rare variants, producing genealogies more consistent with multiple-merger coalescent than Kingman coalescent. Rare variants cause πN/πS ratio statistics to be systematically inflated even without selection, but filtering this variation reveals antigens previously masked as candidates for selection, including merozoite surface proteins
articles.title_original: Rare variation in malaria parasites biases population-genetic inference.

articles.headline: UHPLC-HRMS/MS metabolomic analysis of field-derived permethrin-resistant versus susceptible A. gambiae from Cameroon found resistant mosquitoes exhibited upregulated inosine, nicotinic acid, dipeptides, amino acids, fumarate, uracil, and aldopentose, suggesting altered detoxification and enhanced energy production. The findings elucidate metabolic pathways underlying permethrin resistance and mechanisms of pyrethroid cross-resistance emergence
articles.title_original: Metabolomic changes underpinning permethrin resistance in the Anopheles gambiae malaria vector from Cameroon.

articles.headline: Studies in P. chabaudi-infected mice demonstrated that TNF signaling enhances glucose uptake by liver and spleen and controls parasitemia through expression of iNOS, stabilization of HIF-1α, and GLUT1 expression in monocytes. iNOS-/-, TNFRΔLyz2, and HIF-1αΔLyz2 mice showed impaired glycolysis, increased parasitemia, and disease tolerance, indicating that TNF-iNOS-HIF-1α-induced glycolysis in monocytes is critical for host defense
articles.title_original: Reprogramming of host energy metabolism mediated by the TNF-iNOS-HIF-1α axis plays a key role in host resistance to Plasmodium infection.

articles.headline: Descriptive analysis of 532 imported malaria cases in Sri Lanka (2013–2023) found P. falciparum 48.1% (mostly from Africa) and P. vivax 40.5% (mostly from India); 68.5% were work-related travelers. P. ovale took longer to manifest (median 95 days) than other species (median 14 days), and P. malariae took longer to diagnose (median 15.5 days) versus others (4–6 days). Forty-six severe cases occurred (45 P. falciparum), with one death; diagnostic delays were primarily physician-related rather than due to patient delay
articles.title_original: The profile of imported malaria in Sri Lanka from 2013 to 2023.

articles.headline: In a longitudinal study of 765 Anopheles mosquitoes from mainland and island settings in southwestern Senegal (Sept 2020–Nov 2021), A. gambiae s.l. comprised 91.1% of specimens. Mainland indoor-resting females had higher human blood indices than outdoor resting (76.2% vs 47.6%, P=0.035), while island populations showed no significant difference (25.1% vs 31.1%, P=0.51). Indoor resting densities did not differ significantly between locations (P=0.082). Findings provide baseline understanding of vector feeding and resting behaviors to guide evidence-based malaria control in regions where outdoor-resting populations complicate indoor interventions
articles.title_original: Feeding and resting behaviours of natural Anopheles gambiae s.l. populations in an area of low malaria transmission in south-western Senegal: A comparative study between mainland and island settings.

articles.headline: A scoping review of 2015–2024 literature on malaria in Madagascar documents disparities between urban and rural areas, with remote regions bearing higher burden and insufficient healthcare infrastructure limiting timely diagnosis and treatment. Key interventions include artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs), insecticide-treated nets (ITNs), indoor residual spraying (IRS), seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC), and rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs). Drug resistance, diagnostic failures, and socioeconomic inequalities undermine control efforts. Recommendations emphasize strengthening healthcare systems and supply chains, scaling up IRS and ITNs, and improving access in underserved populations
articles.title_original: Understanding inequities in the malaria landscape of Madagascar: a scoping review of current evidence.

articles.headline: Analysis of 8,487 hospitalized patients (Kenya, June 2017–July 2024) identified 2,197 malaria-positive cases (25.9%), of whom 713 (32.5%) had severe disease and 16 (0.7%) died. Fever duration ≥5 days strongly predicted severe illness (OR 3.67) and death (OR 8.00) versus ≤1 day; referral from another facility also increased odds (OR 3.01 and 3.15, respectively). Only 15% of severe cases received guideline-concordant IV artesunate plus artemether-lumefantrine; 21% of microscopy-positive patients were PCR-negative. Delayed care-seeking and treatment protocol gaps contribute to preventable mortality
articles.title_original: Using sentinel surveillance system data to characterize severe malaria illness and quality of malaria case management among hospitalized patients in Kenya, 2017-2024.

articles.headline: A new regional network, MMS Américas, was established at a meeting of international researchers and public health professionals in Panama (August 2025) to promote sharing of expertise, protocols, and data on malaria molecular surveillance strategies across the Americas to accelerate elimination efforts
articles.title_original: MMS Américas: a regional network for malaria molecular surveillance.

articles.headline: A mixed-methods study (May 2021–April 2024) of 189 participants in conflict-affected southwestern Cameroon evaluated three co-designed interventions: Community Health Participatory Approach (CoHPA), community health worker supervision, and health vouchers for treatment and transport. Qualitative findings from 19 focus group discussions and 18 interviews reported increased malaria prevention knowledge and household participation, greater trust in community health worker services with reduced use of informal drug sellers, and expanded treatment access through vouchers. Community leaders questioned voucher long-term sustainability, yet participants felt empowered in intervention design and evaluation
articles.title_original: The role of community co-designed interventions for malaria prevention and control in conflict-affected communities in Cameroon's Southwest and littoral regions.

articles.headline: Investigation of ATP2B4 genotypes and PMCA4b Ca²+-ATPase expression in Indian populations with severe and uncomplicated malaria found no significant genotype-disease association. Low PMCA4b expression correlated with increased intracellular calcium, oxidative stress, and reduced in-vitro P. falciparum growth, with oxidative-stress regulation emerging as the key protective mechanism rather than dehydration. Critically, low PMCA4b expression reduced P. falciparum artemisinin sensitivity, indicating host genetic variation influences drug efficacy. Host erythrocyte polymorphisms prevalent in malaria-endemic areas may serve as biomarkers for predicting artemisinin therapeutic response
articles.title_original: Decoding the multifaceted role of erythrocyte PMCA4b in oxidative stress-mediated malaria protection and artemisinin resistance.

articles.headline: Proximity-dependent biotinylation of P. falciparum gametocyte subpellicular microtubule-associated protein PfSPM3 identified 37 high-confidence interacting proteins; endogenous GFP tagging of 11 enriched candidates revealed distinct localization patterns within the microtubule network. Functional knockout of MAP PF3D7_1003400, localized near the inner membrane complex suture, disrupted gametocytogenesis and prevented falciform gametocyte development. This microtubule atlas reveals essential cytoskeletal components required for gametocyte morphogenesis, offering new targets for blocking parasite transmission
articles.title_original: An atlas of novel microtubule-associated proteins in the malaria parasite Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Molecular screening of 406 blood and tissue samples from 146 Humboldt penguins and 260 individuals from 10 avian orders across six Thai zoos detected haemosporidian DNA in 40 penguin samples (27.4%) and 125 other-avian samples (48.0%). Four confirmed Plasmodium species (P. collidatum, P. relictum, P. juxtanucleare, P. gallinaceum) and four lineages, plus Haemoproteus sacharovi, Haemoproteus lineages, and Leucocytozoon caulleryi were identified. Infected species included critically endangered taxa (Malayan peacock-pheasant, green peafowl, white-rumped vulture). Circulation among domestic and wild Thai birds suggests ongoing transmission, necessitating strengthened surveillance, vector control, and biosecurity in zoo conservation programs
articles.title_original: Malaria outbreaks in Humboldt penguins and haemosporidian infections in avian hosts in zoos across Thailand.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis

articles.headline: Immunoprecipitation-mass spectrometry and functional analysis in Malian children identified four P. falciparum antigens whose high antibody levels significantly reduced risk of high parasite density: DBLγ11 (OR 0.74, 95% CI 0.63–0.86), DBLζ5 (OR 0.80, 0.69–0.93), PF3D7_0201600 (OR 0.81, 0.70–0.94), and PF3D7_0532300 (OR 0.79, 0.68–0.92). Opsonic phagocytosis of DBLγ11 and DBLζ5-coated beads significantly correlated with antibody levels (r²=0.61 and 0.49, p<0.001). Results implicate antibody-mediated opsonic phagocytosis as a mechanism of naturally acquired malaria protection
articles.title_original: Antibody levels to variant and conserved Plasmodium falciparum antigens predict reduction in parasite burden in Malian children.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 10 studies (6,754 patients) evaluating AI-based malaria diagnosis found pooled sensitivity 87.7% (95% CI 78.2–93.4) and specificity 91.4% (95% CI 77.3–97.1) versus microscopy, with sensitivity 90.7% and specificity 88.3% versus PCR. AI-based systems provide diagnostic accuracy comparable to reference methods while enabling automation and reducing costs, positioning AI as a promising complementary tool for improving malaria detection and control in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Diagnostic Accuracy of Utilizing Artificial Intelligence for Malaria Diagnostic: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional comparison of blood and urine rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs) in febrile children showed malaria positivity rates of 67.4% (SD Bioline), 65.3% (CareStart), and 45.3% (Fyodor urine test). The urine-based test demonstrated sensitivity 58.6%, specificity 100%, PPV 100%, and NPV 40%, with only moderate agreement versus blood RDTs (Cohen's kappa 0.491, P<0.001) versus excellent agreement between blood tests (kappa 0.859). Low sensitivity limits the urine test's utility as a standalone diagnostic tool in febrile children
articles.title_original: Comparative Performance of Blood and Urine Malaria Rapid Diagnostic Tests in Detecting Malaria in Febrile Children: A Cross-Sectional Analysis.

articles.headline: Case-control study of 200 pregnant women (20–42 weeks gestation) stratified by malaria and preeclampsia status found plasma adipsin significantly elevated in both conditions (p<0.001), with highest concentrations in coexisting preeclampsia and P. falciparum infection. Adipsin correlated strongly with C5a (ρ=0.695), IL-6 (ρ=0.687), and TNF-α (ρ=0.645) (p<0.001). Malaria infection reduced adipsin's diagnostic specificity for preeclampsia (AUC 0.719, 62.1% specificity versus AUC 0.823, 87.9% without malaria). Malaria-adjusted reference thresholds are necessary when adipsin is used as a biomarker in endemic regions
articles.title_original: Impact of Malaria Infection on the Diagnostic Performance of Adipsin for Preeclampsia in Pregnancy: A Case-Control Study.

articles.headline: In a case-control study of malaria in Arba Minch, Ethiopia (Oct 2022–Dec 2023), 5,181 confirmed cases (22.7% positivity) were predominantly P. falciparum (71%), with highest risk from proximity to breeding sites (AOR=5.45), stagnant water (AOR=5.69), and poor housing (AOR=4.40). Consistent LLIN use was protective (AOR=0.30). Authors conclude the P. falciparum-driven epidemic was driven by environmental conditions, poor housing, and inadequate malaria knowledge
articles.title_original: Epidemiological investigation of a possible malaria outbreak in Arba Minch town, Gamo Zone, Southern Ethiopia, 2023.

articles.headline: Computational screening targeting multidrug-resistant P. falciparum kinases (CK2 and PKG) identified Ligand-13 as the top compound against CK2 (−11.468 kcal/mol) and Ligand-9 against PKG (−7.490 kcal/mol). Molecular dynamics and density functional theory confirmed Ligand-13's stability and reactivity. Authors propose Ligand-13 as a promising novel antimalarial candidate
articles.title_original: Computational screening of natural inhibitors against Plasmodium falciparum kinases: Toward novel antimalarial therapies.

articles.headline: Examines malaria outbreaks in endemic settings; specific study design and findings cannot be extracted from title alone
articles.title_original: Malaria outbreaks in endemic settings?

articles.headline: A review of glutathione (GSH) redox pathway as a therapeutic target against malaria parasites discusses key enzyme inhibitors (GST, GR, GS, GP). Authors highlight GSH pathways' difference from human hosts and potential in combating multidrug-resistant malaria, though selective targeting and drug resistance remain challenges
articles.title_original: Glutathione redox system as a therapeutic target for Plasmodium: Opportunities, Challenges, and future perspectives.

articles.headline: A qualitative study with 10 Tanzanian civil society organizations examined perspectives on gene drive-modified mosquitoes (GDMMs) for malaria control. Participants expressed cautious support but raised concerns about scientific uncertainty, limited expertise, and transboundary effects. Authors emphasize need for robust evidence before environmental release, stronger accountability, and sustained community engagement
articles.title_original: Exploratory conversations with biodiversity-oriented civil society groups on the potential applications of gene drive-modified mosquitoes for malaria control in Tanzania.

articles.headline: A cost-effectiveness analysis of four malaria diagnostic approaches in Lyon, France found XN-31 haematology analyser+microscopy most cost-effective compared to microscopy, RDT+microscopy, and LAMP+microscopy combinations, due to superior accuracy and short time-to-result. Authors note generalizability limited to French healthcare system costs and recommend similar studies in other settings
articles.title_original: A comparison of the cost-effectiveness of the current standard of care and of the haematology analyser XN31-microscopy combination for diagnosing malaria in a nonendemic country.

articles.headline: A pre-post study of 51 rural Burkina Faso health facilities found Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention (SMC; monthly sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine) reduced pneumonia diagnoses 0.7 per 1,000 person-weeks (IRR=0.86), diarrhea 0.2 (IRR=0.83), acute malnutrition 0.05 (IRR=0.71), and antibiotic prescriptions 0.90 (IRR=0.88). Authors conclude SMC provides benefits beyond malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Indirect benefits of seasonal malaria chemoprevention for non-malarial pediatric infections and routine antibiotic use in real-world programmatic settings: a pre-post study using positive and negative controls.

articles.headline: Microsatellite analysis of 654 Anopheles funestus s.s. across ten Tanzanian regions found no significant differences in allelic richness or genetic diversity among populations. Genetic divergence (FST) between southeastern coastal and inland populations reached 0.288; highest divergence was between An. funestus and An. parensis (>0.2). Authors suggest anthropogenic gene flow, not geographic isolation, drives differentiation
articles.title_original: Microsatellite analysis of genetic differentiation among populations of the malaria vector Anopheles funestus across mainland Tanzania reveals contrasted patterns of geographic isolation and gene flow.

articles.headline: Cambodia's Malaria Information System (MIS) is a locally developed, real-time, case-based digital platform integrating surveillance, diagnostics, treatment, and logistics across all health levels. Mobile/web applications enable offline reporting by village malaria workers with customizable dashboards supporting the 1-3-7 surveillance model. Authors demonstrate locally developed systems can transform surveillance and accelerate malaria elimination
articles.title_original: A bespoke, real-time surveillance system for malaria elimination in Cambodia: architecture, applications and impact.

articles.headline: A systematic synthesis of five qualitative studies (2022–2024) on Plasmodium knowlesi malaria in Malaysia identified three themes: knowledge and lived experiences shape community risk perception; environmental/social barriers constrain prevention engagement and healthcare seeking; and prevention practices reflect local knowledge. Authors conclude prevention behaviors are shaped by social and cultural contexts, supporting community-integrated vector control
articles.title_original: Synthesis of qualitative evidence on community experiences and perceptions of Plasmodium knowlesi malaria and factors influencing prevention and healthcare-seeking behaviours in Malaysia.

articles.headline: A baseline household survey of 7,768 children aged 5–48 months in Ghana prior to RTS,S/AS01E vaccine introduction found 21% (95% CI 18–23%) malaria parasitemia by RDT. Insecticide-treated net ownership was 87%, with 62% of children sleeping under an ITN. Among 22% with fever, 72% sought care, 40% tested for malaria, and 42% took antimalarial drugs
articles.title_original: The Malaria Vaccine Implementation Programme study area in Ghana: results of a household survey prior to the introduction of the RTS,S/AS01 vaccine.

articles.headline: Genomic analysis of 54 P. falciparum isolates from Gambia (1966–1971) found no drug resistance alleles, high genetic complexity within infections, and strong positive selection at surface protein loci. Over ~50 years, major allele frequency changes occurred at gdv1 (sexual conversion regulation), Pfsa1/Pfsa3 (sickle-cell trait association), and other genes. Authors identify long-term adaptations critical for understanding parasite evolution
articles.title_original: Major features of parasite adaptation revealed by genomes of Plasmodium falciparum population samples archived for over 50 years.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling of spleen-RBC dynamics in P. falciparum and P. vivax infections using Papuan splenectomy data shows splenic retention of uninfected RBCs far exceeds circulating RBC loss from parasitization (17:1 for P. falciparum, 82:1 for P. vivax), indicating the spleen's primary role in malaria-induced anemia is nonselective RBC sequestration rather than parasite-mediated hemolysis
articles.title_original: The role of the spleen in red blood cell loss caused by malaria: A mathematical model.

articles.headline: Systematic review of Wolbachia pipientis as a vector control agent identifies stable maternal transmission and cytoplasmic incompatibility in transinfected Anopheles (achieved 100% infection frequency in An. stephensi with wPip strain), but notes persistent challenges requiring host-symbiotic interaction research and long-term field evaluation before Wolbachia is viable for widespread malaria control
articles.title_original: The symbiotic Wolbachia in Anopheles and its role in reducing the transmission of Plasmodium: updates and prospects.

articles.headline: Retrospective case-control study of 345 participants in Ghana found malaria prevalence of 42.6% overall (38.7% in Adaklu, 53.3% in Nkwanta North), with significant onchocerciasis-malaria co-infection (81.7% in Nkwanta North) and weak but significant correlation (OR = 2.03; adjusted OR = 3.52 in Adaklu); RDT versus nPCR showed 89.2% sensitivity and 71.9% specificity
articles.title_original: Malaria in onchocerciasis-endemic populations: patterns in two districts in the Oti and Volta regions of Ghana.

articles.headline: Screening of Drymaria cordata and Macaranga monandra plant extracts against P. falciparum identified high activity in M. monandra bark (IC50 PfDd2: 2.46 μg/mL, SI > 203; IC50 Pf3D7: 1.02 μg/mL, SI > 487), with fractions F2 and F3 showing potent activity (IC50 PfDd2: 0.60–0.92 μg/mL) against multiple asexual blood stages; UHPLC-MS identified lecanoric acid as a promising candidate
articles.title_original: Targeting the intra-erythrocytic life cycle of Plasmodium falciparum unveils antimalarial drug discovery starting points from Drymaria cordata and Macaranga monandra.

articles.headline: Review synthesizing mechanistic advances in hepatocyte autophagy against liver-stage Plasmodium reveals parasite evasion via PVM proteins UIS3/UIS4 blocking LC3 lipidation and endolysosomal fusion, alongside parasite exploitation of GABARAP paralogs for nutrient access; selective modulation of noncanonical autophagy (CASM, PAAR) offers host-directed therapeutic potential for restricting liver-stage development
articles.title_original: Hepatocyte Autophagy in Malaria: Current Concepts, Emerging Mechanisms, and Future Therapeutic Directions.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional serology study of 120 malaria-exposed individuals and 24 controls across four Indonesian villages (Menoreh Hills) using protein microarray found PfEBA175 seroprevalence 10–40% with focal hotspots (Kembaran 40%, Wadas 23%, p < 0.001) and PfRh5 universal immunogenicity of 53–70% linked to behavioral risk factors, indicating micro-epidemiological heterogeneity in pre-elimination settings informative for multi-antigen vaccine strategies
articles.title_original: Serological evidence of continued exposure to Plasmodium falciparum among residents in a near-elimination setting.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 1,000 caregivers in Cameroon found 89.6% acceptance of malaria vaccines (RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M), with acceptance significantly influenced by information source (community health events vs. social media, OR = 1.663), individual perception (OR = 0.098 for very rejecting vs. very accepting), sex (OR = 0.704 male vs. female), religion, and occupation
articles.title_original: Determinants of caregivers' acceptance of the malaria vaccine: The case of Bamenda and Bamenda III Health Districts in Cameroon.

articles.headline: Case series documenting transient neuropsychiatric manifestations following P. falciparum malaria (specific clinical features, prevalence, and case numbers not provided in title-only record)
articles.title_original: A Case Series of Transient Neuropsychiatric Manifestations Following Plasmodium Falciparum Malaria.

articles.headline: Retrospective analysis of 15 years (2009–2023) in Burkina Faso found malaria investments totaled USD 13.545 billion (15.3% of health sector funding) and reduced under-5 malaria case fatality rate from 3.4% to 1.3%, with case incidence per capita increasing from 0.8 to 2.1 contacts/year; Sahel, Boucle du Mouhoun, and Nord regions showed 3-4-fold higher mortality risk than Centre region
articles.title_original: Funded investments contributed to the reduction of malaria morbidity and mortality in children under five: fifteen years retrospective study from 2009 to 2023 in Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: Case report of 36-year-old woman in Burkina Faso with rare triple co-infection of P. falciparum, dengue (DEN3), and COVID-19 complicated by sepsis (SOFA 7) and multiple organ dysfunction, highlighting diagnostic complexity with 10-day COVID-19 diagnosis delay and advocating for systematic multiplex PCR testing in endemic areas during rainy season
articles.title_original: Sepsis secondary to co-infection with malaria, dengue and COVID-19: a case report.

articles.headline: Prospective arboviral surveillance in Kenya using real-time PCR across two counties found dengue in 1.23% (4/326) of non-malarial febrile cases in Garissa County (July 2023) and 9.00% (26/289) in Mombasa County (July 2024); no other arboviruses (Chikungunya, yellow fever, Rift Valley fever, Zika, Mayaro, West Nile) detected, indicating need for scaled arboviral diagnostics in peripheral health facilities
articles.title_original: Molecular diagnosis of arboviruses (dengue, Chikungunya, yellow fever, Rift Valley fever, Mayaro, West Nile, and Zika viruses) in non-malaria acute febrile illnesses in Kenya's Garissa County, July 2023, and Mombasa County, July 2024.

articles.headline: Review of Plasmodium malariae, the least-studied human malaria parasite despite global distribution, identifies critical gaps: chronic low-grade parasitemia, emerging antimalarial drug resistance, absent P.m.-specific diagnostics, and diagnostic challenges mask its contribution to malaria burden; enhanced molecular diagnostics, targeted therapies, and improved surveillance are urgently needed to prevent resurgence as other Plasmodium species are controlled
articles.title_original: Plasmodium malariae is an overlooked malaria parasite with emerging challenges.

articles.headline: Malaria surveillance assessment in Jimma Town, Ethiopia (2012–2016) found cases surged from 432 to 11,824, with P. falciparum comprising 56.4% of infections; school-aged children and adults experienced largest increases, and private facilities' contribution to reporting grew from 47% to 66%. The system was functional but limited by incomplete data reporting, weak feedback mechanisms, and poor private-sector engagement, requiring strengthened data quality assurance and private provider collaboration
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the malaria surveillance system and trends in Jimma town, Southwest Ethiopia, 2024: a mixed-method study.

articles.headline: Qualitative study (Oct–Nov 2023) in Indonesia, Peru, and Rwanda with 24 stakeholder interviews and 16 focus groups (139 participants total) exploring non-invasive malaria diagnostic tools (saliva, exhaled VOCs, transdermal detection). Users perceived comfort with non-invasive approaches versus painful blood draws, valued portability and rapid results; major concerns were unverified accuracy compared with established methods, distrust of non-blood specimens, and inability to differentiate Plasmodium species
articles.title_original: "It doesn't require blood!": Perceptions around non-invasive malaria testing tools in Indonesia, Peru, and Rwanda.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 40 RCTs (33,785 children) found iron supplementation in malaria-endemic areas resulted in little difference in clinical malaria risk (RR 0.93, 95% CI 0.87–1.00; 16 trials, 7,843 children; high-certainty) and slightly reduced severe malaria (RR 0.90, 95% CI 0.81–0.98; 5 trials, 3,421 children; high-certainty). Iron plus folic acid showed little difference in severe malaria (RR 1.11, 95% CI 0.96–1.28; 2 trials, 17,575 children; low-certainty)
articles.title_original: Oral iron supplements for children in malaria-endemic areas.

articles.headline: Phase I/II open-label RCT of 40 children (mean age 6.54 years) with malaria-induced acute kidney injury in Uganda comparing paracetamol 20 mg/kg 6-hourly for 48 h (n=20) versus tepid sponging (n=20). Mean time to renal recovery: paracetamol was 0.491 h longer than control (95% CI −9.265 to 10.248; p=0.921), not statistically significant. Paracetamol was safe but did not improve renal recovery
articles.title_original: A phase I/II randomized controlled clinical trial to assess the feasibility, safety, and preliminary effectiveness of paracetamol in resolving acute kidney injury in children with severe malaria.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional telephone survey (2023) of 795 rural Senegalese adults found 35.6% had received ≥1 COVID-19 dose and 58.6% favored a hypothetical free adult malaria vaccine. COVID-vaccinated individuals were 60% more likely to favor malaria vaccination (p<0.001); perceived disease severity drove COVID-19 uptake while perceived susceptibility drove malaria vaccine intention; women favored malaria vaccination more (p=0.005), older adults ≥59 had higher COVID uptake (p<0.001)
articles.title_original: Exploring factors affecting malaria vaccination intention and COVID-19 vaccine uptake: evidence from a joint analysis in rural Senegal.

articles.headline: In vitro and in silico evaluation of seven synthesized thymol derivatives against P. falciparum (3D7 strain) and P. knowlesi. Compounds 4 and 6 showed highest potency: EC50 6.01±1.7 µM and 6.8±1.1 µM respectively with selectivity indices 16.5 and 14.6; docking binding affinities −8.3 to −8.4 kcal/mol to PfCRT; non-toxic to mammalian cells (CC50 >99 µM for most derivatives). Compounds 4 and 6 identified as promising lead antimalarial candidates
articles.title_original: Thymol Derivatives as Antimalarial Agents: Synthesis, Activity Against Plasmodium falciparum, ADMET Profiling, and Molecular Docking Insights.

articles.headline: Prospective cohort study (Jan–Sep 2025) of 96 P. falciparum gametocytemia cases in Ethiopia comparing artemether-lumefantrine (AL) alone versus AL plus single-dose primaquine (SLD-PQ). Gametocyte prevalence declined faster with AL+SLD-PQ: 15.2% day 3 versus 28.9% AL alone (p=0.001), 0% versus 12.2% by day 7 (p=0.033). Post-treatment mosquito infectivity: AL+SLD-PQ 6.8% versus 28.6% AL on day 3 (p=0.001), 0% versus 7.3% on day 7 (p=0.01). Addition of SLD-PQ markedly accelerates gametocyte clearance and blocks transmission
articles.title_original: The Effect of Artemether-Lumefantrine Combined with a Single Dose of Primaquine on Plasmodium falciparum Gametocyte Clearance and Post-Treatment Infectivity to Anopheles arabiensis.

articles.headline: Molecular study conditionally knocking out PfAPR1 (apical polar ring protein) in P. falciparum shows this protein is essential for asexual replication. Loss of PfAPR1 caused defects in daughter cell segmentation and microtubule organization while preserving IMC formation and apical polarity; parasites could contact red blood cells but failed to form tight junctions, completely blocking invasion. PfAPR1 is identified as a promising antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: The essential function of the apical polar ring during the blood stage of Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Genotyping study of P. falciparum Msp1 Block 2 (281 samples) and Msp2 Block 3 (194 samples) in Bamenda, Cameroon. For Msp1: RO33 most prevalent at 44.7%; K1 allele linked to severe malaria. For Msp2: 3D7 most prevalent at 56.7%; Msp2 alleles associated with sex (χ²=8.856, p=0.012). No alleles showed seasonal predominance. Findings inform selection and design of malaria prevention measures for Cameroon
articles.title_original: Genetic Diversity of Plasmodium falciparum Merozoite Surface Protein 1 and 2 Genes and Their Allele Association With Malaria Severity, Seasons, and Sex in Bamenda, North West Cameroon.
articles.journal: Can J Infect Dis Med Microbiol

articles.headline: Co-creation engagement project (April 2023–June 2024) in northeastern Thailand involving 36 in-person activities with ~550 participants from communities, stakeholders, and health workers to design locally-owned malaria health education materials integrated with priority local health issues. Co-created health calendar offered potential entry point to maintain malaria awareness in low-transmission areas; responding to local health concerns emerged as key enabler for sustainability, though additional funding sources needed to maintain local health worker capacity
articles.title_original: Sustaining community-based malaria services through stakeholder engagement: lessons from co-creation in northeastern Thailand.

articles.headline: Multicentric entomological survey (2021–2023) across 14 districts in eight Indian states characterized malaria vector bionomics. Anopheles culicifacies widespread in all states; highest indoor man-hour density (MHD) 11.95 (Kanker, Chhattisgarh), outdoor 27.16 (Bareilly, Uttar Pradesh). An. minimus showed differential resting behavior in Assam/Tripura with high anthropophagic behavior (human blood index 0.94); An. baimaii had high sporozoite infection rate 5.88%. An. culicifacies resistant to alpha-cypermethrin and deltamethrin, An. stephensi resistant to multiple insecticides
articles.title_original: A multicentric study on understanding the bionomics of Indian malaria vectors across diverse eco-epidemiological settings.

articles.headline: Quasi-experimental pre-post study (Jan–May 2024 and Jan–May 2025) of 346 school-aged children (55.5% aged 11–14 years) in Tanzania evaluating intermittent preventive treatment with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DP) impact on absenteeism. Overall absenteeism decreased from 13% to 10% (p=0.001); malaria-related absences declined from 27.8% to 11.3%; school attendance increased 6.7%. Most pupils (91.9%) received all three doses. IPTsc demonstrated dual benefit for health and education in malaria-endemic areas
articles.title_original: The impact of intermittent preventive treatment for malaria on school absenteeism among primary school-aged children in Handeni District Council, Tanzania: quasi-experimental study.

articles.headline: Among 155,669 suspected cases at an Ethiopian hospital (2015–2021), 11,495 (7.4%, 95% CI 7.2–7.5%) were confirmed microscopically: 90.8% P. falciparum, 6.2% P. vivax, 3.0% mixed. Males had higher prevalence (8.8% vs 6.2%, OR=1.48, p<0.001). Prevalence fluctuated with resurgence after 2018, indicating P. falciparum remains predominant and intensified surveillance is needed
articles.title_original: Analysis of a seven-year trend in malaria prevalence at Pawe General Hospital, Northwestern Ethiopia: a retrospective cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: A hybrid high-resolution model (WRF/WRF-Hydro + VECTRI) in western Kenya (2007–2022) showed insecticide-treated net (ITN) coverage reduced entomological inoculation rate by 58% and malaria incidence by 41%, with highest efficacy under warm and moderately wet conditions. Physically grounded environmental modeling combined with targeted vector control offers cost-effective malaria management under climate variability
articles.title_original: High resolution physically based modelling reveals malaria incidence reduction by vector control measures.

articles.headline: Enhanced antenatal care provider training for integrated sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (IPTp-SP) delivery through seasonal malaria chemoprevention significantly improved directly observed therapy rates in Mali (79.2% vs 68.7%, p=0.006) and Burkina Faso (52.3% vs 28.7%, p<0.0001) versus standard care. ANC providers found the integrated strategy feasible and acceptable, supporting scalable IPTp-SP deployment
articles.title_original: Effectiveness of antenatal care delivery of intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine in the context of an integrated strategy with seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Mali and Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: A 35-year-old man with acute P. ovale sensu lato infection developed splenic rupture after initiating atovaquone-proguanil followed by artesunate. Spleen histology revealed 10-fold accumulation of intact P. ovale-infected erythrocytes compared to peripheral circulation with evidence of cytoadherence in small blood vessels
articles.title_original: Splenic accumulation of intact Plasmodium ovale sensu lato-infected red blood cells in a patient presenting with splenic rupture.

articles.headline: Systematic review protocol to map miRNA expression patterns in malaria-arbovirus coinfections (dengue, chikungunya, Zika). No quantitative results reported; aims to synthesize miRNA data from observational studies and trials to identify biomarkers and therapeutic targets for improved coinfection management
articles.title_original: MicroRNAs profiling in malaria and arbovirus coinfection: A systematic review protocol.

articles.headline: Interviews with 42 malaria program experts (2023–2025) identified seven key social development factors enabling China's malaria elimination (WHO-certified June 2021): health education (66.7%), living environment (71.4%), health system (100%), water/sanitation (59.5%), agriculture (57.1%), employment (52.4%), working environment (64.3%). Social development and health systems are inseparable for sustained elimination
articles.title_original: Malaria elimination practices in China from the perspective of health system and social development.

articles.headline: Scoping review of 24 studies on hemozoin-based malaria detection across eight technology classes. Magneto-optical systems (Hz-MOD, Gazelle™, RMOD) showed highest operational readiness with robust specificity but reduced low-parasitemia sensitivity; Raman/SERS achieved sub-100 infected cells/mL sensitivity; photoacoustic demonstrated promising in vivo detection. Multiple viable pathways exist but clinical validation and standardized comparison are needed before field deployment
articles.title_original: Hemozoin as a Diagnostic Biomarker: A Scoping Review of Next-Generation Malaria Detection Technologies.

articles.headline: Community-based home management (CBHM) in Matam, Senegal detected 2,307 childhood illness cases including 1,464 fevers in transhumant herder communities. Malaria testing rate reached 98.9% with 21.1% positivity; 96.4% of positives received artemisinin-based combination therapies. CBHM-Ferlo improved childhood illness care and demonstrated value of One Health integration
articles.title_original: How the One Health approach can make effective the World Health Organization's recommendations on integrated management of childhood illnesses: Case of community-based home management of malaria in the Ferlo of Matam, Senegal.

articles.headline: Satellite imagery and panel regression (2016–2023) quantified malaria impact of illegal gold mining among Yanomami in the Amazon. Approximately 300% increase in malaria cases occurred; each 1 s.d. increase in mining activity predicted 20–46% rise in malaria incidence 1–2 years later, and each 1 s.d. increase in forest-edge perimeter predicted 55% increase in cases. Illegal mining-driven forest fragmentation substantially drives malaria burden
articles.title_original: Investigating the Yanomami malaria outbreak: gold mining and malaria.
articles.authors: de Angeli Dutra D, et al.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 12 Ethiopian studies (4,511 patients) found 62% (95% CI 46–77%) of malaria patients delayed treatment-seeking beyond 24 hours after symptom onset. Key determinants were lack of malaria knowledge, distance from facilities, costs, limited transport, farming occupation, self-medication, low income, lack of insurance, and fear of drug side effects. Health literacy, behavioral change, and infrastructure expansion are critical to reduce delays
articles.title_original: Determinants of delay in treatment-seeking among malaria patients attending health facilities in Ethiopia from 2010 to 2024: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: In Mali's Dogon Longitudinal Study (N=317, follow-up to age 5), severe placental malaria parasite density was associated with reduced linear growth at 6 months (−0.83 cm, 95% CI −1.63 to −0.03; p=0.042) and a trend continuing to age 5, plus increased BMI at 2, 3, 4, and 5 years (0.56, 0.82, 1.09, 1.35 kg/m² respectively). Severe placental malaria is associated with stunted linear growth and elevated postnatal BMI
articles.title_original: Association between placental malaria, postnatal linear growth, and body mass index in the Dogon Longitudinal Study, Mali.

articles.headline: In Côte d'Ivoire (11,161 RDT-register pairs), external panel interpretation (57.9% positive) versus register recording showed strong agreement (κ=0.83, 95% CI 0.77–0.88), PPV 90.7%, NPV 94.8%. However, negative/invalid results were misrecorded as positive 5.9% vs false negatives 2.0%, with systematic bias toward false positives when malaria diagnosis or antimalarials were documented. Regular cassette-register comparison and enhanced HCW training can reduce false-positive distortion of surveillance data
articles.title_original: Accuracy of recording of malaria rapid diagnostic tests in Côte d'Ivoire.

articles.headline: In a longitudinal cohort of 60 children (ages 6–10) in Togo treated with artemether-lumefantrine, symptomatic malaria from P. falciparum (33.3% of those infected) was characterized by elevated IL-6 (p=0.0004 vs non-infected), IFN-γ (p<0.0001 vs non-infected), and IL-10 (p<0.0001 vs non-infected). In symptomatic children, IL-10 showed strong negative correlation with qPCR Ct values (r=−0.973, p=0.001) and strong positive correlation with parasite density (r=0.911, p=0.001), suggesting these cytokines serve as prognostic biomarkers for symptomatic malaria
articles.title_original: Distinct systemic cytokine signatures define symptomatic malaria in children: insights from a longitudinal study in Togo.

articles.headline: Genomic analysis of An. coluzzii across West African bioclimatic zones (Ghana, Mali, Côte d'Ivoire) revealed restricted gene flow between northern savannah and southern forested regions, with marked differences in insecticide resistance profiles. The North showed greater pyrethroid resistance at the Vgsc gene and metabolic resistance at carboxylesterases and Gste2; the coastal Greater Accra region exhibited distinct resistance mechanisms including a unique selection signal at the Cyp12f cytochrome P450 gene, indicating environmental conditions restrict gene flow and drive geographic distribution of resistance alleles
articles.title_original: Genomic population structure and insecticide resistance mechanisms in the malaria vector An. coluzzii across contrasting bioclimatic zones in West Africa.

articles.headline: This study characterized chromatin state dynamics throughout the 48-hour asexual intraerythrocytic developmental cycle (IDC) of P. falciparum using genome-wide profiles of chromatin accessibility, histone modifications, and HP1 occupancy. Several chromatin states remained stable across the IDC while others were dynamic and linked to gene activation or repression; integration of transcription factor binding data grouped Plasmodium TFs by chromatin preferences, revealing how chromatin remodeling coordinates blood-stage development
articles.title_original: Chromatin state dynamics during the Plasmodium falciparum intraerythrocytic development cycle.
articles.authors: Brown AS Jr, Llinás M, Mahony S

articles.headline: A household wealth index constructed from cross-sectional surveys of 19,686 Tanzanian households (2015 and 2018) showed the poorest tertile had significantly lower odds of owning (OR=0.62, 95% CI 0.54–0.70) or using LLINs (OR=0.53, 95% CI 0.45–0.62) than the wealthiest. A difference-in-differences analysis found the 1,7-mRCTR intervention yielded disproportionately greater increases in LLIN ownership (OR=1.26, 95% CI 1.03–1.56) and use (OR=1.88, 95% CI 1.25–2.82) among the poorest households, effectively reducing wealth-based inequalities
articles.title_original: Development of a new wealth index for Tanzania: the moderated effect of the implementation of 1,7- malaria reactive community-based testing and response (1,7-mRCTR) by socioeconomic position (SEP) with malaria prevention.

articles.headline: A scoping literature review of 26 papers (2013–2023) on congenital malaria (CM)—neonatal Plasmodium parasitemia presenting within 28 days of birth or in infants <1 year with no travel history—found diagnosis remains challenging in both endemic and non-endemic settings. The authors propose a diagnostic algorithm emphasizing polymerase chain reaction to detect very low parasitaemia below microscopy's limit of detection, highlighting the critical importance of investigations informed by maternal malaria history
articles.title_original: Congenital Malaria in Non-Endemic Settings: A Literature Review and Update on Diagnosis and Management.

articles.headline: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 8 RCTs in moderate sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP) resistance areas compared azithromycin (AZ)-containing intermittent preventive therapy (IPTp) regimens to SP-based IPTp in pregnant women. AZ-containing IPTp reduced peripheral parasitemia at delivery (OR=0.71, 95% CI 0.57–0.88) with no significant differences in adverse pregnancy outcomes and a safety profile similar to SP-based IPTp (OR=1.11, 95% CI 0.76–1.62), suggesting AZ-IPTp as a promising alternative given escalating SP resistance
articles.title_original: Safety and efficacy of azithromycin-containing regimen as an intermittent preventive therapy for malaria in pregnant women: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.

articles.headline: This review synthesizes evidence on drug stockouts and treatment delays in African health systems, which are persistent barriers to timely artemisinin-based combination therapy (ACT) for malaria. Stockouts result from weaknesses in procurement, supply chains, and health infrastructure, leading to increased morbidity and mortality, facilitation of drug resistance, and eroded public trust. The authors advocate for comprehensive strategies including strengthened supply chains, improved forecasting and inventory management, and innovative technologies to ensure consistent drug availability and timely treatment
articles.title_original: Drug stockouts and treatment delays in African health systems: impact on malaria morbidity and mortality.
articles.authors: Obeagu EI, Abdulrahman AO, Anetor JI

articles.headline: This case report describes a 2-year-11-month-old boy returning from Sudan who presented with fever and diarrhea; P. falciparum malaria was ultimately identified through routine blood examination informed by epidemiological history and confirmed by rapid diagnostic testing and microscopy. The case exemplifies a 'clinical suspicion → targeted laboratory testing → definitive pathogen identification' diagnostic pathway and underscores that imported malaria in children is rare but often initially missed in non-endemic areas due to atypical presentations
articles.title_original: Alert for Imported Malaria in Non-Endemic Areas: A Case Report of Atypical Falciparum Malaria in a Young Child and Diagnostic Experience.

articles.headline: Four cross-sectional surveys over two wet and two dry seasons enrolled 4,176 children (ages 7–13) from 21 schools in Malawi's Chikwawa District, establishing baseline prevalence of malaria (10.8%, 95% CI 9.8–11.7%), urogenital schistosomiasis (36.5%, 95% CI 35.1–38.0%), intestinal schistosomiasis (1.9%, 95% CI 1.5–2.4%), and co-infection (5.2%, 95% CI 4.5–5.9%). Malaria exceeded 25% and urogenital schistosomiasis surpassed 50% in certain schools with strong spatial heterogeneity; malaria showed seasonal fluctuations. These findings establish critical surveillance data prior to the anticipated disease-altering impact of the Shire Valley Transformation Programme irrigation scheme
articles.title_original: Repeated biannual cross-sectional surveys in primary schools set baseline seasonal and spatial surveillance for malaria and schistosomiasis in the Shire Valley Transformation Programme (SVTP), Malawi.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis

articles.headline: In 30 BALB/c mice inoculated with P. berghei (NK65 strain), treatment with graded doses of ethanol extract P. longifolia (EEPL) (100, 200, 400 mg/kg/day) or artemether-lumefantrine (2 mg/kg/day) steadily reduced parasitemia; all treatments significantly elevated spleen antioxidants (GSH, GPx, CAT, SOD, TAC, ferritin) and reversed parasite-induced splenic disorganization. Artemether-lumefantrine achieved the highest survival rate, followed by 400 mg/kg/day EEPL, demonstrating that EEPL can control macrophage response in P. berghei infection, though characterization of bioactive compounds requires further investigation
articles.title_original: Spleen protective role, Antiplasmodial and Antioxidant activity of Polyalthia longifolia in mice infected with Plasmodium berghei parasite.

articles.headline: Digital transition of seasonal malaria chemoprevention campaigns in Nampula, Mozambique using the DIGIT Health Campaign Management platform improved sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine administration and reduced drug wastage (from >10% on first day to <5% after targeted supervision). Administrative coverage rates (75–109%) were more consistent with survey-based household coverage (~74%) in the digital campaign than paper-based (93–105% vs ~79%), and the digital approach enabled real-time visualization of first-dose recording and direct observation of therapy adherence, key quality indicators for malaria chemoprevention
articles.title_original: Lessons from using SALAMA (DIGIT HCM-health campaign management platform) to implement and optimize seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Nampula, Mozambique.

articles.headline: Of 148 individuals with suspected malaria in Mizan Aman, Ethiopia, 29 (27.9%) tested P. falciparum PCR-positive with 26 (89.7%) false-negative by a PfHRP2/pLDH RDT. Among the 29 P. falciparum infections, 27 (93.1%) carried deletions in both pfhrp2 and pfhrp3 genes and 22 (75.9%) had negligible pfhrp2 transcripts; proteomics detected PfLDH peptides in 10 samples but no PfHRP2 or PfHRP3 peptides. Molecular, transcriptomic, and proteomic characterization revealed a heterogeneous array of mechanisms (gene deletions, transcriptional silencing, protein absence) underlying RDT failure in these P. falciparum infections
articles.title_original: Molecular, Transcriptomic, and Proteomic Characterization of Plasmodium Infections that Evade Detection by Rapid Diagnostic Tests in Mizan Aman, Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Compared three VA algorithms (GPT-4o, InSilicoVA, InterVA-5) against MITS in 3,129 under-five deaths across six SSA countries (Dec 2016-Dec 2022). GPT-4o correctly classified 46.2% of malaria deaths versus 30.0% (InSilicoVA) and 23.1% (InterVA-5), with cause-specific mortality fraction accuracy of 0.36 versus 0.07–0.08. Sierra Leone had highest malaria attribution at 30.3%, Kenya 17.3%, Mozambique 13%. GPT-4o demonstrated superior performance in identifying malaria-attributed deaths
articles.title_original: Comparison of verbal autopsy using a large language model to biologically confirmed causes of death for malaria and other communicable diseases among children in six sub-Saharan African countries.

articles.headline: Described UPHFP contributions to Uganda's malaria control (2015-2022). The 2-year post-Master's fellowship program supported 50 malaria projects: 14 surveillance, 11 outbreak investigations, 7 epidemiological studies, 5 case studies, 6 quality improvement, 3 policy briefs, 4 training initiatives. Projects informed policy decisions and strengthened surveillance and outbreak response, enhancing epidemiologic workforce capacity despite single-source funding vulnerability
articles.title_original: Uganda Public Health Fellowship Program's Contributions to Malaria Control Programs 2015-2022: Strategies, Implementation Challenges, and Opportunities.

articles.headline: Investigated LILRB1 regulatory variants in 267 Senegalese individuals (116 severe malaria, 74 mild malaria, 77 controls). The rs7246537 A allele showed protective association: clinical malaria (OR=0.50, 95% CI 0.28–0.88, p=0.0165) and cerebral malaria (OR=0.44, 95% CI 0.22–0.86, p=0.0176). Luciferase assays confirmed allele-specific effects with A allele driving twofold lower promoter activity than G allele. rs7246537 reduces malaria susceptibility via LILRB1 modulation
articles.title_original: A Functional Intronic Variant of LILRB1 Associated with Clinical Malaria in the Senegalese Population.

articles.headline: Analyzed NK cell phenotype and ADCC in Ugandan children <5 years with severe malaria (n=21) versus controls (n=19) across moderate and low transmission areas. Severe malaria cases had higher LILRB1+ NK cells and, in low-transmission areas, elevated degranulating NK cells but reduced IFN-γ production across both areas. Transmission level influenced NK function, revealing compound functional differences during severe malaria
articles.title_original: Altered natural killer cell function in children with severe malaria.

articles.headline: Case report of 30-year-old Moroccan soldier who developed Plasmodium vivax malaria six months postdeployment despite mefloquine prophylaxis. Treated with Artemether-Lumefantrine; within 24 hours developed spontaneous splenic rupture with subcapsular hematoma and hemoperitoneum. Conservative management (bed rest, monitoring, transfusion) achieved complete resolution without splenectomy. Illustrates that splenic rupture should be considered in malaria patients presenting with abdominal pain and hemodynamic instability
articles.title_original: Spontaneous splenic rupture as a rare complication of Plasmodium vivax: a case report.

articles.headline: Applied Temporal Fusion Transformer to long-term climate and weekly malaria/dengue data from South Africa and Vietnam. Best malaria model achieved R²=0.95 (MAE=4.98); dengue models reached R²≤0.90. Extreme temperature and rainfall were strongest predictors; ENSO and IOD improved longer-range malaria forecasts. Climate-disease relationships revealed specific temperature/rainfall thresholds associated with elevated transmission. TFT demonstrated accurate, interpretable forecasting for early warning systems
articles.title_original: Application of a Temporal Fusion Transformer and Long-Term Climate and Disease Data to Assess the Predictive Power and Understand the Drivers for Malaria and Dengue.
articles.journal: Int J Environ Res Public Health

articles.headline: Eight sulfur-containing chalcogen-phosphorane analogues showed submicromolar potency against P. falciparum 3D7 (IC50s 0.7–1.9 μM) with selectivity indices ≥12 over hepatocyte/kidney cells. Compound 5 demonstrated fast-acting activity, no cross-resistance with standard antimalarials, and additive interaction with artesunate. In murine P. berghei model, modest in vivo activity with slightly improved survival. Chalcogen-phosphoranes represent attractive hit-to-lead series for antimalarial development
articles.title_original: Fast-Acting Chalcogen-Phosphoranes Inhibit Sensitive and Resistant Plasmodium falciparum Strains.

articles.headline: Prospective evaluation of DHAP (5 sites) and AP (4 sites) in Ghanaian children 6 months–9 years (Aug–Dec 2023) using WHO drug efficacy surveillance protocol. DHAP day-42 PCR-uncorrected cure rate 99.2% (95% CI 97.6–99.8); AP 96.2% (95% CI 92.9–98.0). PCR-corrected: DHAP 100%, AP 97.3% (95% CI 94.3–98.8). No early treatment failures. Both drugs maintained high efficacy (>90%) warranting continued use
articles.title_original: Therapeutic efficacy of dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine and artesunate-pyronaridine combinations in the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Ghana, 2023.

articles.headline: Investigated de novo CNV dynamics in P. falciparum genome using low-input genomics following treatment with sub-lethal antimalarial compound inhibiting replication. Observed significant increase in genome-wide de novo CNVs following replication stress. De novo CNVs encompassed genes from various cellular pathways participating in human infection. Findings emphasize connection between replication stress, DNA repair, and CNV generation in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Replication stress increases de novo CNVs across the malaria parasite genome.

articles.headline: Prospective comparison of miLab automated microscopy against conventional microscopy and NM-PCR in 400 malaria samples (2021–2024). miLab versus thin blood smear: 90.8% concordance, kappa=0.8, sensitivity 92.1%, specificity 89.4%. Versus NM-PCR: sensitivity 62.8%, specificity 95.4%, concordance 68.9%. Parasite counts 11.6% lower than conventional microscopy. miLab achieved microscopy-equivalent sensitivity without expert microscopists but requires improvements in species identification
articles.title_original: Automated microscopy for malaria diagnosis in a reference laboratory in nonendemic settings.

articles.headline: Analyzed 23 Kenyan counties (Jan 2019–June 2021) using mixed-effect regression and spatial analysis to assess climate-mosquito distribution-malaria relationships. Malaria prevalence by zone: Lake Endemic 19%, Coastal 5%, semi-arid 2%, low-risk 0.9%. Anopheles gambiae dominated coastal/semi-arid areas; An. funestus highest in highland/lake zones. Environmental factors and ITN coverage were important predictors informing targeted control strategies
articles.title_original: Mapping mosquito diversity in Kenya correlating species distribution with malaria prevalence across varied climatic parameters.

articles.headline: Human landing catches indoors/outdoors in Porto-Novo, Benin (April–Sept 2019) captured 6,201 mosquitoes (55.6% Cx. quinquefasciatus, 25.7% Ma. africana, 16.2% An. gambiae s.l.). An. gambiae s.l. biting rates similar indoors/outdoors in 3 neighborhoods but higher outdoors in 2 (exophagic trend). P. falciparum infections heterogeneous across sites. 86% surveyed households slept indoors; 14% outdoors. Findings emphasize context-specific control strategies
articles.title_original: An underestimated risk? Analysis of outdoor malaria transmission in an urban area of Southern Benin.

articles.headline: Healthcare workers' knowledge about chikungunya and non-malarial febrile illness management in Ethiopia was fair (mean knowledge score 10.7) with only 23.4% achieving good knowledge despite 93.6% prior awareness. In retrospective case review, 95.1% were diagnosed as malaria but 59.2% were actually non-malarial. Key barriers included diagnostic tool shortages, inadequate training, and reliance on clinical judgment for overlapping symptoms
articles.title_original: Practices and challenges related to management of chikungunya and non-malarial acute febrile illnesses in malaria-endemic areas of Afar region, Northeast Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Between 2023-2024, 1,718 non-gambiae Anopheles were collected across Burkina Faso (688 in 2023, 1,030 in 2024), with An. nili overwhelmingly dominant (87.5% and 93% respectively). P. falciparum was detected in all species except An. funestus, with highest infection in Diébougou. Secondary vectors An. nili and An. coustani demonstrate outdoor anthropophilic feeding, limiting effectiveness of indoor-focused control measures
articles.title_original: Beyond Anopheles gambiae sensu lato: exploring the impact of non-dominant Anopheles species on malaria persistence in high-transmission endemic areas of Burkina Faso.
