articles.headline: Cambodia maintained malaria elimination progress toward 2025 through mobile malaria worker (MMW) services during COVID-19 pandemic restrictions. As malaria burden declined, transmission became focal among remote populations requiring MMW outreach. The report describes programmatic adaptations enabling MMWs to continue case detection and treatment while managing pandemic logistical challenges, though specific outcome metrics are not provided
articles.title_original: Progress towards malaria elimination: insights from Cambodia's mobile malaria workers during the COVID-19 pandemic.

articles.headline: Pyronaridine-artesunate (n=58) showed slower SARS-CoV-2 clearance than artemether-lumefantrine (n=59) in co-infected patients: day-7 clearance 41% vs 58% (aRR=0.78, p=0.37), median viral load day 7 of 855 vs 81 copies/mL (p=0.023), and slower time to clearance (aHR=0.55, p=0.004). Both achieved high parasitological cure (100% and 99%) with similar symptom resolution (aHR=1.01, p=0.79)
articles.title_original: Artemether-lumefantrine versus pyronaridine-artesunate for the treatment of malaria in patients with mild to moderate COVID-19 in Kenya and Burkina Faso: a randomised open-label trial (MALCOV).

articles.headline: Malaria-GENOMAP is a web-based tool integrating genomic variation data from 1,359 P. vivax, 158 P. malariae, 139 P. knowlesi, 47 P. ovale wallikeri, 36 P. ovale curtisi, and 38 P. simium samples. The tool visualizes allele frequencies, population structure, geographic mutation associations, and drug resistance markers to support malaria control planning
articles.title_original: Malaria-GENOMAP: a web-based tool for exploring genomic variation of malaria parasites.

articles.headline: Among 4,417 Ghanaian children <5 years, HRP2-based RDT prevalence was 20.3% versus 9.9% by microscopy. RDTs demonstrated sensitivity 89.7% (95% CI 86.5-92.2), specificity 87.4% (86.3-88.4), positive predictive value 43.9%, negative predictive value 98.7%. False negatives were more likely in rural areas and IRS-sprayed dwellings, with regional variation, indicating potential prevalence overestimation in high-transmission areas
articles.title_original: False-positive and false-negative malaria rapid diagnostic test results: evidence from a nationally representative survey of children in Ghana.

articles.headline: Cambodia's National Center for Parasitology, Entomology and Malaria Control disseminated EFFORT trial results comparing 7-day high-dose primaquine (7 mg/kg) and single-dose tafenoquine (300 mg) versus 14-day low-dose primaquine (3.5 mg/kg) for P. vivax treatment. Findings inform policy shift toward more effective regimens addressing relapse, particularly as P. vivax becomes predominant species in low-transmission settings
articles.title_original: Moving towards high-dose primaquine or single-dose tafenoquine for Plasmodium vivax treatment in Cambodia: a meeting report from dissemination of results of the EFFORT trial to stakeholders.

articles.headline: In P. berghei-infected mice, Fe, Cu, and Zn isotopic compositions were altered in parasitized red blood cells: hepatic Fe overload with depletion of heavier isotopes, discrete Cu deficiency with lighter isotopic composition, and unchanged Zn concentration but lighter isotopic composition. Findings indicate Fe and Cu central roles in malaria anemia while Zn responds to host immunity
articles.title_original: Iron, copper and zinc isotope ratios in malaria infection reveal signatures of the Plasmodium biology and host mice response to the infection.

articles.headline: Conditional knockout of transcription factor PbAP2-TR in P. berghei caused developmental arrest at trophozoite stage. Target gene expression was upregulated in knockout parasites, and PbAP2-TR was responsible for transcriptional downregulation from early to late trophozoite development. The PbAP2-TR-PbMORC complex mediates precise transcriptional control essential for asexual blood stage development
articles.title_original: Precise gene regulation through transcriptional repression is essential for Plasmodium berghei asexual blood stage development.

articles.headline: Among 2,265 pregnant women comparing monthly IPTp regimens, IPTp-sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP) reduced C. trachomatis by 80% (2.5% vs 12.4%, reference IPTp-dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine) and GBS by 35% (7.7% vs 11.7%). C. trachomatis was associated with preterm delivery (PR 1.86, 95% CI 1.07-3.25) and GBS with term low birthweight (PR 2.08, 1.06-4.08), suggesting IPTp-SP's antimicrobial benefits
articles.title_original: Impact of intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine on sexually transmitted and reproductive tract infections: results from a randomized trial in Uganda.

articles.headline: Among 2,706 microscopy-PCR paired samples from 11 Indian states, microscopy misidentified 15% of Plasmodium infections (408/2706). Ninety-eight percent of mixed infections (400/405) were misidentified as mono-infections, predominantly P. falciparum. Jharkhand and Madhya Pradesh contributed >20% of misidentifications, resulting in overestimation of P. falciparum burden and underestimation of non-falciparum species in elimination planning
articles.title_original: Plasmodium mono and mixed-infections in India: a tale of see-saw in species misidentification.
articles.authors: Deora N, Pande V, Sinha A

articles.headline: A 63-year-old severe P. falciparum malaria patient with cytokine storm received therapeutic plasma exchange and continuous venovenous hemodiafiltration, achieving dramatic interleukin-6 reduction (729.65 to 26.42 pg/mL) and interferon-gamma reduction (170.38 to 42.47 pg/mL) with recovery from multiple organ dysfunction. Literature review identified 19 of 29 severe malaria cases receiving TPE/blood purification with good outcomes
articles.title_original: The blood purification therapy-based strategy effectively rescued the severe falciparum malaria patient experiencing cytokine storm-driven MODS.

articles.headline: Four neonates with P. vivax congenital/neonatal malaria in Ethiopia (median age 18 days, parasitaemia +1 to +3 on Giemsa smears) all received IV artesunate 3 mg/kg plus antibiotics and discharged by hospital day 7 with no deaths; case series emphasizes diagnostic challenges of P. vivax CNM and need for heightened clinical suspicion in endemic regions
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax congenital and neonatal malaria with atypical presentation in Western Ethiopia: four cases highlighting diagnostic pitfalls.

articles.headline: Multi-scale integrative study of eight triterpenic esters from Keetia leucantha identified inhibition of P. falciparum aminopeptidase PfA-M17, with activity onset in early-ring stage and perturbations in haemoglobin metabolism; further structure-activity studies warranted for potential antimalarial drug development
articles.title_original: Natural triterpenic phenolic esters target PfA-M17 in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Experimental hut trials in Uganda with highly resistant An. funestus and An. gambiae showed Interceptor G2 (chlorfenapyr) bednet superior mortality vs pyrethroid-only: OR 18.7 (95% CI 8.05–48.6, p<0.0001), achieving 70.6% mortality An. funestus and 63.2% An. gambiae, compared to PermaNet 3.0, Olyset Plus, and Royal Guard; Interceptor G2 recommended for highly resistant vector regions
articles.title_original: Chlorfenapyr bednets effectively overcome pyrethroid resistance escalation in highly resistant Anopheles malaria vectors in Uganda.

articles.headline: Exploratory 1H NMR spectroscopy study of 48 school-aged children (27 malaria-positive, 21 controls) from high-transmission Tanzania found modest group-level metabolic alterations in P. falciparum-infected children (lower lactate, pyruvate, branched-chain amino acids, threonine), but KODAMA analysis identified metabolically distinct subgroup (18.5%) with amino acid/lipid shifts resembling symptomatic malaria
articles.title_original: Exploratory Serum Metabolomic Profiling of Asymptomatic Malaria Children Infected with Plasmodium falciparum in Tanzania.

articles.headline: Pv3Rs is a statistical inference tool for P. vivax genetic data that distinguishes relapse (hypnozoite activation) from recrudescence (treatment failure) and reinfection; applicable to length polymorphic loci and amplicon sequencing data, freely available on CRAN under GNU license
articles.title_original: Pv3Rs: Plasmodium vivax relapse, recrudescence, and reinfection statistical genetic inference.
articles.authors: Foo YS, White MT, Taylor AR

articles.headline: Review of phosphatidylinositol 4-kinase beta (PI4Kβ), an essential lipid kinase in Plasmodium species involved in intracellular signaling, vesicular trafficking, and host immunity modulation; PI4Kβ inhibition disrupts parasite survival and may enhance immune recognition, suggesting dual-action therapeutic potential as antiparasitic and immunomodulatory target
articles.title_original: Plasmodium Phosphatidylinositol 4-Kinase Beta (PI4Kβ): An Emerging Target at the Interface of Parasite Biology and Host Immunity.

articles.headline: Hypothesis proposing that exported P. falciparum Hsp70 (PfHsp70-x) on infected RBC membranes activates NK cells to release granzyme B, potentially contributing to severe malaria pathogenesis; author discussion suggests Hsp70-GrB interaction could be leveraged for antimalarial therapy, though requiring empirical investigation
articles.title_original: Is the Association of Granzyme B and the Exported Parasite Hsp70 a Possible Trojan Horse of Severe Malaria?

articles.headline: Review of global malaria status (2000–2019: deaths fell from 864,000 to 576,000, but mortality slowed post-2020); discusses P. falciparum drug resistance emergence, vaccine advances (RTS,S/AS01, R21/MM), and Eastern Mediterranean region increases since 2015; indigenous malaria endemic in Saudi Arabia and Oman among GCC countries
articles.title_original: Malaria Public Health Status: Global Context and Update on Gulf Cooperation Council Countries.

articles.headline: Case report of 7-year-old boy with fever and abdominal pain 6–7 days presenting with leukocytosis (16.3×10³/µL), normocytic normochromic anemia (Hb 9.2 g/dL), severe thrombocytopenia (35,000/µL), elevated CRP (144.8 mg/L), and unusual Plasmodium spp. findings on peripheral blood smear
articles.title_original: An unusual presentation of Plasmodium spp. in peripheral blood smear.

articles.headline: Retrospective study of 32 imported severe P. falciparum malaria cases (median age 41, 89% male) in Moscow 2020–2023 meeting WHO criteria found: 99% presented with high parasitemia, 98% with altered consciousness, 78.1% with profound weakness, 52% with jaundice; no patients used chemoprophylaxis; emphasizes importance of prompt diagnosis and pretravel consultation
articles.title_original: Clinical and epidemiological profile of severe imported Plasmodium falciparum malaria in travelers at hospital admission: A retrospective single-center study in Moscow, Russia.
articles.authors: Emerole K, Tokmalaev A, Mvuania V

articles.headline: Corrigendum to published article on analysis of relationship between malaria and inflammatory biomarker levels in children in rural Iraq, including impact of nutritional deficiencies, anemia, and hematological disorders
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "Analysis of the Relationship Between Malaria and Inflammatory Biomarker Levels in Children in Rural Iraq: Impact of Nutritional Deficiencies, Anemia, and Hematological Disorders" published in Trop Parasitol. 2025 Jul-Dec;15(2):110-117. doi: 10.4103/tp.tp_26_25. Epub 2025 Oct 25. PMID: 41244076; PMCID: PMC12617583.

articles.headline: Discussion of malaria vaccine integration into Southeast Asian elimination strategies; abstract not provided
articles.title_original: Integrating malaria vaccines into Southeast Asian elimination strategies: A cautious path forward.

articles.headline: Study of 12,608 pregnancies in 7 African countries estimated P. falciparum exposed 13.1 million pregnancies in 2023, which would cause 2.41 million (95% CI 1.98–3.04 M) moderate/severe anaemia cases (Hb <9 g/dl) and 600,000 (408,000–906,000) severe cases (Hb <7 g/dl) without prevention. Current IPTp with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine averted approximately 1.10 million (0.72–1.61 M) cases, though malaria remains a major maternal anaemia driver with interruption to IPTp risking resurgence
articles.title_original: The burden of malaria-attributable maternal anaemia and the impact of preventive treatment across sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 618 pregnant women with malaria, pre-eclampsia, or both showed routine pre-eclampsia diagnosis achieved 50.2% concordance with reference criteria (κ=0.233, p<0.001), with 61.8% sensitivity and 62.2% specificity. Malaria parasite density was not independently associated with blood pressure or diagnostic discordance but was associated with proteinuria (β=0.17, p<0.001), indicating considerable misclassification particularly with concurrent malaria
articles.title_original: Diagnostic Concordance and Misclassification of Pre-Eclampsia in a Malaria-Endemic Setting: A Comparative Cross-Sectional Study.

articles.headline: Facility-based cross-sectional of 308 pregnant women in North Gondar Zone found 13.6% malaria prevalence, with risk factors including age ≥40 (AOR=4.8, 95% CI 1.4–16.0), rural residence (AOR=2.8, 95% CI 1.2–6.5), and no/inconsistent ITN use (AOR=7.2, 95% CI 2.9–18.0). Among malaria-infected women, 40% were anemic; malaria significantly increased anemia risk, highlighting need for integrated malaria-anemia screening during antenatal care
articles.title_original: Prevalence, Associated Risk Factors, and the Impact of Malaria on Anemia in Pregnant Women Attending ANC in North Gondar Zone, Northern Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Alula GA, Endale T, Yibeltie G

articles.headline: Comparative analysis of DBLMSP sequences from the China-Myanmar border identified nine genotypes each for DBLMSP1 and DBLMSP2, with variable modules showing high nucleotide diversity and Tajima's D while conserved structural elements (receptor-binding cleft, SPAM domain) showed purifying selection. Modular recombination patterns suggest a framework for understanding structural diversity and evolutionary dynamics with implications for immunogen design and parasite surveillance
articles.title_original: Module-level recombination drives DBLMSP polymorphism and functional conservation in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Review examining Plasmodium-Anopheles interactions focuses on mosquito innate immunity (pathogen recognition receptors, antimicrobial peptides, melanization) and genetic control strategies including CRISPR-Cas9, Wolbachia, RNAi, and RIDL. Discusses Plasmodium evasion mechanisms and potential of genetic approaches to reduce vector population and transmission, though noting advantages and limitations requiring further research into mosquito immune genes
articles.title_original: Advancing Malaria Vector Control: Insights Into Mosquito Immunity and Genetic Strategies.

articles.headline: Nationwide surveillance in Cameroon over 2024 using P.f/Pan RDTs tested 3,843,507 individuals (43% positive): P. falciparum monoinfection 82%, mixed infections 15%, Pan 3%. Regional variation showed Far North at 92.1% and East at 80.6% P. falciparum monoinfection; P.f/Pan RDTs cannot reliably differentiate non-falciparum species, highlighting need for molecular techniques and genomic surveillance for species-specific identification
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium Species Using Rapid Diagnostic Tests in Cameroon: First-Year Data From Routine Malaria Surveillance.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 49 pregnant women in Yaounde, Cameroon showed placental malaria associated with increased cTfh cell frequencies; PM-negative women had higher ICOS- cTfh while PM-positive women had elevated ICOS+ cTfh correlating with parasitemia. cTfh frequencies in placental blood positively correlated with birth weight while negatively associated with maternal hemoglobin; BTLA and LAG-3 expression in placental blood negatively correlated with birth weight
articles.title_original: cTfh Cell Activation and Changes in BTLA and LAG-3 Expression Differentially Associate With Poor Pregnancy Outcome in Plasmodium falciparum Placental Malaria.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 184 participants in Sisaket Province, Thailand across three seasons measured anti-gSG6-P1 IgG as biomarker for Anopheles exposure. Seasonality was strongest influence on anti-gSG6-P1 IgG levels; rubber tapping frequency 5–7 days/week increased anti-gSG6-P1 IgG (1.08±0.36) compared to 0–4 days/week (0.96±0.35). Findings highlight need for species-tailored serological tools for Greater Mekong Subregion to improve malaria risk assessment
articles.title_original: Immunological surveillance using anti-gSG6-P1 IgG biomarker reveals spatio-temporal dynamics of Anopheles exposure and gaps in malaria risk assessment in the Greater Mekong Subregion.

articles.headline: Mixed-methods study (2019–2023) evaluating IRS Etofenprox 20% PM custody chain in Brazilian Amazon found insecticide volume significantly associated with malaria cases nationally (β=0.161, p=0.038, R²=0.74) and deforestation (β=0.626, p=0.034), but analyses revealed reactive allocation lagging behind incidence peaks with weak local planning. Custody chain lacks risk-based integration with surveillance and environmental data; structural reforms needed including interoperable systems and vector resistance monitoring
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the insecticide custody chain and its relationship with malaria burden in the Brazilian Amazon: a process and exploratory impact assessment (2019-2023).

articles.headline: Computational analysis of Plasmodium proteomes found P. falciparum and P. vivax exceptionally rich in tandem repeats (TRs), with P. falciparum showing longer TRs predominantly in exported proteins important for immune evasion. TR-containing variable surface antigens (VSAs) were all sero-reactive with antigenic peptides within repeats; three-dimensional predictions showed repeats form ordered super-secondary conformations facilitating intermolecular interactions, suggesting TRs play critical roles in parasite survival and immune evasion
articles.title_original: Structural and functional insights from detailed computational analysis of Plasmodium repetome.

articles.headline: Quality improvement quasi-experimental study in four primary health centers identified inadequate IHIP data entry knowledge and poor surveillance feedback as root causes of low malaria reporting sensitivity (baseline 44%). Implementing refresher training and weekly district feedback achieved 100% sensitivity within 5 weeks, demonstrating feasibility of QI methods for addressing setting-specific barriers in malaria surveillance
articles.title_original: Quality Improvement Initiative to Improve the Sensitivity of Malaria Reporting under Integrated Health Information Platform, Raigarh, Chhattisgarh, 2023.

articles.headline: Analysis of National Malaria Program surveillance data in Kanker district (2018–2022) found Annual Parasite Index decreased from 3.0 to 1.3 and Annual Falciparum Index from 2.5 to 1.1 (2019–2022), with P. falciparum comprising >90% of cases 2020–2021. Test Positivity Rate and Test Falciparum Rate declined by half 2018–2022; however, 2/7 blocks retained API >2 and 1/7 blocks AFI >2 in 2022, indicating persistent high transmission in focal areas
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of Malaria Cases Reported from Government Health Facilities in District Kanker, Chhattisgarh, January 2018 - December 2022.

articles.headline: Koraput district's annual parasite incidence (API) rose sharply from 2.7 per 1,000 (2019-2022) to 4.5 per 1,000 in 2023, with marked regional variation (Narayanpatna 38 per 1,000, Bandhugaon 25 per 1,000) and consistently higher rates in males. This surveillance analysis (blood examination rate 28%) identifies seasonal peaks offering opportunities for pre-emptive malaria control measures
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of Malaria in Koraput District, Odisha, 2019-2023: A Descriptive Analysis of National Vector Borne Disease Control Programme Surveillance Data.

articles.headline: Malaria surveillance in six Chhattisgarh districts showed inadequate sensitivity, capturing only 5.8% (84/1,442) of presumptive cases and 29% (3,334/11,510) of laboratory-identified cases; among reported cases, 47.6% of presumptive forms and 28% of laboratory forms lacked valid entries in key variables. This evaluation of 1,276 surveillance units identifies major gaps in case capture and data quality across the state's system
articles.title_original: Evaluation of Malaria Surveillance System in Government Health Facilities in Six Districts of State Chhattisgarh, India, 2023.

articles.headline: A 26-day-old Pakistani infant presented with the first documented case of congenital Plasmodium knowlesi malaria in Bahrain, co-infected with cytomegalovirus (high blood and urine viral loads). Parasites were confirmed on blood smear despite negative rapid antigen testing; the infant received intravenous artesunate and ganciclovir. The case highlights that blood smear microscopy remains essential for diagnosis even when rapid tests are negative, particularly in non-endemic regions
articles.title_original: Co-infection of Neonatal Plasmodium knowlesi Malaria With Congenital Cytomegalovirus: The First Reported Case From Bahrain.

articles.headline: Commentary examining whether neutrophil extracellular trap formation (NETosis) in severe malaria may represent a new therapeutic target
articles.title_original: [Malaria: Could NETosis, neutrophils' defense mechanism involved in severe cases of the disease, be a new therapeutic target?].
articles.authors: Kociánová L, Larribau S, Veltz N

articles.headline: Sequencing of pvmsp-1 ICB 5-6 from 117 Vietnamese Plasmodium vivax isolates (4 provinces, 2018-2022) identified 34 distinct amino acid haplotypes with recombinant types predominant; high genetic polymorphism occurred in Central Highland regions (Dak Lak, Dak Nong, Gia Lai) while Khanh Hoa showed lower polymorphism. Findings underscore substantial genetic heterogeneity of Vietnamese P. vivax populations requiring continuous molecular surveillance
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphisms of merozoite surface protein-1 ICB 5-6 in Vietnamese Plasmodium vivax isolates.

articles.headline: A traveler with P. falciparum malaria (26% parasitemia, ~1.5 × 10⁶ parasites/μL) experienced treatment failure after intravenous artesunate and artemether-lumefantrine, with recurrent parasitemia 3 weeks post-treatment. Genotyping revealed A675V pfk13 mutation, reduced lumefantrine susceptibility, and increased ring-stage survival consistent with partial artemisinin resistance. Case demonstrates high risk of treatment failure in hyperparasitemic patients with drug-resistant parasites
articles.title_original: Treatment Failure and Post-Artesunate Delayed Haemolysis in a Returned Traveller From Uganda With Partially Drug-Resistant Severe Plasmodium falciparum Malaria.

articles.headline: Empirical testing of gene drive efficacy against naturally diverse Plasmodium populations encountered in real-world field settings
articles.title_original: Gene drives tested against real-world malaria diversity.

articles.headline: Analysis of 2,702 probable malaria deaths in 19th-century Ontario (1831-1900) found malaria persisted despite declining trends, with children under 5 representing over half of deaths and agricultural workers disproportionately affected in rural western Ontario. Generalized additive modeling identified a strong nonlinear association between rainfall and malaria mortality (p<0.001), while temperature was not significant. Findings demonstrate how settler-driven landscape transformation created conditions sustaining malaria in a temperate region
articles.title_original: Ecologies of Risk: Malaria and Settler Landscape Transformation in 19th-Century Ontario.

articles.headline: Editorial proposing CXCL10high TNFαhigh Ki67+ microglia identified in experimental cerebral malaria as a candidate 'neuroimmune endotype' requiring translational validation in human disease through single-nucleus transcriptomics, CSF/plasma biomarker correlation, and mechanistic assays before precision-targeted interventions are proposed
articles.title_original: CXCL10(high) Microglia in Cerebral Malaria: Toward Translational Validation.

articles.headline: Sentiment analysis of social media discourse (X and Facebook) regarding RTS,S (Mosquirix) vaccine rollout in Cameroon revealed nearly balanced public opinion: 42% negative, 40% positive, and 18% neutral reactions. Findings underscore the necessity for targeted communication addressing public concerns about vaccine safety and efficacy to build confidence in immunization programs
articles.title_original: Assessing RTS, S malaria vaccine rollout perception in Cameroon: Sentiment analysis from X and facebook using hugging face.

articles.headline: Phase 2b Kenya trial comparing R21/Matrix-M (n=38), ME-TRAP (n=24), and control (n=18) vaccines found R21 highly protective against intradermal sporozoite challenge (0/12 infected vs. 7/8 controls; p<0.0005) but failed against direct venous injection (5/5 vaccinated infected; p<0.0005). Secondary efficacy endpoints showed protection only against intradermal challenge. Route of parasite inoculation critically determines vaccine efficacy and warrants separate assessment
articles.title_original: Malaria vaccine protection against intradermal or venous parasites: a randomized phase 2b human challenge trial.

articles.headline: Retrospective review of 64 Spanish malaria patients (2015-2023) found 23.4% (15/64) had severe malaria; treatment was appropriate by WHO criteria in 71.8% (46/64). Three severe cases were undertreated with oral therapy due to misclassification; fifteen non-severe cases received parenteral artesunate, primarily due to thrombocytopenia (median 33,000/mm³), which is not a WHO criterion for severe malaria. Findings highlight the need to distinguish WHO-defined severity from laboratory abnormalities
articles.title_original: Appropriateness of malaria treatment according to World Health Organization criteria for severe malaria in a non-endemic country.

articles.headline: Forward genetic screen using piggyBac mutagenesis to identify P. falciparum genes essential for gametocyte development and sexual stage differentiation, categorizing genes as hypo- or hyper-producers of transmissible parasite forms
articles.title_original: Large-Scale Mutagenesis Screening for Genetic Determinants of Plasmodium falciparum Sexual Development.

articles.headline: Phase I Pfs230D1-EPA/AS01 vaccine trial (171 serum samples) showed P230Compete ELISA predicts transmission-reducing activity: EUF and IgG1F demonstrated strong correlation and excellent TRA prediction (AUC 0.81–0.80) after vaccine doses 3–4; combined assessment improved predictive performance, offering a scalable proxy to replace standard membrane feeding assay
articles.title_original: Epitope-specific competitive ELISA predicts malaria transmission-blocking vaccine Pfs230D1 activity measured in standard membrane feeding assay.

articles.headline: Review of P. yoelii lethal (17XL) and non-lethal (17XNL) strains: 17XL triggers excessive pro-inflammatory cytokines and immune exhaustion; 17XNL sustains regulated Th1/Th2 responses enabling parasite control. Malaria severity reflects immune response timing and resolution, involving MIF, TLR7 signaling, and PD-1/LAG-3 checkpoints
articles.title_original: Immune Modulation by Plasmodium yoelii: Insights From Lethal and Non-Lethal Strains.

articles.headline: Review of obstacles to malaria vaccine availability and implementation in Africa: major barriers include insufficient manufacturing capacity, limited national health budgets, vaccine misinformation and community distrust, cold-chain requirements, multiple-dose schedules, shortages of skilled health workers, and weak pharmacovigilance systems in resource-constrained regions
articles.title_original: Challenges to malaria vaccine availability and successful implementation of malaria vaccine program in Africa.
articles.authors: Ojo RJ, Igbayilola YD, Gyebi GA

articles.headline: Prospective cohort of 2,840 military recruits (median age 27, 91.5% male) in Niger showed malaria prevalence 11% (353 confirmed cases), with 74.5% anemia, 44% hospitalization, 55.8% unfit for duty. Estimated ~2,000 annual workdays lost by young adults, or ~12 million nationally, highlighting need for integrated control strategy
articles.title_original: Burden of malaria in young adults in Niger: A prospective study in a military setting.

articles.headline: Systematic review: malaria infection consistently associated with reduced serum iron, zinc, magnesium; variable copper (often elevated acutely). Alterations linked to dysregulated TNF-α/IL-10, higher parasitemia, worse outcomes. Zinc supplementation improved status but showed limited impact on incidence. Targeted micronutrient interventions may enhance immune competence
articles.title_original: Essential Trace Elements and Their Impact on Immune Response and Disease Severity in Malaria Infection: A Systematic Review.
articles.authors: Hanifian H, Nateghpour M, Naderi M

articles.headline: Two severe P. falciparum cases imported to Italy showed recrudescence after intravenous artesunate and dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine. One carried R561H artemisinin partial-resistance marker; second lacked Pfkelch13 mutations but had possible nasogastric malabsorption. First documented artemisinin-resistant P. falciparum in Italy, underscoring need for continued surveillance
articles.title_original: Recrudescence of Plasmodium falciparum malaria imported from Sub-Saharan Africa: a report of two cases and the first detection of artemisinin resistance in Italy.

articles.headline: Editorial on malaria elimination capacity and implementation challenges
articles.title_original: We have the means to beat malaria, do we have the will?

articles.headline: Dominican Republic study of 1,095 suspected malaria cases negative by HRP2-RDT and microscopy: nucleic acid extraction from RDT cassettes successful in 88.5% (969/1095); P. falciparum DNA detected in 1.9% (18/969); 16.7% (3/18) carried pfhrp2 deletions undermining RDT performance, highlighting need for strengthened diagnostics in elimination settings
articles.title_original: Molecular Detection of Plasmodium falciparum Infections Missed by Microscopy and HRP2-Based RDTs in the Dominican Republic.

articles.headline: Engineered P. falciparum PLP synthase nanoparticle vaccine presenting CSP and P. vivax CelTOS elicited high anti-CSP/anti-CelTOS antibodies; three doses provided complete sterile protection in mice. Cryo-EM resolved 2.95-Å structure. PLP synthase lacks human ortholog, reducing autoimmunity/pre-existing immunity risk and offering favorable manufacturing characteristics
articles.title_original: A Plasmodium-derived nanoparticle vaccine elicits sterile protection against malaria in mice.

articles.headline: Open-label RCT of one-step vs two-step injectable artesunate in 200 children (3 months–16 years) with severe malaria in DRC and Tanzania: one-step administration faster (2 min 22 s vs 3 min 41 s, p<0.0001) and cheaper ($0.53 vs $0.84, p=0.0001); parasite clearance (2.1 vs 2.0 h, p=0.173) and adverse events equivalent
articles.title_original: Single-step versus conventional injectable artesunate for severe malaria in children: an open label, non-inferiority randomized clinical trial, Democratic Republic of the Congo and United Republic of Tanzania.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 13 studies (2,190 participants) in Ethiopia: chloroquine-resistant P. vivax prevalence 7.32% (95% CI 2.55–12.08), higher during 2003–2009 (10.70%), Oromia region (9.58%), and 42-day follow-up (17.91%). Resistance moderate but clinically significant; surveillance and alternative therapeutic approaches warranted
articles.title_original: Chloroquine-resistant Plasmodium vivax malaria in Ethiopia: A systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Scoping review examining Plasmodium vivax circumsporozoite protein-based vaccine strategies evaluated in mouse models
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax circumsporozoite protein and vaccine strategies in murine models: A scoping review.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 7 Phase I/II trials (n=776) of antimalarial monoclonal antibodies against P. falciparum. CIS43LS: 75–88% protective efficacy (≥10 mg/kg IV, 56–80 day half-life); L9LS: 66–77% efficacy (subcutaneous, 46.1 day half-life); TB31F: >80% transmission-reducing activity (≥1 mg/kg, sustained 160 days). All showed grade 1–2 adverse events, supporting potential for seasonal deployment
articles.title_original: Efficacy and safety of Monoclonal Antibodies in Malaria Protection and Prevention of Transmission: A Systematic Review.

articles.headline: P. falciparum falcilysin, a zinc metalloprotease essential for parasite survival, reviewed as emerging drug target. Recent structural studies reveal druggable hydrophobic allosteric pocket; multiple small-molecule classes show potent inhibition. Key challenges: selectivity over human homologs and dual-organelle localization; future strategies include PROTAC modalities and AI-driven design
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum falcilysin as an emerging potential drug target for antimalarial drug discovery.

articles.headline: Transgenic Anopheles gambiae expressing antimicrobial peptides with non-autonomous gene drive capability robustly suppressed genetically diverse P. falciparum isolates from naturally infected Tanzanian children. Genetic modifications efficiently inherited by progeny with supplied Cas9 endonuclease, advancing gene drive technology toward field application for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Gene-drive-capable mosquitoes suppress patient-derived malaria in Tanzania.

articles.headline: Population genetic analysis of 223 DARC genes from sub-Saharan Africa reveals minimal genetic diversity overall with evidence of selection pressures in Western Africa. P. vivax detection among Duffy-null individuals in previously P. falciparum-endemic regions represents a public health concern threatening malaria eradication programs
articles.title_original: Genetic dynamics of the Duffy antigen receptor for chemokines gene and Plasmodium vivax circulation within sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: In vitro binding study of Plasmodium cynomolgi Duffy-binding protein haplotypes from Peninsular Malaysia. Recent PcDBP1(II) isolate showed significantly higher binding to macaque erythrocytes versus old isolate (P<0.05); both DBPs showed no binding with human erythrocytes, suggesting additional parasite-host interactions required for human invasion
articles.title_original: Characterisation of erythrocyte binding activity of Plasmodium cynomolgi duffy binding protein haplotypes from Macaca fascicularis in Peninsular Malaysia.

articles.headline: Diagnostic comparison in 234 participants with suspected uncomplicated malaria in Keerom Regency, Papua. PCR detected 42.3% cases versus microscopy (26.5%) and rapid diagnostic tests (22.6%), revealing substantial case-detection limitations in low-parasitemia settings and highlighting need for enhanced diagnostic strategies
articles.title_original: Performance comparison of rapid diagnostic test, microscopy, and polymerase chain reaction for Malaria detection in high-transmission area, Keerom Regency, Papua Province, Indonesia.

articles.headline: Long-term study of wild bird population in temperate Northern Europe shows significant increase in avian malaria parasite prevalence under climate warming, indicating climate disruption poses threats to global ecosystem infectious disease transmission
articles.title_original: Hidden effects of avian malaria under climate warming.

articles.headline: Retrospective analysis of 67,114 Brazilian Amazon blood donors tested with NAT PLUS Malaria assay. Plasmodium spp. positivity: 0.01% (8/67,114), all asymptomatic at screening; one donor developed symptomatic P. falciparum post-donation. Malaria-related deferrals accounted for 2.6% of all deferrals, highlighting need for standardized molecular screening
articles.title_original: Improving blood safety: NAT-based detection of Plasmodium spp. in blood donors in endemic areas of Brazil.
articles.authors: Gomes de Almeida AC, et al.

articles.headline: Synthesis of hypofuran B and analogs (yields 20–83%) with IC50 values of 5.35–10.35 µg/mL against P. falciparum. Most promising compound demonstrated CC50 >400 µg/mL (lower cytotoxicity than chloroquine) with favorable in silico drug profiles, establishing hypofuran B derivatives as promising antimalarial leads
articles.title_original: Synthesis and In Vitro Activity of Hypofuran B and Analogs Against Plasmodium Falciparum and Trypanosoma Cruzi.

articles.headline: Retrospective review of malaria screening in 57 hematopoietic cell transplant donors and 52 sickle cell disease recipients. Three asymptomatic donors identified; one initially missed by blood smear transmitted malaria to recipient. Malaria PCR showed superior diagnostic sensitivity compared to conventional methods; all treated cases achieved negative PCR and successful transplant outcomes
articles.title_original: Malaria Screening and Treatment in Hematopoietic Cell Transplant Donors and Sickle Cell Disease Candidates/Recipients: A Case Series Using Malaria Polymerase Chain Reaction Testing and a Literature Review.

articles.headline: Case report of 30-year-old with high-grade fever and progressive splenomegaly, initially negative on multiple peripheral blood smears and rapid diagnostic tests but positive on bone marrow microscopy and PCR for Plasmodium spp. Successfully treated with Artemether-Lumefantrine and Primaquine, demonstrating diagnostic value of bone marrow examination for occult malaria
articles.title_original: Out of Sight, Should not be out of mind: A perplexing case of Malaria.

articles.headline: Intermittent preventive treatment with sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine (IPTp-SP), originally designed to protect pregnant women from malaria, appears to reduce low birthweight even when Plasmodium infection is absent or parasites are sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine-resistant. The protective effect may operate through antimicrobial activity against maternal genitourinary infections and gut bacteria, direct effects on maternal physiology, or anti-inflammatory mechanisms. Mechanistic studies and placebo-controlled trials in non-malarious settings are needed to confirm whether IPTp-SP could reduce small vulnerable newborns in high-prevalence areas
articles.title_original: Beyond malaria: can intermittent preventive treatment with sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine reduce the number of small vulnerable newborns globally?

articles.headline: Corrigendum notice; no substantive abstract available for summary
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "Naturally acquired immune responses to alpha-gal in malaria endemic settings and pre-clinical efficacy testing with R21/MM" [Vaccine 68 (2025) 127897].

articles.headline: Mosquito blood-meal and parasite survey from Eastern Balkans deciduous forest and reedbeds (April–October, 2017 and 2019) identified blood sources in 75 engorged individuals: 58.7% avian (25 bird species), 40% mammalian (13 species), 1.3% reptilian. Culex pipiens showed highest bird-feeding frequency; Culex modestus fed on greatest host variety; Aedes vexans, Anopheles maculipennis, and A. hyrcanus were predominantly mammophilic. Avian haemosporidians detected in 11.6% abdominal and 4.2% head-thorax samples, with Plasmodium relictum (SGS1 lineage, n=4) most prevalent
articles.title_original: Feeding Preferences of Mosquitoes (Culicidae) from the Eastern Balkans and Their Role in Transmission of Avian Malaria.

articles.headline: Review of Amazon malaria ecology examining deforestation as the primary environmental driver of persistent Plasmodium transmission. Amazon forest degradation influences anopheline vector abundance and human-vector contact rates, mediating malaria risk. Regional zoonotic malaria is increasingly important; sustainable elimination requires understanding mosquito adaptation to anthropogenic pressures on the rainforest
articles.title_original: Deforestation, Mosquito Ecology, and Malaria Elimination in the Amazon Region.
articles.authors: Grillet ME, Sallum MAM, Conn JE

articles.headline: Phase 4 real-world effectiveness study of RTS,S/AS01E in 39,463 children <18 months (Ghana, Kenya, Malawi) followed 1 year post-vaccination. Compared to unvaccinated children: any malaria IRR 0.70 (95% CI 0.67–0.73; p<0.001), severe malaria IRR 0.42 (0.30–0.60; p<0.001), malaria-related hospitalizations IRR 0.64 (0.56–0.72; p<0.001), all-cause hospitalizations IRR 0.79 (0.74–0.84; p<0.001), and anaemia among hospitalized children OR 0.81 (0.73–0.90; p<0.001). Vaccination significantly reduced malaria burden and hospitalization
articles.title_original: Effectiveness of the RTS,S/AS01(E) malaria vaccine in a real-world setting over 1 year of follow-up after the three-dose primary schedule: an interim analysis of a phase 4 study in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi.

articles.headline: Review advocating malaria serology integration into India's elimination strategy. India accounts for >50% of Southeast Asia's malaria burden (P. falciparum and P. vivax); cases increased from 176,522 (2022) to 255,500 (2024), ~58% increase since 2021. Serology can detect past exposure, low-density infections, and assess intervention impact; several countries including Indonesia, Malaysia, Myanmar, Thailand, and Suriname have integrated serology successfully
articles.title_original: India should consider sero-epidemiology as an additional tool to bolster malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Parinari curatellifolia leaf and bark mixture extract showed in vitro antiplasmodial activity against P. falciparum 3D7 and Dd2 strains. In Plasmodium berghei ANKA-infected rats, extract reduced parasitemia (p<0.001), improved hematological parameters, increased bone osteocalcin, TGF-β1, and alkaline phosphatase activity (p<0.001), and reversed malaria-induced inflammation. Identified compounds exhibited strong inhibitory binding in silico, supporting extract efficacy as antiplasmodial and osteoprotective agent
articles.title_original: Osteoprotective Effects and In Silico Profiling of Parinari curatellifolia Planch. Ex Benth. (Chrysobalanaceae) Extract on Acute Plasmodium berghei-Compromised Rat Bone.

articles.headline: Hospital-based longitudinal study (July 2022–November 2023) screening 4,259 Delhi febrile patients for malaria: 87 cases (2.04%) infected with P. vivax and P. falciparum. Clinical presentation dominated by chills (80.46%), hepatosplenomegaly (64.37%), myalgia (56.32%), and jaundice (51.72%); ~45% co-infected with dengue, chikungunya, or Salmonella typhi. Risk factors: July–September period (aOR 2.18; p<0.05) and male sex (aOR 1.42; p<0.05); severe anaemia was most frequent severe form
articles.title_original: Malaria epidemiology, clinical characteristics, and concurrent infections in Delhi, India: A hospital-based study.

articles.headline: Molecular surveillance of 200 P. falciparum isolates from Kumasi, Ghana (2023) revealed: 2.2% carried non-synonymous pfk13 mutations including candidate artemisinin partial-resistance marker P527H (plus S522C, C532S, I590V); pfcoronin P76S in 29.7% associated with lower parasite density; pfmdr1 pattern NFD predominant suggesting impaired lumefantrine sensitivity; pfaat1 S258L in 64.7%. Despite isolated critical pfk13 mutations and considerable pfcoronin prevalence, artemisinin-based combination therapy efficacy remains high, warranting continuous monitoring
articles.title_original: Molecular Markers of Antimalarial Drug Resistance in Plasmodium falciparum From Kumasi, Ghana, 2023.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 8 preclinical studies (2001–2022, predominantly North America) evaluating cannabis-derived compounds against Plasmodium sp. In vitro IC50 values ranged 0.16–4.1 μg/mL, indicating mild to high activity. All in vivo studies reported positive effects including moderate antimalarial activity and disease tolerance. Toxicity profiles remain incompletely characterized; most studies have unknown or unclear risk of bias due to insufficient methodological reporting
articles.title_original: Cannabis-Derived Compounds Against Plasmodium sp.: A Systematic Review of Preclinical Studies.
articles.authors: de Mendonça Lima T, et al.

articles.headline: 4-Nitrobenzoate (4-NB) inhibits P. falciparum 4-HB polyprenyltransferase (Pf4-HPT), enhancing atovaquone efficacy and selectivity while preserving proguanil synergy. In Saccharomyces cerevisiae expressing PfCOQ2, 4-NB suppressed ubiquinone biosynthesis. In Plasmodium berghei-infected mice, 4-NB improved atovaquone efficacy in vivo. Structure-activity analysis defined chemical features for potentiation, establishing PfCOQ2 as a druggable target for rational antimalarial combination therapy
articles.title_original: 4-nitrobenzoate inhibits 4-hydroxybenzoate polyprenyltransferase in malaria parasites and enhances atovaquone efficacy.

articles.headline: CRISPR-Cas9 disruption of P. falciparum gametogenesis essential protein 1 (GEP1) completely blocked male and female gametogenesis despite stimulation with xanthurenic acid or temperature drop, and persisted despite phosphodiesterase inhibitor treatment. Field samples (49 cases) carried GEP1 SNPs V241L (12%) and S263P (20%). PfGEP1 loss blocks life cycle progression and supports GEP1 as a transmission-blocking drug target for malaria control
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum gametogenesis essential protein 1 (GEP1) is a transmission-blocking target.

articles.headline: New Plasmodium (Novyella) species identified in white-shouldered fire-eyes from Brazil, genetically divergent 4.18% from P. homopolare and distinguished by morphological differences in amoeboid trophozoite placement and merozoite counts in mature meronts
articles.title_original: A New Species of Plasmodium of the Subgenus Novyella Infecting White-Shouldered Fire-Eyes (Pyriglena leucoptera) (Aves: Thamnophilidae) in Brazil.

articles.headline: Volume electron microscopy reveals subcellular ultrastructure during Plasmodium ookinete development, including mitochondrion-nucleus interactions, microtubule arrangements, and apicoplast interactions; provides an atlas for mechanistic molecular studies
articles.title_original: Cellular Hallmarks From Volume Electron Microscopy Reveal Developmental Progression of Plasmodium Ookinetes.

articles.headline: Phase 2 trial of radiation-attenuated PfSPZ Vaccine in 192 Gabonese children (1-12 years) showed poor efficacy against P. falciparum: 19% of vaccine recipients vs 23% placebo developed parasitemia by weeks 2-26 post-vaccination, with adjusted vaccine efficacy of 9% (95% CI −75 to 53; p=0.78); vaccine was safe but failed to prevent infection
articles.title_original: Safety, tolerability, and protective efficacy of a radiation-attenuated, whole sporozoite malaria vaccine in children in Gabon: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 2 trial.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional analysis of malaria in Meta, Colombia (2016-2021) found significant age-related differences in malaria acquisition, with highest case concentrations in Puerto Gaitán, Villavicencio, and Mapiripán near rivers where vector contact is enhanced
articles.title_original: Malaria in the department of Meta, eastern Colombia: 2017-2021.

articles.headline: Perspective on South-South collaborations (e.g., ANDI, Elimination 8, India-Africa Health Sciences Platform) as catalysts for malaria research and control through partnerships among low- and middle-income countries; identifies funding constraints and fragmented systems as barriers and proposes regional investment and regulatory harmonization as solutions
articles.title_original: Can India-Africa synergies accelerate malaria elimination? A perspective on South-South Collaboration.

articles.headline: Phase 4 trial of four ACTs in 793 Ugandan children (6 months–10 years) with uncomplicated P. falciparum found artemether-lumefantrine had substantially lower uncorrected ACPR (51.8–79.4% across sites) versus dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (98.0%), artesunate-amodiaquine (99.0%), and artesunate-pyronaridine (73.7%); PfK13 mutations prolonged parasite clearance half-lives (Cys469Tyr: 4.2h; Ala675Val: 4.9h)
articles.title_original: Efficacies of artemether-lumefantrine, artesunate-amodiaquine, dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine, and artesunate-pyronaridine for the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in children aged 6 months to 10 years in Uganda: a randomised, open-label, phase 4 clinical trial.

articles.headline: Commentary on strategies for diversifying antimalarial treatment options for uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria in Uganda
articles.title_original: Diversifying antimalarial treatment for uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Uganda.

articles.headline: Review of nuclear medicine imaging techniques for malaria that have added value in understanding severe malaria pathogenesis and complications; identifies stagnation in development of malaria-specific radiopharmaceuticals and proposes strategies to overcome implementation barriers
articles.title_original: Mini-review and situation report on the role and usefulness of nuclear medicine imaging for malaria.
articles.journal: Eur J Nucl Med Mol Imaging

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey in Lokoja, Nigeria (2018-2021) found P. falciparum prevalence of 40.5% (133/328) with hyperparasitemia; 12.4% asymptomatic and 85.7% symptomatic individuals were parasitemic with no significant difference in mean parasite density (p>0.05), highlighting asymptomatic reservoirs as critical elimination target
articles.title_original: A cross-sectional survey of malaria in asymptomatic and symptomatic individuals in an endemic area of Northcentral Nigeria.
articles.authors: Akoma ON, Okoh FN, Amoke OC

articles.headline: Global Burden of Disease modeling (1990-2030) shows Western Sub-Saharan Africa carries half of global malaria and NTD burden, with upward ASIR trends in high-income regions 1990-2019 and predicted slow mortality rate increases 2020-2030; children <5 years bear the most serious burden, dengue the highest NTD burden
articles.title_original: The past, present and future of global malaria and neglected tropical diseases: a disease burden assessment from 1990 to 2030.

articles.headline: Ten-year molecular surveillance in Chad (2014-2023) during SMC shows triple dhfr mutants decreased (100% to 83.9%), but quadruple mutants (triple dhfr + dhps A437G) increased significantly (28.0% to 47.9%, p<0.001); SP and AQ remain effective for SMC, but quadruple mutant rise linked to partial SP resistance requires continued monitoring
articles.title_original: Trends in Plasmodium falciparum resistance markers to sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine and amodiaquine over ten years of seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Moissala Health District, Chad.

articles.headline: Study of pregnant Malawian women with P. falciparum shows VAR2CSA-specific IgG at delivery associated with reduced low birth weight (aOR 0.43, 95% CI 0.19–0.97, p=0.04) and IgM at enrollment associated with 23% reduction in maternal anemia (aOR 0.77, p=0.04); VAR2CSA antibodies are protective markers against placental malaria complications
articles.title_original: VAR2CSA-specific IgG and IgM antibodies are markers of exposure and protection against adverse malaria pregnancy outcomes.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 25 studies in Nigeria: asymptomatic malaria pooled prevalence 33% (95% CI 26–41), ranging from 25% in general population to 52% in children. Prevalence 19% related to education level, 36% by sex, 39% associated with inadequate ITN usage. High asymptomatic burden particularly in children requires complementary transmission-reduction strategies
articles.title_original: Prevalence and associated risk factors of asymptomatic malaria in Nigeria: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: OpIE2-DsRed fluorescent marker in transgenic Anopheles gambiae caused female sterility by disrupting blood-feeding and shortened male lifespan due to distally curved proboscis. OpIE2b promoter variant drove strong expression without phenotypic effects, providing a neutral alternative marker for gene drive and research applications
articles.title_original: An OpIE2-DsRed marker disrupts female blood-feeding and shortens lifespan in the malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.

articles.headline: Qualitative study (14 key informant interviews, 10 FGDs, 11 household interviews) in Uganda identified livelihoods—crop production, livestock husbandry, construction, dusk/night activities—as key social determinants of malaria through increased mosquito exposure and breeding site creation. Recommends integrating malaria prevention into socio-economic development strategies
articles.title_original: Livelihoods as a key social determinant of malaria: Qualitative evidence from Uganda.
articles.authors: Deane K, Atusingwize E, Musoke D

articles.headline: Mathematical model of malaria–COVID-19 co-infection dynamics analyzing backward bifurcation, competitive exclusion, and co-existence conditions. Local sensitivity analysis and Pontryagin's maximum principle applied to determine optimal disease control strategies; numerical simulations support theoretical findings
articles.title_original: Optimal control analysis of a mathematical model of malaria and COVID-19 co-infection dynamics.
articles.authors: Diabaté AB, Sangaré B, Koutou O

articles.headline: Case–control study (50 malaria patients, 50 controls): malaria patients showed significantly higher AST, ALT, ALP levels (p=0.001); liver enzymes correlated significantly with parasitemia (p<0.001), with higher parasite loads linked to greater hepatic dysfunction. ABO blood group showed no association with liver enzyme levels (p>0.05); liver enzyme profiles may serve as severity biomarkers
articles.title_original: The Association between Parasitemia and Liver Enzyme Alterations in Malaria Patients: ABO Blood Group as A Non-Contributory Factor.

articles.headline: Baseline mixed-methods study (198 households, children 6 months–5 years) in Kenya: Plasmodium falciparum prevalence 21% by RDT and PCR (11–42%), with highest positivity of 73% by PCR in some villages. SMC perceived positively but challenges include physical access, stigma, and acceptability; tailored implementation with door-to-door delivery and pastoralist outreach recommended
articles.title_original: A Baseline Mixed-Methods Study to Inform Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention Delivery in a Semi-Nomadic Population in Turkana, Kenya.

articles.headline: Review of real-world effectiveness evaluation approaches for RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccines. Observational designs (cohort, test-negative) offer promising methods but require accurate individual-level vaccination data. Strengthening electronic immunization registries in African countries essential for robust vaccine monitoring and policy decisions
articles.title_original: Need for Real-World Evaluation of Malaria Vaccines and Reliable Vaccination Records in Africa.

articles.headline: Plant-produced MS-1797 monoclonal antibodies against Plasmodium falciparum circumsporozoite protein: MS-01 yielded 457.3 µg/g fresh weight, MS-02 yielded 415.7 µg/g, both at >90% purity with human or high-mannose glycans. Both variants retained antigen specificity in vitro and bound native PfCSP on sporozoites, demonstrating feasibility for malaria research, diagnostics, and prophylaxis
articles.title_original: Plant-produced monoclonal antibodies recognize the native circumsporozoite protein on Plasmodium falciparum sporozoites.

articles.headline: Retrospective analysis of 27,171 malaria tests (2020–2022) in Ghana: overall test positivity rate 8.8%, declining from 9.9% (2020) to 7.2% (2022). School-age children 5–12 years highest-risk group (18.0% TPR); P. falciparum dominant (79.9%); peak transmission August (13.9%). School-age and adolescent groups show higher parasite densities; targeted school-based chemoprevention urgently needed
articles.title_original: Malaria test positivity, Plasmodium species distribution, and risk factors in Ho Municipality, Ghana: a retrospective analysis of seasonal and demographic trends (2020-2022).

articles.headline: Prospective cohort of 2,757 pregnant women in Uganda: 38.1% parasitemia at enrollment, 6.5% after IPTp initiation, 6.4% high-grade placental malaria, 6.8% low birth weight. Younger age (RR=1.62–1.72) and primigravida status (RR=1.86–2.40) strongly associated with parasitemia; primigravida women also at highest risk for low birth weight (RR=2.14) and high-grade placental malaria (RR=4.20)
articles.title_original: Maternal and household risk factors for malaria in pregnancy and low birthweight: a prospective cohort study from Uganda.

articles.headline: Geostatistical analysis of malaria incidence in Ethiopia (2022–2023): significant spatial clustering confirmed (Moran's I 0.558 in 2022, 0.483 in 2023, p<0.01). Average maximum temperature (β=0.945, p=0.017) and proportion of highland terrain (β=0.543, p=0.040) identified as key environmental predictors. Spatial autoregressive model superior to OLS, supporting geographically targeted interventions
articles.title_original: Spatial analysis of malaria incidence and environmental determinants in Hadiya Zone, Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 614 community members in South Africa: 26.2% exposed to arts-based community engagement (music, drama, radio) for Sterile Insect Technique. Exposed participants more likely to correctly identify female blood-feeding (95.0% vs 85.8%, p=0.008) and support SIT releases (98.1% vs 89.4%, p=0.003). Arts-based approaches effective for improving knowledge and acceptance of novel vector control
articles.title_original: From song to stories: assessing the impact of exposure to arts-based community engagement tools in shaping knowledge, attitudes, and acceptability toward the Sterile Insect Technique for malaria control in South Africa.
articles.authors: Manana PN, Jewett S, Munhenga G

articles.headline: XN-31 automated hematology analyzer achieved 98.4% sensitivity for malaria detection in 349 Thai patients (103 P. vivax, 17 P. falciparum, 3 P. knowlesi, 1 P. ovale, 1 P. malariae), outperforming RDT (88.0%), with 100% species identification accuracy and strong quantitative correlation with microscopy (r=0.903). First study confirming XN-31 diagnostic accuracy in P. vivax-dominant endemic regions
articles.title_original: Performance evaluation of the automated haematology analyzer XN-31 for malaria diagnosis in Plasmodium vivax-dominant regions of Thailand.

articles.headline: P. vivax genomes in Sudanese Duffy-positive individuals exhibited significantly higher parasitemia and much greater genetic diversity than Duffy-negative infections across all 14 chromosomes and 44 erythrocyte-binding gene candidates, with many genes under positive and negative selection pressure. Genetic differences suggest alternative invasion mechanisms in Duffy-negative infections and parasite adaptability in Duffy-positive hosts
articles.title_original: Highly conserved Plasmodium vivax genomes in Duffy-negative individuals from Sudan.

articles.headline: Correction notice; no substantive research content
articles.title_original: Correction: Machine learning-based phenotypic imaging to characterise the targetable biology of Plasmodium falciparum male gametocytes for the development of transmission-blocking antimalarials.

articles.headline: Selection-linked integration (SLI) system successfully generated P. falciparum parasites expressing uniform PfEMP1 variants in IT4 strain, enabling functional studies of PfEMP1 trafficking, receptor binding to common endothelial receptors, and identification of new proteins required for sequestration. System demonstrates utility for studying malaria's major virulence factor and variant-specific disease mechanisms
articles.title_original: A system for functional studies of the major virulence factor of malaria parasites.

articles.headline: A 55-year-old woman with P. falciparum malaria from Cameroon presented with jaundice, transaminitis, and biopsy-confirmed cholestatic hepatitis; only 1 of 4 follow-up blood smears remained positive. After >17 months, liver enzymes and bilirubin normalized without additional antimalarial treatment, attributed to drug-induced hepatotoxicity
articles.title_original: Deciphering Hepatic Dilemmas: A Case of Acute Hepatitis Following Malaria Treatment.

articles.headline: Mini-dbPCR-NALFIA for P. falciparum detection in 438 febrile patients in high-transmission rural Burkina Faso (65.5% malaria prevalence by qPCR reference) demonstrated 96.5% sensitivity, 98.0% specificity, and κ=0.90, outperforming RDT specificity (80.8%) and microscopy sensitivity (90.2%), and detecting 67.9% of submicroscopic qPCR-positive infections missed by microscopy
articles.title_original: Field evaluation of the miniature direct-on-blood PCR nucleic acid lateral flow immunoassay (mini-dbPCR-NALFIA) for the detection of Plasmodium falciparum in high seasonal malaria transmission setting in Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: Among 128 artisanal gold miners and 113 community members in Mudzi, Zimbabwe, both groups showed good malaria knowledge but low risk perception with <1% parasitemia prevalence and no significant differences in knowledge or practices between groups. High knowledge was not translating to sustained preventative behavior, highlighting need for integrated livelihoods and malaria interventions
articles.title_original: An assessment of knowledge, practices, and malaria parasitaemia among artisanal gold miners and community members in Mudzi, Zimbabwe.

articles.headline: Type II PDGFRA inhibitor GSK190937 demonstrated antiplasmodium activity against P. falciparum asexual stages (PfNF54 IC50=0.22 μM); optimized analogs (compounds 20, 23, 29) showed improved metabolic stability and efficacy through hemozoin formation inhibition, killing late-stage trophozoites
articles.title_original: Repositioning a Human Kinase Platelet-Derived Growth Factor Receptor Alpha Type II Inhibitor for Malaria and Inhibition of Hemozoin Formation.

articles.headline: P. yoelii experimental evolution in single-infected and Heligmosomoides polygyrus-coinfected mice (5-7 passages) showed increased parasitemia and virulence with serial passage in both conditions, with similar replication rates and virulence profiles between lines. COI-lines displayed slower early replication in mismatched environments, indicating rapid environmental adaptation without selection for altered virulence under coinfection
articles.title_original: Experimental evolution of Plasmodium yoelii in single and helminth-coinfected mice.

articles.headline: Isfahan Province, Iran (569 malaria cases, 2009–2025): incidence steeply declined post-2009 with 2024 resurgence, dominated by P. vivax (88.0%) among Afghan migrants (80.8% of cases). P. falciparum (4.6%) was strongly associated with severe malaria (adjusted OR 22.6; 95% CI 1.24–410.8; p=0.035), emphasizing need for species-specific strategies targeting imported cases
articles.title_original: Epidemiological and clinical profile of malaria patients in Isfahan Province, Iran: a retrospective analysis from 2009 to 2025.

articles.headline: Country-level analysis in sub-Saharan Africa (Kruskal-Wallis, Cliff's delta, machine learning) revealed lowest water/sanitation access groups had highest malaria incidence with large effect sizes, while vitamin A supplementation showed weaker associations. Tree-based machine learning outperformed logistic regression, demonstrating infrastructure's critical role in malaria control
articles.title_original: Investigating the relationship between malaria incidence and public health infrastructure in sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: Among 15 insect-specific viruses screened, four (Herbert herbevirus, Agua Salud alphavirus, Gouléako goukovirus, invertebrate iridescent virus 6) efficiently replicated in vivo in Anopheles stephensi. Coinfection trials showed sporozoite formation and P. falciparum infection rates did not differ between virus-infected and control mosquitoes, indicating tested viruses do not significantly affect P. falciparum development
articles.title_original: Systemic infection with insect-specific viruses does not affect Plasmodium sporozoite formation in Anopheles mosquitoes.

articles.headline: Expert consensus (30 epidemiologists/public health experts) developed a framework of 34 indicators across 6 domains for assessing malaria surveillance and response capacity in post-elimination settings, with domain weights determined via analytic hierarchy process and entropy methods; framework achieved expert authority coefficient of 0.895 in second round
articles.title_original: Building a tool to assess malaria surveillance and response capacity in malaria post-elimination contexts: a modified and dual-weighted Delphi approach.

articles.headline: Identified a novel Plasmodium falciparum cytochrome c variant (Pfcyt c-2) with anomalously low redox potential of -278 mV (>500 mV lower than canonical cyt c at +250 mV); the variant showed pentacoordinate heme structure, thermostability, and low-level peroxidase activity, revealing a new clade of eukaryotic cytochrome c variants with divergent biochemical properties
articles.title_original: A Divergent Cytochrome c in Malaria Parasites with an Anomalously Low Redox Potential.

articles.headline: Among 422 febrile patients in Ethiopia, malaria prevalence was 27.0% (P. falciparum 84.2%, P. vivax 7.9%, mixed 7.9%). Bioline Ag Pf/Pv mRDT showed 99.1% sensitivity, 99.3% specificity, κ=0.98 versus microscopy. Males were 1.88× more likely infected (p=0.011); households with ≥5 members 2.19× higher odds (p=0.001)
articles.title_original: Prevalence of malaria and diagnostic performance of malaria rapid diagnostic tests compared to microscopy among febrile patients at Pawe General Hospital, Northwest Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Bradford assay validated for R21 malaria vaccine protein quantification with 2-phenoxyethanol preservative in multi-dose formulation, achieving limit of quantitation of 2 µg/mL with demonstrated specificity, accuracy, precision, and repeatability; method showed comparable performance to ELISA and robust immunogenicity in release and stability studies
articles.title_original: Systematic Development and Validation of a Bradford-Based Protein Quantification Method for Novel Multi-Dose R21 Malaria Vaccine Formulated with 2-Phenoxy Ethanol (2-PE).

articles.headline: In Kampong Speu Province, Cambodia 2019-2023, annual parasite incidence dropped from 23.8 to 0.7 per 1000 at-risk individuals; radical cure treatment completion improved from 78% to 98%. Males aged 15-49 years, particularly forest-goers, remained the highest-risk group despite substantial progress toward 2025 elimination goal
articles.title_original: Assessing progress and challenges towards malaria elimination in Kampong Speu, Cambodia: analysis of Plasmodium vivax and mixed infections, 2019-2023.

articles.headline: Testing 6 RDT products with P. falciparum cultures, procedural errors significantly impaired performance: increased sample volume >5µL caused 83.3% invalid results in 4/6 products due to faulty migration; insufficient buffer caused ≥1 invalid outcome in >50% of products. Higher parasitaemia associated with fewer deviations and increased band intensity
articles.title_original: The effects of introduced procedural errors on malaria rapid diagnostic test performance in a laboratory setting.
articles.authors: Wilson S, Ah Y, Aidoo M

articles.headline: Housing modification costs in Uganda: screening $116 (annualized $4.22/person/year), eave tubes $50 ($3.03/person/year). Installation costs higher in wealthiest versus poorest quintiles (screening $151 vs $69; eave tubes $95 vs $31). Among 1500 surveyed, >75% willing to pay but values represented only 7-18% of actual costs across wealth quintiles
articles.title_original: Housing modification to prevent malaria in Uganda: an analysis of costs, willingness to pay, and equity.

articles.headline: Among 683 PLWH under ART and co-trimoxazole in Brazzaville, microscopy detected malaria in 1.2% (8/683) while 9.2% (62/675) carried submicroscopic P. falciparum with mean multiplicity 2.1±1.09 and 68.1% polyclonal infections. High resistance mutations: Pfdhfr 51I 78.2%/59R 84.2%/108N 84.0%; no artemisinin resistance mutations detected
articles.title_original: Characterization of Plasmodium falciparum infections among people living with HIV under antiretroviral and co-trimoxazole chemoprophylaxis in Brazzaville, Republic of Congo.

articles.headline: Controlled human malaria infection with attenuated P. falciparum sporozoites identified correlates of protection: baseline IFN-γ secretion and HLA-DRhigh expression; vaccine-induced IL-4 secretion and CD57- γδ T cell frequency
articles.title_original: IFN-γ and IL-4 correlate with protection induced by an attenuated malaria vaccine.

articles.headline: Community survey of 1496 adults in Gabon (2023) found asymptomatic P. falciparum prevalence 15.3% overall (rural 22.4%, urban 4.1%). Independent risk factors: rural residence (aOR 7.1; 95% CI 4.4-9.8), employment (aOR 5.2; 95% CI 3.4-7.8), school attendance. ITN use 32.7%; not significantly protective
articles.title_original: Burden and determinants of asymptomatic malaria among adults living in urban and rural areas in Gabon in 2023: a community-based cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Title indicates review of integrated vector control and community-based interventions for malaria in Madagascar; abstract not provided
articles.title_original: Integrated Malaria Control in Madagascar: From Vector Dynamics to Community Interventions.

articles.headline: Blood donor screening in Tuscany over 8 years: 3754 tested for anti-Plasmodium antibodies, 6.8% seroprevalence (Sub-Saharan Africa 42.9%, Southeast Asia 10.6%); 0/20 seropositive positive by Plasmodium DNA LAMP. Among 4285 tested for anti-T. cruzi, 0.7% seroprevalence. High seroreversion indicated repeat screening after 1 year recommended
articles.title_original: Prevention of Transfusion-Transmitted Malaria and Chagas Disease in Non-Endemic Countries: An 8-Year Study of Seroprevalence Among Donors at Risk in Tuscany (Central Italy).

articles.headline: Spatial-temporal modeling of 182,071 genotyped samples across 3,806 surveys (2014–2025) tracked rapid expansion of Plasmodium falciparum k13 ART-R mutations in East Africa: k13 prevalence rose to 26.36% in Uganda and 39.44% in Rwanda by 2024, driven by distinct regional emergences (k13 561H, 675V, 622I). This expansion mirrors Southeast Asian patterns preceding ACT failure, signaling urgent need for targeted molecular surveillance to safeguard artemisinin-based combination therapy efficacy
articles.title_original: Mapping the prevalence of molecular markers of Plasmodium falciparum artemisinin partial resistance in Africa: a spatial-temporal modelling study.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 51 Indian studies (2000–2024) on Anopheles insecticide resistance revealed DDT and malathion resistance prevalent across most states, with deltamethrin resistance concentrated in Chhattisgarh and Madhya Pradesh (89–100% mortality in A. culicifacies, A. stephensi, and A. fluviatilis). North-eastern states retained susceptibility, suggesting geographic opportunities for targeted vector control strategies
articles.title_original: Spatio-temporal trends of insecticide resistance in Indian malaria vectors (2000-2024): a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Mullick R, Yadav CP, Singhal R

articles.headline: Interrupted time series of Guinea's national surveillance data (2018–2022) showed COVID-19 caused immediate declines in pentavalent vaccination (IRR 0.940) and TB notification (IRR 0.850), with sustained pandemic-period decreases in inpatient malaria cases (0.8% monthly, IRR 0.992) and infant HIV PCR tests (8%, IRR 0.920). Malaria indicators largely persisted while HIV and TB services were heavily disrupted
articles.title_original: The impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on HIV, malaria, tuberculosis and childhood vaccination services in Guinea: an interrupted time series analysis.

articles.headline: Experimental P. vivax infections in Anopheles darlingi produced two phenotypes distinguished by metabolic reprogramming: low-infection mosquitoes activated detoxification, redox regulation, and purine depletion with reduced bacterial diversity, while high-infection mosquitoes showed permissive physiology with expanded Flavobacteriales. Low outcomes correlated with lowest gametocyte densities, indicating early bloodmeal-derived signals prime mosquito resistance or susceptibility
articles.title_original: Metabolic reprogramming and gut microbiota ecology drive divergent Plasmodium vivax infection outcomes in Anopheles darlingi.

articles.headline: 16S rRNA sequencing of 120 Anopheles gambiae (60 P. falciparum-infected, 60 uninfected) identified 99 bacterial taxa, with 97 shared between groups; 14 genera were more abundant in uninfected mosquitoes, only 2 in infected. Higher bacterial diversity in uninfected samples during wet season and infected samples during dry season suggest specific bacterial associations with infection susceptibility
articles.title_original: Microbiota diversity of Anopheles gambiae in Bankeng, southern Cameroon, and its association with Plasmodium falciparum infection.

articles.headline: Semi-field evaluation in Iran demonstrated Serratia AS1 successfully colonized Anopheles stephensi midguts and ovaries, persisted in larval water for 14 days, and remained stable on sugar sources 4–6 days. Transmission occurred via transstadial, venereal, vertical, and aquatic routes, establishing feasibility of water-based bacterial delivery for paratransgenesis-mediated malaria control
articles.title_original: Environmental persistence and transmission dynamics of Serratia AS1 in mosquito habitats: advancing paratransgenesis for malaria control.

articles.headline: Case report: 14-year-old Ethiopian girl developed symmetrical lower-extremity gangrene during severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria despite prompt antimalarial therapy and preserved vital signs and perfusion. The case highlights an uncommon severe malaria complication requiring early recognition and demonstrates the spectrum of severe malaria pathology beyond organ failure
articles.title_original: Symmetrical lower extremity gangrene in a patient with severe malaria: a case report and literature review.

articles.headline: Perspective outlining essential components for a baseline evolutionary model of P. falciparum population genetics. With access to tens of thousands of genomes via MalariaGEN and other initiatives, a unified framework accounting for drug resistance, demographic history, and multiple concurrent evolutionary processes is needed to improve inference accuracy and identify novel drug-resistance candidates
articles.title_original: Towards an evolutionary baseline model of Plasmodium falciparum for population-genomic inference.

articles.headline: Cross-species analysis of B cell responses to Plasmodium circumsporozoite protein (CSP) revealed that germinal center inhibition during infection and skewed B cell differentiation due to CSP's repetitive epitopes limit durable protective immunity. These mechanisms underlie waning vaccine efficacy and reduced responsiveness in malaria-experienced populations
articles.title_original: Dysfunctional memory B cell responses to the protective repeat region of the Plasmodium circumsporozoite protein are associated with waning humoral immunity.

articles.headline: Catalytic-deficient biliverdin reductase A (BVRA) mutants (G17A, E97A) succumbed to otherwise non-lethal malaria in mice, failing to maintain protective bilirubin thresholds. These findings establish that BVRA enzymatic activity—not its non-canonical kinase or transcription factor functions—is essential for malaria resistance, defining a bilirubin concentration threshold required for parasite control
articles.title_original: Biliverdin Reductase Catalytic Activity Is Essential for Malaria Resistance.

articles.headline: Computational modeling and microfluidic experiments revealed rosettes (infected RBCs surrounded by healthy RBCs) exhibit significantly weaker margination toward vessel walls than single infected RBCs due to deformability and cluster dynamics. This suggests rosette formation protects parasites from splenic clearance by maintaining them in circulation rather than enhancing endothelial adhesion
articles.title_original: Rosette margination in blood flow during malaria pathogenesis.

articles.headline: CHILDPRIM RCT (Brazilian Amazon, n=100) compared primaquine regimens for Plasmodium vivax in children: standard 3.5 mg/kg/7 days vs high-dose 7.0 mg/kg/14 days or 7.0 mg/kg/7 days. Day 180 recurrence-free rates were 50%, 82.3%, and 79.4% respectively (p=0.0065). High-dose regimens were safe with higher adverse events (methaemoglobinaemia, anaemia) but no increase in serious adverse events; the 7-day schedule offers improved adherence
articles.title_original: Safety, tolerability, and efficacy of high versus low-dose, short versus long-course daily primaquine for the radical cure of uncomplicated Plasmodium vivax malaria in children under 15 years of age: an open-label, non-inferiority, randomized controlled trial (CHILDPRIM).

articles.headline: Analysis of 232 P. vivax isolates from Thailand identified Pvdbp duplication in 22 isolates (9.48%) across 254 sequences, with highest nucleotide diversity in PvdbpII and 26 positively selected codons (22 overlapping predicted immune epitopes). Cambodian-type duplications with identical sequences may enhance immune evasion via increased gene dosage, while positive selection occurred broadly across the 5' region and PvdbpII. Results indicate host immune pressure drives positive selection outside PvdbpII, though relevance for vaccine design requires investigation
articles.title_original: Complete gene sequence diversity, amplification, and natural selection of the Plasmodium vivax Duffy binding protein locus.

articles.headline: Among 295 patients with PCR-confirmed P. knowlesi at Kapit Hospital, Malaysian Borneo, viable gametocytes (measured by pks25 mRNA transcripts) were present in 67.5% (199/295), with positive correlation between gametocyte load and total parasitaemia (ρ = 0.32, p = 0.01). High densities were rare: 20% had <500 copies/µL, but 12.5% had elevated levels (13 with 10,001–100,000 and 12 with >100,000 pks25 copies/µL). Substantial gametocyte carriage underscores human transmission potential, though direct evidence of natural human-to-human transmission remains absent
articles.title_original: Plasmodium knowlesi gametocyte carriage and load among malaria patients at Kapit Hospital in Sarawak, Malaysian Borneo.
