articles.headline: Monthly household surveys of 13,786 participants in five Burkina Faso villages (March 2022–February 2023) measured P. falciparum prevalence: 39.6% overall (95%CI 38.79–40.42), highest near Soum dam at 42.1% vs lowest in distant Séguédin at 33.2%. Children aged 5–19 years showed highest prevalence (43.6–69.4%); asymptomatic carriage (24.5%) exceeded clinical cases (19.5%); monthly rainfall unexpectedly associated with decreased infection (OR = 0.77, 95%CI 0.66–0.9). Dam construction significantly elevated malaria risk, requiring integrated health and water management interventions
articles.title_original: Epidemiological profile and environmental determinants of Plasmodium falciparum infection in villages surrounding the Soum Agropole, Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: In vitro study using primary human hepatocytes demonstrated that Wnt pathway inhibition significantly improved size and maturity of P. falciparum liver stage schizonts while preserving hepatocyte metabolic activity and epithelial character. Wnt inhibition delayed hepatocyte dedifferentiation and enhanced parasite permissiveness, as revealed by transcriptomic integration with phenotypic analysis. Findings elucidate hepatocyte determinants of P. falciparum liver stage development and provide improved in vitro model for novel therapeutic screening
articles.title_original: Inhibition of Wnt signaling in primary human hepatocytes promotes Plasmodium falciparum liver stage development.

articles.headline: Qualitative study with 20 mothers and caregivers across urban and rural South West Region Cameroon revealed urban caregivers had high RTS,S/AS01 awareness (though limited understanding), while rural caregivers displayed uneven awareness but strong health worker trust. Perceived vaccine benefits (reduced malaria severity) enhanced confidence across settings; barriers included communication gaps, misinformation, gender dynamics, long wait times, and facility distance. Clear, consistent community-based communication through churches and health talks is essential for equitable vaccine uptake
articles.title_original: Awareness, perception and influences on uptake of the RTS,S/AS01 malaria vaccine among caregivers for children under 5 years in South West region, Cameroon.

articles.headline: Single case report of 54-year-old woman with P. falciparum cerebral malaria presenting with seizures, altered sensorium, and multiorgan failure who developed bilateral T2/FLAIR hyperintensities in basal ganglia, thalami, and white matter with patchy diffusion restriction on MRI (normal CT). Despite prompt artesunate, dialysis, and organ support, patient died of refractory multiorgan failure. MRI revealed cytotoxic oedema patterns correlating with disease severity and poor prognosis, highlighting diagnostic value in adult cerebral malaria risk stratification
articles.title_original: Radiological findings in fulminant cerebral malaria: a rare neuroimaging presentation.

articles.headline: Molecular characterization of Anopheles gambiae complex mosquitoes from three Houet Province (Burkina Faso) field sites identified Serratia species via luxS PCR and 16S rRNA sequencing, with overall prevalence of 13.3% (varying by mosquito developmental stage, locality, and organ examined). Phylogenetic analysis revealed Burkina Faso strains were distinct from previously reported Serratia in Ghana and Nigeria; one Anopheles coluzzii was co-infected with Serratia and P. falciparum. Findings confirm potential Plasmodium-blocking Serratia in wild vectors, supporting biocontrol evaluation
articles.title_original: Molecular characterization of potential Plasmodium-Blocking Serratia spp. bacteria in field-caught malaria mosquito in Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: Perspective synthesizing China's malaria elimination, recognized by WHO certification in 2021 after seven decades of adaptive surveillance and evolving strategies. Four pillars identified: integrated surveillance, regional cooperation, ecological intervention, and technological innovation. Framework demonstrates sustainable vector control governance pathway applicable to other vector-borne diseases amid ecological disruption and climate change
articles.title_original: China's malaria elimination: One Health lessons for vector-borne disease governance.
articles.authors: Liu J, Liu Y, Cheng G

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 336 pregnant women at two Kinshasa hospitals measured geohelminth-malaria co-infection (5.7%) and geohelminthiasis alone (7.7%) via thick smears and stool examination. Ascaris lumbricoides was most common parasite (6.3%), followed by Trichuris trichiura (1.5%) and Ancylostoma duodenale (0.3%); third trimester was significantly associated with increased co-infection risk (ORa = 5.8; 95%CI 1.36–9.23; p = 0.0077). No significant association with maternal anemia; systematic screening and integrated management recommended for this risk period
articles.title_original: Geohelminthiasis and Malaria Co-Infection in Pregnant Women in Kinshasa: Case of Lisungi and Lukunga Hospitals in Democratic Republic of the Congo.

articles.headline: In vitro study of blood-stage P. falciparum exposed to dihydroartemisinin (DHA) using Mtphagy pH-sensitive fluorochrome revealed decreased mitochondrial pH in early ring-form trophozoites; mitochondrial fission and PI3K pathway inhibition reduced Mtphagy-positive parasites and delayed recrudescence. DHA-induced growth arrest showed slight reduction in MitoTracker/Mtphagy colocalization despite undetectable lysosomes. Mtphagy-based assay monitors mitochondrial dynamics during artemisinin-induced arrest, suggesting mitochondrial fission targeting may overcome artemisinin resistance
articles.title_original: Fluorescence-based bioassay for the detection of changes in mitochondrial physiology during artemisinin-induced growth arrest of blood-stage Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 222 Sudanese healthcare workers (January–April 2025) assessed malaria vaccine knowledge and attitudes: mean age 31.5 years, 66.7% female, 44.6% pharmacists; only 9% demonstrated adequate knowledge (≥70%) and 21.6% positive attitudes (≥70%). Of respondents, 71.2% were unaware of WHO vaccine recommendations, 76.6% did not know target parasite, and 78.4% reported negative attitudes regarding vaccine safety, affordability, and institutional trust. Critical knowledge gaps and predominant vaccine hesitancy among HCWs signal urgent need for targeted training to support Sudan's vaccination scale-up
articles.title_original: Preliminary insights into knowledge and attitudes toward malaria vaccination among Sudanese healthcare workers: an exploratory cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Retrospective analysis of Brazil's Health Information System (2021–2024) examined federal government intervention in Yanomami Indigenous Land (YIL), where 117,214 indigenous malaria cases were reported, predominantly P. vivax. Post-intervention (January 2023), confirmed cases paradoxically increased from 52,210 to 65,004 (p = 0.0005), hospitalizations decreased (787→638; p = 0.0304), and deaths remained stable (111 total; p = 0.9089). Case detection shifted from adults (20–59 years) to vulnerable groups (0–19, 60+ years); exported cases to other states dropped sharply (Maranhão –95.5%, Pará –86.8%, Amazonas –71.5%), suggesting improved case identification with reduced interstate transmission
articles.title_original: What impact did the Brazilian Federal Government's intervention have on malaria cases in the Yanomami Indigenous lands?
articles.authors: de Aguiar Barros J, et al.

articles.headline: Survey of 30,521 Anopheles mosquitoes across 67 health districts in Burkina Faso (Sept-Dec 2022) found An. gambiae s.l. dominated at 94.4%, with distinct regional composition: An. coluzzii predominated in Sahelian (74.9%) and Sudano-Sahelian (71.2%) zones, while An. gambiae s.s. was most frequent in Soudanian zone (53.8%). Findings emphasize the need for locally adapted malaria vector control strategies based on spatial and ecological differences in Anopheles composition
articles.title_original: Spatial Distribution and Biodiversity of Anopheles Mosquito Species Across Climatic Zones in Burkina Faso: Implications for Malaria Vector Control.

articles.headline: In vitro study testing recombinant human periostin (OSF-2) on phagocytosis of P. falciparum and P. knowlesi-infected erythrocytes by THP-1 cells showed dual effects: OSF-2 increased IRBC phagocytosis when rosetting was prevented (CD36-dependent mechanism) and when cells were primed with OSF-2, but reduced phagocytosis when rosetting was induced by addition of uninfected erythrocytes. Results highlight complex parasite-host interactions affecting infection pathogenesis
articles.title_original: Effect of periostin (OSF-2) on phagocytosis of Plasmodium-infected erythrocytes.

articles.headline: Molecular surveillance of P. falciparum in two Western Kenya regions (lake-endemic Nyando and highland Marani) detected Pfcrt mutations M74I/N75E/K76T at 5.4%, Pfmdr1 Y184F at 48.2% and D1246Y at 3.6%, and Pfk13 A675V at 12.8%. Kisii region showed significantly higher Pfcrt (11.5%) and Pfk13 A675V (27.5%, p<0.05) prevalence; rare Pfk13 S522C/R561H and novel Pfmdr1 N146I mutations emerged in Kisumu. Continuous surveillance essential given artemether-lumefantrine is Kenya's first-line treatment
articles.title_original: Comparative molecular surveillance of polymorphisms in chloroquine resistance transporter, multidrug resistance 1, and Kelch 13 genes associated with antimalarial drug resistance in Plasmodium falciparum isolates from Western Kenya.

articles.headline: Feasibility study of RDT self-testing in Kenya (n=225), South Africa (n=80), and Zambia (n=163) found malaria positivity of 11.5%, 0%, and 1.8% respectively. Self-interpreted RDTs showed 100% sensitivity and 99.7% specificity versus trained team interpretation, with >96% of participants across all sites reporting ease of use and >85% (Kenya 92%, Zambia 86%, South Africa 66%) feeling confident for future self-testing. RDT self-testing is highly acceptable and feasible in SSA settings with high malaria burden
articles.title_original: Feasibility of self-testing for acute malaria using rapid diagnostic tests in three peri-urban sub-Saharan African community settings.

articles.headline: Before-and-after study at a malaria clinic in Timika, Papua over 16 months (7,471 total patients) evaluated biometric fingerprinting added to existing malaria card system. Proportion of patients linked to pre-existing card increased from 44.4% (Phase 1) to 48.9% (Phase 3), difference 4.5% (95% CI 1.4–7.7%); among fingerprinted patients in Phase 3, 55.8% linked to pre-existing card (11.4% improvement, 95% CI 8.1–14.8%). Refusal rate <0.4%; biometrics improve patient identification in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Enhancing unique identification of patients with malaria using biometric fingerprinting in Papua, Indonesia.

articles.headline: Poly-α microsatellite genotyping of P. falciparum from two Kenyan sites (Busia 2016: n=93; Kilifi 2020: n=92) revealed 97% and 87% successful genotyping respectively. Busia predominantly polyclonal (80%) with mean complexity of infection 2.9 (p<0.0001); Kilifi mostly monoclonal (52.5%) with mean COI 1.7. Both regions showed high expected heterozygosity (He: Busia 0.92, Kilifi 0.90), supporting poly-α microsatellite utility as molecular correction tool in therapeutic efficacy studies
articles.title_original: Characterizing Plasmodium falciparum genetic diversity and complexity of infections in clinical malaria infections in Western and Coastal Kenya using the poly-alpha microsatellite marker.

articles.headline: Selective whole genome amplification (SWGA) protocol optimization on 13 P. falciparum clinical samples tested incubation durations of 2, 8, and 16 hours, plus 3 hours with newer EquiPhi29 polymerase. Eight and 16-hour durations showed comparable sequencing yield with >90% SNP concordance. Optimized 3-hour protocol achieved >63% P. falciparum genome coverage with ~60% cost reduction. Advance enables faster, more cost-effective malaria diagnostics and genomic surveillance
articles.title_original: Enhancing whole genome DNA amplification of Plasmodium falciparum for advanced molecular surveillance in malaria control.

articles.headline: Deep learning models evaluated on 27,090 blood smear images from Kaggle: ResNet18 achieved highest F1-score of 96.33% among four baseline CNNs. Fine-tuned hybrid architectures (Xception, Inception-ResNetV2) attained ~98% classification accuracy on validation and test sets, maintaining 97–98% accuracy on independent Harvard Dataverse dataset with varied staining/imaging. Explainable AI techniques (Grad-CAM, LIME, SHAP) enhanced interpretability, demonstrating scalable solution for malaria detection in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Trustworthy deep learning for malaria diagnosis using explainable artificial intelligence.

articles.headline: Study characterizing epigenetic signatures of rRNA transcription in P. falciparum found rDNA silencing relies on aerobic glycolysis (sole energy pathway in human host). Disruption of NAD+ regeneration during lactate fermentation promotes rDNA de-repression; sirtuin histone deacetylase Sir2a mediates between fluctuating NAD+ levels and transcriptional outcome. rDNA activation couples to parasite metabolic state during transition from aerobic glycolysis to mitochondrial respiration in host-to-vector transmission
articles.title_original: A metabolism-chromatin axis promotes differential ribosomal RNA transcription in the human malaria parasite.

articles.headline: Experimental study of gastrointestinal microbiome in rhesus macaques (infected with P. fragile) and controlled human malaria infection trial participants (P. falciparum, NCT04072302 testing KAF156) identified distinct pre-infection 16S rRNA signatures associated with increased risk for above-median parasitemia in both models. Bifidobacterium feature set accurately stratified parasitemia risk, potentially serving as biomarker panel for malaria prevention in endemic regions
articles.title_original: Distinct gastrointestinal microbial signatures predict parasite levels in controlled Plasmodium infections in both rhesus macaques and humans.

articles.headline: Characterization of histone lactylation as a new epigenetic mark detected in P. falciparum, P. knowlesi (human parasites), and P. yoelii, P. berghei (rodent models). Histones increasingly lactylated in response to elevated lactate levels in vitro and in vivo; P. falciparum showed greater lactylation than P. knowlesi, suggesting species-specific epigenetic responses. In P. yoelii, hyperlactataemia correlated with transcriptomic programmes indicative of metabolic 'dormancy'; lactylation uniquely positioned as metabolic sensor
articles.title_original: Histone lactylation: A new epigenetic mark in the malaria parasite Plasmodium.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 200 febrile patients in Niger River Basin, Nigeria identified 19% with anti-DENV antibodies (13.5% IgG, 4% IgM, 0.5% NS1), 52% malaria parasite positivity, and 62.5% typhoid positivity. Dengue monoinfection 13.5%, malaria 18.5%, typhoid 29%; dual infections: malaria+typhoid 29%, dengue+malaria 1%, dengue+typhoid 1%; triple infection 4%. High dengue and co-infection burden with endemic malaria and typhoid highlights importance of differential diagnosis to avoid inappropriate treatment
articles.title_original: Dengue virus infection amongst malaria and typhoid fever suspected acute febrile patients in the Niger river basin of Nigeria.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 271 pregnant women in Ethiopia found 13.4% malaria prevalence by RDT/microscopy. Non-use of insecticide-treated nets (AOR=3.2), lack of IPTp-SP (AOR=2.8), <4 antenatal care visits (AOR=4.6), proximity to stagnant water (AOR=2.5), and rainy season ANC attendance (AOR=1.9) were independently associated with infection. Reinforcing ITN distribution, IPTp-SP uptake, and routine ANC attendance is essential to reduce malaria burden in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Prevalence and associated factors of malaria among pregnant women in Maraki Health Center, North West, Gondar, Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Protocol for identifying and sorting antigen-specific B cells from Plasmodium-infected mice using SpyCage reagents targeting MSP1-19 antigen in a rodent malaria model. Describes splenic single-cell isolation, B cell enrichment, and sorting of live cells for downstream single-cell RNA, V(D)J, and ATAC sequencing
articles.title_original: Protocol to identify and sort antigen-specific B cells from Plasmodium-infected mice.

articles.headline: Protocol for genomic surveillance of P. falciparum antigenic variants using amplicon-based PacBio long-read sequencing. Identifies and classifies structural variants of msp1, msp2, glurp, and csp genes, determining infection complexity, constructing isolate phylogenies, and supporting vaccine design
articles.title_original: Protocol for genomic surveillance of Plasmodium falciparum antigens using amplicon-based PacBio long-read sequencing.

articles.headline: Health facility-based malaria surveys integrated with environmental data in a forested Philippine district achieved >3-fold increase in probability of detecting malaria foci compared to routine surveillance. Risk-based surveillance incorporating forest data proved highly cost-effective, though routine diagnostic sensitivity decreased in forest environments. Combining landscape covariates with molecular methods optimized detection in heterogeneous transmission settings
articles.title_original: Integrating forest data and health facility surveys to optimise risk-based malaria surveillance in the Philippines.

articles.headline: Correction to prior article on immune response and evasion mechanisms of P. falciparum parasites
articles.title_original: Correction to "Immune Response and Evasion Mechanisms of Plasmodium falciparum Parasites".

articles.headline: In C57BL/6J mice infected with P. berghei ANKA and in cultured primary cortical neurons, purified hemozoin directly adhered to neurons, induced internalization, and caused axon rupture, mitochondrial damage, reduced ATP and increased ROS, elevated nNOS activity, and decreased neurotransmitter levels. Hemozoin represents a direct contributor to neuronal injury in experimental cerebral malaria, informing development of adjuvant therapies
articles.title_original: Hemozoin induces neuronal injury primarily characterized by axon rupture and mitochondrial damage in experimental cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 26 studies (2010–2024) on antimalarial drug resistance in Nigeria identified high prevalence of chloroquine and sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance mutations (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr) with regional variation, and less common but concerning Pfk13 artemisinin-resistance mutations. Chloroquine may be reconsidered only in areas with consistently low Pfcrt resistance, contingent on clinical trials and ongoing molecular surveillance
articles.title_original: Molecular surveillance of antimalarial drug resistance genes in Nigeria: a systematic review and roadmap to malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Perspective on 2025 global health funding cuts affecting African malaria programs. US and multiple donor countries reduced malaria funding, disrupting insecticide-treated net distribution, seasonal chemoprevention campaigns, and surveillance. African governments must increase domestic funding and establish robust public-private partnerships to sustain control gains and prevent resurgence across the continent
articles.title_original: African leaders and regional institutions need to take the bull by the horns: a perspective on the impact of the 2025 funding cuts to malaria programmes.

articles.headline: Geospatial analysis of 12,371 children (6 months–10 years) in Ghana predicted malaria prevalence from 0–49% based on satellite-derived covariates (tasselled cap brightness, enhanced vegetation index) and urban variables. Fine-scale spatial mapping identified districts with >10% prevalence exceedance probability, enabling microstratified targeting of elimination interventions
articles.title_original: Fine-scale spatial mapping of urban malaria prevalence for microstratification in an urban area of Ghana.

articles.headline: Repeated cross-sectional survey of 293 asymptomatic individuals in pre-elimination Ecuador (Putumayo, 2023 rainy season) detected P. vivax by microscopy in 1.0% (95% CI 0.2–2.4%) but qPCR identified 1.7% (95% CI 0.6–3.9%) including two submicroscopic infections (mean Cq 32.6), while RDTs detected none. Molecular surveillance revealed low-density asymptomatic reservoir undetected by routine diagnostics, requiring sensitive tools and cross-border collaboration for elimination
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic parasitaemia in a malaria pre-elimination setting in the Putumayo area of the Ecuadorian Amazon basin.
articles.authors: van der Ende J, et al.

articles.headline: MEG-01 megakaryoblastic cells co-cultured with P. falciparum-infected RBCs showed suppressed megakaryocytic differentiation, downregulated NOTCH3, and dysregulated apoptosis genes; platelet-like particles increased but showed impaired clotting function. P. falciparum-infected RBCs, not extracellular vesicles, primarily inhibit megakaryopoiesis through transcriptional dysregulation and inflammatory activation (elevated IL-8, RANTES, MCP-1), mechanisms contributing to malaria-associated thrombocytopenia
articles.title_original: Interactions between Plasmodium falciparum-infected red blood cells and their extracellular vesicles with megakaryocytes: implications for platelet-like particle formation.

articles.headline: Retrospective analysis (1990–2020) in coastal Kenya found endemic Burkitt's lymphoma incidence declined dramatically: first decade 93.1 per 100,000 pediatric cases, second 130.3 per 100,000, third decade 2010–2020 only 10.2 per 100,000 (p<0.0001). P. falciparum parasite density simultaneously decreased from median 13,966 to 7,224 (p=0.0021), with strong positive correlation (r=0.53, p=0.0024), suggesting malaria control directly impacts endemic BL incidence
articles.title_original: A "Familiar Foe Revisited": examining the relationship between endemic Burkitt's lymphoma and changing malaria admissions in the coastal region of Kenya.

articles.headline: Review of experimental liver-stage malaria models examining how organoid technology can improve in vitro systems to better represent intrahepatic parasite growth and enable development of preventive strategies and radical cures, particularly for P. vivax hypnozoites
articles.title_original: Experimental models of liver-stage malaria: Progress, gaps, and challenges.
articles.authors: Nitaramorn N, Kulkeaw K, Imwong M

articles.headline: In P. berghei-infected mice, the n-butanol fraction (nBF) and purified sub-fraction (CCF1) from D. dumetorum tuber extract inhibited parasite growth by 49.13% and 40.80% respectively (p<0.05); CCF1 contained 11 compounds with allantoin and protodioscin predominant, suggesting a promising source for antimalarial drug development
articles.title_original: n-Butanol Fraction of Dioscorea dumetorum Tuber Aqueous Extract Exhibited Potent Antimalarial Activity in Plasmodium berghei-infected Mice.

articles.headline: In 962 rural Malawian participants (7,293 visits), 82% sought malaria treatment in formal sector; longer walking time (>90 min vs <30 min: OR=0.28) and higher education (OR=0.41) reduced formal treatment-seeking; fever associated with health-centre visits (OR=1.60, 95% CI 1.16–2.21), but P. falciparum infection and parasite density were not; barriers persist despite free care
articles.title_original: Predictors and characteristics of malaria treatment-seeking in Malawi: a longitudinal cohort study.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 926 indigenous Peruvians found 17.0% P. falciparum/P. vivax infection; 8.5% submicroscopic (74.7% asymptomatic); asymptomatic individuals showed 2.32-fold higher submicroscopic prevalence (aPR=2.32, 95% CI 1.63–3.29); household animals reduced risk 36% (aPR=0.64, 95% CI 0.48–0.85), highlighting need for molecular diagnostics in surveillance
articles.title_original: Hidden burden: submicroscopic Plasmodium spp. infections in indigenous populations of the Peruvian Amazon.

articles.headline: Among 187 guardians of children hospitalized with severe malaria in Malawi, mild anxiety was most common (79.7%), with moderate (10.7%) and minimal (9.6%); socio-demographic factors (age, guardian type, education, residence, income; all p<0.001) and clinical factors (longer stay and repeated admission; p<0.001) significantly increased anxiety severity
articles.title_original: Prevalence of anxiety and associated factors among guardians of children admitted with severe malaria at Thyolo District Hospital in Malawi.

articles.headline: Genetic study in P. berghei and T. gondii found that K40 acetylation of α-tubulin is not essential for parasite survival: Q40K mutation in P. berghei and deletion of α-TAT in T. gondii had no impact on development or in vitro growth, indicating functional redundancy in cytoskeletal regulation
articles.title_original: Luminal acetylation of microtubules is not essential for Plasmodium berghei and Toxoplasma gondii survival.

articles.headline: Secondary analysis of Tanzania's 2022 survey found clinical breast examination uptake very low (5%) among women aged 15–49; low income, unemployment, and lack of health insurance reduced odds of CBE; interactions between age and residence/education suggest barriers vary by demographic group
articles.title_original: Factors associated with the uptake of clinical breast examination for early detection of breast cancer: interaction analysis of the 2022 Tanzania Demographic and Health Survey and Malaria Indicator Survey.

articles.headline: Case report of 67-year-old woman from Sub-Saharan endemic area presenting with severe malaria complicated by hemolytic anemia, severe hypoglycemia, status epilepticus, respiratory failure, hepatocellular failure, acute kidney injury, and hyperkalemia (6.24 mmol/L), ultimately fatal from sepsis, illustrating multiple WHO-listed complications
articles.title_original: We Still Have to Fear Malaria: A Case Report of Severe Malaria With Almost all the Listed WHO Complications in a Patient Living in a Sub-Saharan Endemic Area.

articles.headline: Analysis of 7,000+ P. falciparum isolates found PfENT1 and PfENT4 highly conserved with low genetic diversity: missense variant frequencies 4.5% and 6.2% respectively; common variants in PfENT1 (F36S 4.0%, V129I 4.1%, L133F 3.0%) and PfENT4 (S383A 38.9%, I188F 2.2%, N209I 1.6%, A260T 1.5%), supporting suitability as antimalarial drug targets
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity of Plasmodium falciparum equilibrative nucleoside transporters PfENT1 and PfENT4: Implications for purine-based antimalarial drug development.

articles.headline: Longitudinal genomic study (1996–2017) in Asembo, Kenya using 24-SNP barcodes showed multiplicity of infection declined from 4.32 (1996) to 3.34 (2012), then increased to 3.49 (2017); strain-relatedness metrics revealed transmission shifted towards co-transmission 2001–2012, returning to superinfection by 2017, tracking insecticide-treated bed net campaigns
articles.title_original: Assessing the impact of temporal changes in transmission on Plasmodium falciparum strains in Asembo, western Kenya (1996-2017) using within-host metrics via 24-SNP barcodes.

articles.headline: Three-year entomological study (2019–2022) in Burkina Faso comparing ITN types across 73,101 Anopheles showed dual-AI Interceptor®G2 achieved sustained 88.7% density decline over 2 years in Banfora; standard pyrethroid nets (Gaoua) rebounded year 3; pyrethroid-PBO nets (Orodara) remained stable, highlighting durability limits and need for complementary vector control
articles.title_original: Three-year trends in malaria transmission parameters under deployment of Interceptor®G1, Interceptor®G2 and PermaNet®3.0 insecticide-treated bed nets in South-Western Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: Analysis of malaria data (2012–2021) from Jimma Zone, Ethiopia across 1,718,471 screened found 359,655 confirmed cases (20.93%): P. falciparum 52.71%, P. vivax 43.62%, mixed 3.67%; annual parasite incidence fell 96.13% from 87.97 per 1,000 (2012) to 3.40 per 1,000 (2021); ARIMA forecasting (2022–2026) predicts stability with seasonal peaks but persistent risk in specific areas
articles.title_original: Unraveling the spatiotemporal clustering of malaria incidence and modeling a decade of epidemiological data in Jimma Zone, Oromia, Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Assessment of malaria-free status in Cabo Verde based on available title information
articles.title_original: "Cabo Verde: is it really malaria-free?".

articles.headline: Examination of delayed diagnosis and systematic detection gaps in imported malaria cases in Vietnam
articles.title_original: Delayed diagnosis and severe outcomes in imported malaria in Vietnam highlighting systematic gaps in detection.

articles.headline: Dehydrated colorimetric LAMP assay for P. knowlesi detection in 75 Malaysian blood samples achieved 100% sensitivity (95% CI: 92.1–100.0%) and 100% specificity (95% CI: 88.4–100.0%), with room-temperature stability for 15 days, offering an ideal diagnostic tool for resource-limited endemic settings
articles.title_original: Rapid Detection of Knowlesi Malaria Using Room-Temperature Stable and Ready-to-Use Colorimetric Loop-Mediated Isothermal Amplification Reagents.

articles.headline: Pilot feasibility study of 6-month home tablet-based neurorehabilitation (Dino Island) and Brain Vital Signs monitoring in children aged 3–12 years surviving cerebral malaria demonstrated 5% attrition, 100% positive family ratings, 60% parent-reported behavioral improvements, and adequate data acquisition, confirming feasibility and acceptability in sub-Saharan African settings
articles.title_original: A Pilot Feasibility Study of a Home Tablet-Based Neurorehabilitation Program and Serial Brain Vital Sign Monitoring for Survivors of Pediatric Cerebral Malaria.

articles.headline: Transfer learning with pretrained CNN architectures for automated P. falciparum detection in blood smear images: ResNet-50 and ResNet-101 achieved ~89% accuracy, Xception ~88%, and VGG-16 ~80%, demonstrating strong balanced performance for parasitized cell classification
articles.title_original: Malaria Parasite Cell Classification Using Transfer Learning with State-of-the-Art CNN Architectures.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling of next-generation ITNs (Interceptor®G2: chlorfenapyr-pyrethroid; Olyset® Plus: piperonyl butoxide-pyrethroid) identified operational factors—functional survival, ITN use, and in-bed exposure—as critical determinants of effectiveness beyond entomological parameters, enabling setting-specific product selection
articles.title_original: Cascades of effectiveness of next-generation insecticide-treated nets against malaria, from entomological trials to real-life conditions.

articles.headline: Surveillance of 60 P. falciparum isolates from Iranian patients (2022–2023) revealed 98% (49/50) wild-type pfk13 alleles and one novel R513C mutation, with no validated artemisinin resistance-associated mutations, confirming continued artemisinin efficacy 18 years after nationwide ACT introduction
articles.title_original: Absence of Artemisinin Resistance-Associated Mutations in the Plasmodium Falciparum Kelch 13 (K13) Propeller Domain Gene Following Widespread Use of Artemisinin-Based Combination Therapy in Iran.

articles.headline: VAR2CSA-reactive IgG measured in pregnant Brazilian women at delivery showed FV2CHO antigen correctly restricted IgG to P. falciparum-exposed women with marked Fc-afucosylation, while P. vivax-exposed women had higher PvDBP-specific IgG with no cross-reactivity to VAR2CSA, cautioning against insect cell-expressed recombinant proteins for pregnancy exposure assessment
articles.title_original: VAR2CSA-reactive IgG in Brazilian women exposed to Plasmodium falciparum or P. vivax infection during pregnancy.

articles.headline: In silico structural analysis identified four P. knowlesi Hsp90 isoforms with plasmodial-specific glycine-rich helix loop conformations distinct from human Hsp90s; virtual screening identified compounds (ZINC22007970, ZINC724661072, ZINC724661078) predicted to bind PkHsp90 strongly with minimal human Hsp90 binding, supporting rational drug design for zoonotic malaria
articles.title_original: Plasmodium knowlesi Heat Shock Protein 90s: In Silico Analysis Reveals Unique Druggable Structural Features.
articles.authors: Daniyan MO, Singh H, Blatch GL

articles.headline: Literature review of 31 articles (2000–2024) on non-falciparum malaria in West Africa documented P. falciparum in 93.5%, P. malariae in 80.6%, P. ovale in 77.4%, and P. vivax in 35.5% of papers, with 54.8% using RDTs (70.6% PfHRP2-based); concludes need for more sensitive diagnostic techniques and modified control strategies for asymptomatic infections
articles.title_original: Circulation of non-falciparum malaria species in West Africa: A literature review from 2000 to 2024.

articles.headline: Descriptive study of 350 imported malaria cases in Pernambuco, Brazil (2001–2022) found P. vivax and P. falciparum predominant (49.7% and 47.5%), with median diagnostic delays varying by ethnicity (Indigenous 10 days vs. Mixed race/White 4 days); multivariate analysis indicated complex factors beyond ethnicity or education responsible for delays, highlighting need for clinician awareness in non-endemic settings
articles.title_original: Clinical and epidemiological profiles of confirmed malaria cases in the state of Pernambuco, Brazil, from 2001 to 2022.

articles.headline: Modeling study of Burkina Faso's 2016 free healthcare policy using 344,935 clinical visits from 199,664 children (2015–2018) showed clinical visits increased 82% (1.1 to 2 per child, p<0.001), malaria prevalence decreased from 98% to 80% (p<0.001), and severe malaria declined from 6% to 2.2% (p<0.001), though disparities persisted (children within 5 km: 2.2 visits vs. remote: 1.7 visits); Bayesian forecasts predict continued improvements through 2020
articles.title_original: Evaluating the effects of the free healthcare policy on clinical visits and malaria among children in Burkina Faso: a modeling study of past trends and future forecasts.

articles.headline: LLM-assisted review of 963 malaria culture studies (2000–2025) found only 314 reported RBC sourcing, with 61% failing to specify donor origin, 73% omitting blood group, and 6% reporting anticoagulant; experimental work with P. falciparum 3D7 and 69 donors revealed parasite growth varied 6.4-fold (3.0–19.3-fold) over 96 h, with low-growth phenotypes reproducibly associated with lower hemoglobin (12.3 vs 14.8 g/dL, p=0.03), anaemia history (p=0.01), and endemic ancestry (p=0.03), but no consistent clinical predictor identified. Proposed STRuBeC minimum reporting standard to improve transparency and cross-study reproducibility
articles.title_original: Improving reproducibility of Plasmodium falciparum culture: a large-language-model-driven literature review and a parasite growth variability assessment of donor red blood cells.

articles.headline: Integrated transcriptomic and machine-learning analysis of P. vivax mitochondrial function in severe malaria revealed natural antisense transcripts (NATs) as potent regulators tightly linked to mitochondrial pathways and translational machinery, distinct from transcriptional by-products. Findings position mitochondrial NATs as promising antimalarial targets but represent preliminary insights from limited clinical cohort; validation in larger, diverse populations essential before clinical interpretation
articles.title_original: Integrative transcriptomic and machine learning approaches to decipher mitochondrial gene regulation in severe Plasmodium vivax malaria.

articles.headline: Abbott-Bioline Malaria Ag P.f/P.v RDT compared to microscopy in 187 febrile persons during P. vivax outbreak in Myanmar: 11 RDT-positive vs 46 microscopy-positive cases; sensitivity 0.24 (95% CI 0.12–0.36), specificity 1.0. Among parasite-positive patients (≥200/µL, WHO threshold), sensitivity 0.39 (95% CI 0.21–0.57) with objectively low color-band intensity by ImageJ. RDT insensitivity caused dangerous treatment delays in resource-limited areas despite WHO approval
articles.title_original: The underperforming Abbott-Bioline Malaria Ag P.f/P.v rapid diagnostic test: a whiter shade of pale - where the truth is not plain to see.

articles.headline: Surface plasmon resonance analysis of CIS43ε (IgE variant) binding to P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein (PfCSP) showed biphasic kinetics with higher affinity for core Peptide 21 than NANP repeats; multimeric Peptide 21 induced significant basophil/mast-cell degranulation plateauing at trimeric state, while full-length PfCSP triggered greater degranulation than tetrameric Peptide 21, highlighting multivalent interactions' therapeutic potential
articles.title_original: Biophysical and functional characterization of IgE interactions with a malaria antigen.
articles.authors: Adam AI, Sutton BJ, McDonnell JM

articles.headline: Human Aurora kinase inhibitors tested against P. falciparum: hesperadin, TAE684, and AT83 exhibited multistage potency (<250 nM) against asexual blood stages, liver schizonts, and male gametes with >1000× selectivity. Hesperadin specifically inhibited PfArk1, causing failure to complete mitosis with unsegregated, multi-lobed nuclei from aberrant microtubule organization, establishing PfArk1 as primary mitotic kinase and hesperadin as tool compound for drug discovery
articles.title_original: Targeting Aurora Kinases as Essential Cell-Cycle Regulators to Deliver Multi-Stage Antimalarials Against Plasmodium Falciparum.

articles.headline: Study of naturally acquired B and antibody responses to P. falciparum asparagine-rich merozoite antigen (PfARMA) found IgM+ B cells dominated by N-terminal IDR1 recognition; both plasma IgM and IgG predominantly targeted IDR1 and increased with P. falciparum exposure, yet IDR1-directed responses did NOT correlate with protection in age/exposure-matched children, whereas ~30% of unexposed individuals had polyreactive IgG to IDR1
articles.title_original: The N-terminal region of malaria vaccine candidate Plasmodium falciparum asparagine-rich merozoite antigen is immunodominant and targeted by polyreactive antibodies.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study evaluated malaria self-testing practicability among 40 artisanal gold miners in French Guiana (median age 37, 27.5% without formal education) using Bioline Malaria Ag RDT after training: mean knowledge 83.9%, practice 84.6% unassisted (100% with guidance), interpretation 77.2%; correct interpretation rates 96.0% positive, 70.0% negative, 65.8% invalid strips; overall practicability score 81.9% with 100% valid results, supporting community-based diagnosis in hard-to-reach populations
articles.title_original: Practicability of a malaria rapid diagnostic self-test among artisanal gold miners in French Guiana: a cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Seasonal survey of 10,807 Anopheles mosquitoes across 11 southern Odisha districts (March 2021–February 2023) identified 18 species; An. subpictus predominated in summer (43%) and rainy season (47%); An. culicifacies (primary vector) consistent at 32–38% during rains; An. varuna peaked in winter (26–36%). Ganjam district showed highest diversity (1.26–2.2); seasonal variation statistically significant for diversity (p<0.001), surpassing district-level factors
articles.title_original: Seasonal dynamics and species diversity of Anopheles mosquitoes in malaria endemic districts of Southern Odisha India.

articles.headline: School-based cross-sectional survey of 416 Ethiopian schoolchildren (March–May 2024) found composite asymptomatic Plasmodium prevalence 3.4% (95% CI 1.7–6.3), with P. falciparum 64.3% of cases. Independent predictors: age (AOR=3.53, p=0.001), gender (AOR=2.87, p=0.021), stagnant water near household (AOR=3.76, p=0.031), never used LLINs (AOR=2.90, p=0.007), and past-year malaria history (AOR=2.23, p=0.047). Schoolchildren identified as significant asymptomatic reservoirs, requiring active case detection and improved prevention
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic Plasmodium infection and predictors among schoolchildren in Bahir Dar Zuria District, Northwest Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Database of 2,371 malaria vector distribution records (1929–2023) across Colombian ecoregions and altitudes revealed An. albimanus and A. neivai s.l. predominant coastally; A. darlingi, A. nuneztovari s.s., A. calderoni mainly inland in Magdalena Valley, Urabá-Bajo Cauca, Atrato, and Cauca River Valley. Vectors predominantly <500 m elevation, though An. albimanus and A. calderoni present >1000 m; both A. albimanus and A. neivai showed elevation increase over time, informing stratification and prevention prioritization
articles.title_original: Ecoregional and altitudinal distribution of the principal malaria vectors in Colombia.

articles.headline: Retrospective cohort of 6,410 South Sudanese children with malaria (ages 1 month–15 years, mean 23.66 months; 4.73% mortality) analyzed via Cox regression: age increased death hazard (HR=1.0223, p<0.01), body weight decreased it (HR=0.8513, p<0.01); quinine reduced death risk vs. artesunate (HR=0.7107, 95% CI 0.55–0.92, p<0.01); absence of comorbidity protective (HR=0.5530, p<0.01). Findings suggest quinine—contrary to other literature—associated with lower mortality and remains South Sudan's drug of choice
articles.title_original: A Cox Proportional Hazards Model Approach to Identifying Malaria Risk Factors in Children in South Sudan.

articles.headline: Assessment of Anopheles host-seeking activity in Manhiça district, Mozambique, found only one An. ziemanni with daytime (06:00–18:00) activity, collected when sunset was 17:06 (nighttime collection). Analysis of African sunrise/sunset times across latitudes and longitudes revealed >2 h seasonal variation, indicating entomological surveillance strategies misaligned with actual daylight cycles and human behavior, requiring temporal adjustment for actionable malaria control data
articles.title_original: Malaria Mosquito Host-Seeking Activity Times in Manhiça District, Rural Mozambique, and the Need to Better Match Entomological Surveillance Strategies to Daylight Cycles.

articles.headline: Hospital-based retrospective analysis of 36,438 malaria cases in Western Tigray (2011–2019) found P. falciparum predominant (62.1%), 0.14% hospital mortality, and statistically significant fluctuating trends (p=0.001) with peak deaths in ages ≥15 years; disease remains a major health problem despite Ethiopia's control progress
articles.title_original: Malaria Trends, Burden, Seasonal Variation, and Interventions in Western Tigray, Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Clinic-based cross-sectional survey of 1,031 individuals across three Meghalaya districts (2018–2021) documented 4.4% Plasmodium prevalence (60% P. falciparum), with heterogeneous ranges 1.8–12.3%; prior malaria (OR=9.2, p<0.001) and outdoor activities at peak mosquito hours (OR=2.0–3.1) were significant risk factors
articles.title_original: Malaria risk, treatment-seeking, and prevention in Northeastern India: clinic-based surveillance of persistent transmission.

articles.headline: Dried blood spot preservation study of 900 samples containing P. falciparum PfHRP2 demonstrated reliable detection by malaria RDT at room temperature, 6°C, and −60°C through 120 days, with positivity declining to 80–90% at 150 days; room temperature offers cost-effective preservation
articles.title_original: The reliability of malaria rapid diagnostic test kit for detecting P. falciparum PfHRP2 in dried blood spot samples preserved under different conditions and durations.

articles.headline: Genomic characterization of P. simium (zoonotic malaria) from Brazil using Oxford Nanopore and Illumina sequencing generated a 21.8 Mb reference genome; comparative analysis revealed known mitochondrial mutations and deletions in reticulocyte-invasion genes (RBP2a, DBP) versus P. vivax, advancing diagnosis and control strategies
articles.title_original: Novel zoonotic cases of Plasmodium simium from São Paulo with a reference genome of the Brazilian strain.

articles.headline: Rodent model (Plasmodium berghei) comparing intradermal versus intravenous delivery of late-arresting GAP sporozoite (GA2) vaccine showed intradermal immunization produced lower CD8+ tissue-resident memory T cells in spleen (1.3%, p=0.3), lungs (8.1%, p=0.005), and liver, with reduced Granzyme B and perforin (p<0.0001); optimization of injection volume may improve efficacy
articles.title_original: Intradermal immunization with Plasmodium berghei late-arresting genetically attenuated sporozoites induces PD-L1 expression on regulatory macrophages and dendritic cells.

articles.headline: Multi-arm randomized trial of 1,050 Cambodian military volunteers comparing monthly malaria prophylaxis (MMP) versus focused screening and treatment (FSAT) found MMP reduced P. falciparum positivity 90% (10% to 1%, ARR 9%) but 32% recrudesced; MMP with low-dose weekly primaquine (22.5 mg) was safe, including in 15% with G6PD-deficiency, and superior to FSAT for elimination
articles.title_original: Evaluating malaria elimination strategies among military forces in Cambodia: a multi-arm clinical trial comparing monthly prophylaxis and focused screening and treatment.

articles.headline: Chemoproteomic studies of peptidomimetic inhibitors targeting Plasmepsin V (PMV) aspartyl protease confirmed on-target activity against P. falciparum by demonstrating PMV target engagement; whole-genome sequencing of resistant parasite lines identified a single nucleotide variant in pmv gene, validated by reverse genetics, supporting PMV as a validated antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: Deconvolution of the On-Target Activity of Plasmepsin V Peptidomimetics in Plasmodium falciparum Parasites.

articles.headline: Preclinical evaluation of cytochrome P450-humanized mouse line (8HUM) infected with P. berghei demonstrated improved drug metabolic stability and better alignment with human metabolism for quinine derivatives than wild-type mice; model enables early-stage antimalarial efficacy testing while circumventing species differences in drug metabolism
articles.title_original: Humanization of Drug Metabolism in the Plasmodium berghei Mouse Model for Antimalarial Drug Discovery.

articles.headline: Laboratory assay of Anopheles gambiae (Kisumu strain) exposed to essential oils from Eucalyptus grandis and Cymbopogon citratus via Y-tube olfactometer identified dose-dependent repellency; main constituents were L-α-terpineol and eucalyptol (Eucalyptus) and lavandulol and citral (Cymbopogon), supporting development of botanical spatial repellents for vector control
articles.title_original: Spatial Exposure Responses of Malaria Vectors to Eucalyptus grandis (W. Hill ex Maiden) and Cymbopogon citratus (DC.) Stapf Essential Oils.

articles.headline: Experimental study of Anopheles stephensi infected with P. vivax found sterol carrier protein (SCP) upregulated in midgut and salivary glands but suppressed in hemocytes; RNAi knockdown of SCP significantly reduced mosquito fecundity, identifying SCP as a metabolic regulator of reproduction and possible transmission-blocking target
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax infection-driven modulation of sterol carrier protein reveals a metabolic link to reproductive physiology in Anopheles stephensi.

articles.headline: Single-cell RNA sequencing of spleens from C57BL/6 mice infected with P. yoelii NSM revealed splenic CD103+CD8+ T cells declined after infection and exhibited lower activation markers (CD44, CD69, TIGIT) and minimal IFN-γ and granzyme B secretion compared to CD103−CD8+ cells; transcription factor LEF1 may regulate Itgae expression in these cells
articles.title_original: Characteristics of CD103(+)CD8(+) T cells in the spleen of Plasmodium yoelii NSM-infected mice.

articles.headline: Retrospective surveillance analysis of Uganda (2016–2023) documented 110.1 million malaria cases, 1.6 million typhoid, and 0.36 million brucellosis cases; malaria prevalence decreased 4% (43,316 to 29,271 per 100,000), with northern region bearing highest burden; regional variation highlights need for tailored control strategies
articles.title_original: Spatial and Temporal Distribution of Febrile Illnesses in Uganda: Case Studies of Brucellosis, Malaria and Typhoid Fever (2016-2023).

articles.headline: Qualitative study (focus group discussions and interviews) in Osun State, Nigeria found caregivers, community leaders, and fathers reported high acceptability of perennial malaria chemoprevention (PMC) for children <24 months when delivered through the EPI platform. Acceptability was driven by perceived effectiveness, child-friendliness, and free services, but was negatively affected by transportation costs, fear of side effects, and health worker attitudes
articles.title_original: Investigating factors enabling the acceptability of perennial malaria chemoprevention implementation in Osun State, Nigeria: evidence from a qualitative process evaluation.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional mixed-methods study of 159 caregivers in Sudan (November 2024–January 2025) found high malaria vaccine acceptance (86.2%) despite low awareness (11.3%); acceptance was significantly associated with perceived malaria severity (p<0.001), caregivers' vaccination history (p=0.034), and children's complete immunization (p=0.047). Safety concerns predominated in qualitative responses; 66% of caregivers trusted healthcare providers for vaccine information
articles.title_original: Awareness and acceptance of the malaria vaccine among caregivers attending primary health care centres in Sudan: a mixed-methods study.

articles.headline: Behavior-incidence modeling of historical malaria case reports and insecticide-treated net (ITN) usage across Sub-Saharan Africa classified countries into distinct categories by transmission rates and socioeconomic conditions. Some countries can meet 2025/2030 Global Technical Strategy targets through current ITN efforts, while others require enhanced social awareness campaigns, economic assistance, and improved ITN efficacy; human behavioral practices were identified as critical for ITN effectiveness
articles.title_original: Insecticide-treated net use and elimination of malaria in Sub-Saharan African countries: assessing the Global Technical Strategy using an evolutionary game approach.

articles.headline: Geostatistical analysis of routine surveillance data (2014–2021) from 507 village-based malaria posts in Hpapun Township, Myanmar found P. falciparum incidence decreased 93.9% between 2014–2019. Intervention villages had substantially higher baseline incidence (IRR=4.72, 95% CI 4.56–4.90) with dramatically reduced post-intervention incidence (IRR=0.26, 95% CI 0.24–0.27); in 2021, 12 high-risk villages were identified, with 8 persistently high-risk (≥90th percentile difference ≥6 months)
articles.title_original: Spatiotemporal trends in P. falciparum malaria and identification of high-risk villages in Eastern Myanmar: an 8-year observational study.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 48 studies evaluating malaria rapid diagnostic test (RDT) performance across sub-Saharan Africa found considerable variability in sensitivity and specificity across different RDT brands. Variability was influenced by antigen persistence, cross-reactivity with other infections, and HRP2 gene polymorphism causing false positives and negatives; region-specific diagnostic strategies and advanced tools for detecting low-level parasitemia and differentiating Plasmodium species were recommended
articles.title_original: Performance and challenges of malaria rapid diagnostic tests in endemic regions of Africa.

articles.headline: Year-long genomic surveillance of 903 P. falciparum infections in Gondar Zuria and Tach Armachiho, Ethiopia found the artemisinin-resistance k13 R622I mutation in 44.3% of samples (52% vs. 35% between districts; p<0.001) and HRP2-based RDT negativity in 39.3% (48.7% vs. 27.6%; p<0.001). Critically, R622I and HRP2-RDT negativity co-occurred in 22% of samples (28.9% vs. 12.9%; p<0.001), with HRP2-RDT negativity significantly more common in R622I parasites (48.3% vs. 30.7%; p<0.05)
articles.title_original: High prevalence and emerging positive association of kelch13 R622I and HRP2-based RDT negativity in Plasmodium falciparum in northern Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Cohort study of 422 children in Tororo and Busia Districts, Uganda (2021–2023) with monthly P. falciparum surveillance and biweekly mosquito collection found each doubling of annualized household entomologic inoculation rate (aEIR) was associated with 29% increased P. falciparum hazard (adjusted HR 1.29, 95% CI 1.25–1.33). An. funestus-specific aEIR doubling increased hazard 29% (HR 1.29, 1.25–1.34), while An. gambiae showed smaller effect (HR 1.04, 1.01–1.08), supporting vector species replacement as a driver of malaria resurgence
articles.title_original: Disentangling the roles of different vector species during a malaria resurgence in Eastern Uganda.

articles.headline: Annual WHO insecticide susceptibility assays (2015–2020) at 30 sentinel sites across Ghana found high pyrethroid and DDT resistance in all sites with decreasing susceptibility over time; carbamate and organophosphate susceptibility also declined. L1014F (kdr-west) target-site resistance was significantly more frequent than G119S across sites, indicating widespread resistance to deltamethrin, permethrin, DDT, malathion, pirimiphos-methyl, bendiocarb, and propoxur in An. gambiae sensu lato
articles.title_original: Trends and patterns of insecticide susceptibility of Anopheles gambiae between 2015 and 2020: implications for malaria vector control interventions in Ghana.

articles.headline: Two imported P. falciparum cases in Shanghai, China (2021) demonstrated diagnostic delay impacts: Case 1 with 96-hour diagnostic delay progressed to severe malaria with delirium, thrombocytopenia (33×10⁹/L), and acute kidney injury (creatinine 278 μmol/L) requiring intensive care; Case 2 diagnosed within 24 hours by RDT and microscopy received immediate artesunate without severe complications. Parallel pathogen testing for both COVID-19 and malaria in returning febrile travelers from endemic areas is critical
articles.title_original: Diagnostic delay inducing severe imported falciparum malaria: a time-sensitivity comparison of two imported cases during COVID-19 co-circulation.

articles.headline: Oxford Nanopore amplicon sequencing of five drug resistance genes (crt, dhfr, dhps, mdr1, k13) and vaccine target csp in P. falciparum isolates from northern Nigeria found dhfr-IRNI at 90% and dhps-SGKAA at 45% frequency (sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance markers), Pfcrt-76T at ~25%, no WHO-validated k13 artemisinin-resistance mutations, and CSP polymorphisms potentially affecting vaccine target protein stability, with resistance haplotypes geographically clustered in Kano and Yobe
articles.title_original: Molecular epidemiology of Plasmodium falciparum drug resistance and vaccine targets in high-transmission settings in Africa.

articles.headline: Review of evidence demonstrating that erythrocyte surface blood-group antigens—including ABO, Duffy, MNS, Gerbich, Knops, and Diego systems—are key modulators of malaria susceptibility by affecting parasite invasion, cytoadherence, immune evasion, and disease severity. Population heterogeneity, parasite-host adaptations, and clinical implications for risk stratification, transfusion safety, and vaccine design are discussed
articles.title_original: Blood group antigens and malaria susceptibility.

articles.headline: Epidemiological investigation and genetic analysis of a P. falciparum infection in a 65-year-old Westchester County, New York resident (diagnosed October 2, 2023) with no recent international travel implicated blood-borne transmission in a healthcare setting from a concurrently hospitalized traveler with confirmed malaria. No mosquito-transmitted local cases or additional healthcare-associated secondary cases were identified; the mechanism likely involved a lapse in infection control procedures
articles.title_original: Case Report and Epidemiological Investigation of Healthcare-associated Plasmodium falciparum Malaria Transmission in Westchester County, New York-2023.

articles.headline: Opinion paper on Suriname's malaria elimination strategy combining surveillance, early diagnosis/treatment access, retreatment, insecticide-treated nets, and sustained community engagement in stable and nonstable Amazon communities. No quantitative efficacy outcomes reported; authors conclude strategy adaptable to other Amazon regions
articles.title_original: Is malaria elimination in the Amazon rainforest feasible? The case of Suriname.

articles.headline: Qualitative study (60 interviews, 28 FGDs; March-May 2023) in Burundi exploring perceptions of LLINs, IRS, IPTp-SP, and environmental management. Identified multilevel factors (intrapersonal, organizational, policy/environmental) influencing prevention behaviors; concluded multifaceted approach addressing communication, contextual adaptation, adverse effects, and socio-economic constraints essential for malaria control
articles.title_original: Understanding perceptions of malaria control measures in Burundi: a qualitative descriptive study using a socio-ecological approach.

articles.headline: Title only: Genetically engineered mosquitoes block development of circulating malaria strains. No abstract available; specific methodology and quantitative data not provided
articles.title_original: Genetically engineered mosquitoes block development of circulating malaria strains.

articles.headline: In silico and experimental validation of Plasmodium falciparum inositol phosphate pathway kinases in laboratory strains and clinical isolate. Identified p.Pro375Gln substitution in IPMK1—first evidence in field isolates. Authors identify IPMK as promising antimalarial drug target but recommend future experimental validation
articles.title_original: Unveiling the Plasmodium inositol (pyro)phosphate pathway: Highlighting inositol polyphosphate multikinase as a novel therapeutic target for malaria.

articles.headline: Commentary on Angola diagnostic practices. Heavy reliance on rapid diagnostic tests limits species identification and parasite quantification. No quantitative data; authors conclude microscopy strengthening and molecular diagnostics integration essential for improved clinical management and surveillance
articles.title_original: The risks of exclusive reliance on rapid diagnostic tests for malaria in Angola.

articles.headline: Response to 2023 autochthonous Plasmodium vivax outbreak in Florida (7 symptomatic cases, Sarasota County, May-July 2023). Blood center screened 258 Sarasota and 178 Miami donor samples by transcription-mediated amplification (detection limit 2–7 infected RBCs/mL). All samples nucleic acid test nonreactive; no transfusion-transmitted malaria cases reported
articles.title_original: Heightened Nontesting Risk Mitigation Blood Donor Screening with Retrospective Transcription-Mediated Amplification Testing during a 2023 Autochthonous Florida Malaria Cluster.

articles.headline: Vaccine study comparing adjuvants for Plasmodium falciparum CSP-epitope-targeted VLP vaccine. Five formulations significantly increased anti-CSP IgG titers; several enhanced antibody durability. Authors identify promising adjuvants enhancing both magnitude and longevity of VLP immunity; specific antibody fold-increases not reported
articles.title_original: A Comparative Evaluation of Adjuvants for Enhancing Antibody Responses to a Nanoparticle-Based Malaria Vaccine.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 3,563 Plasmodium falciparum dried blood spots from 26 Uganda health facilities (2023). Prevalence 5.0%–49.23%, incidence 91–1,062 (Round 1) and 33–1,667 (Round 2) cases/1,000 person-years. Microhaplotype complexity of infection (COI) strongly associated with prevalence (cross-validated r=0.79); between-host relatedness best predicted incidence (r=0.37). Genomic metrics reliably capture prevalence variation but show limited incidence prediction
articles.title_original: Accuracy of Plasmodium falciparum genetic data for estimating parasite prevalence and malaria incidence in Uganda.

articles.headline: Molecular study of Plasmodium berghei NIMA-related kinase 4 (NEK4) in zygotic meiosis and ookinete maturation. Nek4 deletion causes complete developmental arrest: blocked MTOC duplication, impaired chromatin condensation, failed nuclear migration. Phosphoproteomics shows NEK4 regulates HOP1, REC8, AP2-O. Identifies NEK4 as key meiotic regulator with potential antimalarial implications; no clinical efficacy data
articles.title_original: A divergent Plasmodium NEK4 acts as a key regulator driving the early events of meiosis.

articles.headline: Fractional-order malaria transmission model incorporating vector and non-vector pathways with memory effects, validated against Nigeria incidence data. Numerical experiments show decreasing fractional order delays epidemic peaks, prolongs oscillatory persistence, and amplifies long-term infection memory. Provides analytical forecasting framework; applied to modeling structure rather than specific field intervention outcomes
articles.title_original: Analytical and data-driven fractional-order malaria transmission model with vector and non-vector pathways.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 15 Nigerian studies comparing malaria RDT versus microscopy. Pooled RDT sensitivity 69.7% (95% CI 67.7–71.6%), specificity 81.5% (95% CI 79.9–83.1%), DOR 21.87 (95% CI 7.45–64.21), SROC 0.902 (I²=96.8%). Urban settings showed better performance. Authors conclude RDT sensitivity suboptimal versus WHO benchmarks; recommend diagnostic algorithm integrating microscopy or molecular methods
articles.title_original: Performance of rapid diagnostic tests for malaria diagnosis in Nigeria: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Machine learning design of Plasmodium falciparum CSP multiepitope vaccine scaffold (M-TIM) presenting three structured CSP minor repeats capable of binding monoclonal antibody L9. Cryo-EM structure (3.6 Å resolution) confirms M-TIM successfully orients three L9 copies recapitulating critical homotypic interactions. Demonstrates MESODID utility for multiepitope vaccine design; no clinical efficacy data reported
articles.title_original: Machine learning enables de novo multiepitope design of Plasmodium falciparum circumsporozoite protein to target trimeric L9 antibody.
articles.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A

articles.headline: Study synthesized and tested acyclic nucleoside phosphonates against chloroquine-sensitive P. falciparum (3D7 strain), finding several compounds with in vitro activity (IC50 11.3–38.8 µM). Molecular docking supported biological data. Authors propose these nucleotide analogues as promising antimalarial scaffolds needing further optimization
articles.title_original: Acyclic Nucleoside Phosphonates As Potential Inhibitors of Plasmodium Falciparum Egress.

articles.headline: Retrospective geospatial analysis of malaria in Oti Region identified Krachi East (91,376 cases) and Nkwanta South (73,592 cases) as persistent hotspots over 3 years. Elevation, land surface temperature (22–32°C), vegetation index, and river proximity were significant environmental factors (27.60%, 25.48%, 23.12%, 20.00% importance) influencing Anopheles breeding and malaria susceptibility
articles.title_original: Mapping Malaria Susceptibility in the Oti Region of Ghana Using Remote Sensing and GIS: A Retrospective Observational Study.

articles.headline: CDC bottle bioassays tested An. gambiae s.l. from Nigeria against permethrin (standard 21.5 µg/bottle) and deltamethrin (12.5 µg/bottle). Standard doses achieved 84.4% mortality at 30 min (permethrin; p=0.031) and 76% (deltamethrin; p=0.04), with complete mortality by 35–40 min; sub-lethal doses (≤0.8 µg/bottle) caused no mortality after 24 h (p=0.061), documenting pyrethroid resistance in this An. gambiae population
articles.title_original: Pyrethroid Resistance in Anopheles gambiae s.l.: A Focus on Permethrin and Deltamethrin for Malaria Vector Control.
articles.authors: Adesoye OA, Awoniyi AM, Adeogun AO

articles.headline: Study leveraged anonymized smartphone location data from 200+ malaria-infected individuals to identify transmission hotspots via density-based clustering (Sickness Positioning System). Field validation confirmed two previously unrecognized Anopheles breeding sites. Tool provided more precise, timely spatial assessment than Brazil's annual federal surveillance system
articles.title_original: Smartphone-based retrospective analysis for malaria hotspot detection.

articles.headline: Prospective study of 879 P. vivax/mixed malaria patients receiving 14-day primaquine (0.25 mg/kg/day) without G6PD testing in Ethiopia. Median hemoglobin fractional fall was −0.9% (day 3) and −0.8% (day 7); 9 patients had >25% drop but none developed hemolytic symptoms. By day 13, 91.9% achieved hematological recovery with no serious adverse events, supporting low-dose primaquine policy without G6PD screening if adverse-event monitoring systems exist
articles.title_original: Haematologic responses to primaquine used for radical cure of Plasmodium vivax in public health facilities in Ethiopia: a prospective observational study.

articles.headline: Study monitored biting rates of diverse Anopheles species (A. arabiensis, A. christyi, novel A. spp. 14) across three ecological sites in Kenya. Outdoor exposure peaked 1800–2100 h; indoor exposure peaked 1900–0100 h when people were awake or transitioning to sleep. Long-lasting insecticidal nets prevented 24.5–44.9% of bites (site-dependent), indicating significant residual transmission despite LLIN use
articles.title_original: Human and vector behavioural determinants of malaria transmission in the Nandi highlands, western Kenya.

articles.headline: Modeling study simulated malaria transmission across South Asia (Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Iran, India, Nepal, Pakistan) from 2020–2097 under stratospheric aerosol injection (GLENS-SAI stabilizing temperatures at 2020 levels) vs. unmitigated warming (RCP 8.5). GLENS-SAI reduced EIR in most regions but increased it in SE Pakistan, W Afghanistan, and parts of Iran/Nepal, highlighting need for region-specific control strategies
articles.title_original: Malaria transmission dynamics under climate change and solar geoengineering in South Asia: a GLENS-based assessment.
articles.authors: Hussain A, Shoaib M, Latif M

articles.headline: In silico study identified three glycogen synthase kinase-3β (pfGSK3β) inhibitors from CHEMBL database as potential P. falciparum drugs via molecular docking and 100 ns molecular dynamics simulations. Compound S56 showed superior binding affinity (−5.64 vs. −7.12 for control), lower root-mean-square deviation (1.0 vs. 4.5 nm), and comparable binding energy (−14.45 vs. −13.03 kJ/mol), supporting repurposing as antimalarial agents
articles.title_original: In silico investigation of novel Plasmodium Falciparum glycogen synthase kinase (pfGSk3β) inhibitors for the treatment of malaria infection.

articles.headline: Analysis of 170,245 malaria cases in Pará, Brazil (2018–2023) found cases declined during COVID-19 critical period (44,830 cases, 2020–2021) vs. pre-pandemic (79,125 cases, 2018–2019) but rebounded (47,502 cases, 2022–2023). P. falciparum prevalence increased 2.6% to 11.3%; cases among miners surged 17.2% to 58%. Disease seasonality was maintained throughout
articles.title_original: Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on malaria transmission in the state of Pará, Brazil.

articles.headline: Study identified CD44 as a host factor promoting P. falciparum invasion via forward genetic screening. Anti-CD44 antibody (BRIC 222) significantly enhanced invasion through CD44 cross-linking, increasing accessibility of the essential invasion receptor Basigin and enhancing binding of the PfRH5/PCRCR invasion complex, suggesting CD44 coordinates ligand–receptor interactions during RBC invasion
articles.title_original: CD44 cross-linking promotes Plasmodium falciparum invasion.

articles.headline: Study released Adir1.0, a curated catalog of 540 An. dirus whole-genome sequences, and updated Amin1.0 API for cloud-based genomic analysis of Southeast Asian malaria vectors. Initial exploratory analysis of An. dirus populations from Bangladesh, Thailand, and Cambodia identified population structure, candidate physiological resistance regions, and genomic structural variation to inform vector control strategies
articles.title_original: A genomic toolkit for surveillance and elimination of the principal malaria vectors in Southeast Asia.

articles.headline: Reviews ivermectin's role in malaria control, examining both its straightforward applications and associated complexities in disease control strategies
articles.title_original: Ivermectin's role in malaria control: the simplicity and the complexity.
articles.authors: Schwartz E, Orshan L, Schlagenhauf P

articles.headline: In murine malaria models, a formula combining arteannuin B, artemisinic acid, and scopoletin strengthened host defense by modulating phagocytic activity in splenic macrophages, dendritic cells, and natural killer cells via Fcγ receptor-mediated pathways, providing mechanistic insights into a novel ACT strategy to overcome artemisinin resistance
articles.title_original: Proteomics-driven screening of artemisinin-based combination ratios and mechanistic insights into Plasmodium berghei infection in mice.

articles.headline: Review of host defense peptides (defensins, cathelicidins, NK-2 peptide, platelet factor 4, MIP-3α, hepcidin) that target multiple Plasmodium developmental stages through direct antimicrobial activity and immunomodulation, highlighting their dual role in parasite control and immune response modulation, though specific roles and targets remain incompletely understood
articles.title_original: Host defense peptides in malaria infection: their contributions, significance and constraints.

articles.headline: Scoping review of 38 studies from Northern Ghana (2013–June 2023) on malaria control interventions. Most research focused on case management (28.9%), intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy (23.7%), and long-lasting insecticidal nets (15.8%), with limited evidence on combined intervention effects and malaria vaccines (2.6%)
articles.title_original: Malaria control in Northern Ghana: a scoping review.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 40 neonates with confirmed malaria (≤28 days) from 26 publications: P. falciparum (45%) and P. vivax (43%) were predominant. Only 28% of P. falciparum cases received artemisinin-based therapy; most received oral amodiaquine or chloroquine monotherapy. All patients survived with parasitemia clearance in 2–7 days, but management practices deviated substantially from current guidelines
articles.title_original: Management of malaria in newborns: a systematic review.

articles.headline: Literature review of outdoor malaria vector control interventions including larval source management, spatial emanators/repellents (recently WHO-endorsed for indoor use), topical repellents, endectocides, and gene drives. No universally applicable tool exists; integrated approaches combining complementary techniques with community engagement offer the best strategy
articles.title_original: Outdoor biting by malaria vectors: what are the options for intervention?

articles.headline: Assessment of Cambodia's malaria surveillance system using WHO toolkit on 68 indicators (2021–2023 data): 82% of Performance (14/19), 77% of Context and Infrastructure (8/13), 79% of Technical and Process (4/7), and 100% of Behavior indicators (3/3) were met. Notable improvements in completeness and timeliness of reporting and case investigation
articles.title_original: Key findings and recommendations from assessment of Cambodia's national malaria surveillance system.

articles.headline: Evaluation of naturally acquired anti-α-gal antibodies in UK volunteers (no significant increase at 90 days post-sporozoite challenge), Kenyan adults, and Burkina Faso infants (increases in IgG and IgM over time, protection correlated with anti-α-gal IgM). Pre-clinical testing of BSA-linked α-gal with Matrix-M adjuvant in mice elicited antibodies but failed to protect against sporozoite challenge
articles.title_original: Naturally acquired immune responses to alpha-gal in malaria endemic settings and pre-clinical efficacy testing with R21/MM.

articles.headline: Review of population-level malaria prevention strategies showing insecticide-treated nets reduced mortality most substantially but face insecticide resistance; chemoprevention protects tens of millions primarily in sub-Saharan Africa; R21/Matrix-M vaccine offers greater efficacy than RTS,S/AS01 with lower cost; monoclonal antibodies and gene drives offer potential. Multifaceted integration needed to reinvigorate progress toward elimination
articles.title_original: Advances in population-based interventions to control falciparum malaria.
articles.authors: Glossop SE, Peto TJ, Adhikari B
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: Ultrasensitive RT-PCR survey in North Sumatra: on Mursala Island, 10.9% (7/64) adults had P. knowlesi infections (5 agricultural workers, 2 with severe WHO anemia) and 3.1% had P. vivax, all negative by microscopy and rapid diagnostics. Mainland sites (n=947) showed 26% PCR-positive (17.5% P. vivax, 7.5% P. falciparum) with 30% submicroscopic; P. knowlesi transmission is low but can cause severe malaria
articles.title_original: Submicroscopic Burden of Zoonotic Plasmodium knowlesi Malaria on Mursala Island and Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax Transmission in Mainland North Sumatra, Indonesia.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional comparison of three national standard malaria rapid diagnostic tests (mRDTs) and one research mRDT against qPCR in Tanzania revealed differences in accuracy by age, sex, and regional transmission rate. Research test underperformed with poor sensitivity; transmission intensity may affect mRDT agreement, offering insights to reduce diagnostic bias in malaria prevalence estimation
articles.title_original: Varying Malaria Rapid Diagnostic Test Accuracy by Regional Transmission Level and Demographics in Tanzania.

articles.headline: Multi-tissue profiling (blood, spleen, liver) in mice infected with four P. yoelii strains and P. berghei ANKA on days 4–6 post-infection using LC-MS/MS showed glycolysis intermediates (bisphosphoglycerate, phosphoenolpyruvate, dihydroxyacetone phosphate) negatively correlated with parasitemia. Uridine supplementation significantly reduced parasitemia in PbA-infected mice and P. falciparum in vitro, suggesting metabolic interventions could enhance treatment
articles.title_original: Temporal multi-tissue profiling reveals metabolites linked to immune response and malaria parasitemia control.

articles.headline: Retrospective analysis of malaria cases in Korea's capital region (2022–2024): 620 cases reported in 2024 (94.1% of nationwide total), a 78.7% increase from 2022. Forty-seven case clusters identified, concentrated in high-risk border areas; primary risk factors were residence (57.8%), work (12.9%), military service (12.9%), and outdoor activities. Post-COVID rise linked to resumption of social activities
articles.title_original: Individual Malaria Case and Case Cluster Status in the Capital Region of the Republic of Korea, 2024.

articles.headline: Multi-method qualitative study examining barriers and facilitators to adopting multiple first-line ACTs in Tanzania found artemether-lumefantrine comprised 73.5% of imports versus 12% artesunate and 2.6% dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine. Malaria cases in children <5 declined significantly (p=0.011) but mortality did not improve (p=0.802) from 2020-2024. Key barriers included high drug costs and weak supply chains; implementation requires diversified ACT imports, strengthened supply chains, and capacity-building to extend ACT efficacy
articles.title_original: Barriers and facilitators to the adoption of multiple first-line therapies for management of uncomplicated malaria in Tanzania: a multi-method qualitative study.

articles.headline: Retrospective surveillance analysis (2020-2022) in Pemba Island, Zanzibar identified 1,411 (53.3%) of 2,646 malaria cases as imported, predominantly from Tanga (61%), with significantly higher rates among males (57.3%, p<0.0001) and adults >18 years (68%, p<0.0001). Imported cases were higher in 2021 (OR=3.58, p<0.001) and 2022 (OR=5.62, p<0.001) versus 2020. Human biting rate correlated with importation (84.81-unit increase per HBR unit, 95% CI 13.29-156.32, p=0.021), identifying Mkoani as most receptive area
articles.title_original: Epidemiological and entomological analysis of imported malaria in Pemba Island, Zanzibar: a study from 2020-2022.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional analysis of 7,124 Somali women from 2020 DHS found sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (IPTp-SP) use critically low at 6.3% overall, with paradoxically lower use among women attending ≥4 ANC visits (4.2%) versus 1-3 visits (10.5%) or none (1.8%). Overall ANC attendance was only 31%; multilevel analysis identified nulliparity (AOR=0.6), attending 4+ ANC visits (AOR=0.38), low household education, and poverty as protective factors against reported SP use, suggesting fundamental gaps in maternal healthcare delivery
articles.title_original: Factors associated with intermittent preventive treatment (IPTp-SP) use for malaria during pregnancy in Somalia: a multilevel analysis of the 2020 demographic and health survey.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 693 under-5 children in Nigerian internally displaced persons camps found 68.5% malaria prevalence. Random Forest machine learning model achieved best performance (AUC=0.892), with caregiver occupation and residential camp as most significant predictors. Caregiver lack of education increased odds (aOR=3.23, p=0.026), while female caregivers decreased odds (aOR=0.53, p=0.024). Despite 60.3% of caregivers having prevention knowledge, bed net usage was only 2%
articles.title_original: From risk factors to predictive modelling: applying machine learning to childhood malaria surveillance in resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: No abstract available. Title indicates a collaborative approach between Indonesia and Papua New Guinea for malaria control on New Guinea addressing persistent malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Two Nations, One Front: Indonesia and Papua New Guinea forge a One-Island approach to fight persistent malaria on New Guinea.

articles.headline: Time-lagged cross-correlation analysis (2000-2021) of district-level dengue and malaria in Loreto, Peru identified growing co-occurrence of both diseases over time.
