Northwest districts showed malaria increases preceding dengue increases, while Northeast showed malaria increases preceding dengue decreases. Spatial correlation of coefficients in the Northwest suggests geographic characteristics influence disease associations, informing targeted integrated vector control interventions
articles.title_original: Spatiotemporal co-distribution and time lagged cross correlation of malaria and dengue in Loreto, Peru.

articles.headline: Structure-based virtual screening of 1,830 compounds from medicinal fungi library identified two Plasmodium falciparum glutamyl-tRNA synthetase inhibitors: MSID000152 (binding affinity -10.4 kcal/mol, RMSD 3.94 Å) and MSID000974 (-10.1 kcal/mol, RMSD 2.35 Å). MSID000152 showed favorable binding free energy by MMPB/GBSA. Both compounds demonstrated stable protein-inhibitor complex dynamics over 100 ns molecular dynamics simulations, qualifying as promising antimalarial lead compounds for further investigation
articles.title_original: Rational design of novel Plasmodium falciparum glutamyl-tRNA synthetase inhibitors for the development of next-generation antimalarial drugs.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 178 observational studies (93,809 women, 2000-2024) found placental malaria (PM) prevalence of 25.1% (95% CI 22.0-28.3), with detection rates varying by method: RDT 16.9%, microscopy 19.1%, PCR 24.8%, histopathology 34.8%. Risk factors included younger age (OR=2.50), primigravidae (OR=2.65), absence of ITNs (OR=2.11), no ANC (OR=7.43), and no IPTp (OR=1.98). PM increased odds of maternal anemia (OR=2.18) and low birthweight (OR=1.71); gestational malaria prevalence was 22.1% and congenital malaria 7.6%
articles.title_original: Prevalence, predictors and adverse perinatal outcomes of placental malaria in sub-Saharan Africa: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Literature review synthesizing evidence for shift from chloroquine to artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs) for Plasmodium vivax in India. ACTs demonstrated superior efficacy to chloroquine with faster parasite clearance, better tolerability, effectiveness against mixed-species infections, and reduced relapse rates when combined with primaquine. India has sparse therapeutic efficacy study coverage and molecular resistance surveillance. Authors recommend expanding therapeutic efficacy studies and adopting ACT-based regimen to sustain P. vivax elimination momentum and prevent chloroquine resistance-driven therapeutic failure
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax elimination from India may need therapeutic efficacy studies - Informed shift to artemisinin-based treatment.

articles.headline: Biochemical characterization of Plasmodium berghei proteasome regulatory subunit Rpt3 demonstrated binding to protein phosphatase 1 catalytic subunit (PP1c) with enhanced PP1c activity in vitro and in Xenopus oocyte model. RVxF motifs and ATP-binding capacity of PbRpt3 contribute to phosphatase regulatory function. Reverse genetic studies and interactome analysis confirmed PbRpt3 is essential during the erythrocytic cycle and belongs to the 19S regulatory particle, suggesting critical roles in parasite protein regulation
articles.title_original: Characterization of the Regulatory AAA-ATPase Subunit Rpt3 in Plasmodium berghei as an Activator of Protein Phosphatase 1.

articles.headline: In vitro study of hemozoin (malaria pigment) effects on macrophage polarization found HZ induced M1/pro-inflammatory phenotype in non-polarized primary human monocyte-derived macrophages and THP-1-derived macrophages, increasing CXCL8, TNF-α, and IL-1β expression and secretion. HZ further amplified CXCL8 and IL-1β in M1/M2-stimulated conditions but showed no effect on M2 markers (PPARG, IL-10, STAT6 phosphorylation). Model-specific effects were observed, highlighting the importance of experimental model choice in studying HZ's role in severe malaria pathogenesis
articles.title_original: Malaria pigment hemozoin drives M1 pro-inflammatory macrophage polarization in vitro.

articles.headline: Nationally representative cross-sectional survey of 2,112 Sierra Leoneans (2022) using qPCR found 63.0% (95% CI 61.0-65.1) Plasmodium positivity, with P. falciparum comprising 97.75% of 400 typed samples and 20.5% carrying multiple species. Low parasitemia occurred in 18.6% and 93.5% of infected individuals were asymptomatic. Positivity was lower in those ≥60 years, higher in males, and similar between urban and rural areas. High prevalence of asymptomatic low-density infections indicates sustained transmission reservoir requiring molecular diagnosis for effective surveillance
articles.title_original: Molecular investigation of Plasmodium spp. in Sierra Leone: recommendations on diagnostic methods for malaria control in high-epidemic regions - a nationally representative cross-sectional epidemiological survey.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 386 pregnant women with/without malaria in the Amazon (2013–2015) measured plasma angiogenic proteins at delivery. Tie2, sFlt1, sENG, and leptin were associated with newborn growth parameters; sFlt1 correlated with reduced weight and length in P. vivax-infected women, while Tie2, sFlt1, and leptin correlated with reduced length in P. falciparum-infected women. Identifying maternal proteins may improve understanding of malaria-associated intrauterine growth restriction in low-transmission areas
articles.title_original: Association between peripheral markers in women with malaria in pregnancy and small newborns: A cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Prospective cross-sectional study of 222 pregnant women in Gabon (May 2022–May 2023) screened for HBV and P. falciparum co-infection. HBV prevalence 4.05%, with 1.35% co-infected with P. falciparum (3/222); P. falciparum mono-infection 26.1% (58/222). Elevated AST in HBV mono-infected women but normal liver enzymes in co-infected; inverse but non-significant relationship between HBV surface antigen and parasitemia (ρ = –0.5, p = 0.67) suggests potential biological antagonism warranting integrated screening during pregnancy
articles.title_original: Hepatitis B Virus and Plasmodium falciparum Co-Infection Among Pregnant Women in Gabon: Prevalence, Biochemical Impact, and Antagonistic Replication Dynamics.

articles.headline: Experimental study evaluating post-infection dietary treatments on Anopheles darlingi and A. albimanus mosquitoes infected with P. berghei. Dietary para-aminobenzoic acid (PABA), alone or combined with uric acid and penicillin/streptomycin, modulated vector susceptibility in both species; sporozoites were detected in hemolymph by day 28 but not in salivary glands until day 28. First demonstration that post-infection dietary treatments influence American vector susceptibility to P. berghei
articles.title_original: Dietary para-aminobenzoic acid, uric acid, and antibiotics modulate the susceptibility of Anopheles darlingi and Anopheles albimanus to Plasmodium berghei.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study analyzed k13 propeller domain in 52 P. falciparum isolates from Ethiopia (May–December 2023) using Sanger sequencing. No k13 mutations detected; high genetic conservation across samples (pairwise similarity 0.982) with no evidence of artemisinin resistance markers. Findings indicate continued effectiveness of artemisinin-based combination therapy in the region and support ongoing molecular surveillance
articles.title_original: Preliminary analysis of the conserved Plasmodium falciparum k13 gene in Arbaminch and Mirab Abaya, Ethiopia.

articles.headline: In silico-designed multi-epitope P. falciparum diagnostic antigen (PfMEDA1) validated with ELISA using 223 infected and 81 non-infected serum samples from Cameroon. Diagnostic sensitivity 89.69% (95% CI: 84.93–93.35), specificity 75.31% (95% CI: 64.47–84.22), Cohen kappa 0.64. PfMEDA1 demonstrates potential as a diagnostic antigen, though field validation in varied-endemicity settings is needed
articles.title_original: In silico design and validation of a Plasmodium falciparum multi-epitope diagnostic antigen (PfMEDA1) for the diagnosis of falciparum malaria.

articles.headline: Case-control investigation of malaria outbreak in Mbale District, Uganda (January–June 2019) identified 8,827 confirmed cases (attack rate 33%, higher in females 36% vs males 27%; peak in 5–18-year-olds at 2.6%). Stagnant water in erosion control pits strongly associated with infection (95% cases vs 49% controls, OR = 18, 95% CI 7.0–50); sleeping under bed nets protective (OR = 0.17, 95% CI 0.07–0.38). Outbreak driven by vector breeding sites created by conservation practice sustained by intermittent rainfall
articles.title_original: Malaria outbreak facilitated by increased vector-breeding sites from erosion control pits sustained by intermittent rainfall, Mbale District, Uganda, January-June 2019.

articles.headline: Metagenomic analysis using Hidden Markov Models to characterize viral pathogens in non-malaria acute febrile illness, applied to simulated and existing infectious disease datasets (Ebola, hepatitis C, chikungunya, arboviruses). Analysis identified multiple viral etiological agents co-occurring within single disease samples. Approach enables granular detection of concurrent viral pathogens to improve disease surveillance and case management in endemic regions
articles.title_original: NOMAD: metagenomic characterisation of the viral pathogen composition in outbreaks of non-malaria acute febrile illness cases.

articles.headline: Functional genetic study of P. falciparum pyrimidine biosynthesis pathway enzymes (PfATC, PfCPSII, PfDHO). PfATC knockdown proved lethal; N-terminal truncation impaired growth. PfCPSII confirmed essential. PfDHO deletion caused uracil auxotrophy, confirming essential function. Study reveals multiple vulnerable pathway nodes exploitable for antimalarial drug development
articles.title_original: Genetic analysis of pyrimidine biosynthetic enzymes in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: In vitro testing of novel heterospirocyclic compounds (25 and 26) against drug-resistant P. falciparum strains. Both compounds displayed antimalarial activity within 24 hours against artemisinin- and multidrug-resistant parasites. Untargeted metabolomics suggests mechanism involves disruption of pyrimidine biosynthesis and hemoglobin catabolism. No major toxicity in kidney or hepatic cell lines, positioning these compounds as promising candidates for drug-resistant malaria treatment
articles.title_original: Novel heterospirocyclic antimalarials with activity against artemisinin- and multidrug-resistant P. falciparum malaria.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist

articles.headline: Case report of young Danish boy who developed cerebral malaria and multiorgan failure after travel to Indonesia without chemoprophylaxis. Flu-like symptoms delayed diagnosis. Rapid IV artesunate treatment resulted in full recovery. Case illustrates critical importance of appropriate pre-travel advice, early symptom recognition, and prompt malaria testing for travelers to endemic areas
articles.title_original: [Cerebral malaria after travel to Indonesia].

articles.headline: Geospatial analysis of DHS data from eight West African countries quantified malaria prevalence in children under five at 10×10 km resolution. Insecticide-treated net use significantly reduced odds of infection in 4/8 countries; younger age (<2 years) consistently protective across all countries. Marked spatial heterogeneity observed, with highest predicted prevalence in Benin and Côte d'Ivoire. Findings emphasize need for context-specific, geospatially-informed vector control strategies
articles.title_original: Malaria hotspots and risk factors among children under-five years of age across eight West African countries: A geospatial analysis of DHS data.

articles.headline: Time-series analysis of 12 months of monthly malaria data (2011–2022) from Upper River Region, Gambia correlated with meteorological variables. Each 1% increase in relative humidity increased malaria odds by 3–4% (<5 years) and 6–9% (>5 years); each 1% increase in maximum temperature decreased odds by 14–20% (<5 years) and 15–21% (>5 years). Relative humidity identified as major transmission driver; findings support aligning malaria control programs with climatic indicators
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the significant association between Climatic Variables and Malaria Cases in the Upper River Region of the Gambia.

articles.headline: Genome-wide SNP analysis of 110 Ethiopian P. vivax isolates (38 Duffy-negative, 72 Duffy-positive) revealed Duffy-positive parasites had 477,561 SNPs versus 197,461 in Duffy-negative parasites. Duffy-positive isolates showed positive selection in drug-resistance genes (CG1, RPN2) and erythrocyte-binding gene MAEBL, while Duffy-negative isolates had selection in organellar and vesicle-trafficking genes. Duffy-negative P. vivax maintained reduced genomic diversity with high conservation, indicating strong selective constraints and specialized host-parasite interactions required for Duffy-negative RBC invasion
articles.title_original: Adaptive evolution of Plasmodium vivax in Duffy-negative hosts: insights from East African genomes.

articles.headline: Female An. aquasalis infected with P. vivax were exposed to control and three IPCC climate scenarios (RCP 4.5, 6.0, 8.5). Infection rates were similar (54–68%) across all conditions. Mosquito survival declined under RCP 6.0 (18.6% by day 7) and RCP 8.5 (6.5% by day 7) versus 40% controls (p<0.001), with altered immune gene expression under higher stress. Future climate stress may compromise vector fitness despite maintained infectivity
articles.title_original: Predicted future climate scenarios impact survival and immune response but not Plasmodium vivax infection in Anopheles aquasalis (Diptera, Culicidae), the primary vector of coastal Central and South America and the Caribbean Islands.

articles.headline: Narrative review of P. falciparum malaria treatment in nonendemic countries covering artemisinin-based combination therapy and atovaquone-proguanil. Key challenges include artemisinin-related hemolysis and increasing treatment failures possibly related to drug resistance. Authors propose adaptations for travelers, including structured clinical follow-up to detect toxicity and failure, and systematic genomic surveillance, noting nonimmune populations face greater vulnerability to complications
articles.title_original: Treatment of Plasmodium falciparum malaria in the nonendemic setting: a narrative review of toxicity, failure, and emerging resistance.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 390 residents in Woreta Town, Ethiopia (January–June 2024) on traditional medicine for malaria treatment. Results: 259 (66.4%) believed combining traditional and modern medicine beneficial; 254 (65.1%) knew anti-malarial plants (Bisana 27.9%, Girawa 26.7%, Nechshinkurt 14.6%, papaya 4.6%, Shifarraw 20.1%); 33 (19.3%) mentioned animal byproducts. Authors conclude high community awareness of traditional remedies with recognition of integrated TM-modern medicine approach
articles.title_original: Assessment of knowledge, attitudes, and practices regarding the use of traditional medicine for treating malaria among residents of Woreta Town, Amhara Regional State, Northwest Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Therapeutic efficacy study in 1,078 children with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria in Burkina Faso (2020–2021, 28-day follow-up). Uncorrected 28-day efficacy: artemether-lumefantrine 57.1–64.4%, dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine 96.6–98.9%, artesunate-pyronaridine 82.2–90.7%. PCR-corrected efficacy exceeded WHO 90% threshold for all drugs except artemether-lumefantrine in Nanoro (89.4%). Authors recommend multiple first-line non-artemether-lumefantrine ACT to preserve lumefantrine and improve outcomes
articles.title_original: Efficacy of artemether-lumefantrine, dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine, and artesunate-pyronaridine for the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Burkina Faso, 2020-2021.

articles.headline: Abstracts from the 9th International Conference on Plasmodium vivax Research (ICPvR) 2025; no specific study results available
articles.title_original: Abstracts of the 9th International Conference on Plasmodium vivax Research (ICPvR) 2025.

articles.headline: Prospective reactive case detection study of 1,990 contacts (≥5 years) of 51 P. falciparum index cases in Myanmar (2017–2018). Among contacts, 1.4% tested positive by conventional RDT, 3.9% by highly sensitive RDT (hsRDT), and 8.6% by ultra-sensitive PCR. hsRDT sensitivity 27.9% (95% CI 18.2–39.6%), specificity 98.8%; conventional RDT sensitivity 13.7%, specificity 100% (p<0.01). hsRDTs identified more infected contacts but have low sensitivity versus PCR; use should consider field feasibility and cost-effectiveness
articles.title_original: Assessment of a highly sensitive rapid diagnostic test for reactive case detection of Plasmodium Falciparum infections in Myanmar.

articles.headline: Micro-costing study across 15 Burundi sites estimated cost per malaria episode in children <5: societal US$12.1 (household US$3.3 out-of-pocket + US$4.7 productivity losses over 8 caregiving days; health system US$2.6 outpatient, US$44.0 inpatient). Household costs equaled 44% monthly individual income; health system costs represented nearly half per-capita government health expenditure. Authors conclude substantial burden requires investment in preventive strategies and new interventions like vaccines
articles.title_original: Economic burden of malaria in children under the age of five in Burundi.

articles.headline: Fractional-order mathematical model for malaria dynamics in Northern Cyprus using Mittag-Leffler kernel with eight-compartmental structure (human and mosquito subpopulations). Qualitative analysis established existence, uniqueness, and global stability via Lyapunov functions. Numerical simulations indicated asymptotically stable endemic equilibrium with increasing susceptible human populations and declining infectious mosquito numbers. Authors conclude major efforts needed to reduce malaria
articles.title_original: Fractal fractional approach to investigate the forecasting and dynamics of malaria disease for early control precaution.

articles.headline: Spatiotemporal mapping of malaria across 700+ Indian districts (2019–2023) revealed 0.33 million cases in 2019 with pandemic-driven reduction. P. falciparum widespread in North-eastern and Central India; post-pandemic P. vivax geographic expansion into north-eastern regions observed. Vulnerable populations: scheduled castes, scheduled tribes, economically marginalized groups. Associated factors: drinking water practices, maternal education, healthcare accessibility. Authors developed public digital dashboard for district-level tracking
articles.title_original: Mapping malaria risk in India between 2019 and 2023: a tool for the public to track malaria.

articles.headline: Analysis of 2021 Niger Malaria Indicator Survey with 4,724 children aged 6–59 months; 1,121 (23.7%) RDT-positive. Malaria significantly clustered (Moran's I=0.434, p<0.001). Prevalence associated with child age, anemia levels, maternal education, bed net usage, number of children <5, residence, and region. Authors conclude demographic and behavioral factors (especially insecticide-treated net coverage) require tailored interventions based on local conditions
articles.title_original: Exploring spatial variation and multilevel modeling of malaria prevalence among children aged 6-59 months based on RDT in Niger: Insights for public health decision-making.
articles.authors: Mulugeta SS, Masresha BM, Ejigu LA

articles.headline: Longitudinal analysis of care-seeking delays in febrile patients with suspected malaria: Myanmar (n=2,960, mean fever 1.8 days) and Thailand (n=15,576, mean fever 2.9 days). Myanmar delays associated with Shan ethnicity (TR 1.48), farmers (TR 1.12), uncertain malaria history (TR 1.26), P. falciparum (TR 1.10), P. vivax (TR 1.23). Thailand: farmers (TR 1.07), pre/primary education (TR 1.02), P. falciparum (TR 1.27), P. vivax (TR 1.20). Shorter delays with prior malaria episodes and bed net use
articles.title_original: Drivers of delay in reported malaria care-seeking in Myanmar and Thailand.

articles.headline: MAP-X thermal proteome profiling in intact P. falciparum cells across seven intraerythrocytic development cycle timepoints mapped >20,000 protein-protein interactions, resolving conserved protein complexes, reproducing known interactions, and identifying previously unreported associations and moonlighting subunits that dissociate from native complexes to perform alternative biological functions
articles.title_original: MAP-X reveals distinct protein complex dynamics across Plasmodium falciparum blood stages.

articles.headline: Immunoproteomic profiling identified a RIFIN subset bearing a conserved LILRB1-binding motif (disulfide-bonded cysteines) associated with reduced severe malaria risk; LILRB1 binds both RIFINs and MHC-I via overlapping interfaces, suggesting vaccine design targeting this structurally constrained functional site could overcome RIFIN sequence diversity while disrupting immune evasion
articles.title_original: A subset of Plasmodium falciparum RIFINs is linked to severe malaria risk reduction and engages LILRB1 through a conserved structural motif.

articles.headline: Among 320 Chadians with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria, HbS allele frequency was 6.72%, with no significant association between HbS genotype and parasite density, confirming a non-negligible HbS prevalence in this population
articles.title_original: Allele frequency of Hemoglobin S among patients with uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in N'Djamena, Chad.

articles.headline: In a cluster-randomized trial in Mozambique (1,951 participants across 39 clusters), ivermectin 400 μg/kg monthly reduced scabies prevalence from 8.10% baseline to 2.11% at 3 months versus albendazole control (13.71%), with aOR 0.18 (95% CI 0.07–0.45, p=0.0002); protective effects extended to directly exposed participants and indirectly exposed children <5 years (aOR 0.16–0.21, p<0.05)
articles.title_original: Direct and indirect protection against scabies through ivermectin mass drug administration designed for malaria in Mozambique: a substudy nested within a cluster-randomised, controlled trial.

articles.headline: Review highlighting how expanding knowledge of Plasmodium liver-stage immunity mechanisms is shaping current and future malaria interventions, emphasizing that blocking parasite entrance or exit through the liver stage prevents both illness and onward transmission
articles.title_original: Exposing the hidden: establishing immunity to Plasmodium liver stage infection.

articles.headline: Case report of 19-year-old woman with P. falciparum cerebral malaria presenting with sudden unconsciousness without reported fever in an endemic area; rapid diagnostic test positive, microscopy confirmed infection; treated with intravenous artesunate followed by primaquine and dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine with clinical improvement and confirmed parasite clearance
articles.title_original: When malaria hides: a case report of sudden coma without prior fever in cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional analysis of 18,747 Tanzanians found malaria knowledge varied significantly (p<0.05) by age (highest 20–24 years, lowest >50), location (urban>rural), education level, media exposure, and mobile phone ownership, indicating targeted education and improved rural health infrastructure are essential for effective control
articles.title_original: Evaluating the General Public's Knowledge of Malaria: A Nationally Representative Cross-Sectional Study.

articles.headline: Among 587 Saudi children, HbS carriers had OR 0.40 (95% CI 0.22–0.72, p=0.002) for severe versus no malaria; HbC carriers showed stronger protection (P<0.01); blood group A was more frequent in severe cases; uncomplicated malaria showed higher total and subclass IgG levels than severe malaria or uninfected controls (P<0.001)
articles.title_original: Distribution of HbS, HbC variants, ABO blood groups in relation to malaria infection among Saudi children in Jazan region.

articles.headline: In 466 Malawian pregnant women, a simplified diaminofluorene-based CSA adhesion inhibition assay (r=0.82 reproducibility) identified adhesion-inhibitory antibodies as a potential correlate of placental malaria protection: infected multigravidae had 46.3% median activity versus 9.7% in primigravidae (p<0.001), and those without placental malaria had 33.8% activity versus 12.5% with past infection (p=0.002)
articles.title_original: A high-throughput assay to measure antibodies that block adhesion of Plasmodium falciparum infected erythrocytes to chondroitin sulfate A.

articles.headline: Modelling study across three African transmission settings estimated monthly dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine IPT for school-age children averted 70–90% of cases in treated children (~2.0/child/year) and 20–60% in younger children and adults (~0.5/person/year), with greater benefit at lower transmission levels; shorter-acting drugs or less frequent dosing averted 40–60% and 15–50% respectively
articles.title_original: Effect of malaria chemoprevention for school-age children across transmission archetypes: a modelling study.

articles.headline: Mali case-control RNA-seq study found significant differential expression of fikk, phist, and surf multigene families in cerebral malaria and concurrent disease but minimal expression differences in severe malarial anemia; children with severe malaria developed antibody responses to subsets of these antigens, suggesting roles in pathogenesis and potential as vaccine targets
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum gene expression signatures and antibody profiling implicate the fikk, phist, and surf multigene families in severe malaria syndromes.

articles.headline: Review of P. falciparum drug resistance molecular markers covering Kelch13 mutations (artemisinin), cytochrome b mutations (atovaquone), PfCRT/PfMDR1/Plasmepsin II–III (4-aminoquinoline), DHFR/DHPS (antifolate), and PfMRP1/PfNHE1 (amino alcohol) mutations; recommends transcriptomic and proteomic research, combination therapies targeting multiple lifecycle phases, and strengthened global monitoring to track drug-resistant strains
articles.title_original: A review on molecular markers of Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Literature review (83 studies, 2018–2024) of P. falciparum Pfhrp2/3 deletions, which undermine RDT-based diagnosis in sub-Saharan Africa. Review identifies diagnostic and control challenges and proposes alternative approaches including novel RDTs targeting multiple antigens to overcome deletion-driven false negatives
articles.title_original: Overcoming the challenges of Plasmodium falciparum histidine-rich protein 2 and 3 deletions in malaria diagnosis and control.

articles.headline: Genetic analysis of pvmsp-3α in 129 Pakistani P. vivax isolates (2020–2021) showed Pakistani parasites exhibited the highest genetic diversity globally, driven by size polymorphisms in block I and amino acid changes/recombination in block II. Substantial heterogeneity in global P. vivax populations limits pvmsp-3α vaccine utility without multi-strain coverage
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphisms of merozoite surface protein-3α in Plasmodium vivax isolates from Pakistan.

articles.headline: Investigation of PvCyRPA in P. vivax VCG-1 strain demonstrated the protein is transcribed in late schizonts, localizes to the membrane, binds both reticulocytes and normocytes, and is recognized by >90% of sera from malaria-exposed individuals. Strong antigenicity and erythrocyte binding support PvCyRPA as a vaccine candidate for P. vivax
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax cysteine-rich protective antigen (PvCyRPA), an important element for vaccine strategies targeting P. vivax, interacts with human erythrocyte surface.

articles.headline: P. yoelii infection in CT26.WT colon cancer-bearing BALB/c mice (0.2 mL of 5×10⁶/mL inoculum) significantly downregulated HIF-1α and VEGFA (P<0.01) and upregulated VHL and p53 (P<0.01) in tumor tissue; tumors ceased growing by day 21 post-inoculation and shrank significantly (P<0.001), demonstrating Plasmodium-mediated tumor growth inhibition via HIF-1α pathway modulation
articles.title_original: Plasmodium yoelii infection inhibits colon cancer in mice via modulation of hypoxia-inducible factor-1α expression.

articles.headline: Retrospective screening of 85 pregnant migrant women in Verona, Italy (Feb 2023–Mar 2025) identified malaria in 5/29 tested (17.2%, all asymptomatic/mildly symptomatic), Schistosoma seropositivity in 27.8%, Strongyloides in 5.9%, and HTLV-1/2 in 5.0%. Risk-based testing reveals significant neglected infection burden in pregnant migrant populations not captured by routine antenatal screening
articles.title_original: Malaria and other neglected infections in pregnant migrants: A retrospective study in Northern Italy.

articles.headline: In vitro study of ART metabolism in ART-sensitive (Pf3D7/PfDd2) and ART-resistant P. falciparum (Pf6320, K13 C580Y) showed ART-heme formation was time- and concentration-dependent in sensitive strains (50–450 nM) but concentration-independent in resistant strains; ATP-dependent transport via P-type ATPase. ART-heme may serve as biomarker for resistance, and efflux transporter inhibition synergizes with ART
articles.title_original: Elucidating artemisinin (ART)-heme as a biomarker for ART resistance and ATP-dependent transport of ART in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Review of T follicular helper (Tfh) cell development during malaria. Plasmodium infection induces nonconventional Tfh subsets and impairs sterilizing immunity, yet mechanisms governing Tfh memory differentiation and phenotypic heterogeneity during infection remain poorly understood; identifies key knowledge gaps for improving vaccine and therapeutic responses
articles.title_original: T follicular helper cell development during malaria.
articles.authors: Antonetti RM, Shah M, Butler NS

articles.headline: Single-cell RNA sequencing of P. falciparum and Anopheles cells across developmental stages identified PfSIP2 transcription factor as essential for sporozoite hepatocyte infection. Parasites preferentially interact with midgut progenitor cells during epithelial crossing and establish tight connections with muscle cells; findings replicated in multiple mosquito-parasite combinations including field isolates
articles.title_original: Mapping Plasmodium transitions and interactions in the Anopheles female.

articles.headline: Compound 31 (2-hydroxyphenyl benzamide) displays potent in vitro activity against blood-stage and sexual-stage P. falciparum with no human cell toxicity. Resistance mediated by novel pfmdr1 point mutation (confirmed by CRISPR/Cas9); no cross-resistance to first-line antimalarials. Mode of action: direct binding to ribosomal subunits inhibiting protein synthesis
articles.title_original: A Novel Antimalarial Agent that Inhibits Protein Synthesis in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 601 Guinean samples (2017 survey): sickle cell trait (SCT) carriers comprised 32% of population and had significantly higher microscopic P. falciparum infection risk overall (OR=2.3, 95% CI 1.4–3.5) and in 15–24 year-olds (OR=5.0, 95% CI 1.8–13.8), but infected SCT carriers <5 years had lower parasitemia and higher hemoglobin than non-carriers—indicating age-dependent paradox in SCT protection
articles.title_original: Association between sickle cell trait and Plasmodium falciparum infection: evidence for age-specific protection in a cross-sectional study in Guinea-Bissau.

articles.headline: Review of sex determination mechanisms in Plasmodium gametocytes involving two checkpoints: chromatin remodeling-driven AP2-G activation triggering sexual commitment and gene expression, followed by post-transcriptional processes (RNA-binding proteins, transcript stabilization) that direct male vs. female differentiation via threshold-dependent mechanisms
articles.title_original: Pink or blue: sex determination in malaria gametocytes.

articles.headline: Systematic review (PRISMA 2020) of 24 studies (9 human, 15 experimental) on GFAP in cerebral malaria: experimental models showed consistently elevated GFAP correlating with astrocyte activation and neuroinflammation, but human studies were variable with several reporting no significant plasma GFAP elevation in CM vs. controls. GFAP shows promise as neuroinflammation biomarker but lacks diagnostic specificity; standardized methods and longitudinal studies needed
articles.title_original: Current status of the GFAP's potentials in cerebral malaria, a systematic review.

articles.headline: Review of P. falciparum modulation of erythrocyte proteome during blood-stage development, including parasite remodeling of RBC cytoplasm, surface, and deformability. Discusses targeting host-proteome engagements for host-directed antimalarial therapy development
articles.title_original: Crosstalk between malaria and host proteome during the intraerythrocytic developmental cycle.

articles.headline: Study demonstrating that vesicle adaptors AP-1, AP-3, and AP-4 are essential for parasite survival: AP-1 required for intracellular growth and specialized organelle biogenesis; AP-3 and AP-4 required for host cell invasion. Adaptor sorting machinery shows conservation with model organisms but includes unconventional features such as tepsin functioning with AP-1 and clathrin with AP-4
articles.title_original: Vesicle adaptors in malaria parasites show conservation and flexibility of protein sorting machinery.

articles.headline: Spatio-temporal expression profiling of P. berghei chloroquine resistance transporter (CRT) using fluorescent tagging. CRT expressed during asexual blood stage (localizing to digestive vacuole), gametocytes, and ookinetes through mosquito midgut colonization; expression silenced during sporogony and early liver infection but reactivated in mature liver stages
articles.title_original: Stage-dependent expression and vacuolar localization of Plasmodium berghei chloroquine resistance transporter (CRT).

articles.headline: Retrospective study of 98 South Korean patients receiving intravenous artesunate (2014–2023) developing risk-scoring system for treatment failure prediction. Treatment failure (WHO-defined) occurred in 12.2%. Independent predictors: female sex, parasitemia >5%, impaired consciousness. Risk scores 0–4 predicted failure rates of 5%, 16%, 41%, 72%, 90% respectively (AUROC=0.768, 95% CI: 0.605–0.931)
articles.title_original: Development of a clinical risk scoring system for artesunate treatment failure prediction in malaria patients.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist

articles.headline: Review of global drug-resistant malaria threat examining molecular mechanisms of antimalarial resistance, its impact on malaria control programs, and urgent need for real-time surveillance systems to guide improved treatment strategies
articles.title_original: The evolving threat of drug-resistant malaria in a globalized world.

articles.headline: Review of telomere dynamics in malaria pathogenesis. Acute malaria causes rapid telomere degradation, accelerated cellular senescence, and telomerase suppression with partial recovery during treatment. Prolonged endemic exposure may exacerbate pathogenesis and increase age-related disease susceptibility. Discusses oxidative stress and inflammation as mechanisms and telomere length as potential biomarker for malaria severity
articles.title_original: Malaria infection and telomere length: A review.

articles.headline: Molecular surveillance of 1582 P. falciparum isolates from Malawi (December 2020–June 2021). No pfk13 or pfcrt mutations detected, supporting artemisinin and chloroquine susceptibility. pfmdr1-NFD haplotype (reduced lumefantrine susceptibility) present in 42.9% of isolates. Quadruple pfdhfr-pfdhps mutant haplotype (high-level sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance) in 87.5% of isolates. Absence of pfcrt mutations suggests chloroquine-sensitive parasite re-emergence
articles.title_original: Emergence of chloroquine-sensitive Plasmodium falciparum and rising resistance to first-line artemisinin partner drugs in Malawi.

articles.headline: Development of chimeric FIKK kinase (C-FIKK) as P. falciparum diagnostic target using antigenic B- and T-cell epitopes. Anti-FIKK9.1 antibodies achieved 3 μM detection limit with high species specificity (no cross-reactivity with bacterial, viral, fungal, algal organisms or host blood components). Validation in mock patient samples showed 95.45% average accuracy for semi-quantitative parasite load detection
articles.title_original: Antigenic determinant analysis of FIKK Kinase family to identify novel candidates for diagnosis of Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Phase IV randomized trial in 350 Nigerian patients aged 12–65 comparing arterolane maleate-piperaquine (150 mg+750 mg once daily) versus artemether-lumefantrine (80 mg+480 mg twice daily) for 3 days in uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria. PCR-corrected adequate clinical and parasitological response (ACPR) 100% on Day 28 in both arms. PCR-uncorrected ACPR comparable: AMP 98.2% versus AL 99.4% Day 28 (p=0.352). No drug-related adverse events
articles.title_original: Arterolane and piperaquine Vs. artemether and lumefantrine in uncomplicated malaria: A randomized study in Nigeria.

articles.headline: Physiologically-based pharmacokinetic (PBPK) modeling of pyronaridine in 52 healthy subjects and 25 malaria patients. Models incorporating lysosomal sequestration and parasite compartments scaled by parasitemia demonstrated free pyronaridine concentrations in parasite compartment >10-fold higher than whole blood. Higher parasitemia on day 1 associated with increased area under curve (AUC₀₋₂₄) and maximum concentration (Cmax); subsequent hemoglobin decline had opposite effect
articles.title_original: Importance of Lysosomal Trapping and Plasmodium Parasite Infection on the Pharmacokinetics of Pyronaridine: A Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic Model-Based Study.

articles.headline: In vitro and in vivo evaluation of ONX-0914, a dual-targeting proteasome inhibitor. In vitro: potent inhibition of chloroquine-sensitive/resistant and artesunate-sensitive/resistant P. falciparum strains with IC50 <50 nM, low human cell toxicity (CC50 >100 μM), and synergistic activity with artesunate. In vivo (P. berghei ANKA): >95% parasite suppression after 4 days; <2% recrudescence after one month; ED50 7.62 mg/kg oral and 6.52 mg/kg intraperitoneal; bioavailability 23.83%
articles.title_original: Dual-targeting proteasome inhibitor ONX-0914 demonstrates potent antiplasmodial activity for malaria treatment.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist

articles.headline: Post-mortem pathology study in 50 Malawian children (27 cerebral malaria, 23 non-malarial death) examining expression of 5 P. falciparum cytoadhesion receptors in 12 brain regions. ICAM-1 most widely expressed, followed by CD36, VCAM-1, E-selectin, and thrombospondin. Expression highest in frontal lobe, lowest in brainstem and cerebellum. Sequestration ratios approximately 100% across all brain regions. Greater receptor expression in brain versus peripheral organs
articles.title_original: Mapping the expression of endothelial adhesion receptors for Plasmodium falciparum-infected erythrocytes in fatal cerebral malaria in Malawian children.

articles.headline: In 148 symptomatic P. vivax malaria cases from Pará, Brazil, TLR9 rs352140TT homozygotes showed higher parasitaemia than C carriers (p=0.034), and TLR4 rs1927911GG homozygotes had higher clinical index than A carriers (p=0.018). This first describes the rs1927911 TLR4 variant association with P. vivax malaria symptoms in Brazilian Amazonian populations
articles.title_original: New Evidence of TLR4 and TLR9 Variants Influencing Parasitaemia and Symptoms of Plasmodium vivax Infection.

articles.headline: Perspective review: ACKR1/Duffy antigen, long recognized for P. vivax erythrocyte invasion, plays emerging roles in inflammation, cancer, hematopoiesis, and other diseases with potential therapeutic applications beyond malaria parasitology
articles.title_original: The emerging roles of ACKR1/Duffy antigen beyond malaria.
articles.authors: Shaikh R, Ghosh K, Gorakshakar A

articles.headline: A multiepitope fusion antigen (MEFA) vaccine construct based on conserved STEVOR domains from P. falciparum achieved 97.15% global HLA coverage and recognized significantly more anti-STEVOR IgG in nonsevere versus severe malaria sera. Preclinical testing induced CD4+/CD8+ T-cell proliferation and IgG antibodies in mice, positioning STEVOR MEFA as a promising severe malaria vaccine candidate
articles.title_original: Conserved multiepitopes in Plasmodium falciparum STEVORs enable rational design of a fusion antigen vaccine construct with broad immunogenicity.

articles.headline: Crude extract and fractions from Lepidobotrys staudtii demonstrated potent antiplasmodial activity against P. falciparum (Pf3D7 drug-sensitive and PfINDO chloroquine-resistant strains), with hexane and ethyl acetate fractions most active (IC50 3.73 and 3.4 μg/mL). Ethyl acetate fraction showed rapid action at 12 hours and no cytotoxicity up to 500 μg/mL, indicating promise as an antimalarial source
articles.title_original: In vitro antiplasmodial activities of extract and fractions from Lepidobotrys staudtii against sensitive and resistant blood-stage parasites of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist

articles.headline: Molecular docking and dynamics simulations of P. falciparum PfCRT and PfDHFR variants with piperaquine revealed PfCRT-K76A complex binding affinity of −9.5 kcal/mol with moderate structural deviation (0.970 ± 0.202 nm), while PfDHFR-N51I showed stronger binding (−10.8 kcal/mol) but greater fluctuation, suggesting how these mutations contribute to artemisinin-based combination therapy resistance
articles.title_original: Computational investigation of mutations in PfCRT and PfDHFR proteins for emerging resistance of Plasmodium falciparum to antimalarial drugs.

articles.headline: In vitro and computational characterization of P. falciparum Hsp90 C-terminus revealed specific ATP binding, with associated nucleotides ATP, dATP, and ADP but not AMP or GTP, providing foundation for developing selective C-terminal Hsp90 inhibitors as novel antimalarial agents
articles.title_original: Investigation of a cryptic ligand binding site on Plasmodium falciparum Hsp90.

articles.headline: In 929 P. falciparum samples from West and Central Africa, a novel intron variant (a548383t) in pfdhps was strongly associated with the resistance-conferring 431V mutation, particularly the VAGKGS haplotype (OR=211.7, p<0.001), followed by VAGKAS (OR=39.2, p<0.001) and VAGKAA (OR=33.6, p<0.001), suggesting either a common ancestral origin or functional phenotypic advantage
articles.title_original: A novel intron variant is associated with emerging pfdhps mutant haplotypes in West and Central African Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist

articles.headline: In 2,326 Congolese children under 5 receiving integrated community case management, 63.8% had malaria; 94.8% of clinically diagnosed cases received RDT versus 2.8% of negative cases (p=0.0001), though 18.5% of RDT-negative children still received malaria treatment. Adherence to guidelines varied by province (Kasai Oriental AOR=1.89, CI 1.51–2.37 versus baseline; Tanganyika AOR=0.40, CI 0.81–0.92)
articles.title_original: Assessment of adherence to the National Malaria Guidelines in treatment management of malaria among children under five in integrated community case management in the DRC.

articles.headline: Among 24 pentyloxyamide-based HDAC inhibitors tested against P. falciparum asexual blood stages, compound 4o was most potent (IC50 20 nM) with >250-fold selectivity over human cells. It inhibited exoerythrocytic stages (IC50 24 nM) but lacked gametocyte activity (IC50 >2 μM) and induced histone H4 hyperacetylation, indicating potential as a transmission-control therapeutic
articles.title_original: Antiplasmodial activity of pentyloxyamide-based histone deacetylase inhibitors against Plasmodium falciparum parasites.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist

articles.headline: Epidemiological analysis revealed indigenous P. vivax upsurges in >50% of countries across Eastern Mediterranean, Southeast Asia, and Western Pacific regions. Papua New Guinea and Yemen showed consistent increases since 2015; Indonesia and Pakistan experienced sharp rises of +100% and +83.4% respectively between 2020–2023. Thailand appears unlikely to achieve WHO 2025 elimination despite initial targeting
articles.title_original: Indigenous Plasmodium vivax upsurge in the Eastern Mediterranean, Western Pacific, and South East Asia regions - beyond the constant culpability of climate change, COVID-19, and armed conflicts.

articles.headline: Screening 812 microbial metabolites identified 219 compounds inhibiting P. falciparum ≥80% at 2 μg/mL and 149 active against T. gondii tachyzoites. Four compound groups reached low nanomolar IC50; monocyclic thiazole peptides and pleuromutilins showed both potent antiparasitic activity and low mammalian cytotoxicity, highlighting potential for further development
articles.title_original: Screening and hit evaluation of a microbial metabolite library against the pathogenic Plasmodium falciparum and Toxoplasma gondii parasites.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist

articles.headline: NMR spectroscopy assigned 90% of backbone resonances in P. falciparum flap endonuclease (PfFEN349), identifying 298 of 337 theoretically-detectible amide resonances. Secondary structure predictions align with AlphaFold modeling, establishing structural foundation for rational design of PfFEN inhibitors as antimalarial agents
articles.title_original: (1)H, (15)N and (13)C backbone resonance assignments of flap endonuclease from Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Recombinant expression of Plasmodium heme detoxification protein in E. coli proved challenging; despite extensive optimization strategies, only a single construct (HDPpf-C10 with 44-residue N-terminal truncation) expressed in soluble form, yet exhibited no detectable heme-to-Hz transformation activity, revealing the essential role of the flexible N-terminal region in hemozoin formation
articles.title_original: Recombinant expression and purification of Plasmodium heme detoxification protein in E. coli: Challenges and discoveries.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 122 studies (148,693 participants) comparing 3 vs 2 doses of sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine for intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy showed marked dose-response decline with P. falciparum resistance: in west/central Africa, aRR for malaria parasitaemia at delivery was 0.71 (0.65–0.78) at very low resistance but 0.83 (0.72–0.95) at low resistance; in east/southern Africa, aRR declined from 0.63 (0.57–0.69) at moderate resistance to 0.93 (0.85–1.01) at very high resistance (p<0.0001)
articles.title_original: The impact of sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance on the effectiveness of intermittent preventive treatment for the prevention of malaria in pregnancy in Africa: an updated systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: High-speed flickering spectroscopy revealed that PKLR-deficient erythrocytes exhibit reduced ATP-dependent membrane fluidity compared to healthy controls; when infected with Plasmodium yoelii, metabolic rigidity impaired parasite-induced membrane remodeling, suggesting glycolytic insufficiency creates a biomechanical barrier against malaria
articles.title_original: Metabolic Rigidity as a Mechanical Barrier to Malaria: Flickering Loss in PKLR-Deficient Erythrocytes.

articles.headline: Case report of a 33-year-old pregnant woman (33 weeks gestation) with severe malaria requiring mechanical ventilation demonstrated that continuous fetal Doppler monitoring revealed reassuring parameters including normal umbilical artery and middle cerebral artery flow patterns with no signs of fetal hypoxia, supporting the feasibility of serial fetal assessment during maternal respiratory support
articles.title_original: Severe Malaria and Mechanical Ventilation in Pregnancy: The Crucial Role of Fetal Doppler in Monitoring Fetal Well-Being.

articles.headline: Non-inferiority trial in Kenya comparing broflanilide-based VECTRON™ T500 to pirimiphos-methyl Actellic™ 300CS for indoor residual spraying found VECTRON™ T500 maintained significantly higher mosquito mortality against pyrethroid-resistant An. gambiae Bungoma (98.73% ± 3.51% vs 80.22% ± 11.23%, p<0.001) and susceptible Kisumu (100% vs 89.60% ± 6.34%, p<0.001), remained >95% effective throughout 6 months while Actellic™ declined below 80% after month 4, with zero adverse events versus 8% for Actellic™
articles.title_original: Community evaluation of VECTRON™ T500 (broflanilide) for indoor residual spraying for malaria vector control in Siaya county, Kenya.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 276 eligible studies (screened from 3,637 publications) documented 45 Anopheles species in WHO Eastern Mediterranean region across 18 countries, with research concentrated on five principal species (An. stephensi in 102 studies, An. superpictus in 57, An. dthali in 49, An. culicifacies in 47, An. maculipennis in 45), emphasizing the critical need for ongoing surveillance to detect shifts in vector distribution
articles.title_original: Diversity and spatial distribution of malaria vectors in the WHO Eastern Mediterranean region from 1900 to 2024: a systematic review.

articles.headline: Longitudinal molecular surveillance of 2,826 P. falciparum isolates from Kagera, Tanzania (2021–2023) revealed K13 R561H artemisinin-resistance mutation persisted at 14–26% in border districts and spread eastward (Muleba 5.0%, Bukoba 0.7% in 2023), with regional R561H prevalence rising from 5.5% to 11.3% then stabilizing at 6.9%; antifolate-resistance markers DHFR I164L and DHPS A581G peaked at 38.1% and 48.1% in eastern districts, indicating micro-geographic heterogeneity and ongoing resistance spread
articles.title_original: Temporal Trends in Artemisinin Partial Resistance and Other Antimalarial Drug Mutations in Plasmodium falciparum from Kagera Region, Northwestern Tanzania, 2021-2023.

articles.headline: Differential transcriptome prospecting identified two P. vivax hypnozoite-expressed RNA-binding proteins (IESI-1 and IESI-2); functional validation in P. yoelii showed individual expression of each protein blocked liver stage schizogony, maintaining parasites in a small uninucleate state with distinct RNA-binding motifs, providing unprecedented functional evidence for a molecular mechanism of hypnozoite dormancy
articles.title_original: Two Plasmodium vivax hypnozoite-expressed RNA-binding proteins inhibit liver stage replication.

articles.headline: Perspective on unusual DNA replication dynamics in P. falciparum schizogony, wherein parasites undergo alternating asynchronous rounds of DNA replication and nuclear division within shared cytoplasm followed by mass cytokinesis producing numerous daughter cells; clarifying pressures underlying these dynamics may reveal parasite-specific vulnerabilities and inform antimalarial development
articles.title_original: Unusual replication dynamics during Plasmodium falciparum schizogony.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional KAP study in Mozambique (May 2021 and June 2022) among household heads found high baseline knowledge of malaria symptoms, transmission, prevention, and treatment (96.8% before intervention, 91.1% after), with no differences between intervention and control groups, suggesting the higher malaria burden was not attributable to knowledge gaps and the REPELMALARIA intervention did not alter KAP scores
articles.title_original: Knowledge, attitude and practice in high endemic settings for malaria: a cross-sectional study in a rural community in central Mozambique.

articles.headline: Cohort study of 2,371 children (1–18 years) with 2,610 febrile illness visits found 41.8% (95%CI 39.8–43.9%) had malaria parasitaemia and 2.5% (95%CI 1.9–3.2%) experienced recurrent infections; malaria risk increased with age (aRR 1.57 for ≥5 vs <2 years) but was protected by higher maternal education (aRR 0.66), piped/well water (aRR 0.78), and electricity/solar lighting (aRR 0.61); recurrent malaria linked to poor housing and proximity to rice paddies
articles.title_original: Sociodemographic and ecological factors associated with malaria and malaria recurrence risk in children in Msambweni, Kwale County, Kenya.

articles.headline: Predictive chemoproteomics framework using activity-based protein profiling with pirimiphos-methyl-oxon identified 18 serine hydrolases in susceptible An. gambiae; carboxylesterase Coeae6g was functionally validated to confer pirimiphos-methyl resistance and cross-resistance to malathion, bendiocarb, and permethrin, providing a scalable approach to identify enzyme-mediated resistance mechanisms before field dissemination and guide insecticide management
articles.title_original: Predictive chemoproteomics and functional validation reveal Coeae6g-mediated insecticide cross-resistance in the malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.

articles.headline: Global placental malaria (P. falciparum) prevalence 17% (95% CI 12–21%), rising to 23% in histology-based studies; 19% in sub-Saharan Africa vs 4% elsewhere. HIV-positive women showed 45% vs 17% in HIV-negative. Stillbirth OR 3.81 (95% CI 1.22–11.94); primigravidae at higher risk, reflecting high PM burden in high-transmission areas and vulnerable populations
articles.title_original: Placental malaria: a systematic review and meta-analysis of global burden, risk factors, and maternal and foetal outcomes.

articles.headline: Compartmental model integrating age-specific migration with human and vector dynamics, validated in Burkina Faso, demonstrates that nonlinear amplification and synchrony in P. falciparum infection prevalence are partially driven by age-cohort migration patterns, providing evidence for targeted intervention design in regions with seasonal labor mobility
articles.title_original: Impact of age-structured migration on malaria burden: A modelling-empirical analysis in sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: Review of Kenya evidence showing climate variability exerts effects on malaria outcomes comparable to or stronger than vector control interventions at local scales, with non-linear, lagged transmission responses to temperature and rainfall; advocates 'climate-smart' targeting of insecticide-treated nets and case management according to climate-favored transmission windows
articles.title_original: Climate, Interventions, and Malaria Outcomes in a Warming World: Towards Climate-Smart Malaria Control in Kenya.

articles.headline: Submicroscopic congenital malaria (Plasmodium sp.) detected in 12.2% (95% CI 9.3–15.6%) of cord blood samples in low-endemicity Guatemala (2009–2011); submicroscopic maternal infection (aOR 7.55, 95% CI 1.84–51.5) and gravidity (aOR 1.49, 95% CI 1.13–2.04) significantly increased congenital malaria risk
articles.title_original: Prevalence of submicroscopic congenital malaria and associated risk factors in an area of low endemicity of Guatemala.

articles.headline: Review of Plasmodium knowlesi transmission in Southeast Asia examining climate-driven changes in temperature and precipitation, deforestation-facilitated zoonotic spillover from simian hosts, and attitude–behavior gaps in prevention; identifies challenges for controlling P. knowlesi as human–macaque–mosquito interactions intensify
articles.title_original: Plasmodium knowlesi and global changes: socio-ecological drivers and challenges.

articles.headline: Cost-effectiveness analysis (1,000 pregnant women, Nigeria, 2019–2020) comparing sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (IPTp-SP) alone vs IPTp-SP plus insecticide-treated nets: incremental cost-effectiveness ratio US$2.12 per DALY averted (15.63 DALYs prevented with US$100.19 incremental cost); ~60% probability of cost-effectiveness at willingness-to-pay US$150/DALY
articles.title_original: Cost-effectiveness of intermittent preventive therapy and insecticide-treated bed nets for malaria prevention among pregnant women in Jos North, Plateau State, Nigeria.

articles.headline: Multi-country RDT quality study (100,000+ tests by 499 healthcare workers across four countries) found 5.1–7.3% misrecorded as positive and 0.7–3.7% as negative; overall agreement 90.2–94.3%. Misrecording patterns varied by country and were influenced by healthcare worker age, education, and facility-level factors
articles.title_original: Characteristics of healthcare workers and health facilities associated with inaccurate recording of malaria rapid diagnostic test results: a multi-country study.

articles.headline: Acceptability and feasibility study of forest-exposure malaria prevention (Cambodia, 2022–2024): 28,000 forest packs containing volatile pyrethroid spatial repellent and topical repellent distributed to 2,935 individuals; reported acceptability reached 93–100% and product use was high among forest dwellers, supporting implementation with community-led social behavior change communication
articles.title_original: Evaluating the acceptability and feasibility of new mosquito bite prevention tools in a "forest pack" to support malaria elimination in Cambodia.

articles.headline: P. vivax isolates from three Amazon regions (n=191) showed regional variation in dbp gene amplification: 25% of western isolates harbored up to 8 copies vs 2–9% with 2–3 copies elsewhere; ebp2 amplification detected in 15% (western) vs 1–4% (elsewhere), with no significant association with host DARC genotypes
articles.title_original: The pattern of gene amplification of members of the Plasmodium vivax erythrocyte binding-like proteins family across the Amazon rainforest.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 185 Ugandan household heads found 63.8% aware of repellents and 59.5% positive, but only 8.2% used them consistently. Age ≥60 years (aOR 2.50, 95% CI 1.43–4.38) and large households reduced use, while tertiary education (aOR 0.42) and positive attitudes (aOR 3.28) increased use; cost and policy gaps were barriers
articles.title_original: Knowledge, attitudes, practices and determinants of mosquito repellent use for malaria prevention in Nangabo Ward, Wakiso District, Uganda.

articles.headline: Larviciding pilot in Tanga Region, Tanzania (June 2022–April 2024) using Bacillus thuringiensis israelensis and B. sphaericus showed reduced Anopheles larvae densities across risk strata with incidence rate ratios of 0.20 (95% CI 0.12–0.35) in high-risk, 0.51 (0.26–1.01) in moderate-risk, and 0.40 (0.24–0.68) in low-risk areas; A. gambiae s.l. reduced but A. funestus unchanged
articles.title_original: A large-scale mosquito larviciding in Tanga Region, Tanzania, reduced mosquito densities to varying degrees across malaria transmission risk strata.

articles.headline: RNA-seq of P. falciparum NF54 treated with iso-mukaadial acetate identified downregulation of glycolytic, pentose phosphate, and DNA replication genes (GAPDH, G6PD, MCM complex, topoisomerases); upregulation of adhesion genes (PfEMP1, RIFIN, STEVOR) paralleled chloroquine effects, suggesting IMA impairs parasite energy metabolism and genome integrity mechanisms
articles.title_original: The impact of Iso-mukaadial acetate on Plasmodium falciparum transcriptional gene regulation.

articles.headline: Conditional depletion of P. falciparum PfAnchor protein disrupts apicoplast fission, causing parasite death, with azithromycin treatment rescuing defects through loss of apicoplast elongated structure. PfAnchor is identified as the first apicoplast-specific dynamin adaptor protein essential for organelle inheritance in blood-stage parasites, highlighting parasite-specific organelle division as a potential therapeutic vulnerability
articles.title_original: An essential adaptor for apicoplast fission and inheritance in malaria parasites.

articles.headline: In vitro study of Cambodian P. falciparum isolates found mefloquine (MQ) resistance confers significant cross-tolerance to the MQ-piperaquine combination, whereas PPQ-resistant and -sensitive strains remain susceptible. Under four months repeated MQ-PPQ pressure, parasites rapidly acquired tolerance via pfmdr1 amplification with MQ inhibiting PPQ accumulation dose-dependently, undermining triple artemisinin-based combination therapy efficacy and questioning this regimen's strategic rationale
articles.title_original: Tolerance of Plasmodium falciparum mefloquine-resistant clinical isolates to mefloquine-piperaquine with implications for triple artemisinin-based combination therapies.

articles.headline: Implementation of 'Accelerator Strategies' in Lao PDR (2022) combining targeted chemoprevention for forest goers and mobile populations with community engagement achieved high intervention coverage and community acceptance. Three key enablers—service delivery by community members, strong political commitment, and people-centered services with granular local data—resulted in decreased malaria burden among previously unreached forest-based and vulnerable populations
articles.title_original: Early and sustained community engagement to reach unreached populations for malaria elimination in Lao People's Democratic Republic.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional analysis of hospital discharge data across Ecuador's 24 provinces (2015–2023) identified 125 dengue deaths concentrated in Amazonian and coastal provinces, and 5 malaria deaths (predominantly P. falciparum: 3 in 2019, 1 in 2021, 1 in 2023), with highest malaria mortality of 16.3 per million in Napo province in 2021. Fragmented patterns and persistent underreporting in endemic zones expose critical gaps in arboviral and Plasmodium surveillance
articles.title_original: Dengue and malaria mortality in Ecuador: A population analysis of hospital deaths across 24 provinces, 2015-2023.

articles.headline: Mixed-methods study of 400 pregnant women in endemic Colombian region (6% with gestational malaria; 44% with malaria history) found psychological health domain highest (median 78) while physical health lowest (median 43), with gestational malaria, comorbidities, and rural residence associated with poorer physical and environmental health. Qualitative analysis revealed gestational malaria was socially normalized and not perceived as significant determinant of well-being despite impaired health-related quality of life
articles.title_original: Health-related quality of life among pregnant women exposed to malaria in an endemic region of Colombia.

articles.headline: Analysis of 81,010 Sina Weibo posts (2019–2024) found malaria discussions were event-driven with peaks during China's health assistance to Africa, public health incidents, and World Malaria Day (accounting for ~20% annual posts), negative events sustaining engagement 1.5 times longer than positive ones. Africa-related discussions comprised over 60% of region-specific posts, while official health accounts posted infrequently, highlighting limited official engagement and the need for tailored communication strategies
articles.title_original: Event-driven dynamics, regional disparities, and netizen engagement: characterizing and exploring malaria discourse on Sina Weibo for enhanced health communication.

articles.headline: Implementation study on Bioko Island leveraged routine annual indoor residual spraying to identify 589 pregnant women in 1,567 households for follow-up visits on LLIN and intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy (IPTp) uptake. High long-lasting insecticide-treated net awareness (76.9%) and nightly use (54.8%) contrasted with low IPTp awareness (34.5–37.1%) and uptake (35.2% on track for three doses), with uptake associated with disease-prevention awareness and dose requirement knowledge
articles.title_original: Indoor residual spraying household visits as a platform for engaging pregnant women in malaria prevention on Bioko Island, Equatorial Guinea.

articles.headline: Multiplexed amplicon sequencing of P. falciparum from highland health facilities (n=599) and lowland agricultural worksites (n=581) in Ethiopia found lowland populations had higher complexity of infection (mean COI 2.62, 62% polyclonal) versus highland residents (mean COI 2.00, 40%), with high genetic connectivity and extensive parasite sharing (27 related clusters highland, 10 seasonal workers) demonstrating agricultural seasonal migration drives parasite gene flow sustaining transmission
articles.title_original: High connectivity and low differentiation of Plasmodium falciparum parasite populations in a setting with high seasonal migration.

articles.headline: Non-human primate study using P. coatneyi-infected male rhesus macaques demonstrated methylene blue reverses infection-induced brainstem damage. Integrated transcriptomic analysis across macaque brainstem and blood and human P. falciparum blood datasets identified nine genes associated with cerebral malaria severity predominantly expressed in neutrophils, suggesting potential as blood biomarkers for diagnosing P. falciparum-induced fatal cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Methylene blue treatment of fatal cerebral malaria and identification of potential blood biomarkers.

articles.headline: Double-blind randomized trial (n=20) of ruxolitinib, a JAK1/2 inhibitor, as adjunctive to artemether-lumefantrine in malaria-naïve volunteers experimentally infected with blood-stage P. falciparum found ruxolitinib safe and well-tolerated, attenuating inflammatory markers (C-reactive protein, angiopoietin-2, ICAM-1) while enhancing T cell-mediated immunity (elevated HLA-DRA and 4-1BB) after second infection, supporting further evaluation as adjunctive therapy
articles.title_original: Adjunctive ruxolitinib attenuates inflammation and enhances immunity in volunteers experimentally infected with Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 62 articles (2014–2025) on artificial intelligence applications in malaria diagnosis (image-based parasite detection), treatment optimization (predictive analytics), and outbreak prediction found AI models achieved improved diagnostic accuracies and reduced diagnostic time and human error. Major limitations included poor data interoperability, limited rural infrastructure, and healthcare worker training gaps, highlighting areas requiring attention for AI adaptation in India's 2030 malaria elimination goal
articles.title_original: The role of artificial intelligence (AI) in the elimination of malaria: a systematic literature review.

articles.headline: Molecular survey of 1,990 febrile patients across four southern Senegalese regions (2020–2022) detected Plasmodium in 73.43% (67.92% single-species, 32.08% co-infections), with non-falciparum species (P. vivax, P. ovale, P. malariae) identified in three regions including first reports in Kolda and Tambacounda and first P. ovale in Ziguinchor, revealing ongoing circulation of previously undetected non-falciparum parasites overlooked by national surveillance focused on P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Common Molecular Detection of the Neglected Human Malaria Parasite Among Febrile Patients in Southern Regions in Senegal.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 332 pregnant women in Ethiopia (Sept-Dec 2024) assessed malaria, soil-transmitted helminth (STH) co-infection, and anemia. Malaria prevalence 24.4%, STH 33.7%, co-infection 12.7%; overall anemia 57.5%. Among infected women, anemia prevalence was 18.4% (malaria), 26.2% (STH), 9.6% (co-infection). Malaria and STH showed strong statistical association with anemia
articles.title_original: Malaria and Soil-Transmitted Helminth Co-Infection and Its Association With Anemia Among Pregnant Women Visiting Mizan-Tepi University Teaching Hospital, Southwest Ethiopia.
articles.journal: Can J Infect Dis Med Microbiol

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 332 Ugandan adults evaluated malaria prevalence in those presenting with upper respiratory symptoms; 16.0% confirmed malaria by RDT and microscopy. Fever and cough were strongest predictors; malaria positivity increased from 0.8% (one symptom) to 100% (four symptoms). A four-symptom score showed 98.1% sensitivity and 47.3% specificity, potentially optimizing malaria testing in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Syndromic presentation by patients with malaria and respiratory symptoms in a malaria-endemic urban-and-peri-urban setting.

articles.headline: Qualitative study with 111 interviews and 16 focus groups examined community intermittent preventive treatment with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (c-IPTp) for malaria in pregnancy in Senegal. Healthcare providers perceived benefits but noted concerns about reduced antenatal care; pregnant women found it acceptable despite concerns about adverse events. Key barriers were sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine stockouts and pregnancy test unavailability; recommendations included ensuring supplies and incentivizing community health workers
articles.title_original: Initiating community intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy in Senegal: qualitative evaluation of stakeholder acceptability and experience.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 58 Kenyan articles (2002–2023) examined Plasmodium falciparum genetic diversity and drug resistance. Expected heterozygosity varied by region (Lake Basin 0.79, Highlands 0.80, Coastal 0.96); multiclonal infections ranged 34–80%. Pfcrt CVIET haplotype declined from 96% (1999) to 1% (2017); Pfdhfr and Pfdhps mutations increased temporally; Pfk13 mutations detected in Lake Basin and Highlands. Findings highlight temporal shifts in drug resistance and ongoing transmission
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity, population structure, and drug resistance of Plasmodium falciparum in Kenya: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Secondary analysis of GBD 2021 database assessed neglected tropical disease and malaria burden 1990–2021. Global age-standardized incidence increased 0.59%; prevalence declined 58.05%, mortality 25%, DALYs 32.36%. Western Sub-Saharan Africa bore highest burden; lower-SDI regions had higher disease burden. Projections to 2050 suggest prevalence decline but increases in incidence, mortality, and DALYs with sustained regional and gender gaps
articles.title_original: Global, regional, national burden, trends and health inequality of neglected tropical diseases and malaria from 1990 to 2021.

articles.headline: Study developed convolutional neural network pipeline for automated Plasmodium stage detection from Giemsa-stained blood smears (70% training, 15% validation, 15% test). CNN achieved 92.4% overall accuracy (±2.1%) with precision and recall exceeding 0.90 across all classes. Schizont stage achieved highest accuracy (94.7%), Ring Form 91.2%, demonstrating robustness for resource-constrained diagnostic deployment
articles.title_original: Mobile phone-based plasmodium parasites stage detection from Giemsa stained blood smear by convolutional neural networks.

articles.headline: Retrospective analysis of surveillance data from 166 Ethiopian districts (Jan 2014–Dec 2024) documented 7,710,733 malaria cases and 162 deaths, with adults comprising 62% of cases. Significant trend inflection late 2018 shifted from decline (APC −13.2%) to sharp resurgence (APC +12.6%, 2018–2024). Annual Parasite Incidence rose from 10.9 to 74.8 per 1000; Plasmodium vivax increased from 25% to 43% with 11-fold incidence surge (2.7–32.4 per 1000); resurgence in 83% of districts including urban and highland areas
articles.title_original: Resurgence of malaria in the Amhara Region, Ethiopia (2014-2024): trends, spatial expansion, and control challenges.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 149 articles identified 24 eligible studies on malaria reintroduction prevention in malaria-free zones. All studies implemented imported case surveillance; 21 reported case detection, 4 case management; some included mass drug administration (3 articles), chemoprophylaxis (3), vector surveillance (6), and vector control (7). Comprehensive strategies combining surveillance, vector control, and cross-border initiatives are necessary to prevent malaria reintroduction
articles.title_original: Imported malaria: a silent and forgotten threat in malaria free zones?

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 171 asymptomatic pregnant women in Mogadishu (Feb–March 2025) assessed Plasmodium infection by RDT and microscopy. Asymptomatic malaria prevalence 2.9%, with P. falciparum as only species detected. Younger age (OR 0.24), urban residence (OR 0.12), and ITN use (OR 0.06) associated with lower infection odds, suggesting protective effects
articles.title_original: Prevalence of asymptomatic malaria and associated factors among pregnant women in Mogadishu, Somalia: a cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Abdi MY, Mohamud FA, Abdullahi SA

articles.headline: In vitro study evaluated Terminalia ivorensis and T. brownii extracts against P. falciparum (Dd2, 3D7, Dd2-GNF156 strains). Subfractions showed activity (IC50 0.15–5.73 µg/ml); isolated eschweilenol C exhibited IC50 490.74 nM (Dd2) and 379.20 nM (3D7). Three compounds demonstrated IC50 <1 µg/ml, selectivity >10, no efficacy loss against resistant strains, and reduced haemozoin production. Eschweilenol C identified as promising candidate for artemisinin-resistant malaria drug discovery
articles.title_original: Eschweilenol C and subfractions from Terminalia plants disrupt haemoglobin metabolism, inhibiting Plasmodium falciparum growth at rings and trophozoite stages.

articles.headline: Multilevel logistic regression analysis of 2022 Ghana DHS data assessed prevalence and risk factors for co-occurring malaria and anemia in children under five. Double burden prevalence 6.4%; fifth-born status (aOR 3.92), recent fever (aOR 2.49), and maternal primary education (aOR 1.87) increased odds, while older household heads (aOR 0.33) and higher household wealth decreased odds. Co-occurrence remains major health concern requiring integrated malaria prevention and anemia control
articles.title_original: The double burden: co-occurrence of malaria and anaemia in children under five in Ghana - a multilevel mixed-effects logistic regression analysis.

articles.headline: Case report of 17-year-old gravida presenting with fever, headache, and abdominal pain, diagnosed with malaria and treated with artemisinin-based therapy. She developed bilateral lower extremity weakness and neurogenic bladder; investigations revealed malaria parasites on blood film and Schistosoma ova in urine. Treated with prednisone and praziquantel; patient became ambulatory after three weeks, demonstrating reversible paralysis from dual malaria-schistosomiasis infection
articles.title_original: Reversible paralysis in pregnancy, thinking beyond malaria: a case report.

articles.headline: Idelalisib (PfPI3K inhibitor) combined with dihydroartemisinin reversed artemisinin resistance in P. falciparum by reducing PI3P levels, increasing ROS accumulation and ubiquitinated proteins, and disrupting parasite antioxidant systems and proteostasis
articles.title_original: PfPI3K inhibition reversed artemisinin resistance by increasing ROS and ubiquitinated protein levels in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: In 10 Kenyan children with acute P. falciparum malaria vs. 10 healthy controls, T-cell activation was comparable, but acute malaria shifted CD4+ and CD8+ T-cell cytokine secretion to favor IL-10 during active disease and 4 weeks post-recovery, suggesting malaria induces systemic immune suppression, not EBV-specific changes
articles.title_original: CD4 and CD8 T-cell response is dominated by IL-10-secreting cells in children with uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 13,528 African children (3 months–16 years) with severe P. falciparum malaria found 47% had KDIGO-defined acute kidney injury (46% Stage 1, 30% Stage 2, 23% Stage 3), which increased mortality odds 2.87-fold (95% CI 2.28–3.61) with monotonic dose-response by stage; WHO creatinine threshold (>3 mg/dL) missed 93% of cases
articles.title_original: Identification of acute kidney injury in African children with severe malaria: a multinational individual participant data meta-analysis.

articles.headline: In P. berghei, the NIMA-related kinase NEK4 accumulates at the MTOC and APC immediately post-fertilization and drives meiotic entry and zygote maturation; nek4 deletion blocks MTOC duplication, microtubule formation, chromatin condensation, and nuclear migration, collapsing transcriptional and phosphoregulatory networks
articles.title_original: A divergent Plasmodium NEK4 acts as a key regulator driving the early events of meiosis.

articles.headline: Rare case report of locally acquired P. falciparum malaria in Lebanon in a 70-year-old female with no travel history; presented with fever, chills, headache, nausea, abdominal pain, anemia, thrombocytopenia, leukopenia, and high parasitemia; successfully treated with IV artesunate followed by oral artemether-lumefantrine
articles.title_original: Locally Acquired Plasmodium falciparum Malaria in a Non-endemic Region of Lebanon: A Case Report.

articles.headline: Molecular docking and dynamics simulations identified a novel antimalarial compound targeting PfMDR1 with GLIDE score −12.169 kcal/mol (vs. benchmark −5.435 kcal/mol) and binding free energy −130.971 ± 14.283 kJ/mol, showing superior inhibition of this drug-resistance transporter over experimentally tested drugs
articles.title_original: In-silico identification of antimalarial compounds targeting PfMDR1 of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Preetha TL, Yadav V, Subbarao N

articles.headline: In two Côte d'Ivoire districts, clothianidin-based indoor residual spraying over three rounds (2020–2022) reduced human biting rates 60% by round three (IRR 0.40, p=0.002), decreased sporozoite infection odds 49% after rounds one and two (OR 0.51), and reduced entomological inoculation rate 4-fold (3.92 to 1.25 ib/p/night), with An. gambiae and An. coluzzii as main vectors
articles.title_original: Entomological impact of three years of clothianidin-based indoor residual spraying in two high malaria endemic districts in Côte d'Ivoire.

articles.headline: Mixed-methods audit of 384 patient records from three Uganda hospitals found 33.07% adherence to severe malaria treatment guidelines; non-adherence centered on IV drug dosing (38.3%) and treatment without confirmed diagnosis (25%); medical doctors showed higher adherence (aIRR 1.7, p=0.035) than other professionals
articles.title_original: Adherence to severe malaria treatment guidelines in Northern Uganda: a mixed-methods study.
