articles.headline: OpenMalaria modeling of hybrid malaria chemoprevention (adding monthly sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine doses during rainy season to perennial coverage) predicted 2.1-fold and 2.9-fold greater burden averted against clinical cases, with protective efficacy 31.8% and 44.9% for children 3–24 and 3–36 months respectively, compared to perennial-only in sub-perennial settings
articles.title_original: Rethinking a hybrid malaria chemoprevention delivery strategy for children in sub-perennial settings: a modelling study integrating age- and seasonally-targeted delivery.
articles.authors: Sen S, Schellenberg D, Penny MA

articles.headline: In Kokrajhar District, Assam, secondary anopheline vectors showed sporozoite positivity: An. maculatus (4%; 5/80), An. minimus (4.8%; 1/21), and An. kochi (4.6%; 1/22), suggesting these species sustain P. vivax and P. falciparum transmission alongside primary vectors despite LLIN and IRS deployment
articles.title_original: Evidence of secondary anopheline vectors in sustaining malaria transmission in Kokrajhar District, Assam, Northeastern India.

articles.headline: Editorial commentary celebrating Linblade et al.'s assessment of malaria rapid diagnostic test recording and reporting accuracy across four African countries, contextualizing findings within 15 years of successful global scale-up of malaria diagnostic testing
articles.title_original: A reason to celebrate in a time of uncertainty. Response to: Linblade et al. "Assessing the accuracy of the recording and reporting of malaria rapid diagnostic test results in four African countries-methods and key results".

articles.headline: UA2239, an acyclic nucleoside phosphonate guanine analog, rapidly and irreversibly blocked P. falciparum merozoite and gamete egress by inhibiting cyclic GMP synthesis, acting on guanylyl cyclase α; parasites developed resistance through mutations in downstream effectors rather than the target, highlighting UA2239 as a first-in-class dual-stage antimalarial candidate
articles.title_original: An acyclic nucleoside phosphonate effectively blocks the egress of the malaria parasite by inhibiting the synthesis of cyclic GMP.

articles.headline: Among 146 children with severe malaria at a north-central Nigerian secondary facility, 69.2% (101/146) presented >24 hours post-symptom onset. Failure to recognize illness (44.6%) and lack of funds (38.6%) were primary barriers; waiting for father's decision and lower socioeconomic class (AOR 3.05, 95% CI 1.10–8.48) independently predicted delayed presentation. Household decision-making dynamics and socioeconomic status were key drivers of late presentation
articles.title_original: Influence of Sociodemographic Factors and Household Decision-Making on the Delayed Presentation of Childhood Severe Malaria at a Secondary Facility in North-Central Nigeria.

articles.headline: Phylogenetic analysis of 115 Colombian bird species found migratory species, larger body sizes, and more colorful plumage associated with higher haemosporidian infection probability, while habitat specialization was protective. These host traits influence susceptibility to haemosporidian infection in tropical avian communities, with implications for understanding parasite exposure and species adaptability to environmental change
articles.title_original: Host Traits and Avian Malaria Parasites: Understanding the Intrinsic Connections.

articles.headline: Author correction for a report on a male-drive female-sterile genetic system for self-limited control of the malaria vector Anopheles gambiae
articles.title_original: Author Correction: A male-drive female-sterile system for the self-limited control of the malaria mosquito Anopheles gambiae.

articles.headline: High-resolution proteomics of Anopheles gambiae salivary glands revealed that Plasmodium sporozoite invasion causes structural damage, leading to hemolymph protein influx and saliva protein leakage. Both P. berghei and P. falciparum infection reduced salivary immune proteins with remarkably similar proteomic signatures. These findings elucidate molecular interactions between Plasmodium sporozoites and mosquito salivary glands during malaria transmission
articles.title_original: High-resolution proteomics unveils salivary gland disruption and saliva-hemolymph protein exchange in Plasmodium-infected mosquitoes.
articles.authors: Alves E Silva TL, et al.

articles.headline: Ganaplacide (Novartis) has succeeded in clinical trials for malaria, easing concerns about drug resistance. Specific efficacy metrics and optimal treatment deployment strategies are not detailed in the available abstract
articles.title_original: 'A sigh of relief': New malaria drug succeeds in clinical trial as existing treatments falter.

articles.headline: Among 3,322 asymptomatic residents in Malaysia's supposedly malaria-free areas, nested PCR detected low-density infections in 1.86% (62/3,322), undetected by microscopy. Species included P. malariae (40.3%), P. vivax (29.0%), P. knowlesi (24.2%), P. falciparum (1.6%), P. cynomolgi (1.6%), and mixed P. vivax/P. knowlesi (3.2%); all cases originated from Sabah and one Orang Asli settlement. Hidden parasite reservoirs persist despite elimination declarations, posing reintroduction risks and necessitating sensitive molecular surveillance
articles.title_original: Persistent low-density infection in areas at risk of malaria reintroduction in Malaysia.

articles.headline: Plasmodium spp. was detected in 293/1,200 (24.4%) acute febrile illness patients across two Nigerian hospitals (Aug 2023–Sept 2024). Risk factors included larger household size (COR 3.70, 95% CI 1.12–12.28), healthcare worker status (COR 3.39, CI 1.22–9.41), age 11–14 years (COR 1.86, CI 1.15–3.02), and FCT enrollment (COR 1.30, CI 1.00–1.69). Coinfections occurred in 37.2% (109/293) of Plasmodium-positive cases, including nine NIAID biodefense pathogens in 105 patients
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of Plasmodium spp. Detection Among Acute Febrile Illness Patients in Two Regions of Nigeria.

articles.headline: Scoping review of 41 studies found malaria vaccine costs of US$4.20–$52.35 per fully vaccinated child, with costs per DALY averted ranging US$22.63–$3,314.01. Measles vaccine costs were US$1.66–$4.31 per child (US$1.60–$1,239.35 per DALY averted). Most vaccines were cost-effective compared to no intervention. Estimated budget impact for malaria vaccine introduction in 41 African countries is ~US$185 million, though limited evidence constrains policy decisions
articles.title_original: Cost-effectiveness and budget impact of malaria, measles, and meningitis vaccines in Africa: a scoping review.

articles.headline: Bioecological assessment in Metehara, Ethiopia (Nov 2023–Oct 2024) identified 767 potential larval breeding habitats, 98.3% anthropogenic and 95.2% accessible. Among 2,078 adult mosquito catches, An. arabiensis (63.7%) and An. pharoensis (26.4%) predominated; An. arabiensis showed seasonal variation (LR 46.96, p<0.01) while An. stephensi remained stable (LR 13.06, p=0.11). Sporozoite rates: An. pharoensis 4.2% (23/548), An. arabiensis 0.4% (5/1,321), An. stephensi 0% (0/173), identifying An. pharoensis as critical for urban malaria control
articles.title_original: Co-occurrence of native and invasive malaria vectors in anthropogenic habitats in Metehara, Ethiopia: Opportunities for urban malaria control.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis

articles.headline: Ultrasensitive ELISA combined with thio-NAD cycling (TN-cyclon™) detected PfHRP2 and pLDH at 0.782 pg/mL and 1.33 pg/mL respectively, with parasitemia detection thresholds of 2.57 × 10⁻⁵% (PfHRP2) and 2.41 × 10⁻⁵% (pLDH) in culture, and 0.5 parasites/μL in whole blood. System sensitivity was ~10-fold greater than existing ultrasensitive antigen-detection kits and successfully detected pfhrp2-deletion strains without additional sample processing
articles.title_original: Ultrasensitive ELISA for Accurate Detection of Plasmodium falciparum Infection.

articles.headline: Case report of a 21-year-old male from rural Ethiopia who developed Guillain-Barré syndrome with ascending paralysis following Plasmodium vivax malaria, with CSF showing albumin-cytologic dissociation. Treated with intravenous immunoglobulin and supportive care, the patient achieved full neurological recovery after 8 weeks. This rare complication highlights importance of considering GBS in malaria-endemic regions with recent infection and neurological deficits
articles.title_original: Guillain-Barré Syndrome Following Malaria: A Rare Case Report From Rural Ethiopia and Literature Review.
articles.authors: Abdela HA, Kimo NH, Woyimo TG

articles.headline: Researchers developed a navigable CAR-T cell system using an rVAR2 catenane (AXVB) to target pl-CSA+ tumors, achieving improved thermostability and target affinity compared to monomeric rVAR2. In xenograft models, AXVB-directed CAR-T cells potently eliminated both CD19+ and CD19– pl-CSA+ tumor cells and significantly prolonged survival. Protein catenation enhanced the malaria-derived VAR2CSA platform for safer, more effective cancer immunotherapy with reversible targeting mechanisms
articles.title_original: Protein catenation potentiates antitumor activity of malaria VAR2CSA navigation CAR-T cells in a mouse model of hematological malignancies.

articles.headline: Pooled analysis of 4,950 pregnant women across seven malaria high-burden sub-Saharan African countries found ITN use prevalence of 63.8% [95% CI 61.8–65.9], ranging from 49.2% in Ghana to 90.5% in Niger. Primary education (aOR=1.36, 95% CI 1.21–1.53) and 1–2 prior births (aOR=1.24, 95% CI 1.06–1.44) predicted higher ITN use; 32% within-country regional variation suggests tailoring interventions to local and regional disparities
articles.title_original: Pooled prevalence and factors associated with insecticide-treated net use among pregnant women in malaria high-burden countries in sub-Saharan Africa: a multilevel mixed-effects analysis.

articles.headline: Review article consolidating dual challenges of artemisinin (ART) production and Plasmodium falciparum resistance. Examines biotechnological advances in ART biosynthesis, pfk13 resistance mutations, current artemisinin-based combination therapy (ACT), and novel triple/quadruple ACT strategies, advocating interdisciplinary approaches to ensure ART efficacy and sustainable malaria control
articles.title_original: Safeguarding artemisinin: integrating bioproduction innovation and resistance management for malaria eradication.

articles.headline: WHO-standard bioassays in Bobo-Dioulasso, Burkina Faso found high resistance in Anopheles gambiae s.l. to DDT and pyrethroids, but susceptibility to bendiocarb and pirimiphos-methyl. Among 622 specimens, An. arabiensis predominated (90.8%); sporozoite infection rates reached 45% in An. coluzzii, 27.4% in An. arabiensis, 16.2% in An. gambiae s.s., with overall entomological inoculation rate 10.6 infectious bites/person
articles.title_original: Insecticide resistance and malaria transmission indicators in Anopheles gambiae s.l. in Bobo-Dioulasso, Burkina Faso: implications for vector control strategies.

articles.headline: Systematic review of five studies on knockdown resistance (kdr) in An. albimanus, An. darlingi, An. dirus, and An. punctipennis found no kdr mutations (codons 1010, 1013, 1014) in any species. Organochlorine resistance was instead attributable to metabolic mechanisms—elevated mixed-function oxidase and esterase activity—indicating broader genetic screening needed
articles.title_original: Knockdown resistance (kdr) and organochlorine insecticide resistance in malaria vectors: a systematic review.

articles.headline: Modeling of malaria control in Tanzania under varying pyrethroid resistance shows that at 50% resistance, 71% ITN coverage combined with 50% IRS or 32% biolarvicide coverage reduces Re<1; at >75% resistance, Re remains >1. Adding ≥40% immunization coverage (RTS,S/AS01 or R21/Matrix-M) to 80% ITN and 80% artemisinin-based combination therapy coverage reduces infectious individuals to <20% and Re<1 even at high resistance
articles.title_original: Modelling the impact of different intervention packages for malaria control under varying intensities of pyrethroid resistance.

articles.headline: Scoping review of 20 studies (2010–2024) on mobile app-based malaria surveillance found platforms including MCBR, SOCH/MCS, MRRS, and FeverTracker significantly improved reporting timeliness and enabled precise vector control targeting. GIS-enabled tools supported hotspot identification; however, few platforms were institutionalized within national strategies, with sustainability impeded by poor internet access and limited policy integration
articles.title_original: Mobile app-based malaria surveillance and vector control integration: a scoping review of evidence from endemic settings.

articles.headline: Genotyping of 315 P. falciparum isolates in Cameroon identified 12 msp1 and 28 msp2 alleles, with msp2 FC27 100% prevalent and msp1 K1 in 87.8% of samples. Mean multiplicity of infection significantly higher for msp2 (4.85) versus msp1 (2.40; p<0.05); 99% showed polyclonal infections with expected heterozygosity 0.98 (msp2), 0.83 (msp1), indicating intense transmission
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of Plasmodium falciparum using merozoite surface proteins 1 and 2 (msp-1 and msp-2) genes in Oveng and Mintom, South region of Cameroon.

articles.headline: Implementation research in Tanzania demonstrated that school-based intermittent preventive treatment with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (IPTsc) was safe, cost-effective, and highly acceptable, significantly reducing malaria prevalence in school-aged children (5–15 years) who harbor 70% parasite carriage asymptomatically in high-transmission settings, addressing school absenteeism and anemia
articles.title_original: Implementation of the intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in school-aged children (IPTsc) in moderate and high endemic areas in Tanzania: a policy brief.

articles.headline: Post hoc analysis of 96 DLBCL patients in Malawi (59 CHOP, 37 R-CHOP; June 2013–Dec 2019) found 14 developed symptomatic malaria: 8% with CHOP versus 24% with rituximab-plus-CHOP (OR 3.5, 95% CI 1.1–12; p=0.039). Among HRP2-antigenemic cases, R-CHOP patients showed significantly higher antigenemia (3.6 vs. 0.92 ng/mL; p=0.011), suggesting rituximab increases symptomatic malaria risk in endemic areas
articles.title_original: Rituximab is associated with an increased risk of malaria among adult lymphoma patients in Malawi: a prospective observational cohort.

articles.headline: Qualitative study from MVIP pilot (2019–2023) in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi of four-dose RTS,S/AS01E vaccine found high acceptance but substantial dose-completion barriers. Overlapping challenges—schedule complexity, tracking four individual doses within crowded immunization schedules—required tailored strategies; pilot provided guidance tools for national vaccine expansion programs
articles.title_original: Recommendations to mitigate barriers to uptake and delivery of a four-dose malaria vaccine schedule: insights from the MVIP's qualitative evidence.

articles.headline: Fifty-year spatio-temporal analysis (1975–2023) across Rajasthan's four ecogeographical regions showed malaria peaks in 1976 and 1996, sharp decline post-2000. Median API 1.0–1.93; rainfall positively correlated with API in Desert (r=0.39, p=0.006) and Gangetic Plain (r=0.35, p=0.014). An. stephensi and An. culicifacies were resistant to organochlorines/organophosphates but susceptible to pyrethroids; Aravalli region showed highest P. falciparum prevalence
articles.title_original: Echoes of resilience: spatio-temporal analysis of five-decade battle against malaria in Rajasthan, India.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 310 pregnant women and nursing mothers in Enugu, Nigeria found 262 (84.5%) unaware of R21/Matrix-M vaccine but 292 (94.2%) willing to vaccinate their children; 247 (79.7%) declared willingness to pay with median US $0.38 (₦300). Age and health insurance were significantly associated with willingness to pay, suggesting increased awareness could substantially improve vaccine uptake
articles.title_original: Awareness, acceptability, and willingness to pay for the R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccine: a cross-sectional study among pregnant women and nursing mothers in Enugu State, Nigeria.

articles.headline: mRNA-delivered nanoparticle vaccines displaying junctional and NANP repeat epitopes of P. falciparum CSP achieved 99% reduction in liver burden and sterilizing immunity in mice, significantly exceeding protection from current subunit vaccines and radiation-attenuated sporozoites
articles.title_original: mRNA delivery of circumsporozoite protein epitope-based malaria vaccines induces protection in a mouse model.

articles.headline: In Western Kenya cohort (n=768, 2017-2023), PBO LLINs provided greater malaria protection than conventional nets (OR 0.70, 95% CI 0.47-1.03), though not statistically significant; PBO nets warrant scaled implementation in pyrethroid-resistance areas
articles.title_original: Do Piperonyl Butoxide Long-Lasting Insecticide Treated Nets Provide Additional Protection Against Malaria Infections Compared with Conventional Nets in an Operational Setting in Western Kenya?

articles.headline: In Colombian municipalities (2010-2023), diagnostic delay >48 hours (aOR 4.1) and very low healthcare access (aOR 3.5) were strongest predictors of malaria mortality; only 39.7% diagnosed within 48 hours; travel time >2 hours (aOR 2.8) and age <5 years (aOR 1.9) independently associated with poor outcomes
articles.title_original: Inequitable Access to Healthcare and the Malaria Burden in Colombia: A Value-Based Policy Approach for Sustainable Elimination.
articles.authors: Olivera MJ, Magali Caro H, Ricardo Gómez J

articles.headline: In Colombian cohorts (41 pregnant, 46 non-pregnant with P. vivax), recurrence frequency was 41.6% in pregnant women vs. 8.7% in non-pregnant; recurrences showed genetic diversity with only 23-33% homologous genotypes; Fox-P3 expression differed significantly in pregnant women between primary and recurrent infections
articles.title_original: Parasite genotypes and host immune mediators in primary and recurrent episodes of vivax Malaria in Colombia.
articles.authors: Duque-Isaza YY, Carmona-Fonseca J, Arango-Flórez EM
articles.journal: Rev Peru Med Exp Salud Publica

articles.headline: Ethnographic study (250 hours observation, 25 interviews) in six Emberá communities identified malaria as perceived as a 'Western disease' transmitted by mosquitoes; Jaibaná spiritual healers play fundamental role in care alongside Western treatment; indigenous practices incorporated empirically and on-demand
articles.title_original: Between their own and the western: the experience of Emberá communities in Chocó with Malaria.
articles.journal: Rev Peru Med Exp Salud Publica

articles.headline: In 269 Malawian children (cerebral malaria, severe anemia, uncomplicated malaria), lower IgG response to MSP-2 FC27 correlated with cerebral malaria while lower MSP-2 IC1 response correlated with severe anemia; discordant antibody-genotype responses associated with neurological sequelae post-cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Antibody responses to polymorphic Plasmodium falciparum merozoite antigens in Malawian children with severe and uncomplicated malaria.

articles.headline: Zambian low-transmission outbreak prediction model (15-year data, 2010-2016 training, 2017-2024 validation) identified mean nighttime temperature Nov-Jan (β=2.1) and December rainfall (β=23.5) as optimal lags; model predicted 2020 outbreak within 4% accuracy (2367 observed vs. 2467 predicted cases) four months ahead
articles.title_original: Malaria outbreak prediction at the sub-district level in Zambia using remote sensing satellite data.

articles.headline: Time series forecasting across eight Indian states found log-transformed polynomial regression model outperformed ARIMA and lagged difference models; interactive R Shiny tool developed for real-time malaria case predictions to support surveillance and resource allocation
articles.title_original: Forecasting Malaria in Indian States: A Time Series Approach with R Shiny Integration.

articles.headline: In 261 Malawian children, SMA patients had higher parasite genetic diversity (2.70±1.20) than CM patients (2.25±0.89, p<0.01); MSP-2 FC27 associated with SMA (66%) while IC1 associated with CM (61.5%, p<0.025); parasite genetic diversity correlates with malaria severity
articles.title_original: Genotypic and antigenic diversity of Plasmodium falciparum merozoite proteins in Malawian children with severe and uncomplicated malaria.

articles.headline: Mathematical model comparing three control strategies (treatment, LLINs, IRS) in children <5 and pregnant women found treatment most effective alone, but combined LLINs+treatment achieved highest cost-effectiveness; all three strategies simultaneously provided no significant improvement over LLINs+treatment
articles.title_original: Optimal control analysis of malaria infection in children under 5 years and pregnant women under the influence of temperature.
articles.authors: Robert PK, Irunde JI, Mtunya AP

articles.headline: CNN framework with Otsu thresholding on 43,400 blood smear images achieved 97.96% accuracy for malaria detection (baseline 95%, improved to 97% with EfficientNet-B7); Otsu segmentation showed mean Dice coefficient 0.848 and IoU 0.738; five-fold cross-validation confirmed robustness (94.8%-97.8%)
articles.title_original: Optimized CNN framework for malaria detection using Otsu thresholding-based image segmentation.
articles.authors: Singh R, Prabha C, Abdulla S

articles.headline: WHO vaccine value profile for malaria indicates two WHO-recommended vaccines show 13% all-cause mortality reduction and 22% severe malaria hospitalization decline in pilot African introduction; emerging monoclonal antibody prophylaxis represents promising new intervention alongside next-generation vaccine development targeting transmission interruption
articles.title_original: Value profile for Malaria vaccines and monoclonal antibodies.

articles.headline: Homology modeling reveals P. falciparum type-P5 ATPases contain intrinsically disordered loops absent in yeast and human templates; subtype-P5A likely functions as ER transmembrane dislocation, while P5B subtype diverges from fungal polyamine-transporter roles
articles.title_original: Homology Modeling of Type-P5 ATPases from the Malaria Parasite: Insight into Their Functions and Evolution, and Implications About the Effect and Role of Intrinsically Disordered Protein Structure.

articles.headline: Essential oil and 6-ishwarone from Piper alatipetiolatum inhibited P. vivax dose-dependently (IC50 9.25 µg/mL and 3.93 µg/mL), with selective binding to P. vivax DHFR (-7.7 kcal/mol, Ki 2.27 µM) over LDH; no cytotoxicity at 24 h in mammalian cells (94.7–98.3% viability), with favorable in silico pharmacokinetics supporting development as antimalarial leads
articles.title_original: First Evidence of Anti-Plasmodium vivax (Plasmodiidae): Activity of the Essential Oil and 6-Ishwarone Isolated from Piper alatipetiolatum Yunck. (Piperaceae).

articles.headline: Subnational stratification of 98 Liberian health districts by modeled P. falciparum prevalence (median 29%, range 17–37%) identified 84 districts with moderate and 14 with high transmission; stratification informed targeting of eight malaria control interventions and guided national operational plan revision
articles.title_original: Epidemiological stratification and sub-national tailoring of malaria interventions in Liberia.

articles.headline: Living systematic review compiled 9,270 individual patient records from 41 studies (through January 2025) evaluating daily primaquine regimens for P. vivax efficacy, tolerability, and safety; automated open-access R Shiny reports enable policy makers to assess location-specific effects of primaquine dose on antirelapse activity and adverse events
articles.title_original: Automated reporting of primaquine dose efficacy, tolerability and safety for Plasmodium vivax malaria using a systematic review and individual patient data meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 57 formal drug retail shops (9 community pharmacies, 48 over-the-counter sellers) in Ghana found 89.5% lacked malaria documentation/reporting tools; only 10.5% used paper-based methods; 61.4% preferred digital reporting despite barriers like resource constraints and inadequate training
articles.title_original: Exploring documentation and reporting practices in malaria tracking: a mixed-methods study among formal drug retail shops in Hohoe Municipal, Ghana.

articles.headline: Blighia sapida ethanol leaf extract showed dose-dependent activity against chloroquine-resistant P. berghei in mice (maximum suppression 77.02%, maximum cure 71.94% at 800 mg/kg), with hepatoprotective effects (400 mg/kg: AST 84.93 U/L vs control 133.88 U/L) and improved antioxidant status (800 mg/kg: MDA 0.22 nmol/mg protein, nitrite 3.79 µmol/mg protein)
articles.title_original: Antimalarial, Antioxidant and Hepatoprotective Activities of Ethanol Extract of Blighia sapida K.D. Koenig (Sapindaceae) in Plasmodium berghei-infected Mice.

articles.headline: Smoke from Vitex payos and Peltophorum africanum achieved 100% knockdown of An. gambiae s.l. (KdT50 37.8 and 44.8 min respectively) and 91.7% and 80% 24-h mortality; field repellency tests showed V. payos reduced An. funestus s.l. biting by 86% (p=0.008) and P. africanum by 73% (p=0.028), suggesting additive benefit with ITNs for pre-bedtime vector control
articles.title_original: Field evaluation of ethnomedicinal plants' smoke knockdown and repellent efficacy against pre-bedtime biting malaria vectors in Mazowe district, Zimbabwe.

articles.headline: PRC1584 and potent analog PRC1697, β-carboline compounds, demonstrated high resistance barrier and retained potent in vitro activity against fresh Ugandan P. falciparum isolates and dihydroartemisinin-induced dormant stages; PfKelch13-C580Y was associated with increased susceptibility to PRC1584, while PfKelch13-R549T and Pfcoronin mutations showed no association
articles.title_original: Characterization of β-Carboline Derivatives Reveals a High Barrier to Resistance and Potent Activity against Ring-Stage and DHA-Induced Dormant Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Review of P. falciparum histone lysine methyltransferases, which regulate parasite-specific epigenetic marks on AT-rich genome with limited transcription factors; identified as promising novel drug targets distinct from host histone modifications and supporting development of multistage antimalarials to overcome drug resistance
articles.title_original: Yet to SET: Plasmodium falciparum Histone Lysine Methyltransferases.

articles.headline: Review identifies platelet-derived TGF-β1 as key pathogenic mediator in cerebral malaria, inducing endothelial apoptosis and microangiopathy; reduced TGF-β1 in tissue/plasma correlates with CM severity; proposes brain-targeted exosomal delivery of TGF-β1 antibodies as novel therapeutic strategy
articles.title_original: Platelet-Derived Transforming Growth Factor-β1: A New Hope for Cerebral Malaria Treatment.

articles.headline: Membrane-permeable 5-fluorodeoxyuridine triphosphate derivatives (cpFdUTP) potently inhibited P. falciparum proliferation, arresting development at trophozoite-to-schizont transition via DNA replication stalling; thymidine supplementation selectively rescued human cells while maintaining parasite inhibition, establishing a potential therapeutic window for combination therapy
articles.title_original: Membrane-Permeable 5-Fluorodeoxyuridine Triphosphate Derivatives Inhibit the Proliferation of Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Recombinant AsSGU (secretory glycoconjugate expressed in An. stephensi midgut) was cloned in E. coli BL21(DE3), expressed at 0.2 mg/L, and demonstrated immunogenic potential via in-silico analysis and rabbit immunization; protein conserved across Anopheles, Culex, Aedes and critical for ookinete invasion, supporting investigation as transmission-blocking vaccine candidate
articles.title_original: Cloning, expression and purification of AsSGU protein and its immunogenic response analysis as target candidate for transmission blocking vaccine against malaria.

articles.headline: Observational study (2015-2022) of malaria in children <5 across North and Far North Cameroon. Maximum incidence 190.6–243.8/1000 person-years. Rainfall ≥1mm/day increased transmission risk 1.2–1.4 fold; 70% humidity increased risk 2–3.7 fold. Year-to-year variations showed strong correlation with meteorological factors, suggesting potential for weather-based forecasting
articles.title_original: Spatio-temporal dynamics of malaria incidence in children under five years of age and correlation with meteorological variability in the North and Far North regions of Cameroon between 2015-2022.

articles.headline: Genomic analysis of 458 P. falciparum isolates from Mali (2023). Strongest selection signal around pfcrt gene. PfCRT K76T prevalence 55.8% in Pfsa1-carrying vs 26.6% wild-type parasites (p<0.005). Pfsa1–PfCRT K76T linkage indicates critical axis of parasite adaptation to drug pressure and host genetics, warranting surveillance
articles.title_original: Genome-wide SNP genotyping of Plasmodium falciparum isolates across Mali reveals major impacts of Pfsa1 and PfCRT K76T selections on parasite populations.

articles.headline: Molecular study disrupting DNA repair enzymes in P. falciparum. PfRad51 essential for homologous recombination in core genome; subtelomeric regions use Rad51-independent homology-directed repair. These differential repair pathways maintain unique chromosome structure with conserved core and hypervariable var gene-containing subtelomeric regions
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum DNA repair dynamics reveal unique roles for TLS polymerases and PfRad51 in genome diversification.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis (69 studies, 43 quantitative) of reactive surveillance/response for malaria elimination. Implementation fidelity of case investigation: 100% completeness/timeliness in China vs 56% timeliness in Africa and 57% in Greater Mekong Subregion. Regional variations reflect disparities in health system capacity requiring country-specific strategy refinement
articles.title_original: Implementation and fidelity of reactive surveillance and response strategies for malaria elimination: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 81,678 migrants across 11 MENA countries. Hookworm prevalence 4.7% (vs 0.03% non-migrants), Trichuris 1.8%, Ascaris 1.75%, taeniasis 1.8%. Malaria incidence reported only in Sudan (6.8–7.3/1000 in refugees), case fatality 0.2–1.5%. Migrants disproportionately affected by NTDs and malaria
articles.title_original: The burden, clinical outcomes and risk factors related to neglected tropical diseases and malaria in migrant populations in the Middle East and North Africa: a systematic review and meta-analyses.

articles.headline: Experimental study of P. falciparum digestive vacuole across CQ-sensitive NF54 and CQ-resistant Dd2 parasites. Three developmental phases: lag-type (20–28h), rapid growth (28–40h), plateau (40–48h). Distinct phase-specific variations in vacuole size, plasmepsin abundance, and hemozoin levels between sensitive/resistant strains inform drug targeting
articles.title_original: Heme processing in the malaria parasite, Plasmodium falciparum: a time-dependent basal-level analysis.
articles.authors: Garnie LF, Egan TJ, Wicht KJ

articles.headline: Case report of 15-year-old with sickle cell disease presenting with protracted hemolysis, fever, and neuropsychiatric symptoms (5 weeks duration). Cerebral malaria diagnosed and treated with artesunate with prompt recovery, though hemolysis recurred 12 days post-treatment. Illustrates complex interplay of malaria, SCD, and autoimmune hemolytic anemia
articles.title_original: Protracted Febrile Illness and Coma in an Adolescent with Sickle Cell Disease and Malaria.

articles.headline: Mass spectrometry metabolomics of P. knowlesi blood stages (ring, trophozoite, schizont). Ring stage showed distinct metabolic profile. Glycerophospholipid metabolism and sphingolipid biosynthesis key across all stages; phosphatidylserine synthesis ring-stage-biased. CDP-diacylglycerol-inositol 3-phosphatidyltransferase identified as promising drug target
articles.title_original: Mass spectrometry-based metabolomics uncovers distinct metabolic signatures and potential therapeutic targets in Plasmodium knowlesi.

articles.headline: Title queries appropriateness of sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine/amodiaquine seasonal malaria chemoprevention in East Africa; abstract not provided for specific efficacy data
articles.title_original: Is Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention with Sulfadoxine-Pyrimethamine/Amodiaquine Appropriate in East Africa?

articles.headline: Pharmacometric trial in Northern Uganda with 1,250 children (46% baseline parasitemia). SPAQ protective efficacy 56% against all parasitemia, 46% P. falciparum, 60% clinical malaria; dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DP) efficacy 84%, 86%, 86% respectively. Clinical malaria: SPAQ 8%, DP 2%, no drug 17%. DP substantially more effective in high-resistance setting
articles.title_original: Pharmacometric evaluation of sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine- amodiaquine and dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine seasonal malaria chemoprevention in northern Uganda.

articles.headline: Drug development optimizing species-selective reversible proteasome inhibitors against P. falciparum 20S proteasome from carboxypiperidine scaffold via iterative SAR. Lead compounds achieved nanomolar potency against blood-stage parasites with selective inhibition vs human proteasome. Cryo-EM confirmed β5 subunit binding; in vivo PK identified promising candidates supporting proteasome inhibition as viable antimalarial strategy
articles.title_original: Optimization of Species-Selective Reversible Proteasome Inhibitors for the Treatment of Malaria.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 288 febrile patients in Yaoundé (June–August 2023). Malaria prevalence 44.79%, typhoid 37.15%, coinfection 19.79%; malaria leading cause. Coinfection associated with fever (p=0.008) and profuse sweating (p=0.05). Water/grass accumulation around homes and drinking water source identified as risk factors
articles.title_original: Malaria and Typhoid Fever Coinfection Among Febrile Patients Attending a Health Facility in Yaoundé, Cameroon: A Cross-Sectional Study.

articles.headline: In experimental cerebral malaria at terminal stage, β-dystroglycan cleavage by MMP-9 and astrocyte alterations correlate with BBB disruption, brain edema, and loss of tight junction proteins ZO-1 and collagen IV; artemether treatment reduced Evans blue extravasation and FITC-dextran leakage, suggesting BBB stabilization through DGC protein preservation
articles.title_original: β-Dystroglycan Downregulation and Astrocytic Alterations: A Possible Role in Blood-Brain Barrier Disruption During Experimental Cerebral Malaria.

articles.headline: P. berghei IMC1j knockout impaired asexual fitness, virulence, and ookinete motility through disruption of subpellicular microtubule (SPMT) organization; the IMCp domain mediates interactions with δ-tubulin and ISC1 protein, showing C-terminal coiled-coil domain requirement for proper IMC function
articles.title_original: Plasmodium berghei IMC1j interacts with δ-tubulin to orchestrate subpellicular microtubule organization in ookinetes.

articles.headline: P. berghei RON6 deletion reduced parasite infectivity and virulence, prolonging mouse survival and inducing chronic malaria with hyper-reactive splenomegaly characterized by decreased B/T lymphocytes; RON6 localizes to sporozoite membrane with extracellular C-terminus, supporting its role in pre-erythrocytic invasion
articles.title_original: Plasmodium berghei rhoptry neck protein 6 maintains parasite infectivity and virulence.

articles.headline: P. falciparum shows A-type rRNA predominance during intraerythrocytic development and gametocytogenesis with S-type increase in mosquito stages; extensive rRNA degradation occurs during stage II–V gametocytogenesis, potentially linked to translational repression in quiescent stage V gametocytes
articles.title_original: Degradation of ribosomal RNA during Plasmodium falciparum gametocytogenesis.

articles.headline: Cas9/gRNA gene-drive-mediated target-site mutagenesis in A. gambiae produced mutant alleles at 0.08% (paternal) and 0.33% (maternal) rates per generation; 47% of inherited mutant alleles originated from germline mutations with clustering effects amplifying inheritance frequencies, informing realistic gene-drive modeling
articles.title_original: Mutant allele formation and inheritance during Cas9/guide RNA-mediated gene drive in a population modification mosquito strain for human malaria control.

articles.headline: For each P. falciparum or P. vivax-infected erythrocyte destroyed, ≥10 nonparasitized erythrocytes are cleared via C3b opsonization from hemin/hematin, digestive vacuole contents, and hemozoin released from lysed parasitized cells; complement inhibitors targeting C3 activation are proposed as potential therapy for malarial anemia
articles.title_original: Inhibition of Complement Will Substantially Reduce the Anemia of Malaria.

articles.headline: Review of Plasmodium sporozoite motility factors in human skin: ECM composition, tissue stiffness, and surface topography significantly influence parasite movement; gaps remain in understanding skin aging/inflammation effects on sporozoite navigation and identifying underlying signaling pathways for therapeutic targeting
articles.title_original: Influence of biophysical and biochemical cues on Plasmodium sporozoite dynamics in skin tissue.

articles.headline: Seasonal malaria chemoprevention in three Nigerian states reduced uncomplicated malaria incidence 50% (95% CI: 39–60) comparing 2022 (9 cases/1000 children aged 3–59 months) to 2021 (20/1000); all-cause fever incidence was 29% (95% CI: 14–41) lower, with variable impact across locations reflecting delivery quality differences
articles.title_original: Impact of seasonal malaria chemoprevention: a plausibility evaluation of routine data from health facilities in three implementing states in Nigeria.

articles.headline: Uganda's '24,2 Hours Initiative' targets timely malaria case management: uncomplicated cases within 24 hours and severe cases within 2 hours of facility arrival with pre-referral treatment; emphasizes community-based case management for all ages and post-discharge follow-up to reduce progression to severe malaria
articles.title_original: The 24, 2 hours initiative: a game changer in malaria mortality reduction.

articles.headline: EFFORT trial found tafenoquine (single-dose 300 mg) and high-dose primaquine (7 mg/kg, 7 days) well tolerated and superior to standard 14-day low-dose primaquine (3.5 mg/kg) for P. vivax radical cure in Pakistan and other South Asian countries; WHO 2024 endorsed both regimens as replacements despite implementation and G6PD testing challenges
articles.title_original: Effectiveness of tafenoquine and primaquine for radical cure of Plasmodium vivax: a meeting report from dissemination of results of the EFFORT trial to stakeholders in Pakistan.

articles.headline: Protocol for PCPI trial in Zambia comparing single-dose sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP) vs. 7-day artesunate for perennial malaria chemoprevention in asymptomatic children 3–5 years; designed to evaluate K540E dhps mutation impact on SP efficacy and measure parasite clearance time, protection duration, and symptom-free status through day 63
articles.title_original: Parasite clearance and protection from Plasmodium falciparum infection (PCPI): a two-arm, parallel, double-blinded, placebo-controlled, randomised trial of presumptive sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine versus artesunate monotherapy among asymptomatic children 3-5 years of age in Zambia.

articles.headline: In Mbouda, Cameroon, P. falciparum prevalence was 25.34% with Pfdhps resistance gene in 74.66% of positive samples; A437G (56%) and A581G (64%) mutations showed mutant allele frequencies of 57.14% and 91.66% respectively, predominantly in ages 6–35 with higher female prevalence for A437G
articles.title_original: Polymorphism of the dihydropteroate synthase resistance gene of Plasmodium falciparum in three hospitals in Mbouda, Western Cameroon.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 33 studies (Jan 2021–Mar 2025) on malaria vaccine awareness and acceptance in sub-Saharan Africa. Awareness ranged 7.26% (DRC) to 95.6% (Ghana); acceptance 10.3–100%, highest in Nigeria and Ghana. Education and occupation were consistently associated with greater awareness/acceptance; awareness correlated with acceptance. Healthcare workers were the primary information source
articles.title_original: Public awareness and acceptance of malaria vaccine in Sub-Saharan Africa: a systematic review.

articles.headline: Longitudinal qualitative study of BOHEMIA ivermectin mass drug administration trial in Kwale County, Kenya (5 villages: 22 in-depth interviews, observations, 15 focus group discussions). Trust and institutional confidence fostered community participation; prior negative MDA experiences reduced involvement. Ivermectin MDA was widely perceived as effective against mosquitoes and malaria in both intervention and control arms, and against bedbugs in the intervention arm
articles.title_original: Community Experiences and Perceptions of the Broad One Health Endectocide-Based Malaria Intervention in Africa Trial of Ivermectin Mass Drug Administration: A Longitudinal Qualitative Study in Kwale County, Kenya.

articles.headline: Prospective cohort of 75 P. falciparum and 75 P. vivax patients receiving 3-day dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine in Timika, Indonesia (May–Dec 2023). P. falciparum cumulative same-species recurrence by day 42: 1.5% (95% CI 0–7.9%, PCR-adjusted); P. vivax: 5.2% (95% CI 1.1–14.1%, unadjusted). No kelch13 mutations or elevated plasmepsin 2–3 copies detected. No evidence of treatment failure or selection of resistant parasites
articles.title_original: Treatment Efficacy of Dihydroartemisinin-Piperaquine for Uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax Malaria in Timika, Papua, Indonesia.

articles.headline: Genetic study in P. berghei identifying actin-like proteins Alp5a and Alp5b as essential for DNA segregation during male gametogenesis. Knockout parasites produced significantly smaller oocysts that failed to mature due to impaired male gamete integrity. Alp5a and Alp5b are individually dispensable for blood-stage development but jointly indispensable for parasite viability and malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Plasmodium actin-like proteins are essential for DNA segregation during male gametogenesis and malaria transmission.

articles.headline: Cryo-EM structure of endogenous Pfs230:Pfs48/45 complex (leading transmission-blocking vaccine candidate) bound to six transmission-blocking antibodies. Structure revealed Pfs230 comprises domain clusters rigidified by insertion domains, with membrane-anchored Pfs48/45 forming a disk-like structure. Analysis identified conserved epitopes and structural basis for complement-dependent antibody activity, informing next-generation P. falciparum transmission-blocking interventions
articles.title_original: Cryo-EM structure of endogenous Pfs230:Pfs48/45 complex with six antibodies reveals mechanisms of malaria transmission-blocking activity.

articles.headline: Quantitative survey of 37 respondents (of 300 invited) involved in TB, malaria, and NTD clinical trials in sub-Saharan Africa (2008–2019). 43% did not provide post-trial access (PTA) plans; 70.3% expressed need for PTA training. Overall lack of formalized PTA policies and commitments, highlighting urgent need for structured PTA policy training programs and stakeholder collaboration in low- and middle-income countries
articles.title_original: Post-trial access practices in conducted clinical trials for Malaria, Tuberculosis, and Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) across Sub-Saharan African countries: A quantitative study.

articles.headline: Retrospective study of 109 children aged 5–15 with confirmed severe malaria in Senegal (2019–2024, mean age 9.56±2.87 years). Common features: prostration (27%), convulsions (21%), coma (17%), severe anemia (33%), thrombocytopenia (62%). Outcomes: 90% favorable, 5.5% sequelae, 4.5% mortality. Mortality significantly associated with respiratory distress and ≥2 WHO severity criteria
articles.title_original: Characteristics and mortality factors of severe malaria in children and teenager aged from 5 to 15 years in Thiès region, Senegal.

articles.headline: Epitope excision study of VAR2CSA peptides on P. falciparum-infected erythrocytes, comparing IgG responses in primigravidae versus multigravidae. While most epitopes showed similar recognition, multigravidae IgG differentially recognized 10 epitopes conserved across VAR2CSA alleles (Log2 fold change >1, p<0.05). These differentially recognized epitopes inform design of next-generation VAR2CSA subunit vaccine for placental malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Identification of Plasmodium falciparum VAR2CSA peptides differentially recognized by IgG of multigravidae through epitope excision.

articles.headline: Genetic study of inner membrane complex protein IMC55 in P. falciparum using conditional inducible expression systems. Transposon mutagenesis screen predicted IMC55 essential; however, targeted deletions revealed IMC55 is dispensable or functionally redundant for asexual blood-stage replication. Findings demonstrate discordance between genome-wide screens and targeted genetic validation
articles.title_original: The inner membrane complex protein, IMC55, is dispensable for intra-erythrocytic development of Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 459 participants across three ecological zones in Ghana (forest, coastal, savannah). Malaria prevalence 15.2% (RDT), 9.7% (microscopy), highest in savannah. Geometric mean parasite density: coastal 11,715.6/µL, savannah 7,616.4/µL, forest 5,880.1/µL (p=0.0003). Anemia prevalence 79.3% overall (94.1% savannah, 82.9% coastal, 69.8% forest; p<0.0001). ITN ownership 69.7%, usage 61.3%
articles.title_original: Clinical malaria in three ecological zones in the Volta and Oti Regions of Ghana.

articles.headline: Prospective validation of 37,137 malaria RDT images from 16 Ugandan facilities against external expert interpretation (June–Nov 2023). Overall agreement strong (κ=0.81, 95% CI 0.76–0.85). Healthcare workers misrecorded more results as positive (7.2%) than negative (1.9%); agreement higher in Busoga (κ=0.86) than Lango (κ=0.78). Certain RDT products and faint lines associated with lower accuracy
articles.title_original: Accuracy of recording of malaria rapid diagnostic test results in Uganda.

articles.headline: Descriptive cross-sectional household survey in Sennar State, Sudan (2018–2020) covering 2,027 households, 8,372 individuals. Malaria prevalence: 2.8% (2018), 4.8% (2019), 3.3% (2020); fever prevalence 41.0%, 58.5%, 48.4% respectively. Malaria deaths increased from 12 (2018–2019) to 22 (2020). Abu Hujar locality highest prevalence (13.6% in 2019). Locality-specific fluctuations suggest need for tailored interventions
articles.title_original: Epidemiological trends of malaria in Sennar State, Sudan.

articles.headline: Cuproptosis exacerbates experimental cerebral malaria (P. berghei ANKA in mice) by promoting astrocyte reactivity; copper ionophore disulfiram worsened disease progression while copper chelator tetrathiomolybdate suppressed pathological manifestations
articles.title_original: Cuproptosis-driven astrocyte reactivity exacerbates experimental cerebral malaria pathogenesis.

articles.headline: LAMP training (15 technicians; 14 with no prior experience): technical knowledge increased 4 points to median 5/5 (range 4-5); LAMP chemistry knowledge increased 9 points to median 10/10 (range 6-10); follow-up assessments sustained confidence with 10/10 median for result interpretation
articles.title_original: Evaluation of laboratory workers competency on loop mediated isothermal amplification (LAMP) technology for malaria diagnostics: a technology transfer assessment study (LAMP-TTA) during a multi-site clinical trial.
articles.authors: Tegegne B, Kamaliddin C, Pillai DR

articles.headline: Uganda Malaria Indicator Survey 2018-19 (8,868 women, 6,915 children <5): 69.3% of women (95% CI 67.2-71.2) and 71.8% of children (95% CI 69.1-74.4) slept under ITNs. Malaria message exposure increased ITN use 5.1% in women (95% CI 1.5-8.9%) and 4.3% in children (95% CI 1.0-7.6%)
articles.title_original: Effect of exposure to malaria messages on insecticide-treated net use among women and under-five children in Uganda: a propensity score matched analysis.

articles.headline: Year-long study in Amazonian Peru (353 residents): 17:1 ratio submicroscopic to microscopic P. vivax infections detected by qPCR vs microscopy. Outdoor occupation associated with 35% P. vivax infection vs 21% indoor workers (p=0.012). Secondary vector An. near costai infected with P. vivax and P. malariae (first in Peru)
articles.title_original: Submicroscopic infections and two malaria vectors are major contributors to residual malaria in an Amazonian village in Peru.

articles.headline: Match-counting methods for recrudescence vs reinfection in polyclonal P. falciparum have unacceptably high false-discovery rates. PfRecur software provides Bayesian posterior probabilities for accurate treatment failure classification in high-transmission endemic settings
articles.title_original: Probabilistic classification of late treatment failure in uncomplicated falciparum malaria.

articles.headline: 13-year observational study (Luangwa, Zambia, 2009-2021): Pirimiphos-methyl (PM-CS) IRS reduced inpatient admissions (RR 0.28 [95% CI 0.19-0.98] p=0.0019 for ≤3 months) and total cases (RR 0.25 [95% CI 0.01-0.67] p=0.0017). Deltamethrin-clothianidin reduced total cases (RR 0.15 [95% CI 0.10-0.68] p=0.0017 for months 1-3)
articles.title_original: Impact of malaria vector control interventions implemented in Luangwa District southeastern of Zambia: A 13-year observational time series analysis of malaria trends.

articles.headline: Qualitative study in rural Ingwavuma, South Africa (8 in-depth interviews with healthcare workers, 8 focus groups with 88 community members): Communities positively viewed malaria control and health education; healthcare workers reported surveillance challenges from cross-border movement with Mozambique; some negative IRS experiences noted
articles.title_original: Experiences of Community Members and Health Workers Regarding Malaria Control Programmes in the Rural Ingwavuma Community, uMkhanyakude District Municipality, KwaZulu-Natal Province, South Africa.
articles.authors: Thembakazi MT, Herbert C, Nelisiwe K

articles.headline: No abstract provided for: Leveraging malaria microscopy infrastructure to diagnose common and neglected skin diseases using direct microscopy in Sumba, Indonesia
articles.title_original: Leveraging malaria microscopy infrastructure to diagnose common and neglected skin diseases using direct microscopy in Sumba, Indonesia.

articles.headline: BOHEMIA trial (Mozambique, 22,724 participants, 100 clusters): Ivermectin 400 mcg/kg monthly ×3 months vs albendazole control for malaria incidence in children <5. Comparable incidence: human-only vs control AIRR 0.99 (95% CI 0.86-1.14, p>0.9). Safety profile comparable
articles.title_original: Ivermectin to reduce malaria transmission- safety and efficacy results from the BOHEMIA cluster randomized trial in Mozambique.

articles.headline: Cluster-randomized trial in Tanzania (127 schools, >73,000 schoolchildren; 1,971 intervention, 1,781 control): Dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine via schoolteachers achieved 77-80% coverage across three rounds. Reduced clinical malaria incidence 41% overall (95% CI 31-49, p<0.001) and 54% in high-endemic areas (95% CI 44.2-62.6, p<0.001)
articles.title_original: Implementation and effectiveness of intermittent preventive treatment in school aged children using dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine to reduce malaria burden: an implementation research of a cluster randomised trial in Tanzania.

articles.headline: Pyrethroid-resistant Anopheles coluzzii microsomes metabolized fluorene 73% (Km 58.69 µM, turnover 37 min⁻¹) and fluoranthene 43%. Recombinant CYP6P3 and CYP6Z3 metabolized fluorene 47.9% and 52.8% (p=0.001); CYP6Z2/6Z3 metabolized fluoranthene 50.4% and 60.3% (p≤0.003). No naphthalene metabolism observed
articles.title_original: Investigating the potential role of metabolic resistance genes in conferring cross-resistance to pyrethroids and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon pollutants in the major malaria vector Anopheles coluzzii.

articles.headline: N'Djamena, Chad (302 patients, 4 health facilities): P. falciparum prevalence 40.72%; MSP-2 gene in 101 samples (78.21%) with 12 alleles and 9.37% genetic diversity. Multiplicity of infection 1.26: 73.26% monoclonal, 25.74% double clonal, 1% triple clonal; 11 phylogenetic clades identified
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of MSP2 and its implications for Plasmodium falciparum resistance in N'Djamena, Chad.

articles.headline: Among 18,319 RDTs recorded in Nigerian health facility registers, 6.2% were misrecorded as positive and 3.7% as negative compared to independent panel interpretation, yielding 87.2% PPV (95% CI 86.4–87.8%) and 92.9% NPV, with overall 90.2% agreement (kappa 0.80, 95% CI 0.75–0.85). Faint test lines (8.8% of RDTs) and lower malaria prevalence areas showed higher misrecording rates. Improved training and supervision are needed to reduce RDT misrecording despite strong overall interrater agreement
articles.title_original: Accuracy of recording and reporting of malaria rapid diagnostic tests in Nigeria.

articles.headline: Survey of 381 household heads in Upper River Region, The Gambia found 81% knowledge index for basic malaria concepts. Treated bed nets were the most effective prevention tool, while malaria cases declined 87% in those >5 years and 45% in those <5 years over 2011–2023, with mortality reductions of 98% and 89% respectively. High baseline knowledge fostered positive attitudes toward malaria prevention and treatment
articles.title_original: Evaluation of knowledge, perception, and attitude of malaria burden in the Upper River region of The Gambia.

articles.headline: Among 3,692 women from the 2022 Ghana Demographic and Health Survey, 61.3% received at least three doses of IPTp-SP. Receipt of ≥8 ANC visits (OR 1.50, 95% CI 1.18–1.90, p=0.001), first visit in first trimester (OR 1.74, 95% CI 1.44–2.09, p<0.001), and all ANC components (OR 1.62, 95% CI 1.29–2.03, p<0.001) were independently associated with higher IPTp-SP uptake. Adequate ANC services should be targeted to improve intermittent preventive treatment uptake during pregnancy
articles.title_original: Association between adequacy of antenatal care and uptake of intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy among women in Ghana.

articles.headline: Structural equation modeling of 2018–2019 Uganda Malaria Indicator Survey data identified Child Health as the strongest predictor of child malaria prevalence (β=0.22, p<0.001), with Environment showing marginal negative association (β=−0.36, p=0.056), while Socioeconomic Status and Maternal Health were not statistically significant. Model fit indices (CFI 0.786, RMSEA 0.064) showed meaningful but moderate relationships. Child-level health and environmental factors warrant prioritization in integrated malaria control interventions
articles.title_original: Structural equation modelling (SEM) for malaria prevalence and risk factors in Uganda.

articles.headline: Metapopulation modeling of malaria transmission in Chittagong Hill Tracts, Bangladesh revealed substantial heterogeneity in transmission intensity across districts. Vector control interventions had greatest impact in areas with both high transmission and high connectivity to populated western districts. Local elimination efforts in low or intermediate incidence areas produced only moderate overall incidence reduction, suggesting that targeting high-incidence areas alone is insufficient for regional elimination
articles.title_original: Identifying malaria elimination strategies in the presence of human movement in Bangladesh.

articles.headline: Eight single-domain Pfs230 fragments produced in Drosophila S2 cells were tested for transmission-blocking vaccine (TBV) potential. Pfs230 Domain 12 (D12) induced strong functional transmission-reducing antibodies in mouse immunizations with complement-dependent activity in membrane feeding assays and reduced mosquito transmission from naturally infected parasite carriers. D12 antigens were naturally recognized by sera from P. falciparum-exposed individuals with age-related increases, identifying Pfs230D12 as a promising TBV candidate
articles.title_original: Pfs230 domain 12 is a potent malaria transmission-blocking vaccine candidate.

articles.headline: Updated malaria risk stratification for all 75 Mali Health Districts (2018–2022) classified 54 districts in highest/moderate transmission zones covering 84% of the population (~18 million). Selection identified 61 districts eligible for LLIN mass distribution, 57 for SMC, 67 for IPTp, and 19 for vaccination. Stratification revealed that interventions must address 55 high-burden districts plus connectivity patterns to optimize control efforts given Mali's 37% malaria morbidity and 25% mortality rates
articles.title_original: Geo-epidemiological risk stratification to select malaria interventions, case of Mali.

articles.headline: Over 2018–2022, confirmed babesiosis cases (9,799) exceeded malaria cases (7,722) in 4 of 5 years, with 70 babesiosis deaths versus 32 malaria deaths in the US. NIH funding for babesiosis was ~$4 million versus ~$169 million for malaria in 2024. Most US malaria was imported (>90%) and P. falciparum–associated; domestic babesiosis (likely Babesia microti) predominated without chemoprophylaxis. Leveraging malaria-level funding and research infrastructure for babesiosis is warranted
articles.title_original: Babesiosis and Malaria in the United States: Epidemiology, Research Funding, Medical Progress, & Recommendations for Improvement.

articles.headline: Ensemble learning combining VGG16, ResNet50V2, DenseNet201, and VGG19 achieved 97.93% test accuracy with F1-score and precision of 0.9793 for automated malaria parasite classification in blood smears, outperforming standalone models (Custom CNN 97.20%, VGG16 97.65%, CNN-SVM 82.47%). The approach reliably classified parasitized and uninfected blood smears. This scalable automated solution reduces manual microscopy dependence and is applicable in resource-constrained settings
articles.title_original: An optimized transfer learning approach integrating deep convolutional feature extractors for malaria parasite classification in erythrocyte microscopy.

articles.headline: Cryoelectron tomography and cryoelectron microscopy resolved ten ribosomal states of P. falciparum in infected erythrocytes, including five previously unseen in eukaryotes. Visualization revealed parasite-specific translation elongation cycle dynamics and showed how the antimalarial cabamiquine disrupts elongation. This first in situ view of P. falciparum translation provides a comprehensive understanding of parasite-specific translation mechanisms
articles.title_original: Seeing is believing-Plasmodium falciparum translation in action.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 124 hospitalized malaria patients in Rwanda (December 2023–November 2024) found 27.4% severe thrombocytopenia prevalence. Age ≤5 years (AOR 5.17, 95% CI 1.67–16.26, p=0.004), Plasmodium species type (AOR 3.4, 95% CI 2.41–8.3, p=0.04), positive CRP (AOR 3.45, 95% CI 2.23–3.56, p=0.045), jaundice (AOR 2.88, 95% CI 1.36–26.5, p=0.045), and high serum urea (AOR 1.73, 95% CI 1.23–1.83, p=0.001) were associated with severe thrombocytopenia; cerebral malaria was protective (AOR 0.19, p=0.028). Early identification of severe thrombocytopenia in high-risk malaria patients is essential
articles.title_original: Prevalence and factors associated with severe thrombocytopenia among hospitalized patients with malaria attending Kigeme District Hospital, Southern Province, Rwanda: cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: The CALINA phase 2/3 trial evaluated optimized artemether-lumefantrine (5 mg:60 mg, 1:12 ratio) in 22 infants <5 kg with P. falciparum malaria. Artemether Cmax was 68.0 ng/mL (90% CI 45.1–103 ng/mL), meeting the primary endpoint for exposure matching to standard-dose recipients 5–<15 kg; lumefantrine C168h and Cmax were comparable. PCR-corrected Day 29 ACPR was 95.5% (Cohort 1) and 100% (Cohort 2) with good tolerability and normal developmental assessments at 12 months. The optimized dose achieves efficacy and safety targets in <5 kg P. falciparum patients
articles.title_original: Pharmacokinetics, safety and efficacy of an optimized dose of artemether-lumefantrine in the treatment of acute uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in neonates and infants of less than 5 kg body weight: a multicentre, open-label, single-arm phase 2/3 study (CALINA).

articles.headline: A transdisciplinary study in Chiredzi District, Zimbabwe (2010–2022) combined quantitative weather data, malaria incidence surveillance, and focus group discussions to examine climate change links to malaria. Statistical analysis yielded p=0.0479, showing a positive relationship between climate change and malaria incidence, though negative associations in some years were attributed to other factors including poverty and migration. The authors call for new prevention programs accounting for climate, environmental, and socioeconomic factors
articles.title_original: Climate change and malaria in Chiredzi District, Zimbabwe: emerging evidence and pathways towards malaria prevention.

articles.headline: A pre-post analysis in 285 villages in Nouna District, Burkina Faso, evaluated Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention (SMC) impact on children under 5 using surveillance data (2021). During SMC administration weeks, uncomplicated malaria incidence was 8.5 per 1000 person-weeks (95% CI 7.0–10.1) and severe malaria 0.31 per 1000 person-weeks (95% CI 0.22–0.40). Post-SMC reductions were 41% (95% CI 31–50%), 34% (23–43%), and 22% (12–31%) for uncomplicated malaria in weeks 1–3; severe malaria declined 47%, 47%, and 34% respectively. Protection waned faster than previously observed
articles.title_original: Trends in uncomplicated and severe malaria following seasonal malaria chemoprevention administration in Nouna, Burkina Faso: a quasi-experimental pre-post study.

articles.headline: A longitudinal analysis of 4,726 individuals aged ≥16 from 20 malaria-endemic African countries (Premise COVID-19 survey) assessed malaria healthcare utilization before, during, and 1 year into the COVID-19 pandemic. One year into the pandemic, malaria testing declined to 59.12% (45% less likely; AOR=0.55, 95% CI 0.48–0.63), healthcare provider visits fell to 1.25% (99.7% less likely; AOR=0.003, 95% CI 0.002–0.005), and treatment receipt dropped to 15.23% (99.6% less likely; AOR=0.004, 95% CI 0.002–0.006). Significant disruption in malaria control services persisted 1 year into the pandemic
articles.title_original: Malaria healthcare service utilization before, during, and 1 year into the COVID-19 pandemic in 20 sub-Saharan African countries using Generalized Estimating Equation.

articles.headline: Analysis of 3,004 women from the 2019 Ghana Malaria Indicator Survey using the Multi-Population Curie-Weiss Model examined factors influencing malaria vaccination decisions. Overall, 93.54% intended vaccination and 6.45% did not. Among vaccinators, 72.10% were educated and 40.10% aware of the vaccine; among non-vaccinators, 78.35% were educated and 28.87% aware. Rural educated women showed modest 1.62% increased intrinsic motivation, while rural uneducated women exerted greater social influence on their educated counterparts
articles.title_original: Untangling choices: how awareness and socio-economic status shape malaria vaccination decisions among Ghanaian women.

articles.headline: A comparative analysis of post-2020 Malaria Indicator Surveys across Liberia, Mali, Niger, and Nigeria (nationally representative samples of women aged 15–49) examined malaria message exposure and under-5 children's mosquito net use. Net use varied from 47% in Nigeria to 86% in Niger. In the full regression model, Liberia (OR=1.31), Mali (OR=9.90), and Niger (OR=3.67) showed increased odds of net use, while Nigeria showed reduced odds (OR=0.31). Malaria information from health facilities and household composition were significant predictors
articles.title_original: A comparative analysis of the predictive influence of access to malaria messages on utilization of mosquito nets by under-five children in West Africa.

articles.headline: A 16S rRNA metabarcoding study assessed whether avian Plasmodium relictum exposure altered Culex pipiens microbiota. Mosquitoes fed on naturally infected house sparrows versus uninfected controls; 2,006 ASVs were identified, dominated by Wolbachia. No significant differences in alpha or beta diversity emerged between exposed and unexposed mosquitoes. However, exposed mosquitoes showed higher relative abundance of Bacteroidaceae and Bacteroides, with enriched metabolic and biosynthesis pathways. Causality between Plasmodium exposure and microbiota shifts requires further investigation
articles.title_original: Effects of avian Plasmodium exposure on the microbiota of Culex pipiens.

articles.headline: Correction notice only; no study content to summarize
articles.title_original: Correction to: Electroencephalogram Features Distinguish Cases of Cerebral Malaria Among Malawian Children With Fever and Coma.

articles.headline: A retrospective case-control study compared EEG recordings of 203 Malawian children with cerebral malaria to 87 with nonmalarial febrile coma (bacterial meningitis, viral encephalitis, or unknown cause) admitted 2013–2021. Qualitative EEG interpretation yielded AUROC=0.86, quantitative waveform analysis AUROC=0.85, and combined interpretation AUROC=0.90, distinguishing malarial from nonmalarial coma. EEG features differentiating cerebral malaria included higher voltage, slower frequency, more sleep elements, and reduced continuity compared to other etiologies
articles.title_original: Electroencephalogram Features Distinguish Cases of Cerebral Malaria Among Malawian Children With Fever and Coma.

articles.headline: A census of 211 malaria cases in Trinidad and Tobago (2009–2019) evaluated outbreak risk from migrant populations. P. vivax accounted for 68.2% of cases, P. falciparum 22.3%; 91.9% were imported (39.7% from Guyana, 38.7% from Venezuela, 15.5% from sub-Saharan Africa). Log-transformed Venezuelan arrivals showed moderate correlation with malaria cases (r=0.642, R²=0.412, p=0.033). Venezuelan migration significantly predicted malaria incidence, indicating substantial transmission risk
articles.title_original: Assessing the Potential for a Malaria Outbreak in Trinidad and Tobago Due to Migrant Populations From Guyana and Venezuela for the Period 2009-2019.

articles.headline: A controlled study treated 35 Swiss mice infected with chloroquine-resistant Plasmodium berghei (ANKA strain) with daily oral 10 mg/kg doses of amodiaquine-artesunate (AA), artemether-lumefantrine (AL), sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP), or artesunate (ART). Artesunate opened the mitochondrial permeability transition pore 7-fold and enhanced FoF1 ATPase (P<0.01); SP and AA induced mitochondrial lipid peroxidation (P<0.01) and silenced mitochondrial complex expression. Effects on mitochondrial dynamics and mitophagy genes varied significantly across all four antimalarials
articles.title_original: Comparative effects of antimalarial drugs on oxidative phosphorylation, mitochondrial dynamics and mitophagy in Plasmodium berghei-infected mice.

articles.headline: A population genomics study of Anopheles coluzzii using whole-genome SNPs and inversion karyotypes across West African bioclimatic zones revealed restricted gene flow between northern savannah and southern forested regions. Target-site pyrethroid and metabolic cross-resistance were greater in the North, while Greater Accra's coastal region showed distinct resistance mechanisms. Environmental barriers constrain gene flow, restricting the geographic spread of insecticide resistance alleles
articles.title_original: Genomic population structure and insecticide resistance mechanisms in the malaria vector An. coluzzii across contrasting bioclimatic zones in West Africa.

articles.headline: An evaluation of novel 4-quinolone derivatives in ex vivo and in vivo assays assessed activity against asexual intraerythrocytic, liver, and transmission-blocking stages of Plasmodium spp. Compound 1, a natural 2-substituted-4-quinolone, and synthetic derivatives 2–6 displayed significant activity against sexual stages and effectively prevented hepatic cell infections. Compound 1 showed the highest activity against both sexual and asexual parasitic stages, warranting further development as a multistage antimalarial
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the Activity of 4‑Quinolones against Multi-Life Stages of Plasmodium spp.

articles.headline: Targeted Oxford Nanopore sequencing of 72 P. falciparum isolates from southwest Cameroon revealed very high prevalence of pfdhfr mutations (98.6% at positions N51I and C59R, 97.7% S108N), pfmdr1 Y184F in 76.1%, and pfdhps mutations (54.2% S436A, 38.9% A581G, 2.8% A437G), with no WHO-validated artemisinin-resistance markers but K189T at 63.4%. Findings support continued artemisinin-based combination therapy use but emphasize need for sustained molecular surveillance of drug-resistance and vaccine-related CSP polymorphisms
articles.title_original: Targeted Sequencing Identifies SNPs Associated with Antimalarial Drug Resistance and the CSP Vaccine Antigen in Plasmodium falciparum from Southwest Cameroon.

articles.headline: Among 3,875 malaria cases presenting to 94 health facilities across Zanzibar (May 2022–December 2023), 1,172 (30.2%) reported mainland Tanzania travel in preceding 30 days; children and females had elevated odds (aOR 3.2, 95% CI 2.2–4.8 and aOR 1.5, 95% CI 1.3–1.8) of travel-associated malaria. Meta mobility data and rainfall patterns showed imported cases peaked when travelers visited high-risk mainland regions during dry seasons, identifying predictable movement flows that can target high-risk travelers for intervention
articles.title_original: Integrating mobility, travel survey, and malaria case data to understand drivers of malaria importation to Zanzibar, 2022-2023.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional household survey of ITN use factors in Burkina Faso identified polyester nets (OR 1.23, 95% CI 1.13–1.33) and newer ITNs associated with greater use, while nets ≥12 months old (OR 0.52, 95% CI 0.401–0.675) and excessive possession (OR 0.66, 95% CI 0.597–0.74) reduced use; wealth was negatively associated with ITN utilization. Findings indicate ITN textile type, age, and number possessed are critical factors driving household adoption
articles.title_original: Factors associated with insecticide-treated mosquito nets utilization for malaria prevention in Burkina Faso: finding from cross-sectional household survey.

articles.headline: In vitro ferulenol treatment of P. falciparum inhibited asexual blood-stage proliferation by 88%, early-stage gametocyte development by 82%, stage V gametocyte maturation by ~90%, male gamete formation by 81%, and female gamete formation by 27%. These results position ferulenol as a dual-target antimalarial capable of disrupting both disease severity and transmission
articles.title_original: Disruption of mitochondrial function in blood and sexual stages of Plasmodium falciparum by ferulenol.

articles.headline: Mixed-methods evaluation of national insecticide-resistance-management (IRM) plans in Cameroon, Côte d'Ivoire, and Tanzania (2023) found each country had developed 5-year plans aligned with national strategies and international guidelines, but implementation was severely hindered by inadequate domestic funding and reliance on external donors, leading to inactive monitoring sites and limited resistance data. Critical gaps include insufficient sentinel-site coverage and underfunded vector-control programs despite documented insecticide resistance in malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Managing insecticide resistance in malaria vectors in Africa: case studies from Cameroon, Côte d'Ivoire and Tanzania.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 30 studies found no significant difference in IL-2 levels between individuals with Plasmodium infection and uninfected controls (P=0.25, SMD=4.56, 95% CI –3.16 to 12.29) or between severe and non-severe malaria cases (P=0.57, SMD=0.37, 95% CI –0.91 to 1.67), though geographic region significantly influenced IL-2 expression patterns. Substantial heterogeneity limited interpretability, indicating IL-2 is not a reliable biomarker for malaria infection or severity without additional immunological markers
articles.title_original: Association between interleukin-2 cytokine levels and Plasmodium infections: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Pediatric CNS-M patients (n=37) showed prolonged dysregulation at 6 weeks post-recovery: upregulated interleukin/cytokine signaling, neutrophil degranulation, and overexpression of LAG3, CCR5, PDGFRB, ICMT, with uniquely downregulated hemostasis pathway; biomarkers of coagulation, fibrinolysis, neutrophil activation, and cell-free DNA remained elevated at 6 weeks, unlike uncomplicated malaria (UM) and acute febrile non-malaria (AFN) cohorts which normalized. Recovery from CNS-M is thus associated with prolonged immune and hemostatic dysregulation that may correlate with neurocognitive sequelae
articles.title_original: Prolonged dysregulation of gene expression and biomarkers of coagulation, fibrinolysis, and endothelium in pediatric central nervous system malaria.

articles.headline: Structural Equation Modeling of 329 febrile patients in Dire Dawa, Ethiopia identified direct effects: wealth index negatively affected ITN utilization (–0.66, p<0.001) and malaria knowledge (–0.63, p<0.001), while attitude positively influenced ITN use (0.16, p=0.049) and travel history increased malaria infection risk (0.969, p=0.01). Indirect mediation pathways showed attitude and ITN utilization significantly influenced malaria risk (indirect coefficients –0.091, p=0.038 and 0.029, p=0.048), highlighting complex socioeconomic and behavioral drivers of urban malaria
articles.title_original: Modeling urban malaria infection in Anopheles stephensi hotspot area in Eastern Ethiopia: application of Structural Equation Modeling.

articles.headline: This review synthesizes nanotechnology advances in magnetic and mesoporous silica nanoparticles as drug-delivery platforms for sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP), identifying their potential to improve bioavailability, enable sustained release, reduce toxicity, and enhance efficacy against resistant Plasmodium strains through in vitro and in vivo studies. Despite promising pharmacokinetic and antimalarial improvements, clinical translation faces challenges including scalability, biocompatibility, and regulatory approval needed to realize this approach's potential for resource-limited malaria settings
articles.title_original: Advancing malaria treatment with magnetic and mesoporous silica nanoparticles loaded with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine: a review.
articles.authors: Egza TF, Kebede EM, Perumal RD

articles.headline: Proteome analysis identified 16 transporter proteins in P. falciparum food vacuoles; SNP analysis of 2,517 isolates revealed coassociation of 6 mutations in 4 genes (PfCRT, PfNT1, PfCTR2, PfMDR2) with PfKelch13 polymorphisms (P<0.0001). Yeast complementation studies demonstrated mutant transporter forms (PfNT1-F394L, PfMFR5-S278T, PfMFR5-Y570F) conferred dihydroartemisinin resistance, indicating transporters beyond K13 contribute to artemisinin resistance evolution
articles.title_original: Role of novel mutations in food vacuole transporters beyond K13-mediated artemisinin resistance in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Community-based cross-sectional study in North India (May–September 2019) of 440 mothers and 30 ASHAs found only 1.8% (4 of 222 febrile children) sought ASHA care first, far below the 80% benchmark (p<0.05), though among 8 malaria-positive cases, ASHAs achieved 100% treatment initiation and completion, with 87.5% follow-up. ASHAs demonstrated strong case-management fidelity but remain critically underutilized as initial care-seekers, limiting reach toward India's malaria elimination goal
articles.title_original: Implementation Fidelity of Accredited Social Health Activist (ASHA) Workers in Malaria Case Management in a High-Burden District of North India.

articles.headline: Survey of 27 semi-wild chimpanzees in Gabon revealed 48.15% Plasmodium infection rate (P. gaboni, P. reichenowi, P. vivax-like) with younger animals more heavily infected, particularly with triple infections. Infected animals showed elevated urea, reduced creatinine, and increased pro-inflammatory (TNF, IL-1β, CCL3) and anti-inflammatory (IL-10) cytokines with regulatory-skewed immune responses, suggesting chronic infections are associated with systemic immune modulation and signs of physiological stress, including possible renal dysfunction
articles.title_original: Hidden hematological, biochemical and immune costs of asymptomatic malaria infections in semi-wild chimpanzees.

articles.headline: Among 245 P. falciparum isolates from Kigali, Rwanda (Oct 2021–June 2023), sequencing identified 48 distinct circumsporozoite protein (Pfcsp) haplotypes with high haplotype diversity (Hd=0.8899) but moderate nucleotide diversity (π=0.00834). The N-terminal KLKQP motif was fully conserved, while central (NANP/NVNP) and C-terminal (Th2R/Th3R) regions showed extensive polymorphism, with fewer than 1% matching the 3D7 vaccine strain and high mutation frequencies at vaccine-escape sites. This genetic divergence of circulating csp variants may influence RTS,S and R21 vaccine performance in Rwanda
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of Plasmodium falciparum circumsporozoite protein in Kigali, Rwanda.

articles.headline: An eDelphi consultation (Sept 2022–Sept 2023) with 67 experts from 20 countries ranked 25 malaria research priorities for seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine. Top-ranked priorities were evaluating the duration of SPAQ prophylactic protection and assessing the effect of integrating SMC with other interventions on antimalarial resistance development.
