Research priorities emphasizing sustainability, resistance, and integration with other malaria prevention methods featured most prominently, aiming to guide SMC program adaptation and research uptake
articles.title_original: Global medium-term research priority setting for seasonal malaria chemoprevention: results from an eDelphi study.

articles.headline: Among 422 pregnant women in Sekyere South District, Ghana, 78% had adequate knowledge of malaria prevention, yet 56% wrongly believed malaria spreads person-to-person, and only 48% knew the recommended ≥3 intermittent preventive therapy in pregnancy (IPTp) doses; 63% used insecticide-treated nets (ITNs). Knowledge of IPTp dosage was significantly associated with uptake (p<0.001), whereas sociodemographic factors did not predict antenatal care attendance. Gaps in prevention stem from misconceptions and incomplete IPTp/ITN adherence rather than supply shortages
articles.title_original: Assessing the knowledge, attitudes and practices towards malaria prevention and determinants of antenatal care utilization among pregnant women in Sekyere South district, Ghana; a cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Analysis of 38 P. ovale wallikeri isolates from Africa identified 10 SNP sites in the powgama gene, with nucleotide diversity π=0–0.00435 and 13 haplotypes (Hd=0.812); conserved regions approximately 2.3 kb showed π≈0. Recombinant PowGAMA immunized mice elicited robust Th1-polarized responses with high IFN-γ, endpoint antibody titer 1:5,120,000, and avidity 78.37%, with 83.3% sensitivity and 70% specificity for detecting natural P. ovale infections. The significant conservation of powgama and strong immunogenicity suggest rPowGAMA as a candidate for dual protective immunity
articles.title_original: Immunogenicity and gene polymorphism analysis of Plasmodium ovale wallikeri glycosylphosphatidylinositol-anchored micronemal antigen (GAMA).

articles.headline: Analysis of malaria incidence trends over two decades in African countries found mean temperature as the main climatic factor affecting transmission in Angola, Cameroon, Ivory Coast, Ghana, Mozambique, and Uganda. Warmer temperatures generally increased malaria risk, except in Ghana where higher temperatures associated with decreased risk; increased precipitation significantly reduced transmission in Burkina Faso and Uganda. These findings demonstrate climate change's influence on malaria in Africa and underscore the need for intensified action by policymakers to combat transmission in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Climate change and malaria: an old enemy of Africa is back.

articles.headline: Among 332 febrile patients in Upper East Region, Ghana (July–Nov 2023–2024), prevalence was 37.7% for malaria, 36.7% for typhoid fever, and 14.7% for co-infection. Nabdam district showed increased malaria odds (aOR=3.87, p<0.01); tertiary education increased typhoid odds (aOR=5.33, p=0.03); tap water use reduced typhoid (aOR=0.36, p=0.02) and co-infection odds (aOR=0.07, p=0.02); vomiting predicted malaria (aOR=2.45, p<0.01). These prevalence rates exceed national averages, highlighting the need for integrated diagnostic and treatment strategies addressing regional disparities
articles.title_original: Malaria and typhoidal co-infection among febrile patients: a health facility-based cross-sectional study in Northern Ghana.

articles.headline: A randomized trial in Madagascar (four sites, 765 enrolled, 709 reached endpoint) compared artesunate-amodiaquine (ASAQ) and artemether-lumefantrine (AL) in children 6 months–14 years with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria. ASAQ achieved 99–100% crude adequate clinical and parasitological response (ACPR) and 100% PCR-corrected ACPR; AL achieved 90–100% and 97.6–100%, respectively. No artemisinin-resistance-associated pfK13 mutations were detected (n=727 samples), and adverse events occurred in 10.2% (mild to moderate). Both ASAQ and AL remain safe and efficacious for Madagascar
articles.title_original: Efficacy and Safety of Artesunate + Amodiaquine and Artemether + Lumefantrine for the Treatment of Uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum Malaria in Madagascar, 2020.

articles.headline: Molecular diagnosis in 181 children <5 years in Timika, Papua, Indonesia (April–July 2013) revealed Trichuris trichiura prevalence of 13.8% by Kato-Katz microscopy versus 31.5% by qPCR (17.7% increase, p=0.0001). Children aged >3 years had 3-fold increased risk (aOR=3.29, p=0.006); hemoglobin ≤10 g/dL increased risk 2-fold (OR=2.46, p=0.020). Malaria co-infection (9.9% prevalence) increased anemia risk (OR=11.7, p=0.004). Trichuriasis is underestimated by microscopy; together with malaria, it is associated with anemia in young children in malaria-endemic areas
articles.title_original: Molecular diagnosis of Trichuris trichiura: Prevalence and associated risk factors in children under five living in a malaria-endemic area in Papua, Indonesia.

articles.headline: In vitro synthesis and evaluation of lupeol and betulin derivatives against P. falciparum FCR3 yielded eight compounds with IC₅₀ values of 0.03–9.9 µM; compounds 1, 4a, and 4b were most potent (0.3±0.3, 0.03±0.05, and 0.7±0.8 µM respectively), exceeding artemisinin (0.7±0.3 µM) and chloroquine (10.3±2.9 µM). Molecular docking showed binding affinity to PfATP6 of −9.8 to −8.4 kcal/mol via hydrophobic interactions and hydrogen bonds. These compounds represent promising lead structures for developing new antimalarial drugs addressing drug resistance
articles.title_original: Hemisynthesis of lupeol and betulin derivatives and their in vitro antimalarial activity against Plasmodium falciparum FCR3.

articles.headline: Quantitative image analysis and single-particle tracking revealed that hemozoin crystals in malaria parasites exhibit tight confinement but motion faster than thermal diffusion. Hydrogen peroxide, generated abundantly in the food vacuole during hemoglobin digestion, stimulated hemozoin crystal motion in vitro; conditions suppressing peroxide formation slowed crystal motion inside parasites. Surface-exposed metals on hemozoin catalyze peroxide decomposition to drive self-propelled nanoparticle motion, which likely reduces oxidative stress to the parasite from high hydrogen peroxide levels
articles.title_original: Chemical propulsion of hemozoin crystal motion in malaria parasites.
articles.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A

articles.headline: Asymptomatic P. falciparum prevalence in Fanteakwa South District, Ghana was 53.1% overall, significantly higher in 240 artisanal mining-community residents (64.6%) than 101 mining-free residents (27.7%, p<0.001). In mining communities, key risk factors were female gender (AOR=2.23), being unmarried (AOR=3.87), schooling (AOR=2.32), lacking nets (AOR=2.3), using open-market nets (AOR=4.2), net sharing (AOR=3.0–4.9), and >2 hours outdoors at night (AOR=8.4). Concentrated transmission risks highlight priority areas for targeted ITN distribution, behavior change, and housing improvements
articles.title_original: Factors Associated With Plasmodium falciparum Infection Transmission in Artisanal Mining and Mining-Free Communities: The Case of Fanteakwa South District, Ghana.

articles.headline: Among 95 Kenyan children (45 with uncomplicated, 50 with severe malaria), proxy-reported EQ-5D-5L utility scores differed significantly across three value sets. Uncomplicated malaria median utilities were 0.82 (Ethiopian), 0.59 (Ugandan), and 0.73 (Dutch); severe malaria utilities were 0.30, −0.13, and 0.09 respectively. Pain/discomfort and usual activity domains were most affected; vaccination status and malaria severity were significant determinants of utility scores. Malaria considerably reduces children's health-related quality of life, and value-set choice significantly affects cost-effectiveness estimates
articles.title_original: Determining health-related quality of life among children with malaria in Kenya: does the choice of EQ-5D value set matter?

articles.headline: Anopheles gambiae s.l. dominated vector surveillance in Kebbi State, Nigeria, with human biting rates of 42.2 per person per night indoors and 14.3 outdoors during rainy season; 97.9% (460/635) of sampled larval habitats tested positive for Anopheles larvae, predominantly rice fields within 100 m of human habitations, supporting implementation of larval source management
articles.title_original: Utilization of vector bionomics, remote sensing and larval habitat characterization data to inform larval source management decision in the high malaria burden state of Kebbi, Nigeria.

articles.headline: Correction notice regarding the mitochondrial genome sequence of Plasmodium pitheci — no novel research findings or quantitative results
articles.title_original: Correction: The mitochondrial genome sequence of the Bornean orang-utan (Pongo pygmaeus) malaria parasite, Plasmodium pitheci.

articles.headline: Of 393 children hospitalized with cerebral malaria (2018–2025), 52 (13%) died, with cerebral herniation the most common cause (46%), followed by status epilepticus with apnea (17%); non-neurologic causes accounted for 37% (cardiac 13%, respiratory 12%, renal 6%, hepatic 4%), with multiple organ dysfunction prevalent regardless of immediate cause, suggesting need for organ-specific trial designs
articles.title_original: Cause-specific mortality in a cohort of paediatric cerebral malaria patients.

articles.headline: Among the Yanomami in the Brazilian Amazon, a 1% increase in annual mining area was associated with a 24% increase in monthly malaria cases (95% credible interval: 17–32%); an estimated 102,870 excess malaria cases occurred from 2018–2023 due to mining expansion, with 83% underreporting of cases in 2022, representing approximately US$6.9 million in additional public health costs
articles.title_original: Mining and malaria in the Brazilian Amazon and in the Yanomami indigenous land.

articles.headline: Among 10,290 febrile participants tested across 12 Indian sites (September 2023–April 2024), rapid diagnostic tests detected malaria in 14.7% (1,516 cases), of which 72.9% were Plasmodium falciparum mono-infections, 19.1% P. vivax, and 8.0% mixed; RDT sensitivity was 95% for P. falciparum but only 83% for P. vivax, highlighting urgent need for improved P. vivax diagnostics
articles.title_original: A cross-sectional survey of Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax in India using rapid diagnostic test and microscopy across 12 sites of varying transmission, 2023-2024.

articles.headline: Two-dimensional correlation spectroscopy analysis of Raman spectra from Plasmodium falciparum-infected red blood cells identified parasitemia-dependent heme markers (deoxy-heme vibrations at 1354 and 1580 cm⁻¹) with distinct spectral patterns according to laser excitation wavelength (442 nm versus 785 nm), suggesting potential utility of relative heme oxy/deoxy-intensity ratios for quantifying parasitemia levels
articles.title_original: Two-dimensional correlation spectroscopy (2D-COS) in the analysis of parasitemia in Raman spectra of red blood cells of patients diagnosed with malaria.

articles.headline: A systematic review of 14 integrated vector management (IVM) studies (2009–2024) across Africa and Asia found that 6 reported statistically significant reductions in malaria transmission (p<0.05), with longitudinal studies from Uganda, Ethiopia, and Nigeria demonstrating sustained reductions in malaria cases and vector populations; IVM approaches consistently outperformed single interventions, supporting their potential for sustainable malaria control
articles.title_original: Integrated vector management for malaria control: a review of approaches and effectiveness.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg

articles.headline: Title only (no abstract provided): Tackling the Emerging Threat of Zoonotic Malaria in India
articles.title_original: Tackling the Emerging Threat of Zoonotic Malaria in India.

articles.headline: Title only (no abstract provided): Cross-specific anti-CelTOS antibodies for different Plasmodium species inhibiting the liver stage and transmission to the vector
articles.title_original: [Cross-specific anti-CelTOS antibodies for different Plasmodium species inhibiting the liver stage and transmission to the vector].
articles.authors: Varloteau A, Fouquoire L, Ravier L

articles.headline: Recombinant Plasmodium vivax cysteine protease inhibitor (rPvICP) demonstrated broad inhibitory activity against multiple cysteine proteases including vivapain-3, vivapain-4, falcipain-3, malapain-2, and malapain-4, with highest potency against vivapain-3 and vivapain-4 and pH-independent activity, suggesting its potential role as a novel antimalarial therapeutic target
articles.title_original: Partial characterization of a cysteine protease inhibitor of Plasmodium vivax.

articles.headline: Species distribution modeling of six dominant African malaria vectors projected that Anopheles gambiae, A. coluzzii, and A. nili s.l. will experience substantial habitat expansion driven by climate change, potentially exposing approximately 200 million additional people to highly suitable vector habitat by century's end, with new transmission hotspots emerging in Central and East Africa
articles.title_original: Climate Change Favors African Malaria Vector Mosquitoes.
articles.authors: van der Deure T, et al.

articles.headline: Global analysis of vector diversity and malaria prevalence revealed that the relationship between vector species richness and malaria risk is latitude-dependent, with distinct regional mechanisms: in Africa, environmental factors exert top-down control of disease through vector community assembly, whereas in Southeast Asia, disease prevalence depends on complex interactions among rainfall, GDP, and vector diversity
articles.title_original: Vector diversity and malaria prevalence: global trends and local determinants.
articles.authors: Hoi AG, Gilbert B, Mideo N

articles.headline: Two HSP90 inhibitors targeting the identical ATP-binding site in P. falciparum showed dramatically different resistance profiles: geldanamycin selected 10 distinct mutations conferring up to 22-fold resistance, while AUY-922 required 44 weeks to yield a single A41S mutation with only 2-fold resistance to AUY-922. Enhanced drug-target binding paradoxically correlated with resistance, and a multi-target hypothesis suggests AUY-922's lower resistance risk stems from engaging multiple HSP90 family members, demonstrating that resistance risk cannot be predicted from binding site identity alone
articles.title_original: Shared Binding Site but Divergent Resistance Profiles Uncover Novel Resistance Mechanisms in Plasmodium HSP90 Inhibitors.

articles.headline: Using P. knowlesi cultured in whole blood as a mock P. vivax natural infection, optimal low-volume transcriptomic protocols combined WBC depletion (increasing mapped Plasmodium reads from 5.4% to 37.2–54.5%), globin/rRNA depletion (reducing human contamination by 90%), and mRNA library preparation. This integrated approach enables untargeted Plasmodium transcriptomics in resource-limited settings to advance parasite biology research for non-falciparum species
articles.title_original: Low-volume Plasmodium blood sample processing protocols for untargeted transcriptomics optimized using Plasmodium knowlesi.

articles.headline: Historical examination of early sustainable rural malaria control implementation by Zionists in Palestine beginning in 1922, analyzing how they navigated political and logistical obstacles analogous to modern challenges in disease control and public health interventions
articles.title_original: Why and when successful rural malaria control became a local problem - Palestine 1922.

articles.headline: Article discusses a novel malaria treatment approach showing promise against drug-resistant parasites, representing the first new treatment class in decades
articles.title_original: First new type of malaria treatment in decades shows promise against drug resistance.

articles.headline: DeepSeek artificial intelligence successfully identified Plasmodium parasites and their developmental stages in peripheral blood smear images of an imported malaria case with high accuracy and efficiency, completing detection in minutes compared to hours or days for traditional microscopy. An 'AI primary screening plus manual review' workflow demonstrated significant advantages in reducing misdiagnosis and missed diagnosis risks, with potential to support malaria prevention and control in resource-limited grassroots medical institutions
articles.title_original: A Case of Plasmodium Identification in Peripheral Blood Smears based on the DeepSeek Intelligent Algorithm.

articles.headline: Comparative genomic analysis of P. falciparum across life stages revealed weaker negative selection in genes exclusively expressed in sporozoites, consistent with theoretical predictions of limited clonal competition and transmission bottleneck effects. These findings suggest that therapeutic intervention timing targeting specific parasite life stages may influence the rate of resistance evolution
articles.title_original: Heterogeneous constraint and adaptation across the malaria parasite life cycle.
articles.authors: Perkins SA, Neafsey DE, Early AM

articles.headline: Experimental infection of birds with P. relictum exposed to bites from competent (Culex quinquefasciatus) and non-competent (Anopheles gambiae) vectors showed parasitemia increased significantly more in response to non-competent mosquito bites, with no species-specific modulation of gametocyte conversion. P. relictum employs a generalist bet-hedging strategy regardless of vector competence, suggesting that shifts in mosquito community composition from climate change or control measures may have unanticipated effects on transmission dynamics
articles.title_original: Plastic responses of Plasmodium relictum to competent and non-competent mosquito bites.

articles.headline: Profile of malaria researcher Fitsum Tadesse and his contributions to advancing malaria research and public health initiatives across sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Fitsum Tadesse: shaping malaria research in sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: Laboratory-reared An. aquasalis phenotypes (30BS and 50BS) infected with P. vivax from Brazilian patients via membrane feeding assays (286 midguts examined across 5 experiments) showed similar infection prevalence (67.63% vs 55.10%; p=0.68), but 30BS mosquitoes had significantly higher mean oocyst intensity (72.91 vs 56.94; p=0.042). Intraspecific phenotypic variation influences P. vivax vector competence, with 30BS phenotype more permissive to parasite development, with implications for transmission dynamics and vector-based malaria control strategies
articles.title_original: Phenotypic variation and vector competence in Anopheles aquasalis: A comparative analysis of susceptibility to Plasmodium vivax.

articles.headline: Italian network study (NOMAL) describing epidemiology, clinical presentation, management strategies, and patient outcomes for severe malaria cases in a non-endemic setting
articles.title_original: The Italian network for severe malaria treatment (NOMAL): epidemiology, clinical presentation, management and outcomes in a non-endemic setting.

articles.headline: Chemical adjuvanting of P. falciparum whole sporozoite vaccines (SPZ) using dye-adjuvant complexes (Cy5-CL307 and Cy5-MDP) successfully loaded ~90% of sporozoites while maintaining viability (84% vs 71%) and motility (78.7% vs 83.6%). SPZCL307 significantly increased macrophage activation markers (CD80, CD25, IL-6, IL-8, TNF, IL-1β, IL-10; p=0.018–0.043) and boosted CD137 expression in circumsporozoite-specific memory CD8+ T cells by 18% (p=0.01), suggesting adjuvanting enhances immunogenicity without compromising sporozoite infectivity
articles.title_original: A Trojan horse: Chemical boosting of Plasmodium falciparum whole sporozoite vaccine immunogenicity.

articles.headline: CDC port health station analysis of 2,912 drug shipments (May 2006–February 2024) for malaria, diphtheria, and botulism antitoxins showed 83% (702/850) of recent distributions (December 2019 onward) delivered within 8 hours of authorization. Commercial air transport (aOR 11.43; p<0.001) and authorization outside routine work hours (aOR 5.10; p<0.001) were significantly associated with delays, indicating that staffing, distribution site locations, and expanded transportation options are critical for minimizing delays in emergency drug delivery
articles.title_original: A Review of Malaria, Diphtheria, and Botulism Medication Distributions by CDC Port Health Stations From May 2006 to February 2024.

articles.headline: In a 1-year cohort of 763 participants in Senegal (April 2021–March 2022), P. falciparum prevalence was <10% in SMC-eligible children (<10 years) but rose to 32.1% in ages 15–24 and 24.7% in ages 35–49 during wet season; clinical burden peaked in adolescents aged 10–14 (527 cases/1000 person-years) and 15–19 (631 cases/1000 person-years)—over five-fold higher than younger children. Intensive malaria control protected SMC-eligible children, but older individuals maintained high wet-season prevalence and substantial clinical burden, indicating need for community-wide elimination interventions
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum carriage in a population under long-term, intensive malaria control in Kedougou region, Senegal: a 1-year cohort study.

articles.headline: Title indicates study of malaria burden in a region under long-term chemoprevention; no abstract provided
articles.title_original: Malaria burden in a region under long-term chemoprevention.

articles.headline: A retrospective study of 90 patients (89 from sub-Saharan Africa) treated with three-day dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine for uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria showed 93.3% parasitaemia clearance by day 3 (6.7% still parasitaemic; no early or late treatment failures). No significant trend in day-3 positivity across 2013–2023 (p=0.339), indicating stable DHA-PPQ effectiveness for sub-Saharan African P. falciparum over time
articles.title_original: Effectiveness of dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine for treating Plasmodium falciparum malaria from sub-Saharan Africa: a retrospective study.

articles.headline: A 3D in vitro brain microvessel model with human endothelial cells and pericytes showed that P. falciparum-iRBC egress products decreased angiopoietin-1 secretion, increased vascular permeability, and caused minor pericyte morphological changes. Pre-treatment with recombinant Ang-1 and the Tie-2 activator AKB-9778 partially reversed barrier disruption, highlighting pericytes' mechanistic role in cerebral malaria and potential of angiopoietin-Tie axis therapeutics
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum impairs Ang-1 secretion by pericytes in a 3D brain microvessel model.

articles.headline: A review of erythropoiesis and age-related RBC production changes, examining chronic disease, sepsis, and unexplained anemia in elderly populations, with malaria and microgravity presented as underexplored models that mirror key age-related erythropoietic dysfunction and morphological RBC alterations
articles.title_original: Exploring erythropoiesis: aging through the lens of malaria and microgravity.
articles.authors: Foucambert P, Hang JW, Malleret B

articles.headline: A systematic review of 40 studies (2005–2025) found functionalized nanocarriers with ligands targeting infected RBCs enhanced drug efficacy and reduced systemic toxicity for blood-stage malaria treatment. In vaccine development, nanoparticles as antigen carriers achieved up to 83.3% survival in in vivo preclinical models, though clinical translation and regulatory approval remain limiting challenges
articles.title_original: Nanotechnology-based delivery strategies for drugs and vaccines targeting blood stage malaria: A systematic review.

articles.headline: Entomological surveys in Bobo-Dioulasso (2023–2024) identified An. arabiensis (91.20%), An. coluzzii (5.09%), and An. gambiae (3.71%) among 1,455 collected mosquitoes. P. falciparum infections detected year-round with 0.16% dual P. falciparum/P. malariae coinfections in July; mixed feeding (59.63%) and high trophic plasticity sustained persistent transmission with elevated entomological inoculation rates during humid season
articles.title_original: Influence of the dry and humid seasons on the assessment of human exposure to malaria vector bites in Bobo-Dioulasso, Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: A new species, Plasmodium aramidis n. sp., was identified in five Grey-necked Wood Rails from Southeastern Brazil, displaying pulmonary edema and hemorrhage, hepatic hemosiderosis in all examined individuals, and exoerythrocytic meronts in 2/3 birds. P. aramidis appears common in this host but is pathogenic; further studies needed to identify additional hosts and clarify pathogenicity mechanisms
articles.title_original: Hidden in plain sight: Morphological and molecular description of Plasmodium aramidis n. sp. in blood and tissues from Brazilian Wood Rails.

articles.headline: An ecological survey of 6,899 Anopheles mosquitoes in Indonesian rice fields (An. vagus most abundant) found human biting rate peaked at ~23.5°C and >88% relative humidity (R²=0.656; RMSE=0.212), while animal biting rate peaked at ~22°C and >97% RH. pH and illumination positively influenced Anopheles species; species-specific climatic sensitivities emphasize integration of ecological factors into malaria vector surveillance
articles.title_original: Ecological diversity of Anopheline mosquitoes in highland agroecosystems in relation to microclimate dynamics: implications for malaria control.

articles.headline: In a clinical trial of atovaquone-proguanil-attenuated PfSPZ-CVac, three doses of 5.12×10⁴ or 1.5×10⁵ sporozoites by direct venous inoculation with single-dose AP prevented blood-stage infections during immunisation. At challenge 10 weeks later with 3.2×10³ sporozoites, only 2/8 and 2/10 vaccinees were protected—inferior to chloroquine-attenuated vaccine. Complete sporozoite arrest by single-dose AP permits dose-frequency reduction in chemoprophylaxis regimens
articles.title_original: Complete attenuation of Plasmodium falciparum sporozoites by atovaquone-proguanil.

articles.headline: Two subunit malaria vaccines received WHO prequalification: RTS,S/AS01 (Mosquirix) in 2022 and R21/Matrix-M in 2023, both requiring four-dose primary series over 12–18 months targeting circumsporozoite protein on sporozoites. While protection levels and durability remain uncertain, especially in infants and high-intensity transmission areas, these licensures have bolstered development of complementary vaccine strategies amid over 500,000 annual malaria deaths in children under 5
articles.title_original: Better late than never: defining the ideal vaccine that targets pre-erythrocytic malaria infection.

articles.headline: A nationwide case-control study of 632 malaria-related deaths in Brazil (2011–2020; 454 in Amazon [71.8%], 178 outside) identified risk factors including age <6 months or >60 years (p≤0.01), delayed treatment >48 hours (p<0.001 Amazon; p=0.049 Extra-Amazon), illiteracy, indigenous residence, and P. falciparum/mixed infections. Active case detection was protective in Amazon (p=0.01). Deaths concentrated in vulnerable groups; timely treatment and active surveillance essential for Brazil's zero-malaria-death target by 2030
articles.title_original: Towards Malaria Elimination: A Nationwide Case-Control Study to Assess Risk Factors for Severe Malaria-Related Deaths in Brazil.

articles.headline: Plasmodium sporozoite mutants with truncated or scrambled circumsporozoite protein (CSP) repeats exhibited impaired motility due to altered adhesion site formation. Single-molecule fluorescence-force spectroscopy showed CSP repeats behave as a stiff, linear spring with elastic properties dependent on repeat length; scrambled repeats lost these properties, demonstrating a functional role for CSP repeat structure in parasite infection and motility
articles.title_original: The Plasmodium CSP repeats have elastic properties with a critical role in sporozoite motility.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 13 trials (5,226 HIV-positive pregnant women) found intermittent preventive treatment (IPT) reduced malaria odds by 55% (OR=0.45, 95% CI 0.36–0.56, P≤0.001), with IPTp-DP most effective and 48% reduction in placental malaria (OR=0.52, 95% CI 0.39–0.70, P≤0.001). IPT reduced maternal anemia by 18% (OR=0.82, 95% CI 0.69–0.97, P=0.03) but had no significant effect on low birth weight, fetal loss, stillbirth, or preterm labor
articles.title_original: Intermittent preventive treatment regimens for malaria in HIV-positive pregnant women: Systematic review and meta-analysis of clinical trials.

articles.headline: Imaging flow cytometry (ImageStream) identified parasitized erythroblasts (pEBs) in mice infected with Plasmodium yoelii 17XNL at 14 days post-infection, with significant populations in spleen and bone marrow but not peripheral blood. pEB frequencies by ImageStream matched conventional flow cytometry, and imaging revealed doublets/triplets and apoptosis-like blebbing, demonstrating ImageStream's capacity for high-throughput detection and visualization of host-parasite interactions
articles.title_original: Quantification and visualization of malaria-infected erythroblasts by imaging flow cytometry.

articles.headline: Systematic review evaluating AI-driven approaches for Plasmodium blood-stage classification, particularly P. knowlesi diagnosis. Convolutional neural networks, transfer learning, ensemble learning, and object detection models (YOLO, Faster R-CNN) show promise for automating blood-stage identification and reducing diagnostic variability, but key challenges persist: limited annotated datasets, class imbalance, and interpretability concerns that require enhanced dataset curation and explainable AI approaches
articles.title_original: Insights into AI-Driven malaria diagnosis: A systematic review with implications for Plasmodium knowlesi.

articles.headline: Twenty-year space-time analysis (2000–2020) of Plasmodium falciparum incidence, mortality, and prevalence in Kenya identified four key malaria zones plus two areas with increasing transmission, with general malaria decline interrupted by slight increase 2016–2018. Multidimensional hotspot methods revealed malaria concentrated in cross-border regions; bed net and antimalarial use increased over time, with space-time methods effective for identifying high-burden areas and refining elimination strategies
articles.title_original: A multidimensional space-time geospatial analysis for examining the spatial trends of vector-borne diseases: 20 years of malaria in Kenya.

articles.headline: Sequencing of 30 P. vivax clinical isolates and 3 chloroquine-resistant reference strains from Chennai, India identified nonsynonymous mutations in food vacuole enzymes: plasmepsin IV (c.493G>A, Val165Ile), falcilysin (c.1537A>G, c.2027G>A), and a novel in-frame duplication in heme detoxification protein (c.28_33dup). Structural modeling showed mutations did not affect active binding sites; genes were highly conserved, highlighting their essential roles as potential drug targets
articles.title_original: Characterization of genes involved in hemoglobin degradation in Plasmodium vivax isolates from Chennai, India, and species of non-human primate malaria.

articles.headline: Review of malaria epidemiology in Spain and Europe documenting official eradication by 1964 and 1970s, respectively, followed by imported cases from travelers and endemic-region immigrants. Currently, autochthonous transmission is extremely infrequent due to early diagnosis and efficient healthcare; however, warming climates could favor anopheline proliferation and transmission in southern Europe, and massive migrant influx may create infectious parasite reservoirs, warranting strengthened disease surveillance and vector control
articles.title_original: Historical evolution of the epidemiology of Malaria in Spain and Europe. Future perspectives.
articles.journal: Eur J Clin Microbiol Infect Dis

articles.headline: Association study in pregnant women from northern Brazil found the NLRP1 p.Met1154Val variant protective against Plasmodium infection with healthier placental state, while IL1B SNVs were more prevalent in severe cases. First demonstration that infected red blood cells induce NLRP1- and caspase-1-dependent pyroptosis in BeWo trophoblast cells, identifying a novel inflammasome pathway in gestational malaria pathogenesis
articles.title_original: Impact of NLRP1 Met1154Val and IL1B variants on gestational malaria: an unexplored role of NLRP1 in inflammasome activation by Plasmodium spp.

articles.headline: Fourteen commercially available malaria RDTs tested on 131 blood samples from non-human primates infected with P. cynomolgi (73.2 parasites/μL), P. inui (272.8 parasites/μL), or P. coatneyi (158.4 parasites/μL) showed overall sensitivity 34.2–94.3%, with >90% sensitivity when parasite density exceeded 200 parasites/μL. Zero false positives in uninfected controls; 1–17% false-positive PfHRP2 results in malaria samples; sensitivity highest for P. inui
articles.title_original: Can malaria rapid diagnostic tests be used to detect simian malaria?

articles.headline: PCR screening of 1,094 mosquito specimens from Iranian malaria vectors detected Wolbachia exclusively in Anopheles dthali (73.4% infection) and Culex species (77.78%–96.67%), with higher prevalence in males and detection in all regions except one northern province. Phylogenetic analysis identified Wolbachia supergroup B in An. dthali and Culex, closely related to strains in African anophelines, suggesting broader biocontrol potential for malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Wolbachia Infection in Iranian Malaria Vectors: Prevalence and Biocontrol Implications.

articles.headline: RNA interference and CRISPR/Cas9-mediated screening identified Core 1 Synthase Glycoprotein-N-Acetylgalactosamine 3-Beta-Galactosyltransferase 1 (C1GALT1) as a novel host determinant for P. falciparum invasion. C1GALT1-dependent α(2-3) sialic acid structures within O-glycans were crucial for efficient invasion by both sialic acid-dependent and -independent P. falciparum strains but not P. knowlesi; different P. falciparum strains showed variable dependency on distinct RBC sialic acid configurations
articles.title_original: Sialyl-T Antigen: A Novel Red Blood Cell Determinant for Plasmodium falciparum Invasion.

articles.headline: Male Balb/c mice infected with Plasmodium chabaudi exhibited increased cardiac contraction indexes at baseline and under isoprenaline, associated with elevated lipid peroxidation (TBARS) and reduced superoxide dismutase activity. Apocynin treatment (30 mg/kg/day) and NOX2 knockout reversed malaria-induced cardiac hypercontractility without affecting parasitemia, while mitigating oxidative stress and reducing cardiac IL-1β and IL-6 upregulation, demonstrating NOX2-driven inflammation in malaria-associated cardiac dysfunction
articles.title_original: NOX2-induced oxidative stress triggers cardiac hypercontractility during severe malaria.

articles.headline: Systems transcriptional profiling and mass cytometry in symptomatic and asymptomatic P. vivax malaria identified profound downregulation of monocyte function transcripts and significant CCR2+CXCR4+ classical monocyte depletion in symptomatic patients, while asymptomatic patients showed dysregulation of monocyte activation and inflammasome genes. Monocyte dysregulation emerged as a key feature of P. vivax response, suggesting asymptomatic infection may not adequately support parasite elimination or vaccination responses
articles.title_original: Systems approach identifies monocyte imbalance in symptomatic and asymptomatic P. vivax malaria.

articles.headline: Three-laboratory harmonisation of competition ELISA using 1st WHO reference reagent (10/198) for P. falciparum AMA1 antibody detection demonstrated reagent effectiveness as a standard. ADAMSEL software and R-platform analytical tool were developed to streamline cELISA data analysis, providing cost-free accessibility to resource-limited laboratories
articles.title_original: Evaluation of 1(st) WHO anti-malaria reference reagent for competition ELISA harmonisation and development of ADAMSEL analytical platform.

articles.headline: Phenotypic characterization of phenolic compounds with hydroquinone and naphthoquinone scaffolds revealed several inhibitors of P. falciparum blood-stage growth, including di-alkylated hydroquinones, naphthoquinone disulfide, and hexachlorophene. Molecular docking identified hydrogen bonding and hydrophobic interactions as primary binding mechanisms with PfATP6
articles.title_original: Small phenolic inhibitors of PfATP6, a Plasmodium falciparum calcium ATPase, as prototype antimalarials.

articles.headline: PiggyBac transposon-based phenotypic screen in P. falciparum cultured in HbAS versus HbAA RBCs identified genes sensitive to HbAS growth in signaling pathways, exported proteins, and host-interaction categories, with genetic overlap to oxidative stress and artemisinin/proteasome inhibitor responses. Findings elucidate mechanisms underlying the >90% protective effect of HbAS against severe P. falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: Phenotypic screens reveal Plasmodium falciparum genetic factors associated with infection of sickle-trait cells.

articles.headline: CYP2D6 genotyping in 71 P. vivax patients on supervised chloroquine-primaquine treatment revealed 81.7% normal/ultrarapid and 18.3% poor/intermediate metabolizers, with median day-2 primaquine plasma levels of 660.4 ng/ml versus 313.5 ng/ml respectively (effect size r −0.51, 95% CI −0.82 to 0.03). Adjusted hazard ratio for relapse was 1.45 (95% CI 0.39–5.39), showing no significant difference between phenotypes
articles.title_original: Polymorphisms of the CYP2D6 gene and its relationship with Plasmodium vivax relapses after chloroquine-primaquine treatment in Turbo, Colombia.

articles.headline: Review comparing metabolomic signatures in Plasmodium and Babesia-infected RBCs identified shared perturbations in glycolysis, amino acids, lipids, and redox homeostasis, with key difference in hemoglobin breakdown pathways. Distinct metabolic profiles reflect parasite lifecycle differences and suggest targets for biomarker development and therapeutics
articles.title_original: Shared host, distinct invaders: metabolomic footprints of plasmodium and babesia in host red cells.
articles.authors: Beri D, Rodriguez M, Lobo CA

articles.headline: Uncertainty-guided attention learning network incorporating Bayesian channel attention for malaria parasite detection in thick blood smears achieved superior performance on two public datasets compared to state-of-the-art methods. Network demonstrated highest average precision scores at both parasite and patient levels through uncertainty-guided pixel attention learning
articles.title_original: Uncertainty-guided attention learning for malaria parasite detection in thick blood smears.

articles.headline: Narrative review of AI applications in Sub-Saharan African malaria programs, including Kenya's drone-assisted vector surveillance, Nigeria's mobile health platforms for real-time reporting, and Tanzania's climate-informed forecasting models. Review synthesizes opportunities for enhanced surveillance, outbreak prediction, diagnostics, and resource allocation while examining challenges in data quality, ethics, infrastructure, and potential inequities
articles.title_original: Artificial intelligence in African malaria control programs: opportunities and risks.

articles.headline: Dissolving microarray patches (MAPs) for transdermal amodiaquine and artesunate delivery achieved in vitro deposition of 40% amodiaquine and 90% artesunate, with pharmacokinetic extension of artesunate half-life 8.5-fold (54.05 vs 6.37 h) and 21% Cmax increase (1796.02 ± 154.50 vs 1421.93 ± 209.61 ng/mL). In P. berghei-infected mice, MAP treatment reduced parasitaemia 99.5% within seven days, comparable to oral administration
articles.title_original: Polymeric microarray patches for transdermal delivery of amodiaquine and artesunate: A novel strategy against Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Age-structured mathematical model integrating RTS,S and R21 vaccination with long-lasting insecticide-treated nets, treatment, and demographic parameters calibrated for Ethiopia (low transmission) and Nigeria (high transmission) showed preliminary findings that vaccination combined with existing control strategies could significantly reduce incidence and mortality. Model enables comparative assessment of projected transmission rates and disability-adjusted life years under different vaccination scenarios
articles.title_original: An age-structured mathematical model to assess the combined effects of vaccine and non-vaccine interventions on malaria transmission and burden.

articles.headline: Analysis of 19,280 publicly available P. falciparum genomes found PSSP17 (Plasmodium sexual stage protein 17, identified in saliva from subclinical infections) dispensable in asexual stages but essential for mosquito transmission, with no naturally occurring pssp17 deletions observed. Data support PSSP17 candidacy for non-invasive, saliva-based rapid diagnostic tests to circumvent limitations of pfhrp2/3 deletion strains
articles.title_original: Evaluation of Plasmodium falciparum PSSP17 essentiality in human-to-mosquito transmission and its prospective candidacy as a biomarker.

articles.headline: Case report of P. vivax malaria in returning traveler from Amazon diagnosed by Karius test (plasma cell-free DNA sequencing) despite negative blood smears, requiring multiple antimalarial regimens (chloroquine-primaquine, artemether-lumefantrine, quinine-doxycycline-primaquine) before complete clinical and hematologic resolution. Case highlights limitations of microscopy for P. vivax diagnosis and importance of molecular diagnostics and adequate hypnozoite clearance
articles.title_original: Travel-Related Malaria Diagnosis on Karius Test Despite Negative Blood Smear.
articles.authors: Weigold JE, Lal S, Youssef DA

articles.headline: Cross-sectional seroprevalence study (N=805, Cameroon, April-May 2022) found overall SARS-CoV-2 seroprevalence 60.1% (urban 66.6%, rural 54.0%) and anti-Plasmodium seroprevalence 22.2% (rural 31.4%, urban 14.8%), with 21% P. falciparum and 2.11% P. vivax. No association between SARS-CoV-2 and Plasmodium antibodies detected
articles.title_original: Seroprevalence of anti-SARS-CoV-2 and anti-Plasmodium falciparum and vivax antibodies in urban and rural zones in the West Region of Cameroon.

articles.headline: Laboratory study showing ivermectin induces 100% mortality in An. stephensi and An. culicifacies within 24-48 hours at higher dosages, with topical LD50 values of 0.017 ng/mg and 0.002 ng/mg respectively. Ivermectin reduced oocyst numbers by 71% at 0.01 µM concentration, establishing it as an effective contact mosquitocidal and transmission-blocking agent
articles.title_original: Contact exposure to ivermectin induces acute mortality and inhibits parasite development in malaria vectors.

articles.headline: Analysis of stool samples from children with severe malaria (two African studies) revealed dysbiosis with increased Enterobacteriaceae abundance. Increased E. coli was a predictor of post-discharge mortality, suggesting treatments targeting Enterobacteriaceae may reduce post-discharge mortality in severe malaria
articles.title_original: Gut bacterial dysbiosis in pediatric severe malaria associates with post-discharge mortality.

articles.headline: Deterministic decision tree model comparing vector surveillance methods for Kenyan NMCP (15,000 samples annually) found MALDI-TOF MS would achieve 74.48% time savings, 84% material cost savings, and 77% labour cost savings, resulting in 83% overall direct cost reduction compared to current molecular methods
articles.title_original: MALDI-TOF MS for malaria vector surveillance: A cost-comparison analysis using a decision-tree approach.

articles.headline: Prospective cohort study (N=380, March 2010–September 2014, Thailand) of G6PD-normal volunteers receiving supervised primaquine (0.5 mg base/kg/day × 14 days) for P. vivax radical cure showed primaquine failure rate 75% lower than predicted, with annual P. vivax incidence declining from 0.19 to 0.09 infections per person-year
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax Malaria Relapse Risk Depends on Transmission Intensity: Evidence From a Longitudinal Study in Northwest Thailand.

articles.headline: Laboratory study demonstrating NUAK1 inhibitors (HTH-01-015, WZ4003) inhibited P. falciparum growth throughout asexual life cycle including erythrocyte invasion. NUAK1 genetic overexpression partially rescued inhibitor phenotypes, identifying NUAK1 as a critical host factor and vulnerable therapeutic target for host-directed malaria control
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum exploits NUAK1 to establish infection in human erythrocytes.

articles.headline: Review synthesizing evidence on PfEMP1-specific antibodies in cerebral malaria protection, demonstrating that anti-EPCR-binding CIDR-α1 and DBL-β domain antibodies correlate with reduced cerebral malaria risk through opsonic phagocytosis and complement activation. Identifies methodological challenges and research priorities for vaccine and therapeutic development
articles.title_original: Breaking the bind: PfEMP1-specific antibodies in cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Development and evaluation of YOLOv3 deep learning tool for P. falciparum erythrocytic stage diagnosis in thin blood smears (262 test images). Achieved 94.41% overall recognition accuracy with 1.68% false negative rate and 3.91% false positive rate, providing feasible technical support for malaria control in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Rapid and accurate recognition of erythrocytic stage parasites of Plasmodium falciparum via a deep learning-based YOLOv3 platform.

articles.headline: Vector surveillance across 18 Benin communes using human landing catches and pyrethrum spray catches with ELISA/CSP and real-time PCR for P. falciparum sporozoite load. An. coluzzii responsible for 63.01% transmission (EIR 87.7), An. gambiae 36.7% (EIR 51.1), An. funestus 0.24%; An. gambiae carries higher sporozoite loads (~30% with >10,000 sporozoites vs <10% An. coluzzii)
articles.title_original: Spatial and temporal distribution of four malaria vector species and their relative contributions to Plasmodium falciparum transmission along the south-north transect of Benin, West Africa.

articles.headline: Field and molecular study in DRC examining Coustani Group mosquito (An. paludis, An. caliginosus) diversity using ITS2 and CO1 sequencing, identifying 2–4 species groups with different biting behaviors. Sporozoite detection was minimal (none in Lodja, one in Kapolowe), suggesting these species are minor malaria vectors despite previous DRC transmission reports
articles.title_original: Coustani Group mosquito (Diptera: Culicidae) diversity and malaria transmission in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

articles.headline: Mixed-method evaluation of Thailand's 1-3-7 reactive surveillance and response strategy (Jan-April 2023, three provinces, N=74 participants). Case notification within 1 day: 54.1%; investigation/response within timeframes: 98.7% overall (except border areas); 100% awareness of strategy. Key challenges: limited human resources and budget, low community participation, difficult terrain and telecommunications
articles.title_original: Evaluating Thailand's malaria reactive surveillance and response strategies for malaria elimination: a mixed-method study.

articles.headline: Randomized controlled trial comparing chloroquine vs trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole (TS) prophylaxis vs no prophylaxis in adults with HIV on antiretroviral therapy (Malawi). Pyrosequencing revealed no significant differences in resistance mutation prevalence across arms; TS and CQ prophylaxis did not increase pfdhfr, pfdhps, or pfcrt polymorphisms; no chloroquine resistance mutations detected in any breakthrough infection
articles.title_original: Impact of Trimethoprim-Sulfamethoxazole and Chloroquine Prophylaxis on Drug-Resistant Plasmodium falciparum Mutations in Adults with HIV in Malawi.

articles.headline: In C57BL/6 mice infected with P. berghei ANKA (cerebral malaria) versus P. yoelii YM (non-cerebral), IL-35+ regulatory B cells expanded only in P. yoelii YM infection; STING-IRF3 phosphorylation suppressed IL-35+ Breg differentiation in P. berghei ANKA, contributing to experimental cerebral malaria pathology. Conversely, P. yoelii YM activated IRF3 in a STING-independent manner, promoting IL-35+ Breg expansion. IL-35+ Bregs are identified as key immunopathology modulators
articles.title_original: Distinct STING-IRF3 activation determines IL-35(+) Breg differentiation in rodent malaria.

articles.headline: A 72-year-old man returning from Massachusetts presented with fever, neurological symptoms, and ischemic stroke; blood smears suggested P. falciparum, treated with chloroquine-primaquine then artemether-lumefantrine. Despite treatment, hyperparasitemia and multiorgan failure developed; patient died on day 33. PCR was negative for Plasmodium but positive for Babesia 18S rRNA, representing the first documented imported babesiosis case in Honduras. This case illustrates diagnostic challenges distinguishing babesiosis from malaria
articles.title_original: First documented case of imported human babesiosis in Honduras: Diagnostic challenges with malaria falciparum.

articles.headline: In mice where prior blood-stage Plasmodium exposure impaired RAS vaccine efficacy, hemozoin mediated reduced CD8+ T cell responses through impaired dendritic cell antigen uptake. A lipid nanoparticle-encapsulated mRNA vaccine encoding Plasmodium CD8+ T cell epitopes restored protection in Plasmodium-exposed mice and overcame hemozoin-induced defects. Combined RAS+mRNA vaccination enhanced liver resident memory T cells and protection even in malaria-experienced hosts. Hemozoin is identified as a targetable obstacle to vaccine efficacy in endemic settings
articles.title_original: mRNA vaccination overcomes hemozoin-mediated impairment of whole parasite vaccine efficacy for malaria.

articles.headline: Integration of 2019-2022 field surveillance data from humans, macaques, and vectors in Peninsular Malaysia identified statistically significant positive partial correlation between human P. knowlesi prevalence and Anopheles leucosphyrus-group mosquito density within 10-km proximity (rs=0.228, P=0.042). Positive correlations emerged between P. knowlesi-positive macaques and historical human cases within ≥6-km radius. Higher P. knowlesi prevalence in peridomestic-agriculture versus urban areas suggests macaque infection as proxy for persistent human transmission risk
articles.title_original: Multi-pronged surveillance to understand the spatiotemporal correlations among macaques, vectors and humans in Plasmodium knowlesi malaria transmission.

articles.headline: Among 500 symptomatic patients at two Khartoum state sites, the novel Mologic LDH-dependent RDT for P. falciparum achieved sensitivity 91.9% (95% CI 87.3-95.2) versus comparator RDT 93.81% (89.6-96.6) and expert microscopy 95.24% (91.4-97.6); all tests were ~100% specific. Concordance with reference PCR was almost perfect (κ=0.929). Six HRP2-negative P. falciparum samples were detected by Mologic LDH RDT, supporting its utility for HRP2-negative malaria management
articles.title_original: Clinical evaluation of a novel Mologic malaria Plasmodium falciparum LDH-dependent lateral flow rapid diagnostic test diagnostic accuracy: a cross-sectional study, Sudan.

articles.headline: Among African children treated for malaria (2006-2022, 177 nationally representative surveys), 60% [95% UI 45.7-72.8] experienced >24-hour treatment delays (33 million children) and 29% [95% UI 18.9-41.2] experienced >48-hour delays (16 million children). Somalia had the highest delay rate (76% [95% UI 39.7-97.9]) and Tanzania the lowest (35.3% [95% UI 11.5-53.8]). Overall improvements stagnated post-2015; late treatment increases severe disease odds ~2.8-fold
articles.title_original: Estimating rates of treatment delay for malaria fevers among children in Sub-Saharan Africa 2006-2022.

articles.headline: The nanosd integral CRISPR gene drive targeting Anopheles gambiae nanos achieved high drive rates (98.4% females, 99.5% males). Homozygous nanosd females showed impaired fecundity and ovary underdevelopment with decreased nanos transcript levels, but minimal modification did not broadly disrupt transcriptomics or vector competence. Susceptibility to Plasmodium spp. and O'nyong nyong virus remained similar to wild-type. The drive propagated efficiently in caged populations, supporting its potential for malaria vector control via population modification
articles.title_original: The nanosd integral gene drive enables population modification of the malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.

articles.headline: A scoping review of peer-reviewed articles (2017-2025) on machine learning in malaria vaccine research identified five key applications: antigen discovery via supervised/semi-supervised learning, immune signature identification and efficacy prediction, computational tool development, AI/ML application reviews, and epidemiological modeling. Most studies were conducted in Europe/North America with African collaborations. ML transforms vaccine research by accelerating antigen discovery and predicting efficacy, though data quality and model interpretability remain challenging
articles.title_original: From data to immunity: the role of machine learning in advancing malaria vaccine research: a scoping review.

articles.headline: PbGAC (encoded by PbANKA_1137800), a Plasmodium berghei merozoite protein localized to the extreme apical region, binds heparin and erythrocyte surfaces during invasion via heparan sulfate-dependent pathways. Mass spectrometric analysis revealed PbGAC interactions with secreted proteins critical for erythrocyte invasion. Mice immunized with recombinant PbGAC or passively immunized with vaccination sera demonstrated enhanced immunity against lethal P. berghei challenges, identifying PbGAC as a potential vaccine target
articles.title_original: The Plasmodium berghei merozoite protein PbGAC is critically involved in erythrocyte binding during invasion.

articles.headline: Correction notice to prior research on Plasmodium vivax infection in human liver organoids
articles.title_original: Correction: Human liver organoids are susceptible to Plasmodium vivax infection.

articles.headline: A 24-SNP high-resolution melting barcode was applied to 181 P. falciparum samples imported to China from Central and West Africa. No significant differences in multi-clone infection proportions among four populations; nucleotide diversity was low. Pairwise FST values ranged 0.001-0.054, indicating low to moderate genetic differentiation. Principal component analysis revealed lack of prominent clustering. The 24-SNP barcode was insufficient for distinguishing Central and West African isolates due to minimal genetic differentiation in these regions
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity and population structures of Plasmodium falciparum parasites imported to China from Central and West Africa determined using a SNP barcode.

articles.headline: Genome-wide analysis compiled olfactory gene catalogs for four malaria vectors: Indian A. stephensi and A. culicifacies, African A. gambiae and A. funestus. African vectors showed significantly higher numbers of odorant binding proteins, odorant receptors, and ionotropic receptors. Invasive A. stephensi clustered closer to A. gambiae than A. culicifacies. Pyrethroid-resistant A. stephensi larvae exhibited elevated expression of chemosensory proteins SAP2 and SAP3, linking olfactory gene dynamics to insecticide resistance
articles.title_original: Olfactory gene dynamics in invasive Indian and non-invasive African malaria vectors at the crossroads of development, infection and resistance.

articles.headline: Comprehensive geolocated dataset of malaria seasonality timeseries in sub-Saharan Africa since 2000, compiled from literature review (with novel NLP screening), routine surveillance data, and entomological studies. Includes prevalence, incidence, mortality, and entomological timeseries plus qualitative seasonality descriptions, enabling modelling of geographically-specific seasonal patterns for optimizing deployment of seasonal interventions
articles.title_original: A malaria seasonality dataset for sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 406 febrile patients in Rwanda (Dec 2023–Feb 2024): malaria knowledge high (81% identified symptoms, 72% understood transmission) but treatment adherence poor. Of 180 who received treatment in prior 6 months, only 46% completed 3-day artemisinin-based course; 37% stopped within 2 days. Poor adherence despite high knowledge exacerbates artemisinin resistance risk, requiring urgent intervention to improve antimalarial adherence
articles.title_original: Understanding Malaria Treatment Adherence in Rwanda: Implications for Artemisinin Resistance.

articles.headline: Review of Anopheles mascarensis, main malaria vector in east and southeast Madagascar, secondary vector elsewhere. Populations differ significantly between east coast and Central Highlands, suggesting possible sibling species complex. Updated historical distribution map and analysed vector biology, geographic variants, and transmission role; molecular tools now within reach to test sibling species hypothesis
articles.title_original: Anopheles mascarensis, a Malaria Vector Endemic to Madagascar and the Comoros Archipelago: A Review.

articles.headline: Tracking ~2000 residents in high-transmission northern Ghana (2012–2017) through sequential IRS and SMC: IRS reduced transmission >90% and prevalence ~40–50%, causing significant reductions in var diversity and parasite population size. Changes were short-lived; 32 months after IRS discontinuation and SMC introduction, var diversity and population rebounded in all ages except 1–5 years (SMC target). Demonstrates P. falciparum resilience to short-term interventions in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Measuring changes in Plasmodium falciparum census population size in response to sequential malaria control interventions.

articles.headline: Development and field testing of MR-1, a DNA-specific hemicyanine fluorescent probe with detection limit ~20 parasites/μL. Flow cytometric analysis enabled precise detection and classification of malaria parasites and differentiation from Babesia. Cost-effective, sensitive rapid diagnostic platform suitable for both fluorescence microscopy and flow cytometry-based clinical malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Advanced Clinical Diagnosis of Malaria Using a Simple Red-Emissive Smart Fluorescent Probe.

articles.headline: Genomic surveillance of 250 P. falciparum isolates from Kigali (Oct 2021–June 2023) using Nanopore and Sanger sequencing: pfcrt 76T in 26%, pfmdr1 184F in 72.8% (indicating lumefantrine pressure); all isolates carried pfdhfr/pfdhps mutations (IRNI-SAEAA 45.6%, IRNI-SAEGA 24.8%); Pfkelch13 mutations in 50.4% including R561H (25.6%), A675V, P441L, plus novel K189T + R561H (24.8%). Complex, evolving resistance patterns require continued molecular surveillance and functional validation
articles.title_original: Genomic Surveillance of Plasmodium falciparum Drug Resistance Markers Between October 2021 and June 2023 in Kigali, Rwanda.

articles.headline: Whole-genome shotgun sequencing and assembly of Anopheles gambiae strain G3 (isolated from Gambia 1975), the main malaria vector. Produced 216.3 Mbp draft genome with ~82,000 scaffolds, 53X coverage, and 44.5% G+C content
articles.title_original: Whole-Genome Shotgun Sequencing and Assembly of Anopheles gambiae G3, the Host of Malaria Parasite Plasmodium sp.

articles.headline: Retrospective analysis of malaria surveillance in Ethiopia (2017–2021): 6,292,147 suspected cases, 999,687 (15.89%) confirmed (P. falciparum 68%, P. vivax 32%), plus 16,023 (1.6%) clinical diagnosis. Cases increased from 214,095 (13 per 1,000) July 2021 to 270,274 (16 per 1,000) Dec 2021. Annual average 206,942 cases at 12 per 1,000 incidence. Ongoing transmission in 9 moderate-transmission zones (API 10–100); seasonal peaks and increasing 2021 trends highlight need for enhanced surveillance before peak seasons
articles.title_original: Retrospective analysis of malaria surveillance data in the former Southern Nations, Nationalities, and peoples' region, Ethiopia (2017-2021).

articles.headline: Laboratory proof-of-concept and modelling study of Male Drive Female Sterile (MDFS) genetic construct targeting Anopheles gambiae. Repeated laboratory releases led to elimination of caged populations; modelling suggests MDFS could be highly effective self-limiting strategy for suppressing wild malaria mosquito populations
articles.title_original: A male-drive female-sterile system for the self-limited control of the malaria mosquito Anopheles gambiae.

articles.headline: In vitro study of P. falciparum resistance to compounds MMV007224, MMV665852, and MMV665794: resistance associated with pfatp2 gene amplification. PfATP2 (phospholipid flippase) localizes to parasite surface and is critical for growth; overexpression reduces compound sensitivity while knockdown increases hypersensitivity. PfATP2 mediates ATP-dependent phosphatidylserine internalization; compounds inhibit this mechanism, identifying PfATP2 as potential antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: PfATP2 is a flippase on the Plasmodium falciparum surface that is important for growth and influences parasite sensitivity to antiplasmodial compounds.
articles.authors: Qiu D, England E, Lehane AM

articles.headline: Field evaluation of four malaria RDTs in southern Ethiopia (708 patients; 46% qPCR-positive: 77 P. falciparum mono, 198 P. vivax mono, 51 mixed): at ≥20 parasites/μL, all RDTs achieved ≥96% sensitivity for P. falciparum (vs 63% microscopy) and ≥92% for P. vivax (vs 72% microscopy); specificity >99% for P. falciparum, >98% for P. vivax. Novel BIOCREDIT and Bioline RDTs showed high performance; all LDH-based RDTs detected 2/2 P. falciparum with hrp2/3 deletions
articles.title_original: First field evaluation of novel LDH- and HRP2-based rapid tests for Plasmodium vivax and Plasmodium falciparum malaria diagnosis.

articles.headline: Kenya DHS 2022 cross-sectional study of 3,146 under-five children: malaria prevalence 22.72% (95% CI: 21.29–24.22). Protective factors include maternal age 25–34 years (AOR 0.75), richest wealth (AOR 0.48), treated bed net (AOR 0.39), and breastfeeding (AOR 0.66). Community-level poverty increased risk (AOR 1.82). Findings support interventions targeting insecticide-treated bed net promotion, poverty alleviation, and maternal health education
articles.title_original: Individual and community level factors of malaria among under-five children in Kenya: Based on the Kenya Demographic and Health Survey.

articles.headline: Among 15,009 children under five in six Sub-Saharan African countries, malaria prevalence averaged 25.9%. A one-degree Celsius temperature rise increased malaria risk 1.77-fold (95% CI: 1.297–2.414, p<0.001), while annual precipitation of 250–500 mm increased risk by 72% compared to >500 mm (95% CI: 1.331–2.212, p<0.001). The findings highlight climate's nonlinear influence on malaria transmission and support incorporating climate data into regional control strategies
articles.title_original: Associations of temperature and precipitation with malaria in children under 5: A multi-country study in Sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: In a Phase Ib randomized double-blind trial, 40 participants in Tanzania received three monthly intramuscular doses of either SUM-101 (150 μg full-length P. falciparum MSP1 with 5 μg GLA-SE adjuvant) or Verorab rabies vaccine. SUM-101 elicited robust MSP1-specific IgM and IgG antibody responses (peaks on days 56 and 84), with 50% of vaccinees experiencing solicited adverse events (mostly mild-moderate injection site pain, headache, fatigue) within 7 days and no severe adverse events reported
articles.title_original: Full-length merozoite surface protein 1 formulated with GLA-SE adjuvant in malaria pre-exposed adults: a randomised, controlled, double-blind, parallel-group, single-centre Phase Ib trial.

articles.headline: Corrigendum to a previously published study on malaria epidemics in India examining the role of climatic conditions and control measures
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "Malaria epidemics in India: Role of climatic condition and control measures" [Sci. Total Environ. Volume 712, 10 April 2020, 136368].

articles.headline: Among 1.5–12-year-olds in Burkina Faso, anti-GLURP R2 and anti-Rh5.1 antibodies were associated with reduced malaria risk (adjusted IRR = 0.52 and 0.40, respectively). The antibody combination of AMA1, GLURP R2, and Etramp5 Ag1 achieved best predictive performance (AUC = 0.70). Older children (5–12 years) had higher antibody levels than younger children (1.5–5 years), with distinct patterns between infected and uninfected children, suggesting these markers could improve malaria diagnosis and vaccine design
articles.title_original: A Multiplex Serological Assay to Evaluate the Antibody Responses to a Set of Plasmodium falciparum Antigens and Their Protective Role Against Malaria in Children Aged 1.5 to 12 Years Living in a Highly Seasonal Malaria Transmission Area of Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: TCF25 knockout P. falciparum parasites showed dysregulated transcription (168 downregulated and 24 upregulated genes at ring stage; 53 downregulated and 4 upregulated at schizont stage), with ChIP-seq identifying 44 high-confidence TCF25-binding targets including rDNA. TCF25 deficiency resulted in upregulated rRNA expression, particularly 28S rRNA. TCF25 regulates gametocytogenesis through the ap2-g pathway and controls A-type rRNA expression and ribosomal subunit homeostasis
articles.title_original: Transcription factor 25 modulates gametocytogenesis and ribosome biogenesis in the malaria parasite Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Among 172 asymptomatic P. falciparum isolates from Tanzania, PfEBA-175 region II exhibited high nucleotide diversity (π = 0.00359 ± 0.00012) with adaptive evolution (dN-dS = 3.15, p<0.001) and recent population expansion (Fu's Fs = -30.614). A recombinant region II protein showed 84.8% average seropositivity (range 50.0–95.4% across villages), with antibody response increasing with age (ρ = 0.333, p<0.001). Despite substantial polymorphism, glycan-binding residues remained conserved, supporting PfEBA-175 as a blood-stage vaccine candidate
articles.title_original: Micro-geographic variation in antigenic diversity of PfEBA-175 region II in asymptomatic Plasmodium falciparum infections in Tanzania.

articles.headline: This review examines how the System of Rice Intensification (SRI)—an alternative cultivation practice aimed at enhancing yields while reducing water and agricultural inputs—may impact the ecology of Africa's dominant malaria vector species, particularly in the context of climate change pressures increasing SRI adoption across the continent
articles.title_original: Climate change adapted rice production: does the system of rice intensification impact malaria vector ecology?

articles.headline: This qualitative study of 31 Malawian stakeholders assessed perceptions of post-discharge malaria chemoprevention (PDMC) for children hospitalized with severe anaemia. Key barriers included traditional beliefs, financial constraints, and logistical challenges; gaps in health worker knowledge; and implementation obstacles around medication supply and caregiver adherence. The study emphasizes need for targeted health worker training, community engagement, supply chain strengthening, and health system integration to reduce childhood malaria and anaemia-related mortality
articles.title_original: Assessing stakeholder perceptions and contextual factors in implementing post-discharge malaria chemoprevention in children with severe anaemia in Malawi.

articles.headline: Among 113 children aged 6–59 months in Nigeria receiving either artemether-lumefantrine (AL) or dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHAPQ), AL achieved faster fever clearance (46.16±3.31 h vs 48.83±8.95 h; p=0.032). Day 42 PCR-uncorrected cure rates were AL 94.7% versus DHAPQ 92.9% (p=0.679), both reaching 100% after PCR correction. Parasite clearance time did not significantly differ (61.30±23.75 h vs 63.97±23.42 h; p=0.541), with both treatments demonstrating good efficacy and minimal adverse events
articles.title_original: Parasite clearance of two commonly administered artemisinin-based combination therapies in under-five children with uncomplicated malaria in a comprehensive healthcare facility in South-Western Nigeria: a randomised controlled trial.

articles.headline: A hybrid diagnostic model combining Vision Transformer (ViT_Large_16) and EfficientNetB0 convolutional neural networks, with SHAP-based feature filtering and ensemble algorithms (CatBoost, XGBoost, Logistic Regression), achieved 95.61% accuracy in malaria diagnosis from microscopic blood smear images with 1.6-second processing time—approximately 204 times faster than traditional machine learning. ViT-based models best captured complex image details, providing clinically applicable explainability and speed for early malaria detection
articles.title_original: Ensemble-Based Early Detection of Malaria via Explainable ViT-CNN Feature Fusion and SHAP.

articles.headline: Correction to a previously published study on anticoagulant therapy, tissue factor expression, and protection against experimental placental and cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Correction: Anticoagulant therapy and altered tissue factor expression protect against experimental placental and cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: In Plasmodium berghei-infected rats versus uninfected controls, high-resolution metabolomics revealed elevated urea and reduced 1,5-anhydroglucitol, D-(+)-Talose, and arachidonic acid. Pathway analysis identified significant enrichment of glucose-alanine cycle, alpha-linolenic acid, and linoleic acid metabolism in infected rats. The upregulated glucose-alanine cycle suggests increased gluconeogenesis in response to parasite-induced glucose depletion and heightened energy demand, highlighting metabolomics' potential as a diagnostic and prognostic tool
articles.title_original: Application of a Non-Targeted Metabolomics Study in Plasmodium berghei-Infected Rats: Towards Unravelling Metabolic Alterations During Malaria Infection.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 11 studies (1959–2019) from Africa and Asia found minimum temperature showed the strongest association with malaria incidence (r=0.454, 95% CI 0.358–0.549, p<0.001), followed by mean temperature (r=0.417, 95% CI 0.098–0.735, p=0.01) and maximum temperature (r=0.356, 95% CI 0.263–0.450). Substantial heterogeneity was observed (I²>99%), reflecting regional variations. Integrating minimum temperature monitoring into surveillance and early warning programs is recommended as climate change progresses
articles.title_original: Association between temperature and malaria (1959-2019): a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Title suggests exploration of novel malaria control approaches adapted to evolving epidemiological challenges, though specific innovations and findings cannot be determined without an abstract
articles.title_original: Innovation under pressure: bold ideas for a changing malaria landscape.

articles.headline: Case report and mini-review examining post-artesunate delayed haemolysis (PADH) diagnostic challenges in travel-associated malaria. Artemisinin derivatives are highly effective when timely implemented, but PADH—a rare but potentially severe complication—may be difficult to distinguish from malaria manifestations in medical laboratories. Maintaining diagnostic proficiency through continuous training is essential for accurate recognition
articles.title_original: The usual suspect-malaria? Diagnostic challenges in post-artesunate delayed haemolysis: a clinical laboratory perspective through a case report.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 30 studies involving 76,045 children found pooled ITN utilization prevalence of 69.50% (95% CI 64.5–74.5%), with substantial regional variation: Central Africa 85.44%, Southern Africa 80.7%, Eastern Africa 69.2%, and West Africa 67.0%. Substantial heterogeneity was observed (I²=100%, p=0.001). ITN utilization among African children remains inadequate and requires region-specific improvement strategies
articles.title_original: Utilization of insecticide-treated nets for malaria prevention among children in Africa: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Surveillance of 4,471 samples from South African malaria-eliminating districts (January 2022–August 2024) found validated artemisinin partial resistance markers were rare: P574L in 2 samples, P553L in 1 sample. Chloroquine susceptibility markers were prevalent (crtK76 99.8%, mdr1N86 99.0%), while dhfr triple mutations (99.9%) and dhps double mutations (98.2%) neared fixation; no hrp2/3 gene deletions were detected. Current South African treatments and diagnostics remain effective
articles.title_original: Very Low Prevalence of Validated kelch13 Mutations and Absence of Histidine-Rich Protein 2/3 Double Gene Deletions in South African Malaria-Eliminating Districts (2022-2024).

articles.headline: Nine-month experimental hut study of SumiShield 50WG (clothianidin neonicotinoid) sprayed on hut walls in Madagascar. Mortality in wild-caught anopheline mosquitoes remained above WHO's 80% threshold for 8 months post-spray when assessed up to 96 hours after capture, but fell below threshold at month 9. The formulation's 8-month residual efficacy suffices for the malaria transmission season in Madagascar
articles.title_original: Efficacy of the Insecticide Formulation SumiShield® 50WG for Malaria Vector Control in Experimental Huts in Madagascar.

articles.headline: Assessment of DawaPlus 2.0 and PermaNet 2.0 ITNs at arrival and 12, 24, 36 months post-distribution in Madagascar showed mosquito mortality on arrival was 86.4% and 83.6% respectively (exceeding 80% WHO threshold); by 12–36 months, mortality fell below 56%, and deltamethrin residue fell below expected ranges. Bio-efficacy loss occurred within 1 year, necessitating investigation of manufacturing, storage, transportation, or use practices
articles.title_original: Early Bio-Efficacy Loss of Nets Mass Distributed for Malaria Vector Control in Madagascar in 2018: Implications for Malaria Prevention.

articles.headline: Entomological and epidemiological investigation in Farafangana District, Madagascar following a 2018 malaria outbreak showed 25 of 1,488 mosquitoes (1.7%) carried P. falciparum sporozoites; 24 were outdoor-caught (54.2% were Anopheles coustani), with exophagy rates of 59.8–100%. Among 226 individuals tested, 71 (31.4%) were RDT-positive; 85.9% were asymptomatic. Secondary vectors and outdoor biting sustained transmission despite indoor-targeted control measures
articles.title_original: Malaria Outbreak in Farafangana District, Southeast Madagascar, 2018: Are Secondary Vectors a Threat to Current Malaria Control Approaches?

articles.headline: Cross-sectional screening of 87 asymptomatic sub-Saharan African migrants in Rome (February 2024–January 2025) using RDT, LAMP, thick smear, and RT-PCR found malaria prevalence of 6% (all male). LAMP detected 5 cases (6.0%), but RDT identified only 2 (2.3%). Species included P. falciparum, P. malariae, P. ovale, and one mixed infection, with longest time since arrival 181 days. RT-PCR detects asymptomatic infections missed by RDT
articles.title_original: Burden of asymptomatic malaria in adult sub-Saharan migrants attending an outpatient clinic in Rome from February 2024 to January 2025.

articles.headline: Theoretical and experimental analysis of P. falciparum var gene hierarchical switching using Markov chain modeling and RNA-seq revealed that PfEMP1 variants cleared efficiently by the immune response showed stable, higher-level expression, while slowly cleared variants were rapidly downregulated. Stable variants exhibited greater IgM binding affinity. The hierarchical switching pattern provides a survival advantage under variable host immunity
articles.title_original: Hierarchical switching pattern in antigenic variation provides survival advantage for malaria parasites under variable host immunity.

articles.headline: Structure-activity relationship optimization of 2,8-diaryl-1,5-naphthyridine phosphatidylinositol 4-kinase inhibitors identified compound 27, which reduced parasitaemia by 91% at 4×50 mg/kg daily doses in humanized NOD-scid IL-2Rγnull mice with P. falciparum malaria. Compound 27 showed improved pharmacokinetics, no hERG channel inhibition, and no mammalian cytotoxicity, making it a promising candidate for further antimalarial development
articles.title_original: Optimization of 2,8-Diaryl-1,5-naphthyridines as Plasmodium falciparum Phosphatidylinositol 4-Kinase Inhibitors with Improved ADME Profiles and In Vivo Efficacy.

articles.headline: Structure-activity relationship optimization of amodiaquine analog antimalarials (amodiachins) identified compound 43 (ADC-028), which demonstrated nanomolar activity against drug-sensitive and multidrug-resistant P. falciparum, minimal cytotoxicity, and favorable microsomal stability (t1/2=48.2 min). Compound 43 achieved nonrecrudescence at 16 mg/kg/day and single-dose cure at 50 mg/kg with excellent bioavailability (F=76%) and half-life (T1/2=84h). It represents a potential candidate to overcome drug-resistant malaria
articles.title_original: Discovery of Amodiachins, a Novel Class of 4-Aminoquinoline Antimalarials Active against Multidrug-Resistant Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Novel fluorescent marinoquinoline (MQ) compound 19 showed potent activity against P. falciparum (IC50 0.28 μM in vitro; EC50 1.2 μM ex vivo) and P. vivax (EC50 0.53 μM), with a 3 × 50 mg/kg oral dose achieving 96% parasitemia reduction in P. berghei-infected mice. The compound selectively accumulates in infected erythrocytes and inhibits parasite proteolytic activity, positioning MQs as promising antimalarial lead candidates
articles.title_original: Fluorescent Marinoquinoline Derivative as Inhibitors of Plasmodium falciparum: SAR Analysis, Mode of Action and In Vivo Studies.

articles.headline: Conditional knockdown of transcription factor PfAP2-06B in P. falciparum resulted in impaired asexual growth and erythrocyte invasion. Bulk and single-cell RNA-seq revealed PfAP2-06B modulates invasion-related gene expression during the schizont stage, suggesting it is critical for parasite development and a potential therapeutic target for malaria control
articles.title_original: An ApiAP2 Family Transcriptional Factor PfAP2-06B Regulates Erythrocyte Invasion Indirectly in Plasmodium falciparum.
