articles.headline: PKCθ-deficient mice infected with P. berghei ANKA showed significantly improved survival without altered parasite burden, as PKCθ loss skewed CD8+ T cells toward central memory phenotypes rather than pathogenic effector memory, reducing brain-infiltrating T cells and preventing neurovascular damage characteristic of cerebral malaria. PKCθ inhibitors may serve as host-targeted interventions for cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Inhibition of PKCθ Abrogates CD8(+) T Cell-Mediated Neurotoxicity in Murine Cerebral Malaria.

articles.headline: Narrative review examining intracellular parasitic infections (P. falciparum, Leishmania, Toxoplasma, Echinococcus) across transmission dynamics, immune evasion, and vaccine advances, with a One Health approach. Uses Armenia as a case study illustrating how ecological, socio-economic, and health-system factors shape parasitic disease burden and highlight needs for surveillance, diagnostics, and cross-sectoral collaboration
articles.title_original: Intracellular Parasitic Infections Caused by Plasmodium falciparum, Leishmania spp., Toxoplasma gondii, Echinococcus multilocularis, Among Key Pathogens: Global Burden, Transmission Dynamics, and Vaccine Advances-A Narrative Review with Contextual Insights from Armenia.

articles.headline: Mini review synthesizing gametocytogenesis induction methods in human Plasmodium species, from conventional environmental stressors to genome-editing approaches for sexual conversion. Integration of both conventional and genome-editing methods is crucial for developing efficient gametocytogenesis induction protocols to support understanding of parasite life cycles and novel malaria control strategies
articles.title_original: Induction of gametocytogenesis in human malaria parasites: from stress to genome editing.

articles.headline: Transcriptomic analysis of infected and uninfected mosquitoes identified differential expression of metabolic pathways critical for avian Plasmodium development, including those supporting parasite energy metabolism, midgut barrier traversal, and blood feeding. Results suggest parasites modulate mosquito metabolic pathways to enhance transmission and indicate non-immune physiological processes are equally crucial as immune responses in vector competence
articles.title_original: Avian malaria and the overlooked metabolic pathways underlying mosquito-Plasmodium interactions.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional household survey in Lake Victoria region, Kenya (n=45,155 RDTs from school children) found sufficient LLIN ownership (≤2 people per net) reduced malaria infection in middle SES households (OR 0.32, 95% CI 0.12–0.92) and combined low/middle SES groups (OR 0.37, 95% CI 0.17–0.87), with similar but non-significant trend in low SES alone (OR 0.59, 95% CI 0.15–2.91). WHO-recommended LLIN coverage remains effective for reducing malaria risk
articles.title_original: Long-lasting insecticidal net ownership and malaria infection by socio-economic status: a cross-sectional household study in an area along Lake Victoria, Kenya.

articles.headline: Prospective observational study across 48 health facilities in Benin, Nigeria, Uganda (45,155 RDTs analyzed) demonstrated high stability of malaria RDT results stored for one month: 95.1% of positive and 95.3% of negative results remained unchanged, with PPV 96.3% (95% CI 96.1–96.5) and NPV 93.8% (95% CI 93.4–94.1) at one month. Stored RDTs reliably verify surveillance data and support antimalarial stewardship
articles.title_original: Are malaria rapid diagnostic test results stable over time to support verification of surveillance data?

articles.headline: Characterization of P. falciparum phosphoinositide-binding protein PfPX2 (Pf3D7_0704400) showed its PX domain binds all PIP species with preference for PI3P, PI5P, and PI(3,5)P2. PfPX2 localizes to Golgi and micronemes with interactions including clathrin heavy chain and PfSortilin, suggesting a role in vesicular trafficking between Golgi and micronemes during erythrocyte invasion
articles.title_original: Characterization of a phosphoinositide-binding protein containing a PHOX homology domain in the malaria parasite Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Nanopore-based AmpSeq assay targeting six microhaplotypes distinguished P. falciparum recrudescence from new infections in 17/20 paired clinical trial samples (85%), with high sensitivity (detecting minority clones at 1:100:100:100 ratios), specificity (<0.01% false-positive haplotypes), and reproducibility (intra-assay 98%; inter-assay 97%). The assay provides rapid, robust molecular correction for antimalarial drug efficacy trials
articles.title_original: Rapid multiplexed nanopore amplicon sequencing to distinguish Plasmodium falciparum recrudescence from new infection in antimalarial drug trials.
articles.authors: Holzschuh A, Lerch A, Nsanzabana C

articles.headline: Correction notice only; no original research content
articles.title_original: Correction: The ESCRT-III machinery participates in the production of extracellular vesicles and protein export during Plasmodium falciparum infection.

articles.headline: Spatiotemporal analysis of 23,569 confirmed malaria cases in Zanzibar's November 2023–March 2024 epidemic identified higher risk in males, ages 15–35, urban residents, and non-LLIN users. One significant space-time cluster occurred in urban southwest Unguja, and weekly cases were significantly associated with average weekly temperature (8-week lag). Results indicate a setback in malaria elimination requiring intensified interventions
articles.title_original: The November-2023-March-2024 malaria epidemic in Zanzibar: a spatiotemporal epidemiological analysis.

articles.headline: Antimalarial drugs—chloroquine, dihydroartemisinin, piperaquine, primaquine—and bradykinin activate the Plasmodium falciparum membrane receptor PfSR10, inducing coupling with human Gi/Gq proteins (confirmed by cryo-EM). PfSR10 mediates dual roles in drug action and host-parasite communication, broadening understanding of antimalarial mechanisms and therapeutic targets
articles.title_original: Identification of antimalarial drugs and bradykinin as ligands of the Plasmodium membrane protein PfSR10.

articles.headline: Comprehensive review of how Plasmodium falciparum infection mechanisms, host immune responses, and parasite evasion strategies interact with cellular aging biomarkers in malaria pathophysiology, with implications for understanding parasite evolution and developing novel therapeutic strategies toward eradication
articles.title_original: Critical Understanding of the Influence of Cellular Aging Biomarkers on Host-Parasite Relationships Serving as a Key Platform for Malaria Eradication.

articles.headline: Soil analysis in three Brazilian Amazon municipalities detected DDT (mean 2.694 mg/kg in Cantá) and alphacypermethrin (0.364 mg/kg in Porto Velho). Incremental lifetime cancer risk reached 2.93×10⁻³ for DDT and 1.17×10⁻¹ for alphacypermethrin (hazard index maximum 336.61), indicating substantial human health risks despite negligible ecological impact
articles.title_original: Potential Risks to Human Health Caused by the Use of Pesticides in Soils of Three Municipalities Impacted by Localized Malaria in the Brazilian Amazon.

articles.headline: Case report: 22-year-old with P. falciparum treated with IV artesunate and artemether-lumefantrine developed pure red cell aplasia despite parasitemia clearance with parvovirus B19 PCR of 2.1×10³ IU/mL; complete recovery followed IVIG and prednisone, highlighting B19V coinfection as a cause of persistent anemia in malaria patients
articles.title_original: Transient Pure Red Cell Aplasia in a Case of Plasmodium falciparum and Parvovirus B19 Coinfection.

articles.headline: Multiplex long-amplicon panel targeting Pfk13, Pfcoronin, Pfap2μ, Pfubp1, Pfmdr1, and Pfcrt achieved 100% gene coverage with analytical sensitivity of 50 parasites/μL (dried blood spots) and 5 parasites/μL (venous blood) at $15.60 per sample, providing a scalable tool for comprehensive P. falciparum artemisinin and partner-drug resistance surveillance
articles.title_original: Multiplex long-amplicon sequencing for comprehensive molecular surveillance of Plasmodium falciparum resistance to artemisinin and partner drugs in artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs).

articles.headline: Second-round cluster-randomized trial (92 clusters, Lake Victoria Basin, Kenya) comparing conditional cash transfer, lottery incentive, and control arms with educational content and financial incentives tied to negative malaria test results, addressing demand-side barriers to complement supply-side malaria control approaches
articles.title_original: The second-round evaluation of financial incentives on malaria prevalence in the Lake Victoria Basin, Kenya: updated study protocol for a cluster-randomized controlled trial.

articles.headline: Five-year surveillance (130,401 cases, Shangla District, Pakistan): annual parasite incidence rose from 2 to 61 per 1,000 (2020–2023) then declined to 46 per 1,000 in 2024; P. vivax comprised 95%, P. falciparum 4.6%. Critical barriers included poor treatment adherence (14% completed primaquine) and absence of G6PD screening
articles.title_original: Fighting malaria in Shangla District, Pakistan: insight into epidemiology, risk factors, and strategic control measures (2020-2024).

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study (797 caregivers, Southwest Nigeria): 689 (86.4%) demonstrated good malaria knowledge, which was significantly associated with better prevention practices in under-five children (OR 2.347, 95% CI 1.251–4.406, p=0.008), highlighting knowledge as a key driver of preventive behavior
articles.title_original: Malaria knowledge and preventive practices among caregivers of under-five children in Southwest Nigeria.

articles.headline: In silico analysis identifies harmala alkaloids (harmine, harmaline) as inhibitors of P. falciparum protein kinase 4, showing lower toxicity than existing drugs and efficacy against blood-stage growth and transmission, warranting further in vitro and in vivo validation for novel antimalarial development
articles.title_original: Computational insights into harmala alkaloids as novel antimalarial candidates against Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: DANet, a lightweight deep learning model (2.3 million parameters), achieved 97.86% F1-score, 97.95% accuracy, and 0.98 AUC-PR on 27,558 blood smear images for automated P. falciparum parasite detection, enabling practical deployment on edge devices for resource-constrained settings
articles.title_original: DANet a lightweight dilated attention network for malaria parasite detection.

articles.headline: Community-based study (410 caregivers, Cameroon): only 32.2% RTS,S/AS01 vaccine uptake in under-five children. Positive predictors included female caregivers (aOR 4.16), health professionals (aOR 2.87), biological parents (aOR 11.44), income 89–179 USD (aOR 2.76), and trust in health workers (aOR 6.12); prior malaria infection reduced uptake (aOR 0.31)
articles.title_original: Caregivers' practices and factors associated with malaria vaccine uptake among under-five children in the Tiko Health District, Cameroon: A community based cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Stepped-wedge trial of ceiling-mounted mosquito nets (69 households, Peru): renovated homes showed 55% reduction in indoor Anopheles counts (95% CI 33%–74%, p=0.004), 60% fewer bites per person per night (95% CI 27%–78%, p=0.003), and 61% fewer bites per hour (95% CI 15%–83%, p=0.018); peridomestic effects were not significant
articles.title_original: Efficacy of ceiling-mounted mosquito nets for malaria vector control in a Peruvian Amazon riverine community: A stepped-wedge cluster randomized trial.

articles.headline: Single-cell RNA-Seq and VDJ-Seq in healthy individuals during controlled human Plasmodium infection identified two pTfh1 subsets (distinguished by CCR7 expression) alongside pTfh2 cells. All three subsets clonally expanded and activated during infection, but only Tfh1-CCR7+ and Tfh2 cells positively associated with protective antibody production, revealing heterogeneity in CD4+ T follicular helper responses to malaria
articles.title_original: Tfh2 and a subset of Tfh1 cells associate with antibody-mediated immunity to malaria.

articles.headline: A nanozyme-based immunoassay detected Plasmodium falciparum female gametocyte activation antigen PfG377 released during gametogenesis. In patient samples from a transmission-focused trial, primaquine-treated individuals showed significantly lower PfG377 signals 2 days after treatment, consistent with gametocyte sterilization and blocked transmission, validating this approach for identifying transmission-competent individuals
articles.title_original: Evaluation of Malaria Parasite Transmission Competency Using a Nanozyme-Based Immunodiagnostic Targeting Female Gamete Antigen Release.

articles.headline: A trial comparing monthly sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP), dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DP), and DP+SP for intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy in Uganda (1377 samples) found SP had poor efficacy with 57.8% recurrent parasitemia risk versus 4.1% (DP) and 3.9% (DP+SP), and selected for drug resistance—PfDHFR I164L and PfDHPS A581G mutations increased from 24.9% to 35.2%. DP and DP+SP were superior for preventing P. falciparum infection in pregnant women
articles.title_original: Recurrent Plasmodium falciparum parasitemia and drug resistance mutations during intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy in Uganda.

articles.headline: Correction notice with no quantifiable results presented
articles.title_original: Correction: Simião et al. Involvement of Inflammatory Cytokines, Renal NaPi-IIa Cotransporter, and TRAIL Induced-Apoptosis in Experimental Malaria-Associated Acute Kidney Injury. Pathogens 2024, 13, 376.

articles.headline: Interviews with 98 Zanzibari residents revealed minimal familiarity with 'acquired immunity' and 'rebound malaria' concepts despite 70 years of international malaria interventions, with no evidence that researchers previously explained these risks. A brief visual tool plus 5–10 minute conversation changed participants' understanding, indicating that informed consent and public education about loss of immunity and epidemic risk remain absent from malaria control programs
articles.title_original: Reconsidering rebound malaria: Zanzibari voices on acquired immunity, risk, and history.

articles.headline: Title only; no abstract provided to extract quantitative results
articles.title_original: A simple strategy to cut malaria infection rates in babies.

articles.headline: A computational pipeline couples machine learning and artificial intelligence with physiologically-based pharmacokinetic (PBPK) and non-linear mixed effects (NLME) modeling to identify drug-gene pairs and optimize doses for African populations. The study prioritizes malaria and tuberculosis therapies, addressing Africa's genetic diversity and historical lack of pharmacogenetic data
articles.title_original: Artificial intelligence coupled to pharmacometrics modelling to tailor malaria and tuberculosis treatment in Africa.

articles.headline: A mixed-methods study of 301 Northern Ugandan residents found only 19.9% aware of gene-drive modified mosquitoes (GDMMs), yet 64.1% (193/301) supported their use for malaria control. Independent predictors of acceptance included favorable attitude (aPR 1.25, 95% CI 1.05–1.49), high subjective norm (aPR 1.28, 95% CI 1.08–1.51), and high perceived behavioral control (aPR 1.23, 95% CI 1.05–1.46), demonstrating that attitudes and social norms drive GDMM acceptance despite low baseline awareness
articles.title_original: "Have Scientists Done Enough?" Community Perspectives on Gene Drive-Modified Mosquitoes for Malaria Control in Northern Uganda: A Mixed-Methods Study.

articles.headline: PlasmoFP, a deep learning model trained on SAR supergroup proteins, reduced the proportion of Plasmodium proteins lacking Gene Ontology annotations from 15–59% to 3–28% across 19 species, and increased fully annotated proteins from 7–42% to 36–68%. The approach addresses functional characterization of Plasmodium proteins with low sequence similarity to model organisms, advancing malaria parasite research
articles.title_original: PlasmoFP: leveraging deep learning to predict protein function of uncharacterized proteins across the malaria parasite genus.

articles.headline: Post-mortem PCR screening of 857 wild British birds over 15 years detected 13.5% (116 birds) infected with avian haemosporidian parasites: 8.9% with Plasmodium and 4.7% with Haemoproteus spp. Highest infection rates were in Paridae (36.3%) and Turdidae (34.5%); 23 of 30 detected lineages were novel to Great Britain. Histopathology of 13 Plasmodium-positive blackbirds revealed exoerythrocytic parasites in 4 and other lesions in 5, with only 1 death attributed to acute malaria
articles.title_original: The lineage diversity, spatiotemporal distribution and pathological significance of Plasmodium and Haemoproteus spp. infection of wild birds in Great Britain.

articles.headline: An age-structured mathematical model of malaria vaccination established that the malaria-free equilibrium is globally asymptotically stable when Re < 1, and the endemic equilibrium is stable when Re > 1. Sensitivity analysis identified parameters for control prioritization, with numerical results showing that effective vaccination combined with treatment and vector control could reduce transmission to nearly zero, potentially achieving eradication
articles.title_original: Age-structured vaccination strategies in mathematical modeling of malaria transmission.
articles.authors: Haringo AT, Obsu LL, Bushu FK
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis

articles.headline: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 11 studies (2000–2025) found a pooled prevalence of 35.64% (95% CI 21.43–49.85) of Plasmodium falciparum HRP2/3 gene deletions in Ethiopia: PfHRP2 deletions 8.48%, PfHRP3 deletions 23.74%, and concurrent deletions 8.14%. This high prevalence poses significant diagnostic challenges for HRP2/3-based rapid diagnostic tests in malaria control programs
articles.title_original: High Prevalence of Plasmodium falciparum HRP2/3 Gene Deletions in Ethiopia: Implications for Malaria Diagnosis and Treatment-A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
articles.journal: Can J Infect Dis Med Microbiol

articles.headline: Retrospective study (174 pregnant women: 78 with malaria, 96 controls) found platelet distribution width (PDW) significantly lower in malaria group and superior for diagnosis (AUC=0.857) versus other complete blood count parameters. PDW remained the only significant predictor in multivariate analysis (p<0.05), suggesting it may serve as a reliable hematological marker for malaria in pregnancy, particularly in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Predicting malaria in sudanese pregnant women: reliability of complete blood count.

articles.headline: Controlled before-after study of intermittent larviciding (Bacillus spp., 3 rounds/year June 2022–April 2024) across 1.1M population in three Tanzanian councils (high/moderate/low transmission) found no significant association with malaria incidence: incidence rate ratio 1.16 (95% CI 0.98–1.37) high, 0.86 (0.72–1.02) moderate, 0.96 (0.76–1.22) low stratum. Under current intermittent application design, no strong evidence supported larviciding's contribution to malaria control
articles.title_original: Large-scale intermittent larviciding intervention and associations with key malaria epidemiological parameters in Tanga Region, Tanzania.

articles.headline: Three in vitro experiments evaluating ivermectin-based endectocides against Anopheles dirus and A. minimus found diet substantially altered LC50 values; blood meal ingestion increased ivermectin lethality for A. dirus (inverse for A. minimus); and ~20% ivermectin concentration loss occurred in mosquito midgut versus blood meal. Mix diet minimized control mortality without altering ivermectin response, suggesting incomplete ivermectin bioavailability in mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Optimizing endectocide and ectocide compound evaluation in Anopheles malaria vectors.

articles.headline: Conceptual framework article proposing Integrated Vector and Parasite Management (IVPM) with 'Pummel and Pin' strategy for preventing malaria resurgence in high-endemic sub-Saharan African areas. Addresses WHO 2030 targets (90% mortality reduction) currently out of reach; emphasizes reducing both parasite reservoir and vectorial capacity; calls for operational research on feasibility and cost-effectiveness across endemicity levels
articles.title_original: Integrated vector and parasite management for malaria control in high-endemic areas: pummel and pin strategy to prevent malaria setbacks.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of Nigerian physicians across six geopolitical zones found 68.9% aware of RTS,S/AS01 and R21 Matrix M vaccines versus 29.9% unaware; 64% correctly identified 5 years as appropriate vaccination age. Despite majority willingness to recommend, substantial knowledge gaps persist regarding vaccine efficacy, safety, dosing, cost, and availability—indicating urgent need for physician education before implementation
articles.title_original: Physicians' knowledge of, and willingness to encourage caregivers to partake in the RTS S/AS01 and R21 matrix M malaria vaccines roll out - a national survey in Nigeria.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling study using fractional-order differential operators (Caputo, Caputo-Fabrizio, Atangana-Baleanu) incorporating human treatment and vector control found increasing fractional order slowed malaria spread, reduced peak infections, and prolonged outbreaks versus classical models. Fractional-order models captured memory-dependent dynamics more accurately, supporting combined therapeutic and vector control strategies for sustained transmission reduction
articles.title_original: Analysis of fractional-order model for the transmission dynamics of malaria via Caputo-Fabrizio and Atangana-Baleanu operators.

articles.headline: Analysis of 1,453,451 febrile cases from 13 Saudi Arabian regions (January 2022–December 2023) found 0.7% (10,779) malaria positive; positivity declined from 0.9% (2022) to 0.6% (2023). Plasmodium falciparum predominated (63.5%), followed by P. vivax–ovale (33%); highest regional incidence in Jazan (59%) and Makkah (20%, p=0.046). Imported malaria predominated; cases concentrated in age ≥15 years, necessitating targeted traveler interventions
articles.title_original: Malaria in travelers and local populations: Comprehensive study of incidence patterns and origin-based classification in Saudi Arabia.

articles.headline: Comparative modeling study (Goa, India, 2010–2019 data) found machine-learning models lower accuracy (RMSE 13–37) but high precision; time-series models higher accuracy (RMSE 5–41) but lower precision; hybrid models (RF-ARMA, SVM-ARMA, XGB-ARMA) achieved optimal balance with RMSE 0.5–15. Integrating time-series components into machine-learning substantially improved forecast accuracy and precision for malaria incidence planning
articles.title_original: Hybrid models combining trend and seasonality components with machine learning algorithms provide accurate forecasting of malaria incidence.

articles.headline: Development of vinyl-2'-deoxyuridine (VdU)-based metabolic probes (vinyl-DNADetect) enabling catalyst-free, live-cell detection of Plasmodium DNA proliferation via inverse-electron-demand Diels-Alder click reaction with tetrazine. Demonstrated high-efficiency labeling in P. falciparum-infected erythrocytes and enabled first dual-probe pulse-chase experiment (VdU and EdU), bypassing thymidine kinase requirement and enabling live-cell imaging
articles.title_original: Vinyl-DNADetect Probes to Assess DNA Proliferation in Plasmodium Parasites using Inverse-Electron-Demand Diels-Alder Click Reactions.

articles.headline: Longitudinal entomological survey (March 2013–March 2015, five Yanomami villages, Brazil) collected 1,962 Anopheles from 7 species; An. darlingi predominated (52.5–87.9%) with P. vivax and P. falciparum infection rates to 2.1%, while An. nuneztovari s.l. achieved highest extradomestic nightly human biting rate (359 bites/night). Outdoor entomological inoculation rates reached 49.3–79.3; village-specific vector control including insecticide-treated hammock nets and residual spraying recommended
articles.title_original: Bionomics and malaria infection rates in anopheline species in Yanomami villages of the Brazilian Amazon Forest.

articles.headline: Rolling cross-sectional survey (May 2017–October 2018, Zanzibar) of 7,286 non-travelers tested by qPCR found 2.6% (95% CI 2–3%) locally acquired malaria prevalence. Ages 5–15 years had 3.5-fold higher odds (aOR=3.5, 95% CI 2.2–5.7); bed net use protective (aOR=0.5, 95% CI 0.3–0.9); open windows increased odds (aOR=2.1, 95% CI 1.4–3.3). Promotion of bed nets and housing improvements targeting younger age groups essential for elimination
articles.title_original: Determinants of locally acquired malaria infections in Zanzibar: a cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: In vitro study of P. falciparum CLAG3-null parasites showed inability to expand under nutrient-limiting conditions; selected mutant restored solute uptake despite lacking CLAG3 protein. Whole-genome sequencing revealed dramatically increased clag2 copy number (quantitative PCR and transfection confirmed), without other genomic changes, implicating CLAG2 in nutrient-channel formation and demonstrating parasite genome plasticity under nutrient stress
articles.title_original: Nutrient stress dramatically increases malaria parasite clag2 copy number to increase host cell permeability and enable pathogen survival.

articles.headline: Proof-of-concept metabolomics study identified specific hypnozoite-related metabolites in supernatants of in vitro Plasmodium cynomolgi hypnozoite cultures, an accessible proxy for P. vivax. These findings suggest that following in vivo validation in the P. cynomolgi/rhesus monkey model and subsequent studies in P. vivax-infected individuals, a rapid diagnostic test for hypnozoite infection may be developed, enabling precise identification of dormant liver stage carriers and targeted primaquine treatment
articles.title_original: In vitro cultured malaria hypnozoites leave a footprint of specific metabolites.

articles.headline: Review consolidating evidence on malaria in the Democratic Republic of Congo, identifying key challenges (limited healthcare access, insecticide resistance, surveillance gaps) and innovations (genomic surveillance, rapid diagnostic tests, mHealth solutions, RTS,S and R21 vaccines, long-lasting insecticidal nets). Emphasizes integrated approaches combining maternal/child healthcare, community engagement, and public-private partnerships as essential for sustaining control efforts and preventing resurgence in this high-burden setting
articles.title_original: Malaria in the Democratic Republic of Congo: how support programs consolidate elimination gains and protect at-risk populations from recrudescence.

articles.headline: Longitudinal studies in humans and mice revealed that memory Tregs undergo antigen-driven expansion and inflammation-induced epigenetic reprogramming during Plasmodium reinfection, transitioning from Foxp3+ immunosuppressive to Bcl6+ Tfh-like effector cells. These reprogrammed mTregs enhance germinal center responses and parasite-specific antibody generation to facilitate parasite control; precluding mTreg-to-Tfh differentiation abolished protection, revealing a previously unrecognized adaptive plasticity enabling context-dependent functional switching
articles.title_original: Memory Regulatory T Cells Reprogram into Protective Tfh-like Effectors in Recurrent Malaria.

articles.headline: Comparative review analyzing thioester-containing protein (TEP) immune mechanisms in the malaria vector Anopheles gambiae and schistosomiasis vector Biomphalaria glabrata, examining pathogen opsonization, reactive oxygen species generation, phagocytic elimination, and melanization responses. Highlights both conserved and species-specific TEP characteristics to advance understanding of invertebrate innate immunity evolution and provide theoretical frameworks for vector control approaches
articles.title_original: Comparative immunological roles of TEP1 in Anopheles gambiae and Biomphalaria glabrata: implications for malaria and schistosomiasis control.

articles.headline: Molecular surveillance of 14 markers (6 P. falciparum, 8 P. vivax) in Honduras near malaria elimination identified all P. falciparum isolates as chloroquine-susceptible (wild-type pfcrt); polymorphisms in pfmdr1, pfama1, pfglurp, and pfs47 distinguished migrant from local P. falciparum. P. vivax analysis revealed three novel pvcsp VK210 haplotypes and first VK247 variant; markers pfmdr1, pfs47, pvs47, and pvs48/45 proved informative for inferring geographic origin of imported infections
articles.title_original: Molecular Characterization of Plasmodium Species to Strengthen Malaria Surveillance in Migrant Populations in Honduras.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 434 caregivers in Kenya (April–May 2023) identified determinants of timely P. falciparum treatment (within 24 hours) in under-five children: caregivers' symptom recognition (AOR=2.9, 95% CI 1.4–6.3, p=0.006), belief in appropriate drugs (AOR=6.9, 95% CI 2.5–18.9, p<0.0001), and health insurance (AOR=2.1, 95% CI 1.3–3.6, p=0.005) increased odds, while herbalist visits (AOR=0.1, 95% CI 0.1–0.3) and fear of drug side effects (AOR=0.5, 95% CI 0.3–0.9) reduced timely treatment
articles.title_original: Determinants of timely malaria treatment among under-five children attending public health facilities in Kisumu East sub-county, Kenya: a health facility-based cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Randomized, placebo-controlled trial of 408 P. falciparum-infected Ugandan and Congolese children (6 months–11 years) treated with artemether-lumefantrine or dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine plus single low-dose primaquine (0.25 mg/kg) or placebo found mean baseline plasma folate ~10.8–10.9 ng/ml with initial 1.65 ng/ml decline (p<0.0001) then recovery to 11.04 ng/ml by day 28. Critically, SLDPQ and G6PD-deficient status (16.2% of cohort) did not compromise posttreatment folate, supporting SLDPQ safety without G6PD testing
articles.title_original: Plasma folate dynamics in Plasmodium falciparum-infected African children treated with artemisinin combination therapy and single low-dose primaquine or placebo.

articles.headline: Field and laboratory evaluation (Oct 2024–Jan 2025) on Thailand-Myanmar border found Abbott-Bioline Malaria Ag Pf/Pv RDT had dramatically reduced sensitivity compared to First Response RDT: P. falciparum sensitivity 0.18 vs 0.89; P. vivax 0.44 vs 0.59. Laboratory analysis showed Bioline detected only 45% (9/20) of P. falciparum and 74% (37/50) of P. vivax cases versus First Response 90.3% and 84.0% respectively. Abbott-Bioline 2024 stock is not fit for purpose and should be replaced
articles.title_original: Investigating the Abbott-Bioline™ malaria antigen Pf/Pv rapid diagnostic test.

articles.headline: Single-arm trial of artesunate-amodiaquine in 173 Ghana children (ages 2–11) with P. falciparum (July 2018–June 2019) achieved 92.07% adequate clinical and parasitological response (ACPR) on day 28 by intention-to-treat and 100% by Kaplan-Meier method, with parasite clearance in all by day 3. No pfk13 artemisinin-resistance mutations detected; pfmdr1 NFD haplotype present in 27.1%, pfcrt K76T in 1.9%. AS-AQ remains effective for uncomplicated falciparum malaria in children in Ghana
articles.title_original: Efficacy of artesunate-amodiaquine for the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria among children aged 2-11 years in the Asante Akim North Municipality, Ghana: a single-arm open trial.

articles.headline: High-resolution cryo-electron tomography and iterative ultrastructure expansion microscopy of Plasmodium yoelii male gametocytes revealed a unique microtubule singlet-to-doublet (not triplet-to-doublet) transition at the basal body templating axoneme assembly. δ-Tubulin localized to the proximal B-tubule end modulates doublet assembly; both δ-Tubulin and ε-Tubulin are expressed in male gametocytes and regulate axoneme formation during male gametogenesis
articles.title_original: A basal body microtubule singlet-to-doublet transition in Plasmodium male gametogenesis.

articles.headline: ELISA analysis of 928 plasma samples (886 P. falciparum-positive) evaluated novel biomarkers glutamate dehydrogenase (GDH) and heam detoxification protein (HDP) versus conventional antigens (HRP2, LDH, aldolase) across varying parasitemia and transmission settings in India. All peptides showed high diagnostic accuracy (ROC AUC >96%) for P. falciparum detection; GDH2 and HRP2 effectively distinguished parasitemia levels, while GDH2, HDP2, and LDH showed promise for distinguishing endemicity levels, suggesting GDH and HDP as reliable serological biomarkers
articles.title_original: Evaluation of GDH and HDP as novel serological biomarkers for Plasmodium falciparum malaria diagnosis across varying parasitemia and transmission settings in India.

articles.headline: Community-based panel study of ~1300 Amazonian Brazilians genotyped for CYP2D6 and assessed repeatedly for P. vivax over 4 years found children with impaired CYP2D6 activity had higher infection frequency; this pattern reversed in adolescence (normal activity: p=0.0008 higher prevalence), and adults with impaired activity had lower parasite densities (p=0.0383). CYP2D6 polymorphism shapes P. vivax infection dynamics and malaria immunity development in endemic populations
articles.title_original: How CYP2D6 Polymorphism Modulates the Community-Wide Risk of Plasmodium vivax Infection: A Panel Study in Amazonian Brazil.

articles.headline: mRNA-1273 and mRNA-1273.222 booster vaccines achieved ID50 neutralizing antibody geometric mean titers >22,000 in asymptomatic HIV-infected adults both with and without PCR-confirmed P. falciparum parasitemia at 1 month post-vaccination, suggesting COVID-19 vaccine immunogenicity is unimpaired by subclinical malaria
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Parasitemia Does Not Diminish Neutralizing Antibody Responses After mRNA COVID-19 Booster Vaccination in HIV-infected Adults.

articles.headline: In 203 children aged 1-3 years from Papua New Guinea's East Sepik region, the IGHG3*30 allele (present in 78% of children) was associated with fewer Plasmodium spp infections (β=−1.736; p=0.038), particularly for P. vivax asymptomatic infections (β=−1.06; p=0.028) over 18 months, despite lower anti-P. vivax IgG responses in IGHG3*30 carriers
articles.title_original: Association of a Novel IgG3 Allele With Malaria in Children From the Sepik Region of Papua New Guinea.

articles.headline: Flow cytometry of neutrophil subsets from malaria-exposed children and children with Burkitt lymphoma from Western Kenya revealed increased aged neutrophil frequencies in malaria-exposed children, with strong positive correlation (rs=0.7; p<0.0001) in immature neutrophils between malaria-exposed and BL children, suggesting malaria-driven neutrophil shifts may promote BL development
articles.title_original: The Impact of Malaria-Induced Neutrophil Subset Shift and a Link to Burkitt Lymphoma.

articles.headline: Cryo-EM structures of P. falciparum apicoplast DNA polymerase in five pre-chemistry states revealed an open-fingers configuration with a nascent base pair checkpoint selectively recognizing Watson-Crick pairs, mechanistically explaining how a single enzyme balances replicative and lesion-bypass DNA synthesis
articles.title_original: Structural basis of multitasking by the apicoplast DNA polymerase from Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: The FLAME study is a 3-year cluster RCT in Loreto, Peru (30 communities, 1:1 randomization) comparing focal mass drug administration (chloroquine 25 mg/kg plus tafenoquine 300 mg for age ≥16 years; chloroquine plus 7-day primaquine 0.5 mg/kg/day for younger) versus control, powered to detect 68% relative reduction in cumulative symptomatic P. vivax incidence
articles.title_original: FocaL mass drug administration for Plasmodium vivax malaria elimination (FLAME): study protocol for an open-label cluster randomized controlled trial in Peru.

articles.headline: In 38 sub-Saharan African countries (2010-2022), geospatial analysis found overall malaria burden reduction with significant spatial autocorrelation; increased health worker density, institutional effectiveness, and development aid were associated with malaria cases and deaths per 1,000 population, with limited cross-border spillover, suggesting local rather than regional drivers
articles.title_original: Institutional quality, aid flows, and malaria burden: a geospatial analysis of sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: Among 404 febrile children in Nandom Municipality, Ghana (July 2024), microscopy-confirmed malaria prevalence was 26% (95% CI 21.9–30.5), significantly exceeding Ghana's 2024 target of 9.7%; non-ITN users had 2.01-fold higher odds (aOR 2.01; 95% CI 1.02–3.93), as did those requiring >30-minute travel to pharmacies (aOR 2.19; 95% CI 1.08–4.45)
articles.title_original: Prevalence of malaria infection and associated factors among children under five years attending health facilities in the Nandom Municipality, Upper West Region, Ghana.

articles.headline: Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi successfully introduced RTS,S/AS01E malaria vaccine from 2019 in 4-dose schedules for children from 5 months; post-introduction evaluation documented dose-1 coverage 69–91%, dose-2 62–82%, dose-3 58–81% within 24–30 months, achieved through early stakeholder involvement, comprehensive health worker training, community engagement, and robust defaulter tracing
articles.title_original: POST introduction evaluation (PIE) of the malaria vaccine introduced in three pilot countries (Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi) in 2021.

articles.headline: Qualitative analysis of 49 multi-level stakeholders in Ethiopia identified facilitators for pregnant women's malaria services (bed net prioritization, NGO partnerships, expanded diagnostics, antenatal care expansion) and barriers (chloroquine shortages, fragmented delivery, staff shortages, policy gaps, inadequate surveillance), highlighting need for integrated 'one-stop-shop' ANC models and WHO-recommended IPTp adoption
articles.title_original: Facilitators and barriers of malaria prevention and treatment services to pregnant women in Ethiopia: a multi-level health system analysis, 2025.

articles.headline: Correction to article on Anopheles species associated with malaria transmission in gold-mining areas of northwest Colombia
articles.title_original: Correction: Anopheles species associated with malaria transmission in gold-mining areas of northwest Colombia.

articles.headline: In a Rwanda case-control study across 9 endemic districts (Nov 2021–Dec 2023), vitamin E and iron deficiency were positively associated with malaria (aOR 7.46; 95% CI 4.43–12.58 and aOR 1.80; 95% CI 1.11–2.93), while age (aOR 0.58; p<0.04), female sex (aOR 0.67; p<0.001), and selenium deficiency (aOR 0.62; p=0.013) were protective, implicating nutrient imbalances in malaria susceptibility
articles.title_original: Associations between undernutrition and malaria infection: a case-control study from Rwanda.

articles.headline: In 30 Ghana health facilities (Nov 2019), T3 policy adherence for 590 malaria-suspected patients showed high testing/treatment rates but very low tracking; southern-zone facilities had 2.87-fold higher adherence than middle-zone (aOR 2.87; 95% CI 1.7–4.8; p<0.001), and males were significantly less likely to return for review (aOR 0.6; 95% CI 0.3–0.9; p=0.018)
articles.title_original: Adherence to the test, treat and track malaria policy among selected health facilities in Ghana: the clients' perspective.

articles.headline: Spatial ensemble modeling of 2018 malaria transmission in Indian districts identified API and AFI hotspots concentrated in central/northeastern regions; high-transmission districts had elevated risk in socioeconomically marginalized populations with water inaccessibility, no mobile phone access, and use of unprotected water sources; maternal education inversely associated with transmission
articles.title_original: Determinants of malaria transmission in Indian districts in 2018: insights from ensemble models.
articles.authors: Sam AK, Khatoon A, Phuleria HC

articles.headline: In 314 Ghanaian participants undergoing 13 months of quarterly mass testing, treatment, and tracking, P. falciparum PCR prevalence declined from 56.2% to 46.8% (67.5% infected ≥1 time); anti-PfEBA175RIII-V IgG titers fluctuated but remained elevated and did not significantly decrease, indicating the intervention did not substantially alter naturally acquired immunity to this antigen
articles.title_original: Increased Plasmodium falciparum EBA175RIII-V antibody titres despite a 13-month malaria mass testing, treatment, and tracking intervention in a high malaria endemic community in Ghana.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 19 studies found significantly higher erythrocyte sedimentation rate in malaria patients versus non-malarial controls (P=0.0171, SMD: 3.00, 95% CI: 0.53–5.47) and in severe versus non-severe malaria cases (P=0.0014, SMD: 2.26, 95% CI: 0.88–3.64); however, high heterogeneity (I²≥96%) and publication bias limit confidence in ESR's independent utility for malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: A systematic review and meta-analysis of Plasmodium infections and erythrocyte sedimentation rate in relation to disease severity.

articles.headline: Correction notice for prior published study on C-reactive protein as marker for bacterial infection and malaria in febrile children; no substantive results reported
articles.title_original: Correction: Evaluation of CRP as a marker for bacterial infection and malaria in febrile children at the Douala Gyneco-Obstetric and Pediatric Hospital.

articles.headline: Cochrane review of 15 RCTs (7,295 participants) found pyronaridine-artesunate superior to artemether-lumefantrine for uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria: PCR-adjusted day 28 treatment failure RR 0.40 (95% CI 0.19–0.85), unadjusted day 28 RR 0.27 (95% CI 0.14–0.52), unadjusted day 42 RR 0.61 (95% CI 0.46–0.82), and PCR-adjusted day 42 RR 0.86 (95% CI 0.49–1.51)
articles.title_original: Pyronaridine-artesunate for treating uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling and imaging showed liver-localized CD8 T cells (~1 cell per imaging volume or <10⁵ cells per liver) moving without directed attraction through sinusoidal constraints could encounter Plasmodium sporozoites within 24 hours, well within the parasite's liver-stage lifespan, indicating sinusoidal geometry enhances random search efficiency
articles.title_original: Liver sinusoid constraints make random search of CD8 T cells for Plasmodium parasites efficient.

articles.headline: Integrated analysis of flow cytometry, transcriptomics, and untargeted metabolomics from simian and human malaria identified conserved immune responses including elevated HMOX1 and coagulation genes, and complement activation; parasite species distinctly influenced chemokine responses, T cell differentiation modules, and kynurenine/androgen-estrogen metabolite regulation
articles.title_original: Systems Biological Analysis of Immune-Metabolic Host Responses to Distinct Malaria Parasites.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 12 studies (from 484 retrieved) confirmed molecular biology techniques reliably detect and identify Plasmodium species in Anopheles spp. with high accuracy and specificity, supporting effective epidemiological surveillance of malaria vectors in endemic areas
articles.title_original: Detection of Plasmodium in Anopheles spp (Diptera: Nematocera: Culicidae) using molecular biology techniques: a systematic review.
articles.journal: Rev Inst Med Trop Sao Paulo

articles.headline: In Maranhão, Brazil (2003–2022), 83,517 malaria cases with declining Annual Parasitic Incidence; P. falciparum proportion fell from 17.7% to 6.01%; imported cases increased; males aged 15–45 years predominated (59.1%→70.5%), indicating epidemiological shift toward importation in a low-endemic region near elimination
articles.title_original: Changes in the malaria transmission profile in a low-endemic area of the Brazilian Amazon: a cause for concern.
articles.journal: Rev Inst Med Trop Sao Paulo

articles.headline: Five mathematical models showed malaria recurrence patterns depend critically on transmission scenarios: models incorporating post-treatment immunity and vaccination reduced recurrent infections; high-transmission subpopulations experienced near-100% reinfection rates; seasonal models demonstrated cyclical dynamics; framework enables sample-size estimation for longitudinal studies
articles.title_original: How do recurrent malaria infections occur in clinical cohorts: a mathematical modelling study to support study planning.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 812 male armed forces in Cameroon found P. falciparum and P. vivax combined prevalence 13.6% (P. falciparum 86.6% of infected, predominantly mild); soil-transmitted helminths 22.8%; malaria, Trichuris trichiura, and Schistosoma mansoni co-infections significantly reduced hemoglobin concentrations
articles.title_original: The impact of malaria and intestinal helminths on haemoglobin concentrations among armed forces of the North West and South West Cameroon.

articles.headline: Geometric morphometric analysis of Anopheles wing venation in Western Siberia distinguished An. messeae, An. daciae, An. beklemishevi, and hybrids (An. m-d) using radial and medial vein landmarks; hybrids showed intermediate morphologies; combined with molecular identification, wing morphometrics reliably identifies cryptic malaria vector species
articles.title_original: Unraveling Wing Shape Variation in Malaria Mosquitoes from the Arctic Edge: A Geometric Morphometric Study in Western Siberia.

articles.headline: Study measured naphthalene, phenanthrene, and anthracene in Caño La Malaria surface water (4 sites, wet/dry seasons) via GC-MS. Σ3PAHs ranged 7.4–2,198.8 ng/L with higher wet-season levels (mean 745.79 vs 186.71 ng/L). Hazard quotient <0.01 indicated negligible acute toxicity; however, phenanthrene and anthracene exceedances suggest potential chronic ecological risks. Results support integrated watershed management in tropical urban wetlands
articles.title_original: Occurrence and Distribution of Three Low Molecular Weight PAHs in Caño La Malaria, Cucharillas Marsh (Cataño, Puerto Rico): Spatial and Seasonal Variability, Sources, and Ecological Risk.
articles.authors: Berríos-Rolón PJ, Márquez F, Cotto MC

articles.headline: Cross-sectional coinfection study, 220 Ethiopian participants (110 Plasmodium monoinfected, 110 with soil-transmitted helminths). Coinfected patients had significantly higher mean parasitemia (40,093.54 parasites/µl). Hookworm and Trichuris trichiura positively associated with parasitemia; hemoglobin significantly lower in coinfected individuals (hookworm p<0.001, Trichuris p=0.038). Authors conclude malaria–STH coinfection, particularly with hookworm and Trichuris, exacerbates parasitemia and anemia; integrating deworming into malaria case management may improve outcomes
articles.title_original: Integrated assessment of malaria parasite load, anemia, and associated factors in patients with and without soil-transmitted helminthes coinfection in Southern Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 68 articles (1989–2025, PubMed/DOAJ databases) on West African malaria. Environmental determinants: air temperature, rainfall, relative humidity, vegetation most common predictive factors. Socio-economic determinants: education level, place of residence, housing structure, poverty, information quality. Authors conclude these factors are indicators for targeted malaria elimination and prevention programs in West Africa
articles.title_original: Environmental and socio-economic determinants of malaria transmission in West Africa: a systematic review.

articles.headline: 2-year pilot (June 2020–2022) comparing adaptive cycling of 4 ACTs (artemether-lumefantrine, dihydroartemisin-piperaquine, amodiaquine-artesunate, pyronaridine-artesunate) in Homa Bay to artemether-lumefantrine-only control (Migori) for uncomplicated malaria in adults. Implementation county showed 12.5 percentage points lower malaria positivity (10.8% vs 23.3%); cost per patient USD 3; one minor adverse effect (vomiting) reported. Authors conclude multiple first-line treatment feasible with adequate health systems planning
articles.title_original: Health system challenges and facilitators associated with adaptive cycling deployment of multiple first-line treatment for uncomplicated malaria: a pilot study in a malaria-endemic region of Kenya.

articles.headline: VECTRI model study across 4 Indian climatological zones using CMIP6 climate data (baseline 1985–2014 vs 2080s projections, SSP5-8.5 scenario). Overall entomological inoculation rate (EIR) decreased 10–30% over most regions with temperature increase of 4–5°C and rainfall increase of 10–40%, though some western and peninsular regions showed EIR increases. Authors conclude temperature and monsoon rainfall are dominant drivers; results inform regional malaria control policy and climate impact assessment
articles.title_original: Changing climate and its impacts on the dynamics of future malaria transmission over certain endemic regions in India.

articles.headline: Near point-of-care LAMP-based Pan/Pf molecular detection on capillary blood, 672 community samples from Gambia and Burkina Faso (146 qPCR-confirmed P. falciparum), limit of detection 0.6 parasites/µL, sample-to-result <45 minutes. Sensitivity 95.2% (95% CI 90.4–98.1), specificity 96.8% (95% CI 94.9–98.0). Detected 94.9% asymptomatic infections (130/137) and 95.3% submicroscopic cases (41/43), substantially outperforming expert microscopy (70.1%, 0%) and rapid diagnostic tests (49.6%, 4.7%). Supports community-level test-and-treat strategies for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Sensitive near point-of-care detection of asymptomatic and submicroscopic Plasmodium falciparum infections in African endemic countries.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study, 443 febrile patients in Bayelsa State, Nigeria (May–June 2022). Acute dengue virus seroprevalence 14.5% (n=64), malaria 42.4% (n=188), coinfection 6.5% (n=29); additional 17.6% (n=78) with past dengue exposure. Dengue serotype 3 dominant; metagenomic NGS identified adenovirus, EBV, hepatitis A, Coxsackievirus A5, and mPox clade IIb also circulating. First documentation of human dengue in Bayelsa State; findings support routine dengue testing and unbiased surveillance for emerging pathogens
articles.title_original: Detection of dengue, malaria, and additional causes of acute febrile illness: The need for expanded testing, Bayelsa State, Nigeria.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey, 3,661 participants from 871 households across 7 health areas in Dschang, Cameroon. P. vivax 0.1%, P. ovale spp. 0.64%, P. falciparum 8.1% by qPCR; co-infections of P. ovale with P. falciparum detected in 23.1% of ovale cases. Gametocytemia common in falciparum infections but uncommon in ovale. Children and those with recent domestic travel had higher P. falciparum odds; no significant associations for ovale infections. Authors note relapse malaria species less widespread than previously reported in West Cameroon
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of ovale, vivax and falciparum malaria in the highlands of Cameroon: An integrated community survey of human infection and vector abundance.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study, 420 febrile patients attending healthcare facilities in Jawi District, Ethiopia (April–November 2023). Malaria prevalence 41.2% (95% CI 0.365–0.459); Plasmodium falciparum predominant (64.8%). Associated factors: lack of formal education (AOR 2.452), proximity of residence to mosquito breeding sites (AOR 43.224), awareness gaps regarding malaria transmission (AOR 1.384). Authors conclude transmission is multifactorial; recommend education, improved housing, and sustained access to malaria diagnosis, treatment, and prevention services
articles.title_original: Prevalence of malaria and associated factors among febrile patients in selected healthcare facilities of Jawi District, northwest Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Review article examining Glutathione S-transferase (GST) inhibition as a novel antimalarial drug target. GST plays a critical and unique role in Plasmodium's xenobiotic detoxification and parasite survival. Authors propose inhibition of Plasmodium falciparum and P. vivax GSTs as a promising therapeutic approach given widespread parasite resistance to conventional antimalarials and GST's established cellular role in parasite resilience
articles.title_original: Targeting phase II detoxification enzyme inhibition in Plasmodium for malaria treatment.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional genetic analysis, 366 pfceltos sequences from 366 Vietnamese P. falciparum isolates (3 malaria-endemic provinces, 2018–2022). 29 polymorphic amino acid positions identified; 4 common substitutions (K100N, S104L, F117V, N119K) detected. Low genetic diversity overall with regional differences (Dak Lak showed greater diversity than Gia Lai and Phu Yen); biologically significant amino acids tightly conserved. Authors conclude low diversity and regional variation support PfCelTOS as an attractive vaccine candidate
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity and natural selection of cell-traversal protein for ookinetes and sporozoites (CelTOS) in Plasmodium falciparum isolates from Vietnam.

articles.headline: Mouse infection model. Plasmodium yoelii infection induced M1 macrophage polarization and enhanced phagocytosis through TLR7/STAT3 signaling pathway. TLR7 agonist R848 alleviated infection symptoms in mice. Macrophage depletion via Clodronate liposomes increased parasitemia and weakened T and B cell immune responses. Authors conclude Plasmodium infection promotes M1-type macrophage polarization through TLR7–STAT3 signaling, initiating both innate and adaptive immune responses
articles.title_original: Plasmodium yoelii infection regulates M1 macrophage polarization and function through TLR7/STAT3 signaling pathway.

articles.headline: ZY19489 (Phase IIb triaminopyrimidine) resistance in P. falciparum mediated by novel PfCRT mutation with major fitness cost; paradoxically, resistant parasites lose chloroquine resistance and become hypersusceptible to piperaquine through an 'evolutionary trap' where ZY19489 blocks PfCRT-mediated efflux of both piperaquine and chloroquine
articles.title_original: Collateral hypersensitivity between ZY19489 and piperaquine neutralizes PfCRT-mediated drug efflux and Plasmodium falciparum resistance.

articles.headline: AI-based clinical decision-support system (CDSS) plus training for 50 Sudanese physicians improved adherence to 2023 malaria protocol: protocol recognition 38%→100% (RD +62%, p<0.001), IV/IM artesunate first-line awareness 53%→98% (RD +45%, p<0.001), severe malaria attitudes 15%→91% (p<0.001), and mean compliance 50.7%→96.8% (p<0.001)
articles.title_original: Enhancing Physicians' Adherence to the 2023 Sudan Malaria Case Management Protocol Using AI as an Intervention Tool.

articles.headline: Computational immunoinformatics design of multi-epitope P. falciparum vaccine using B cell, Tc cell, and Th cell epitopes from 5 conserved antigens; vaccine construct achieves 87.07% global population coverage by HLA type, shows strong TLR4 interactions, and immune simulations indicate long-lasting memory B and CD4+ T cell responses, pending experimental validation
articles.title_original: Immunoinformatics-Based Multi-Epitope Vaccine Design Against P. falciparum-Causing Malaria: A Computational Approach.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 1582 Malawians identified pfhrp2/pfhrp3 gene deletions in 15.0% (Lilongwe) and 10.4% (Mzuzu) of P. falciparum isolates; HRP2-based RDTs showed 78.5% sensitivity (vs microscopy 64.8%), but all dual-deleted isolates were false-negative by RDT while positive by microscopy and qPCR, compromising diagnostic accuracy
articles.title_original: First report of pfhrp2 and pfhrp3 gene deletions compromising HRP2-based malaria rapid diagnostic tests in Malawi.

articles.headline: Repeated cross-sectional study in Eastern Ethiopia found schoolchildren malaria prevalence 5.1% (high season) and 2.24% (low season), with P. falciparum dominant (83% and 59% respectively); urban prevalence 7.3% exceeded rural 2.9% (p=0.007); non-ITN use (AOR 2.2, 95% CI 1.2-3.99) independently predicted infection
articles.title_original: Malaria among schoolchildren in urban, peri-urban and rural settings in Eastern Ethiopia: repeated cross section study.

articles.headline: Experimental study in mice infected with artemisinin-resistant P. berghei K173: chrysosplenetin-artemisinin combination therapy achieved 100% survival and 30% parasite inhibition (~20% parasitaemia) versus monotherapy, reshaping ABC transporter/heme-ROS/GSH axis expression (fold changes 1.35-38.03 and 1.02-10.18 respectively) through both ABC transporter and redox balance mechanisms
articles.title_original: Chrysosplenetin acts as a homeostasis stabilizer with dual-function in shattering Plasmodium berghei K173 resistance to artemisinin driven by both ABC transporters and heme-ROS/GSH axis.

articles.headline: Qualitative thematic analysis of 160 malaria narratives from Sub-Saharan Africa (Kenya n=121, Uganda n=33) revealed two domains: perceived vulnerability contexts (poor housing, agricultural/pre-dawn occupations) and experienced consequences including severe financial burden forcing healthcare-basic needs tradeoffs, lost workdays, school dropout, and lasting sequelae (mobility impairment, vision loss, cognitive challenges)
articles.title_original: Malaria in their words: thematic analysis of online narratives from Sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 480 Zimbabwean adults in Manicaland Province with 36.7% experiencing malaria in 3 months; association rule mining revealed strong patterns between church prophet visits and avoidance of traditional healers (confidence 0.91, lift 1.08), with indigenous knowledge sourced from older people (27.7%), reflecting complex interactions between spiritual, traditional, and biomedical systems
articles.title_original: Indigenous knowledge systems and malaria prevention in Zimbabwe: a market basket analysis.

articles.headline: Correction notice: Survey on knowledge, attitudes and practices (KAP) of malaria prevention and control among Chinese expatriates in South Sudan; full abstract not provided
articles.title_original: Correction: Survey on knowledge, attitudes and practices (KAP) of malaria prevention and control among Chinese expatriates in South Sudan.

articles.headline: Statistical modeling using 5-year Anopheles gambiae complex density data from Burkina Faso determined CRCT design requirements: 50% vector suppression required 21-22 clusters (all species), 24-26 (An. coluzzii only), or 60-66 (An. gambiae only) for 90% power; 90% suppression required only 9-10 clusters regardless of species
articles.title_original: Requirements for designing cluster randomised control trials to detect suppression of malaria vector population densities.

articles.headline: Case report of 66-year-old Taiwanese traveler with severe P. falciparum malaria (20% parasitaemia) from Central Africa with multi-organ failure; initial IV artesunate plus artemether-lumefantrine achieved parasite clearance but recrudescence occurred at 4 weeks without pfk13 mutations; revised regimen with artesunate plus doxycycline and clindamycin cleared parasites within 2 weeks with no recurrence
articles.title_original: Recrudescent severe malaria due to Plasmodium falciparum following treatment in a Taiwanese patient: a case report.

articles.headline: Pre-rollout cross-sectional assessment of 211 healthcare professionals in Uganda before R21 malaria vaccine implementation (planned April/May 2025): 99% (95% CI 96-100) had poor knowledge, 70% (95% CI 63-76) displayed unfavorable attitudes citing affordability/accessibility/effectiveness concerns (mean attitude score 3.30±0.489), indicating substantial training gaps and infrastructure readiness deficits
articles.title_original: General knowledge and attitude of healthcare professionals and infrastructural readiness prior to implementation of malaria vaccine in selected hospitals in Tororo district, Uganda: a cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: In vitro assays of 20 Ghanaian herbal antimalarials against P. falciparum laboratory and clinical strains found only 30% (6/20) with potent activity (IC50 < 50 μg/ml); 3 of 6 potent formulations showed trophozoite-stage activity. Majority of herbal preparations exhibited poor anti-plasmodial activity, highlighting the need for stronger regulatory oversight of traditional medicines in Ghana
articles.title_original: The efficacy of selected Ghanaian herbal antimalarials against laboratory strains and clinical isolates of Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Nested case-control study (317 cases, 317 controls) of 5,753 Kenyan highland individuals in a low-malaria transmission area found elevated opsonic phagocytosis levels paradoxically associated with increased clinical malaria risk: aOR 3.32 (95% CI 1.13–9.77) in ages 5–14 years and aOR 4.85 (95% CI 1.02–23.00) in ages ≥15 years. OP responses appear to mark malaria exposure rather than protection in low-transmission settings
articles.title_original: A Novel Phagocytosis Assay Reveals the Association between Elevated Opsonic Phagocytosis Levels and an Increased Risk of Clinical Malaria in a Low-Malaria Transmission Area.

articles.headline: Theoretical model of Plasmodium chabaudi transmission investment strategy shows that parasites sensing a single parasite-density cue approach optimal fitness only with non-linear perception, but sensing two cues (log-transformed infected and uninfected RBC abundance) enables terminal investment and recovers optimal time-varying fitness. Two-cue parasites achieve higher virulence but experience larger fitness losses under environmental noise
articles.title_original: Malaria parasites can optimize transmission investment by sensing two within-host cues.
articles.authors: Wang A, Greischar MA, Mideo N

articles.headline: Scoping review of literature (2000–May 2024) identified that both malaria and typhoid fever elevate oxidative stress, further exacerbated in co-infections, with endogenous antioxidant responses often insufficient to neutralize reactive oxygen species. Antimalarial and antibacterial drugs modify oxidative-antioxidant dynamics, suggesting that understanding this balance may enable adjunctive therapies targeting oxidative stress
articles.title_original: Oxidative stress in malaria and typhoid fever: A scoping review of the pathogenic mechanisms and therapeutic implications.

articles.headline: Longitudinal entomological survey in Tripura, India (2019–2024) found morphological identification misclassified 9 An. fluviatilis and 500+ An. minimus, with molecular confirmation that fluviatilis-identified specimens were actually An. minimus (including 3 new haplotypes) due to hypermelanization. Combined morphological and molecular identification is essential; morphological misidentification can mislead vector control policies
articles.title_original: Diagnostic puzzle of Anopheles species using morphological vs. molecular approach: a case study in Tripura, a hyperendemic malaria state, Northeast India.

articles.headline: Double-blind RCT of 400 mother–child pairs (6–18 months, Uganda) randomized to permethrin-treated or sham-treated baby wraps (retreated every 4 weeks) found permethrin wraps reduced clinical malaria incidence to 0.73 per 100 person-weeks versus 2.14 in controls (incidence rate ratio 0.34, 95% CI 0.23–0.51, P<0.001). Rash occurred in 8.5% of intervention versus 6.0% of control participants
articles.title_original: Permethrin-Treated Baby Wraps for the Prevention of Malaria.

articles.headline: Generalized nonparametric temporal models with double-kernel smoothing applied to MAL-094/MAL-095 malaria vaccine efficacy trial demonstrated how prior infection and vaccination status modify future malaria infection risk over time. Models showed satisfactory finite-sample performance and provided new insight into vaccine protective effects and efficacy modification by infection history
articles.title_original: Generalized nonparametric temporal modeling of recurrent events with application to a malaria vaccine trial.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 300 P. falciparum-positive Kenyan children aged 2–5 years (June–August 2021) found pfdhfr triple mutant in 99.5% (217/218), pfdhps A437G in 92.1% (211/229), K540E in 19.1% (42/220), quintuple mutant in 4.6% (7/151), and zero sextuple mutants; chloroquine-resistance CVIET in 36.6%; no validated pfK13 artemisinin-resistance markers detected. Low SP-resistance prevalence supports continued use of SP-containing IPTp and ACT regimens
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Molecular Markers of Resistance to Antimalarial Drugs Three Years After Perennial Malaria Chemoprevention in Sierra Leone.

articles.headline: Sequencing of 95 pfcsp genes from P. falciparum isolates across five Vietnamese endemic provinces (2018–2022) and comparative analysis of 836 global sequences revealed limited diversity in N- and C-terminal non-repeating regions, notable polymorphisms including A98G and 19-amino-acid insertion in the N-terminus, and high polymorphism in the central repeat region. Findings provide critical insights for designing effective PfCSP-based vaccine candidates
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity and natural selection of circumsporozoite surface protein in Vietnam Plasmodium falciparum isolates.

articles.headline: Complete mitochondrial genome sequence of Plasmodium pitheci (Bornean orang-utan parasite) spans 5,871 bp with conserved gene arrangement typical of Plasmodium; Bayesian phylogenetics confirm previously identified Plasmodium sp. VM, VS, and Pongo clades A and B correspond to P. pitheci, though clade C classification remains unresolved. Genomic data enables development of species-specific diagnostics and informs zoonotic malaria control strategies
articles.title_original: The mitochondrial genome sequence of the Bornean orang-utan (Pongo pygmaeus) malaria parasite, Plasmodium pitheci.

articles.headline: In vitro antiplasmodial activity of green-synthesized Matricaria chamomilla–derived MgO nanoparticles (30–80 nm) against P. falciparum 3D7 (chloroquine-sensitive) and K1 (chloroquine-resistant) strains showed IC50 values of 0.19–0.22 mg/mL and 0.41–0.45 mg/mL, respectively, across varying solvent ratios. MgO NPs demonstrated activity against both drug-sensitive and drug-resistant P. falciparum strains, supporting further preclinical investigation
articles.title_original: Antimalarial potential of Matricaria chamomilla-derived MgO nanoparticles against Plasmodium falciparum strains: an experimental study.

articles.headline: Retrospective analysis of malaria testing in 46,734 pregnant women from Ghana's Savannah Region (2018–2022) found 54.5% test positivity (95% CI 54.0–55.0), with highest rates in women <20 years (59.3%, 95% CI 58.1–60.6) and Bole District (70.1%, accounting for 40.5% of regional cases). Test positivity declined from 60.4% (2018) to 51.6% (2022), but geographic disparities persist, indicating need for targeted SP-IPTp and ITN interventions
articles.title_original: Trend of malaria test positivity rate among pregnant women, Savannah Region, Ghana, 2018-2022.

articles.headline: Review examining genetically attenuated parasite (GAP) vaccines arresting at different liver-stage time points; late-arresters (6-7 day arrest) demonstrated superior efficacy compared to early-arresters (24-48 hour arrest), suggesting liver stage immunity is critical for sterile protection, with focus on safety and implementation strategies for human vaccination
articles.title_original: The path from early- to late-liver stage arresting genetically attenuated parasites as a malaria vaccination strategy.

articles.headline: Cost analysis in Nigeria comparing Sulphadoxine-Pyrimethamine plus Amodaiquine (SPAQ)-based seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) alone versus SMC with vitamin A supplementation in 170,681 children; integration increased cost per child by $0.24 ($0.94 to $1.18), with distribution and drug costs as largest contributors, supporting feasibility of integrated delivery to reduce child mortality
articles.title_original: A cost analysis comparing seasonal malaria chemoprevention with and without Vitamin A supplementation among under-5 children in Nigeria.

articles.headline: Review of severe cerebral malaria (P. falciparum) with impaired consciousness (Glasgow Coma Score <11) showing 15-25% mortality and long-term neurological sequelae; management focuses on intravenous artesunate as first-line therapy with targeted interventions for seizures and systemic complications, emphasizing neuromonitoring strategies and multidisciplinary ICU approaches
articles.title_original: Management and monitoring strategies for severe cerebral malaria: a guide for the intensivist.

articles.headline: Small molecule 116-9e inhibits assembly of P. falciparum Hsp40-cochaperone PfYdj1 with Hsp70-1, destabilizing ribonucleotide reductase subunit PfR2 and reducing dNTP production to block DNA replication; combination with benzo-hydroxamate catalytic inhibitor shows synergistic antiparasitic effects
articles.title_original: Inhibition of the assembly of Plasmodium Hsp70-1 and Hsp40 complex blocks DNA replication by destabilizing ribonucleotide reductase subunit-2.

articles.headline: First immune complex application to parasitic vaccine: R21/Matrix-M administered subcutaneously achieved 100% protective efficacy in BALB/c mice with two immunizations, and R21:2A10 immune complex formulation induced sterile protection, demonstrating potential for improved malaria vaccination with alternative delivery strategies
articles.title_original: Subcutaneous administration of the malaria R21/Matrix M vaccine and immune complex formation with anti-circumsporozoite protein mAb 2A10 elicit protective efficacy in mice.

articles.headline: Comparative study of Anopheles stephensi gonotrophic cycles found insulin-like peptides AsILP3 and AsILP4, but not ovary ecdysteroidogenic hormone, stimulate yolk deposition and vitellogenesis; mated females produced more eggs than unmated females due to male-transferred ecdysteroids, revealing species-specific differences in egg formation regulation
articles.title_original: Insulin-like peptides activate egg formation in the Asian malaria mosquito Anopheles stephensi.
articles.authors: Phipps BL, Brown MR, Strand MR

articles.headline: Rapid review of 30 studies on malaria vaccine implementation (2000-2024); RTS,S/AS01 most commonly studied (n=23) with efficacy 30-75% and cost-effectiveness $20-$50 per DALY averted; routine immunization was predominant delivery strategy (40%), though barriers included logistical constraints, community acceptance, and healthcare infrastructure limitations
articles.title_original: Costs, delivery strategies and implementation challenges for malaria vaccines: a rapid review of literature.

articles.headline: Systematic review of γδ T-cell responses in P. falciparum malaria and tuberculosis; malaria rapidly activates Vγ9Vδ2+ cells with antiparasitic cytokine production, but chronic/repeated exposure leads to functional exhaustion with reduced responsiveness; active TB associated with reduced peripheral γδ T-cell frequencies, suggesting context-dependent immune dynamics critical for vaccine development
articles.title_original: Exploring γδ T-Cell Responses in Malaria and Tuberculosis: Implications for Immunity, Coinfection, and Disease Management.
articles.authors: Mohamud YN, Chaijaroenkul W, Na-Bangchang K

articles.headline: Distributed lag nonlinear analysis of weekly malaria cases from Uganda (July 2018-February 2023) found 2-8 week lag between rainfall exceeding 200 mm/week and increased symptomatic malaria risk, with no statistically significant temperature association; school-aged children showed reduced climate sensitivity compared to other age groups
articles.title_original: Quantifying the Lagged Effects of Climate Variables on Malaria Risk in Eastern Uganda.

articles.headline: Experimental P. fragile infection in a male rhesus macaque produced clinical malaria signs including anemia, altered blood cell distribution, and histopathological changes in vital organs (liver, lungs, heart, brain) with parasitized erythrocytes in organ vasculature, establishing P. fragile infection in NHPs as a translatable human malaria model
articles.title_original: Clinical and Pathological Outcomes of Experimental Plasmodium fragile Infection in a Rhesus Macaque.

articles.headline: Whole-genome sequencing identified P. falciparum PfAcAS mutations S868G and V950I in parasites recurring after artesunate or dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine therapy; CRISPR-engineered parasites carrying these mutations showed reduced in vitro susceptibility to artemisinin derivatives (dihydroartemisinin, artesunate, artemether) and chloroquine, though absolute changes were modest
articles.title_original: Acetyl-CoA synthetase mutations affect the susceptibility of Plasmodium falciparum to antimalarial drugs.

articles.headline: P. falciparum mitochondrial Complex III serves dual functions: conditional expression studies showed mitochondrial processing peptidase α (MPPα) remains essential even when electron transport chain function is bypassed, and MPPα knockdown increases parasite hypersensitivity to proguanil, indicating Complex III processes imported mitochondrial proteins beyond ubiquinone regeneration
articles.title_original: Dual Function of Mitochondrial Complex III in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Difference-in-Differences geospatial analysis across Ghana, Côte d'Ivoire, and Gabon found no statistically significant increase in P. falciparum malaria incidence or prevalence within 15 km of hydropower sites post-implementation (p-values 0.089–0.990), challenging the assumed causal link. Environmental and socioeconomic factors (urbanization, elevation, climate) showed strong associations with malaria transmission (p<0.01). Hydropower presence alone is not a primary driver of malaria dynamics
articles.title_original: Hydropower development and malaria transmission: A geospatial econometric study.

articles.headline: C. elegans infrared-based motility assay screening of 400 compounds from MMV COVID and Global Health Priority Boxes identified 12 hits including 9 known anthelmintics plus three novel compounds: flufenerim, flucofuron, and indomethacin with EC50 values 0.211–23.174 µM. HEK293 toxicity screening showed EC50 0.453–>100 µM. These three compounds warrant development as new anthelmintics
articles.title_original: Discovery of small molecules with anthelmintic potential in the Medicines for Malaria Venture's COVID and Global Health Priority Boxes using an infrared-based assay for Caenorhabditis elegans motility.
articles.authors: Sun YU, Liu LJ, Caffrey CR

articles.headline: Therapeutic efficacy study of 305 children (6–59 months) with uncomplicated P. falciparum comparing artemether-lumefantrine (AL, n=152) and artesunate-amodiaquine (ASAQ, n=153) over 28 days showed PCR-corrected ACPR of 100% (Saclepea) and 95.9% (Sinje) for AL, and 100% (Saclepea) and 94.4% (Sinje) for ASAQ. Both exceeded the 90% WHO efficacy threshold with no adverse events
articles.title_original: Efficacy and safety of artemether-lumefantrine (AL) and artesunate-amodiaquine (ASAQ) for the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Liberia, 2022-2023.

articles.headline: Experimental hut trial in Benin testing Vector Guard (alpha-cypermethrin-PBO net, n=6,799 wild pyrethroid-resistant An. gambiae) showed mortality of 36.4% unwashed and 17.2% after 20 washes versus Olyset Plus 17.5% unwashed and 8.7% washed (both p<0.001). Non-inferiority analysis: Vector Guard mortality OR 2.71 (95% CI 2.26–3.24) with superior wash retention (>94% alpha-cypermethrin vs 69.6%)
articles.title_original: Efficacy of Vector Guard®, a mosaic alpha-cypermethrin and piperonyl butoxide-treated net, for the control of pyrethroid-resistant malaria vectors: a non-inferiority experimental hut evaluation in Benin.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 301 caregivers in Ondo State found 94% suspected malaria in under-five children within 12 months, yet only 9.3% visited health facilities; 37.4% purchased from patent vendors and 25.2% used herbs. Multivariate predictors of facility care: female caregiver aOR 3.32 (95% CI 1.29–8.54), urban residence aOR 4.25 (1.72–10.48), proximity <5 km aOR 3.38 (1.03–11.07), and good malaria knowledge aOR 4.16 (1.61–10.77)
articles.title_original: Care-seeking behaviour of caregivers for suspected malaria in under-five children in a Southwestern State of Nigeria.
articles.authors: Akinrinade OT, Isere EE, Ajayi IO

articles.headline: Monoclonal antibody mAb02 selectively recognizes PfEMP1 proteins bearing the DBLβmotif associated with cerebral malaria pathogenesis. mAb02 inhibits binding of DBLβmotif-positive PfEMP1 to ICAM-1 and prevents adhesion of infected erythrocytes to ICAM-1 in vitro. The epitope is located in the ICAM-1-binding site, suggesting therapeutic potential for antibody-based cerebral malaria intervention
articles.title_original: A monoclonal antibody selectively recognizing PfEMP1 proteins associated with cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Flow cytometry and scRNA-seq of spleens from C57BL/6 mice infected with Plasmodium yoelii NSM revealed significant upregulation of LAG3 in CD8+ T cells, which displayed enhanced activation, proliferation, and cytokine production. Dual-fluorescence assays identified NFATc1 as a transcription factor binding the LAG3 promoter; siRNA knockdown of NFATc1 reduced LAG3 expression, suggesting positive regulation of LAG3 by NFATc1
articles.title_original: NFATc1 regulates LAG3+CD8+ T cells in the spleen of mice infected with Plasmodium yoelii NSM.

articles.headline: Structure-activity optimization identified PPG and 9-HPF as PfSSB inhibitors effective against drug-sensitive P. falciparum 3D7 (IC50 54.95 and 9.13 µM, respectively) and artemisinin-resistant C580Y (resistance index 0.79 and 1.26). MTT assays confirm low cytotoxicity. Virtual screening of 59,807 structurally similar compounds identified additional candidates with higher binding affinities
articles.title_original: Targeting Plasmodium falciparum Single-Stranded DNA-Binding Protein: Discovery of New Scaffold Compounds Effective against Drug-Sensitive and Artemisinin-Resistant Strains.
