articles.headline: Self-assembling redox nanoparticles (RNP) administered intraperitoneally in Plasmodium berghei-infected mice remained in circulation >24 h. RNP significantly reduced blood ROS levels compared to low-molecular-weight TEMPOL, markedly improving disease severity and survival. RNP suppressed oxidative damage and fragility of infected erythrocytes, demonstrating potential as a breakthrough therapeutic for malaria and oxidative stress-driven infections
articles.title_original: Self-Assembling Antioxidant Nanoparticles Increase Survival and Reduce Disease Symptoms of Severe Malaria.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 130 adults in Nnewi North LGA at end of rainy season (November 2024) detected 26.15% (34/130) asymptomatic P. falciparum infection by mRDT. Covering water containers was protective (OR 0.29–0.27). Financial ability to afford prevention tools (ITNs, insecticides, repellents) was associated with lower asymptomatic infection (OR 0.29–0.27, 95% CI 0.06–1.24), highlighting economic empowerment's role in malaria reduction
articles.title_original: Economic empowerment and various preventive strategies play a role in reducing asymptomatic malaria towards the end of the rainy season.
articles.authors: Okpala C, Umeh I, Anagu LO

articles.headline: C1'-branched acyclic nucleoside phosphonates targeting P. falciparum HGXPRT showed Ki values of 0.4 µM (parasite enzyme) and 0.7 µM (human enzyme). A phosphonodiamidate prodrug exhibited IC50 of 4.3 µM against drug-sensitive P. falciparum grown in human erythrocytes, demonstrating in vitro activity and merit as potential drug leads
articles.title_original: C1'-Branched Acyclic Nucleoside Phosphonates as Inhibitors of Plasmodium Falciparum 6-Oxopurine Phosphoribosyltransferase.

articles.headline: Proteomic analysis of IgG responses in 10 malaria-naïve adults vaccinated with R21/Matrix-M revealed polarized anti-NANP (circumsporozoite protein tetrapeptide) repertoires heavily skewed for IGHV3-30/3-33 genes with minimal somatic mutation, remaining static after controlled human malaria challenge. Vaccine-induced antibodies cross-reacted with the N-terminal junction CSP epitope; mined NANP-specific mAbs blocked sporozoite invasion in vitro and prevented parasitemia in vivo
articles.title_original: Repertoire, function, and structure of serological antibodies induced by the R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccine.

articles.headline: CDC light trap collections at 24 Nchelenge households over 6 weeks captured 12 anopheline species; Anopheles funestus dominated (n=1,865, 68.2%, primarily indoors). P. falciparum-positive specimens appeared in all trap types during early evening (16:00–22:00), with highest EIR among indoor An. funestus (18.74 bites/person/6 months). Similar anopheline activity before and after 22:00 indicates transmission is not confined to late-night indoor settings
articles.title_original: Early-evening indoor and outdoor foraging by major malaria vectors in Nchelenge, Zambia.

articles.headline: Codesign workshops in Benin, Cameroon, Côte d'Ivoire, and Mozambique generated four context-specific perennial malaria chemoprevention (PMC) strategies with 4–8 contact points each, leveraging distinct health system platforms. The collaborative approach enhanced stakeholder buy-in and identified implementation insights generalizable beyond malaria programming
articles.title_original: Tailoring malaria control interventions to suit local context: codesign of perennial malaria chemoprevention (PMC) programmes through the Plus Project.

articles.headline: In 90 Ghanaian children (1–10 years: 60 with malaria, 30 healthy controls), anti-erythropoietin antibodies were detected in 5.6% of malaria patients versus 0% of controls (not statistically significant), with 5.0% in the anaemia subgroup and 3.37% without anaemia (p=0.640). Antibody positivity was significantly associated with elevated erythropoietin and younger age, but not P. falciparum infection or malarial anaemia
articles.title_original: Serum Anti-Erythropoietin Autoantibodies and Their Association With Younger Age in Paediatric Malaria Cases.

articles.headline: A review examining how genetic complexity and multiple Plasmodium life stages complicate malaria vaccine development. WHO has approved only two vaccines to date; the review discusses novel vaccine candidates targeting different parasite life stages and identifying host immune responses to enhance efficacy
articles.title_original: Malaria vaccines: Current developments and immunological insights.

articles.headline: A historical review of Plasmodium vivax circumsporozoite surface protein (PvCSP) vaccine development, noting immunogenic potential and preclinical protection but absence of consistent efficacy in clinical trials. Reviews innovative approaches to overcome P. vivax-specific challenges including hypnozoite formation and limited in vitro culture systems
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax circumsporozoite surface protein (PvCSP) as a vaccine candidate: a brief review.
articles.journal: Rev Inst Med Trop Sao Paulo

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 446 pregnant women in Gambella, Ethiopia (Feb–Apr 2024) found malaria prevalence of 15.2% (95% CI: 11.4–17.5), with P. falciparum 8.96% (40/446), P. vivax 2.7% (12/446), and comorbidity 3.6% (16/446). Independent risk factors: no formal education (AOR=1.32), lack of malaria knowledge (AOR=2.7), stagnant water nearby (AOR=3.61), non-ITN ownership (AOR=5.74), and low altitude (AOR=5.26)
articles.title_original: Malaria in Pregnancy and Predisposing Factors in Jor District of Gambella Region, Southwest Ethiopia: A Cross-Sectional Study.

articles.headline: Mixed-methods assessment of 539 artisanal/small-scale gold miners (median age 38 years, 73.5% male) across Guiana Shield gold mining communities revealed high awareness of mosquito vectors but limited knowledge of P. vivax characteristics and radical treatment requirements. Understanding community disease interpretations is essential for context-specific malaria elimination strategies
articles.title_original: Malária incubada: a mixed methods analysis on knowledge and experiences on Plasmodium vivax and asymptomatic malaria infections in a hard-to-reach and mobile population in the Amazon.

articles.headline: Quantitative proteomics and lipid analysis defined detergent-resistant membrane (DRM) composition and dynamics in P. falciparum schizonts and gametocytes, revealing conserved and stage-specific features. Host DRMs are remodeled after parasite invasion, with erythrocyte junctional complex components repositioned to the parasitophorous vacuole membrane
articles.title_original: Dynamics of detergent-resistant membranes of Plasmodium falciparum during blood stage development.

articles.headline: In intravenous challenge models, tobacco mosaic virus capsid particles displaying P. falciparum CSP epitopes were compared: minor repeat candidates failed to confer sterile protection despite high in vitro activity, whereas major repeats (NPNAx20, NPNAx5) and junctional+minor epitope constructs induced protection. TMV-NPNAx20 achieved liver burden reduction equivalent to RTS,S at lower antibody concentrations and was selected for clinical manufacture
articles.title_original: Comparison of major, minor and junctional circumsporozoite protein epitopes for malaria vaccine design.

articles.headline: Sixteen-month cohort of 1–3-year-old children in Papua New Guinea used Bayesian modeling with genotyped P. vivax infections to estimate clinical illness probability. Illness risk declined exponentially with cumulative primary infections; relapses contributed approximately 50% of clinical P. vivax illness despite representing 80% of blood-stage force of infection, informing intervention impact projections
articles.title_original: The probability of Plasmodium vivax acute illness following primary infection and relapse in Papua New Guinea.

articles.headline: Open-label trial of 120 patients with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria in artemisinin-resistant Gia Lai, Vietnam treated with pyronaridine-artesunate daily × 3 days plus primaquine achieved 92.5% (95% CI: 85.5–96.2) PCR-adjusted cure at Day 42. Median parasite clearance was 84 hours; eight recrudescence cases had lower pyronaridine concentrations (median 33.3 ng/mL) than cure cases (median 47.6 ng/mL), suggesting reduced exposure may drive failures
articles.title_original: Therapeutic efficacy monitoring of pyronaridine-artesunate (Pyramax®) in treating uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Gia Lai province, Vietnam from 2022 to 2023.

articles.headline: Computational structure-based metric enabled rapid screening of >3×10⁴⁷ antibody variants targeting P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein (PfCSP), generating high-affinity variants of CIS43 and L9 monoclonal antibodies. Coupling microevolutionary selection principles to in silico screening demonstrates flexibility for improving diverse PfCSP antibodies for passive immunization strategies
articles.title_original: A Single Structure-Derived Computational Metric Predicts High-Affinity Antibody Selection Against a Malaria Antigen.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 154 individuals with clinically confirmed lymphedema in Kamwenge District, Uganda (71.4% female, mean age 54.7 years). Plasmodium falciparum detected in 3.3% (n=5); majority had bilateral lower limb lymphedema. Findings underscore the need for integrated healthcare approaches in areas where multiple parasitic diseases are endemic
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Malaria Among Individuals Living With Lymphedema in Kamwenge District, Western Uganda.

articles.headline: Rwanda's data-driven drug resistance mitigation strategy deployed targeted therapies based on Pfkelch13 mutations: artesunate-pyronaridine for western region (A675V), dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine for eastern/central regions (R561H), and artemether-lumefantrine for southern region (low R561H prevalence). Strategy aims to counter countrywide increase in artemisinin-based antimalarial drug resistance
articles.title_original: Strategies for mitigating emerging artemisinin-based antimalarial drug resistance in Rwanda: a promising approach for managing therapies in malaria-endemic countries.

articles.headline: Repeated cross-sectional study of seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) costs across Mali, Burkina Faso, and Senegal (376–398 caregivers per country, April–November 2022, two cycles). Financial costs per dose: $0.99–1.51; economic costs: $2.96–3.14. Annual costs per child (four doses) ranged $3.97–6.05 (financial) and $11.85–12.57 (economic), with indirect productivity losses comprising 50%–66% of total economic costs
articles.title_original: Estimating the opportunity cost of seasonal malaria chemoprevention implementation in Burkina Faso, Mali and Senegal.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 456 pregnant women in Ghana (April–August 2024) using serological screening and structural equation modeling. Malaria in pregnancy prevalence 21.5% (95% CI 17.7–25.3); Plasmodium falciparum 14.7%, P. falciparum + P. malariae co-infection 3.5%, high parasitemia in 6.4%. Environmental, sociocultural, and socioeconomic factors showed synergistic association with malaria in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Malaria in pregnancy: a holistic exploration of synergistic association through environmental, sociocultural, and socioeconomic lenses in Pru east municipality, Ghana.

articles.headline: Study of 300 suspected malaria cases in southeastern Iran comparing microscopy, real-time PCR with high-resolution melting (HRM) analysis, and sequencing. HRM detected Plasmodium falciparum in 5% and P. vivax in 4.66% with 2.73-degree species differentiation. HRM demonstrated high sensitivity, specificity, and complete agreement with sequencing, supporting its utility as a reliable diagnostic method
articles.title_original: Optimization of the real-time PCR platform method using high-resolution melting analysis and comparison with sequencing and phylogenetic analysis for developing optimal malaria diagnostic methods.

articles.headline: Literature review aggregating kelch13 mutation data from 112,933 P. falciparum samples (1980–2023) assessing artemisinin partial resistance (ART-R) globally. ART-R widespread across Southeast Asia and emerged in East Africa by 2024; recent increases in Africa mirror Southeast Asia patterns from 10–15 years prior, raising concern for accelerated spread and compromised treatment efficacy across the continent
articles.title_original: Understanding the global rise of artemisinin resistance: Insights from over 100,000 Plasmodium falciparum samples.

articles.headline: In vitro study of green-synthesized magnesium oxide nanoparticles from Achillea millefolium tested against chloroquine-sensitive (3D7) and chloroquine-resistant (K1) Plasmodium falciparum strains. Most effective formulation achieved IC50 0.17 mg/ml (3D7) and 0.76 mg/ml (K1), demonstrating potent antiplasmodial activity against both sensitive and resistant strains
articles.title_original: Antiplasmodial Activity of Green-Synthesized MgO Nanoparticles Using Achillea millefolium Against Chloroquine-Resistant and-Sensitive Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Proteomic analysis of serum from 61 naturally infected free-living macaques in Thailand (25 Macaca leonina, 32 M. arctoides, 4 M. fascicularis) using liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry. Plasmodium prevalence 20% (Ml), 50% (Ma, 34.78% zoonotic), 100% (Mf); identified 698 differentially expressed proteins, with 6 of 9 previously reported malaria-associated proteins consistently expressed across species as potential biomarkers
articles.title_original: Comparative serum proteomics of plasmodium-infected free-living macaques in Thailand: Host immune responses and candidate biomarkers for zoonotic malaria.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 4 RCTs comparing arterolane-piperaquine versus artemether-lumefantrine for uncomplicated falciparum malaria. 28-day PCR-corrected risk difference 0 (95% CI −0.02 to 0.02); 42-day PCR-corrected risk difference 0.01 (95% CI −0.01 to 0.03); similar adverse effect profiles. Arterolane-piperaquine demonstrated non-inferiority to artemether-lumefantrine
articles.title_original: A systematic review and meta-analysis of clinical trials comparing arterolane-piperaquine vs. artemether-lumefantrine for the treatment of uncomplicated falciparum malaria.

articles.headline: Laboratory and in vivo study of engineered L9_yd19 monoclonal antibody targeting Plasmodium falciparum circumsporozoite protein (PfCSP). L9_yd19 showed modest increased potency against transgenic Plasmodium with two spaced PfCSP minor repeats and retained potency against 3D7; was protective against parasites expressing only PfCSP major repeats (NANP12). Cryo-EM revealed dual homotypic binding interfaces
articles.title_original: Anti-Malaria Antibody Engineering Broadens Recognition Motifs and Reveals New Homotypic Interactions that Enhance Protective Breadth.

articles.headline: Literature review (1955–2025) on splenic macrophages in malaria pathogenesis and immunity. Splenic macrophages exhibit dual roles in disease progression and severity, polarizing along M1/M2 continuum during Plasmodium infection. Targeting macrophage-specific functions and biomarkers could enhance vaccine development, diagnostics, prognosis, and treatment outcomes
articles.title_original: Emerging Role of Splenic Macrophage in Malaria Pathogenesis and Immunity.
articles.authors: Gupta A, Kalkal M, Das J

articles.headline: Case report of 52-year-old male with high-parasitemia Plasmodium falciparum infection (travel history to Liberia) complicated by metabolic acidosis and acute renal failure. Intravenous artesunate treatment produced rapid clinical response with significant parasitemia reduction. Case emphasizes critical importance of early diagnosis and prompt artesunate treatment in severe malaria
articles.title_original: [Artesunate and Severe Malaria: The Importance of Proper Treatment Steps and Laboratory Monitoring].
articles.authors: Özer D, Çavuş İ, Özbilgin A

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 1,536 malaria case records and 82 health workers across 32 primary healthcare centers in Nigeria found overall ACT compliance for uncomplicated malaria was 83.2% (89.1% in Global Fund-supported centers vs 76.5% government-supported, P=0.00). ACT stock-outs occurred in 100% of government centers versus 43.8% of Global Fund-supported centers. Compliance was enhanced by increased free ACT availability, access to guidelines, case management aids, and supervision frequency
articles.title_original: Compliance with the Use of Artemisinin-based Combination Therapy in the Treatment of Uncomplicated Malaria amongst Primary Healthcare Workers in Anambra State, Nigeria - The Role of Funding.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 407 pregnant women in Maiduguri, Nigeria found 80.8% had good knowledge of malaria prevention strategies but 79.1% showed overall poor utilization. Only 60.9% used long-lasting insecticide-treated nets. Knowledge was significantly associated with pregnancy trimester (P=0.0044), gravidity (P=0.019), and education (P=0.001). Authors conclude that despite awareness, there is a substantial gap in effective utilization of prevention methods
articles.title_original: Knowledge and Utilisation of Malaria Prevention Strategy among Pregnant Women in Some Selected Primary Health Centres in Maiduguri, Borno State, Nigeria.

articles.headline: Retrospective cohort study of 1,558 pregnant women in Northern Uganda found 871 (55.9%) were lost to follow-up in malaria screening programs. Protective factors against loss-to-follow-up included higher education (aOR=0.75, p=0.0798), being primigravida (aOR=0.73, p=0.0275), and experiencing malaria during pregnancy (aOR=0.61, p<0.0001). Women at Aboke Health Center IV were over five times more likely to be lost-to-follow-up than those at Aber Hospital (aOR=5.57, p<0.0001), highlighting geographic and structural barriers to retention
articles.title_original: Determinants of loss to follow-up among pregnant women living in a high malaria burden setting in Northern Uganda.

articles.headline: Multi-omics analysis of Anopheles funestus across Africa (2014–2021) identified novel overexpressed genes (V-ATPase, tubulin alpha-1, transposase) alongside canonical resistance genes driving pyrethroid resistance escalation. CPR gene mutations showed dramatic selection: N70I mutation in Malawi increased from 0% (2009) to 80% (2021), and a 5.9 kb promoter duplication emerged in Ghana. Transgenic studies confirmed CPR-70I enhances pyrethroid resistance with P450-CYP6P9a, identifying candidate markers for tracking intensely resistant mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Genomic Drivers of Pyrethroid Resistance Escalation in the Malaria Vector Anopheles funestus Across Africa.

articles.headline: No abstract available; title indicates study models the threat of pfhrp2/3 gene deletions for malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Modeling the threat of pfhrp2/3 gene deletions for malaria diagnosis.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling incorporating pfhrp2/3 deletion prevalence data found deletions detected in 40 of 47 surveyed countries. Modeling predicts primary spread southward from Horn of Africa through East Africa within 20 years. Four countries not yet switched to alternative diagnostics were consistently classified as high-risk under multiple model assumptions. Study provides refined predictions to guide pfhrp2/3 surveillance and policy responses
articles.title_original: Global risk of selection and spread of Plasmodium falciparum histidine-rich protein 2 and 3 gene deletions.

articles.headline: Investigation of 10 locally acquired malaria cases from 4 states (2023)—the largest US outbreak in 35 years—identified 4 genetic lineages across 783 Anopheles mosquitoes. All 9 P. vivax cases had Central/South American genetic signatures; Maryland P. falciparum had African origin. All three Florida P. vivax cases shared identical strain. Outbreaks remained contained to individual counties; sustained transmission is unlikely, though future risk may increase with global travel, temperature changes, and persistent vectors
articles.title_original: Public Health Response to the First Locally Acquired Malaria Outbreaks in the US in 20 Years.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 1,134 individuals in 3 Tanzanian villages found Plasmodium prevalence of 19.2% by microscopy and 24.3% by rapid diagnostic tests, with highest infection in school children aged 5–<15 years (27.3% microscopy, 37.6% RDTs, p<0.001). Closed eaves were protective (aOR=0.91, p<0.001). Prevalence varied significantly across villages (14.7–24.6%, p=0.001), indicating high heterogeneity at micro-geographic levels. P. falciparum accounted for 97% of single infections
articles.title_original: Prevalence and risk factors associated with infections caused by Plasmodium parasites at micro-geographic level in three villages of Muheza district in Tanga region, north-eastern Tanzania.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 112 P. falciparum isolates from endemic (Kisumu) and epidemic (Kisii) regions in Kenya genotyped SP resistance markers. Fully resistant double mutant SGEAA (70.18% Kisumu vs 51.06% Kisii) and triple mutant IRNI (86.67% vs 98.08%) showed regional differences (p<0.05). Quintuple mutant SGEAA-IRNI approached fixation (57.89% vs 48.94%); sextuple HGEAA-IRNI was significantly higher in Kisii (44.68% vs 16.31%, p<0.05). Findings suggest emergence of super-resistant haplotypes and warrant review of SP therapeutic efficacy
articles.title_original: Regional variation in sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance genotypes and haplotypes of Plasmodium falciparum dihydrofolate reductase and dihydropteroate synthase genes in Western Kenya.

articles.headline: Passive case detection (July 2016–July 2017, 398,424 person-weeks) and active surveys in Korhogo, Côte d'Ivoire found mean weekly malaria incidence of 0.25 per 100 person-weeks (130 per 1000 person-years). Case prevalence ranged from 13.4% in dry season to 43.4% in late rainy season, with highest burden in children under 5 years. Parasite prevalence in 6 villages reached 85.4–100% by thick smear; P. falciparum dominated at 97% of single infections. The region is identified as a malaria transmission hotspot suitable for assessing additional vector control interventions
articles.title_original: Malaria epidemiology in the Korhogo area, Northern Côte d'Ivoire: baseline assessment prior to a randomized controlled trial.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional comparison of schoolchildren in Ghana (1,154 in 2023 vs 1,109 in 2017) showed asymptomatic P. falciparum infection by microscopy decreased from 16.05% to 1.21%, and by PCR from 39.95% to 17.33%. Sahel zone prevalence declined 60% in 2023 compared to 2017. Submicroscopic infections decreased from 24.35% (2017) to 16.46% (2023), with forest zone remaining highest at both timepoints (47.01% and 28.28%). Results demonstrate significant reduction in parasite carriage despite ongoing need for surveillance
articles.title_original: Reduction in Asymptomatic falciparum malaria infection amongst schoolchildren in three ecological zones of Ghana between 2017 and 2023.

articles.headline: Phase 2b RCT of 1,609 children (5–17 months) in Ghana and Kenya over 50 months compared full-dose and fractional-dose RTS,S/AS01E vaccine to rabies control. Vaccine efficacy at month 50 was: R012-20 36% (95% CI 19–50), R012-14-26-38 51% (37–61), Fx012-14-26-38 43% (28–55), and Fx017-20-32 41% (26–53), all p<0.001. Additional annual doses maintained efficacy; fractional-dose regimens averted 1,151–1,134 malaria cases per 1,000 vaccine equivalents. Only 5 serious adverse events were vaccine-related with no deaths, supporting that fractional dosing could increase vaccine availability and reduce costs
articles.title_original: Sustained efficacy of the RTS,S/AS01(E) malaria vaccine over 50 months of follow-up when used in full-dose or fractional-dose regimens in young children in Ghana and Kenya: final results from an open-label, phase 2b, randomised controlled trial.

articles.headline: Review proposing that gut microbiota mediate rhythmic fluctuations in mosquito susceptibility and Plasmodium parasite development within Anopheles vectors, integrating chronobiology with transmission ecology to explain how microbe-driven circadian patterns influence parasite-vector interactions and the timing of malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Do microbes mediate rhythmic malaria parasite-mosquito vector interactions?
articles.authors: Riithi N, Mooney JP, Reece SE

articles.headline: Droplet digital PCR detected Plasmodium infections in 0.25% of samples from endemic Thailand (n=403) and 7.41% from Sudan-returned workers (n=270), compared to 2.59% by microscopy, with superior sensitivity capturing low-parasitemia asymptomatic infections (median 0.95 copies/μL, range 0.16–7033.43) across five species including P. falciparum, P. vivax, P. malariae, P. ovale, and P. knowlesi, enabling surveillance of hidden parasite reservoirs
articles.title_original: High-sensitivity detection of asymptomatic malaria in high-risk Thai populations using droplet digital PCR.

articles.headline: In a P. berghei-infected mouse model, artesunate-loaded nanoparticles (16 mg/kg, 3 injections over 24 hr) significantly improved parasite clearance and prevented hyperparasitaemia (≥5%), while dihydroartemisinin and artemisinin nanoparticles (10 mg/kg, 4 injections over 24 hr) showed stronger clearance than non-encapsulated drugs, demonstrating enhanced efficacy of metal-organic framework encapsulation against artemisinin-resistant malaria
articles.title_original: Effects of artesunate-loaded nanoparticles on Plasmodium berghei treatment in a mouse model.

articles.headline: Entomological surveillance in western Burkina Faso (Aug–Oct 2023) documented seasonal decline in entomological inoculation rates (0.875 → 0.063 infectious bites/person/month August–October) across seven Anopheles species, with kdr-west/kdr-east mutations prevalent in A. gambiae s.l. complex; despite mass distribution of PBO LLINs and Interceptor G2, substantial residual transmission persisted due to vector behavioral adaptability and insecticide resistance
articles.title_original: Residual malaria transmission in Western Burkina Faso: Vector Behavior, insecticide resistance, and the efficacy limits of next-generation LLINs.

articles.headline: Among 30 1,2,4-triazole amides tested in vitro against P. falciparum 3D7, meta-substituted derivatives showed highest activity: compounds 17 (3,5-dimethyl), 11 (3-Br), and 7 (3-Cl) achieved IC50 values of 0.87, 1.27, and 2.17 μg/mL respectively, with comparable activity against chloroquine-resistant INDO strain (IC50 1.55, 5.70, 2.30 μg/mL), no cytotoxicity, and favorable in silico docking with P. falciparum dihydrofolate reductase, representing the first report of this compound class as anti-plasmodial agents
articles.title_original: 1,2,4-Triazole amides: Design, synthesis, characterisation, in vitro and in silico evaluation against blood stage parasites of Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Review examining tissue-dependent mechanisms by which antibodies targeting the circumsporozoite protein (CSP) of Plasmodium sporozoites confer protection in animal models and humans, exploring how anti-CSP antibodies neutralize sporozoites and mediate multilayered, tissue-specific protection to improve vaccine and antibody-based prophylaxis
articles.title_original: Tissue-dependent protection mechanisms of antibodies targeting Plasmodium sporozoites.

articles.headline: Systematic review of African P. falciparum mutations (2019–2024) documented validated kelch13 mutations (artemisinin resistance) in east Africa—Eritrea (R622I, 68%), Uganda (R561H, 52%)—and emerging mutations in Rwanda (C469F, 36%), Uganda (C469F 59%, P441L 69%), Tanzania (P441L 20%); partner-drug resistance (Pfcrt, Pfmdr1, Pfdhfr, Pfdhps) ranged 30–38% (east) to 44–55% (west), driven primarily by drug pressure (23–35%), drug misuse (10–12.5%), and cross-border movement (10%)
articles.title_original: Mapping Plasmodium falciparum mutations in Africa: A critical review of emerging drug resistance and implications for malaria control.

articles.headline: First detection of 88 specimens of Nyssorhynchus rondoniensis in southern Venezuela with one P. falciparum-infected mosquito (1.14% infection rate), identified by COI barcoding; concern elevated by its detection across seasons in peridomestic and forest fringe habitats with anthropophilic, early-evening biting behavior in a gold-mining region previously unreported outside western Brazil
articles.title_original: First detection of Nyssorhynchus rondoniensis (Diptera: Culicidae) in southern Venezuela and its infection with Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: In mice infected with P. chabaudi, histopathological analysis revealed that treatment with chloroquine alone or combined with vasodilators (captopril, losartan) reduced hepatic tissue changes including sinusoid congestion, leukocyte infiltration, hemozoin deposition, and portal inflammation; captopril particularly reduced liver cytotoxicity, suggesting ACE inhibitors may attenuate malaria-induced hepatic damage
articles.title_original: Captopril reduces liver cytotoxicity in a murine model of Plasmodium chabaudi infection.

articles.headline: Scoping review (957 PubMed records, 49 studies included) examining analytical approaches for assessing malaria intervention effectiveness using routine health management information systems data; demonstrates that HMIS data can regularly monitor intervention efficacy and health outcomes to support progress toward national strategic malaria control targets
articles.title_original: Use of Routine Health Data to Monitor Malaria Intervention Effectiveness: A Scoping Review.

articles.headline: Randomized trial (n=106) comparing artemether-lumefantrine (AL) plus primaquine (PQ) versus AL alone for uncomplicated P. falciparum in Ethiopia: PCR-uncorrected cure rates were 100% (AL) and 98.1% (AL+PQ) (P=0.31); gametocyte clearance achieved 100% by day 7 with AL+PQ versus day 14 with AL alone, though low baseline gametocyte prevalence (9.4%) limits transmission-blocking impact of PQ addition
articles.title_original: Efficacy and gametocyte carriage in Plasmodium falciparum cases treated with artemether-lumefantrine alone versus with single-dose primaquine in Ethiopia: A randomized controlled study.

articles.headline: Small RNA sequencing in P. yoelii-infected mouse splenocytes (day 13) revealed 171 miRNAs dysregulated in lethal Py17XL and 220 in non-lethal Py17XNL strains, with 79 commonly dysregulated, 53 unique to lethal, and 86 unique to non-lethal infection; distinct miRNA signatures (upregulated miR-875-3p, miR-30a-3p in non-lethal; miR-6992-5p, miR-704 in lethal) targeting MAPK, mTOR, Th-17 differentiation, and TGF-β pathways suggest miRNA-mediated mechanisms underlying disease severity
articles.title_original: Differential expression of miRNAs dictates the susceptibility/resistance during malaria pathogenesis.

articles.headline: Analysis of 564 P. falciparum samples from Mali (2019–2020) using next-generation sequencing revealed high prevalence of Pfdhfr 51I-59R-108N haplotype (>93%) and Pfdhps 437G (~60%); the combined Pfdhfr/Pfdhps IRN/IAGKAS and IRN/VAGKGS haplotypes appeared at 9.6% and 1.2% in 2020, while Pfcrt wild-type increased significantly from 39% to 52% (P=0.01). Continued surveillance of emerging resistance markers including IRNI/IAGKAS quintuple mutant and PfK13-Y493H variants is essential for SMC efficacy
articles.title_original: Frequencies of molecular markers of drug resistance in the context of two different Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention (SMC) treatment regimens in the Koulikoro health district, Mali.

articles.headline: In vitro study demonstrates RSL3 synergizes with Pyrimethamine and Cycloguanil (DHFR inhibitors) to suppress P. falciparum proliferation in RBCs; this synergy is specific to these DHFR agents and does not extend to other inhibitors or ferroptosis-inducing compounds. RSL3 and related compounds warrant evaluation as adjuvants to overcome Pyrimethamine resistance and enhance antimalarial efficacy
articles.title_original: Synergistic activity of RSL3 and Pyrimethamine to inhibit the proliferation of Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Review addressing malaria resurgence in Africa, confronting epidemiological and control challenges on the continent
articles.title_original: Malaria resurgence in Africa: confronting the challenges.

articles.headline: Geospatial analysis of malaria incidence data (2011–2019) across Northeast India identified significant persistent hotspots concentrated in Meghalaya, Mizoram, and Tripura, despite overall incidence declining across the region. Findings provide a targeted framework for deploying control resources to high-burden districts toward elimination
articles.title_original: Identification of malaria vulnerable zones in North East Region of India: Spatiotemporal assessment through geographical information system and self-organizing maps.

articles.headline: In a murine P. berghei ANKA cerebral malaria model, genetic ablation of Gsdme or pharmacological inhibition with mannose significantly attenuated brain inflammation and neuronal injury. GSDME-dependent pyroptosis in astrocytes, triggered by brain-infiltrated CD8+ T cells, critically exacerbates neuroinflammation, establishing GSDME as a therapeutic target for cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: GSDME-dependent astrocyte pyroptosis promotes the progression of neuroinflammation in experimental cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: WHO-based standard operating protocol emphasizing parasitological confirmation (peripheral smear or rapid diagnostic test) before treatment initiation; oral artemisinin-based combination therapy for uncomplicated malaria and intravenous artesunate for severe cases including pregnant women and children. Single-dose gametocytocidal primaquine does not require G6PD testing, but testing is essential for radical cure with primaquine or tafenoquine
articles.title_original: Standard operating protocol for malaria management.

articles.headline: Three dichloromethane extracts of Wrightia spp. showed significant antiplasmodial activity: W. dubia wood (IC50 1.89 μg/mL), W. dubia twig (6.38 μg/mL), and W. pubescens twig (1.79 μg/mL); the isolated compound tryptanthrin demonstrated IC50 values of 2.50 μM (P. falciparum 3D7) and 3.14 μM (Dd2 strain). All active extracts and compounds were non-toxic to hepatocytes and non-hemolytic, supporting tryptanthrin as a lead candidate for antimalarial development
articles.title_original: Phytochemical analysis and evaluation of antiplasmodial and cytotoxic activities of Wrightia dubia and Wrightia pubescens traditionally used for malaria treatment in Southeast Asia.

articles.headline: Retrospective study of 24 imported malaria patients presenting to a German university ED (2019–2023): 22 infections with P. falciparum, 1 P. ovale, 1 P. vivax; 19 uncomplicated and 5 severe cases; 20 patients admitted for inpatient care. Zero fatalities occurred during hospitalization, demonstrating the critical role of emergency departments in malaria diagnosis and treatment
articles.title_original: The role of emergency departments in the treatment of imported severe and uncomplicated malaria - a retrospective monocentric observational study in a German university hospital.

articles.headline: In a P. berghei ANKA cerebral malaria model, CD47 knockout mice showed significant resistance to experimental cerebral malaria (log-rank P<0.001) with diminished sequestration of NK, CD4+, and CD8+ T cells in the brain and lower circulating IL-10 and MIP1β. CD47 is critical for maturation, activation, and Ly49h expression in brain-sequestered NK cells, establishing its role in NK-mediated pathogenesis
articles.title_original: CD47-mediated selective accumulation of mature Ly49h+ NK cells to the brain associates with the pathogenesis of cerebral malaria in mice.

articles.headline: Review of pediatric cerebral malaria pathogenesis identifies blood-brain barrier impairment from hypoxemia and sequestration of infected erythrocytes as initial trigger, followed by oxidative stress, TNF-α, reactive oxygen species, and CD8+ T cell-mediated neuronal injury. Acute kidney injury and hyperuricemia worsen outcomes; emerging neuroimaging and electrophysiology studies show promise for identifying interventions
articles.title_original: Pathogenesis of brain injury in cerebral malaria: emerging mechanisms and implications for intervention.

articles.headline: In P. berghei-infected BALB/c mice, combined p-cymene (5–10 mg/kg) plus quinine (10 mg/kg) achieved 100% survival rate and 100% parasite growth suppression (P<0.001), with marked reductions in malondialdehyde and nitric oxide, >4-fold upregulation of SOD and glutathione peroxidase, and reduced TNF-α and IL-1β expression. Combination therapy demonstrates significant efficacy through oxidative stress modulation and anti-inflammatory effects, warranting clinical validation
articles.title_original: Therapeutic Effects of Isopropyltoluene (p-Cymene) Alone and in Combination with Quinine Against Malaria Infection Through Modulation of Inflammation and Oxidative Stress.

articles.headline: A 7-day malaria-on-a-chip model incorporating human liver, spleen, and endothelium with P. falciparum-infected blood sustained all intraerythrocytic life stages. Dose-dependent clearance occurred with chloroquine, lumefantrine, and artesunate in both 3D7 and W2 strains; recrudescence appeared in 3D7 with chloroquine/lumefantrine but not artesunate, while W2 showed parasitemia stabilization or further reduction. PK/PD modeling predicted human in vivo efficacy parameters from in vitro results
articles.title_original: Translation of a Human-Based Malaria-on-a-Chip Phenotypic Disease Model for In Vivo Applications.

articles.headline: In vitro characterization of three P. falciparum small heat shock proteins (PfHsp20a, PfHsp20b, PfHsp20c) demonstrated autonomous chaperone activity in all isoforms. The plant-derived flavonoid quercetin inhibited parasite growth with IC50 values of 5.4 μM (Nf54 strain) and 7.8 μM (Dd2 strain) and disrupted sHsp structure and function in a concentration-dependent manner, with PfHsp20c most sensitive, establishing these proteins as potential antimalarial drug targets
articles.title_original: Comparative Characterization of Plasmodium falciparum Small Heat Shock Proteins and Their Inhibition by Quercetin (3,3',4',5,7-Pentahydroxyflavone).

articles.headline: Review of exosomes (small extracellular vesicles) in malaria pathogenesis, describing their roles in host-pathogen interactions and immune modulation, their function as biomarkers for disease diagnosis and prognosis, and the application of 3D cell culture and in vivo models to elucidating exosome-mediated mechanisms, advocating for exosome-based therapeutic interventions in malaria
articles.title_original: Extracellular vesicles in malaria pathogenesis and Therapy: Emerging insights and future perspectives.

articles.headline: Perspective article proposing the development of a standardized clinical definition for severe malaria in travelers to improve diagnostic precision and clinical management
articles.title_original: Severe malaria in travellers-time to create a specific definition.
articles.authors: Barkati S, Libman M, Schwartz E

articles.headline: Systematic review of 22 studies (2016–April 2025) using genomics, transcriptomics, and multi-omics in sub-Saharan African Anopheles vectors identified insecticide resistance mechanisms: metabolic resistance, target-site mutations, and cuticular changes, plus novel resistance markers (CYP450 reductase, UDP-glycosyltransferases, salivary gland proteins), supporting routine integration of omics approaches into malaria vector surveillance
articles.title_original: Impact of Omics Studies on Understanding Insecticide Resistance Mechanisms in Sub-Saharan Malaria Vectors: A Systematic Review.

articles.headline: In P. falciparum blood-stage parasites, conditional depletion of PfAnchor, an essential protein regulating apicoplast fission and inheritance, disrupted organellar division causing incomplete cytokinesis and parasite death. PfAnchor was identified as the first apicoplast-specific adaptor protein interacting with dynamin-like GTPase PfDyn2, revealing a parasite-specific organelle division pathway as a therapeutic vulnerability
articles.title_original: An Essential Adaptor for Apicoplast Fission and Inheritance in Malaria Parasites.

articles.headline: Case report of a returned traveler from India with concurrent dengue and Plasmodium vivax co-infection, with clinical features suggestive of dengue triggering P. vivax relapse, highlighting the underestimation of dengue-malaria co-infections due to limited diagnostic approaches using rapid diagnostic tests
articles.title_original: A Case of Intercurrent Dengue and Probable Relapsing Plasmodium vivax Malaria in a Returned Traveler to India: Case Report and Literature Review.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional analysis in six sub-Saharan African countries showed district-level malaria prevalence among first antenatal care (ANC1) attendees strongly correlated with prevalence among children (Spearman r=0.79, 95% CI 0.65–0.88, p<0.001) and predicted childhood prevalence (mean absolute error 6.5%), while ANC1-derived insecticide-treated net ownership also correlated well with community estimates, suggesting ANC1 surveillance is a practical alternative to household surveys for monitoring malaria prevalence and net coverage
articles.title_original: Antenatal care surveillance for monitoring malaria prevalence and intervention coverage: a multicountry analysis.

articles.headline: Semi-field study of female Anopheles gambiae sensu lato showed nutritional deprivation drives behavioral plasticity in host-seeking: mosquitoes on low-nutrient diet (0.5% sucrose) were 2–3-fold more likely to seek hosts overall and 5–10-fold more likely to seek hosts at earlier times of day compared to well-resourced mosquitoes (10% sucrose ± blood meal), suggesting nutritional status is an underappreciated driver of residual malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Biting time of day in malaria mosquitoes is modulated by nutritional status.

articles.headline: Among 96 P. falciparum isolates from rural Angola, deletions in pfhrp2 and pfhrp3 exons 1-2 occurred in 7.29% and 11.46% of samples respectively, with no exon 2 deletions; PfHRP2 epitopes 3A4 and C1-13 were present in 100% of samples at high frequency per isolate (15 and 18 repeats respectively), indicating low overall deletion prevalence and supporting continued use of PfHRP2-RDTs as suitable diagnostic tools in this region
articles.title_original: Analysis of pfhrp2 and pfhrp3 gene deletions, and the structure and variability of PfHRP2 and PfHRP3 proteins: implications for the performance of malaria rapid diagnostic tests in Cubal, Angola.

articles.headline: Community survey of 8,166 rural residents across three Tanzanian regions (2021–2023) found overall Plasmodium prevalence of 23.2% with significant temporal variation (22.9% in 2021, 20.6% in 2022, 26.9% in 2023, p<0.001). Prevalence was highest in school-aged children (5–<15 years) and males; among individuals with fever history, prevalence increased from 40.1% (2021) to 58.6% (2023), with elevated risk in non-bed-net users and those in traditional houses or low socioeconomic status
articles.title_original: Temporal and spatial trends of the prevalence of infections caused by Plasmodium parasites among rural community members in three regions with varying transmission intensities in Mainland Tanzania.

articles.headline: Qualitative study of 142 stakeholders in Cambodia found high acceptance of shorter P. vivax radical cure regimens (7-day-high-dose primaquine and single-dose tafenoquine) among policymakers, healthcare providers, and patients, with stakeholders emphasizing reduced treatment burden and improved effectiveness while patients valued faster recovery and earlier return to work, though continued monitoring of adverse events was recommended
articles.title_original: Perceptions of shorter radical cure Plasmodium vivax treatment regimens and their implementation: a qualitative study among stakeholders in Cambodia.

articles.headline: Analysis of Demographic and Health Survey data from 19 sub-Saharan African countries covering 126,424 households found malaria prevalence of 22.47% (95% CI 22.24%–22.70%) with significant geographic clustering (Global Moran's I=0.159, p<0.001) and 34 statistically significant spatial clusters concentrated in Benin, Burkina Faso, Togo, Uganda, Rwanda, Republic of Congo, and Mozambique. Risk factors included rural residence, poor housing, lack of bed nets, and lower socioeconomic status
articles.title_original: Malaria prevalence and its determinants across 19 sub-Saharan African countries: a spatial and geographically weighted regression analysis.

articles.headline: Among 402 at-risk individuals in Sisaket Province (Thailand-Cambodia border) in 2022, 51.5% had good malaria knowledge, 64.7% positive attitudes, and 58.2% good practices. Poor attitudes were associated with primary education or lower (aOR 2.2–2.5) and low socioeconomic status (aOR 1.8). Risk behaviours were significantly more frequent in males (aOR 2.8) and those with poor practices (aOR 3.5). Targeted behaviour change communication is needed to address gaps in attitudes among low-education groups and risk behaviours in males
articles.title_original: Estimating malaria risk behaviours and their determinants among at-risk populations in a pre-elimination setting, Sisaket Province, Thailand-Cambodia border.

articles.headline: Among 1,644 pregnant women at first antenatal visits in rural western Uganda (Feb 2021–Sep 2022), 225 (13.5%) tested positive for P. falciparum malaria by RDT. Younger, primigravid women were at highest risk. Qualitative findings identified barriers: delayed pregnancy testing, chemoprevention hesitancy, financial/transportation constraints, low bed net use, knowledge gaps, and spouse attitudes. Further decentralization of MiP prevention services into communities is recommended
articles.title_original: Barriers to malaria prevention services in pregnancy: a multi-methods study in rural western Uganda.

articles.headline: The HealthPulse AI smartphone application interpreted 106,877 malaria RDT images across Benin, Côte d'Ivoire, Nigeria, and Uganda with 96.8% accuracy (95% CI 96.7–96.9%) and F1 score of 96.6 compared to trained human panel. Recall and precision exceeded 97% for positive/negative outcomes but were lower for invalid tests (recall 84.8%, precision 42.8%). Performance declined significantly when test lines were faint (aOR 0.02 for positive, 0.10 for negative). The tool shows promise for surveillance and quality assurance with refinements needed
articles.title_original: Evaluating the performance of an artificial intelligence-based electronic reader for malaria rapid diagnostic tests across Benin, Côte d'Ivoire, Nigeria and Uganda.

articles.headline: A randomized controlled trial in rural Tanzania compared mosquito sound recording participation with (n incentive group) and without (n control group) weekly airtime incentives using the MozzWear acoustic application over four months. Control participants performed as well or better in terms of nights recording and uploading data, indicating intrinsic motivation. Participants expressed high interest in ongoing mosquito surveillance, demonstrating that community engagement does not depend on financial incentives
articles.title_original: "Fighting against malaria is everyone's concern": a randomized control trial assessing the role of incentives for encouraging local communities to record and upload mosquito sounds using the MozzWear application.

articles.headline: In Accra, Ghana, larval surveys identified 727 potential Anopheles habitats in 2022 (dry and rainy seasons); 65.3% (475/727) contained larvae. Drainage ditches were most common (48.2%). An. gambiae s.l. abundance peaked in irrigated urban farming sites (27.2% of all larvae), especially during rainy season (77.0%, R² = 3.46, p = 0.000). Polluted habitats had higher ammonium (3.4 vs 1.3 mg/L), conductivity (5106 vs 2049 µS/cm), and TDS (3181 vs 1255 mg/L). An. coluzzii dominated (54.4%); An. stephensi was newly detected
articles.title_original: Larval habitat diversity, physicochemical characteristics and their effect on the larval density of malaria vectors in the city of Accra, Ghana.

articles.headline: A cost-effective nanopore sequencing method resolved CYP2D6 allele diversity in 90 Thai individuals from the Greater Mekong Subregion, achieving high-resolution genotyping and identifying complex structural variants and one previously unknown allele. This approach enables sensitive, locally adapted CYP2D6 testing to identify patients suitable for primaquine therapy, supporting P. vivax radical cure and elimination efforts through improved patient stratification
articles.title_original: Enhancing CYP2D6 genotyping with nanopore sequencing to address allele diversity P. vivax malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Among 220 pregnant women in Cameroon, those with EBV reactivation and severe malaria showed significantly lower IL-6 (29.86 vs 55.83 pg/mL, p = 0.02) and significantly elevated TGF-β (17.6 vs 6.1 pg/mL, p = 0.01) compared to those with latent EBV and severe malaria. A strong negative correlation between IL-10 and parasitemia was observed in reactivated-EBV women (r = −0.976, p = 0.0004). EBV reactivation may impair inflammatory response, exacerbating P. falciparum malaria severity
articles.title_original: Impact of epstein-barr virus reactivation on cytokine levels in pregnant women with malaria in the west region of cameroon.

articles.headline: Age-associated differences in malaria inflammatory responses were demonstrated in non-immune children and adults. During malaria, adults showed increased CCL2, CCL3, CXCL8, CXCL9, CRP, and IDO correlating with symptoms. In vitro, adult classical monocytes and Vδ2+ γδ T cells produced more inflammatory cytokines post-parasite stimulation; adult monocytes were dominated by CCL2 while children showed elevated IL-10 and IL-10 signaling. These age-dependent mechanisms explain higher malaria severity risk in non-immune adults
articles.title_original: Age is an intrinsic driver of inflammatory responses to malaria.

articles.headline: Antibody responses to 21 P. falciparum proteins were compared in malaria-naive (n=22), PfSPZ-CVac-vaccinated (n=27), and semi-immune African (n=20) volunteers after controlled human malaria infection (3,200 PfSPZ sporozoites). Vaccinated individuals had higher IgM and IgG to CSP and SSP-2/TRAP; semi-immune Africans mounted broader blood-stage responses, longer pre-patent periods, and fewer symptoms. Cytophilic IgG1 and IgG3 associated with longer parasitemia control; noncytophilic IgG2/IgG4 elevation in semi-immune individuals may reflect regulatory responses limiting vaccine efficacy
articles.title_original: IgM, IgG, and IgG Subclass Antibody Responses to Plasmodium falciparum Proteins in Naïve, Malaria-Vaccinated and Semi-Immune Volunteers after Controlled Human Malaria Infection.

articles.headline: In 322 Vienna psychiatric patients treated for schizophrenia (n=147) or neurosyphilis (n=175) with iatrogenic malaria fever therapy (1951–1969), parasitological analysis revealed first fever peaks ~7 days post-inoculation (4–8 mL blood, typically intravenous). Temperature increased over successive paroxysms while afebrile intervals shortened from 41 to 31 hours. After mean 5–6 fever peaks, standard quinine monotherapy or combination therapy was administered. These data establish P. vivax extra-hepatic incubation period at ~7 days
articles.title_original: Clinical and Parasitological Characteristics of Plasmodium vivax Malaria in Malaria-Naïve Patients: A Review of Malaria Fever Therapy in Patients with Schizophrenia and Neurosyphilis during the 1950s and 1960s in Vienna, Austria.

articles.headline: BiteBarrier transfluthrin spatial emanators were tested over 8 weeks against pyrethroid-susceptible An. gambiae s.s. and Ae. aegypti and resistant An. funestus and Cx. quinquefasciatus in Tanzania semi-field trials. Indoor protective efficacy was >93% (95% CI 92–93%), outdoor 80% (95% CI 78–81%), with indoor mortality 47% (95% CI 43–53%) and outdoor 26% (95% CI 22–30%), regardless of species or resistance status. Feeding-based efficacy estimates were more robust than landing rates
articles.title_original: The BiteBarrier transfluthrin emanator demonstrates significant protection against susceptible and resistant malaria and arbovirus vectors in semi-field trials in Tanzania.

articles.headline: Screening of the ReFRAME library and epigenetic inhibitors identified selective activity against P. vivax and P. cynomolgi hypnozoites, particularly DNA methyltransferase inhibitors and histone post-translational modification inhibitors. Bisulfite sequencing revealed DNA methylation in sporozoites across most coding genes, with promoter/first-exon methylation affecting gene expression. The acetyl-CoA synthetase inhibitor MMV019721 validated the importance of epigenetic targeting for hypnozoite persistence, offering new radical cure antimalarial development pathways
articles.title_original: A drug repurposing approach reveals targetable epigenetic pathways in Plasmodium vivax hypnozoites.

articles.headline: Detailed visualization of Microsporidia MB sporogeny identified octosporogony in mosquito oocytes and spores with characteristic microsporidian structures, establishing localization to germline tissues with both vertical and potential horizontal transmission routes; these findings advance Microsporidia MB's feasibility as a transmission-blocking malaria control tool
articles.title_original: Discovery of sporogeny in the malaria transmission-blocking symbiont of Anopheles mosquitoes.

articles.headline: Deep amplicon sequencing of 89 P. falciparum isolates from Senegal identified 26 non-synonymous RIPR SNPs (15 novel; 71.9% prevalence); computational modeling predicted 7/16 would destabilize and 2/16 increase RIPR stability; three SNPs (Q737K, T738K, V840L) located in EGF5-8 domains where neutralizing antibodies bind
articles.title_original: Genetic Diversity of the Malaria Vaccine Candidate PfRIPR in a High Transmission Region of Senegal.

articles.headline: Qualitative study of 76 Tanzanian stakeholders identified decreasing donor funding as major constraint for malaria, HIV/AIDS, and TB services; proposed domestic strategies included universal health insurance, reduced service exemptions, and product taxation, though implementation faces barriers of poor awareness, conflicting political promises, and accountability issues
articles.title_original: Stakeholders' perspectives on funding malaria, HIV/AIDS and tuberculosis services in Tanzania through domestic resources mobilisation: a qualitative study.

articles.headline: SERVAL is a cluster-randomized trial (30 villages in The Gambia, 24 in Burkina Faso, 1:1 randomization) testing seasonal mass R21/Matrix-M vaccination in low-to-moderate and intense transmission settings; vaccinations May–July 2024, booster June 2025; primary outcome malaria prevalence at peak transmission November 2024 (results pending)
articles.title_original: Seasonal mass vaccination with R21/Matrix-M for malaria elimination (SERVAL): protocol of the cluster randomised trial.

articles.headline: Correction to prior publication regarding human-to-Anopheles dirus transmission of Plasmodium knowlesi (anthropozoonotic malaria)
articles.title_original: Correction: Human-to-Anopheles dirus mosquito transmission of the anthropozoonotic malaria parasite, Plasmodium knowlesi.

articles.headline: Among 331 pregnant women in Ethiopia (April–June 2023), malaria prevalence 8.8% (P. vivax 4.8%, P. falciparum 4.0%), soil-transmitted helminth 18.1% (predominantly Ascaris 13.6%), co-infection 5.7%; no insecticide-treated net use increased malaria risk 3.67-fold, eating soil 9.45-fold for co-infection
articles.title_original: Malaria and soil transmitted helminth infection and associated factors among pregnant women attending public health facilities in Dilla town, South Ethiopia: cross sectional study design.

articles.headline: CHAT trial (341 communities, Burkina Faso) found twice-yearly azithromycin reduced child mortality RR 0.77 (95% CI 0.60–0.98) during seasonal malaria chemoprevention season versus RR 0.89 (95% CI 0.68–1.15) during non-SMC season; stronger effect in <80% SMC coverage clusters (RR 0.73, 95% CI 0.56–0.96) than ≥80% (RR 1.0)
articles.title_original: The role of seasonal malaria chemoprevention in the effect of azithromycin on child mortality: A secondary analysis of the CHAT cluster randomized clinical trial.

articles.headline: YOLOv3 with modified MobileNetv2 backbone for P. vivax detection in blood smears achieved accuracy 1.00, precision 0.98, recall 0.98, F1 score 0.97, mean average precision 0.90; provides superior automated diagnostic performance versus traditional microscopy
articles.title_original: Leveraging MobileNetV2 and deep learning innovation for high accuracy Plasmodium Vivax detection in blood smears.
articles.authors: Prathap VM, Yadav S, Qidwai T

articles.headline: In P. berghei-infected/diabetic mice, Alstonia boonei extract at 80 mg/kg dose-dependently reduced parasitemia and blood glucose, enhanced antioxidant enzymes (SOD, catalase, GSH), restored hematologic parameters, reduced C-reactive protein, elevated interleukin-10, with effects comparable to chloroquine-metformin
articles.title_original: Alstonia boonei stem bark aqueous extract ameliorates elevated parasitemia levels, normalises blood glucose, modulates inflammatory biomarkers and enhances antioxidant status in Plasmodium berghei-infected/diabetic mice.
articles.authors: Agunloye OM, Akintoye OF, Oboh G

articles.headline: Survey of 484 residents in Béré, Chad identified drinking water management (p<0.001), malaria factors (p<0.001), and sanitation/hygiene practices (p<0.001) as significant predictors of diarrhea in both adults and children; integrated interventions addressing these factors recommended
articles.title_original: Association of Diarrhea Outcomes with Drinking Water Factors, Sanitation, Hygiene, and Malaria Practices in the Population of Béré, Chad.
articles.journal: Int J Environ Res Public Health

articles.headline: Case report: 4-year-old unvaccinated Cameroonian patient with concurrent tetanus, cerebral malaria, and probable bacterial meningitis treated empirically with anti-tetanus toxoid, antibiotics, and antimalarial showed marked improvement within 48 hours in ICU, discharged after 5 additional hospital days
articles.title_original: Generalized Tetanus Associated With Malaria and Probable Meningitis in a 4-Year-Old Patient: A Case Report in Limbe Regional Hospital, Cameroon.

articles.headline: In controlled human P. falciparum infection, lifelong but not vaccine exposure associated with increased B cell subset frequencies; vaccination versus placebo showed positive associations with resting classical MBCs (β=0.190, 95% CI 0.011–0.368) and active naive-PD1+ cells (β=0.637, 95% CI 0.058–1.217)
articles.title_original: Effect of controlled human Plasmodium falciparum infection on B cell subsets in individuals with different levels of malaria immunity.

articles.headline: 589 children <5 years with severe malaria at PHCs (N=275) vs community health workers (N=314) in Nigeria showed PHCs treated sicker children (50% ≥4 danger signs vs 39%, p=0.02; 90% CNS involvement vs 74%, p<0.01) despite higher case fatality (19% vs 6%), indicating severity-driven differences in patient selection
articles.title_original: Treatment-Seeking for Children with Suspected Severe Malaria Attending Community Health Workers and Primary Health Centres in Adamawa State, Nigeria.

articles.headline: Malaria transmission suitability in Papua New Guinea expanded due to climate change: population proportion living in suitable areas increased from 58% (1960) to 61% (2019), projected to reach 74% by 2040 (additional 2.8M people), requiring malaria control access to double to 13.4 million
articles.title_original: Climate Change Is Expected to Expand Malaria Transmission Range and Population at Risk in Papua New Guinea.

articles.headline: Among 38,459 Ghanaian women aged 15-49, malaria vaccine awareness was higher with tertiary education (AOR=2.89), internet use (AOR=1.85), Greater Accra residence (AOR=1.75), and age 45-49 (AOR=2.03), showing education, digital connectivity, and geography shape awareness and require targeted campaigns
articles.title_original: Determinants of Malaria Vaccine Awareness Among Women in Ghana: Findings From the 2022 Ghana Malaria Indicator Survey.

articles.headline: Umbrella review of 6 systematic reviews found 10.10% pooled malaria prevalence in vulnerable Ethiopian populations, with non-use of insecticide-treated nets (OR=5.22) and proximity to stagnant water (OR=4.21) driving increased risk, establishing ITN use and environmental management as intervention priorities
articles.title_original: Burden of malaria infection among most vulnerable populations in Ethiopia: an umbrella review of systematic reviews and meta-analyses.

articles.headline: CYP6P9a_R allele conferring pyrethroid resistance in Anopheles funestus causes cross-resistance to Sherlock (mitochondrial complex I inhibitor): carriers had 5-18.6-fold higher survival odds in bioassays (p<0.0001) with elevated CYP6P9a expression, showing metabolic resistance threatens new insecticide classes
articles.title_original: The duplicated cytochrome P450 CYP6P9a/b confers cross-resistance to a mitochondrial complex I inhibitor in the African malaria vector Anopheles funestus.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of treatment delay among under-five children in Horn of Africa found 48% prevalence; major risk factors: expensiveness (OR=4.39), drug side effects (OR=2.94), distance >3 km (OR=2.59); protective factors: middle-class income (OR=0.42) and formal education (OR=0.69)
articles.title_original: Prevalence and associated factors of delay in seeking malaria treatment among under five children in the Horn of Africa: A systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Household vegetation in Burkina Faso increased malaria transmission: associated with vector abundance (RR=2.5), infected mosquitoes (RR=2.5), and clinical cases (RR=1.4); children <5 years (RR=1.52) and female householders (RR=1.23) also increased case risk, suggesting environmental management complements drug interventions
articles.title_original: Housing and household characteristics associated with malaria vectors abundance and clinical malaria incidence in a semi-urban and a rural area of Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: Plasmodium infection alters mouse skin flora and volatiles; elevated ethylbenzene increased female Anopheles attractiveness, suggesting parasite-induced chemical and microbial changes enhance vector host-seeking and transmission potential
articles.title_original: [Skin flora, odor and malaria transmission].
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi

articles.headline: Using erythroid cell lines (JK-1, BEL-A) as P. vivax reticulocyte surrogates, proteomics and proximity labeling identified transferrin receptor 1, basigin, and prohibitin-2 (novel) as invasion receptors, clarifying reticulocyte restriction mechanisms and enabling antimalarial drug development
articles.title_original: The reticulocyte restriction: invasion ligand RBP1a of Plasmodium vivax targets human TfR1, prohibitin-2, and basigin.

articles.headline: Conditional Plasmodium ARK1 (aurora kinase) depletion disrupts mitotic spindle formation, kinetochore segregation, and transmission; interactome revealed ARK1 forms a non-canonical chromosomal passenger complex, identifying ARK1-INCENP as a multistage antimalarial target
articles.title_original: Plasmodium ARK1 regulates spindle formation during atypical mitosis and forms a divergent chromosomal passenger complex.

articles.headline: In 90 Sudanese malaria patients, TNF-α, IFN-γ, and TGF-β1 positively associated with parasite density (r=0.38-0.51; P<0.01); IL-10 elevated in P. falciparum infections (560.0 vs P. vivax 415.6 pg/mL; P=0.019), indicating distinct cytokine signatures by parasite species and burden
articles.title_original: Assessment of Cytokines TNF-alpha, IFN-gamma, TGF-beta-1, and IL-10 in Malaria Patients of the River Nile State , Sudan: A critical study of immune response.

articles.headline: Repeat controlled human malaria infection (CHMI) showed rapid clinical immunity to P. vivax after primary infection (N=19) protecting against fever through attenuated inflammatory response, but without anti-parasite immunity and with no protection against P. falciparum heterologous challenge
articles.title_original: Development of clinical immunity to Plasmodium vivax following repeat controlled human malaria infection.

articles.headline: Qualitative district assessment in Uganda's Elgon region (10 districts) identified malaria incidence rising from 206 to 271 cases per 1,000 population (2012-2022). Major prevention gaps: 70% inadequate insecticide-treated nets, 30% no indoor residual spraying, 80% limited/no rapid diagnostic tests, mean antimalarial resupply 117±9 days. Key themes: lapsed vector control, delayed logistical support, limited chemotherapeutics
articles.title_original: From Endemic to Epidemic: A Qualitative District-Level Assessment of an Increase in Malaria Cases in the Elgon Region of Uganda.

articles.headline: Review proposing standardized minimal and optimal data reporting for malaria molecular surveillance to track artemisinin partial resistance and Plasmodium falciparum pfhrp2/3 deletions, emphasizing individual infection-level polymorphism data, local regulatory adherence, and data governance to prevent colonialism and maximize reusability across borders
articles.title_original: Toward Setting Minimum and Optimal Data to Report for Malaria Molecular Surveillance with Targeted Sequencing: The "What" and "Why".

articles.headline: Causal machine learning analysis of 100 Colombian municipalities (2007-2023) revealed malaria transmission optimal at 15-23.5°C. Average treatment effect on excess malaria cases: -0.007 (0.5°C temperature increase) to -0.063 (2.0°C increase), indicating higher warming associated with greater reduction in excess malaria probability in warm regions
articles.title_original: Stochastic treatment regimes in climate-health research: Reassessing malaria risk under warming scenarios in Colombia.

articles.headline: Mitragyna inermis methanolic and ethanol/water extracts showed potent activity against Plasmodium falciparum Dd2 and 3D7 (IC₅₀ <3 μg/mL, selectivity index >10). Ellagic acid demonstrated cross-activity against multidrug-resistant strains PfDd2, PfINDO, PfW2, PfK1 and sensitive Pf3D7 (IC₅₀ 0.84-1.62 μg/mL; 2.77-5.36 μM) with favorable ADME profile
articles.title_original: A bio-guided investigation of Mitragyna inermis (Willd) O. Kuntze (Rubiaceae) unveils natural product isolates with potent cross-activity against sensitive and multidrug resistant Plasmodium falciparum strains in vitro.

articles.headline: In 64 male mice infected with Plasmodium berghei NK65 (chloroquine-sensitive), bitter leaf and Siam weed aqueous extracts (100-400 mg/kg, 5 days) significantly suppressed parasitemia and reduced testicular oxidative stress (p<0.05) with dose-dependent reductions in ROS and TBARS and increased antioxidant levels compared to untreated infection
articles.title_original: Bitter leaf (Vernonia amygdalina) and siam weed (Chromolaena odorata) aqueous extracts alleviate testicular damage induced by Plasmodium berghei in male mice via modulation of oxidative stress pathways.

articles.headline: Whole-genome sequencing of wild Anopheles minimus females (504/1,997 samples, 25%) from Thailand revealed host-seeking behavior associated with 2,629-4,369 significant non-synonymous SNPs (FDR p<0.001) in olfactory pathway genes (AMIN001807, AMIN001339, AMIN003886, AMIN000912, AMIN003926, AMIN011060, AMIN002342, AMIN015480), demonstrating genetic basis for behavioral variation in this primary malaria vector
articles.title_original: Genetic variation in olfactory pathways associated with host-seeking behavior in natural populations of Anopheles minimus, a primary malaria vector in western Thailand.

articles.headline: Time series modeling of Uganda malaria incidence (2020-2023) revealed cases increased from 200.5 to 265.4 per 1,000 persons annually (2021-2022), with West Nile, Acholi, and Busoga regions highest burden. ARIMA outperformed FB-Prophet (regional mean MAE 0.007 vs 0.01, MAPE 31.2% vs 47.8%, RMSE 0.01 both) in 14 of 15 regions, providing accurate national and regional prediction for resource allocation
articles.title_original: Comparison of ARIMA and FB-Prophet time series models for the prediction of national and regional malaria incidence in Uganda.

articles.headline: Review of human genetic variations conferring malaria resistance including hemoglobinopathies (sickle cell trait, thalassemia), glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency, blood group polymorphisms, and inflammation/immune variants. Geographic prevalence reflects historical malaria burden; precise protective mechanisms remain incompletely understood, requiring comprehensive understanding for improved epidemiology and targeted prevention strategies
articles.title_original: Human genetic variations conferring resistance to malaria.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 30 studies with 30,065 Anopheles sinensis showed pooled insecticide mortality 61% [95% CI 53-68]. Knockdown resistance (kdr) frequency 36%, acetylcholinesterase-1 (ace-1) frequency 78%. Mortality varied by insecticide: DDT 49%, deltamethrin 47%, malathion 81%, propoxur 69%, permethrin 61%, beta-cyfluthrin 28%, fenitrothion 82%, beta-cypermethrin 48%, cyfluthrin 59%, lambda-cyhalothrin 56%
articles.title_original: Insecticide resistance and the role of target-site insensitivity mutations among malaria vectors in China: A systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional analysis of 2021 Nigeria Malaria Indicator Survey (5,021 households; 9,546 women 15-49y; 893 treatment cases) found insecticide-treated net ownership 97.7% but use only 70.3%, with low-income households 3.6× higher odds of ITN use (95% CI 3.07-4.29). Education associated with prevention awareness (p<0.001) but inversely with ITN use; media exposure did not significantly influence treatment-seeking behavior
articles.title_original: Socioeconomic and educational influences on malaria prevention and treatment behaviours in rural Nigeria.

articles.headline: Vaccine immunogenicity study with Plasmodium vivax Duffy binding protein showed adjuvants GLA-SE and alum combined with antigen mutations with mutations in immunodominant epitopes overcame subdominance of a defined region; MHCII restriction largely negated these effects. Boosting altered epitope hierarchy and recruited germinal center B cells to subdominant epitopes, informing vaccine design targeting strain-transcending conserved epitopes
articles.title_original: Adjuvants and MHCII modulate the immunogenicity of subdominant epitopes in Plasmodium vivax Duffy binding protein.

articles.headline: Title announces Suriname as the first malaria-free country in the Amazon region; no abstract provided to extract quantitative results or study design details
articles.title_original: Suriname: first malaria-free country in the Amazon region.

articles.headline: Prospective malaria screening of 53,575 blood donors in Burkina Faso (August 2023–August 2024) using Sysmex XN-31 revealed 5.91% prevalence (3164/53,575), with key predictors: age ≤30 years (OR 2.85, p<0.001), male sex (OR 1.47, p<0.001), and rural residency (OR 2.40, p<0.001). Strong seasonal variability paralleled rainfall with a 3-month lag; only males showed striking seasonal gametocyte variability (1.39% low season vs. 4.42% high season, p<0.001). Blood donor screening via XN-31 can provide dynamic real-time malaria surveillance data with granular spatiotemporal resolution
articles.title_original: Blood donors as a sentinel population for real-time malaria surveillance using the Sysmex XN-31: a one-year review from the National Blood Transfusion Centre in Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: In Anopheles gambiae females, REL2 (NF-κB pathway member) regulates antimicrobial peptide expression and structures midgut bacterial communities post-blood meal. REL2 loss causes Serratia-dominated dysbiosis and mosquito lethality; however, Serratia overgrowth paradoxically reduces Plasmodium falciparum prevalence. These findings reveal REL2's critical role in maintaining midgut bacterial homeostasis and demonstrate complex interactions between mosquito immunity, microbiota composition, and malaria parasite dynamics
articles.title_original: The major role of the REL2/NF-κB pathway in the regulation of midgut bacterial homeostasis in the malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.

articles.headline: Whole genome assembly of female Anopheles stephensi (a primary malaria vector in Asia) yielded a 201 Mbp genome consisting of 32,280 contigs with N50 of 21.1 kb and GC content of 45%
articles.title_original: Whole genome Shotgun Sequence of Anopheles stephensi, The Host of Malaria parasite, Plasmodium sp.

articles.headline: Editorial discussing mechanisms driving drug resistance in tuberculosis and malaria, addressing genetic, environmental, and evolutionary insights
articles.title_original: Editorial: Mechanisms driving drug resistance in tuberculosis and malaria: genetic, environmental, and evolutionary insights.

articles.headline: Peripheral blood film of an 18-year-old male revealed rare dual infection: Plasmodium vivax (trophozoites, schizonts, and exflagellated microgametes) and P. falciparum (trophozoites). Exflagellation occurring in human peripheral blood during parasitic development is highly unusual; correct identification is critical for appropriate treatment and preventing transmission
articles.title_original: Exflagellation of Plasmodium vivax and Dual Infection Diagnosed on Peripheral Blood Film: A Rare Case Report.

articles.headline: Genetic analysis of Plasmodium vivax from five Mexican malaria foci using circumsporozoite gene (PCR-RFLP), pvs25 mutations, and 18S rRNA variants revealed VK210 parasites had higher haplotype diversity (0.860) than VK247 (0.198), with multilocus haplotype frequencies differing significantly among foci (p<0.001). AMOVA and FST analysis indicated a structured P. vivax population, with the Lacandon region harboring the broadest reservoir of haplotypes and the northwestern region showing high parasite adaptation, presenting challenges for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Population Dynamics of Plasmodium vivax in Mexico Determined by CSP, Pvs25, and SSU 18S rRNA S-Type Polymorphism Analyses.

articles.headline: Case report of an 18-year-old male who developed acute motor and sensory axonal neuropathy (AMSAN variant of Guillain-Barré Syndrome) following Plasmodium falciparum infection, presenting with progressive bilateral weakness, cranial nerve involvement, and CSF protein elevation to 172 mg/dL. Treated with intravenous artesunate; weakness stabilized by day 14 with significant functional recovery by 2 months, highlighting the rare but serious neurological complication of falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: Guillain-Barré Syndrome Following Falciparum Malaria in an 18-Year Male Adolescent: A Rare Case Report and Literature Review.

articles.headline: Analysis of estimated-to-reported (ERR) malaria cases (2010–2022) showed persistently high gaps in India (ERR >10) and WHO Southeast Asia Region (>6), with microscopy-positivity in contemporary research 3–55 times higher than NMCP-reported figures. Virtually absent private-sector reporting and unclear WHO adjustment assumptions contribute to large case estimation gaps; addressing private-sector data collection and reporting transparency could narrow this disparity
articles.title_original: Time to address the drawbacks in estimation and reporting of malaria cases in India and beyond.
articles.authors: Deora N, Singhal R, Sinha A

articles.headline: Murine model using live Plasmodium yoelii 17XNL immunization followed by P. berghei ANKA challenge demonstrated that immunized mice were protected from cerebral malaria and survived longer, with parasitized erythroblasts (pEBs) appearing in circulation and detectable via Giemsa stains. All blood-stage parasite forms were identified within pEBs. This model enables direct in vivo/ex vivo analysis of pEB biology without bone marrow or spleen isolation
articles.title_original: Visual Detection of Malaria Parasite-Parasitized Erythroblasts in Peripheral Blood via Immunization-Based Model.

articles.headline: Entomological survey in Shiraz, southern Iran (April 2023–January 2024) of 1,249 mosquitoes identified dominant Culicidae species: Culex pipiens (39.0%), Cx. quinquefasciatus (23.6%), Aedes caspius (19.6%), and Anopheles dthali (80.6% of Anophelinae). Blood-meal analysis of 50 blood-fed females showed Anopheles spp. fed on rodents/dogs (41%) and humans (28%), while Cx. pipiens exhibited marked anthropophily (54% human meals). An. sacharovi demonstrated spatial clustering in irrigation-rich northern Shiraz (χ²=12.7, p<0.01), highlighting ongoing malaria transmission risk and importance of molecular vector surveillance
articles.title_original: Culicidae vector ecology in southern Iran: Integrating Faunistics and molecular analysis of blood-feeding preferences to strengthen malaria surveillance in transition zones.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional analysis of 10,352 Nigerian children from the 2018 Demographic and Health Survey (malaria RDT positive) identified significant sociodemographic predictors: child age (χ²=166.200, p=0.000), mother's education (χ²=762.530, p=0.000), and wealth index (χ²=1,020.442, p=0.000). Children in rural areas had 1.63-fold higher malaria infection odds than urban children (p=0.000). Age, wealth, maternal education, and residence are key predictors of malaria in under-five Nigerian children
articles.title_original: Sociodemographic Factors Associated with Malaria Infection among Under-Five Children in Nigeria.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 234 caregivers of under-five children in Kaduna State, Nigeria found 182 (76.8%) had heard of seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) and 156 (65.8%) knew it as preventive, but only 128 (54.0%) correctly identified it as a rainy-season intervention. Uptake was moderate: 137 (58.4%) received ≥1 SMC dose, but only 65 (27.2%) completed all 4 doses; chief barriers were lack of awareness (54.0%) and child absence (20.0%). Education, occupation, ethnicity, religion, and SMC knowledge significantly predicted uptake (p<0.05)
articles.title_original: Knowledge and Uptake of Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention among Caregivers of Under-five Children in a Tertiary Hospital in Kaduna State, North-western Nigeria.
