articles.headline: A patient with glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency presented with mixed Plasmodium falciparum and P. vivax infection confirmed by PCR. Treatment with intravenous artesunate, oral artemether/lumefantrine, and primaquine resulted in clinical improvement
articles.title_original: Navigating Challenges in Mixed Malaria Infections: A Case of Mixed Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax Infection in a Patient With Glucose-6-Phosphate Dehydrogenase (G6PD) Deficiency.
articles.authors: Lau CKS, Kek XY, Gan WF

articles.headline: Dichloromethane extract of Acanthospermum australe inhibited chloroquine-sensitive P. falciparum strain 3D7 with IC50 1.3 µg/mL and suppressed P. falciparum heat shock protein 70-1 chaperone activity, increasing protein aggregation and inhibiting ATPase activity
articles.title_original: Acanthospermum australe Extract Inhibits the Chaperone Activity of Plasmodium falciparum Heat Shock Protein 70-1.

articles.headline: Review examining compounds targeting P. falciparum mitochondrial function and the use of Saccharomyces cerevisiae as a model system for antimalarial drug discovery. No specific quantitative results provided
articles.title_original: Targeting Mitochondrial Function in Plasmodium falciparum: Insight into Antimalarial Drugs and the Emerging Role of Saccharomyces cerevisiae as a Model System.

articles.headline: Title only; no abstract provided. Appears to address funding and policy for global malaria control efforts
articles.title_original: Supporting the Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis, and Malaria.

articles.headline: Brief discussion of intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy (IPTp) with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine for malaria prevention. Abstract highlights need for better mechanistic understanding of how IPTp-SP reduces low birthweight but provides no quantitative results
articles.title_original: A need for new tools for prevention of malaria in pregnancy.

articles.headline: Review of controlled human malaria infection (CHMI) models that supported development of malaria vaccines RTSS/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M and advanced drug and monoclonal antibody testing. No specific quantitative efficacy data provided in abstract
articles.title_original: Controlled human malaria infection studies: insights into recent advances and key immunological and ethical implementation lessons.

articles.headline: Title only; no abstract provided. Addresses P. vivax malaria persistence in hotspot districts in India
articles.title_original: Hotspot districts of persistent Plasmodium vivax malaria pose a formidable challenge to malaria elimination in India.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 1,154 febrile patients in Ethiopia (2020–2021) found 46% Plasmodium-infected: 49% P. falciparum, 26% mixed P. falciparum/P. vivax, 23% P. vivax mono-infections. Among Duffy-negative individuals (41%), P. vivax mono-infections were rare (2.4%) but 25% of mixed infections occurred in this group. PvDBP gene duplication was present in 78% of samples (94% in Gambela), suggesting P. vivax adaptation to invade Duffy-negative erythrocytes in co-infection contexts
articles.title_original: High prevalence of mixed Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax co-infections in Duffy negative individuals in Ethiopia.

articles.headline: In malaria-endemic Uganda, CXCR6+ CD127− type 1 regulatory T (Tr1) cells comprised ~90% of P. falciparum-specific CD4+ T cells. Higher Tr1 frequencies correlated with reduced symptomatic malaria despite parasitemia but delayed parasite clearance, indicating dual roles in clinical immunity and parasite persistence
articles.title_original: CXCR6(+) CD127(-) Tr1 Cells Balance Immunity and Persistence in Plasmodium falciparum Infection.

articles.headline: Sequencing 656 modern (2014–2018) and 45 historic (1927–1967) Anopheles funestus specimens from 16 African countries revealed stable continental population structure with strong selection signals for resistance alleles under vector control pressure. A promising gene drive target from An. gambiae is highly conserved in An. funestus, enabling strategic insecticide and gene drive deployment
articles.title_original: Genomic diversity of the African malaria vector Anopheles funestus.

articles.headline: Double-blind RCT of 2,757 pregnant women in Uganda found monthly IPTp with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine plus sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine did not improve composite adverse birth outcomes versus either drug alone (30.0% vs 30.9% and 26.4%, p=0.70 and 0.10). Dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine alone achieved 94% reduction in symptomatic malaria (0.03 vs 0.46 episodes/person-year, p<0.001) and 97% reduction in parasitemia (0.6% vs 17.7%, p<0.001) versus sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine but showed higher adverse birth outcome risk (RR 1.17, p=0.04)
articles.title_original: Dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine plus sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine versus either drug alone for intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy: A double-blind, randomized, controlled phase 3 trial from Uganda.

articles.headline: Rwanda reduced malaria cases from 4.8 million (2016/17) to 620,000 (2023/24) but experienced 45.8% increase by 2024 reaching 87,000 cases by March. Review of 22 sources (2022–2025) identified resurgence drivers: meteorological changes expanding Anopheles gambiae habitat, pyrethroid and artemisinin resistance (K13 mutations), suboptimal non-pyrethroid indoor residual spray coverage, and human mobility. Proposed interventions include community health worker programs, drone-mediated larviciding, and new drugs (dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine, artesunate-pyronaridine)
articles.title_original: Investigating the resurgence of malaria in Rwanda: factors contributing to rising cases amidst near-eradication success and strategies for sustained control.

articles.headline: Review synthesizing P. falciparum evolutionary adaptations including antigenic variation and drug resistance mechanisms, Anopheles vector insecticide resistance eroding bed net and indoor residual spraying efficacy, and parasite genomic streamlining enabling obligate parasitic lifestyle; discusses integrated management strategies and therapeutic targets for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Evolutionary Trajectory of Plasmodium falciparum: From Autonomous Phototroph to Dedicated Parasite.

articles.headline: Among 2,728 pregnant women in Uganda receiving intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy (IPTp) from 2020–2024, parasitemia at enrollment was 38.1%, declining to 6.5% post-initiation; 6.8% of births had low birth weight. Primigravida women had highest risk: relative risk 2.40 (95% CI 1.81–3.20, p<0.001) for parasitemia after IPTp, 4.20 (95% CI 2.33–7.59, p<0.001) for high-grade placental malaria, and 2.14 (95% CI 1.18–3.89, p=0.01) for low birth weight
articles.title_original: Maternal and household risk factors for malaria in pregnancy and low birthweight: a prospective cohort study from Uganda.

articles.headline: Scoping review (22 sources) examining climate change impacts on malaria transmission in Zimbabwe found studies demonstrating geographic distribution and intensity shifts in transmission but identified critical gaps in longitudinal data and localized predictive modeling; recommends climate-informed programming and climate-resilient health systems
articles.title_original: Impact of Climate Change on Malaria Transmission and Management in Zimbabwe: A Scoping Review of the Literature.

articles.headline: Systematic review protocol (PROSPERO CRD 42024619945) designed to synthesize evidence on progression from uncomplicated to severe malaria among children receiving concurrent malaria control interventions in sub-Saharan Africa; full review and meta-analysis expected by end of December 2025
articles.title_original: Progression from uncomplicated to severe malaria among children in settings receiving different malaria control interventions in sub-Saharan Africa: a systematic review protocol.

articles.headline: Review examining transmission-blocking vaccine (TBV) candidates targeting Anopheles mosquitoes; notes low efficacy of current parasite-based vaccines (RTS,S/AS01 and R21) that only reduce disease severity; discusses mosquito-based TBVs for targeting diverse Plasmodium parasites and reducing vector survival as transmission-blocking strategy
articles.title_original: Mosquito-based transmission-blocking vaccine candidates for malaria: progress, challenges, and innovations.

articles.headline: Controlled human malaria infection study in 38 malaria-naive adults (21 infected with P. falciparum NF54, 17 with 7G8 strain) using 638,817-peptide diversity-reflecting microarray detected differential antibody breadth across strains: 7G8-infected participants showed higher breadth to 17 of 20 proteins; 6 epitopes had >95% cross-reactivity with ETRAMP10.2 identified as vaccine candidate
articles.title_original: Controlled human malaria infection with NF54 and 7G8 strains elicit differential antibody responses to Plasmodium falciparum peptides.

articles.headline: Title discusses El Niño-Southern Oscillation impacts on malaria transmission risks in the Amazon; abstract not provided to extract specific findings
articles.title_original: El Niño-Southern Oscillation and shifting malaria risks in the Amazon.

articles.headline: Review of P. falciparum cerebral malaria pathophysiology in endemic sub-Saharan Africa examining blood-brain barrier disruption, cerebral edema (vasogenic and cytotoxic), and characteristic MRI findings including basal ganglia infarcts, brainstem lesions, and corpus callosum abnormalities that predict poor outcomes; highlights circulating extracellular vesicles as emerging diagnostic and prognostic biomarkers
articles.title_original: Neurovascular Pathophysiology and Emerging Biomarkers in Cerebral Malaria: An Integrative Perspective.

articles.headline: Retrospective study of 361 P. vivax patients (South Delhi, 2020–2024) found 46.3% (167/361) developed severe complications challenging the perception of P. vivax benignity. Thrombocytopenia was most strongly associated with severity (OR 3.20, p<0.001), followed by elevated creatinine (OR 6.07, p<0.001) and hyperbilirubinemia (OR 3.71, p<0.001); anaemia present in 73.4% of cases
articles.title_original: Clinical spectrum and complications of Plasmodium vivax malaria: A retrospective study from Delhi, India.

articles.headline: Opinion article advocating for increased African governmental representation on the governing board of the Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria
articles.title_original: It is time to increase Africa's governmental representation on the governing board of the global fund to fight AIDS, tuberculosis and malaria.

articles.headline: Retrospective diagnostic accuracy study (84 children, Blantyre autopsy cohort 1996–2010) evaluating malarial retinopathy as predictor of cerebral parasite sequestration in pediatric cerebral malaria: sensitivity 89.4% (95% CI 77.6–95.6%), specificity 73.0% (95% CI 57.2–84.8%), improved to 94.3% sensitivity (95% CI 81.7–98.7%) and 88.0% specificity by reclassifying <5 retinal hemorrhages in single eye as retinopathy-negative
articles.title_original: Re-evaluating malarial retinopathy to improve its diagnostic accuracy in paediatric cerebral malaria: A retrospective study.

articles.headline: Secondary cluster RCT analysis (432 archival samples, Ghana, ages 6–36 months) examined HLA-G 14 bp polymorphism association with malaria severity among iron-fortified children: iron-fortified carriers of HLA-G 14 bp insertion/deletion variants (+/- and -/-) demonstrated elevated severe malaria risk; the +/- variant also showed higher anaemia risk in iron-supplemented participants
articles.title_original: HLA-G 14 bp polymorphism: Implications and its associated risk to malaria severity among iron-fortified Ghanaian infants and young children.

articles.headline: NGS of cytochrome b gene in three Southeast Asian passerine species identified two known and ten novel Plasmodium lineages with high infection rates. Each bird species harbored a distinct subset of parasites; phylogenetic analysis revealed sympatric OTUs are not closely related but share overlapping host preferences, suggesting parasite distribution is filtered by host ecology and habitat use rather than opportunistic infection
articles.title_original: Hidden Plasmodium diversity revealed in southeastern Asian passerines using next-generation amplicon sequencing.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis

articles.headline: 41-year-old man from Romania with P. vivax malaria from Southeast Asia presented with fever, jaundice, thrombocytopenia, and elevated inflammatory markers. Despite primaquine radical cure after first relapse, a second relapse occurred (confirmed by RT-PCR), highlighting P. vivax's high relapse potential from hypnozoites and need for accurate species identification in travelers
articles.title_original: Recurrent Malaria with Plasmodium vivax: A Case Report and Brief Review of the Literature.

articles.headline: Correction notice; no results provided
articles.title_original: Correction: Household practices and infrastructure associated with high Plasmodium falciparum infection rates among children under five years old in Northern Uganda.

articles.headline: Systematic review and meta-analysis of seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC: sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine monthly during transmission season) in Sahelian children <5 years shows SMC effectively reduces uncomplicated and severe malaria incidence and mortality compared to controls. However, epidemiological context is shifting with dry-season transmission persistence and increasing cases in older children, requiring strategy adaptation
articles.title_original: Status and prospects of seasonal malaria chemoprevention among children in Sahelian countries: A systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Gracilaria sp. ethanol extract containing saponins, tannins, flavonoids, and alkaloids inhibited P. falciparum strain D37 growth in vitro, with alkaloids, flavonoids, and derivatives showing an IC50 of 43.38 μg/mL, suggesting seaweed as a potential affordable alternative for drug-resistant malaria parasites
articles.title_original: Antiplasmodial activity of Gracilaria sp. ethanol extract against Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: DNA-based molecular force sensors and high-speed microscopy revealed that trophozoite-stage P. falciparum-infected erythrocytes exhibit complex flipping and lateral-sliding motion on ICAM-1 or CD36, while schizont-stage parasites show rolling motion with occasional sliding/pinning. Adhesion footprints extended across endothelial cell distances, providing mechanistic insights into cytoadhesion and microvasculature pathology
articles.title_original: Contact Dynamics of Cytoadhering Plasmodium falciparum-Infected Erythrocytes in Flow.

articles.headline: Tanzanian serosurvey (n=290) found 56% seropositive for Pfs230D1M antibodies and 49% for Pfs48/45 IgG, with seroprevalence increasing significantly with age (Pfs230D1M aOR=3.16, p≤0.0001; Pfs48/45 OR=3.06, p≤0.0001). Two of ten plasma samples showed significant transmission-reducing activity in membrane feeding assays, suggesting semi-immune populations are suitable for evaluating transmission-blocking vaccine candidates
articles.title_original: Seroprevalence of antibodies to Plasmodium falciparum transmission-blocking target proteins Pfs230D1M and Pfs48/45 in Tanzanian populations of diverse malaria transmission intensity.

articles.headline: Target-based screening of Medicines for Malaria Venture compound libraries against SARS-CoV-2 proteases identified MMV1634397 as a PLpro inhibitor (IC50=0.7 μM), with optimized analogs showing improved potency (IC50 values 0.06–0.34 μM) and antiviral activity in cell culture (EC50=2.9–3.7 μM). Demonstrates value of MMV malaria-drug libraries for accelerating antiviral discovery
articles.title_original: Screening of Medicines for Malaria Venture Open Boxes Identifies Potent SARS-CoV‑2 Papain-like Protease (PL(pro)) Inhibitors.

articles.headline: During Vietnam War, malaria caused mass US military casualties with rates exceeding 10 per 1,000 man-days in front-line units. Chloroquine-primaquine chemoprophylaxis from Korean War proved inadequate; adding dapsone improved efficacy but caused rare agranulocytosis and ≥8 deaths. Post-war, P. vivax relapsing malaria caused thousands of US infections; overall military impact was >200,000 lost man-days annually despite only 78 deaths in Army
articles.title_original: US Malaria Casualties and Chemoprophylaxis during the Vietnam War.

articles.headline: Ugandan cohort of children with severe malaria (n=177, ages 6–48 months, 2014–2017) showed low intestinal helminth prevalence (5.1%) despite sensitive qPCR detection of six species. Helminth infection did not modify risk of severe malaria (aRR=1.0, 95% CI 0.82–1.3, p=0.78), reflecting success of Uganda's national deworming program for children 6 months–5 years
articles.title_original: Low prevalence of helminth infection in Ugandan children hospitalized with severe malaria.

articles.headline: Rwanda 2014–2015 Demographic Health Survey using qPCR in 5,050 adults found 22.3% asymptomatic malaria prevalence (P. falciparum 16.3%, non-falciparum 8.0%), with P. ovale, P. malariae, and P. falciparum broadly distributed and P. vivax rare. Mixed-species infections were common at highest-transmission sites; P. falciparum associated with lower wealth and rural residence, non-falciparum showed fewer significant associations
articles.title_original: Prevalence of asymptomatic non-falciparum and falciparum malaria in the 2014-15 Rwanda Demographic Health Survey.

articles.headline: Cryo-EM structure revealed P. falciparum Pfs48/45 interacts with Pfs230 domains 13 and 14 in the endogenous fertilization complex. Transgenic parasites lacking these domains showed defective gamete localization and reduced oocyst formation. Nanobodies targeting domains 13–14 inhibited complex formation and reduced transmission in mosquitoes; these domains are targets of naturally acquired immunity and mRNA-LNP vaccine-induced immune sera
articles.title_original: Cryo-EM structure of endogenous Plasmodium falciparum Pfs230 and Pfs48/45 fertilization complex.

articles.headline: NYC accounted for 79.1% of imported malaria cases in New York (2018-2024), with 11 counties contributing 88.3% of remaining state cases. A marked decline occurred in 2020 coinciding with COVID-19 travel restrictions, followed by rebound in 2021. These findings highlight the need to incorporate recent travel history and local county-level awareness into emergency department evaluation of febrile patients
articles.title_original: Imported Malaria in New York: Geographic Patterns and Implications for Emergency Physicians.

articles.headline: In 30 female C57BL/6 mice with P. berghei-induced cerebral malaria, Juglans regia leaf extract (JRLE) treatment reduced oxidative stress markers (nitric oxide, malondialdehyde, hydrogen peroxide) and increased antioxidant enzyme activities (catalase, superoxide dismutase, reduced glutathione). These results suggest J. regia leaves could serve as an alternative source of antioxidant and antimalarial agents
articles.title_original: Effect of walnut leaves on oxidative stress caused by murine cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: This review of Plasmodium telomere maintenance as an antimalarial target notes that parasites harbor G-quadruplex DNA structures at telomeres and depend on continuous telomerase activity via PfTERT. Inhibiting PfTERT shows promise for blocking telomere elongation and impairing replication. Combining telomere-directed therapies with existing antimalarials may help overcome resistance
articles.title_original: Plasmodium telomere maintenance: uncovering the Achilles' heel for novel antimalarials.
articles.authors: Wakai TN, Anzaku DO, Afolabi IS

articles.headline: In 1,556 participants from Equatorial Guinea (June-October 2021), SARS-CoV-2 prevalence was 3.03%, malaria prevalence 22.25%, and co-infection 0.37% (6 cases). Whole-genome sequencing identified predominantly Delta lineage SARS-CoV-2 introduced from Europe. This provides first prevalence estimates and variant characterization in this malaria-endemic region
articles.title_original: Circulation of SARS-CoV-2 and Co-Infection with Plasmodium falciparum in Equatorial Guinea.

articles.headline: Review identified 34 medicinal plants with antimalarial compounds showing potent P. falciparum activity: joziknipholone A (IC50=0.17 μM), joziknipholone B (IC50=0.26 μM), knipholone clooxanthrone (IC50=14.58 μM for D6), ceramicine R (IC50=2.80 μM), and cajachalcone (IC50=7.4 μM). Key secondary metabolites include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, offering templates for drugs against resistant strains
articles.title_original: Plant-derived secondary metabolites for malaria treatment: extraction, mechanisms, and therapeutic potential.

articles.headline: Ecological drivers of malaria vector habitat and transmission during long-lasting insecticidal net interventions in Côte d'Ivoire (correction)
articles.title_original: Correction: Ecological drivers of malaria vector habitat and transmission over 1 year of long-lasting insecticidal net intervention in Côte d'Ivoire.

articles.headline: Metagenomic survey of 188 afebrile residents in high-transmission Northern Sahelian Ghana identified 2.67% asymptomatic submicroscopic malaria with mixed-species infections (P. falciparum, P. malariae, P. ovale curtisi, P. ovale wallikeri). Within-host complexity was significantly higher in larger blood volumes. Findings underscore the need for more sensitive measurement of Plasmodium complexity in surveillance and control
articles.title_original: Metagenomic complexity of high, seasonal transmission of Plasmodium spp. in asymptomatic carriers in Northern Sahelian Ghana.

articles.headline: Conditional depletion of P. falciparum protein phosphatase 1 (PfPP1) showed it regulates gametocyte-infected erythrocyte deformability via PKA dephosphorylation and controls gamete activation through the cGMP/PKG pathway. PfPP1 is essential for parasite transmission to mosquitoes, representing a potential transmission-blocking antimalarial target
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum PP1 phosphatase is a key regulator of malaria parasite transmission.

articles.headline: In P. berghei-infected rats treated with Melochia umbellata leaf aqueous extract (MULAE, 100-400 mg/kg), the 400 mg/kg dose achieved 86.20% parasitemia suppression, restored hemoglobin to 14.9 g/dL, and improved serum markers and organ histopathology. LD50 exceeded 5,000 mg/kg orally, validating traditional use and supporting M. umbellata as a source for novel antimalarial agents
articles.title_original: Curative antimalarial efficacy and hepato-renal protective effects of Melochia umbellata leaf extract in rats infected with Plasmodium berghei.

articles.headline: In 2,171 individuals from Gia Lai province, Vietnam, asymptomatic submicroscopic malaria prevalence was 2.67% with P. falciparum/P. vivax mono- and mixed infections. High-frequency drug-resistance mutations were detected: pfk13 C580Y (100%), pfcrt M74I/N75E/K76T (100%), and pvmdr-1 F1076L (100%), predominantly in adults and females. Urgent surveillance and targeted interventions are needed to prevent silent spread of drug-resistant parasites
articles.title_original: Molecular Profiles of Antimalarial Drug Resistance in Plasmodium Species from Asymptomatic Malaria Carriers in Gia Lai Province, Vietnam.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 11 articles on public-private partnerships for climate-resilient malaria control in Sub-Saharan Africa identified challenges (ineffective legal frameworks, bureaucracy, limited private-sector adherence) and opportunities (workforce training, medicines/technologies, emergency preparedness). PPPs remain viable for implementing WHO Operational Framework despite current constraints in SSA countries
articles.title_original: Challenges and opportunities for greater public-private partnership for the implementation of the WHO operational framework for building climate resilient health systems to improve malaria control and elimination in Sub-Saharan Africa: a rapid review.

articles.headline: Longitudinal study of 2,960 febrile patients in Myanmar (mean fever 1.8 days) and 15,576 in Thailand (2.9 days) identified risk factors for delayed malaria care-seeking: Shan ethnicity (TR 1.48), farmers (TR 1.07-1.12), P. vivax diagnosis (TR 1.20-1.23), and uncertain malaria history (TR 1.10-1.26). Daily bed net use and prior malaria episodes shortened delays; targeted interventions are needed
articles.title_original: Malaria care-seeking among febrile patients in Myanmar and Thailand: an accelerated failure time analysis.

articles.headline: Voltage-switching nESI/APCI mass spectrometry correctly classified all 18 malaria samples (9 infected, 9 uninfected), achieving 100% accuracy versus 67% for nESI and 78% for APCI at constant voltages
articles.title_original: Voltage Switching on a Dual Nano-Electrospray/Atmospheric Pressure Chemical Ionization Platform Provides Higher Discrimination Power for Malaria Detection via Direct Infusion Mass Spectrometry.
articles.authors: Ferguson R, Kulyk DS, Badu-Tawiah AK

articles.headline: Recombinant P. falciparum 20S proteasome platform enabled discovery of selective inhibitors: J-80 inhibited Pf20S β5 at IC50 22.4 nM with 90-fold selectivity over human β5; reversible TDI-8304 and irreversible analogs also demonstrated potency and selectivity
articles.title_original: A recombinant expression system for the Plasmodium falciparum proteasome enables structural analysis of its assembly and the design of selective inhibitors.

articles.headline: BinaxNOW Malaria rapid diagnostic achieved 100% sensitivity and specificity in initial evaluation (294 specimens) and 100% positive/negative percent agreement in multi-site verification, supporting implementation in low-prevalence US regions
articles.title_original: BinaxNOW vs malaria: A diagnostic showdown: Design and implementation of a verification strategy across 4 academic hospitals in an area with low malaria prevalence.

articles.headline: Seasonal geometric morphometrics of A. maculatus revealed significant wing size differences between hot and dry seasons (p<0.05) and wing shape differences between dry and hot/wet seasons (p<0.05), indicating seasonal vector adaptation
articles.title_original: Seasonal variation in wing geometry of the malaria vector Anopheles maculatus (Diptera: Culicidae) in Western Thailand.
articles.authors: Sumruayphol S, Chaiphongpachara T, Laojun S

articles.headline: Resistance-associated Pfcoronin mutations disrupt Pf Coronin-Pf Actin interaction and ring-stage localization, impairing endocytosis and hemoglobin uptake; mutations converge with Pfkelch13 mutations to limit heme and artemisinin activation
articles.title_original: Cellular and molecular basis of Pf Coronin function in artemisinin resistance in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Febrile temperature (40°C) enhanced P. falciparum-infected RBC adhesion in 3D brain/lung microvessels via endothelial glycocalyx shedding exposing EPCR and ICAM-1; MMP inhibition prevented temperature-induced cytoadhesion
articles.title_original: Febrile temperature enhances Plasmodium falciparum cytoadhesion by disrupting the endothelial glycocalyx.

articles.headline: CIS43 nanoparticle junctional epitope vaccine at 1 μg dose achieved comparable liver burden reduction to R21; at 0.25 μg showed modest reduction; combination vaccines induced modestly enhanced protection compared to either alone
articles.title_original: Protective immunity against malaria by a nanoparticle CIS43-based junctional vaccine alone or in combination with R21.

articles.headline: Among 558 Senegalese households surveyed 2020–2023, 65.28% reported very strong positive malaria vaccination attitudes, strongly associated with higher COVID-19 vaccine uptake (p<0.001); consistent COVID-19 vaccination intention predicted strong malaria vaccine attitudes (p=0.001)
articles.title_original: Attitudes to malaria vaccination in children: what can be learned from their consistency with the temporal dynamics of COVID-19 vaccination intention? A prospective cohort study in rural Senegal (July 2020-November 2023).

articles.headline: Opinion or perspective on preventable malaria resurgence in Africa and required interventions; no abstract available
articles.title_original: The resurgence of malaria in Africa is an avoidable crisis-here's what we must do.

articles.headline: Genotyping 179 samples from Korhogo district (2019–2020) revealed high P. falciparum genetic diversity: msp1 (44 genotypes), msp2 (92 genotypes), glurp (45 genotypes), with expected heterozygosity ≥0.9 across all markers
articles.title_original: Genetic Diversity of Plasmodium falciparum in Korhogo Health District, Northern Côte d'Ivoire.

articles.headline: Mini-review synthesizing malaria-associated dyserythropoiesis through four converging pathways: cytokine-driven erythropoietin suppression and hepcidin upregulation, hemozoin-induced oxidative damage via Fenton chemistry, parasite infection of orthochromatic erythroblasts, and macrophage-mediated erythroblastic island remodeling
articles.title_original: Malaria and dyserythropoiesis: a mini review.

articles.headline: Among 120 HBV-infected Ghanaians, prevalence of coinfections was: H. pylori 40.8%, T. gondii 19.2%, S. typhi 2.5%, P. falciparum 2.5%; HBV-H. pylori coinfection significantly elevated ALT (33.5 vs 23.5 IU, p<0.001)
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Helicobacter pylori, Salmonella typhi, Plasmodium falciparum, and Toxoplasma gondii infections and levels of liver function markers among Hepatitis B Virus infected Ghanaians: A cross-sectional study in the Greater Accra Region.

articles.headline: PlasmoDocking is a web-based platform for molecular docking simulations enabling users to submit up to 10 molecular structures for automated screening against 38 pre-configured Plasmodium falciparum targets, facilitating multi-target comparison of binding energies and accelerating malaria drug discovery
articles.title_original: PlasmoDocking: A User-Friendly Open-Source Web Tool for Virtual Screening Targeting Plasmodium falciparum Enzymes.

articles.headline: A meta-analysis of 27 studies found 55.3% pooled prevalence of malaria self-medication in sub-Saharan Africa, driven by low income, cheap non-prescription drug availability, large family size, lack of health insurance, and difficulty accessing formal healthcare. Authors recommend stakeholder engagement in the informal drug market to promote enlightened antimalarial use and mitigate drug resistance
articles.title_original: Issues associated with malaria self-medication in sub-Saharan Africa: A systematic literature review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 4 RCTs (6,745 participants, China/Indonesia/Kenya) found spatial repellents reduced malaria infection risk from 31.0% (controls) to 20.0%, yielding 52% relative risk reduction (RR 0.48, 95% CI 0.33–0.70, P<0.001), with no significant effects on mosquito density or sporozoite rates, supporting spatial repellents as complementary malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Efficacy of Spatial Repellents in Malaria Prevention: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.

articles.headline: News report on halted transgenic mosquito program in Burkina Faso; misinformation campaign may have eroded trust and prompted a raid on a research center. [Not a research article; insufficient data for quantitative summary.]
articles.title_original: Burkina Faso halts plan to fight malaria with transgenic mosquitoes.

articles.headline: Correction to prior publication on computational identification of aspartic protease inhibitors for antimalarial drug development against Plasmodium vivax. [Correction article; original abstract not provided.]
articles.title_original: Correction: Computational identification of aspartic protease inhibitors for antimalarial drug development against Plasmodium Vivax.

articles.headline: An immunoinformatic study designed a multi-epitope vaccine targeting Anopheles stephensi FREP1 midgut protein to block Plasmodium transmission. In-silico analyses predicted strong TLR4 binding, high free binding energy by molecular dynamics simulation, and efficient protein expression with codon optimization in E. coli, suggesting FREP1 as an innovative transmission-blocking vaccine candidate
articles.title_original: Designing a multi-epitope vaccine against the midgut-specific fibrinogen-related protein 1(FREP1) of Anopheles stephensi to enhance protection against the malaria parasite: a step beyond traditional vaccine development approaches.

articles.headline: In a high-parasitemia Plasmodium berghei ANKA-luciferase mouse model, tafenoquine/artesunate and pyronaridine/amodiaquine combinations showed fractional curative doses of 0.83 and 0.93 respectively, with other combinations in the additive range (0.9–1.1). All tested combinations, including short half-life azithromycin, demonstrated additive interaction patterns
articles.title_original: Fractional curative killing of pyronaridine or artesunate combinations with tafenoquine, 4-aminoquinolines, or azithromycin in a murine malaria-luciferase model.
articles.authors: Lee G, Aneke JS, Sullivan DJ Jr

articles.headline: Five azithromycin analogs showed 10- to 42-fold improved quick-killing potency against malaria parasites compared to azithromycin, with chloroquinoline-containing analogs achieving 2- to 17-fold further improvement. Cellular thermal shift proteomics revealed analogs stabilized 34–155 different proteins; the most potent analog improved gametocytocidal activity and the mechanism appears to involve broad protein interactions rather than classical chloroquine-like action
articles.title_original: Characterizing the quick-killing mechanism of action of azithromycin analogs against malaria parasites.

articles.headline: In 985 asymptomatic individuals (Nov 2018–May 2019, Ha-Lambani, South Africa), prevalence of Plasmodium infection was 7.1% (95% CI 0.054–0.087), with P. ovale dominant (57.1%), followed by P. falciparum (37.1%), P. malariae (1.4%), and P. vivax (1.4%). Pfk13 gene sequencing identified 57 SNPs with 70.1% non-synonymous; novel mutations (H719Q, P701T, M472I, I526R, P443S) were detected in the propeller domain but none previously associated with artemisinin resistance
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium species in asymptomatic individuals in North-Eastern South Africa: 2018 - 2019.

articles.headline: Title indicates study on impact of armed conflict in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo on malaria outbreak risk in children under five. [No abstract provided; insufficient data for summary.]
articles.title_original: The impact of armed conflict in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo on the risk of malaria outbreaks in children under five years old.

articles.headline: Cost-effectiveness decision model compared tafenoquine (after semi-quantitative G6PD screening ≥70% activity) to 7-day low-dose primaquine (0.5 mg/kg/day) ± screening for vivax malaria radical cure in Brazil. Tafenoquine cost US$2,894 per DALY averted versus current practice (below US$7,752 threshold) and dominated primaquine screening (0.14 DALYs averted, US$13 cost savings), with >98% cost-effectiveness probability in both comparisons
articles.title_original: The cost-effectiveness of tafenoquine following screening with STANDARD™ G6PD screening for the treatment of vivax malaria in the Brazilian Public Health System.

articles.headline: Prospective cohort of 1,679 children (50.5% female) in Mali, followed mean 25.8 months, found pregnancy malaria associated with increased hazard of first malaria infection across all maternal gravidities, while preterm birth (5.7% of cohort) increased hazard of first P. falciparum parasitemia (HR 2.17 in secundigravida, HR 3.63 in multigravida offspring; IRR 2.74 for parasitemia in uninfected multigravida offspring). Maternal gravidity and infection history modified the preterm-malaria association, informing risk stratification for monitoring and chemoprevention in preterm-born children
articles.title_original: Preterm Birth and Malaria Susceptibility in Offspring of Uninfected Multigravid Women.

articles.headline: In 65 asymptomatic Plasmodium PCR-positive vs 54 PCR-negative sub-Saharan African migrants in Sweden, Plasmodium infection was associated with higher anemia (21.1% vs 6.1%), elevated ESR (58.1% vs 25%), elevated IgM (30.5% vs 10.5%), and splenomegaly (25.4% vs 2.5%, all p≤0.05). Improvement occurred after antimalarial treatment, supporting screening and treating subclinical malaria in migrants to prevent adverse outcomes
articles.title_original: Clinical Findings in Migrants With Asymptomatic Plasmodium Infections.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study in Colombian gold-mining areas identified 11 Anopheles species; 6 specimens were Plasmodium-positive: An. darlingi with P. falciparum and An. nuneztovari s.s. and An. punctimacula with P. vivax. Entomological inoculation rates ranged 1.5–6.6 infective bites/person/month. Authors confirm An. darlingi, An. nuneztovari, and An. punctimacula as vectors and recommend larval source management alongside LLINs/IRS
articles.title_original: Anopheles species associated with malaria transmission in gold-mining areas of northwest Colombia.

articles.headline: MALDI-TOF MS identified secondary Anopheles vectors in Kenya and Mozambique with 91% accuracy (kappa 0.87) versus Sanger sequencing, achieving 100% sensitivity/specificity for most species except An. cf. coustani 2 NFL-2015 and An. ziemanni. This technique proved as efficient as DNA sequencing, highlighting MALDI-TOF MS potential for sustainable malaria vector monitoring
articles.title_original: MALDI-TOF MS for identification of Afro-tropical secondary malaria vectors.

articles.headline: Among 583 children aged 12-23 months in Togo, 60.7% were fully immunized and 15.8% zero-dose. Zero-dose status was significantly associated with low caregiver education (aOR 5.02, 95% CI 1.85-13.60), negative vaccine safety attitudes (aOR 8.60, 95% CI 3.32-22.30), and lack of modern healthcare reliance (aOR 3.06, 95% CI 1.26-7.48). Suboptimal coverage requires strengthened community engagement for effective perennial malaria chemoprevention delivery
articles.title_original: Assessing immunization coverage in Southern togo: implications for perennial malaria chemoprevention delivery.

articles.headline: Ferritin nanoparticle vaccine presenting designed CSPj5c and 17-4 immunogens from P. falciparum CSP and Pfs48/45 conferred protection against transgenic sporozoite challenge in mice, and purified immunized rabbit IgGs elicited potent transmission-reducing activity. This multistage vaccine targeting pre-erythrocytic and transmission stages offers potential for simultaneous infection prevention and transmission blocking
articles.title_original: A combined designed CSP and Pfs48/45 infection and transmission blocking vaccine for malaria.

articles.headline: In P. berghei ANKA-induced cerebral malaria mice, five-flower remedy extract (600 mg/kg) combined with artesunate significantly reduced parasitemia (80%), increased survival, and reduced neurological deficits, brain hemorrhage, and neuronal apoptosis versus monotherapy. Authors propose this combination could provide valuable additional neuroprotective treatment for cerebral malaria by reducing inflammation and brain damage
articles.title_original: Antimalarial and neuroprotective effects of ethanolic extracts of the five-flower remedy in an experimental cerebral malaria model.

articles.headline: PbCBP-O, a cyclic nucleotide-binding protein localized to the ookinete apex, is critical for Plasmodium ookinete infection of mosquitoes; protein disruption severely compromised invasion. The protein exhibits dual binding specificity to both cGMP and cAMP, suggesting the ookinete apical tip transduces cyclic nucleotide signals essential for parasite transmission
articles.title_original: A divergent cyclic nucleotide binding protein promotes Plasmodium ookinete infection of the mosquito.

articles.headline: Flavin-deficient erythrocytes (FDE), identified in 23% of individuals in Ferrara and 13% in Huye, Rwanda, inhibited P. falciparum intraerythrocytic proliferation in vitro, likely through oxidative stress susceptibility. FAD synthase gene mutations were identified in three Rwandan FDE individuals, establishing a potential genetic basis for this naturally occurring protective phenotype against malaria
articles.title_original: Flavin-deficient erythrocytes offer protection against malaria parasites.
articles.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A

articles.headline: Fc-dependent effector functions were required for maximal prophylactic protection with anti-CSP monoclonal antibody 317. Fc engineering enhanced multiple mAbs; L9 and CIS43 showed enhanced antibody-dependent phagocytosis and NK activation, while 317 drove enhanced CSP-responsive T-cell activation. Effector functions represent important mechanisms for anti-CSP protection with potential for potency enhancement through Fc engineering
articles.title_original: Analysis of Fc-dependent effector functions of anti-malaria circumsporozoite protein antibodies.

articles.headline: β-caryophyllene exhibited dose-dependent excito-repellency against Thai Anopheles species: An. minimus showed 100% escape at 2.5% (contact) and 5% (noncontact), An. harrisoni 94.34% at 7.5% contact, and An. epiroticus minimal response (12–25%). No knockdown or mortality occurred. Authors conclude β-caryophyllene is promising as a natural spatial repellent against malaria vectors
articles.title_original: COMPARATIVE BEHAVIORAL RESPONSES OF Β-CARYOPHYLLENE AGAINST ANOPHELES MOSQUITO SPECIES, POTENTIAL VECTORS OF MALARIA IN THAILAND.

articles.headline: Among 218 febrile children <5 years in Southern Ethiopia, malaria prevalence was 21.1% with P. falciparum predominating (60.9%). Infection was significantly associated with proximity to mosquito breeding sites (P=0.025), not sleeping under ITNs (P=0.021), and unsuitable beds for ITN use (P=0.002). Authors conclude ITN utilization and breeding site elimination are essential to reduce malaria burden in under-five children
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Malaria and Associated Factors among Under Five Febrile Children Attending Ener Amanuel Health Center, Southern Ethiopia: An Institution-based Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.authors: Tseha ST, Ebabu M, Aberar T

articles.headline: Stakeholder analysis of Malakit and targeted P. vivax drug administration in Brazil, French Guiana, and Suriname (June 2023–March 2024) identified strengths (Malakit effectiveness), weaknesses (data gaps, regulatory challenges), opportunities (elimination initiatives), and threats (funding/regulatory barriers). Authors conclude financial and regulatory barriers hinder scalability, though political and epidemiological factors offer opportunities for regional malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Assessing scalability factors of a malaria intervention using Malakit and target drug administration among hard-to-reach populations: An original approach focusing on key stakeholders - implementers and decision-makers - in the Guiana shield.

articles.headline: Title addresses malaria control strategies in hard-to-reach populations, identifying key challenges and potential solutions for improving access to care and diagnostic tools in remote endemic regions
articles.title_original: Malaria in hard-to-reach populations: Challenges and solutions.

articles.headline: Review of malaria's diverse transmission contexts across 150 years shows that despite historical elimination from temperate regions and post-2000s global mobilization reducing incidence and mortality, transmission diversity and human population mobility have prevented sustained decline. Access to health and reaching hard-to-reach and mobile populations are now essential to continued malaria control progress
articles.title_original: Biodiversity of malaria ecosystems, challenges for control: When human diversity is forgotten.

articles.headline: Analysis of Suriname's 2000–2024 national malaria surveillance shows P. falciparum elimination through targeted, inclusive interventions providing low-barrier care access. P. falciparum ceased being the primary species by 2007, with the last indigenous case in 2018, after disease shifted from indigenous communities to mobile gold miners. Risk of re-introduction from neighboring countries persists, requiring regional Guiana Shield-level coordination
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Suriname: how targeted interventions in marginalized populations led to elimination.

articles.headline: Systematic review screened 14,884 articles; 44 met criteria (65.9% from Africa, 37 from hard-to-reach areas). Village health workers were primarily engaged in diagnosis/treatment (21/44 studies) and prevention activities (nets, IPTc, SMC) in routine control, not malaria elimination. Conclusion: VHWs should be systematically integrated into elimination programs in hard-to-reach areas
articles.title_original: Role of village health worker in control activities for malaria elimination efforts: A systematic review.

articles.headline: In vitro study of Vernonia amygdalina crude leaf extracts against P. knowlesis (schizont assay) showed IC₅₀ values of 1.356 μg/mL (DCM), 1.131 μg/mL (MeOH), 1.973 μg/mL (H₂O), with selectivity indices >10. Methanol and water extracts were non-toxic (CC₅₀ 63.73 and 86.91 μg/mL). Extracts warrant further study for antimalarial drug development
articles.title_original: Vernonia amygdalina (Delile) exhibits in vitro anti-plasmodial activities against Plasmodium knowlesi.

articles.headline: Cost analysis of precision guided sterile insect technique (pgSIT) for A. gambiae suppression in West Africa determined an optimized facility size of 9.2 million mosquitoes suppressed, increasing cost-effectiveness. pgSIT can interrupt transmission, strengthen institutions, create jobs, and enhance regional health security resilience
articles.title_original: Estimated cost and operational structure of pgSIT malaria vector control programs in selected West African countries.

articles.headline: Case-control study (200 severe falciparum malaria, 200 controls) in Sudan found that in children, plasma potassium and sodium were significantly decreased (p<0.05); in adults, urea was significantly elevated (p<0.05). Positive correlation observed between urea and sodium (r=0.322, p<0.001). Malaria is a risk factor for hyponatremia/hypokalemia in children and azotemia in adults
articles.title_original: Exploring the Effect of Malaria on Biochemical Parameters Among Sudanese Patients with Complicated Falciparum Malaria.

articles.headline: Case report of a late-50s patient who developed severe P. falciparum malaria 9 days after returning from Burkina Faso. Despite artesunate treatment, patient died from multiple organ failure complicated by acute subdural hematoma, demonstrating severe malaria's fatal potential
articles.title_original: A fatal case report of Plasmodium falciparum malaria complicated by acute subdural hematoma.
articles.authors: Xie N, Ye H, Jiang X

articles.headline: Experimental study in chloroquine-resistant P. berghei-infected mice showed S. jamaicensis dichloromethane extract at 200 mg/kg mediated parasite clearance with significantly improved survival (p<0.05 on day 11). Extract modulated pro- and anti-inflammatory responses via TGF-β and IFN-γ, suggesting potential for antimalarial and immunomodulatory drug development
articles.title_original: Antiplasmodial and immunomodulatory activities of dichloromethane extract of Stachytarpheta jamaicensis in Plasmodium berghei infected mice.
articles.authors: Amaechi EC, Owolodun OA, Lambe KO

articles.headline: Bioassay of Crateva adansonii ethanolic extracts against A. gambiae larvae showed 100% mortality at 15–20% concentrations (12–24 h). Root extract: LC₅₀=0.04%, LC₉₀=8.07%. Leaf extract: 100% at 20% in 6 h (LC₅₀=1.78, LC₉₀=14.75). Plant-based extracts demonstrated strong insecticidal activity, offering eco-friendly alternative to synthetic chemicals
articles.title_original: Bioactive profiling, larvicidal and pupicidal activities of Crateva adansonii against two developmental stages of malaria vector, Anopheles gambiae.
articles.authors: Ileke KD, Babatunde NA, Akeju AV

articles.headline: Review of host immune mechanisms against P. falciparum malaria, covering innate responses (macrophages, dendritic cells, natural killer cells) and adaptive responses (T cells, B cell antibodies). Notes that protective immunity development is complex, influenced by parasite diversity, host genetics, and prior exposure, with some individuals developing partial immunity while others remain susceptible to severe disease
articles.title_original: Overview of the host immune response to P. falciparum malaria.
articles.authors: Antwi-Baffour S, Mensah BT, Annison L

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 650 household members in SMC coverage areas (Nanoro, Burkina Faso) detected P. falciparum infections in 68.6% (95% CI: 64.7–72.5): patent 27.4% (95% CI: 23.9–30.8), sub-patent 41.2% (95% CI: 37.3–45.2). Patent infections declined with age; sub-patent peaked at 49.2% in ages 15–24. Supports extending SMC to school-age children and implementing household member testing/treatment
articles.title_original: Patterns of patent and sub-patent Plasmodium falciparum infections in household members of children under seasonal malaria chemoprevention coverage in the health district of Nanoro, Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: Indonesia subnational elimination review (1950-2023): 389 of 514 districts and 5 of 38 provinces verified malaria-free by 2023. Papua accounts for >90% of national cases with incidence exceeding 400 per 1000 person-years. Zoonotic P. knowlesi infections increasing; outbreaks and re-established transmission occurring in western regions. New intervention combinations required to achieve 2030 elimination goal
articles.title_original: Shrinking the malaria map in Indonesia: progress of subnational control, elimination, and future strategies.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 300 Myanmar migrants in Thailand (mean age 34.5 years, March-April 2025): 2% prevalence of asymptomatic P. vivax by PCR. Despite >60% recognizing need for care, only 35% sought treatment within 24 hours. Lower malaria knowledge (OR=13.5) and lower care-seeking scores (OR=5.86) significantly associated with infection. Daily wage labor, infrequent returns to Myanmar, prior malaria, and higher knowledge enhanced care-seeking
articles.title_original: Malaria care-seeking behaviours and infection prevalence among short-term Myanmar migrants in Thailand.

articles.headline: Brazil HESMS intervention (2010-2012, 10 communities, 8,083 LLINs distributed): 99.93% acceptance rate; 92.13% achieved adequate household net coverage; 57.41% reported consistent use. Pre-intervention median malaria cases 7-24 per month across villages. No reduction in timely diagnosis-seeking behavior (<24 hours: 61-65%). Health education successfully improved LLIN acceptance but did not accelerate early care-seeking
articles.title_original: Contributions of health education and social mobilization in the implementation of a malaria control programme using long-lasting insecticidal nets.
articles.authors: da Costa FM, de Souza Rodrigues MM, Scarpassa VM

articles.headline: Experimental inoculation of P. homocircumflexum across bird species revealed shift from donor-dependent (2,647 differentially expressed genes at 8 days) to recipient-dependent (271 DEGs at 16 days) gene expression. Low genetic differentiation excludes haplotype selection. Findings support epigenetic inheritance and transcriptional plasticity as adaptation mechanisms enabling generalist malaria parasites to adjust to diverse host environments
articles.title_original: Generalist Malaria Parasites and Host Imprinting: Unveiling Transcriptional Memory.

articles.headline: Case of congenital P. falciparum transmission: 3-month-old infant in Portugal infected 13 months after mother's asymptomatic malaria in Angola. Mother remained asymptomatic throughout pregnancy and after. Molecular testing confirmed congenital acquisition, documenting vertical transmission risk from asymptomatic maternal infections
articles.title_original: Imported Malaria and Congenital Acquisition in Infant, Portugal, 2024.

articles.headline: Response to nosocomial P. falciparum transmission case reported in Spain 2024—specific epidemiological data unavailable from title alone
articles.title_original: Nosocomial Transmission of Plasmodium falciparum Malaria, Spain, 2024 (Response).

articles.headline: Nosocomial transmission of P. falciparum malaria documented in Spain 2024—specific epidemiological details unavailable from title alone
articles.title_original: Nosocomial Transmission of Plasmodium falciparum Malaria, Spain, 2024.
articles.authors: Gómez Perales JL, Mendoza AG, Amares MTG

articles.headline: Review of imported malaria diagnosis and management in children presenting to non-endemic countries, with emphasis on travel history assessment, diagnostic challenges including differential diagnosis in acute settings, and management strategies
articles.title_original: Diagnosis and Management of Imported Malaria in the Emergency Department.

articles.headline: CYP2D6 genotyping in 96 suspected malaria patients (Venezuela): approximately 25% intermediate or poor metabolizer phenotypes identified. Of 44 confirmed malaria patients, 9 (20%) experienced P. vivax recurrence. Findings suggest evaluating increased primaquine dosing based on CYP2D6 metabolizer phenotype
articles.title_original: CYP2D6 Genotype and Primaquine Treatment in Patients with Malaria, Venezuela.

articles.headline: P. vivax microgamete identification in Colombian malaria case presenting diagnostic challenge—specific quantitative outcomes unavailable from title alone
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax microgametes in a Colombian malaria case: A diagnostic challenge.

articles.headline: Pilot eave-screening intervention in Cameroon (wire mesh on open eaves): 5.5-fold reduction in entomological inoculation rate from 37.70 to 6.90 infected bites per person-year in improved houses (pre-intervention baseline 73 ib/p/y; malaria prevalence 38.85%). Cost $23.6 per modified house. Community-appreciated integrated vector control strategy effective in forested endemic areas
articles.title_original: A low-cost house eave screening technique complements insecticide-treated nets in reducing indoor human exposure to the bites of anopheles mosquitoes in a malaria endemic area of south forested Cameroon: results from a pilot study.

articles.headline: Cluster-randomized trial (39 villages, 10,750 inhabitants, Burkina Faso/Côte d'Ivoire): long-lasting insecticidal nets plus non-pyrethroid pirimiphos-methyl indoor residual spraying reduced malaria incidence 23% (RR 0.77, 95% CI 0.64–0.93, p=0.0073); LLIN plus intensive behavior change communication reduced 22% (RR 0.78, 95% CI 0.63–0.96, p=0.020) versus LLIN alone over 215,000 person-months. Both supplementary interventions effective without adverse events
articles.title_original: Efficacy of non-pyrethroid indoor residual spraying or intensive behaviour change communication in combination with long-lasting insecticidal nets for malaria control in west Africa: a pragmatic, cluster-randomised, controlled trial.

articles.headline: Applying the Freedom From Infection framework to 1515 facilities and 10,767 survey participants across five malaria-eliminating countries, only 51% of facilities (841/1515 for P. falciparum, 771/1455 for P. vivax) achieved sufficient surveillance sensitivity for high probability of freedom from malaria using routine data. Strong surveillance systems featured access to testing and treatment supplies, recent diagnostics training, and shorter health facility travel times. The FFI model improved estimates when active case detection was combined with passive surveillance, though routine data alone often proved insufficient to ensure elimination confidence
articles.title_original: Evidence-based decision making for malaria elimination applying the Freedom From Infection statistical framework in five malaria eliminating countries: an observational study.

articles.headline: Examines whether behaviour change communication interventions can improve malaria control outcomes
articles.title_original: Can behaviour change communication improve malaria control?

articles.headline: Discusses the critical importance of robust surveillance systems for achieving and maintaining malaria elimination
articles.title_original: The road to malaria elimination: why a robust surveillance system is imperative.

articles.headline: Using pharmacophore modeling, virtual screening, molecular docking, and in vitro studies, researchers identified 28 novel compounds targeting mutant P. falciparum dihydrofolate reductase (pfDHFR) to overcome resistance to pyrimethamine and cycloguanil. The top candidate demonstrated IC₅₀ values of 30.8 μg/mL against 3D7 and 126.1 μg/mL against Dd2 strains, significantly outperforming other compounds (p < 0.05). This integrated computational-experimental approach provides potential alternatives to antifolate-resistant malaria
articles.title_original: Integrated computational strategies for the identification of Plasmodium falciparum dihydrofolate reductase inhibitors.

articles.headline: In vitro studies evaluated cationic lipid stearylamine (SA) against chloroquine-susceptible (Pf3D7) and chloroquine-resistant (PfINDO) P. falciparum blood stages using SYBR-Green I assay. SA showed fast-acting activity with IC₅₀ values of 2.17–1.33 μg/mL for 3D7 and 3.50–1.94 μg/mL for INDO at 12–48 hours, sustained at 72–96 hours. SA induced reactive oxygen species production with minimal hemolysis and demonstrated irreversible parasite growth arrest (IC₅₀ 1.68 μg/mL), suggesting utility as a delivery vehicle for antimalarial drugs
articles.title_original: Cationic Lipid Stearylamine Displays Fast-Acting Antimalarial Action Against In Vitro Blood Stages of Chloroquine Susceptible and Resistant Plasmodium falciparum Strains.
articles.authors: Rajendran V, Gurukkalot K, Naveen NC

articles.headline: Reviews two decades of BioMalPar research contributions to malaria research and control interventions
articles.title_original: BioMalPar at 21 - a coming of age for malaria research and interventions.

articles.headline: A dual in vitro and in silico study of ten 1,4-naphthoquinone-1,2,3-triazole hybrids against atovaquone-resistant (FCR3) P. falciparum found hybrids D12 and D13 displaying 61-fold and 52-fold greater antiplasmodial activity than atovaquone, respectively. Molecular modeling with simulated Y279S cytochrome b mutation explained observed binding interactions. In silico evaluation predicted good oral bioavailability and high gastrointestinal absorption with no significant toxicity risk, identifying D12 and D13 as promising lead candidates
articles.title_original: A dual In vitro and In silico approach to evaluate 1,4-naphthoquinone-1,2,3-triazole hybrids against atovaquone-resistant malaria.

articles.headline: A case series of two pediatric siblings returning from Ethiopia revealed mixed P. falciparum/P. vivax co-infection initially missed by PCR, which failed to detect P. vivax despite positive blood smear results; recurrent symptoms prompted re-testing identifying both species. The case highlights PCR's limited sensitivity for detecting mixed infections in patients from co-endemic regions and the continued diagnostic value of blood smear microscopy in malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Polymerase chain reaction fails to detect mixed malaria infections in siblings from Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Proposes a two-tier strategy for preserving malaria control gains in Africa: (1) expanded species sanitation targeting ecological vulnerabilities of primary Anopheles vectors, and (2) gradual structural resilience through improved housing and environmental management. Future innovations like gene-drive mosquitoes and longer-lasting vaccines could amplify impact. The strategy emphasizes human-centered policies, increased domestic funding, and cross-sector partnerships beyond typical 5-year planning cycles
articles.title_original: Safeguarding malaria control gains in Africa through 'species sanitation' and structural resilience.

articles.headline: Analysis of 100 online travel blogs found 92 contained malaria information; ten preventive drugs were mentioned, five inappropriate for P. falciparum prevention. Pre-travel consultation was recommended by approximately 30% of blogs, with most having commercial sponsors from travel industry or health product sectors. The inconsistent preventive advice warrants development of reliable, lay-accessible malaria prevention guidance by the travel medicine community
articles.title_original: Content analysis of malaria-related health information published in online travel blogs.

articles.headline: A natural experiment across 40 sites in Brazilian Amazon deforestation frontier found landscapes with intermediate forest cover exhibited higher Nyssorhynchus abundance and anopheline infection rates, increasing risk for P. vivax and P. falciparum infections; all 16 human infections detected were asymptomatic. Findings suggest malaria may persist in intermediate forest cover despite antimalarial treatment, requiring integration of radical cure with alternative vector control strategies for outdoor-biting anophelines
articles.title_original: Intermediate forest cover and malaria risk in an Amazon deforestation frontier.

articles.headline: A case series of four patients with spontaneous splenic rupture following recent malaria treatment found one requiring emergency splenectomy while three were hemodynamically stable and managed conservatively; no conservative cases required surgery during follow-up. Malaria-associated SSR, caused by vascular congestion and splenic capsule fragility, is rare but more common in endemic areas and requires high clinical suspicion in malaria patients presenting with abdominal pain
articles.title_original: Case series of spontaneous splenic rupture in patients with malaria: A rare surgical emergency in non-traumatic cases.

articles.headline: Malaria exhibits slower resistance development to monotherapies than HIV and TB. Combination therapy reduces de novo resistance risk, though pre-existing resistance persists, requiring continuous surveillance and innovative management approaches
articles.title_original: A timeline of reckoning: Tracking the historical rise of antimicrobial resistance across HIV, TB, and malaria.

articles.headline: Engineered Anopheles stephensi carrying FREP1Q224 allele confers robust resistance to human and rodent Plasmodium parasites with negligible fitness costs. An allelic-drive system efficiently drives this protective allele into wild populations, rendering them refractory to parasite infection
articles.title_original: Driving a protective allele of the mosquito FREP1 gene to combat malaria.

articles.headline: Mixed-dye LAMP assay for P. knowlesi achieved 100% sensitivity (95% CI: 91.9–100%) and 100% specificity (95% CI: 78.2–100%) on 55 clinical samples versus nested PCR. This rapid, cost-effective diagnostic tool is suitable for resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Establishment of a visual mixed-dye loop mediated isothermal amplification assay for detecting Plasmodium knowlesi.

articles.headline: Of 27 screened mycosporine-like amino acids, 7 showed favorable drug-like properties with no toxicity. Shinorine and mycosporine-hydroxyglutamicol emerged as top candidates with favorable protein-ligand binding and stability, suggesting significant antimalarial potential
articles.title_original: Screening mycosporine-like amino acids (MAAs) as potential inhibitors of a malaria-associated cysteine protease falcipain-2.

articles.headline: In 150 suspected malaria cases, peripheral blood smear detected 30 positives (20%) while RDT detected 20 (13.33%). RDT missed P. falciparum but identified 2 P. vivax cases microscopy missed, demonstrating complementary diagnostic value
articles.title_original: Comparative evaluation of rapid diagnostic test and peripheral smear microscopy for malaria diagnosis.

articles.headline: Serratia ureilytica Su_YN1 did not affect Anopheles aquasalis longevity, blood-feeding, fecundity, or fertility while impairing P. falciparum and P. berghei development. These findings support potential implementation for transmission-blocking control in South America
articles.title_original: Compatibility of Serratia ureylitica Su_YN1, Malaria Transmission-Blocking Bacterium, with the Anopheles aquasalis Vector.

articles.headline: A 48-hour-old neonate with P. falciparum and P. vivax coinfection (parasite density 1,120/μL) achieved complete recovery with intravenous artesunate followed by oral artemisinin-lumefantrine. Mother had received artemether-lumefantrine one week before delivery
articles.title_original: Congenital malaria in a neonate born in a malaria-endemic area: a case report.

articles.headline: First reported coinfection with falciparum malaria and hepatic amoebic abscess (12×11 cm) in pregnancy at 22 weeks' gestation resulted in averted maternal mortality despite fetal death. Management of two parasitic infections requiring distinct therapies was successful in a rural setting
articles.title_original: Management of a Pregnant Woman With Malaria, Stillbirth, and a 12 × 11-cm Amoebic Liver Abscess in a Burundian Hospital: A Case Report.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine plasma concentrations decreased with dolutegravir coadministration in HIV-malaria coinfection (day-7 lumefantrine: 741.5 ng/mL monoinfection vs 426.0 ng/mL with DTG in 261 participants). DTG levels also decreased, suggesting suboptimal drug exposure in coinfected patients
articles.title_original: Drug-drug interaction between dolutegravir and artemether-lumefantrine in HIV and malaria mono- and co-infections: a pharmacogenetic analysis from Ghana.

articles.headline: Analysis of 4,717 articles (1995–2024) reveals fluctuating upward publication trends on HIV-malaria interactions. Major research hotspots include African epidemiology, pathogenic mechanisms, prevention/treatment strategies, antimalarial-antiretroviral drug interactions, and malaria vaccine development for HIV-positive populations
articles.title_original: Unraveling the HIV-malaria interactions: a bibliometric analysis of global research trends and emerging insights.

articles.headline: Of 321 severe malaria patients, 158 (49.2%) developed acute kidney injury with independent risk factors: herbal medicine use (OR=1.509, p=0.045), diabetes (OR=3.375, p=0.044), and respiratory distress (OR=2.758, p=0.034). AKI independently increased mortality risk 3.6-fold (OR=3.584, p=0.004)
articles.title_original: Prevalence, Associated Factors, and Prognosis of Acute Kidney Injury in Severe Malaria Among Sub-Saharan Africans.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 7 RCTs (1,225 children) found artemether significantly improved parasite clearance versus quinine (MD: −7.63 days, 95% CI: −11.06 to −4.21, p<0.0001). Fever clearance, coma clearance, and mortality showed no significant differences between treatments
articles.title_original: Comparison of Artemether Versus Quinine for the Treatment of Cerebral Malaria in Children: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 202 P. falciparum-infected children in Republic of Congo; full-length Pfcsp gene sequencing identified high polymorphism relevant to RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix M vaccines, with 30 haplotypes in N-terminal region, 50 in central repeat, and 76 in C-terminal region showing major mutations K317E (87.4%) and N321K (83.9%); study provides baseline diversity data but calls for immunological assessment of vaccine efficacy
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of circumsporozoite protein of Plasmodium falciparum isolates in children in Brazzaville, Republic of Congo.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 90 African studies (2010–2024) examining climate-malaria interactions; findings show temperature increases accelerate vector and parasite development while rainfall changes alter breeding sites and transmission incidence; authors conclude integrating ecological data and climate-responsive policies into malaria control strategies is essential as traditional interventions become less effective under changing conditions
articles.title_original: Impact of climate change on malaria transmission in Africa: A scoping review of literature.
articles.authors: Chapoterera B, Naidoo K, Marume A

articles.headline: Evaluated ethanol extracts of Allium sativum (garlic) and Azadirachta indica (neem) against Anopheles gambiae and An. pharoensis larvae; A. sativum demonstrated highest efficacy with 90.66% mortality at 250 ppm and LC50 of 39 ppm, showing significant effects on An. gambiae (p = 0.008687) and An. pharoensis (p = 0.02655), while A. indica significant only against An. pharoensis; A. sativum more effective for vector control
articles.title_original: Insecticidal Activities of Plants Extract Against Malaria Vectors in Hadiya Zone, Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Genome-wide study mapping chromatin accessibility, histone modifications, and transcription factor binding during the P. falciparum 48-hour intraerythrocytic developmental cycle; identified both stable and dynamic chromatin states linked to gene expression regulation and grouped transcription factors by chromatin preferences; findings provide mechanistic insight into epigenetic coordination of asexual blood stage development
articles.title_original: Chromatin state dynamics during the Plasmodium falciparum intraerythrocytic development cycle.
articles.authors: Brown AS Jr, Llinás M, Mahony S

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 143 pregnant women in Nigeria; P. falciparum prevalence 61.2% with intermittent preventive treatment (IPTp) alone vs 17.0% with IPTp plus insecticide-treated nets (ITNs), with adjusted logistic regression showing 86% reduced odds of infection in combined group; findings emphasize ITN's critical role in pregnancy malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Do women attending antenatal clinics who use only intermittent preventive treatment (IPTp) have higher Plasmodium falciparum prevalence compared to those who used both IPTp and slept under insecticide-treated nets?

articles.headline: Systematic evaluation of 73 P. falciparum isolates (30 culture-adapted, 43 field subclones) identified PfMYST histone acetyltransferase as sole HAT fully associated with artemisinin ring-stage survival; CRISPR/cas9 and multi-omics validation confirmed PfMYST-PfK13 interplay in resistance and extended PfMYST's role to amodiaquine, pyrimethamine, chloroquine, and pyronaridine
articles.title_original: Epigenetically conferred ring-stage survival in Plasmodium falciparum against artemisinin treatment.

articles.headline: Novel phosphatidylinositol 4-kinase IIIβ (PI4K) inhibitor derived from anticancer compound AZD0156 demonstrated activity against all P. falciparum life cycle stages with lower resistance and toxicity propensity than prior PI4K inhibitors; lead compound 18 achieved ED90 of 4.6 mg/kg in humanized NOD-scid IL-2Rγnull mice infected with P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Novel Inhibitors of Plasmodium Phosphatidylinositol 4-kinase IIIβ with Low Propensity for Resistance: Life Cycle Stage Activity and In Vivo Efficacy in a Humanized Mouse Malaria Infection Model.

articles.headline: Identified Aurora-related kinase 1 (ARK1) as essential for Plasmodium spindle formation and kinetochore dynamics during schizogony and gametogony; ARK1 depletion disrupts mitosis and transmission; interactome analysis revealed ARK1 forms a divergent chromosomal passenger complex lacking canonical Survivin and Borealin; authors propose ARK1-INCENP interface as multistage antimalarial target
articles.title_original: Plasmodium ARK1 regulates spindle formation during atypical mitosis and forms a divergent chromosomal passenger complex.

articles.headline: Three Salacia debilis-derived compounds (SD03, SD04, SD05) targeting P. falciparum enoyl-ACP reductase (PfENR) in type II fatty acid biosynthesis demonstrated binding affinities –8.50 to –8.90 kcal/mol with stable enzyme-ligand complexes in 500 ns molecular dynamics; compounds engaged active site residues (Tyr111, Tyr267, Leu315) matching triclosan, with SD04 as lead candidate based on favorable pharmacokinetics
articles.title_original: Computational elucidation of Plasmodium falciparum enoyl-ACP reductase inhibition by Salacia debilis compounds: adaptive ligand binding drives antimalarial activity.

articles.headline: Analysis of 4,247 Mozambican youth aged 15–24 from 2015 national survey found 6.9% HIV prevalence (9.8% females); female infection associated with age 20–24 (AOR = 2.06), widowed status (AOR = 15.59), separated status (AOR = 2.11), early cohabitation <18 years (AOR = 1.71), Zambézia province (AOR = 3.52), and wealth (AOR = 2.20); higher education protective (AOR = 0.07)
articles.title_original: Factors associated with HIV infection among adolescents and young people in Mozambique: sub-analysis of immunization, malaria and HIV/AIDS indicator survey in Mozambique-IMASIDA 2015.

articles.headline: Dietary L-DOPA in Anopheles mosquitoes enhanced cuticular melanization via non-canonical pathways with transcriptional effects on immunity and increased killing capacity against P. falciparum, while reducing lifespan and altering thermoregulation through enhanced electromagnetic radiation absorption; microbiome bacteria act as dopamine sources for melanization; authors propose L-DOPA as environmentally friendly vector control agent
articles.title_original: Dietary L-3,4-dihydroxyphenylalanine (L-DOPA) augments cuticular melanization in Anopheles mosquitos reducing their lifespan and malaria burden.

articles.headline: Ecological niche modeling of Anopheles species in malaria-free Paraguay (WHO-certified 2018) identified bioclimatic suitability in Humid Chaco, Cerrado, Atlantic Forest, and Mesopotamian savanna ecoregions; precipitation (wettest month 80.4%, driest month 18.4%) primary drivers; An. albitarsis (59.4%) and An. strodei (21.5%) negative for Plasmodium; principal vector An. darlingi absent; cryptic species may be present
articles.title_original: Unravelling Anopheles Dynamics in a Malaria-Free Paraguay: Species Distributions, Bioclimatic Niches, and Implications for Resurgence Risks.

articles.headline: In a 2023-2024 study of 782 residents from Mampikony District, northern Madagascar, malaria prevalence (primarily P. falciparum) increased from 4.5%/8.2% (2023, mRDTs/RT-PCR) to 8.4%/12.4% (2024, mRDTs/RT-PCR). RT-PCR detected significantly more cases than mRDTs, indicating need for more sensitive diagnostics and revised treatment strategies to address growing asymptomatic carriage
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of Malaria in Northern Madagascar as Exemplified by the Mampikony District.

articles.headline: Study reducing a comprehensive malaria control model while preserving essential dynamics, validated via Bayesian MCMC. The simplified system exhibits similar transmission characteristics as the full model with greater flexibility and computational efficiency for evaluating malaria control strategies
articles.title_original: Model reduction and analysis: A case study of a malaria control model.

articles.headline: In a murine malaria model, parasites evolved in vaccinated mice showed reduced targeted immune killing and lower anemia-driven erythropoiesis versus controls, with no evolution at vaccine target antigen (AMA-1) sites. Results suggest parasite virulence evolution occurs through host response modulation rather than antigenic escape
articles.title_original: Understanding the traits underlying vaccine-driven virulence evolution in malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Jeong Y, Kamiya T, Mideo N

articles.headline: In a murine Plasmodium berghei ANKA cerebral malaria model, neutrophil depletion protected mice from neuropathology with reduced CD8+ T cell infiltration in the brain. Neutrophil extracellular traps (NETs) activate dendritic cells via TLR9/NF-κB signaling; inhibiting NETs with Sivelestat effectively prevented cerebral malaria progression
articles.title_original: TLR9/NF-κB-mediated dendritic cell activation by neutrophil extracellular traps drives pathogenesis in experimental cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Title only available (abstract not provided). Focus: Yemen's malaria crisis and implications for Gulf Cooperation Council countries
articles.title_original: Yemen's Malaria Crisis and its Implications for the GCC (Gulf Cooperation Council) Countries.

articles.headline: From a 9-year-old malaria-exposed child, researchers identified broadly reactive human monoclonal antibodies (clonal family) targeting P. falciparum PfEMP1 CIDRα1 domains with potent EPCR binding inhibition across multiple variants. Findings support that broadly inhibitory antibodies against severe malaria-associated PfEMP1 target conserved epitopes commonly developed in malaria-exposed populations
articles.title_original: Identification of broadly inhibitory anti-PfEMP1 antibodies by mass spectrometry sequencing of plasma IgG from a malaria-exposed child.
articles.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A

articles.headline: In an Indian cohort study (398 patients), plasma levels of hsa-miR-21-5p, hsa-miR-150-5p, and hsa-miR-3158-3p distinguished cerebral malaria (CM) from other malaria types (p<0.0005). A random forest machine learning model using these microRNAs achieved 83% sensitivity, 100% specificity, and 92% balanced accuracy for early CM diagnosis
articles.title_original: Potential of microRNAs as diagnostic markers for distinguishing malaria severity in samples from an Indian cohort.

articles.headline: P. falciparum modeling with 25 drug-resistance loci showed 80-97% of multi-drug resistant (MDR) recombinants emerged from single-host multi-clonal infections. Over 15 years, triple artemisinin-combination therapies (ACT) showed largest treatment failure reductions and longest delays to artemisinin resistance (1% threshold), outperforming multiple first-line therapies and ACT cycling
articles.title_original: Effects of recombination on multi-drug resistance evolution in Plasmodium falciparum malaria.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 68 papers (1969-2022) identified 115 European malaria cases: 68 airport/seaport cases (case fatality 13.24%) and 47 autochthonous cases (case fatality 2.13%). High fatality rates in airport/seaport malaria attributed to delayed/missed diagnoses; rising temperatures and human migration may facilitate reemergence
articles.title_original: Airport/seaport and autochthonous malaria in Europe from 1969 to 2022: A systematic review.

articles.headline: Among 398 pregnant women in Somalia, 59.8% tested malaria-positive, predominantly P. falciparum (91.6% of cases).
