Second and third trimester pregnancy and indoor residual spraying (IRS) significantly reduced malaria odds (OR 0.524, p=0.044; OR 0.442, p=0.007; OR 0.192, p<0.001 respectively)
articles.title_original: The Prevalence of Malaria Among Pregnant Women at a Tertiary Care Hospital in Somalia: A Cross-Sectional Study.

articles.headline: Using time-lapse imaging of live P. falciparum parasites, signal-dependent exocytosis of egress-specific vesicles began 3 hours before merozoite egress, with parasitophorous vacuolar membrane (PVM) rupturing at a single site. Knockdown of calcium-binding protein PfERC inhibited this exocytosis, demonstrating PfERC's requirement for egress vesicle trafficking
articles.title_original: Spatiotemporal dynamics of signal dependent exocytosis and parasitophorous vacuolar membrane rupture during Plasmodium falciparum egress.

articles.headline: Review of P. falciparum protein export mechanisms via PEXEL-mediated and non-PEXEL-mediated (PEXEL-negative exported proteins, PNEPs) pathways during infection of hepatocytes and red blood cells, discussing structural diversity, function, and remaining mechanistic questions
articles.title_original: An overview of the interconnectedness of the PEXEL and non-PEXEL mediated protein export in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Prospective cohort of 380 febrile children <5 years in Kenya (1,004 screened) found P. falciparum at 21.4%, dengue at 8.9%, and coinfection at 7.5%, with distribution significantly associated by location (X²=19.45, P<0.001); irrigated agriculture around Lake Victoria favors vector multiplication and emerging dengue-malaria coinfections
articles.title_original: Correlation and epidemiologic distribution of emerging coinfections of Plasmodium falciparum and dengue virus among febrile children in malaria-endemic zones in western Kenya.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 634 rural Ethiopians (≥18 years) showed only 44.2% demonstrated good understanding of malaria signs, symptoms, and prevention; education, religion, marital status, and household composition were associated with better knowledge, indicating low overall malaria literacy in endemic areas
articles.title_original: Malaria signs, symptoms, prevention knowledge and its associated factors among rural Ethiopians.
articles.journal: Afr J Prim Health Care Fam Med

articles.headline: Analysis of Ghana Demographic and Health Surveys (2003–2022) revealed ITN use increased 58.1%→62.9% and IPTp rose 0.85%→60%, but ITN remained pro-poor (ENCI −0.04 to −0.32) while IPTp shifted pro-rich; 85% of ITN inequality among pregnant women occurred within SES groups, indicating targeted equity strategies are needed despite overall coverage gains
articles.title_original: Between-and within-socioeconomic groups temporal inequality in the uptake of malaria prevention strategies among pregnant women and under-five children in Ghana (2003-2022).

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 289 P. falciparum-infected adults in Cameroon found diabetes in 21.7% (26/120) of severe vs. 6.0% (10/169) of non-severe cases (p<0.001); metabolic syndrome in 32.5% (39/120) severe vs. 16.3% (27/166) non-severe (p=0.001); multivariate analysis showed diabetes (aOR=3.24; 95% CI 1.38–7.62) and metabolic syndrome (aOR=3.18; 95% CI 1.60–6.37) independently associated with severe malaria
articles.title_original: Diabetes and metabolic syndrome in adults with malaria and associations with severe disease: results from two tertiary hospitals in Cameroon.

articles.headline: Hospital-based comparative cross-sectional of 196 HIV-infected adults (98 HIV alone, 98 HIV-malaria coinfected) in Ethiopia found statistically significant differences in RBC, hemoglobin, and hematocrit (p<0.05); anemia prevalence 44.9% (88/196) overall, rising to 61.2% (60/98) in coinfected (p<0.001), with negative correlation between viral load and hematological parameters
articles.title_original: Hematological Profiles of Adults Coinfected With HIV and Malaria Receiving Highly Active Antiretroviral Therapy at Bonga Gebretsadik Shawo General Hospital, Southwest Ethiopia: A Comparative Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.authors: Demango F, Tadasa E, Kiya GT

articles.headline: Label-free digital holographic microscopy method using phase-driven feature classification detected P. falciparum-infected RBCs with 61% sensitivity (improved from 48% with feature-specific approach), 98–100% specificity, and 90% compared to Giemsa staining, offering real-time automated diagnosis without staining
articles.title_original: Enhanced detection of malaria infected red blood cells through phase driven classification.

articles.headline: Post-distribution household survey in Dominican Republic malaria foci found LLIN sleeping-space coverage 64–63%, retention 88.4–80.9%, and access 58.8–65.4%, but actual use only 32.3–50.5%; 65–86% of LLINs washed with aggressive products at frequencies exceeding recommended protocols, with holes not repaired despite 88.9–96.4% remaining serviceable
articles.title_original: Insights from LLIN post-distribution monitoring surveys in the malaria transmission foci of the Dominican Republic: implications for quantification and distribution strategies.

articles.headline: In vitro study of pepstatin A (aspartyl protease inhibitor) at 100 µM showed 47% inhibition of P. falciparum asexual stage and 73% inhibition of early-stage gametocytes with morphological defects (chromatin condensation, vacuolization, hemozoin clumping), but no effect on late-stage gametocytes, suggesting potential for reducing disease severity and transmission
articles.title_original: Aspartyl protease inhibition interferes with Plasmodium falciparum asexual blood-stage and early gametocyte development.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional entomological survey collecting 41,747 mosquitoes in Accra found Anopheles 52.54% (20,945/39,863) of host-seeking collections; irrigated urban farming sites had highest An. gambiae s.l. abundance (48.63%, 10,466/21,520); indoor sporozoite infectivity 4.56% (16/351) significantly exceeded outdoor 3.78% (9/238) (χ²=6.78, P=0.009), with Tuba site reaching 6.41% (10/156)
articles.title_original: Urban malaria in Accra, Ghana: transmission risk and influence of irrigated vegetable farms.

articles.headline: Mixed-methods study of 528 households and 20 key informants across 3 Kenyan counties found 72% had received malaria services from community health volunteers, with 85% satisfaction; facilitators included commodity availability and support supervision, while barriers included stockouts of mRDTs and antimalarials, myths about bed nets, and inaccessible roads
articles.title_original: Facilitators and barriers of Community Case management of Malaria implementation in Homabay, Busia and Kakamega Counties, Kenya.

articles.headline: Hospital-based cross-sectional of 314 pregnant women in Cameroon found 14.6% (46/314) P. falciparum parasitemia; bushes around houses increased risk (OR=2.40, p=0.03), while ceilings (OR=0.20, p<0.01), IPTp (OR=0.23, p<0.01), and window screens (OR=0.14, p=0.01) were protective; geometric mean parasite density highest in 2nd trimester (2,190/µL, p=0.016)
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of malaria in pregnant women attending antenatal consultation in Dschang, West Cameroon.

articles.headline: Mouse model study comparing Indigofera oblongifolia leaf extract with silver nanoparticles versus chloroquine 10 mg/kg for P. chabaudi lung infection found IOLE treatment significantly restored normal lung architecture and reduced alveolar congestion, edema, and necrosis, whereas chloroquine showed limited efficacy with persistent inflammatory pathology and cytokine expression
articles.title_original: Morphometric analysis and immunobiological investigation of Indigofera oblongifolia on the infected lung with Plasmodium chabaudi.

articles.headline: One canopy swab detected Plasmodium DNA (Ct 28.7, 29.23) via multiplex qPCR assay targeting five human-infecting species (LOD 0.2 parasites/μL) during a proof-of-concept 24-hour drone-based environmental DNA survey in the Amazon rainforest. The end-to-end workflow took 1.5 hours per assay without cold-chain logistics
articles.title_original: Real-time malaria detection in the Amazon rainforest via drone-collected eDNA and portable qPCR.

articles.headline: A case-control study (197 cases, 557 controls, May–August 2023) in Zanzibar identified occupational malaria risk in urban districts: night watchmen/police (OR 5.3, p<0.001), construction workers (OR 3.0, p=0.007), and farmers (OR 1.6, p=0.01). Night-time activities (OR 2.8) and outdoor meals (OR 2.0) also increased risk, while net use was protective only in rural areas (p=0.015)
articles.title_original: Occupations at high risk for malaria in Zanzibar: a case-control study, may-august 2023.

articles.headline: During Ethiopia's 2022 An. stephensi outbreak in Dire Dawa, 6.21% of 160 mosquitoes carried P. falciparum (African haplotype only at Pfs47 positions 707/725) and 4.37% carried P. vivax. An amino acid change in P47Rec was identified as a potential molecular marker for An. stephensi outbreak propensity
articles.title_original: Genetic surveillance of Plasmodium-Anopheles compatibility markers during Anopheles stephensi associated malaria outbreak.

articles.headline: Eight-generation laboratory exposures of An. stephensi to high temperature (35.5±1°C) and low nutrition (33 mg/100 larvae) increased P. falciparum oocyst infection rates 1.46- and 1.15-fold versus controls. Development times were 7.5 days (HT), 11 days (NT), and 25 days (LT); paracentric chromosomal inversions appeared in the nutrition-stressed line
articles.title_original: Effects of temperature and nutrition stress on Anopheles stephensi, an Indian urban malaria vector.

articles.headline: Data concordance between DHIS2 and source documents at 12 Ugandan health facilities fell below WHO standards (≥80%): 36.7% for suspected cases (95% CI 25.2–49.9), 53.6% for cases tested, and 56.8% for test positivity rates. Presence of HMIS personnel (aPR 1.41) and timely reporting (aPR 1.15) improved concordance
articles.title_original: Concordance of data on key malaria indicators between DHIS2 and source documents, and influencing factors at public primary health facilities in eastern Uganda: a mixed methods study.

articles.headline: An age-structured, temperature-dependent malaria model using partial differential equations fitted six years of Jimma zone prevalence data with R² = 0.68 (adjusted R² = 0.63), demonstrating superior predictive accuracy versus existing models for temperature-dependent transmission trends
articles.title_original: Age-structured malaria model with temperature-dependent dynamics and optimal control analysis within a partial differential equation framework.

articles.headline: Over 20 years in Tanzania, malaria incidence declined from 0.347 (95% CI 0.336–0.357) to 0.118 (0.114–0.122) coinciding with insecticide-treated bednets increasing from coverage 0.037 to 0.496. Higher temperature (OR 1.06–1.13) and vegetation index (OR 5.28–6.22) increased malaria risk, indicating persistent climate impacts despite overall progress
articles.title_original: Modelling the impact of climatic and environmental variables on malaria incidence in Tanzania: Implications for achieving the WHO's 2030 Targets.

articles.headline: A 29-year-old returning from Côte d'Ivoire to Colombia presented with P. ovale malaria, initially misidentified as P. falciparum by microscopy; PCR confirmation enabled appropriate treatment with chloroquine and primaquine (15 mg base daily × 28 days) for radical cure without adverse effects, highlighting the need for molecular diagnostics in imported cases
articles.title_original: Imported plasmodium ovale malaria from Côte d'Ivoire: a case report.
articles.authors: Plata-Puyana JD, Vanegas Beltrán JK, Martínez-Ávila MC

articles.headline: A qualitative study of malaria patients in Mhinga village, South Africa (purposive sampling, semi-structured interviews) revealed that participants experienced complicated symptoms after discharge from both clinic and hospital despite initial treatment, suggesting inadequate post-discharge management and follow-up care
articles.title_original: Post-discharge experiences of Malaria patients in a Vhembe district village, South Africa.
articles.authors: Kwinda W, Luhalima TR, Mudzweda AD

articles.headline: Over a 10-year interrupted time-series in Ghana, indoor residual spraying (IRS) reduced P. malariae from 13.7% to 1.4% and P. ovale from 5.7% to 0.4%, but both rebounded after IRS discontinuation (P. malariae 2.9%, P. ovale 4.0% in 2017). With sustained seasonal malaria chemoprevention, P. malariae continued increasing (5.8%–7.4%) while P. ovale declined (1.3%–2.6%)
articles.title_original: Longitudinal analysis of the prevalence of minor Plasmodium spp. in the reservoir of asymptomatic infections through sequential interventions in Northern Sahelian Ghana.

articles.headline: A review of Ethiopia's malaria challenges identifies invasive An. stephensi as a major new threat, transmitting both P. falciparum and P. vivax with full resistance to all four insecticide classes. Coupled with widespread drug-resistant Plasmodium species and civil war-driven population displacement causing malaria case surges, these factors constitute major impediments to Ethiopia's 2030 elimination target
articles.title_original: Challenges to Malaria Elimination in Ethiopia by 2030: A Review.

articles.headline: Experimental hut studies of wild and colonized An. darlingi in the Peruvian Amazon revealed bimodal biting at 1800 h and 2200 h, with wild mosquito house entry peaking 2200–2300 h. Both populations preferred resting in low-door locations (29.8%–32.7%) and windows (11.8%–14.0%), while interception trap capture rates were lower than human landing collection
articles.title_original: Experimental hut resting and entrance behaviour of Anopheles darlingi from Zungarococha, a malaria endemic community in Loreto, Northern Peruvian Amazon.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 34 studies (4,005 P. falciparum isolates, 2009-2019) found pooled pfpm2/3 gene amplification prevalence of 16.0% (95% CI 8.0-26.0%), significantly higher in Asia (25.0%) than Africa (8.0%) and increasing over time (11% pre-2016 vs 19% 2016-2020). Pfpm2/3 amplification confers piperaquine resistance and drives artemisinin combination therapy failure; authors call for urgent monitoring and control of partner drug resistance to prevent total ACT resistance
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium falciparum plasmepsin2/3 gene duplication in Africa and Asia: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Longitudinal analysis of 1,088 primary health facilities across 23 Sindh districts (2012-2024) documented malaria incidence of 92 per 1,000 annually (~1.28 million cases/year; positivity rate 12.3%), with six districts carrying >53% burden and distinct seasonal peaks. Since 2022 floods, incidence has doubled and persists through 2024, highlighting the need for targeted district-level interventions and flood-related disruption recovery
articles.title_original: Subnational malaria burden in Sindh, Pakistan: over a decade of evidence for tailored strategies.

articles.headline: Molecular docking of 519 compounds against P. falciparum PfSUB2 and PfTRAMP invasion proteins identified compounds 139,974,934 and 154,414,021 as superior inhibitors compared to the standard PMSF, with compound 155,204,487 demonstrating dual-target potential. These candidates merit development as antimalarial drugs targeting asexual blood-stage growth and RBC invasion
articles.title_original: Targeting invasion-associated proteins PfSUB2 and PfTRAMP in Plasmodium falciparum: identification of potential inhibitors via molecular docking.

articles.headline: Mathematical model of malaria transmission using fractional derivatives identified complex dynamics including bifurcation, quasi-periodic patterns, chaos, and multistability, offering theoretical insight into transmission nonlinearities. Findings provide a framework for more accurate predictive modeling but are computational rather than empirically validated
articles.title_original: Complex travelling wave solutions of fractional nonlinear coupled malaria model: bifurcation, chaos, and multistability.

articles.headline: Longitudinal genotyping of P. vivax isolates from Santa Emilia, Peru (16 microsatellites; n=221 in 2015-2016, n=139 in 2013) showed 56% prevalence with 44% submicroscopic infections, extremely high genetic diversity, and temporal population differentiation (4 populations detected). Polyclonal infections decreased from >40% (2013-2015) to 29% (2016); the persistence of high diversity and clonal structure poses significant challenges to local elimination efforts
articles.title_original: High Prevalence, Genetic Diversity and Temporal Differentiation of Plasmodium vivax in a Remote Hard-to-Reach Community from the Peruvian Amazon Region.

articles.headline: Whole-genome sequencing of P. falciparum from Bioko Island's 2019 Malaria Indicator Survey revealed unexpectedly high genetic diversity and polyclonal infections despite elimination efforts, with genetic flow predominantly from Gabon. This pattern indicates sustained parasite importation is hampering island elimination; sustained progress requires targeted traveler interventions and cross-border malaria control collaboration
articles.title_original: Regular Plasmodium falciparum importation onto Bioko Island, Equatorial Guinea, hampers malaria elimination from the island.

articles.headline: Discovery of an atypical Arp2/3 complex in P. falciparum consisting of five divergent subunits plus a kinetochore-associated factor, essential for kinetochore-spindle attachment during male gametogenesis. Disruption of this complex or actin polymerization causes defective DNA segregation and blocks parasite development in the mosquito, identifying novel vulnerability in transmission
articles.title_original: Discovery of an Atypical Arp2/3 Complex in Malaria Parasites Sheds New Light on Nuclear Actin.
articles.authors: Hentzschel F, Frischknecht F, Marti M

articles.headline: Comprehensive benchmarking of identity-by-descent (IBD) callers (hmmIBD, isoRelate, hap-IBD, phased IBD, Refined IBD) across simulations and empirical P. falciparum genomes showed that high recombination rates cause high false-negative rates for short segments, while hmmIBD uniquely provides unbiased effective population size estimates. Parameter optimization and context-specific evaluation are essential for accurate genomic surveillance and targeted interventions
articles.title_original: Benchmarking and optimization of methods for the detection of identity-by-descent in high-recombining Plasmodium falciparum genomes.
articles.authors: Guo B, Takala-Harrison S, O'Connor TD

articles.headline: Interrupted time-series analysis of malaria diagnoses in Lambaréné hospitals (2018–early 2023) showed an initial pandemic-related drop (−47.32, P=0.031) followed by resurgence above pre-pandemic levels, attributed to disrupted control activities and waning tool efficacy. Maternal malaria incidence did not increase, suggesting pregnancy-specific control remained relatively stable despite broader malaria resurgence
articles.title_original: Malaria amidst the COVID-19 pandemic in Gabon: an application of autoregressive integrated moving average (ARIMA) models within an interrupted time series (ITS) framework to hospital-based data.

articles.headline: Molecular screening of free-ranging wildlife in the Peruvian Amazon (44 coatis, 66 armadillos) detected Plasmodium DNA in 2 coatis (4.7%) with sequences identified as P. vivax/P. simium and P. malariae/P. brasilianum; no infections in armadillos. Findings indicate coatis may serve as incidental hosts for diverse Plasmodium parasites, with implications for sylvatic malaria ecology and zoonotic risk
articles.title_original: Molecular Screening of Plasmodium spp. in Free-Living Ring-Tailed Coatis (Nasua nasua) and Nine-Banded Armadillos (Dasypus novemcinctus) in the Peruvian Amazon.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 39 studies in sub-Saharan African urban settings found overall malaria prevalence of 23.01% (95% CI 19.42–26.59; I²=99.8%). Travel history (POR=4.17, 95% CI 2.33–7.46) and household livestock (POR=4.1, 95% CI 1.62–10.39) were significant risk factors. High country-level heterogeneity and persistent transmission in cities underscore the need for integrated, socio-economically tailored urban malaria control strategies
articles.title_original: Urban malaria and population mobility in sub-Saharan Africa: systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: WHO bottle bioassay of Ethiopian An. stephensi populations showed 100% mortality to broflanilide and clothianidin at 24 hours, and 100% to chlorfenapyr by 48 hours, indicating full susceptibility to three novel chemical insecticides despite resistance to pyrethroids and carbamates. Pyriproxyfen achieved 100% oviposition inhibition, supporting its use for larval control of this invasive vector
articles.title_original: Susceptibility of the invasive malaria vector Anopheles stephensi in Ethiopia to novel chemical insecticides and insect growth regulator.

articles.headline: Time-series analysis in Uganda (IMHDSS) found delayed associations between climate and malaria mortality: rainfall >646mm increased mortality at 11-12 week lag; temperature 25.2-29.9°C increased mortality at 5-11 week lag. Children <5 and 5-14 years showed greater sensitivity to temperature than rainfall; male children 5-14 years more vulnerable than females
articles.title_original: Climate-driven malaria mortality among children in malaria-endemic areas of Uganda.

articles.headline: Bayesian geostatistical models predicting P. falciparum prevalence in Senegalese children <5 incorporating vulnerability and hazard factors achieved median R²=0.64, with vulnerability-only models outperforming hazard-only models. MIS-based models (2020-21) performed poorly (R²=0.13) due to dry-season data collection
articles.title_original: Integrating vulnerability and hazard in malaria risk mapping: the elimination context of Senegal.

articles.headline: Volumetric MRI of C57BL/6J mice infected with P. berghei ANKA revealed experimental cerebral malaria more severe in males than females, with sex-dependent differences in brain swelling and lesion distribution across cortex, pons, colliculi, and midbrain
articles.title_original: Characterization of experimental cerebral malaria by volumetric MRI A comparative study across the sexes.
articles.authors: Comino Garcia Muñoz A, et al.

articles.headline: One year post-distribution in western Kenya, PBO-LLINs showed higher ownership (96% vs 89%), usage (87.6% vs 80.2%), lower attrition (7.6% vs 17.9%), and lower malaria prevalence (29.0% vs 34.7% by qPCR, PR=0.84, 95% CI 0.65-1.03) compared to pyrethroid-LLINs in cross-sectional survey of 380 households
articles.title_original: Net ownership, utilization and malaria burden in the context of Piperonyl butoxide-LLINs intervention in western Kenya.

articles.headline: Review article emphasizing that understanding anopheline mosquito biology—life stages, incubation period, blood-seeking behavior—is essential for designing effective vector control; notes vector control achieved 65% reduction in malaria cases between 2000-2015
articles.title_original: Know your enemy: understanding mosquito biology to advance malaria elimination in Africa.
articles.authors: Zembere K, Kalonde PK, Bickton FM

articles.headline: Case-control study (54 untreated P. falciparum cases vs 54 controls) in Ethiopia found malaria associated with elevated creatinine (1.32±0.29 vs 0.92±0.32 mg/dL, p<0.05) and urea (39.8±8.34 vs 25.78±7.97 mg/dL, p<0.05), and decreased sodium (132.15±3.96 vs 137.11±3.11 mmol/L, p<0.05) and potassium (3.44±0.40 vs 3.94±0.39 mmol/L, p<0.05)
articles.title_original: Evaluation of serum electrolytes and kidney function among untreated malaria (Plasmodium falciparum) patients in health centers, Konso zone, South Ethiopia.

articles.headline: CUT&Tag and DiBioCUT&Tag methods enable genome-wide profiling of P. falciparum chromatin-associated proteins from as few as 10,000 nuclei, producing reproducible heterochromatin profiles concordant with ChIP-seq while reducing GC-content artifacts and sample requirements
articles.title_original: CUT&Tag and DiBioCUT&Tag enable investigation of the AT-rich epigenome of Plasmodium falciparum from low-input samples.

articles.headline: Semi-field study with female A. gambiae demonstrated low-nutrient mosquitoes were 2-3 fold more likely to host-seek and 5-10 fold more likely to seek hosts at earlier times of day compared to well-nourished mosquitoes, indicating nutritional status modulates biting behavior and ITN efficacy
articles.title_original: Biting time of day in malaria mosquitoes is modulated by nutritional status.

articles.headline: Among 2,381 RDTs collected during Grand Magal of Touba, 213 tested positive (8.9%) with 194 confirmed by qPCR (8.1%); Bioline™ achieved 97.3% specificity and 71.1% PPV while ParaHIT® achieved 92.5% specificity and 26.9% PPV, validating use of RDT genetic material for PCR diagnosis
articles.title_original: [Use of genetic material extracted from rapid malaria diagnostic tests using PCR during the Grand Magal of Touba in Senegal].

articles.headline: Among 2,381 RDTs collected during Grand Magal of Touba mass gathering, qPCR identified 13 cases (0.5%) of tick-borne relapsing fever borreliosis, including 2 malaria-borreliosis co-infections; no DENV, Bartonella spp., or C. burnetti detected
articles.title_original: [Identification of tick-borne borreliosis cases by qPCR from rapid malaria diagnostic tests during the Grand Magal of Touba in Senegal].

articles.headline: Case report of 20-year-old female with simultaneous type 1 diabetes inaugural diabetic ketoacidosis (Glasgow score 9, hyperglycemia 6 g/dL), acute stage E pancreatitis, and malaria who achieved favorable outcome with insulin, rehydration, and antimalarial therapy
articles.title_original: Convergence of diabetic ketoacidosis, acute pancreatitis, and malaria: A case report.

articles.headline: Structure-activity relationship study of MMV008138 (P. falciparum IspD inhibitor) generated 19 benzo-ring variants; only 3 maintained asexual blood stage activity within 3-fold of parent. One analog improved microsomal stability but did not improve plasma exposure or achieve oral efficacy in mouse malaria model
articles.title_original: Benzo-ring modification on Malaria Box hit MMV008138: effects on antimalarial potency and microsomal stability.

articles.headline: Literature review synthesizing evidence on Imatinib, a tyrosine kinase inhibitor originally used for chronic myeloid leukemia, as a potential antimalarial agent. Across 9 studies with varying designs, Imatinib reduced parasite density and showed synergism with artesunate, with a favorable safety profile and no reported adverse drug-related events. Authors recommend randomized controlled trials with larger sample sizes and investigations of combination therapies with other antimalarials
articles.title_original: The emerging role of Imatinib in malaria management: a review of evidence and future directions.

articles.headline: Single-cell RNA sequencing of 241,837 cells from liver, spleen, and blood of Plasmodium berghei ANKA-infected mice revealed that artesunate modulates pathogenic CD8+GZMB+ T lymphocytes via the JAK2-STAT3 signaling pathway, reducing their frequency and inflammatory response. This study identifies a novel immunoregulatory mechanism of artesunate and provides insights into malaria-associated hepatic immunopathogenesis
articles.title_original: Single-cell profiling reveals immunoregulation of artemisinin on CD8(+)GZMB(+) T cells via JAK2-STAT3 in malaria-infected mice.

articles.headline: Scoping review of 32 studies from 13 African countries found that larviciding significantly reduced mosquito vector density and, in some cases, malaria incidence when implemented through community engagement strategies. Community engagement and capacity building were critical to program success; however, logistical barriers, lack of awareness, and financial constraints remain significant implementation challenges requiring technological innovation and strengthened monitoring systems
articles.title_original: Assessing the role of community involvement and capacity building in larviciding applications for malaria control in Africa: A scoping review.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis

articles.headline: Ten-year retrospective analytic review of imported and non-travel-related malaria cases in Europe. [Complete abstract not available; specific quantitative results and conclusions cannot be extracted from the provided excerpt.]
articles.title_original: Imported and non-travel related cases of malaria in Europe - a ten-year analytic retrospective review.
articles.authors: Vanitha R, Abelein F, Stoyanova KP

articles.headline: Experimental infections of Anopheles darlingi with field-derived Plasmodium vivax produced two distinct oocyst-burden phenotypes. Low-infection mosquitoes mounted early metabolic restriction via detoxification, redox regulation, and amino acid catabolism, accompanied by reduced bacterial diversity with enrichment of Enterobacteriales and Pseudomonadales. High-infection mosquitoes showed permissive metabolic states and expanded Flavobacteriales, suggesting vector competence to P. vivax results from dynamic metabolic-microbial interactions rather than fixed immune traits
articles.title_original: Metabolic reprogramming and gut microbiota ecology drive divergent Plasmodium vivax infection outcomes in Anopheles darlingi.

articles.headline: A qPCR-based Growth, Resistance, and Recovery assay (GRRA) in 96-well format was developed and validated for rapid phenotyping of Plasmodium falciparum artemisinin resistance, measuring growth (6–96 h), resistance (120 h), and recovery (120–192 h post-drug exposure). Resistance at 120 h showed the most consistent ring-stage susceptibility phenotype; recovery identified additional drug responses not captured by replicative viability. Direct-from-blood methodology enables high-throughput surveillance with dried blood spot compatibility
articles.title_original: Measuring growth, resistance, and recovery after artemisinin treatment of Plasmodium falciparum in a single semi-high-throughput assay.

articles.headline: Cohort study in rural Guinea (239 households, ages 1–19 years, 9-month follow-up) found significant seasonal and socioeconomic disparities in malaria transmission. Children in households with heads aged 50–77 years had 3.44-fold higher odds during the dry season; adolescents in middle-aged-headed households had 15.78-fold higher odds in the rainy season. Secondary education and older household head age provided strong malaria prevention benefits, but these were primarily captured by wealthier households
articles.title_original: Inequity in the transmission of malaria infection among children and adolescents: a cohort study in rural Guinea.

articles.headline: Qualitative study of seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine in children aged 3–59 months in South Sudan (97 participants) found high acceptability driven by perceived effectiveness, door-to-door delivery strategy, and trusted community health workers. Key implementation barriers included environmental obstacles, inadequate worker remuneration, and difficulty reaching all eligible children, though stakeholders expressed strong desire for SMC continuation and expansion
articles.title_original: "We witnessed medicines help our children, then we became proud": experiences and acceptability of seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Aweil, South Sudan.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 41 studies (15 randomized controlled trials, 26 observational studies) from 2012–2023 found single low-dose primaquine (SLDPQ; 0.25 mg/kg) co-administered with artemisinin-based combination therapies to be safe and well-tolerated in Plasmodium falciparum treatment across African settings, including in glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase-deficient individuals and children under 5 years. Eight RCTs confirmed significant reductions in gametocyte carriage; primary implementation barriers include persistent misconceptions about primaquine toxicity and absence of paediatric formulations
articles.title_original: Single low-dose primaquine for malaria control in Africa: a systematic review of safety, efficacy and implementation barriers.

articles.headline: Cohort study of 439 children aged 0.5–5 years across three Ugandan districts (low-, intermediate-, and high-transmission settings) from 2011–2016 examined the relationship between household-level entomological inoculation rate (EIR) and individual malaria incidence. A significant positive association was observed only at the intermediate-transmission site (Kanungu: incidence rate ratio 1.09, 95% credible interval 1.04–1.14), but not in low-transmission Jinja or high-transmission Tororo, indicating site-specific transmission dynamics affect EIR-incidence relationships
articles.title_original: Relating household entomological measures to individual malaria risk.

articles.headline: Two-cycle audit at Dongola Specialized Hospital, Sudan (Cycle 1: n=42, Cycle 2: n=39) assessed physician adherence to the 2023 national malaria case management protocol, followed by targeted training interventions. Protocol adherence increased from 30.95% to 89.74% (58.79% improvement); correct artesunate injection technique from 30.95% to 79.49%; second-line treatment for uncomplicated malaria in pregnancy from 23.81% to 64.10%; and intermittent preventive treatment with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine to 92.31% (49.45% improvement)
articles.title_original: Assessing Physicians' Adherence to the 2023 Sudan Malaria Case Management Protocol.

articles.headline: Machine learning transportability models generalized findings from a cluster-randomized trial in Senegal (55% malaria incidence reduction with mass drug administration in seasonal malaria chemoprevention areas) to 116 non-trial Communes. Estimated incidence reductions ranged from 36–65% in 74 non-trial Communes, with larger reductions in areas with higher precipitation, denser vegetation, and lower temperatures. Mass drug administration effects were not sustained in the post-intervention year in non-trial Communes
articles.title_original: Using transportability methods to map the local effectiveness of mass drug administration for malaria in Senegal.

articles.headline: Case of 51-year-old Chinese man with severe P. falciparum malaria presenting with markedly elevated von Willebrand factor and normal ADAMTS13 activity, initially mimicking thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (TTP) but diagnosed by peripheral blood smear and successfully treated with intravenous artesunate. Demonstrates diagnostic overlap between malaria and TTP, highlighting the importance of differential laboratory diagnosis for timely, lifesaving treatment
articles.title_original: Severe Plasmodium Falciparum Malaria Mimicking Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic Purpura (TTP): A Case Report From China.

articles.headline: Multilevel logistic regression analysis of 50,545 weighted samples from 17 Sub-Saharan African countries (2016–2022) found pooled malaria-anemia comorbidity prevalence of 39.00% (95% CI 29.00–49.00) among pregnant women. Rural residence (OR=2.73, 95% CI 2.54–2.93), poor wealth (OR=2.37, 95% CI 2.18–2.57), and regional factors (South Africa OR=18.76, 95% CI 17.3–20.4) were major determinants. Authors recommend targeted interventions in rural areas and region-specific strategies, particularly in West, Central, and South Sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Malaria-anemia comorbidity and its determinants among pregnant women in high- and moderate-malaria-risk countries in Sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.headline: Analysis of 5,522,650 confirmed malaria cases in Togo (2013–2017) by health district and target group. Children <5 years represented 36.6%, children ≥5 and adults 58.5%, and pregnant women 4.9% of cases. A consistent lag of ≥1 month occurred between precipitation onset and epidemic start across all groups. Savanes region showed later, more intense epidemics; Maritime and Lome-commune regions had short or undetectable patterns. Authors recommend district-level tailoring of malaria control interventions
articles.title_original: Identifying malaria epidemic periods in Togo by health district and target group: a generalised additive model approach.

articles.headline: Mixed-methods evaluation (2021–2023) of community health worker (CHW) involvement in an Attractive Targeted Sugar Bait (ATSB) trial in Zambia. Community acceptance was 97.4% (626/643); CHW fidelity 97.1% (67/69). Trial practices aligned with best practices in training and incentive structures but revealed gender imbalance (32% female CHWs in year 2) and challenges in community involvement and coordination. Adequate funding and rigorous implementation supported adherence to CHW best practices
articles.title_original: Comparative analysis of the use of Community Health Workers while deploying the Attractive Targeted Sugar Bait (ATSB) for malaria control in Western Province, Zambia.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional baseline survey and randomized controlled trial in Tanzanian children (5–15 years; Feb 2019–Dec 2020) found malaria prevalence 27% at baseline, with stunting prevalence 21% overall; underweight 23% in children ≤10 years. Malaria increased stunting odds by 78% (AOR=1.78, 95% CI 1.36–2.33, P<0.001); low socioeconomic status increased underweight odds (AOR=1.50, 95% CI 1.13–2.01, P=0.006). Dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine and artesunate-amodiaquine interventions showed no significant improvement in nutritional markers, suggesting integrated interventions are needed
articles.title_original: Malaria-malnutrition interaction: prevalence, risk factors, and the impact of intermittent preventive therapy for malaria on nutritional status of school-age children in Muheza, Tanga, Tanzania - A cross-sectional survey and a randomized controlled open-label trial.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of diabetic and non-diabetic participants in Ghana examined galectin-3 association with malaria-related insulin resistance. Galectin-3 could not predict HOMA-IR or HOMA-B in any group. Insulin resistance was predicted by glucose (B=0.603, Exp(B)=1.83, 95% CI 1.27–2.63, P=0.001) and insulin levels (B=1.145, Exp(B)=3.14, 95% CI 1.87–5.23, P<0.001), with the model explaining 67.7–91% of variation. Authors conclude the relationship is more complex than linear
articles.title_original: Galectin-3 did not associate with malaria-related insulin resistance in diabetic and non-diabetic respondents at a Ghanaian General Hospital.

articles.headline: Mixed-methods evaluation of expanded community health worker role for malaria elimination in Myanmar included qualitative interviews/focus groups (n=6+14+36 groups; 69 supervision visits) and quantitative community survey (n=643). Community acceptance 97.4% (626/643); CHW fidelity 97.1% (67/69); community reliance 98.3% (632/643). Model was feasible and acceptable; training, supervision, supply chain, referral mechanisms, and data systems could be improved for wider implementation
articles.title_original: Acceptability, feasibility and fidelity of an expanded role for community health workers for malaria elimination in Myanmar: A mixed-method study.

articles.headline: Experimental study in 204 mice (both sexes) with P. berghei (NK65) infection compared Capsicum frutescens aqueous extract sub-fractions (0.5–8.0 mg/kg), chloroquine (10 mg/kg), and saline control. CFL sub-fractions significantly reduced (P<0.05) malondialdehyde (lipid peroxidation marker) in erythrocytes and liver homogenates with dose-dependent increases in antioxidant enzymes (SOD, catalase, glutathione peroxidase, glutathione reductase, GST) on days 4 and 8. Sub-fraction B demonstrated the most pronounced antioxidant effect
articles.title_original: Evaluating the Oxidative Stress Reducing Potentials of Sub-Fractions of Capsicum Frutescens L. Aqueous Extract on Early Established Plasmodium in Mice.

articles.headline: Study examining the recognition of RIFIN (rosette-inducing factor) variant surface antigens by both inhibitory and activating killer immunoglobulin-like receptors (KIRs) on natural killer cells, proposing a dual-edged mechanism of NK cell control in malaria infection
articles.title_original: Both inhibitory and activating KIRs recognize RIFINs: a dual-edged mechanism of NK cell control in malaria.

articles.headline: Mechanistic study of P. berghei liver stage development and host sphingolipid requirements. Exogenous C16-ceramide enhanced parasite development and nuclear replication; host sphingomyelin was essential for invasion. Live microscopy demonstrated active sphingolipid transport into the parasitophorous vacuole throughout liver stage. CERT1 (ceramide transporter) knockdown significantly reduced parasite load and ceramide trafficking. CERS3 and SPHK1 depletion affected parasitophorous vacuole size and infection rate but not invasion, identifying novel targets for multistage therapeutic intervention
articles.title_original: Host sphingolipids support Plasmodium berghei liver stage development.

articles.headline: Quasi-experimental trial in 100 Pakistani patients (18–76 years) with confirmed P. vivax comparing primaquine regimens: Group A (n=50) received 1 mg/kg/day for 7 days; Group B (n=50) received 0.5 mg/kg/day for 14 days. Recurrence rate was substantially lower in Group A (2%) versus Group B (26%; p=0.012); adherence higher in Group A (94%) versus Group B (76%; p=0.012). Both regimens were well tolerated with slightly increased mild gastrointestinal symptoms in Group A, suggesting shorter high-dose regimen improves outcomes in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Shortening the Journey to Cure: A Quasi-experimental Trial Comparing Seven-Day and 14-Day Primaquine Radical Therapy for Vivax Malaria.

articles.headline: Review of phytochemicals with antiparasitic activity against T. cruzi and Plasmodium spp. and their integration into nanocarrier-based drug delivery systems. Addresses current limitations of plant-derived natural products (poor solubility, low bioavailability, chemical instability) and proposes phytonanotechnology as a strategy to improve pharmacokinetics and therapeutic efficacy for neglected parasitic diseases in low-resource settings
articles.title_original: Harnessing Phytonanotechnology to Tackle Neglected Parasitic Diseases: Focus on Chagas Disease and Malaria.
articles.authors: García M, Magi MS, García MC

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 25 studies with 25,867 caregivers in sub-Saharan Africa found pooled acceptance of malaria vaccine at 82% (95% CI 73%-90%) and willingness at 80% (95% CI 70%-90%) among caretakers of children under five. Regional variation was observed with lowest acceptance in DRC at 53%. High heterogeneity (I2>99%) and publication bias suggest strong community readiness for rollout but highlight need for context-specific implementation strategies
articles.title_original: Community readiness and acceptance for the implementation of the malaria vaccine among caretakers of at-risk children in sub-Saharan Africa: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Correction notice to a study evaluating in vitro activity and selectivity of Artemisia afra and Artemisia annua aqueous extracts against artemisinin-resistant Plasmodium falciparum
articles.title_original: Correction: Assessment of the in vitro activity and selectivity of Artemisia afra and Artemisia annua aqueous extracts against artemisinin-resistant Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Cluster RCT in Côte d'Ivoire examined ecological niche shifts for An. coluzzi, An. gambiae s.s., and An. funestus s.s. with long-lasting insecticidal nets over one year. Suitable habitat for vector species decreased from <1% to >60%, with >50% reduction for P. falciparum-infected vectors, driven primarily by land cover, climate, topography, and population density rather than intervention arm differences. Findings support tailoring vector control by species composition
articles.title_original: Ecological drivers of malaria vector habitat and transmission over 1 year of long-lasting insecticidal net intervention in Côte d'Ivoire.

articles.headline: Cluster RCT in Madagascar (November 2020–December 2021) with qualitative surveys of 87 interviews and 12 focus groups assessed expanding community health worker malaria case management to all ages. Stakeholders found age-expanded management acceptable and consistent with existing CHW roles, improving understanding of adult malaria susceptibility. Structural barriers including treatment costs and geographic access remained unresolved, requiring additional support for national scale-up
articles.title_original: An Anthropological Analysis of Acceptability and Feasibility of Expanding Community-Based Malaria Management to All Ages in Madagascar: Levers and Challenges for National Scale-Up.

articles.headline: Proteomic analysis of 1,463 plasma proteins in acute Plasmodium falciparum malaria in adult travelers identified perturbations in >250 proteins. An 11-protein signature distinguished severe malaria cases and enabled clustering by disease severity. Integration with single-cell RNA-seq revealed links between systemic proteomic changes and immune cell-mediated organ-specific responses, opening avenues for biomarker discovery and therapeutic targets
articles.title_original: Systems analysis of clinical malaria reveals proteomic perturbation and innate-adaptive crosstalk linked to disease severity.

articles.headline: Retrospective cohort study of U.S. Military Health System (2012–2018) identified 281 malaria cases, with 5-fold higher incidence in service members and families from malaria-endemic countries (IRR 5.2, 95% CI 2.4–10.4). Sub-Saharan Africa–connected families accounted for 86.6% of endemic-country cases at 42 per 100,000 person-years annually. Only 48% of cases accessed pretravel medical counseling, despite universal health insurance coverage
articles.title_original: Malaria Incidence in US Military Families Is Related to Service Member's Birthplace.

articles.headline: Economic evaluation of Colombia's Malaria Eradication Campaign (1959–1969) estimated total institutional cost at USD $41,788,924, with 74.1% from national sources. Attack phase consumed 80.4% of expenditure at $2.70 per household spray, primarily for personnel (40%) and insecticides (19%). Campaign prevented an estimated 568,439 malaria cases (41,965/year average) but ultimately failed to achieve eradication
articles.title_original: Costs and impact of the Malaria Eradication Campaign in Colombia, 1957-1969.

articles.headline: Molecular surveillance of 468 P. falciparum samples in Libreville, Gabon (2021–2023) detected no validated Kelch13 (K13) mutations conferring artemisinin resistance, but 21 carried novel variants of unknown significance: E433D (n=14, rising from 0.7% to 5.5%), Q613H (n=5), V520I, and V637I. MDR1 Y184F frequency increased from 52.6% to 68.8%; clonal transmission chains documented. Complexity of infection increased, indicating intensifying transmission intensity requiring functional validation of novel K13 mutations
articles.title_original: Clonal Transmission of Emerging Novel Plasmodium falciparum Kelch13 Mutations and Increasing Complexity of Infection in Libreville, Gabon, 2021-2023.

articles.headline: Genomic surveillance of 1,146 P. falciparum samples in Mozambique (2021–2022) detected no pfk13 artemisinin-resistance markers or pfpm2 piperaquine-resistance duplications. Sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance-associated pfdhfr/pfdhps quintuple mutants were prevalent (>92.5% in 2021, >87.8% in 2022), while pfdhps-A581G was rare (1.6%→0.8%). Results support continued artemisinin-based combination therapy and close monitoring of SP efficacy given high prevalence of quintuple mutants
articles.title_original: Antimalarial drug resistance and population structure of Plasmodium falciparum in Mozambique using genomic surveillance at health facilities in 2021 and 2022.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling study using fractional-order derivatives to evaluate treatment and prevention strategies against malaria transmission. Simulations demonstrated that improved treatment and mosquito control measures significantly reduced malaria cases, while increased mosquito contact and ineffective treatments aggravated disease burden. Findings emphasize critical importance of sustained treatment and prevention efforts in mitigating malaria's public health impact, particularly in resource-limited regions
articles.title_original: Fractional-order model of malaria incorporating treatment and prevention strategies.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study analyzing Plasmodium falciparum Duffy binding-like domain sequences from 20 patient samples and case-control analysis of host miR-92a polymorphisms identified a conserved miR-92a target motif in erythrocyte membrane protein despite sequence variability between parasite isolates from uncomplicated and complicated malaria cases. Genetic variants in miR-92a gene detected in nine cases and two controls suggest miRNA polymorphisms influence host-parasite interactions, supporting miR-92a's potential as antisense therapeutic strategy
articles.title_original: Exploring the Potential of miRNA-92a-3p as Lead for Sequence-Based Therapies for Malaria.

articles.headline: In vitro evaluation of ferrocenyl quinoline-benzimidazole hybrids against drug-sensitive NF54 and multidrug-resistant K1 Plasmodium falciparum strains demonstrated potent submicromolar activity with multistage efficacy targeting both immature and mature gametocytes. N,N-dimethylaminomethyl side chain enhanced potency; mechanistic studies revealed hemozoin formation inhibition and reactive oxygen species generation as key modes of action. Findings support ferrocenyl-based molecular hybrids as promising candidates for combating drug-resistant malaria
articles.title_original: Ferrocenyl Quinoline-Benzimidazole Hybrids: A Multistage Strategy to Combat Drug-Resistant Malaria.

articles.headline: Monthly sampling of 15 Anopheles species across Sri Lankan climatic zones (2 years) found An. culicifacies peaked at intermediate-zone sites during inter-monsoon and southwest monsoon seasons; vector abundance significantly correlated with humidity, temperature, rainfall, and collection site (p<0.05). Intermediate zone showed highest malaria receptivity; ongoing entomological surveillance critical for post-elimination malaria reintroduction prevention
articles.title_original: Seasonal abundance and climatic determinants of Anopheles vectors in Sri Lanka: its implications in the malaria post-elimination era.

articles.headline: Global analysis of P. vivax Pv47 revealed most common polymorphisms are non-synonymous with domain 2 showing positive selection signature; greatest haplotype diversity in East/Southeast Asia and Oceania; K27E polymorphism associated with differential infectivity to An. albimanus and An. pseudopunctipennis. Adaptation to diverse Anopheline vectors drives Pv47 diversity, paralleling Pfs47 evolution
articles.title_original: Worldwide genetic diversity of Plasmodium vivax Pv47 is consistent with natural selection by anopheline mosquitoes.

articles.headline: Ex vivo testing of 1,114 P. falciparum isolates from Uganda (2019-2024) found improved chloroquine susceptibility over time but decreased susceptibility to lumefantrine, mefloquine, and dihydroartemisinin (DHA). PfK13 C469Y/A675V and PfMDR1 mutations associated with reduced lumefantrine and DHA efficacy. Declining lumefantrine and DHA activities suggest potential loss of leading artemisinin-combination-therapy efficacy
articles.title_original: Changes in susceptibility of Plasmodium falciparum to antimalarial drugs in Uganda over time: 2019-2024.

articles.headline: P. yoelii-infected mice treated with ferroportin inhibitor VIT-2763 showed increased parasitemia with elevated pro-inflammatory cytokines, erythropoietin, and liver injury markers; VIT-2763 suppressed hepatic hepcidin while reducing RBC ferroportin. Tissue-specific ferroportin regulation in hepatocytes versus RBCs indicates hepcidin-ferroportin axis modulation as therapeutic strategy for malaria
articles.title_original: Pharmacologic Inhibition of Erythrocyte Ferroportin Expression Exacerbates Plasmodium Infection.

articles.headline: Analysis of P. falciparum amodiaquine-resistance pfmdr1 86Y/Y184/1246Y haplotype prevalence in São Tomé and Príncipe (2020-2022) revealed successive SNP accretion in pre-existing pfcrt 76T background following artesunate-amodiaquine introduction (2005), establishing a dominant resistance haplotype. Increased vigilance required for artesunate-amodiaquine efficacy monitoring
articles.title_original: Trajectory of Plasmodium falciparum Molecular Markers of Amodiaquine Resistance in São Tomé and Príncipe.

articles.headline: Hybrid Capsule Network combining CNN feature extraction with capsule routing achieved up to 100% multiclass parasite classification accuracy on four benchmark malaria datasets with only 1.35M parameters and 0.26 GFLOPs, demonstrating consistent cross-dataset improvements over baseline CNNs. Grad-CAM interpretability confirms biologically relevant focus; lightweight architecture enables mobile deployment in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Hybrid Capsule Network for precise and interpretable detection of malaria parasites in blood smear images.

articles.headline: Survey of 391 participants in Thailand found 96.4% reported acceptance of ivermectin MDA, with forest-related workers (aOR 4.2, 95% CI 1.1-16.1) and those believing malaria elimination possible (aOR 9.1, 95% CI 2.8-29.9) showing higher acceptance. However, actual completion was only 59.0% among 3,137 eligible individuals. Substantial gap between reported acceptance and participation highlights need for targeted community engagement
articles.title_original: Community acceptance of Ivermectin mass drug administration for malaria in Southern Thailand.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 12 East African countries (2015-2024) found Uganda, South Sudan, and Burundi report highest malaria incidence (250+ per 1000), with >20% artemisinin partial resistance and pyrethroid resistance undermining vector control. Climate change expands transmission to highlands; integrated strategies reduce severe malaria by up to 47%, but only 48% of required funding secured. Urgent investment in sustainable financing and climate-adaptive interventions essential
articles.title_original: The burden of malaria in East Africa: prevalence, risk factors, and control strategies.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 185 schoolchildren in Ondo State found malaria prevalence 57.1% (Oke Igbo) and 49.2% (Ipogun); urogenital schistosomiasis 28.6% and 4.1%; co-infection 20.6% and 3.3%; malnutrition 75.5%–77.9%. Males were 2.19 times more likely for co-infection (p=0.02, CI 1.13–4.28); chronic malnutrition higher in infected/co-infected participants. Comprehensive health and nutritional interventions needed for parasitic disease burden
articles.title_original: Malaria, urogenital schistosomiasis and co-infection and nutritional status of school children in Ondo state.

articles.headline: P. berghei-infected ICR mice treated with Annona muricata extract (AME) combined with artesunate or pyrimethamine achieved >90% parasitemia inhibition with mean survival extended to 30 days, showing synergistic antimalarial efficacy; AME plus chloroquine showed no synergy. Findings suggest AME with artesunate or pyrimethamine as promising alternative mitigating drug resistance
articles.title_original: Enhanced Antimalarial Efficacy of Annona muricata Leaf Extract Combined With Artesunate, Chloroquine, and Pyrimethamine in Plasmodium berghei-Infected ICR Mice.
articles.authors: Sarakul O, Boonhok R, Somsak V

articles.headline: Genome sequencing of P. vivax from Sudanese Duffy-positive and Duffy-negative individuals found Duffy-positive cases with significantly higher parasitemia (p=0.0000132) and much greater genetic diversity across 14 chromosomes and 44 erythrocyte-binding genes, particularly merozoite surface proteins. Duffy-negative infections exhibit highly conserved genomes with mutations in binding-implicated genes, suggesting distinct alternative invasion mechanisms
articles.title_original: Highly conserved Plasmodium vivax genomes in Duffy-negative individuals from Sudan.

articles.headline: Sequential interpretation of two-step malaria RDTs (HRP2 and pLDH) with dbPCR-NALFIA confirmation in 438 samples from Burkina Faso achieved sensitivity 97.9%, specificity 94.8%, PPV 97.2%, NPV 96.1%, with 87.2% requiring no confirmatory testing. Sequential algorithm significantly improved over single HRP2 RDT (sensitivity 95.2%, specificity 73.2%), enhancing malaria diagnosis accuracy in febrile patients
articles.title_original: Refining malaria diagnosis in high-transmission areas: a dual-approach with rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs) and dbPCR-NALFIA.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 1,480 and 1,274 asymptomatic individuals in two Cameroonian ecological zones identified 59.2% and 63.4% P. falciparum prevalence, respectively, with 36 and 42 msp2 alleles detected. Over 60% of isolates were multiclonal infections; Mfou (forest) showed higher multiclonal frequency (68.9%, mean-of-infection [MOI]=2.08) than Tibati (savanna, 57.29%, MOI=1.80), with MOI inversely correlated with age and higher in males. High allelic diversity and prevalence reflect intense transmission but distinct epidemiological patterns warrant area-specific control strategies
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of msp2 in Plasmodium falciparum isolates among asymptomatic malaria infections from two ecological settings in Cameroon.

articles.headline: Case-control study (94 cases, 91 controls) in Zimbabwe identified independent risk factors for malaria: night outdoor religious/social activities (adjusted odds ratio [AOR]=8.13, 95% confidence interval [CI] 1.74–37.90) and having a garden (AOR=4.51, CI 1.55–13.12). Full-body clothing (AOR=0.13, CI 0.03–0.53) and sleeping in indoor residual-sprayed rooms (AOR=0.04, CI 0.01–0.31) were protective. Despite 96.7% of cases and 92.2% of controls demonstrating good malaria knowledge, outdoor exposure drove transmission, indicating sustained IRS plus targeted community engagement is essential
articles.title_original: Risk factors for contracting malaria in six wards of Mudzi District, Zimbabwe: A case control-study.
articles.authors: Fambirai T, Chimbari MJ, Ndarukwa P

articles.headline: Global Burden of Disease 2021 analysis found quality-of-care index (QCI) ranged from 91.5 in Bahrain and Qatar to 75.2 in Burkina Faso for malaria and neglected tropical diseases. Incidence increased while age-standardized mortality rates and disability-adjusted life years declined 1990–2021; QCI correlated positively with economic development, with lower quality in children under 10 and adults over 95. Substantial regional disparities require urgent improvement in low socio-demographic index areas
articles.title_original: Inequalities in disease burden and care quality of neglected tropical diseases and malaria, 1990-2021: Findings from the 2021 Global Burden of Disease Study.

articles.headline: [No abstract provided; insufficient information to summarize.]
articles.title_original: Malaria. Reply.

articles.headline: [No abstract provided; insufficient information to summarize.]
articles.title_original: Malaria.
articles.authors: Barkati S, Balerdi-Sarasola L, Yansouni CP

articles.headline: Experimental study infected mice with Plasmodium berghei during rest or active phases, in untreated and pyrimethamine-treated conditions, measuring liver parasite burdens by quantitative PCR. Pyrimethamine reduced parasite burdens, but effects were not biologically significant or dependent on host circadian phase; untreated infections also showed no time-of-day effect. Host circadian rhythms do not influence vector-to-host malaria transmission, suggesting vector behavioral shifts are epidemiologically more important
articles.title_original: Time-of-day of infection: impact on liver stage malaria parasites in untreated and drug-treated hosts.

articles.headline: Longitudinal surveillance (2018–2023) of 13,347 febrile patients along the Thailand-Myanmar border found 11.0% cumulative slide positivity for P. vivax. Independent risk factors included school-age children (adjusted odds ratio [aOR]=1.56, 95% CI 1.24–1.97), working-age adults (aOR=1.43, CI 1.02–2.00), males (aOR=1.19, CI 1.06–1.35), Myanmar nationals (aOR=2.37, CI 2.01–2.80), non-Thai foreigners (aOR=5.50, CI 3.36–8.90), recent travel (aOR=2.38, CI 1.94–2.92), and livestock occupations (aOR=2.49, CI 1.14–5.35), while consistent bed-net use was protective (aOR=0.39, CI 0.30–0.51)
articles.title_original: Slide positivity, trends, and risk factors of febrile Plasmodium vivax malaria along the Thailand-Myanmar border, 2018-2023.

articles.headline: Temperature-suitability modeling for Canada (2000–2023) applied thresholds from historical Plasmodium transmission studies to gridded climate data, projecting future conditions. Yearly frequencies of suitable conditions for P. vivax and P. falciparum showed rising trends, with temperature surges reaching 56% and 34% of Canada's population, respectively, creating conditions for autochthonous transmission. Climate change is increasing malaria transmission risk in Canada
articles.title_original: Current and future temperature suitability for autochthonous transmission of malaria in Canada.

articles.headline: Entomological survey of 2,835 Anopheles across three highland altitudes in Cameroon (May–June 2023) identified An. gambiae s.l. (82.9%), An. funestus s.l. (15.9%), An. nili (0.09%), and An. ziemanni (1.1%). An. gambiae s.l. human-biting rates differed dramatically by altitude: 45.25 bites/human/night at Penka Michel (1,500 m) versus 0.41 at Dschang (1,400 m; p<0.001), with entomological inoculation rate 13-fold higher at Penka Michel (1.11 vs 0.08 infective bites/human/night). P. falciparum dominated (62–67%), with P. malariae and P. ovale also circulating
articles.title_original: Diversity of anopheline species and malaria transmission dynamics in high-altitude areas of western Cameroon.

articles.headline: In vitro 3D blood-brain barrier model with primary brain endothelial cells, astrocytes, and pericytes exposed to P. falciparum parasite-egress products or infected erythrocytes showed increased microvascular permeability, downregulated junctional-protein genes, increased ferroptosis markers and interferon signaling, and elevated JAK-STAT pathway activation. The JAK-STAT inhibitor Ruxolitinib prevented permeability increases induced by parasite egress. Results suggest parasite-mediated endothelial disruption mechanisms in cerebral malaria and point toward Ruxolitinib as a potential adjunctive therapy
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum egress disrupts endothelial junctions and activates JAK-STAT signaling in a microvascular 3D blood-brain barrier model.

articles.headline: Time-series analysis of malaria-in-pregnancy (MIP) data across Nigeria (January 2015–January 2025) using wavelet coherence and ARIMA/SARIMAX models revealed significant regional variation: Cross River peaked in 2017 and 2019; Enugu showed lowest trough in 2017. SARIMAX models captured MIP trends more effectively than ARIMA in most states, but Kebbi and Niger required ARIMA; analysis identified state-specific patterns—intervention-driven fluctuations (Cross River, Enugu), cyclical trends (Ondo, Niger, Yobe), and weaker MIP-general malaria correlation (South-South, South-East)
articles.title_original: Time series analysis of malaria in pregnancy, using wavelet and SARIMAX models.

articles.headline: Eco-epidemiological modeling explored parameter values for population-modification gene drives targeting 50% clinical malaria reduction for ≥3 years and <1 year time-to-impact in Burkina Faso and Kenya. When window-of-protection criteria were met, time-to-impact targets also succeeded; target outcomes were most sensitive to fitness costs of the gene-drive allele and effector-gene efficacy. Homing rates already achieved in Anopheles need no further improvement, but critical parameters influencing field efficacy can only be reliably measured in field trials, limiting product-readiness assessment
articles.title_original: A model-informed target product profile for population modification gene drives for malaria control.
articles.authors: Mondal A, Sánchez C HM, Marshall JM

articles.headline: In vitro screening of 568 GSK TCAMS compounds against P. cynomolgi hypnozoites identified 12 with IC50 <2 µM, most showing nanomolar potency, grouped into six chemical clusters with some assigned mechanisms (cytochrome bc1, kinase inhibition). Preselection based on activity against gametocytes and liver stages in other Plasmodium spp. proved effective for identifying hypnozoite-active compounds
articles.title_original: Identification of novel compounds with prophylactic activity against hypnozoites using a Plasmodium cynomolgi in vitro model.

articles.headline: GABA signaling through RDL (GABA(A) receptor) modulates malaria mosquito auditory sensitivity; picrotoxin antagonism produced mechanical changes that were octopamine-dependent and electrical changes that were partially octopamine-independent. RDL plays a critical role in controlling auditory sensitivity and is implicated in natural population mating
articles.title_original: The GABA(A) receptor RDL modulates the auditory sensitivity of malaria mosquitoes.

articles.headline: Microscopy and cox1 sequencing of 12 Burmese chickens identified 2/12 (17%) with onchocercid filarioids (potentially novel species) and 6/12 (50%) with P. juxtanucleare; one chicken carried two distinct microfilarial forms representing two species. Genetic analysis revealed potentially novel onchocercid species or genera within galliforms, highlighting molecular surveillance value and an emerging avian filariasis hotspot in Thailand
articles.title_original: Discovery of potentially novel species of the Onchocercidae (Nematoda: Filarioidea) in Burmese fighting chickens (Gallus gallus): Genetic insights into avian filariasis and co-infection with Plasmodium juxtanucleare.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis

articles.headline: Cluster-randomized crossover trial of PQ-MDA (0.25 mg/kg/day base × 14 days) in northern Myanmar (n=541, 667) showed clinical P. vivax incidence fell to zero within one month. Round 1 aOR 0.46 (95% CI 0.25–0.83) for subclinical prevalence; round 2 aOR 0.15 (95% CI 0.03–0.65). PQ-MDA was effective in reducing clinical and subclinical P. vivax in low-endemicity subtropical settings
articles.title_original: Efficacy of Focal Primaquine Mass Administration for Eliminating Plasmodium vivax Malaria in Northern Myanmar: A Cluster-Randomized Trial.

articles.headline: 93-microhaplotype amplicon sequencing assay evaluated in 745 P. vivax infections (128 RCT infection pairs) achieved low IBD estimation error (RMSE <0.12) with strong geographic clustering. In the RCT, primaquine use was associated with lower frequency of suspected relapses/recrudescence, with greater impact when paired with chloroquine versus artemether-lumefantrine. The assay can refine P. vivax treatment assessment using IBD
articles.title_original: Microhaplotype deep sequencing assays to capture Plasmodium vivax infection lineages.

articles.headline: Tafenoquine blocks β-hematin crystal growth sites and potently suppresses nonclassical growth and nucleation by impairing mesoscopic hematin-rich cluster formation, with stage-specific effects showing greater ring-stage than trophozoite suppression. TQ disrupts parasite survival through dual inhibition of hemozoin synthesis and mitochondrial function
articles.title_original: Tafenoquine Disrupts Intraerythrocytic Stages of Malaria Parasites by Inhibiting Nonclassical Pathways of β-Hematin Nucleation and Growth.

articles.headline: Computational screening identified 42 viable Penicillium metabolite candidates against P. falciparum lactate dehydrogenase; three showed superior binding affinities: Penicilactone B (–8.71 kcal/mol, Ki 414.77 nM), Penicillimide, and Penicillisocoumarin A. Penicilactone B demonstrated greatest stability over 200-ns molecular dynamics with minimal human LDH off-target binding, emerging as most promising pending experimental validation
articles.title_original: Penicillium-Derived Inhibitors of Plasmodium falciparum Lactate Dehydrogenase (PfLDH): A Computational Approach for Novel Antimalarial Therapy Development.

articles.headline: Longitudinal case of severe malaria-associated ARDS on extracorporeal membrane oxygenation showed persistent lymphopenia with dynamic T cell and innate lymphoid cell shifts; type 2 immunity predominated acutely and exhausted T cells decreased during recovery. Blood and bronchoalveolar lavage fluid profiles differed substantially, demonstrating value of multiple-compartment immune monitoring for guiding immune modulation
articles.title_original: Monitoring of deep lymphocyte phenotypes in the blood and bronchoalveolar lavage fluid of patients with severe malaria-associated acute respiratory distress syndrome.

articles.headline: Scoping review of 22 studies (2014–2024, PRISMA-ScR) examining malaria vulnerabilities in Southeast Asian populations identified five thematic domains: biological influences, demographic/socioeconomic factors, built/lived environments, behavior/practices, and healthcare access. Malaria vulnerability in SEA is multifaceted and embedded in complex social determinants, requiring region-specific multi-sectoral strategies
articles.title_original: Malaria and determinants of health: a scoping review of malaria vulnerabilities in Southeast Asia.

articles.headline: Case of 56-year-old Japanese man with P. malariae (quartan malaria) acquired in Sierra Leone showed atypical nested PCR with fainter P. malariae-specific secondary band; sequencing revealed four-base SSU rRNA deletion in species-specific primer-binding site. This first African report of P. malariae SSU rRNA variant highlights need for molecular surveillance to prevent diagnostic failures from emerging genetic variants
articles.title_original: First report of a Plasmodium malariae SSU rRNA gene variant in Africa associated with reduced amplification by nested PCR.

articles.headline: Analysis of 106 countries (2000–2022) using XGBoost machine learning found 251.75 million malaria cases in 2022 with peak mortality in 2020 (99,554 deaths); Nigeria highest incidence (1.33 billion), South Sudan highest mortality (149,753). XGBoost achieved RMSE 0.63, r² 0.93; key determinants were sanitation access, electricity availability, population growth, and under-5 mortality. AI-driven spatial models provide actionable insights for targeted interventions
articles.title_original: Unraveling global malaria incidence and mortality using machine learning and artificial intelligence-driven spatial analysis.

articles.headline: Experimental pantothenate supplementation in Anopheles stephensi across generations via water versus blood showed concentration-, delivery-, and age-dependent effects on feeding and reproduction. Greatest effects were on reproduction; unsupplemented offspring of water-supplemented mothers exhibited increased fecundity (transgenerational), while blood effects appeared only in mothers and supplemented offspring. Targeting Pan–CoA biology could provide novel mosquito control strategies
articles.title_original: Pantothenate regulates feeding and reproduction in the malaria vector Anopheles stephensi, with patterns dependent on supplementation scheme and parental nutrition.

articles.headline: Retrospective analysis of 8,240 P. vivax and 510 P. falciparum cases (2011–2020, Myanmar) identified risk factors for gametocyte carriage. P. vivax gametocyte prevalence was 88.0% overall; density increased with asexual parasite density (P < 0.001) and was associated with dry season, prior infection, and fever, with polyclonal infections showing significantly higher densities than monoclonal (P < 0.0001). A notable cluster occurred May 2015–August 2017 (P = 0.001). For P. falciparum, younger age and prior infection predicted carriage; density was higher in dry season (P = 0.0115). Authors conclude dry season peaks and polyclonal infections drive transmission potential
articles.title_original: Risk factor for gametocyte carriage and gametocytemia in Plasmodium vivax and Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Model-based analysis of pre-erythrocytic vaccine properties found that 90% efficacy with 6–12 month half-life, co-administered blood-stage drug, and yearly boosters achieved 60–80% yearly incidence reduction. Halting after 5 years sustained ≥35% reduction in children <6 years for 12 months (PfPR2-10 <30%). Extending half-life to 12–18 months or increasing coverage achieved equivalent impact with lower efficacy; without a booster dose, >90% efficacy and >12-month half-life were required to sustain impact beyond primary vaccination. Authors emphasize target product profiles should be setting-specific and linked to deployment strategies
articles.title_original: Performance characteristics and potential public health impact of improved pre-erythrocytic malaria vaccines targeting childhood burden.

articles.headline: Correction notice to prior publication (DOI: 10.1371/journal.pone.0305558). No substantive findings to report
articles.title_original: Correction: Detection through the use of RT-MqPCR of asymptomatic reservoirs of malaria in samples of patients from the indigenous Comarca of Guna Yala, Panama: Essential method to achieve the elimination of malaria.

articles.headline: Review article examining glutathione S-transferases (GSTs) as drug-resistance mechanisms in P. falciparum across Southern Africa. While artemisinin resistance has not emerged in the region, Kelch13 mutations conferring resistance have been detected in sub-Saharan Africa. Authors propose GST-targeted inhibition as an adjunct therapeutic strategy to prevent resistance and support malaria control aligned with Sustainable Development Goal 3
articles.title_original: Targeting Plasmodium glutathione S-transferases to overcome antimalaria drug resistance in Southern Africa.

articles.headline: Case report of a 5-year-old with concurrent hepatitis A virus (HAV IgM-positive) and P. vivax parasitemia who developed fulminant hepatic failure with features of autoimmune hepatitis (antinuclear antibody 1:320, speckled pattern). Management with antimalarials, lactulose, rifaximin, and transfusional support prevented need for transplantation; corticosteroid therapy led to clinical stabilization and favorable liver function recovery on follow-up
articles.title_original: Hepatitis A-Induced Fulminant Hepatic Failure Linked to Autoimmune Hepatitis in a Child Amidst Diagnostic Challenges of Dengue and Malaria.
articles.authors: Bilal M, Ajmal MU, Ali A

articles.headline: Case report of a returned traveler to Canada from South America with confirmed intercurrent rickettsial and P. vivax infection, where acute rickettsial disease potentially triggered P. vivax relapse. Authors review literature on diagnostic challenges and missed coinfections in travelers, highlighting geographic distribution overlap and need for expanded testing beyond malaria confirmation alone
articles.title_original: Acute Rickettsiosis Triggering Plasmodium vivax Relapse in a Returned Traveler: A Case Report and Clinical Review of Travel-Related Coinfections.

articles.headline: Whole genome analysis of Ugandan P. falciparum identified a novel phosphoinositide-binding protein (PX1) haplotype (PIN, comprising SNPs L1222P, M1701I, D1705N and two indels) first detected in 2008, reaching >50% prevalence in northern Uganda by 2016 and eastern Uganda by 2023. PIN-carrying parasites showed significantly decreased ex vivo susceptibility (IC50s) to lumefantrine, mefloquine, and dihydroartemisinin, independent of Kelch13 mutations. PX1 polymorphisms may contribute to artemisinin and partner-drug resistance in Africa
articles.title_original: A novel locus associated with decreased susceptibility of Plasmodium falciparum to lumefantrine and dihydroartemisinin has emerged and spread in Uganda.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 134 pregnant women in two Nigerian hospitals (Anambra State) found malaria prevalence 5.97% (8/134), HIV 3.73% (5/134), hepatitis B 4.47% (6/134), preeclampsia 3.73% (5), and premature rupture of membranes 2.24% (3). IPTp use was 73.9% and preterm delivery occurred in 5.2%. Authors conclude the lower malaria burden compared to regional reports reflects high IPTp implementation effectiveness
articles.title_original: Intermittent preventive treatment, malaria, HIV, hepatitis and pregnancy outcomes in Nigerian women: a cross-sectional study in two healthcare facilities.

articles.headline: Molecular surveillance of 95 Culex mosquito pools (1,376 females, 4 species) from Doñana wetland, Spain detected West Nile virus in 5 Cx. perexiguus pools; avian Plasmodium in 28 pools (17 Cx. perexiguus, 11 Cx. pipiens), comprising three lineages: P. vaughani (n=22), P. matutinum (n=4), and Plasmodium sp. (n=1). No Dirofilaria detected. This represents the most extensive pathogen survey in the protected wetland to date
articles.title_original: Vector surveillance of avian Plasmodium and West Nile virus in Culex mosquitoes from Doñana, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
articles.authors: Martínez-de la Puente J, et al.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 1,232 microscopy-confirmed P. falciparum cases (Amhara region, Ethiopia, 2021–2022) identified 123 suspects of pfhrp2/3 deletion (microscopy-positive, RDT-negative). Among 179 analyzed samples, 158 (88.3%) carried one or both deletions: 92 (51.4%) dual deletion, 62 (34.6%) pfhrp3 only, 4 (2.2%) pfhrp2 only. Of 109 false-negative RDTs, 102 (93.6%) were attributable to deletions; prevalence exceeded WHO 5% threshold and spanned all domains. Authors recommend switching to LDH-based RDTs
articles.title_original: Deletion of target gene (histidine-rich protein 2/3) for Plasmodium falciparum rapid diagnostic tests in Amhara region, Ethiopia: a cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: Non-randomized trial comparing community-led Bti, expert-supervised Bti, and control in The Gambia (2023–2024) found that by round 10, community-led application achieved 96.8% larval density reduction, 97.4% pupal reduction, and 96.2% adult reduction; expert-supervised achieved 98.6%, 99.1%, and 98.8% respectively (p < 0.001 by 2024). Over 85% of community participants reported visible mosquito reduction. Community-led biolarviciding offers a scalable, sustainable vector control strategy for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Empowering communities for malaria control: Effectiveness of community-led biolarviciding using Bacillus thuringiensis israelensis in The Gambia.
