articles.headline: Bayesian spatiotemporal analysis of routine malaria data from 189 Cameroonian health districts (2011–2021) classified districts by disease trajectory and hotspot status. For uncomplicated malaria: 42.9% (81) districts showed decreasing trends, 44.9% (85) increasing; 24.3% (46) identified as high-likelihood hotspots. For severe malaria: 44.9% (85) decreasing, 37.6% (71) increasing; 23.3% (44) likely hotspots. For deaths: 28.6% (54) decreasing, 32.8% (62) increasing; 21.7% (41) likely hotspots. Authors recommend proactive, targeted interventions in identified ex-ante hotspots
articles.title_original: Identifying hotspots of malaria incidence and mortality for tailored interventions in Cameroon using routine data from 2011 to 2021: A Bayesian space-time variability modeling.

articles.headline: In P. berghei-infected mice, oral lycopene (3.11 mg/kg/day) significantly reduced infection-induced elevations in thiobarbituric acid reactive substances (TBARS), uric acid, and nitric oxide levels compared to infected controls and NAC-treated groups (p<0.0001 for both comparisons), normalizing these oxidative markers to uninfected Sham levels and suggesting lycopene's therapeutic potential against malaria-related oxidative stress in brain and lung tissues
articles.title_original: Lycopene Mitigates Malaria-Induced Reactive Oxygen and Nitrogen Species and Oxidative Damage in Mice Brain and Lungs.

articles.headline: In a 5-year cluster-randomized trial across 30 Niger communities, twice-yearly mass azithromycin (20 mg/kg) administered to children aged 1-59 months did not significantly lower malaria parasitemia at 48 months compared to placebo (−3.3 percentage points, P=0.05), but analysis before the introduction of seasonal malaria chemoprevention showed a significant 4.8 percentage point reduction (P=0.02), indicating azithromycin's effect was limited to the first 3 years of treatment
articles.title_original: Biannual Mass Azithromycin Distributions for Preschool Children and Malaria Parasitemia: A Secondary Analysis of the MORDOR Cluster Randomized Trial.

articles.headline: Anopheles stephensi exposed from egg to adult to elevated fluctuating temperatures (+4°C) and humidity (+10%) showed primed basal innate immunity and accelerated pre-adult development without reduced lifespan, but post-bacterial infection exhibited complex immune reprogramming and suppression that enhanced larval and adult survival, suggesting elevated conditions may initially reduce vectorial capacity but could increase Plasmodium susceptibility with significant implications for malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Fluctuating Warm and Humid Conditions Differentially Impact Immunity and Development in the Malaria Vector Anopheles stephensi.

articles.headline: A review of behavioral health interventions in malaria control documents that community education initiatives, behavioral change communication, and community-based interventions promote insecticide-treated net use and treatment-seeking behavior, though implementation challenges including cultural barriers, resource limitations, and inadequate monitoring remain; the authors conclude that integrating behavioral strategies with existing medical and technological measures enhances malaria control program effectiveness and sustainability
articles.title_original: Behavioral health interventions in malaria control: Efficacy and implementation.
articles.authors: Obeagu EI, Obeagu GU, Iduh MU

articles.headline: Larval exposure of Anopheles arabiensis to degraded plastic, phthalic acid, bisphenol-A, or latex beads minimally affected alpha-diversity but altered beta-diversity and increased bacteria associated with plastic degradation and pesticide metabolism in both insecticide-unselected and insecticide-selected strains, with inflammation-associated bacterial genera elevated in both groups despite no significant changes in Plasmodium-protective genera, suggesting potential immune modulation through increased inflammation
articles.title_original: The Effect of Larval Exposure to Plastic Pollution on the Gut Microbiota of the Major Malaria Vector Anopheles arabiensis Patton (Diptera: Culicidae).

articles.headline: Among 4,923 Tanzanian children aged 6-59 months, malaria prevalence was 8% (95% CI 6.2–9.5), highest in Tabora (23%) and <1% in Dodoma, Arusha, Kilimanjaro, Singida, Songwe, and Zanzibar; significant risk factors included age 24-59 months (aOR 1.71), rural residence (aOR 6.92), lower economic status (aOR 3.08), and never using insecticide-treated nets (aOR 2.73), highlighting the need for targeted prevention strategies in rural and economically disadvantaged areas
articles.title_original: Prevalence and determinants of malaria among children aged 6-59 months in Tanzania: a nationwide cross-sectional study.

articles.headline: A Bayesian spatio-temporal analysis of 23.4 million malaria cases (≥5 years) and 17.3 million (children <5 years) in Tanzania during 2016–2023 found malaria risk decreased 11% in children <5 years and 10% in those ≥5 years; long-lasting insecticide-treated nets reduced risk by 1.2% in children <5 years and 7% in those ≥5 years, while minimum temperature, wind speed, and high vegetation index increased risk, informing targeted regional control strategies
articles.title_original: Bayesian spatio-temporal modeling and prediction of malaria cases in Tanzania mainland (2016-2023): unveiling associations with climate and intervention factors.

articles.headline: Among 840 rural Malawian children aged 6-18 months in the LCNI-5 trial, those with asymptomatic P. falciparum infection (detected by real-time PCR) had significantly lower mean weight-for-age Z score (−1.03 vs −0.87, 95% CI −0.17 to −0.04) and weight-for-length Z score (−0.03 vs −0.13, 95% CI −0.22 to −0.06) compared to uninfected peers, with the association persisting after confounder adjustment and suggesting asymptomatic malaria contributes to growth faltering in endemic settings
articles.title_original: Impact of asymptomatic malaria infection on children's growth in rural Malawi.

articles.headline: Among 100 Ugandan patients with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria treated with artemether-lumefantrine, the CYP2B6*6 variant allele frequency was 0.37 (43% GG, 40% GT, 17% TT genotypes); heterozygous GT individuals showed 10-fold slower parasite clearance by day 3 compared to homozygotes (p=0.019), with day 3 microscopy positivity of 37.5% in GT versus 14% in GG and 17.64% in TT, indicating that host metabolizer genotype significantly impacts artemether-lumefantrine efficacy
articles.title_original: CYP2B6*6 single nucleotide polymorphism among patients with uncomplicated malaria in Adjumani district, Uganda: Implications on efficacy of artemether-lumefantrine.

articles.headline: In glucocorticoid receptor (GR) knockout mice infected with Plasmodium chabaudi AS, GR deletion caused lethal hypoglycemia, metabolic dysfunction, and cytokine storm driven by JAK/STAT pathway overactivation; treatment with the JAK1/2 inhibitor ruxolitinib significantly improved survival by preventing hypoglycemia and suppressing hyperinflammation, identifying ruxolitinib as a promising adjuvant therapy for managing metabolic complications in severe malaria
articles.title_original: JAK/STAT inhibition protects glucocorticoid receptor knockout mice from lethal malaria-induced hypoglycemia and hyperinflammation.

articles.headline: A review of the Plasmodium apicoplast transportome identifies 11 confirmed and 17 putative transporters critical for apicoplast metabolic functions including isopentenyl pyrophosphate and coenzyme A biosynthesis; these transport proteins represent promising novel antimalarial drug targets, with the authors anticipating discovery of key players such as IPP and CoA exporters through newly developed tools
articles.title_original: The apicoplast transportome of the malaria parasite.
articles.authors: Akkerman LJ, Kooij TWA, de Vries LE

articles.headline: A personal account of the experience of fighting malaria as both a patient and scientist, combining clinical perspectives with scientific insight into the disease
articles.title_original: Fighting malaria as a patient and scientist.

articles.headline: Statistical methods paper recommending generalized linear/mixed models for analyzing mosquito parasite infections with non-normal distributions and high zero-counts, with strategies for transmission-blocking intervention datasets. Provides guidance applicable across parasitic infections; no empirical quantitative results reported
articles.title_original: When a mean can be meaningless: evaluating mosquito infections with Plasmodium parasites.

articles.headline: Artemisinin-resistant P. yoelii (iPy) showed 2.9-fold higher ED90 and 61.1% lower iRBC-normalized ART-heme exposure than wild-type parasites after oral artemisinin dosing in mice, with iPy-infected mice surviving ≥28 days vs. fatal wild-type infection. ART-heme adduct identified as major metabolite and proposed biomarker for artemisinin resistance
articles.title_original: Pharmacokinetics and metabolism of artemisinin (ART) in Plasmodium yoelii: ART-heme adduct as a potential biomarker for its resistance.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist

articles.headline: Malaria early-detection pilot across 47 Korean health facilities (April–October 2024): hematology analyzer screening of 7.27 million samples identified 1,359 infected RBC cases; 239/499 referred cases confirmed malaria-positive by PCR, demonstrating feasibility for asymptomatic detection without additional resources or budget
articles.title_original: [Pilot Project for Early Diagnosis of Malaria Infection Using a Hematology Analyzer].

articles.headline: Molecular surveillance of 298 blood/tissue samples from 10 neotropical primate species across 47 Brazilian municipalities by nested PCR targeting 18S SSU rRNA identified 1 positive (0.3%) for P. brasilianum/P. malariae, representing first detection in free-living C. geoffroyi and indicating low current zoonotic transmission risk
articles.title_original: Molecular Surveillance of Plasmodium spp. Infection in Neotropical Primates from Bahia and Minas Gerais, Brazil.

articles.headline: Glucose-functionalized redox-responsive dihydroartemisinin nanoparticles (D@GLU-PMs-SS) demonstrated enhanced cellular uptake via GLUT-mediated transport, GSH-triggered DHA release, reduced parasite GSH concentrations, and inhibited parasite growth in vitro while maintaining biosafety, warranting further clinical investigation
articles.title_original: Glucose-functionalized redox-responsive dihydroartemisinin prodrug nanosystem for targeted malaria therapy.

articles.headline: Microfluidic sensor detected reduced P. falciparum-infected RBC deformability as asymmetric ionic current waveforms through micropores at single-cell level; waveform asymmetry correlated with deformability reduction independent of RBC size, providing novel diagnostic approach for studying malaria-associated RBC mechanical changes
articles.title_original: Measuring Asymmetric Ionic Current Waveform Through Micropores for Detecting Reduced Red Blood Cell Deformability Due to Plasmodium falciparum Infection.

articles.headline: Anti-PfSDP peptide antibodies inhibited erythrocyte invasion 20–60% dose-dependently in vitro; PfSDP protein expressed across all parasite developmental stages and immunogenic in endemic-area children with antibodies peaking day 7 post-treatment then gradually declining, suggesting vaccine and transmission-blocking potential
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Subtilisin-like Domain-Containing Protein (PfSDP), a Cross-Stage Antigen, Elicits Short-Lived Antibody Response Following Natural Infection with Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: P. falciparum genetic crosses with fluorescent-labeled parasites (8,540 oocysts from 435 mosquitoes) showed decreasing outcrossed oocyst proportions over 7–14 days post-infection with outcrossed parasites maturing faster than selfed, suggesting outcrossing advantage through deleterious mutation removal and maximizing transmission-level recombination
articles.title_original: Advantages of outcrossing in Plasmodium falciparum: insights from genetic crosses using fluorescent labelled parasites.

articles.headline: ENABLE 1.5 prospective cohort study protocol across 5 West African sites: 5,000 age-stratified participants (0–>50 years) followed 12 months with symptom monitoring every 2 weeks and blood draws every 3 months to assess Lassa fever incidence, malaria coinfection, and sensorineural hearing loss by RT-PCR, microscopy, and serology
articles.title_original: Prospective cohort study to evaluate Lassa fever incidence, symptoms and coinfection with malaria in West Africa: the Enable Lassa Research Programme ('ENABLE 1.5') - study protocol.

articles.headline: Abbott Bioline Malaria Ag P.f/Pan RDT showed 93.37% sensitivity (95% CI: 91.15%–95.77%) and 68.50% specificity vs. microscopy for P. falciparum; kappa=0.652 with microscopy but superior qPCR agreement; P. falciparum prevalence 64.9% (microscopy) vs. 71.7% (RDT), supporting RDT reliability in Central Cameroon
articles.title_original: Malaria infection prevalence and diagnostic performance of the Abbott Bioline Malaria Ag P.f/Pan rapid test in rural populations of Central Cameroon.

articles.headline: Genomic surveillance of 2,486 P. falciparum isolates across Zambia (March–July 2023) identified artemisinin resistance markers kelch13 P574L at 0.66% adjusted prevalence and P441L at 1.39%; near-universal sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance (dhps K540E 93.26%, mdp1 N86 100%); heterogeneous facility-level resistance up to 46%
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Genomic Surveillance Reveals a Diversity of Kelch 13 Mutations in Zambia.

articles.headline: Neural network emulator trained on EMOD agent-based malaria model simulations calibrated transmission dynamics across 8 sub-Saharan African sites by linking immunological parameters to age-stratified epidemiological outcomes (incidence, prevalence), demonstrating feasibility of machine learning-guided emulation for calibrating complex disease transmission models
articles.title_original: Multitask deep learning for the emulation and calibration of an agent-based malaria transmission model.
articles.authors: Mondal A, Anirudh R, Selvaraj P

articles.headline: Egypt's retrospective malaria elimination implementation study (2010-2024) evaluated evidence-based interventions (ITNs, IRS, antimalarial treatment, education) in vulnerable populations. Core outcomes: 96% malaria incidence reduction, 78% ITN adherence, 98% case detection within 48 hours, 91% treatment coverage, and $24 per DALY averted over 15 years. Implementation achieved 93% fidelity, 87% stakeholder acceptability, and successful adaptation to insecticide resistance and funding changes, providing a replicable model for other African countries pursuing elimination
articles.title_original: Comparative analysis of Egypt's malaria elimination strategies and implementation science: Pathways to achieve malaria-free status for other African countries.

articles.headline: Nucleoside-modified mRNA-LNP vaccines encoding P. vivax circumsporozoite protein (PvCSP) were evaluated in mice using various delivery routes and schedules. The construct with wild-type signal peptide plus GPI anchor induced significantly higher anti-CSP and anti-repeat region antibodies; intradermal delivery was superior at lower doses. A delayed third dose (5 months after second) intramuscularly yielded significantly higher anti-repeat antibodies, enhanced T cell responses, and strong protection against sporozoite challenge, supporting mRNA-LNP platform potential for P. vivax pre-erythrocytic infection
articles.title_original: mRNA-LNP vaccine encoding the Plasmodium vivax circumsporozoite protein is highly immunogenic and confers protection in mice.

articles.headline: CD44, a host RBC factor, was identified as promoting P. falciparum invasion through cross-linking by monoclonal antibody BRIC 222 and parasite ligand EBA-175. CD44 cross-linking altered RBC membrane phosphorylation, increased surface protein accessibility including Basigin, and enhanced binding of the PfRH5/PCRCR invasion complex. These findings demonstrate that CD44 cross-linking coordinates and enhances downstream ligand-receptor interactions and RBC cytoskeletal signaling, making cells more permissive to P. falciparum invasion
articles.title_original: CD44 cross-linking promotes Plasmodium falciparum invasion.

articles.headline: Deep amplicon sequencing of 118 P. falciparum samples from Busia County, Western Kenya, identified artemisinin resistance-associated Pfkelch13 mutations in 5.1%, all harboring A578S (not conferring artemisinin resistance). Chloroquine-resistant Pfcrt mutations occurred in 11% (62% triple mutant CVIET); 98% carried dhfr mutations; all had dhps mutations with 84% single A437G. No Pfmdr1 or Pfhrp2 mutations associated with chloroquine resistance detected, indicating chloroquine-sensitive parasites predominate despite rare sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine-sensitive strains
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium falciparum parasites harbouring chloroquine-resistant but not artemisinin-resistant alleles in Busia County, Western Kenya.

articles.headline: Virus-like particle (VLP) vaccines displaying the P. falciparum CSP L9 epitope were evaluated in mice. MS2 L9 VLPs with longer peptides elicited strong antibodies and reduced liver burden; Qβ L9 VLPs and RTS,S/AS01E provided significant protection against liver-stage infection. A combination vaccine (MS2 L9 plus Qβ L9) presenting the L9 epitope in distinct structural contexts conferred sterilizing immunity in the highest percentage of mice, demonstrating structural context influences antibody quality and protective efficacy
articles.title_original: Virus-like particle vaccines targeting a key epitope in circumsporozoite protein provide sterilizing immunity against malaria.

articles.headline: A small-scale field trial installed 350 In2Care EaveTubes in 135 housing units at Kagera Sugar Ltd, Tanzania (Oct 2019–July 2020), with two untreated control camps. EaveTubes reduced indoor Anopheles densities by 58% overall and 75% during peak season; insecticide content remained stable after 6 months. Malaria incidence declined 25.6% in the EaveTubes camp versus lower declines in controls. Results indicate high efficacy and durability of EaveTubes as a complementary vector control tool in areas where insecticide resistance compromises existing interventions
articles.title_original: The In2Care® EaveTubes improved protection against malaria vectors in a small-scale field study at Kagera Sugar Ltd, Missenyi, Tanzania.

articles.headline: Hair samples from 6 P. falciparum patients collected 4 weeks after artemether-lumefantrine (AL) treatment were analyzed by LC-MS/MS. Lumefantrine and metabolite desbutyl-lumefantrine were detected in all samples and quantifiable in 5, whereas artemether and dihydroartemisinin were undetectable. No participants developed recrudescence. Hair analysis may serve as a retrospective tool to assess lumefantrine exposure in recrudescent cases, potentially distinguishing inadequate drug exposure from reduced parasite susceptibility to guide malaria treatment optimization
articles.title_original: Can hair concentrations of artemether-lumefantrine be used as a tool to retrospectively determine drug exposure during malaria treatment?

articles.headline: A scoping review of 46 articles examined malaria vector control strategies in Pakistan published before September 2023. Reported WHO-recommended interventions included ITN (29.79%), IRS (29.79%), spatial spraying (12.77%), spatial/airborne repellents (4.26%), larval source management (4.26%), and house modifications (4.26%). Implementation varied by province: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa used ITN as primary method (55%) whereas Punjab used IRS (50%). Findings show increasing ITN use over time but highlight need for improved research and precise reporting to guide malaria control policy
articles.title_original: Malaria vector control strategies in Pakistan: a scoping review.

articles.headline: Monte Carlo simulations assessed statistical power and sample size for vaccine effectiveness in test-negative design (TND) studies incorporating bias correction for diagnostic misclassification. Using a malaria vaccine example with varying test sensitivities (60%, 80%, 95%), observed vaccine effectiveness estimates were underestimated due to misclassification; bias-corrected estimates were unbiased but had wider confidence intervals. At 80% sensitivity, only 3 of 6 vaccination groups achieved 80% power with n=10,000 versus n=6,000 with perfect testing, emphasizing that power calculations must account for diagnostic test imperfection
articles.title_original: Power and sample size considerations for test-negative design with bias correction: a case study on the world first malaria vaccine.
articles.authors: Ko YK, Alfvén T, Yoneoka D

articles.headline: Recombinant P. falciparum glucose-regulated protein 170 (PfGrp170), an ER Hsp110, was expressed and biochemically characterized. The protein exhibited predominantly α-helical structure with Tm >55°C; ATP binding perturbed stability increasing heat sensitivity. PfGrp170 demonstrated basal ATPase activity, suppressed heat-induced aggregation of malate dehydrogenase substrate independent of nucleotides, and showed selective nucleotide binding with ATP preference over ADP. This first direct evidence of PfGrp170's chaperone function and nucleotide sensitivity indicates the protein could be targeted in antimalarial drug development strategies
articles.title_original: Biochemical and biophysical characterization of Plasmodium falciparum glucose regulated protein 170.

articles.headline: MalNet-DAF, a deep learning model combining stacked CNNs for spatial feature extraction and Bi-LSTM for temporal dependencies, was developed for P. vivax and P. falciparum parasite classification. The architecture incorporated Spatial Attention Module (SAM) and Temporal Attention Module (TAM) to enhance interpretability. Testing on NIH malaria dataset achieved 99.24% classification accuracy, outperforming traditional baseline models, demonstrating that dynamic attention-driven deep learning supports real-time clinical decision-making for malaria diagnostics
articles.title_original: MalNet-DAF: Dual-Attentive Fusion Deep Learning Model for Malaria Parasite Classification.

articles.headline: A cross-sectional survey (May–October 2023) assessed long-lasting insecticidal net (LLIN) coverage, access, and use in 1,575 households across 5 malaria-endemic Bangladeshi districts and 10 FDMN camps in Cox's Bazar. Among Bangladeshi households, 97.6% owned ≥1 LLIN with 93.2% sufficient coverage and 96.4% use; 95% of pregnant women and 98.3% of children <5 used LLINs. FDMN households: 98.2% ownership but only 44.3% sufficient coverage and 65.7% use. Bangladesh exceeded WHO 80% threshold among citizens; targeted interventions needed for FDMN population to achieve universal coverage
articles.title_original: Assessing long-lasting insecticidal net coverage, access, and use: Insights from malaria-endemic regions and Rohingya camps in Bangladesh.

articles.headline: Case report of 21-year-old immunocompetent male with P. falciparum malaria from Colombia presenting with severe respiratory distress (O₂ saturation <75%) who rapidly progressed to multiorgan failure including ARDS, refractory shock, acute kidney injury with hyperkalemia, and thrombocytopenia despite intensive management with mechanical ventilation, vasopressors, methylene blue, and transfusions, ultimately fatal with second cardiac arrest
articles.title_original: Severe Malaria Due to Plasmodium falciparum in an Immunocompetent Young Adult: Rapid Progression to Multiorgan Failure.
articles.authors: Sanclemente-Cardoza V, Payán-Salcedo HA, Estela-Zape JL

articles.headline: Experimental cerebral malaria model using Nr2f6-knockout vs wild-type C57BL/6N mice infected with Plasmodium berghei ANKA demonstrated that NR2F6 deficiency significantly improves survival with reduced endotheliopathy, preserved blood-brain barrier integrity, decreased cerebral parasitism, and reduced brain-infiltrating CD8+ T cells, suggesting NR2F6 pathway inhibitors as candidate neuroprotective therapy
articles.title_original: NR2F6 as a Disease Driver and Candidate Therapeutic Target in Experimental Cerebral Malaria.

articles.headline: Vector surveillance at a Florida deer farm identified 38 mosquito species, with four specializing on white-tailed deer (≥75% blood meals): Anopheles quadrimaculatus s.l. (88.9%, 4.1% P. odocoilei infection), An. punctipennis (83.3%, 3.1%), An. crucians s.l. (81.4%, 0.47%), and Culex erraticus (87.7%), incriminating An. quadrimaculatus and An. punctipennis as the principal natural vectors of deer malaria
articles.title_original: Incriminating vectors of deer malaria (Plasmodium odocoilei) at a Florida deer farm.

articles.headline: Case-control study in Mutare, Zimbabwe (2022-2023) comparing confirmed malaria cases to symptomatic negative controls identified risk factors: artisanal mining (RR 22, p<0.001), household proximity to still water/unprotected wells (RR 16, p<0.001), travel to endemic areas past 2 weeks (RR 9, p<0.001), and residential locale (RR 3, p=0.029); males had 4-fold higher odds than females, implicating both autochthonous and imported transmission
articles.title_original: Emergence of urban malaria and the associated risk factors: a case-control study in Mutare city, Zimbabwe.

articles.headline: RTS,S/AS01E malaria vaccine pilot implementation in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi (2021-2023) achieved ~80% coverage for first dose and ~75% for third dose, but fourth dose coverage remained low (median 46% in 2023); key implementation barriers included healthcare worker misunderstanding of age eligibility and service disruptions, though stakeholders reported reduced malaria hospitalizations and strengthened immunization programs
articles.title_original: Subnational introduction of the RTS,S/AS01(E) malaria vaccine into routine immunization: experience and lessons from the three pilot countries.

articles.headline: Transmission model incorporating larvicide+ITN trial data from Kenya and Côte d'Ivoire demonstrated entomological reductions of 82% (90% CI: 64–92%) in An. funestus and 88% (79–94%) in An. gambiae (Kenya) and 82% (56–93%) An. gambiae (Côte d'Ivoire), which modeled to 48–72% malaria prevalence reduction in Kenyan children aged 6 months–10 years and 11–78% all-age clinical incidence reduction in Côte d'Ivoire; high village-level heterogeneity limits simple regional extrapolation
articles.title_original: Heterogeneous impacts for malaria control from larviciding across villages and considerations for monitoring and evaluation.

articles.headline: CRISPR-Cas9 knockouts of plasmepsin II and III in Cambodian P. falciparum parasites with varying gene copy numbers showed deletion increases piperaquine sensitivity; high copy number parasites' enhanced survival was not explained by increased hemoglobin digestion, altered peptide composition, or increased free heme production, with evidence suggesting digestive vacuole pH modulation contributes to piperaquine resistance
articles.title_original: The plasmepsin-piperaquine paradox persists in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Co-infection model in C57BL/6 mice with Plasmodium berghei ANKA and Leishmania major showed acute malaria infection mitigates cutaneous leishmaniasis progression: reduced dermal lesion size and parasite burden, elevated serum TNF, and decreased infiltration of activated T cells and inflammatory monocytes to L. major infection sites, demonstrating cross-regulation of immune responses in co-infections
articles.title_original: Impact of co-infection with Plasmodium berghei ANKA in Leishmania major-parasitized mice on immune modulation and cutaneous leishmaniasis.

articles.headline: Entomological survey of 59 houses in Goden village, Burkina Faso identified Anopheles coluzzii as dominant vector (79% of An. gambiae s.l., n=1,492) with low anthropophily (human blood index 45%, n=687) but high sporozoite rate (10%, n=930) and 43% of blood meals containing erythrocytic parasites; spatial clustering in north-west hotspot showed infective mosquitoes with 1.8-fold higher anthropophily than non-infective ones
articles.title_original: The spatial heterogeneity of malaria transmission: An entomological investigation in a highly endemic setting of Burkina Faso.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis

articles.headline: Experimental cerebral malaria model found polycyclic derivatives SR4-02 and SR4-04, as adjuncts to artemether, significantly improved Barnes maze long-term memory and T-maze performance versus artemether monotherapy, reduced Cdk5-p25-mediated phospho-tau (Ser396) in hippocampus and cortex, and enhanced neuronal arborization in CA1/CA3, suggesting neuroprotective potential for cerebral malaria-associated cognitive impairment affecting ~25% of pediatric survivors
articles.title_original: Polycyclitol Derivatives Restore Long- Term Memory Via cdk5/p25 Activation of Tau Signaling in Experimental Cerebral Malaria.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling study developed novel temperature and altitude-dependent transmission functions for malaria SIR-SI compartmental models; physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) outperformed pure data-driven approaches (ANNs/RNNs) by embedding epidemiological dynamics for superior parameter inference, and Dynamic Mode Decomposition derived a novel data-driven transmission risk index enabling real-time disease risk assessment
articles.title_original: Analysis of a mathematical model for malaria using data-driven approach.
articles.authors: Rajnarayanan A, Kumar M, Tridane A

articles.headline: Bivalent malaria vaccine using vaccinia virus LC16m8Δ prime expressing Pfs25-PfCSP and Pvs25-PvCSP with AAV1 boost achieved 70% protection against both P. falciparum and P. vivax sporozoites and 90% transmission-blocking efficacy against P. vivax patient isolates from the Brazilian Amazon with immune responses sustained >7 months, outperforming monovalent vaccine combinations
articles.title_original: Malaria bivalent viral vectored vaccine protects against Plasmodium falciparum and vivax and blocks parasite transmission.

articles.headline: Mathematical model demonstrates that asymptomatic malaria infections contribute approximately 30% to the basic reproduction number (R₀) while symptomatic infections account for ~70%, revealing backward bifurcation. Findings indicate integrated control strategies addressing both symptomatic and asymptomatic cases are essential for effective malaria transmission management
articles.title_original: Impact of asymptomatic infections on malaria transmission dynamics.
articles.authors: Haringo AT, Obsu LL, Bushu FK

articles.headline: PCR screening of 133 blood samples from Assamese macaques in northern Thailand detected Plasmodium spp. DNA in 24.06% (32/133; 95% CI: 17.07–32.23), with P. knowlesi in 13.53% (18/133), P. cynomolgi in 9.77% (13/133), and P. inui in 0.75% (1/133). This is the first molecular evidence of these zoonotic species in this macaque population, establishing them as natural reservoirs and underscoring need for continued surveillance to prevent human transmission
articles.title_original: First molecular detection of zoonotic Plasmodium knowlesi, Plasmodium cynomolgi, and Plasmodium inui in Assamese macaques from northern Thailand.

articles.headline: Tephrosia villosa root extract (dichloromethane/methanol 1:1) at 400 mg/kg suppressed parasitemia by 24.78% in 4-day suppressive test, 52.16% in curative test, and 49.09% in prophylactic test (all p<0.001) against P. berghei in mice. Extract proved safe at 2 g/kg acute dose and inhibited anemia, weight loss, and hypothermia, supporting antimalarial efficacy in vivo
articles.title_original: Antimalarial activity of root extract of Tephrosia villosa L. Pers. (Fabaceae) on Plasmodium berghei-infected mice.

articles.headline: Comparison of five machine learning models on a validated synthetic dataset (87% representativeness to Sub-Saharan African epidemiology) found XGBoost achieved highest AUROC of 0.956 (95% CI: 0.952–0.961) with 2.8% improvement over Random Forest, and comparable performance to Enhanced Bayesian Logistic Regression (AUROC 0.954). XGBoost demonstrated optimal balance of accuracy and cost-effectiveness for malaria screening; real-world validation required before clinical deployment
articles.title_original: Comparative analysis of machine learning models for malaria detection using validated synthetic data: a cost-sensitive approach with clinical domain knowledge integration.

articles.headline: Transmission-blocking drug discovery platform combines in vitro screening assay using transgenic NF54/iGP1_RE9Hulg8 parasites expressing luciferase for reliable identification of stage V gametocytocidal compounds with preclinical in vivo model using humanized NODscidIL2Rγnull mice. Bioluminescence imaging and mosquito feeding assays revealed markedly different pharmacodynamic response profiles for antimalarial reference drugs and candidates, establishing systematic tool for evaluating transmission-blocking drug efficacy
articles.title_original: An all-in-one pipeline for the in vitro discovery and in vivo testing of Plasmodium falciparum malaria transmission blocking drugs.

articles.headline: Korea 2024 malaria vector surveillance across 76 sites detected cumulative trap index (TI) of 120.6, representing 66.6% increase from 2023 and 59.3% above 2020–2022 average. First peak TI of 18.2 occurred at week 27; Plasmodium vivax-positive mosquitoes identified in 3 pools (MIR=0.15) in Paju-si at weeks 31, 32, and 34, with MIR declining despite increased Anopheles density. Vector surveillance remains core strategy for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: [Malaria Vector Surveillance in 2024].

articles.headline: DRAG2 expanded multiplex assay incorporates additional drug resistance markers, merozoite surface protein 2 (MSP2), and 18S ribosomal RNA gene for targeted P. falciparum molecular surveillance via nanopore sequencing. Assay validated across parasitaemia range and sample types (venous blood, dried blood spots) with synthetic plasmid quality controls including test and control SNPs; transition to diagnostic assay requires further endemic-setting testing and regulatory approval
articles.title_original: An expanded method for malaria parasite genetic surveillance using targeted nanopore sequencing.

articles.headline: Learning curve analysis demonstrated that for P. falciparum artemisinin resistance prediction from gene expression data, balanced error rates declined from 50% (20 training samples) to 14% for sPLSDA+SVMs and 22% for random forests (835 training samples). Learning curve shape suggested further increases beyond 835 samples unlikely to significantly reduce error, providing practical framework for determining minimum sample sizes in malaria prediction modeling studies
articles.title_original: How to use learning curves to evaluate the sample size for malaria prediction models developed using machine learning algorithms.
articles.authors: Zaloumis SG, Rajasekhar M, Simpson JA

articles.headline: Title suggests ivermectin may provide therapeutic benefits for malaria control. No abstract available; additional detail required
articles.title_original: Ivermectin against Malaria - Good News in Bad Times.

articles.headline: Cluster-randomized trial in Kwale, Kenya (84 clusters, 28,932 participants) compared mass monthly ivermectin 400 μg/kg versus albendazole 400 mg for 3 consecutive months. At 6 months, malaria incidence was 2.20 per child-year (ivermectin) versus 2.66 per child-year (albendazole); adjusted incidence rate ratio 0.74 (95% CI: 0.58–0.95, p=0.02), representing 26% reduction. Serious adverse events did not differ significantly (IRR 0.63, 95% CI: 0.21–1.91)
articles.title_original: Ivermectin to Control Malaria - A Cluster-Randomized Trial.

articles.headline: Case report of 30s-year-old woman at 39 weeks gestation with undiagnosed malaria requiring emergent cesarean for severe fetal heart rate decelerations. Rapid induction with etomidate and succinylcholine minimized hemodynamic instability while securing airway; empirical antimalarial treatment initiated perioperatively based on travel history. Case emphasizes importance of flexible, anticipatory anesthetic strategies for urgent obstetric procedures complicated by undiagnosed systemic infections
articles.title_original: Emergent Cesarean Section Due to Malaria: A Perioperative Challenge.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 406 febrile patients in Rwanda (Dec 2023–Feb 2024) found high malaria knowledge (81% identified symptoms correctly, 72% understood transmission) but poor treatment adherence: of 180 patients receiving antimalarial treatment, only 46% (83/180) completed 3-day course; 37% (66/180) stopped within 2 days. Additionally, 27% (109/406) self-treated with antimalarials, majority obtaining from pharmacies. Poor adherence exacerbates artemisinin resistance risk despite high knowledge
articles.title_original: Understanding Malaria Treatment Adherence in Rwanda: Implications for Artemisinin Resistance.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey in Cameroon detected P. vivax in ~11% of febrile and 5.5% of asymptomatic Duffy-negative individuals, with superior sensitivity by qPCR versus PvLDH-RDTs. PvDBP1 mutations I379L (73%) and E225K (61%) predominated; phylogenetic analysis clustered Cameroonian isolates with Botswana but distinct from Ethiopian and Sudanese populations, indicating local adaptation and ongoing transmission despite Duffy-negative status
articles.title_original: Epidemiological Insights and Duffy Binding Protein Evolution of Plasmodium vivax in Duffy-Negative Cameroonians.

articles.headline: Flow cytometry of P. berghei ANKA-infected C57BL/6 mice revealed increases in Ly6C+ T cells, inflammatory monocytes, resident macrophages, and microglia during experimental cerebral malaria. Microglia and macrophages showed elevated inducible nitric oxide synthase expression and IL-1β and TNF production, demonstrating pronounced brain immune cell reactivity in cerebral malaria pathogenesis
articles.title_original: Microglia exhibit a dynamic response, modulating inducible nitric oxide synthase expression and the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines during experimental cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Prospective cohort of 378 children under five in Western Kenya: 81% of public health facilities diagnosed and treated P. falciparum, while only 18% managed dengue; longest hospitalizations (>5 days) occurred in Kisumu; recovery rates were 96.8% in Bunyala versus 91.0% in Kisumu. Facilities were vulnerable and unprepared to manage climate-driven infectious disease burden
articles.title_original: Public health facility vulnerabilities, preparedness, and health outcomes for Plasmodium falciparum and dengue virus-infected children under 5 years with acute febrile illnesses in Western Kenya.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 57 articles (1995–2024) on malaria and gender: 93% employed biomedical approaches that reduced gender analysis to sex-disaggregated data; majority used quantitative, laboratory-based designs focused on sub-Saharan Africa. Authors conclude qualitative, mixed-methods, and community-based research are needed to extend analysis beyond demographic categories to encompass resource access, labour distribution, norms, and decision-making power
articles.title_original: Conceptualization of gender in published malaria and gender research: a systematic descriptive review.

articles.headline: Community-based survey of 592 households in Uganda (August 2023): 368 participants (62%; 95% CI 58.2–65.9%) self-medicated for malaria, significantly associated with age ≥35 years (APR 1.77), distance ≥5 km to facility (APR 3.05), and home antimalarial storage (APR 2.21). Despite 85% using the recommended drug, 66% administered inappropriate doses; 85% recovered without hospitalization, highlighting drug-resistance risk and need for antimalarial supply, policy, and behavioural interventions
articles.title_original: Self-medication for malaria and associated factors in Kakumiro District, Uganda, August 2023: implications for malaria management and mortality prevention.

articles.headline: Multilevel analysis of 4,497 women from Tanzania Demographic and Health Survey 2022: optimal IPTp-SP uptake (≥3 doses) was 31.2% (95% CI 29.1–33.3). Associated factors included age 25–34 years (AOR 1.52) and 35–49 years (AOR 1.47), primary education (AOR 1.34), one or two children (AOR 1.65 and 1.37), and >3 antenatal care visits (AOR 1.77). Community-level ANC rates (AOR 1.49) and geographic zones (Northern AOR 1.61, Lake AOR 1.68) also drove uptake
articles.title_original: Individual and community-level correlates of optimal doses of sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine for intermittent preventive treatment of malaria during pregnancy in Tanzania: a multilevel analysis of the 2022 national survey.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 1,183 children under five: random forest analysis identified vaccine effectiveness perception, educational level, and age as the most influential factors for community engagement in malaria vaccine promotion. Vaccine safety and acceptability emerged as moderate contributors; individuals with higher education prioritized effectiveness and safety, while those with lower education emphasized cost and cultural considerations
articles.title_original: Community engagement in promoting malaria vaccine uptake for children Under-Five.

articles.headline: Systematic review of 122 studies (>6.5 million individuals, 5 decades): Plasmodium parasitaemia prevalence in blood donors ranged from ~0% to 0.87% by microscopy and ~0% to 2.3% by rapid diagnostic test; post-transfusion malaria incidence ranged from 0.8% to 6.8%. P. malariae often overlooked; P. vivax and P. falciparum prevalent; drug-resistant and pfhrp2 gene-deleted P. falciparum emerging threats requiring point-of-care nucleic acid screening
articles.title_original: Transfusion-transmitted Plasmodium spp. infections and safety challenges for malaria in the Indian subcontinent: a systematic review.
articles.authors: Kojom Foko LP, Sharma S, Sharma A

articles.headline: Systematic search of PCR-based malaria diagnosis studies: 93% used 18S rRNA gene as molecular target; 68% employed nested PCR; 85% of studies using 18S rRNA and nested PCR applied the approach developed in 1993. Authors caution that overreliance on a single molecular target is concerning and recommend research on newer multi-copy targets with improved limit of detection, reproducibility, cost, and multiplexing capacity
articles.title_original: Overreliance on Plasmodium 18S rRNA gene for malaria molecular diagnosis-inferences from systematic review.

articles.headline: Electroencephalography comparison in Malawian children (3 months–14 years) with retinopathy-negative cerebral malaria, retinopathy-positive cerebral malaria, and nonmalarial coma: quantitative EEG distinguished retinopathy-negative from nonmalarial coma (AUROC 0.83); combined qualitative and quantitative methods improved discrimination (AUROC 0.87). EEG patterns in retinopathy-negative cerebral malaria resembled retinopathy-positive cerebral malaria, supporting acute malarial pathophysiology in retinopathy-negative cases
articles.title_original: Using Electroencephalography to Assess Coma Etiology in Children with Retinopathy-Negative Cerebral Malaria.

articles.headline: Genetic investigation in Plasmodium parasites revealed that apicoplast chaperonin CPN60 ablation causes rapid parasite death in the first replication cycle, distinct from delayed death with translation inhibitors. CPN60 regulates proteostasis through direct interaction with the caseinolytic protease complex; bacterial GroEL inhibitors demonstrated anti-Plasmodium activity beyond apicoplast function, identifying chaperonin inhibition as a potential antimalarial target
articles.title_original: A chaperonin complex regulates organelle proteostasis in malaria parasites.

articles.headline: Plasma analysis in P. vivax malaria patients from Colombia: IFNγ, IL-6, and IL-10 significantly elevated in thrombocytopenic patients (P<0.05), while TGF-β1 increased in malaria without thrombocytopenia (P<0.0001). Anti-phosphatidylserine autoantibody levels were higher in thrombocytopenic patients and negatively correlated with platelet counts, suggesting thrombocytopenia results from autoantibodies targeting activated platelet phosphatidylserine driven by pro-inflammatory cytokine imbalance
articles.title_original: Thrombocytopenia in patients with Plasmodium vivax in Colombia is associated with anti-phosphatidylserine autoantibodies and IL-6, IFNγ, IL-10 and TGF-β.

articles.headline: Scoping review of 63 studies across 27 countries (Africa 68.3%, Asia 28.6%, 2000-2024) found significant regional variations in community knowledge of malaria transmission and prevention. Higher awareness consistently linked with better preventive practices and earlier treatment-seeking, yet critical gaps persisted regarding environmental risk factors and vector control. Community knowledge identified as pivotal for prevention success, requiring locally tailored education addressing knowledge gaps
articles.title_original: A scoping review of community knowledge in malaria prevention and control programmes.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional linkage of Demographic and Health Survey and Service Provision Assessment data (2012-2019) examined intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (IPTp-SP) across Senegal. Average doses 1.66 (95% CI 1.65-1.68) overall; urban 1.73 vs rural 1.63. Each unit increase in malaria service readiness increased doses by 0.251, but rural women received significantly fewer doses (0.089 vs 0.612 urban; coefficient −0.523, P<0.001). Urban-rural disparities require differential strategies
articles.title_original: Bridging urban-rural disparities in malaria care during pregnancy in Senegal: evidence from household and health facility surveys.

articles.headline: Correction notice to a previously published article on CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing systems in Plasmodium berghei. This correction contains no substantive research findings or quantitative results
articles.title_original: Correction to 'A scalable CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing system facilitates CRISPR screens in the malaria parasite Plasmodium berghei'.

articles.headline: Indoor comparison of three UV light traps (BG-Pro, Silver Bullet 2.1 UV, CDC UV LED) over 64 nights in 8 houses in western Kenya. Silver Bullet 2.1 captured 4.3× more An. gambiae s.l. (95% CI 2.5–7.3) and 7.1× more An. funestus s.l. (95% CI 3.9–13.1) than CDC standard; BG-Pro was 2.0–3.5× more efficient. SB 2.1 also caught 3× more Culex spp. (RR 2.7, 95% CI 1.2–6.0). Overall 3–6 fold higher trapping efficiency
articles.title_original: Assessment of BG-Pro (Biogent AG) and Silver Bullet 2.1 (Lumin8) UV-Light Traps Efficiency for Surveillance of Malaria Vectors in Western Kenya.

articles.headline: Case-control study of 293 participants with malaria or dengue (Nov 2022–Aug 2024) in Southern India: 23.9% with malaria (30% severe) and 76.1% with dengue (35.8% severe). Prior COVID-19 infection dramatically elevated odds of severe disease: 98.1% with dengue plus prior COVID-19 developed severe dengue (aOR 55.93, 95% CI 7.17–435.92, p<0.001); 85.7% with malaria developed severe malaria (aOR 263.7, 95% CI 34.9–1987.4, p<0.001)
articles.title_original: COVID-19 infection and its association with severe malaria & dengue: an epidemiological study from Southern India.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional 2021 study comparing three national standard mRDTs and one research mRDT against qPCR in Tanzania found malaria rapid diagnostic test accuracy varied significantly by age, gender, and regional transmission level. Research test showed poor sensitivity across transmission strata. Transmission intensity influenced mRDT agreement with qPCR, highlighting important test reliability differences and measurement bias concerns for malaria surveillance
articles.title_original: Varying Malaria Rapid Diagnostic Test Accuracy by Regional Transmission Level and Demographics in Tanzania.

articles.headline: Computational analysis identified 2,265 MHC I and 1,992 MHC II conserved epitopes from 42 Plasmodium falciparum vaccine candidate antigens using sequence data from 18 African countries and HLA frequencies from 24 sub-Saharan populations. Key MHC I epitopes from pre-erythrocytic antigens (HSP70-2, SLARP/SAP1, p36, FabZ, LISP1, LSA1, UIS3, p24_2, PL, FabG) achieved near 100% HLA-A/B/C coverage; MHC II epitopes provided 98.5–100% stage-specific coverage, advancing multistage vaccine design
articles.title_original: Computational Prioritization of T Cell Epitopes to Overcome HLA Restriction and Antigenic Diversity in Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Whole-genome analysis of 4,306 mosquitoes from 21 countries identified extensive positive selection in An. gambiae complex and An. funestus. Selection confirmed at established insecticide resistance loci (Vgsc, Rdl, Ace1, Cyp6p, Cyp9k1, Gste, Coeaexf) and novel signals at diacylglycerol kinase and Keap1, with parallel evolution between species. Provides genomic foundation for monitoring emerging resistance variants and informing evidence-based vector control strategies
articles.title_original: An atlas of positive selection in the genomes of major malaria vectors.

articles.headline: Retrospective spatial analysis of malaria surveillance 2007–2023 using Malaria Mobility Networks in Brazil's Yanomami Indigenous Territory revealed 86% of cases concentrated in 12% of grids. Autochthonous malaria in indigenous localities reached 90% (2023); transmission from illegal gold mining (garimpo) peaked ~27% (2022), predominantly linked to urban residence. Spatial patterns demonstrate garimpo-urban networks facilitating disease transmission and local transmission persistence
articles.title_original: Human mobility, garimpos and spatiotemporal malaria transmission in the Yanomami Indigenous Territory: a retrospective observational study.

articles.headline: Facility-based cross-sectional study with qualitative component (March–July 2024) reviewing 1,684 patient cards in Southwest Ethiopia found healthcare provider adherence to malaria diagnosis and treatment guidelines at only 36.99% (95% CI 33.93–38.52%). 95.96% screened via microscopy/RDT; 58.31% diagnosed P. falciparum. Primary barriers: drug shortage, inadequate guideline training, health professional shortage, and laboratory material deficiency. Enhanced supply, training, and supervision urgently needed
articles.title_original: Evaluation of anti-malarial treatment for elimination of malaria in South West Ethiopia: a concurrent triangulation design for prompt decision.

articles.headline: Case-control study (n=99) measured serum endocan in healthy controls, P. vivax (n=36), uncomplicated P. falciparum (n=30), and severe P. falciparum (n=13) malaria at baseline and day 7 post-treatment. All malaria groups showed elevated endocan vs controls (p<0.0001), with severity-dependent gradient: severe P. falciparum 4.67 ng/ml (IQR 2.85–7.93), uncomplicated P. falciparum 3.27 (2.24–4.33), P. vivax 1.85 (1.44–3.23). Endocan correlated with parasite density and thrombocytopenia
articles.title_original: Preliminary assessment of serum endocan as a biomarker of disease severity in Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax malaria.
articles.authors: Nantavisai K, Puttikamonkul S, Viriyavejakul P

articles.headline: Molecular analysis of 1,199 P. falciparum samples from 12 sentinel sites across 5 Ethiopian regions (2019–2023) identified k13 R622I mutation in 15.7% with marked regional variation. Three artemisinin resistance mutations (P441L, P574L, A675V) detected for first time in Ethiopia; A675V found exclusively in clinic serving Sudan/South Sudan refugees. Nearly fixed partner drug resistance polymorphisms (crt, mdr1, dhps, dhfr); 87.2% monogenomic infections suggest clonal transmission
articles.title_original: Rising prevalence of Plasmodium falciparum Artemisinin partial resistance mutations in Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 24 cluster randomized malaria trials examining coefficient of variation (k) for cluster heterogeneity. Empirical k values typically exceeded those used in original sample size calculations, reducing power; elevated k associated with incidence outcomes (vs prevalence), lower endemicity settings, and uneven intervention coverage across clusters
articles.title_original: Characterisation of between-cluster heterogeneity in malaria cluster randomised trials to inform future sample size calculations.

articles.headline: Cost-effectiveness analysis of precision guided sterile insect technique (pgSIT) against Anopheles gambiae in The Gambia, used alongside existing interventions. Predicted ~230 annual deaths averted and 48,000 sick days prevented; costs 0.36–3.03 USD/person/year with 11–94 USD per DALY and 10–86 USD per averted infection
articles.title_original: Evaluating the cost of malaria elimination by Anopheles gambiae precision guided SIT in the Upper River region, The Gambia.

articles.headline: In vitro evaluation of CHIR-124 (human Chk1 inhibitor) against P. falciparum asexual blood stages, liver, and gametocyte forms, both drug-sensitive and drug-resistant strains. Dual inhibition of PfArk1 and hemozoin formation with reduced propensity for resistance generation; polypharmacology supports activity in resistant strains
articles.title_original: The Human Chk1 Inhibitor CHIR-124 Shows Multistage Activity Against Plasmodium falciparum via Dual Inhibition of PfArk1 and Hemozoin Formation.

articles.headline: Full blood count analysis in 1,392 P. falciparum-infected adults across endemic (Gabon) and non-endemic (Germany) settings. Platelets decreased with severity (218 vs 150 ×10³/µL in Gabon, P<0.0001; 85 vs 40 in Germany, P<0.0001); machine learning classifier achieved AUC 0.94 and 0.84 for severity prediction
articles.title_original: Comparative Analysis of Initial Full Blood Count Parameters in Adults Infected With Plasmodium falciparum for Classification of Disease Severity and Previous Exposure Across Endemic (Gabon) and Nonendemic (Germany) Settings.

articles.headline: Case-control study (45 malaria patients, 50 controls) of full blood count-derived inflammatory biomarkers. Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (NLR) showed excellent diagnostic value with AUC 0.937, sensitivity 86.7%, and specificity 92.0%; NLR ≥2.12 predicted inflammatory state at 86.7% sensitivity and 92.0% specificity
articles.title_original: Diagnostic value of full blood count derived systemic inflammatory biomarkers in malaria infection.

articles.headline: P. yoelii murine infection study of splenic macrophage subset functions. Red pulp macrophage depletion elevated parasitemia; marginal metallophilic and marginal zone macrophage depletion reduced CD4+ T-cell IFNγ production without affecting parasitemia progression
articles.title_original: Red pulp macrophages clear parasites, while marginal metallophilic and marginal zone macrophages support CD4(+) T cell activation during Plasmodium yoelii infection.

articles.headline: Review of P. falciparum pathogenesis, immune evasion, and drug resistance mechanisms with discussion of emerging therapeutics including RTS,S and R21 vaccines and antibody-based therapies; identifies extending vaccine duration and ensuring strain-diverse effectiveness as major unmet challenges
articles.title_original: Emerging Molecular Mechanisms in Malaria Pathogenesis and Novel Therapeutic Approaches: A Focus on P. falciparum Malaria.

articles.headline: Case-crossover analysis of 5,381,105 malaria cases in Brazilian Amazon (2003–2022) examining weather and ENSO impacts. Temperature 25.64–30.85°C increased infection risk 9% (95% CI 8–10%); precipitation >4.46 cm increased risk 86% (95% CI 35–155%); El Niño and La Niña reduced cases by 2,179 and 37,258 respectively
articles.title_original: The impact of weather and extreme events on malaria transmission in the Brazilian Amazon: a case-crossover and population-based study.

articles.headline: Analysis of 2022 malaria epidemic in 64 of 146 Uganda districts (DHIS2 data 2017–2022). 3,379,309 (95% CI 1,553,714–5,339,709) excess outpatient cases, 3,018,920 confirmed cases, and 149,789 excess admissions; overall 31% case rise in 2022 versus 2017–2021 baseline
articles.title_original: Malaria epidemics and its drivers in Uganda in 2022.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional household survey (1,377 children <5 years, 254 pregnant women in Guinea/Sierra Leone, May–August 2022) of malaria care cascade. Testing rates 42–96% (children) and 62–91% (pregnant women); treatment within 2 days achieved in 54–81% of children and 24–69% of pregnant women; hospitalization 6.5–4.3% and 9.5–12% respectively
articles.title_original: The malaria care cascade and factors associated with receiving a malaria test in children under 5 years of age and pregnant women with fever: a cross-sectional household survey from three regions in Guinea and three districts in Sierra Leone in 2022.

articles.headline: Analysis of 751 pregnancies from Malawi trial examining placental malaria (PM) and preeclampsia. PM present in 125 (16.7%), preeclampsia in 25 (3.3%), eclampsia in 6 (0.7%); hemozoin (early PM indicator) associated with higher preeclampsia (6.7% vs 3.7%, P=0.16) but study underpowered to reach significance
articles.title_original: The Relationship between Placental Malaria and Preeclampsia in Blantyre, Malawi.

articles.headline: Modeling study of malaria vaccination strategies in Madagascar to address climate-driven disruptions to control interventions. Quantified symptomatic infection reduction across vaccination scenarios; recently available vaccines offer extended protection duration to mitigate critical gaps following extreme weather events
articles.title_original: Vaccination to mitigate climate-driven disruptions to malaria control in Madagascar.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 329 school children in Hawassa, Ethiopia (April-June 2024) found 8.5% malaria and 9.4% anemia prevalence; malaria-infected children were 13.3 times more likely to be anemic (AOR=13.258, 95% CI: 3.188–55.139); insecticide-treated nets (AOR=0.024) and malaria knowledge (AOR=0.205) reduced malaria risk, while meal frequency and wasting history increased anemia risk, suggesting need for integrated prevention and nutrition programs
articles.title_original: Prevalence of malaria, anemia and associated factors among school children in Hawassa city, Sidama, Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Gena A, Asnake S, Menjetta T

articles.headline: Eight-month study (October 2024–May 2025) of 749 Moroccan soldiers in the Central African Republic on weekly mefloquine chemoprophylaxis found 156 (20.8%) experienced malaria episodes with 89% caused by Plasmodium falciparum, and 8 (1.1%) developed severe malaria; ABO and Rh blood groups showed no significant association with malaria incidence, recurrence, or severity, suggesting robust prevention strategies overshadow genetic influences
articles.title_original: Impact of the ABO and Rh Blood Groups on Malaria Incidence, Recurrence, and Severity Among a Moroccan Military Contingent in the Central African Republic.

articles.headline: Molecular study describing how the var2csa gene enables transcriptional switching between var genes through coordinated action of noncoding RNAs, upstream open reading frames, and nonsense-mediated mRNA decay, allowing Plasmodium falciparum parasites to evade antibody recognition and maintain chronic infection by sequentially expressing different PfEMP1 isoforms
articles.title_original: The molecular basis of coordinated antigen switching enabling chronic infection by human malaria parasites.

articles.headline: Perspective article on the malaria crisis affecting children in Uganda and the urgent need for vaccination and coordinated strategic action to address the burden of disease
articles.title_original: Addressing the malaria crisis among children in Uganda: the urgent need for vaccination and strategic action.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 207 children <5 years with uncomplicated malaria diagnosed by HRP2 RDT in Nanoro, Burkina Faso (July 2021–June 2022) revealed nested PCR detected 11 undetected non-falciparum infections (6 P. ovale, 5 P. malariae sub-patent) beyond 4 detected by microscopy; mixed infections had lower parasite densities than P. falciparum monoinfections, highlighting that HRP2-based RDTs significantly underestimate the burden of non-falciparum species
articles.title_original: Undetected Plasmodium malariae and P. ovale infections in HRP2 RDT-positive children with uncomplicated malaria in Nanoro, Burkina Faso.

articles.headline: Comparative study of 30 P. knowlesi-infected humans and 35 wild macaques (Macaca fascicularis) in Sabah, Malaysia found infected macaques had significantly higher parasite gDNA and mtDNA than humans; humans showed IL-10 dominance (positively correlated with parasite gDNA) while macaques showed IL-6 dominance (unrelated to gDNA), and infected patients exhibited higher IL-10/TNF ratios despite lower parasite gDNA, suggesting disease severity and distinct immune responses between host species in different infection phases
articles.title_original: Comparative immunological study of Plasmodium knowlesi infections in humans and macaques: insights into cytokine dynamics.
articles.authors: Abd Rachman Isnadi MF, et al.

articles.headline: Staged implementation study protocol in Indonesia and Papua New Guinea evaluating G6PD-guided primaquine dosing for P. vivax radical cure: high-dose primaquine 7 mg/kg over 7 days for G6PD normal (≥70% activity), 14 days for intermediate (30–<70%), or weekly low-dose over 8 weeks for deficient (<30%), with Stage 1 (n=800) assessing safety and Stage 2 (n≈11,410) assessing feasibility and cost-effectiveness of this revised case management package to eliminate P. vivax by 2030
articles.title_original: High daily dose Short COurse PrimaquinE after G6PD testing for the radical cure of Plasmodium vivax malaria in Indonesia and Papua New Guinea: the SCOPE implementation study protocol.

articles.headline: Automated diagnostic framework using ResNet 50, VGG16, and DenseNet-201 deep learning models with feature fusion, dimensionality reduction, and majority voting validated on 27,558 microscopic thin blood smear images achieved 96.47% accuracy, 96.03% sensitivity, 96.90% specificity, and 96.45% F1-score for malaria detection, demonstrating superior performance of AI-driven solutions in diagnostic reliability
articles.title_original: Automated multi-model framework for malaria detection using deep learning and feature fusion.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 77 newborns within 24 hours of delivery at State Specialist Hospital, Osogbo, southwestern Nigeria found 6.5% prevalence of congenital malaria (parasites in peripheral or cord blood); maternal malarial parasitaemia was a strong predictor (p<0.05), while maternal retroviral status, intermittent prophylactic therapy, antimalarial treatment, and bed net use showed no significant association, indicating the critical role of maternal parasitaemia in congenital transmission
articles.title_original: Prevalance and risk factors of congenital malaria in Osogbo, Southwestern Nigeria.

articles.headline: Quantitative nanomorphological analysis of Anopheles darlingi and Anopheles aquasalis wings using atomic force microscopy, Minkowski functionals, and multifractal formalism revealed pronounced interspecific and dorso-ventral asymmetries; An. aquasalis exhibited higher elevation variance, denser nanomorphological heterogeneity, larger multifractal spectrum width (Δα≈1.073 vs 1.009), and generalized fractal dimensions (D0≈2.00; D2: 1.951–1.982) indicative of greater nanomorphological complexity
articles.title_original: Unveiling the Nanomorphological Architecture of Malaria Vectors' Wings via Minkowski and Multifractal Formalisms.

articles.headline: Corrigendum correcting a previously published article on the Wildlife Malaria Research Network workshop
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "The Wildlife Malaria Research network (WIMANET): Meeting report on the 1st WIMANET workshop" [Int. J. Parasitol: Parasites and Wildlife 25 (2024) 100989].

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey in rural communities near the Singrobo-Ahouaty dam, southern Côte d'Ivoire established baseline malaria epidemiology with 43.1% prevalence (394/915) and mean parasite density 405.7 parasites/µL; school-age children (6–13 years) showed highest prevalence (74.3%, p<0.0001) while children 0–5 years had highest density (1218.0 parasites/µL, p<0.0001); risk factors included informal work (ORa=1.5), raw material sector (ORa=1.4), and frequent mosquito bites (OR=0.4, p=0.017), highlighting need for strengthened vector control and bed net distribution
articles.title_original: Epidemiological Profile and Risk Factors for Malaria in Rural Communities Before the Operationalization of the Singrobo-Ahouaty Dam, Southern Côte d'Ivoire.

articles.headline: Literature review advocating increased research investment in traditional African medicine for malaria control in Nigeria. While emphasizing Artemisia annua's historical contribution to modern antimalarial development, the paper primarily examines existing literature on herbal remedies' historical use and calls for comprehensive scientific validation, enhanced regulation, and integration into national health systems—presenting no new quantitative efficacy data
articles.title_original: Investing in traditional medicine: leveraging evidence and innovative research to strengthen the fight against malaria in Nigeria.

articles.headline: Among 445 individuals with suspected malaria symptoms in Golestan Province, Iran (March–December 2023), 4.94% (22/445) tested positive: 77.27% P. vivax with mean parasitemia 16,029±5,060 parasites/µL, 18.18% P. falciparum (105,460±102,146 parasites/µL), and 4.54% co-infected. All 22 patients showed positive treatment response. The sudden 22-case increase from prior years, combined with detection of gametocytes in some P. falciparum specimens, raises concern for potential local transmission establishment
articles.title_original: The resurgence of malaria in Northern Iran in 2023: a wake-up call.

articles.headline: Prospective multi-site cohort study protocol (trial registration ISRCTN10885288) enrolling 750 children aged 3 months–<16 years with severe malaria and 375 community controls across three Ugandan hospitals. The study will characterize kidney disease prevalence and risk factors following severe malaria-associated acute kidney injury, with 2-year follow-up assessing short- and long-term kidney function, cognitive outcomes, and health-related quality of life—no results yet available
articles.title_original: Malaria associated pathogenesis of chronic kidney disease (MAP-CKD): a prospective study of children hospitalized with severe malaria.

articles.headline: Genetic profiling of >13,000 blood samples from a six-year longitudinal cohort of 464 Malian children (ages 3 months–25 years) revealed molecular force of infection (molFOI, distinct parasite acquisitions) varied markedly (0–55 in 2011) but was consistent within individuals across seasons and independent of age. The HbS allele and functional antibodies to CSP C-terminus and RH5 antigens correlated with low molFOI, identifying immune correlates of protection and indicating heterogeneous exposure to infectious mosquito bites as the primary driver of inter-individual infection variation
articles.title_original: Marked heterogeneity in malaria infection rate in a Malian longitudinal cohort.

articles.headline: Among 220 symptomatic malaria patients in central Ethiopia (2023): P. falciparum prevalence 86.4%, with pfk13 artemisinin-resistance marker R622I in 1.1% (2/174) and candidate P441L in 1.7% (3/174). High-frequency pfhrp2/pfhrp3 deletions (24.0% and 78.8% respectively; 22.7% dual deletions) render these infections undetectable by HRP-based rapid diagnostic tests. pfmdr1 patterns indicated reduced lumefantrine sensitivity, suggesting substantial diagnostic and treatment challenges
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Kelch13 Variants and Histidine-Rich Protein 2/3 Deletions in Central Ethiopia, 2023.

articles.headline: Narrative review synthesizing current knowledge on environmental determinants (temperature, humidity, elevation, precipitation, seasonality, land use) of Anopheles vector bionomics and transmission of zoonotic malaria (P. knowlesi, P. cynomolgi, P. inui, P. fieldi) across Southeast Asia. The paper advocates adopting a One Health approach integrating vector surveillance, sustainable land management, and targeted interventions—presenting no quantitative empirical results
articles.title_original: Impact of environmental factors on the bionomics of Anopheles mosquito vectors of zoonotic malaria: A narrative review.

articles.headline: Antibody screening against 698 P. falciparum recombinant antigens in Kenyan women identified three antigens with statistically significant association to multigravidity: PF3D7_1301800 (SURFIN 13.1), PF3D7_0424400 (SURFIN 4.2), and PF3D7_1252100 (rhoptry neck antigen 3 domain). VAR2CSA domains showed 42–62% seroprevalence but no significant gravidity association, suggesting the three identified antigens as candidates for prioritized vaccine development to reduce malaria in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Exploring the P. falciparum antigens associated with reduced risk of malaria in pregnancy.

articles.headline: In vitro and ex vivo study of P. vivax-infected Brazilian patients showing that IDO-mediated tryptophan catabolism (elevated KYN/TRP ratios) and AhR signaling drive regulatory T cell (Treg) expansion during acute infection. Patients experiencing first malaria episodes exhibited elevated KYN/TRP ratios (mean not specified) and increased Treg frequencies with positive correlation; those with prior infections showed significantly lower Treg frequencies, plasma IFN-γ, and KYN/TRP ratios, linking immune tolerance mechanisms to parasitemia control
articles.title_original: IDO1-AhR axis increases T regulatory cells in Plasmodium vivax malaria infection.

articles.headline: In vivo therapeutic efficacy study of 828 Mozambican children (6 months–11 years) with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria comparing artemether-lumefantrine (AL, n=462), artesunate-amodiaquine (AS-AQ, n=183), dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DP, n=91), and artesunate-pyronaridine (AS-PY, n=92). Day 28 polymerase chain reaction-corrected efficacy: AL 97.63–100%, AS-AQ 100%; day 42 DP and AS-PY efficacy 97.75–100%; adverse events <2%. All four artemisinin-based combinations exceeded WHO acceptable thresholds and remain safe and effective alternatives
articles.title_original: Efficacy of artemether-lumefantrine, artesunate-amodiaquine, dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine and artesunate-pyronaridine for the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Mozambique, 2022.

articles.headline: Secondary analysis of 2022 Tanzania Demographic and Health Survey (58,425 individuals aged 15–95 years) documenting 11% overall disability prevalence with disparities: age ≥50 years (adjusted prevalence ratio [APR] 3.85, 95% CI 3.33–4.52), female gender (APR 2.67, 95% CI 2.63–2.71), secondary education (APR 2.58), separated/widowed status (APR 2.91), wealthiest households (APR 2.74), and health insurance holders (APR 2.83). Northern zone had highest prevalence (APR 2.77) compared to Lake zone—primarily a disability epidemiology study with limited malaria-specific findings
articles.title_original: Socioeconomic and geographical disparities in disability distribution among Tanzanian population: insights from the 2022 demographic and health survey and malaria indicator survey.

articles.headline: Molecular surveillance of 67 P. falciparum isolates from northwestern Ecuador (2019–2021) showing significant shifts in antimalarial resistance haplotypes versus 2013–2015 baseline: chloroquine-resistant CVMET (Pfcrt) and pyrimethamine-resistant CICNI (Pfdhfr) both reached 100% dominance in Esmeraldas; novel NEDFSDFY haplotype (Pfmdr-1) and A16V (Pfdhfr) detected for first time. Crucially, wild-type SAKAA (Pfdhps) and MYRIC (Pfk13) remained 100% prevalent, indicating artemisinin and sulfadoxine sensitivity preserved and current artemether-lumefantrine plus primaquine regimen remains viable
articles.title_original: Tracking Plasmodium falciparum antimalarial resistance markers during a malaria pre-elimination period in the Pacific coast of South America.

articles.headline: Literature review examining artificial intelligence applications in malaria diagnosis, surveillance, treatment optimization, and vector control. The paper highlights AI's role in personalised medicine, accelerating drug discovery, improving diagnostics, and addressing drug-resistant parasites through machine learning, deep learning, and in silico drug repurposing—discussing potential innovations without reporting quantitative efficacy results from clinical trials or implementations
articles.title_original: An insight into the role of artificial intelligence in combating malaria: recent developments.

articles.headline: Mixed-methods study of 1-3-7 reactive surveillance and response (RASR) in northeast Cambodia found 72% (2020) and 59% (2022) of cases notified/investigated same-day, with reactive case detection timeliness declining from 89% to 45%. Despite challenges including poor mobile coverage and heavy workload, RASR was deemed acceptable; achieving malaria elimination requires sufficient budget, capacity building, and enhanced community engagement
articles.title_original: Evaluating malaria reactive surveillance and response strategies in northeast Cambodia: a mixed-methods study.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 220 febrile patients (154 Pf-positive by PCR) in Senegal found BIOCREDIT Pf (pLDH/HRPII) RDT achieved highest sensitivity 78% (95% CI 70.9-84.5%) and specificity 89% (95% CI 79.4-95.6%). Combined HRP2 and LDH performance exceeded microscopy (53% sensitivity), but PfLDH lines alone (61-64%) could not match SD BIOLINE HRP2 performance, indicating dual-target design necessary in populations with low hrp2/hrp3 deletions
articles.title_original: Performance and usability evaluation of three LDH-based malaria rapid diagnostic tests in Kédougou, Senegal.

articles.headline: Case-control study of severe malaria hospitalizations in extra-Amazon Brazil (2011-2019) identified major risk factors: low education (OR 2.21 elementary, OR 1.72 high school), infection outside region (OR 1.50-1.72), high parasitemia (OR 1.51-3.15 with >100,000/mm³), and delayed treatment ≥3 days (OR 1.74-2.08). Active case detection was protective (OR 0.65). Results reveal healthcare access inequality and emphasize prompt treatment to prevent severe malaria
articles.title_original: Factors associated with hospitalizations due to severe malaria in the non-endemic Brazilian region: a case-control study in the extra-Amazon Region from 2011 to 2019.

articles.headline: PRISMA-based systematic review of 20 literature sources identified 40 plant species from 24 families used for traditional anti-malarial treatment in Mayurbhanj, Odisha, with Acanthaceae and Apocyanaceae predominant. Formulations were primarily decoctions, pastes, juices, and powders administered orally using leaves and roots; no side effects reported. Further study required to validate therapeutic efficacy and elucidate mechanisms
articles.title_original: A review on the ethnomedicinal plants used for the traditional treatment of malaria in the Mayurbhanj district of Odisha, India.

articles.headline: Genetic analysis of P. falciparum msp1/2 markers from three Cameroonian regions revealed K1 and MAD20 families with high structural diversity via amino acid repeats (SGT/SGP/SAQ and SGG/SVA), RO33 with multiple mutation points, and variable repeat patterns in IC/3D7 and FC27. K1/RO33 sequences clustered phylogenetically with China/Myanmar isolates, while MAD20/IC/3D7/FC27 grouped with India/Panama/Papua New Guinea, indicating geographically structured population diversity
articles.title_original: Genetic Diversity and Phylogenetic Relatedness of Cameroonian Plasmodium falciparum Isolates and Comparative Analysis with Global Populations.
articles.authors: Kojom Foko LP, Hawadak J, Singh V

articles.headline: Development of Ookluc, a transgenic P. berghei line expressing nanoluciferase exclusively after zygote formation, enables high-throughput screening for transmission-blocking compounds. The luminescence-based assay quantifies gamete-to-zygote and zygote-to-ookinete differentiation, with increased signal correlating directly to ookinete formation, thereby accelerating discovery of novel transmission-blocking interventions targeting sexual parasite stages
articles.title_original: Ookluc: A Plasmodium berghei Line for Identifying Transmission-blocking Compounds.

articles.headline: Two-patch mathematical model of malaria with asymptomatic cases, transmission-blocking drugs (full but waning immunity), migration, and screening found malaria better controlled by allowing detected infected entry with treatment in the better-resourced patch versus deportation, which risked illegal entry. Numerical simulations support treatment-based entry policies over exclusion for achieving superior disease control in heterogeneous-resource settings
articles.title_original: A meta-population model of malaria with asymptomatic cases, transmission blocking drugs, migration and screening.
articles.authors: Tchoumi SY, Banasiak J, Ouifki R

articles.headline: Analysis of 192 P. vivax isolates from three Amazon regions identified dbp gene amplification in 25% of western isolates (up to 8 copies) versus 2-9% in southwestern/eastern regions (2-3 copies), and ebp2 amplification in 15% western versus 1-4% other regions. All individuals were DARC-positive with no association between gene copy number variations and DARC genotypes, suggesting selection pressures independent of Duffy receptor polymorphisms
articles.title_original: The pattern of gene amplification of members of the Plasmodium vivax erythrocyte binding-like proteins family across the Amazon rainforest.

articles.headline: Machine learning analysis of 370 P. berghei ANKA-infected mice across three strains found 64.5% developed experimental cerebral malaria (ECM) with onset days 5-11. Parasitemia increase ≥0.05 between days 1-3 predicted ECM development with 97% sensitivity; Random Forest model predicted exact ECM onset day with high precision (mean absolute error 0.43, R²=0.64), enabling accurate identification of high-risk animals
articles.title_original: Temporal Parasitemia Trends Predict Risk and Timing of Experimental Cerebral Malaria in Mice Infected by Plasmodium berghei ANKA.
