articles.headline: Scoping review protocol (OSF-registered) will systematically search PubMed, Embase, Science Direct, Cochrane, and Google Scholar for scientific articles and grey literature on African stakeholder knowledge, perspectives, and risk perceptions regarding gene drive and genetically modified mosquitoes for malaria control. Results will synthesize views critical for informing community engagement strategies necessary for gene drive implementation success in African settings
articles.title_original: Knowledge, Perspectives, and Risks Perceptions on Gene Drive and Genetically Modified Mosquitoes for Malaria Control among African Stakeholders: A Scoping Review Protocol.

articles.headline: High-throughput proteomics study of malaria-exposed pregnant women in sub-Saharan Africa identified elevated plasma FCGR2B and heme oxygenase-1 (HO-1) in P. falciparum-positive pregnancies, while neurturin (NRTN) and IL-20 were downregulated. IL-20 emerged as the top candidate biomarker for pregnancy-associated malaria and placental infection, warranting validation in large cohorts with concurrent placental histopathology
articles.title_original: Plasma protein biomarkers of Plasmodium falciparum infection in pregnant women: a high-throughput proteomics study.

articles.headline: Global genetic analysis of P. vivax antigens DBPII and MSP5 revealed extensive haplotypic diversity, with sequence identity to reference Sal-1 strain ranging from 0.4% (MSP5) to 99% (S16). Testing antibody responses in Brazil and Thailand cohorts showed most antigens (5/8) maintained serological exposure marker accuracy despite genetic diversity, while region-dependent performance (RBP2a, DBPII) improved using multiple antigens in classification models
articles.title_original: Navigating parasite antigen genetic diversity in the design of Plasmodium vivax serological exposure markers for malaria.

articles.headline: Phase IIb controlled human malaria infection trial (n=17 P. falciparum PfSPZ NF54 challenge; n=19 vaccinated) in semi-immune Kenyan adults found R21/Matrix-M vaccination increased antibody titers across IgA, IgM, IgG1, and IgG3 with complement-fixing capability, boosted memory B cells, and increased CXCR5+/PD1+ cTfh cells (Th2/Th17 predominant). Diverse immune responses suggest R21/Matrix-M protects across varying levels of prior malaria exposure
articles.title_original: R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccine drives diverse immune responses in pre-exposed adults: insights from a phase IIb controlled human malaria infection trial.

articles.headline: Case report: 64-year-old male with P. vivax malaria and severe thrombocytopenia (15,000/mm³), leukocytosis (TLC 29,100/μL), and coagulopathy (INR 4.2). Flow cytometry revealed underlying chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Authors highlight that leukocytosis with thrombocytopenia in malaria without sepsis should raise suspicion of occult CLL
articles.title_original: Underlying Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia in a Case of Vivax Malaria With Thrombocytopenia: A Case Report.

articles.headline: Molecular screening of 527 rodents in rural Gabon (2021–2022) identified P. yoelii in 2 invasive R. rattus individuals (0.38% prevalence), phylogenetically matching native-rodent strains. First detection in invasive rats challenges traditional restriction to native hosts and raises questions about invasive rodents' capacity to integrate into local Plasmodium transmission networks
articles.title_original: Rodent malaria parasites detected in the invasive Rattus rattus in Gabon.

articles.headline: Novel diagnostic platform combining Chelex-100 nucleic acid extraction with multiplex LAMP-MS assay enables simultaneous detection of P. falciparum, P. vivax, pan-malaria, and internal control within 40 minutes with sensitivity and specificity comparable to PCR, offering low-cost, field-deployable solution for resource-constrained settings
articles.title_original: Rapid and Multiplex Diagnosis of Malaria Using Chelex-100 Extraction and LAMP-MS Assay.
articles.authors: Lim MS, Choe YL, Jang WS

articles.headline: Biochemical characterization of four P. falciparum alveolins revealed variable dependence on alveolin domains for cytoskeletal localization: PfIMC1e requires PfIMC1f for basal complex recruitment, while PfIMC1c and PfIMC1g localize independently; each domain contains unique signatures for compartment-specific targeting. Evidence that alveolin domains mediate both alveolin-alveolin interactions and subcellular recruitment
articles.title_original: Malaria Cytoskeletal Proteins Require Alveolin-Alveolin Interactions for Differential Localization: Recruitment and Organization of Alveolin Proteins.

articles.headline: Co-exposure of monocyte-derived dendritic cells to malaria hemozoin and bacterial lipopolysaccharide reduced expression of interferon-γ-regulated genes, HLA-DR, and PD-L1 through NuRD-mediated chromatin remodeling and decreased NF-κB RELA binding. Hemozoin may skew dendritic cell responses to bacterial co-infections toward immunosuppression, potentially explaining increased severity in malaria-bacterial co-infections
articles.title_original: Malaria-derived hemozoin skews dendritic cell responses to bacterial infections by reducing interferon gene-transcription by SWI/SNF-NuRD.

articles.headline: Nine-month longitudinal study in Uganda found antibody affinity (kd) against merozoite antigens AMA1 and MSP2 decreased from birth to 6 months then increased toward mothers' levels by 9 months, while sporozoite CSP affinity remained unchanged. Naturally acquired high-affinity antibodies develop separately across different antigens, with implications for vaccine optimization
articles.title_original: Development of high affinity antibodies to Plasmodium falciparum merozoite and sporozoite antigens during infancy and adulthood.

articles.headline: Spatiotemporal Bayesian analysis of 634 administrative areas across 45 SSA countries (2011–2020) found P. falciparum prevalence in children aged 2–10 decreased from 21.32% to 16.75%; each unit increase in insecticide-treated net users correlated with 34.07% reduction in infection risk. Highest prevalence remains in West-central and Central African regions, requiring intensified vector control efforts
articles.title_original: Effects of vector control interventions on spatio-temporal changes of falciparum malaria risk in children aged 2-10 in sub-Saharan African regions during 2011-2020.

articles.headline: Historical discourse analysis of English-Chinese dictionaries and periodicals (1822–1940s) traces three phases of malaria conceptualization: symptom-based translations (1822–1860s), terminological competition between nueji and zhangqi (1870s–1920s), and institutional standardization with selective assimilation of Western parasitology. 'Plasmodium' was lexicalized as 'nueyuanchong,' but immunity mechanisms remained unexplained in Chinese medical discourse
articles.title_original: Malaria in China: a discourse-historical perspective.

articles.headline: Case report: 22-year-old pregnant woman (9 weeks gestation) from Ethiopia with P. vivax malaria complicated by hemophagocytic syndrome and pancytopenia—first documented case in pregnancy. Presented with fever, hypotension, hepatosplenomegaly, elevated triglycerides and ferritin; achieved full recovery with antimalarial and supportive care. Highlights importance of early P. vivax diagnosis in pregnancy
articles.title_original: A Rare Presentation of Plasmodium vivax Malaria: Pancytopenia due to Hemophagocytic Syndrome in a Pregnant Woman.
articles.authors: Lema GD, Dagnaw AB, Gebeyaw ED

articles.headline: Retrospective analysis of Riau Province, Indonesia (2019–2022) identified 232 recurrent malaria cases among 4,622 total cases; 94.83% were P. vivax, 4.74% P. falciparum, with 69% diagnosed by rapid diagnostic test and 73.45% lacking complete after-treatment evaluation. All recurrent cases occurred >28 days post-initial infection. Malaria relapse remains significant challenge in elimination efforts
articles.title_original: Postmalaria elimination: Overview of recurrent malaria cases in Riau Province, Indonesia.

articles.headline: Case-control study in rural Iraq (150 malaria children vs. 50 controls, aged 5–8) found malaria patients had elevated inflammatory markers (CRP 18.4 vs. 5.2 mg/L, IL-6 10.2 vs. 3.1 pg/mL, TNF-α 12.5 vs. 4.0 pg/mL; all P<0.001), lower hemoglobin (9.8 vs. 12.5 g/dL; P<0.001) and iron (45.3 vs. 90.2 μg/dL; P<0.001), with 65% severe malnutrition, 70% anemia, and 40% blood disorders. Malaria profoundly impairs inflammation, nutrition, and hematology
articles.title_original: Analysis of the relationship between malaria and inflammatory biomarker levels in children in rural Iraq: Impact of nutritional deficiencies, anemia, and hematological disorders.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study (March–July 2024) of 41 humans and 43 cattle in Indonesia using endpoint PCR found all human samples negative (0%, 95% CI 0.0–8.5%) and one cattle sample positive (2.33%, 95% CI 0.06–12.0%) for Plasmodium. Detection of Plasmodium DNA in cattle indicates need for continuous surveillance and integration of animal health into malaria elimination programs under the One Health framework
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium spp. in humans and cattle: Implications for zoonotic malaria transmission in Indonesia.

articles.headline: Case report of two middle-aged men with P. falciparum imported from Africa treated with artemether-lumefantrine (AL) who initially improved but developed clinical failure with recrudescence. One cleared parasites with additional AL; the other required quinine and doxycycline. Cases demonstrate parasitemia clearance by day 3 does not guarantee treatment success, highlighting emerging artemisinin-resistant P. falciparum strains
articles.title_original: [Successful Treatment of Two Cases of Plasmodium falciparum Malaria that Developed Recrudescence after Artemether-Lumefantrine Therapy].

articles.headline: Review of cerebral malaria, the most lethal P. falciparum infection form, defined by unexplained coma with parasitemia. One in 6 children dies; survivors experience neurologic, cognitive, or behavioral sequelae and acute seizures. Review summarizes pathogenesis, neuroimaging/EEG/Doppler findings, clinical management pathways, and care gaps—particularly in rural endemic areas where malaria incidence is highest
articles.title_original: Cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: In vitro evaluation of green-synthesized silver nanoparticles (Ag-NPs) from Aerva lanata against mosquito larvae showed LC50 values of 6.501 ppm (Cx. quinquefasciatus), 20.875 ppm (An. stephensi), and 22.649 ppm (Ae. aegypti). Minimal toxicity to Artemia salina observed. Ag-NPs act via oxidative stress, representing an eco-friendly alternative to synthetic pesticides for vector control
articles.title_original: Eco-Friendly Synthesis of Silver Nanoparticles and Its Larvicidal Property Against Malaria, Filariasis, and Dengue Vectors.

articles.headline: In silico vaccine design targeting four P. falciparum blood-stage proteins (PfPHB1, PfPHB2, PfHSP70, PfGARP) selected 30 epitopes from 133 candidates. Final 570-amino acid construct contained 12 B-cell, 11 CTL, and 7 HTL epitopes with 95.04% predicted global population coverage and strong TLR5 binding. Immune simulation predicted robust response, validating reverse vaccinology for future experimental testing
articles.title_original: Development of a multi-epitope vaccine candidate targeting blood-stage of malaria through immunoinformatics approach.

articles.headline: Population genomic analysis of 31 P. simium and 1,086 P. vivax isolates revealed P. simium is a discrete, recently-originated lineage most similar to P. vivax from Latin America. P. simium has half the nucleotide diversity of P. vivax; sympatric human-monkey isolate pairs were as closely related as meiotic half-siblings. P. simium causes most malaria infections attributed to P. vivax in Serra do Mar, Southeast Brazil
articles.title_original: Plasmodium simium: birth and evolution of a zoonotic malaria parasite species.

articles.headline: Pf8 data resource: 33,325 P. falciparum genome samples from 99 studies, 122 locations, 50+ years with genotypes at 12 million genomic positions. First to include copy-number variation calls in drug-resistance genes (gch1, crt, mdr1, plasmepsin2/3) and deletions in hrp2/hrp3. Provides interactive tools, Python package, and analysis notebooks—the largest open eukaryotic genome variation dataset
articles.title_original: Pf8: an open dataset of Plasmodium falciparum genome variation in 33,325 worldwide samples.

articles.headline: Historical review of Italian scientist Giovanni Battista Grassi (1854–1925), a founder of the 'Italian school of malariology.' Grassi contributed to establishing that Plasmodium (not bacteria) causes malaria and identified the vector for human malaria transmission. His 1898 studies represented a turning point in malaria science during the germ theory era
articles.title_original: Giovanni Battista Grassi (1854-1925): a forgotten Italian scholar and his fundamental studies on malaria.

articles.headline: Case report of 32-year-old male with P. vivax and P. cynomolgi co-infection, initially misdiagnosed as P. vivax only by routine microscopy and qPCR. Real-time PCR and sequencing confirmed co-infection. Case highlights diagnostic limitations of P. cynomolgi, which shares morphological and genetic similarity with P. vivax. P. cynomolgi is emerging as a zoonotic threat in Southeast Asia requiring improved diagnostics
articles.title_original: Molecular and Microscopic Challenges in Detecting Plasmodium cynomolgi Co-Infections with Plasmodium vivax: A Case Report.

articles.headline: In vitro study of Brucea javanica root and fruit extracts against P. falciparum blood-stage parasites showed IC50 values of 0.41±1.14 µg/mL and 0.26±1.15 µg/mL, respectively. Both inhibited ring-stage development, causing nuclear clumping without hemolysis, and showed additive effects with artesunate and chloroquine, reducing IC90 levels. Findings support B. javanica potential for novel antimalarial formulations
articles.title_original: Growth Inhibition and Additive Effect to Antimalarial Drugs of Brucea javanica Extracts on Asexual Blood-Stage Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis of 11 RCTs (21,916 households) comparing long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs): chlorfenapyr achieved 25.96 per 100 children infected vs. 32.38 per 100 (piperonyl butoxide) and 41.60 per 100 (pyrethroid-only control); 10% risk reduction. Vector density reduced 23% (chlorfenapyr), 7% (pyrethroid-only), 12% (piperonyl butoxide). Chlorfenapyr and pyriproxyfen LLINs highly effective for reducing infection and anemia
articles.title_original: Effectiveness and Efficacy of Long-Lasting Insecticidal Nets for Malaria Control in Africa: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
articles.journal: Int J Environ Res Public Health

articles.headline: In silico vaccine design targeting P. falciparum selected 30 epitopes from 133 candidates across variant surface antigens expressed at different parasite life-cycle stages. Final 570-amino acid construct included 12 B-cell, 11 CTL, and 7 HTL epitopes with 95.04% global population coverage and strong predicted molecular binding. Reverse vaccinology demonstrates accelerated pathway for malaria vaccine development toward experimental validation
articles.title_original: Search continues: Exploring immunoinformatics platforms for designing an effective multiepitope malaria vaccine candidate.

articles.headline: In vitro solid-phase binding assays and surface plasmon resonance identified 7 of 10 Pvfam 'a' family proteins (PvTRAg, PvTRAg33.5, PvTRAg35.2, PvTRAg34, PvTRAg36, PvTRAg38, PvTRAg69.4) interacting with the erythrocyte receptor Basigin. Each protein inhibited P. vivax growth in culture; combination treatments showed synergistic growth inhibition, suggesting multiple parasite ligand-receptor interactions are critical for invasion
articles.title_original: Majority of the erythrocyte binding proteins of the Pvfam "a" family of Plasmodium vivax interact with Basigin to assist parasite entry into the host cell.

articles.headline: Mouse model of P. falciparum infection: adoptive transfer of infected donor T cells reproduced tubule-interstitial kidney damage. Selective CD8+ T cell depletion protected from tubular injury, while FTY720 (which sequesters T cells in lymphoid organs) prevented proteinuria and reduced urinary γ-glutamyl transferase. CD8+ T cells specifically mediate tubular pathology in malaria-induced acute kidney injury, independent of glomerular damage
articles.title_original: CD8(+) T cells promote tubule-interstitial damage in malaria-induced acute kidney injury.

articles.headline: OocystMeter, a Mask R-CNN model trained on 11,178 expert-annotated P. falciparum oocysts from A. gambiae midguts, achieved oocyst count accuracy (Spearman ρ=0.998) and size measurement accuracy (ρ=0.978) comparable to parasitologists. Spatial analysis revealed non-random clustered oocyst distribution independent of anatomical regions, particularly in infections <75 oocysts/midgut. Tool reduces human bias and accelerates high-throughput oocyst analysis
articles.title_original: OocystMeter, a machine-learning algorithm to count and measure Plasmodium oocysts, reveals clustering patterns in the Anopheles midgut.

articles.headline: Proof-of-concept study in P. yoelii mouse model comparing two mRNA vaccines encoding PyMSP1/8 (secreted vs membrane-bound) with recombinant protein and lethal P. yoelii 17XL challenge. PyMSP1/8-sec achieved 100% survival with 50% animals showing no detectable parasitemia (<0.01%) and significantly lower peak parasitemia than recombinant vaccine. Membrane-bound PyMSP1/8 provided superior protection, demonstrating mRNA vaccines outperform recombinant formulations for blood-stage malaria
articles.title_original: An mRNA Vaccine Expressing Blood-Stage Malaria Antigens Induces Complete Protection Against Lethal Plasmodium yoelii.
articles.authors: Ott AC, Loll PJ, Burns JM Jr

articles.headline: Deterministic mathematical model analyzing combined vaccination and vector control (mosquito nets, insecticide spraying) for malaria transmission. Sensitivity analysis on R₀ identified key intervention parameters. Current vaccines not 100% effective, but vaccination substantially contributes to malaria control when combined with existing preventive measures. Comprehensive multi-strategy approach essential for reducing transmission in endemic regions
articles.title_original: Vaccination and combined optimal control measures for malaria prevention and spread mitigation.

articles.headline: Mathematical model of ivermectin mass drug administration (MDA) using transmission data from 41 sub-Saharan African countries. Annual MDA to 12.5%, 25%, or 50% of population averted 248.7, 261.4, and 288.7 malaria incidences per 1,000 people and saved 5.4, 5.7, and 6.3 disability-adjusted life years, respectively. Ivermectin MDA would be cost-effective in ≥18 countries and very cost-effective in ≥6 countries across coverage scenarios, substantially preventing malaria in majority of SSA
articles.title_original: Wonder drugs and where to use them: a forecast of ivermectin's impact on malaria in Africa.
articles.authors: Greenhalgh S, Roman A, Provencher A

articles.headline: Sequencing study of putative circumsporozoite protein (PvpuCSP) in 71 P. vivax isolates from endemic regions of India. Repeat region (C-terminal) ranged 1.8–2.2 kb with 37 different alleles identified; N- and C-terminal regions ~80% conserved. Episodic diversifying selection in repeat region (statistically significant p-values and likelihood ratios) indicates recent, exclusive repeat accumulation driving amino acid diversity, establishing PvpuCSP as potential marker for infection complexity
articles.title_original: Evolution of tandem repeats in putative circumsporozoite protein to enhance its function: A recent and exclusive event in Plasmodium vivax in India.

articles.headline: Acridone compound T111 demonstrated potent in vitro activity and ex vivo activity against clinical isolates, achieving oral single-dose cure in asexual blood-stage rodent model and preventing parasite development in mosquitoes. In non-human primate liver cells, T111 inhibited relapsing parasites; showed synergy with tafenoquine against blood and liver stages. Mechanism involves mitochondrial electron transport chain inhibition distinct from existing antimalarials; favorable therapeutic index in rat toxicology studies
articles.title_original: Potent Acridone Antimalarial against All Three Life Stages of Plasmodium.

articles.headline: Unsupervised machine learning and generative deep learning applied to genomic data from Anopheles gambiae complex populations in Guinea and Burkina Faso. Evolutionary model incorporating post-split migration fitted the population pair better than no-migration model. Generative models outperformed summary-statistics methods in capturing genetic differentiation, demonstrating machine learning's utility for inferring vector population structure and adaptation
articles.title_original: On the use of generative models for evolutionary inference of malaria vectors from genomic data.

articles.headline: Genomic analysis of 2,270 P. falciparum infections from 283 malaria posts in Kayin State, Myanmar (2015–2020) during intensive elimination campaign (rapid detection/treatment + MDA). Transmission reduced 97%; parasite effective population size decreased; 1,726 single-genotype infections represented 166 unique genomes; 9 families comprised 62.5% of parasites. By 2020, single predominant kelch13-R561H genotype remained. Sustained localized transmission chains showed positive parasite relatedness ≤20 km, providing genomic evidence for MDA efficacy and parasite population collapse
articles.title_original: Impact of intensive control on malaria population genomics in Eastern Myanmar.

articles.headline: Case report of 28-year-old pregnant woman with severe P. falciparum malaria complicated by massive subamniotic hemorrhage (~800 mL lysed blood in placenta), resulting in preterm delivery. Mother recovered post-delivery; neonate died in ICU from prematurity complications. Highlights severe placental hematoma risk in P. falciparum pregnancy and critical need for early detection and management to mitigate maternal and fetal adverse outcomes
articles.title_original: A rare presentation of massive subamniotic hemorrhage in a pregnant woman with severe malaria: A case report.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study (n=200: malarious and healthy controls) in Nigeria measuring plasma amino acids and micronutrients (Ca, Mg, Na, K, Zn, Fe, Co) by HPLC and ICP-MS. Malaria severity correlated with significant amino acid dysregulation (p<0.01): essential amino acids (leucine, valine) and immune-related amino acids (arginine, tyrosine, tryptophan, glutamine) depleted; deficiencies in Ca, Na, Zn, Fe (p<0.01). Complex metabolic response with nutrient imbalances contributes to disease severity, suggesting potential for targeted nutritional interventions
articles.title_original: Malaria-induced dysregulation of selected micronutrients (Ca, Mg, Na, K, Zn, Fe, Co) and plasma free amino acids; implications for pathogenesis and host immunity in a Nigerian population.
articles.authors: Ekeanyanwu CR, Chikezie PC, Ekeanyanwu CL

articles.headline: CRISPR-generated Pf TRAP glyco-null mutants (T256A, W250F) showed fitness defects comparable to knockout—unable to colonize salivary glands, impaired gliding motility and hepatocyte invasion, and reduced TRAP protein levels despite normal transcripts—indicating TSR glycosylation is critical for proper parasite expression and function in the mosquito stage
articles.title_original: Glycosylation of Plasmodium falciparum TRAP is essential for infection of mosquito salivary glands.

articles.headline: Simulated SPR biosensor configurations achieved sensitivities of 353.14–263.26°/RIU (Sys3) and 315.71–268.65°/RIU (Sys4) for ring, trophozoite, and schizont stages, exceeding comparable platforms, with potential for label-free, stage-specific malaria detection; however, results are from simulation only without experimental validation
articles.title_original: Design and simulation of a graphene-integrated SPR biosensor for malaria detection.

articles.headline: Among 157 P. falciparum isolates from Burundi, no validated artemisinin-resistance kelch13 mutations were found, but a novel A675T mutation was identified; quintuple and sextuple SP-resistance haplotypes occurred in 82.3% and 12.3% of mono-infections respectively, indicating widespread SP resistance threatening IPTp and SMC strategies
articles.title_original: A Novel Plasmodium falciparum Kelch13 A675T Mutation and High Levels of Chloroquine and Sulfadoxine-Pyrimethamine Resistance in Burundi.

articles.headline: Among 903 P. falciparum samples from northwest Ethiopia, kelch13 R622I prevalence reached 44.3% overall (52% in Gondar Zuria vs 40% in Tach Armachiho; p<0.001); R622I mutants showed higher HRP2-based RDT negativity than wild-type (48.3% vs 30.7%; p<0.001) with 98.2% carrying mdr1 NFD; C580Y detected in 2 samples (0.4%), the first African report, suggesting local emergence
articles.title_original: Artemisinin resistant kelch13 R622I and RDT negativity approaching predominance in northern Ethiopia and emerging C580Y of African origin threaten falciparum malaria control.

articles.headline: Gene knockouts of PfRad51 and TLS polymerases (PfPolζ, PfRev1) revealed PfRad51 essential for homologous recombination repair in the conserved core genome, while Rad51-independent homology-directed repair occurred in subtelomeric regions across all knockout lines, demonstrating differential DNA damage responses maintaining chromosomal diversification
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum DNA repair dynamics reveal unique roles for TLS polymerases and PfRad51 in genome diversification.

articles.headline: Among 141 P. falciparum Brazilian samples (2013–2023), SVMNTMCGI pfcrt haplotype occurred in 50%, NYCDY pfmdr1 in 70% with low N86Y at 4%, and no artemisinin-resistance pfk13 mutations were detected, with greater genetic diversity in pfcrt than pfmdr1, supporting ACT efficacy but indicating need for continuous surveillance
articles.title_original: Tracking Drug Resistance in Plasmodium falciparum: Genetic Diversity of Key Resistance Markers in Brazilian Malaria Hotspots.

articles.headline: A compartmental mathematical model revealed that at low mutation rates, optimal malaria control combines antimalarial drugs and MDA with gradual drug reduction, while high mutation rates favor broader MDA deployment with minimal antimalarial use; numerical simulations reinforced that strategic intervention timing is critical for mitigating resistance development
articles.title_original: Optimizing MDA and antimalarial treatment in the presence of drug resistance for effective malaria control.

articles.headline: Phylogenetic reanalysis of ungulate Plasmodium sequences revealed a monophyletic group distinct from primate/rodent/bat parasites, with two main clades; Cephalophus parasites clustered with previously characterized sequences while Tragelaphus and Syncerus formed distinct lineages likely representing novel species, highlighting parasite evolutionary diversity
articles.title_original: Bringing to light unnoticed data on the genetic and host diversity of ungulate Plasmodium.

articles.headline: CD81-targeted, GalNAc-conjugated siRNA efficiently silenced mouse and human hepatic CD81 in vivo, blocking P. falciparum liver infection in dose-dependent manner and preventing blood stage infection in mouse models and human liver-chimeric mice, demonstrating siRNA as a promising host-based approach for malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Protection from malaria infection using liver-targeted siRNA.

articles.headline: A SIRS model fit to Nigerian surveillance data found IRS reduced the malaria transmission rate by 10% over two years in the intervention area compared to control (0.95 vs 1.05), though the difference was marginal, suggesting modest effectiveness likely limited by implementation approach and data quality uncertainties
articles.title_original: Modelling the Effect of Indoor Residual Spraying on Malaria Transmission in Nassarawa State, North-central Nigeria.
articles.authors: Suleiman AG, Kareem L, Obi C

articles.headline: Among 107 asymptomatic pregnant women in Nigeria, P. falciparum prevalence was 14% by microscopy; ultra-sensitive RDT detected 12.1% versus conventional RDT's 2.8%; ultra-sensitive RDT showed superior sensitivity (66.7% vs 13.3%), positive predictive value (83.3% vs 50%), and accuracy (93.5% vs 86%; p=0.002) for low-density infections
articles.title_original: Comparison of diagnostic accuracy of ultra-sensitive and conventional rapid diagnostic tests for malaria in asymptomatic pregnant women at a tertiary hospital in North-Central Nigeria.
articles.authors: Anenga UM, Swende TZ, Hembah-Hilekaan SK

articles.headline: Analysis of 463 imported malaria cases in Chengdu City (2016–2023) found P. falciparum predominant at 71.27%, primarily from Africa (94.17%), with 64.15% reported in Jinjiang District and male-to-female ratio of 21.05:1; initial diagnostic accuracy was 74.78% overall but only 11.11% in township healthcare centers, indicating low diagnostic capacity in grassroots institutions
articles.title_original: [Epidemiological characteristics of imported malaria cases in Chengdu City from 2016 to 2023].
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 496 Nigerian secondary school adolescents (June-Sept 2023) found 68.1% willing to receive malaria vaccine. Adolescents <15 years were 2.2× more likely to accept (AOR=2.2, 95%CI: 1.1-4.7), males 0.6× less likely than females (AOR=0.6, 95%CI: 0.4-0.9), and those with good vaccine knowledge 2.2× more likely (AOR=2.2, 95%CI: 1.4-3.4). Vaccine willingness was independently influenced by age, gender, and knowledge
articles.title_original: Willingness of secondary school adolescents to get vaccinated against Malaria: a cross-sectional study in Enugu, South-East Nigeria.

articles.headline: In vitro and in silico analysis of Propolis extracts from Iranian regions against P. falciparum NF54. Kermanshah Propolis showed highest anti-Plasmodium activity (IC50=6.69±1.44 μg/mL). Molecular docking revealed tectochrysin and galangin bound PfLDH with affinities (-7.8 and -7.5 kcal/mol) exceeding chloroquine and quinine. MD simulations confirmed stable protein-ligand interactions, supporting these compounds as novel PfLDH inhibitors warranting in vivo validation
articles.title_original: In vitro and in silico investigations of Propolis-derived phytochemicals as potential inhibitors of Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: Sequencing 2,270 P. falciparum whole genomes collected over 58 months during Myanmar's malaria elimination program (Nov 2015-Aug 2020) revealed genetically depauperate parasites: 1,726 single-genotype infections comprised 166 unique genomes; nine families accounted for 62.5% of parasites. Kelch13 diversity remained stable 2016-2019, with only kelch13-R561H predominant in 2020, demonstrating MDA efficacy through genomic evidence of parasite founder effects and population decline
articles.title_original: Malaria parasite population genomics during an elimination program in Eastern Myanmar.

articles.headline: In 20 C57BL/6 mice infected with 1×10⁶ P. yoelii, parasitaemia reached 51% and gametocytaemia 1.53% by day 6 (F=165.60, P<0.001 and F=44.58, P<0.001). Anopheles stephensi attraction increased from 50.45% to 66.84% over infection course. Infected mice displayed elevated p-cresol, ethylbenzene, and nonanal but reduced toluene, benzaldehyde, and indole on skin, implicating altered volatile odors in enhanced mosquito attraction during infection
articles.title_original: [Changes in murine skin odors following Plasmodium infections and their impact on mosquito attraction].
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi

articles.headline: Qualitative evaluation of two cohorts in a 4-month faculty enrichment program in malaria modeling at Northwestern University (2022-2023). Participants valued growth in technical expertise, research skills, and communication; instructors adapted approaches based on feedback. Multi-pronged training including technical development, communication, and domain knowledge with continuous feedback mechanisms increases long-term value and establishes knowledge-transfer pathways
articles.title_original: Building contextually-relevant training programs for scientific development: process and lessons learned in implementing two iterations of a faculty enrichment program in applied malaria modeling.

articles.headline: Comparative study of 49 dried blood spot samples evaluated RAA-nanopore sequencing for P. vivax, P. ovale, P. malariae, and P. falciparum species identification versus nPCR gold standard. RAA-nanopore sequencing achieved 92.0% sensitivity, 97.33% specificity, and 96.0% accuracy, superior to RT-qPCR (88.24% sensitivity, 97.32% specificity, 95.0% accuracy), accurately identifying single and mixed infections. This provides a promising supplementary diagnostic approach for Plasmodium species identification
articles.title_original: [Establishment and preliminary evaluation of recombinase-aided isothermal nucleic acid amplification combined with nanopore sequencing for identification of Plasmodium species].
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi

articles.headline: Mini-review of malaria and undernutrition in tribal India, examining their bidirectional relationship within poverty and poor sanitation. Frequent malaria and intestinal infections alter gut flora, exacerbating malabsorption and malnutrition. An integrated approach addressing both nutritional and malaria-specific needs is required to eliminate malaria from tribal areas by breaking the cycle of hunger and infectious disease
articles.title_original: Malaria and malnutrition in tribal areas of India: implications for paediatric health.
articles.authors: Verma AK, Shrivastava S, Saxena N

articles.headline: Review synthesizing mechanisms of Plasmodium immune escape: antigenic variation, epigenetic regulation, antagonism against IgM antibodies, cGAS-STING signaling activation, splenic immune suppression, and molecular camouflage. Understanding these diverse evasion strategies informs development of novel malaria vaccines and antimalarial agents and informs malaria control strategy formulation
articles.title_original: [Progress of researches on mechanisms underlying immune escape of Plasmodium].
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi

articles.headline: Pre-post comparison of 51 health facilities in rural Burkina Faso evaluating indirect benefits of monthly sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine SMC during high malaria season. Pneumonia declined 0.7 per 1,000 person-weeks (95%CI: 0.3–1.0), diarrhea 0.2 (95%CI: 0.1–0.4), acute malnutrition 0.05 (95%CI: 0.001–0.09), and antibiotic prescriptions 0.90 (95%CI: 0.4–1.4); malaria diagnoses declined 3.7 per 1,000 person-weeks (95%CI: 2.6–4.8). SMC has benefits beyond malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Indirect Benefits of Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention for Non-Malarial Pediatric Infections and Routine Antibiotic Use in Real-World Programmatic Settings: A Pre-Post Study Using Positive and Negative Controls.

articles.headline: Case report of 38-year-old Pakistani male presenting with cerebral malaria and multi-organ dysfunction syndrome caused by P. vivax (confirmed by blood smear and PCR), treated with IV artesunate and supportive intensive care with full neurological recovery. This first documented case in Oman highlights P. vivax as a severe malaria cause capable of producing life-threatening complications beyond endemic regions
articles.title_original: Cerebral Malaria and Multi-Organ Dysfunction Caused by Plasmodium vivax in an Adult: A case from Oman with a literature review.

articles.headline: Narrative review (2014-2024) of malaria mitigation in sub-Saharan Africa found RTS,S/AS01 vaccine efficacy 30–55% in children <5 in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi; R21/Matrix-M achieved up to 77% efficacy. Implementation challenges include limited funding and healthcare infrastructure gaps. Vaccination is proposed as a cost-effective, scalable strategy for malaria eradication and achievement of SDG 3 by 2030
articles.title_original: Mitigation of Malaria in Sub-Saharan Africa through Vaccination: A Budding Road Map for Global Malaria Eradication.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional study of 120 asymptomatic hepatitis B surface antigen carriers in Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire (2024) found rheumatoid factor positivity prevalence 12.5% (95%CI: 6.54–18.46). Advanced age ≥30 years was independently associated with positivity (aOR: 3.32; 95%CI: 1.06–10.39; p=0.04). Monitoring rheumatoid factor in older HBsAg carriers may improve assessment of autoimmune risks and diagnostic accuracy
articles.title_original: Prevalence of rheumatoid factor in asymptomatic chronic hepatitis B surface carriers in the context of endemic malaria: cross-sectional study at University Hospital of Cocody, Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire.

articles.headline: Review of health education strategies (community-based, school-based, entry-exit personnel, media) during China's malaria elimination progress, certified malaria-free by WHO in 2021. Health education increased public awareness and prevention action capability, and reduces re-established imported malaria risk in the post-elimination stage
articles.title_original: [Application of health education during the progress towards malaria elimination in China: a review].
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi

articles.headline: Comparison of four Pf/Pan rapid diagnostic test kits for P. vivax using PCR-confirmed samples. Sensitivity: 70.9%–96.5% overall, 43.8%–93.8% in low-parasitemia samples; specificity: 100% for all kits. Results identify differences in RDT effectiveness for P. vivax diagnosis
articles.title_original: [Comparison of Rapid Diagnostic Tests for the Detection of Plasmodium vivax Malaria].

articles.headline: Two chromosomal genome assemblies for Anopheles darlingi (malaria mosquito) from French Guiana and Peru, each approximately 180 megabases in span, with complete mitochondrial genomes of 15.4 kilobases. Assemblies differ by two inversions in chromosome arm 2R
articles.title_original: Two chromosomal reference genome sequences for the malaria mosquito, Anopheles ( Nyssorhynchus) darlingi, Root, 1926 from French Guiana and Peru.

articles.headline: In vitro testing of chloroquine, saffron, and chitosan-conjugated nanocomposites against P. falciparum 3D7 and K1 strains. Sensitive strain IC50s: chloroquine 0.3, nanoparticle-chloroquine 0.8, saffron 42.5, nanoparticle-saffron 6.24 μg/ml; resistant strain: 5, 1, 12.5, 3.12 μg/ml respectively. Combination therapy showed synergistic effects (P<0.05)
articles.title_original: In Vitro Antimalarial Activity of Chloroquine-Crocus Sativus Conjugated to Chitosan Nanocomposits against 3D7 and K1 Strains of Plasmodium falciparum.

articles.headline: 22-year-old with severe P. falciparum malaria (parasitemia 9.7%), acute kidney injury (serum creatinine 3.52 mg/dL), pulmonary edema, and jaundice treated with IV artesunate (6 days), oral dihydroartemisinin/piperaquine (3 days), primaquine, and hemodialysis. Full recovery without sequelae achieved. Early diagnosis and appropriate treatment enable full recovery from AKI in severe P. falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: Managing Acute Kidney Injury in Severe Falciparum Malaria: Insights from a Challenging Case.

articles.headline: Experimental infection of juvenile Eurasian siskins with P. relictum (SGS1) and P. ashfordi (GRW2), measuring resting metabolic rate and interleukin-6 levels. SGS1 infection produced reduced resting metabolic rate and IL-6; GRW2 initially reduced IL-6 (not sustained) with distinct resting metabolic rate dynamics. Study did not conclusively demonstrate tropical malaria has more severe effects on siskins
articles.title_original: Dynamics of resting metabolic rate and innate immune response in malaria-infected Eurasian siskins.

articles.headline: In low malaria transmission areas, most pregnancy infections are undetected by standard diagnostics. Undiagnosed asymptomatic infections have substantial negative impacts for both mother and developing fetus
articles.title_original: Detrimental infant and maternal outcomes of undiagnosed asymptomatic malaria in pregnancy.

articles.headline: Cross-sectional survey of 200–230 children (age 6–13 years) across four ecological strata in Bamako, Mali. P. falciparum prevalence: 0%–15.5% between sites. Anemia increased infection odds (OR=2.38, 95% CI 1.57–3.61); riverside urban location (OR=3.37, 95% CI 2.05–7.45). Travel history and fever not associated (p>0.05)
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium falciparum infection in school-aged children in urban and peri-urban schools of Bamako, Mali.

articles.headline: Historical review of WHO records found the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) was declared malaria-free by 1979, not the Republic of Korea (ROK) as commonly believed. Misattribution originated from U.S. Department of Defense documents misreading WHO records and has persisted through unverified academic citations
articles.title_original: Misattribution of Korea's malaria elimination status in 1979.

articles.headline: In vivo study in female C57BL/6 mice infected with P. chabaudi, treated with Indigofera oblongifolia leaf extracts with silver nanoparticles (IOLE AgNPs) or chloroquine phosphate. IOLE AgNPs effectively restored splenic architecture, reduced inflammatory markers, and improved immune responses compared to control and chloroquine-treated groups
articles.title_original: Histopathologic effect and immunomodulatory of Indigofera oblongifolia with silver nanoparticles against splenic injury by Plasmodium chabaudi infection in mice.

articles.headline: Review of P. falciparum η-carbonic anhydrase (PfCA), involved in pyrimidine biosynthesis and considered a potential antimalarial drug target. In vitro inhibitors with low nanomolar activity identified; acetazolamide and an ureido-substituted benzenesulfonamide showed potent P. falciparum growth inhibition in infected red blood cells. Results are preliminary; further investigation of structure and physiological roles needed
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum η-carbonic anhydrase.

articles.headline: Molecular analysis of drug resistance genes in 9 human P. cynomolgi samples (Cambodia) and 29 macaque samples (Thailand). Human samples 100% identical to RO strain with no DHFR mutations but one DHPS mutation (V451I); macaque samples showed PcyDHFR mutations N44T and C49S with haplotypes (72.41% wild-type I7, 27.59% I7 mutation). Molecular docking suggested mutations did not significantly affect antifolate drug binding affinity
articles.title_original: Molecular analysis of DHFR and DHPS gene mutations in Plasmodium cynomolgi from humans and macaques in Southeast Asia.

articles.headline: In a comparative analysis of mRDT interpretations in Kano, Nigeria, AI algorithms achieved weighted F1 scores of 96.4 vs. 95.3 for frontline health workers. AI demonstrated superior accuracy for faint positives (90.2% vs. 76.1% for FHWs) while matching expert panel readers at 96.38% for positives and 97.12% for negatives. AI-based computer vision can enhance mRDT interpretation accuracy in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Malaria RDT (mRDT) interpretation accuracy by frontline health workers compared to AI in Kano state, Nigeria.

articles.headline: A review of emerging malaria diagnostic tools examines novel biosensor technologies using nanotechnology as alternatives to conventional detection methods. Egypt achieved malaria-free status in 2024, though countries including Nigeria, Kenya, Ethiopia, and others remain high-burden zones with over 435,000 annual deaths. These user-friendly point-of-care devices promise rapid, sensitive, cost-effective diagnosis with minimal technical expertise in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: Advances in malaria detection.

articles.headline: This review examines IgA's emerging role in malaria immunity: evidence shows IgA can block parasite entry, activate complement, and modulate inflammation. Malaria caused an estimated 263 million cases and 597,000 deaths in 2023. While protective efficacy of IgA responses against sporozoites and merozoites remains unestablished, the review identifies critical gaps and proposes vaccine platforms (oral, nasal, prime-boost) to harness IgA-driven mucosal immunity alongside IgG
articles.title_original: Targeting mucosal immunity in malaria control: the underexplored role of IgA.

articles.headline: Analysis of Global Burden of Disease 2021 data from 1990-2021 revealed that both case numbers and age-standardized rates for NTDs and malaria showed recent downward trends. Estimated annual percentage changes correlated positively with Human Development Index scores. Projections indicate continued decline through 2046, with higher-SDI regions showing greater reduction potential, though significant disparities persist across countries and regions
articles.title_original: Global, regional, and national burden of neglected tropical diseases and malaria, 1990-2021.

articles.headline: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 94 studies identified VK210 as the most prevalent P. vivax circumsporozoite protein variant globally. Of 94 Anopheles species analyzed, 22 (23.4%) showed susceptibility to PvCSP variants: 100% to VK210 mono-infections, 63.6% to VK247, and 27.3% to mixed infections. The rare P. vivax-like variant was identified exclusively in Brazil, indicating vector competence and environmental factors drive regional variant distribution
articles.title_original: Updated global distribution of Plasmodium vivax circumsporozoite protein variants and their correlation with vector susceptibility: A systematic review and meta-analysis.

articles.headline: Qualitative interviews with 10 key informants from GFATM-supported programmes in Ghana identified four priority-setting approaches: health needs identification, stakeholder participation, process transparency, and contextual factors. However, public involvement was limited, and major barriers included inadequate funding, alignment with funder priorities, and process interruptions. Explicit priority-setting approaches were underutilized despite their proven importance for resource allocation in low- and middle-income countries
articles.title_original: What are the priority-setting approaches for HIV/AIDS, TB and malaria programmes in Ghana? A qualitative perspective from key informants.

articles.headline: MoTissU-ExM, a new expansion microscopy method, physically expands dissected mosquito salivary glands and midguts ~4.5-fold while preserving host and parasite ultrastructure. The method enables visualization of oocysts and sporozoites in situ within tissues. Samples expanded as expected, providing direct before-and-after comparison. MoTissU-ExM offers potential for studying malaria parasite cell biology in its natural mosquito tissue context
articles.title_original: Mosquito Tissue Ultrastructure-Expansion Microscopy (MoTissU-ExM) enables ultrastructural and anatomical analysis of malaria parasites and their mosquito.

articles.headline: In vitro testing of ellagic acid against chloroquine-sensitive (3D7) and chloroquine-resistant (W2) P. falciparum achieved IC50 values of 1.0 ± 0.7 nM and 1.3 ± 0.1 nM (P < 0.001), surpassing artemisinin (IC50-3D7 = 2.2 ± 0.2 nM; IC50-W2 = 4.3 ± 0.3 nM). Transmission electron microscopy revealed ellagic acid disrupts hemoglobin metabolism in the early trophozoite stage, indicating potential as an antimalarial targeting hemoglobin digestion in both strains
articles.title_original: Effects of Ellagic Acid on Hemoglobin Ingestion, Transport, and Digestion in the Digestive Vacuole of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Thuduhena AC, Abu-Bakar N, Hamzah SN

articles.headline: A case report describes a returned traveler to South Africa with a false-positive malaria rapid diagnostic test who responded quickly to atovaquone-proguanil, though subsequent RDT and malaria blood films failed to confirm infection. Rickettsial serology identified African tick bite fever as the actual diagnosis. The case demonstrates the importance of considering diagnostic test performance characteristics and avoiding premature diagnostic closure in febrile returned travelers
articles.title_original: False-Positive Malaria Rapid Diagnostic Test Likely Due to African Tick Bite Fever: A Case Report.

articles.headline: A chromosomal reference genome assembly for Anopheles nili (malaria mosquito) from Cameroon spans 195 megabases, with most sequence scaffolded into three chromosomal pseudomolecules and the X chromosome fully assembled. The complete mitochondrial genome was sequenced at 15.4 kilobases. This genomic resource provides a reference for studying malaria vector biology and transmission
articles.title_original: A chromosomal reference genome sequence for the malaria mosquito, Anopheles nili, Theobald, 1904.

articles.headline: A longitudinal study of 768 malaria-confirmed patients in Western Kenya (December 2019-November 2020) found P. falciparum comprised 98.7% of infections, with P. malariae, P. vivax, and P. ovale at 1.3%. Age, gender, and location showed effects on prevalence (R² = 0.7, p < 0.136). Malaria prevalence varied seasonally with rainfall and temperature changes, indicating spatio-temporal factors significantly influence local transmission patterns
articles.title_original: Spatio-temporal characterization of malaria prevalence in a peri-urban resource limited setting of Western Kenya Highlands.

articles.headline: A retrospective analysis of 122 severe malaria patients in Rwanda (2016-2017) found 16.3% developed acute kidney injury (AKI). Of 20 AKI cases, 55% recovered completely, 15% died, and 30% were transferred for hemodialysis. Significant clinical factors associated with AKI included dehydration (p=.01), high-grade fever (p=.002), profuse sweating (p=.034), vomiting (p=.043), and diarrhea (p=.025). Enhanced healthcare provider education on AKI management in complicated malaria is needed
articles.title_original: Factors associated with acute kidney injury and outcomes in patients with malaria in a district hospital in Rwanda.

articles.headline: A cross-sectional survey of 250 rural rice farmers in central Kenya found that 62% of women and 47% of men believed in a cultural concept of 'malaria into typhoid progression.' Women were significantly more likely than men to hold this belief (adjusted odds ratio 2.15; 95% CI 1.21–3.79; p = 0.009), suggesting gender-based differences in cultural health beliefs. The authors conclude that these culturally-mediated gender disparities in malaria knowledge and beliefs may influence women's health outcomes
articles.title_original: The relationship between gender and cultural beliefs of malaria into typhoid progression among rural rice farmers in Central Kenya.
